boardgame: Heroscape: Gelryie Vanguards and Krakenling Common Army Expansion
description:
This pack contains common units. This means you can use as many of each figure or squad in your army as point values allow!

The endless wars that rage across the land and skies of Valhalla have sent ripples deep into the planet’s enigmatic and dangerous seas. Breaking a decades-long policy of nonintervention, the Kyrie’s aquatic cousins, the Gelryie, have sent their first wave of soldiers surging to the surface, unfathomable monsters in tow.

This common army expansion contains one 4-figure common squad and one common hero players can use to customize their armies! The Gelryie Vanguards rise with the tide and can surge forward and strike without warning with Tidal Advance. The Krakenling can teleport across the battlefield, swap places with enemies, and unleash multiple attacks upon your foes.

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

boardgame: Heroscape: The Ruins at Laur's Edge Terrain Expansion
description:
Expand your Heroscape battlefield with The Ruins at Laur’s Edge Terrain Pack! This set provides players with versatile Laur wall terrain pieces that can be used to create fortifications and obstacles. Designed to be stackable, the wall pieces allow for multi-level structures and customizable layouts, enhancing both strategy and immersion.

This expansion contains over 30 Wall Terrain pieces and is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions. Build new environments over which to battle for Valhalla in Heroscape: The Ruins at Laur’s Edge! This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for Heroscape gameplay

Alter your battlefields with obstacles that hinder movement and block Line of Sight! Give a ruined aesthetic to your tabletop with crumbling walls, gates, towers, and stack the ruins to make the dynamic walls stand out even more!

The rules for the wall pieces are that they block Clear Sight and Line of Sight unless a power or ability of a figure allows it.

Units cannot move through or over the walls unless they fit through the gate pieces, have abilities like Flying, Tunneling, Grapple guns, or other special movement

boardgame: Heroscape: Roads of Valhalla Terrain Expansion
description:
Much of Valhalla’s infrastructure has crumbled over the past decade of unending conflict. While monuments and works of art have long since collapsed, the Valkyrie generals still maintain a network of old stone roads and bridges to keep their armies supplied—for in Valhalla, all roads lead to WAR.

Add speed and strategy to your battlefield maps! Includes road terrain tiles that boost movement and short walls that attach to create custom bridges–perfect for controlling the flow of battle and building epic new maps. This terrain expansion is fully compatible with all Heroscape terrain. Mix and match this expansion with any terrain for seamless integration into your collection.

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for Heroscape gameplay

Moving on Road Spaces: When moving your figures on road spaces, count each space as a normal space. However, if your figure's entire move consists of road spaces, including the starting space, you may add an additional 3 spaces to your figure's move. The 3 additional spaces must also be on road spaces.

Short Stone Walls rules: They are height 3, with the inside having a height of 2 and the outside having a height of 3. Figures can climb over stone walls, but it requires sufficient movement and height. They also block line of sight. The Walls also have Line of Sight marks for tracing from behind the wall.

Laur Walls Rules: Walls block Line of Sight, Clear Sight, and Movement for some characters.

—description from the publisher

Deflectatron is available as a pre-order promo for the Fall 2025 Heroscape Embers of War releases AND will be available as an organized play promo by playing in local game store tournaments in October and November of 2025!

Alpha Prime was once a thriving world called Mariedian, but it was changed forever by the discovery that it was possible to place a living soul inside a metal shell. The companies that offered this technology became superpowers, promising immortality in exchange for service. Some, like the Omnicron Corporation, saw becoming a Soulborg as a path that every Mariedian
had the right to choose or not, but the ZETTI Combine had a darker ambition: to forcibly transform the population into ZETTI soldiers and take absolute power on Alpha Prime. And so a civil war began that has raged for thousands of years.

In time, the war reached far beyond Alpha Prime, and the ZETTI Combine established prison colonies on nearby planets like Isadora, where the remaining Mariedians who resisted the Combine were taken. Key to the ZETTI Combine’s space power were orbital stations that hovered just above the atmosphere, deploying support for their ground forces at a moment’s notice.

All too often, Mariedian resistance fighters firing lasers at slow-marching ZETTI legions would see a blaze of fire descend from the smoldering sky as a Deflectatron was deployed into the metal wasteland. After landing on burning jets, the strange metal guardian spun steadily and projected a field of blinding orange light. Lasers fired at the shield glanced off harmlessly, granting the mechanical infantry safe passage as they drew relentlessly closer to the Mariedian frontline.

When the Valkyrie Vydar first summoned Deflectatrons to Valhalla, he was fascinated to hear of this technology, and he set a secret project in motion to establish Heimdall Station in orbit over Valhalla. Vydar has since disappeared, but a twinkling light that travels in Valhalla’s sky tells his followers that he is always watching, and when they most need help, a Deflectatron is quickly sent to their aid.

MAGNETIC DEFLECTOR FIELD
Figures adjacent to one or more Deflectatrons cannot
determine clear sight to any figures. When a figure
adjacent to one or more of your Deflectatrons defends
against an attack from a non-adjacent figure, change 1
die result that was not a shield to a shield, if possible.
Magnetic Deflector Field is negated while adjacent to
another figure with the Magnetic Deflector Field
Special Power.

ORBITAL DEPLOYMENT 3
Deflectatrons do not start the game on the battlefield.
At the start of a round, before placing Order Markers,
you may place 1 Deflectatron that has not entered the
battlefield on an empty space within 3 clear sight
spaces of a friendly figure. You cannot use more than
one Orbital Deployment Special Power each round.
PASSIVE

Deflectratons never make leaving engagement attacks.

—description from the publisher

Warlords is a 2 player game based on the traditional game of War with many new strategy components and incredible artwork.

Welcome to OPHYRION, Kingdom of Warlords, where four clans battle for control over the Realm.  Deploy your army with strategic moves to win critical wars on the Battlefield and capture your enemy's most valuable cards.

On the distant world of Ophyrion, an ancient race lives amongst the Dragons that reign supreme.  Through centuries of battle, four clans emerged from the wreckage to claim their territories and preserve their way of life.

The Knights of Jalindhor
The Vikings of Vanaterron
The Samurai of Rytheria
The Orcs of Typhar

Along with powerful Wizards and Phantoms of the underworld, the four clans wage a new war for control over the realm known as, the Kingdom of Warlords.

description:
Become a conservation hero through an exciting trading card game with meaningful impact in the real world. With every FasciNature booster pack you directly plant a real tree in a vital conservation hotspot.

Discover extraordinary wildlife, explore remarkable facts, and master conservation skills as you build your unique collection. Every card you collect deepens your understanding of nature, enabling you to actively contribute to global conservation efforts. Engage friends, family, and colleagues by igniting their passion for wildlife and conservation, creating positive change together.

Easy, Engaging Gameplay

FasciNature’s gameplay is simple yet thrilling:


     Start with 5 cards in your hand, drawing a new card each turn.
     Challenge opponents with fascinating wildlife questions.
     Answer correctly to win cards and expand your collection with every victory.
     Finish the game with the most cards and triumph as a conservation champion.


Each booster pack contains 15 collectable cards, tailored to various knowledge levels (easy, medium, hard), ensuring inclusive, enjoyable competition for all ages and abilities.

Exclusive and Meaningful Collectibles

The first edition offers 300 beautifully designed cards with three tiers of rarity—common, uncommon, and rare. Exclusive, ultra-rare collector boosters featuring Endangered, Critically Endangered, and Extinct species are available solely during our limited-time Kickstarter campaign. These special cards highlight the importance of wildlife protection, making your collection truly exceptional and impactful.

Join the FasciNature community today! Play a game you love, build a collection you treasure, and make a real-world difference in conservation.

—description from the publisher

Cabana is the perfect tourist spot—a magical destination filled with beautiful scenery, exotic birds, marvelous monkeys and stunning ocean views. It’s the ideal location to build a new resort!

You are all contracted builders charged with building the most impressive resort possible. You have just three seasons to construct your dream creation before next year’s summer crowd arrives. Who will create the most impressive new structure to become the crown jewel of the tropical island of Cabana?

Players will take turns placing their workers into a central 3x3 grid, taking the three actions in that row or column. Those actions help recruit new workers to gain ongoing abilities, personal objectives, resources to help build the stackable hotel buildings, and other island resort amenities.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lone Wolf: Vengeance of the Kai
description:
Lone Wolf: Vengeance of the Kai is an asymmetrical 2-player strategy game set in the world of Lone Wolf - the fantasy setting of the series first launched 40 years ago.  It encompasses the storyline of the first 12 books in the series, allowing players to relive the decisive battle between good (Lone Wolf and the Freeland Alliance) and evil (the Darklords of Helgedad and their allies) for the fate of Magnamund, with different outcomes every time.

The game lasts 6 rounds, with each side pursuing different objectives to win.  Game rounds consist of 3 phases - the Action Phase, Quest Phase and Refresh Phase.

The Freeland Alliance begins the game with less resources (troops, gold) and must survive long enough for Lone Wolf to succeed at five (5) Quests to recover the power and wisdom of his slaughtered Order (the Kai Lords of Sommerlund) to destroy the Darklords.

The Darklords possess a more powerful army and greater wealth, which they must use to conquer 5 of 9 strategic Freeland capitals before Lone Wolf succeeds at his Quests.  If neither side achieves their objective by the end of the 6th game round, the winner is determined by whoever accumulated more Victory Points, which are gained by succeeding at Quests (Freeland Alliance only), controlling strategic capitals or damaging/destroying enemy Leaders. The game has multiple sources of variability - mix of Leaders, starting territories, card draws - that allow for endless re-playability (no 2 games will ever be the same). The game play is tense, with every action a trade-off between advancing your own agenda or hindering your opponent's.  Seemingly inconsequential actions taken early in the game often turn out to have major repercussions in later rounds.

—description from the designer

What makes up a country? How can you distinguish the French, English, or Poles from Ukrainians in today's world? Play Alias: Ukraine to learn more or show off your knowledge! This themed version of the popular word-explaining game opens up new horizons.

Language and traditions, clothing and cuisine, symbols and landmarks, hetmans and poets, singers and scientists, songs and TV shows, books and cities, mountains and rivers, fairy-tale characters and fashion designers, holidays and sports competitions, travel agencies, radio stations, movies — how well do you know everything Ukrainian? Keep in mind that around 60% of the words are directly related to Ukraine.

Alias is a team game that tests your awareness, knowledge, and love for the country you live in - through eloquence, quick thinking, and the ability to express ideas clearly.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: HISPANOAMERICA: A Story of Civilization, Discovery and Christianity
description:
HISPANOAMERICA: A Story of Civilization, Discovery and Christianity is a game for 1 to 4 players and between 90 and 180 minutes in length that takes place during the more than three centuries in which America was part of the Spanish Crown and Spain dominated the Atlantic and Pacific oceans. From the end of the fifteenth century and the arrival on the island of Guanahaní in the Antilles of Christopher Columbus in 1492, until the end of the eighteenth century, the game shows the main historic milestones that occurred in the three centuries of history through a main mechanic of Action card game selection and exploration. It attempts to show in a condensed way the civilizing action of Spain in the New World: discovery, exploration, conquest, liberation, alliances with indigenous peoples, miscegenation, founding of cities, education, trade, exchange, evangelization, laws and governance, seeking to reproduce the concept of Spain and its culture in the overseas provinces.

In Hispanoamerica, players compete to explore and discover the New World, as well as to participate in the discoveries, evangelization, and cultural development of the Americas. The game is divided into 4 Eras in which players take turns performing their actions. ERA I runs between 1492 and 1520, ERA II between 1521 and 1550, Era III between 1551 and 1600, and ERA IV, between 1600 and the end of the eighteenth century (1800).

Each player will have 5 action cards that will be displayed on their personal board in positions between 1 and 5, which will mark the force (F) with which the action is performed. On each player's turn, one of the 5 action cards available on their personal board will be selected, with different strength depending on the position of the card. After the selected action has been carried out, the played card will move to position 1 by moving all the other cards to the right, and the turn will pass to the next player. Players will play successively until the end of the game occurs. Certain actions will cause the end of a round and the advancement of the eras.

As game develops, players will advance in 3 different tracks in the main board to show the civilization advance in the 3 categories of the game: Discovery, Culture and Evangelization, as they found cities, play historic milestone cards or construct relevant buildings.

After the end of Era IV or when players reach a certain level of discovery, cultural development, and evangelization, the end of the game is triggered. The player who causes the end of the game receives the card “Isabel La Católica” that will grant him/her 5 VPs, and the rest of the players can perform one last action.

—description from the designer

description:
Step into the role of a forensic investigator in this competitive true crime autopsy game. Examine cadavers by removing layers of cards from specific body parts to uncover hidden clues, then scan QR codes to access lab results, witness interviews, and police reports. Use your tools and deductive skills to determine the manner of death before your opponents. With three unique cases, immersive storytelling, and realistic evidence, it’s a race to see who can solve the mystery first.

BECOME A FORENSIC INVESTIGATOR: Compete against others in a true crime game where you perform detailed autopsies, using only the physical evidence from the body to uncover the true cause of death.
INTERACTIVE AUTOPSY MECHANICS: Use the realistic scalpel to peel back layers of cards from specific body parts, revealing evidence and critical information buried within.
SCAN FOR REALISTIC CLUES: Unlock lab reports, police investigations, and witness interviews by scanning QR codes with your phone for a multi-layered, immersive experience.
RACE TO SOLVE THE CASE: The first player to correctly diagnose the manner of death wins — test your investigative skills and see who can crack the case fastest.
INCLUDES THREE IMMERSIVE CASES: Each cadaver offers about an hour of gameplay with unique storytelling, detailed clues, and authentic artifacts for a truly gripping experience.

—description from the publisher

In Absolute, players make sets of 3 or more cards whose sum equals zero.  Swap cards with played sets, add cards to your sets, or regroup your sets for more points. Cards are colored blue/white for positive numbers or red/black for negative numbers.

Play Description: Each turn you draw a card from the deck or discard pile. You can swap cards up to 2 cards in your hand with up to 2 cards in any one set on the table  (yours or another player's). This can help get you get the numbers or suits you need for better hands, decrease the value of another player's set, or both.

Then you can play your own sets of cards, add cards to your existing sets, or play cards to regroup your sets for more points.

Play 3 sets and discard your last card to "go out" to end a round.  The player with the most points after 3 rounds wins.

—description from the publisher

Hanzō is a solo and 1v1 print & play game, inspired by the legend of Hattori Hanzō — the ninja who walked the line between discipline and deception. Armed with just four dice and a pencil, you'll infiltrate a samurai village, recover ten sacred weapons, and ascend to the Eleventh House — the place of ninja enlightenment. Each turn is a tactical decision. Now the little forest town of Shaddis Horne, with its elemental college, is under siege. The fairies know that without this source of magic, Brighthelm’s chemists will be dealt a severe blow. They must be stopped. Your Hawkguard soldiers have been sent to repel the fairies and their minions, inspire the villagers and keep Shaddis Horne standing until the siege is over. It won't be easy.

Battle of the Gods  is a game for 3 to 5 players, where the gods fight to see who will be the most worshipped! Choose between God, Zeus, Odin, Ra or Oxalá and use your entities, accessories and magic to convert the largest number of believers. In the end, whoever converts the most will be the winner. Simple to learn, fun and strategic, Battle of the Gods is the official game of Um Sábado Qualquer in partnership with Galápagos.
Each game can take from 30 minutes to 1 hour, depending on the number of players. In addition to being strategic and fun, it also has historical and educational value, as most of the cards feature personalities, moments and characteristics that define each religion. It brings a lightheartedness to playing with the gods and provides an understanding of religious diversity.

boardgame: Volley & Bayonet Road to Glory scenarios: Battles of the War of Spanish Succession
description:
The lack of a clear successor to the Spanish throne resulted in a 14 year war between the great Houses of Bourbon (France) and Hapsburg (Holy Roman Empire) the result of which spread war and carnage across much of Western Europe, Italy, and the Iberian Peninsula....

The War of Spanish Succession played out in some of the largest and bloodiest battles of the era. Battles where commanders argued with each other, poor reconnaissance led to costly attacks and stubbornness and pride cost enormous casualties.

This book contains scenarios for 19 of the most important battles—some large and some small.

Each battle includes a Strategic overview, a summary of the historic battle, detailed game maps, roster sheets for each army, deployment, game length and victory conditions. The scenarios are formatted for Volley & Bayonet: Road to Glory rules but should be suitable for conversion to other rules.

—description from the publisher

In Heróis e Monstros ("Heroes and Monsters"), players must work together to ensure the survival of the Last Stronghold and its inhabitants. At the start of the game, each hero will control a General and will have access to a limited set of resources that they must manage by purchasing new Buildings for the Stronghold, recruiting new Units to protect it, or activating powerful Effects from these purchased cards.

The types of resources and the quantity of each resource will dictate which cards are best to purchase. Generals with more Roots control more Buildings and Greenskin Units. Generals with more Soul Crystals control more Immortals; and Generals with more Sacrifices control more Draconians.

At the end of the round, players will be attacked by Invading Units of the Order of Light, which will reduce the number of cards in the Stronghold’s deck. Additionally, each game has an Adversary that will grow stronger as the Stronghold weakens, and will enact plans to try to bring defeat to the players.

The objective of each match is to keep the Fortress standing, with cards in your deck, and defeat the Adversary's plans.

With its compact components, no setup, and the ability to pause and resume anytime, Pencil Book Quest is perfect for commutes, waiting rooms, standing in line, or any moment when your brain needs a short side quest.

description:
Behold! The fast paced and family friendly game of Biscuits & Battles. The only games that brings the age old steampunk sport of Tea Dueling right to you. So, grab your fancy hats and goggles and enjoy this silly and exciting game.

Biscuits and Battles is a fast-paced and whimsical card game inspired by the eccentric tradition of tea dueling. Players take on the role of duelists, settling their disputes not with swords, but with tea and biscuits—represented here by numbered cards and crumb-tracking dice.

Each round, players secretly arrange three cards in order and reveal them one at a time. In each of the three mini-duels per round, the highest card wins while all others collect a "crumb." Once a duelist collects six crumbs, their cookie crumbles and they're out of the game. Special zero cards add surprise twists and strategic options, such as swapping cards or stealing from neighbors. The last cookie left uncrumbled wins!

It’s light, funny, and full of opportunities for playful trash talk—perfect for quick, silly showdowns.

Avoid the mess, betray the rest!
Unexpected Shituation is a fast-paced, humor-packed party card game full of chaos, sabotage, and survival — all wrapped in a delightfully crappy theme. In this game, you're not just holding "bad cards" metaphorically... you're literally holding poop cards. And you’ll do anything to get rid of them before they bury you.

But escaping the mess isn’t easy. Not only do your so-called friends constantly try to dump trouble on you — the game itself is out to get you. Unstoppable event cards strike at the worst possible moments, forcing you to adapt (or collapse) when you least expect it. One moment you’re safe, the next you're neck-deep in a Shituation.

The core objective is simple: get rid of every card in your hand. Each turn starts by drawing 2 cards, followed by playing 2 cards from your hand. Sounds manageable, right? Not quite. If your hand gets clogged with poop and you can’t legally play two cards — boom, you're out. It’s a dirty game, and the crap comes quick.

With constant player interaction, unpredictable twists, and more than one way to win (or lose horribly), Unexpected Shituation delivers a wild experience in just 15–20 minutes. It’s not just a game — it’s a toilet-flushing test of friendship.

—description from the designer

Experience the thrill of the tennis world with Tiebreak Triumph! Step into the shoes of a professional tennis player and embark on a global journey filled with epic tournaments, strategic choices, and fierce competition. From grand slams to smaller, intense matchups, claim your path to the World No. 1 title and immerse yourself in the game that perfectly blends strategy, skill, and chance.

Perfect for All Ages – whether you’re an adult tennis aficionado or a young aspiring player, Tiebreak Triumph offers something for everyone.

Enjoy the likeness of the professional tennis tour with every tennis tournament inside the game waiting for you to win.

—description from the designer

"A game for those who seek to find... a way to leave their world behind."

"Get through 10 challenges while making sure everyone survives!"

This game comprises a deck of riddle cards with challenges overleaf. Each turn a player must guess the answer to the riddle on the front, from a reference list of answers (e.g. Safari; Adventure; Slither & Growl, etc.) or lose a life. If successful they must then complete the accompanying challenge (e.g. mime an animal; name five snakes; sit cross-legged and stand up without using your hands or uncrossing your legs) or, again, lose a life. If anyone loses three lives, everyone loses the game. If the team of players complete ten riddles and their challenges, they win.

Players have to guess if the flight level on the next card will be higher or lower as the value of the card on display.

Each player receives 6 cards, picture side facing up. Lay another card in the centre so that all players can see the flight level indicated on the card. The remaining cards form the draw pile (picture face up). The starting player guesses whether their flying vehicle or animal flies higher or lower than the card in the centre. If they guess right, they read the indication aloud and put their own card picture face up on the pile in the centre.

If the guess was incorrect, they put their own card under the draw pile and draw a new card from the draw pile. The next player takes their turn.

The first player to get rid of all their cards after all players have had the same number of turns, wins. In the event of a tie, play a quick round: Each player receives a card. The player who correctly calls "higher!" or "lower!" first, wins.

Welcome to Maletas a la Fuga, the most chaotic and hilarious party game where players compete to recover the most luggage from a wildly out-of-control airport conveyor belt! Get ready to swap suitcases, survive crazy events, and embrace the chaos of travel.

Your goal in this fast-paced, turn-free game is to score the most points by the end of the game — matching suitcase pairs, overcoming group events, playing your secret character role, and using powerful special cards at just the right time.

Grab your passport, pack your bags, and get ready for the wildest trip of your life!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Sky Garden is a euro-style board game where each player takes on the role of a bird sanctuary owner, tasked with creating a comfortable and welcoming environment for birds, and building a unique and attractive sanctuary of their own. Sky Garden is a standalone title in the Aqua Garden series, featuring charming wooden tokens and satisfying strategic gameplay.

Players bring wooden bird tokens from the main board or the forest into their personal boards. They must carefully choose where to place each bird, considering limited space in each area, as well as trees, lakes, and objective tiles. Birds can form pairs and, when given a pleasant environment, will lay eggs. Collecting these eggs is a key factor in winning the game.

Players must also keep a close eye on bingo-style milestones. Completing a full row of conditions grants bonus rewards.   Plan your actions carefully and decide in what order to achieve them for maximum efficiency.

—description from the publisher

Dino Garden is a euro-style board game where each player becomes the owner of a thrilling dinosaur park, aiming to create a dinosaur park that is uniquely their own. Dino Garden is a standalone title in the Aqua Garden series, featuring charming wooden tokens and highly strategic gameplay.

Players bring wooden dinosaur tokens from the main board or the wasteland into their personal boards. They must decide where to place each dinosaur, considering limited space in each area, the food chain among species, and environmental features such as volcanoes and lakes.

Within the park, a unique ecosystem unfolds. Herbivorous dinosaurs produce meat as generations pass. Zookeepers rush around the park, delivering this meat to carnivorous dinosaurs. Feeding the carnivores efficiently is the key to victory.

Players must also pay attention to variable setup milestones.  By completing randomly generated milestone objectives, you can earn powerful bonus cards to support your strategy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Batti e Ribatti: Un gioco di musica e abilità motorie
description:
Batti e Ribatti is based on techniques of body percussion. The players create rhythmic-musical figures with their bodies which, just like a percussion instrument, can produce sounds with different timbre and tonal qualities.
Before the game starts, shuffle the cards and decide for a rythm - 2/4, 3/4, 4/4. For the first game, you can use 4/4 and therefore 16 cards which are put face up on the table. The players select a conductor which starts to count so that the start player starts with making the sounds according to the cards. If any player does not manage to make the correct sounds, he is removed from the game until there are no more players left.

Each player plays with 3 bicoloured chips. At each turn, the players simultaneously and secretly choose the number (0-3) and colour (red or blue) of the chips they'd like to use in this turn. The players' bets are written on the notepad. The first player places their bet. A bet contains three elements: the predominating colour (red/blue/tie), number of total chips chosen by all players, and if the number of dries (glass) is even/odd/zero. When all players have placed their bets, they show their chips. A point is received for each correctly guessed item.

The player who has gained 11 points wins. The loser pays a round of Dry.

Színkron is a cooperative puzzle game for two players that can be played without a playing surface — while holding it in hand. The only component is a double-sided card. Players progress along their own paths in sync, aiming to complete three increasingly challenging levels by reaching the target spaces on both sides.

—description from the designer

A Színkron egy kooperatív, kétszemélyes logikai játék, amely játékostér nélkül, akár kézben tartva is játszható. Az egyetlen játékelem egy kétoldalas kártya. A játékosok összehangoltan haladnak saját pályáikon, és céljuk, hogy együtt teljesítsék a három, fokozatosan nehezedő szintet azzal, hogy mindkét oldalon elérik a kijelölt célmezőket.

—description from the designer (Hungarian)

boardgame: Puzzles Plus: Das Puzzle ist hier nur der halbe Spaß
description:
Material:
24 piece puzzle
30 action cards e.g. find 2 police men, something yellow, something with first letter A.
wipable pen
rules


     First Action is to puzzle as team the 24 pieces city puzzle together with a total size of 36 cm x 50 cm.
     Every player gets 3 beginner or enhanced cards.
     At players turn, he searchs the things on his card and 
     marks it with a wipable pen on the puzzle image. 


If they find the things in a reachable time, they put the card aside.
Who first has found all things from all his cards, wins the game.
Variants:
Find something which has the same first letter as your name.
The other players specify which object or attribute to be found.

Players decide whether they should buy or sell shares or wait for a better price development.

The player on turn draws shares from the linen bag corresponding to the number of players. Drawn shares are visible for all players. The player on turn chooses one of the drawn shares and decides on their action: sell the shares at current market value or keep in their portfolio. Then all other players choose shares and decide on their actions accordingly.

If a player decides not to draw shares, they can sell shares from their portfolio.

The game ends with the closure of the market, i.e. if there are no more shares for a turn. At the end of the game, each player casts up the value of their portfolio.

The player with the highest portfolio value wins.

This box of loot was exclusive to the Backerkit Munchkin Big Box campaign.

Contains:
11 Loaded Deck Cards, 16 Munchkin Fairy Dust Cards, 1 Kovalic's Munchkin Miscellany Book, 1 Munchkin Big Box Playmat(red), 1 Munchkin Hall of Fame Stand-Up, 1 Munchkin Lanyard, 1 Munchkin Big Box Dice Tray, 1 Munchkin Big Box Patch, 1 Munchkin Big Box Pin, 1 Munchkin Coin, 1 Dry-Erase Marker, and 1 Loaded Die

This is the 2nd educational game of a series of 3 with the background story that the genie Schnatzelwaps is exploring the world.
material:
A)
18 2 sided carton cards with sets of 6 images.
In a set there are always an equal scenario with the same 3 things on it:
Always genie Schnatzelwaps
1 animal or object
the bottle
The difference is the position of the 3 things to each other, before, behimd, on, left, right.
B) 36 playing cards, showing only the positions of the 3 things, without the background scene. 1 for each image on the carton cards.

Basic Game:
The 18 carton cards are placed in the middle of the table with the animal showing side up.
The playing cards (the 18 with the animals) are shuffled and a hidden stack is build.
At players turn he takes the next card from the stack and searches for the exact situation on 1 of the cards in the middle of the table.
If he finds the right card, then he gets the playing card as point. 
If the stack is empty, the player with the most cards wins the game.
2. Version:
The 18 carton cards are shuffled. Each player gets the same number of them in front of him (4 players: 2 cards are not in the game) and decides which side is visible.
All playing cards are shuffled and placed facedown 1 by 1 in the middle of the table.
On players turn, turn 1 card face up. If you have the matching image in front of you take the card and place it on this image, if not turn it back. Who finds first all his images wins the game.
3. Version
All carton cards are placed in the middle (which side up, is not important).
All playing cards are shuffled to a hidden stack.
At players turn he turns fast the top card face up beside the stack. Who ever finds the right image first puts his hand on the image. If this is correct, he gets the playing card as point and flips the carton card. If it's wrong or nobody finds the right image (it maybe hidden), the playing card is played below the stack. Independent from this the next player in turn turn around the next playing card from the stack.
At the end of stack, the player with the most playing cards wins.

A four card mini-expansion for The Unleashed.
 New content includes:
Two new Heroes
Four mini-bosses
One new room card
Two new levels of enemy difficulty

—description from the publisher

Cards contain questions and answers, players have to find suitable answers and match the answer card with the question cards.
The player who finds 5 correct pairs first, wins.

The game consists of 147 cards in 6 colours and 8 marker cards with letters. Every colour stands for a different number of dots, from 1 up to 6 points.

All cards are shuffled and each player gets 10 cards and 3 marker cards. The start player begins and puts one of his cards on the table. He can also add one marker card to makr one color as the one which scores . Then it's the turn of the next player who puts a card on the table and can add one marker card as well. If it's the same colour as one of the previous cards, he add the card to the same row.
In every turn, each player can only add one card and one marker card.

In this card game, players try to catch ghosts in different colours by using glue cards. Players must remember the exact location of their glue cards, they cannot see them during the turn. 
A ghost card is drawn, and the player who presents the glue card in the corresponding colour from the secret stack wins the card.

The player who wins ghost cards in all four colours wins.

In Haithabu - Tribes of the North you direct the expansion of a Northern tribe and by going into new territories you will be able to exert more influence.

The game takes place in the world of Europe, between the 5th and 13th centuries. After the Romans were forced to withdraw from northern Europe in the 5th century, new power structures emerged there, which you will help to fight for in Tribes of the North. Throughout European history, various tribes have left their mark. They spread, giving cities and regions their names and character.

In "Hedeby," you guide the history of a tribe and venture into new realms to exert influence. How far will your influence extend, and will you be the most successful of four tribes in the history of the Tribes of the North? The player with the most points after three rounds wins

boardgame: Havanna Tube Games: Auf mein Wort!
description:
Roll the 4 dices. The player who knows a word with all the letters on the dices, gets the corresponding vowel chips.
The play with the most vowel chips wins.

Monopoly, the world-famous game of property and real estate, takes you into the wide world of the THW with this edition.
Floods, gas explosion, storm damage - The THW normally comes if there are big damage situations or special forces are needed.

The playing figures are special transport vehicles and devices.
The fields are related to the different fields, where THW supports.

The main rules are the same as in the normal Monopoly.

Steam Power: Map Pack contains four additional maps for the retail edition of Steam Power:


     West Country
     SE Australia
     Iberia
     NE USA


These maps are included in Steam Power: Deluxe Edition.

In Steam Power, players "race" to build the best rail network to meet their contractual obligations and make the most money.

On your turn, you can perform two actions from a selection of choices, letting you lay tracks, build factories, fulfill contracts, earn money, or collect more contracts. Building a factory brings resources to the board that all players have access to...at a price.

The game ends when a certain number of contracts are fulfilled, which varies by player count. The player with the most points (and money, which converts to points) wins.

Steam Power: Deluxe Edition differs from the Steam Power base game as a four-map expansion is included in the box, and the components have been upgraded.

Zombombbies: The Exploding Zombies Game! is a two to four player grid and card based movement game with a classic horror movie theme. Control the wandering zombie's to bump into each other or the nuclear bomb to score a point. The first player to score seven points wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dominion of Patriots and Redcoats: Fight Battles of the American War of Independence – Solo Game
description:
This is the Dominion of Patriots and Redcoats Fight Battles of the American War of Independence Solo Game.

This is a standalone set of rules based on the Dominion of the Spear game engine, with units and modifiers appropriate for this war. 
As with other rules in this series, these are very very simple rules with armies of 3-6 units and a battle can be fought within 10 minutes. 
Use figures or paper and pen as there is no measuring (grid used). Uses 2 six sided dice. 
An example battle is included along with a slightly different activation system to better model “someone fired a shot”. The back page is the Quick Reference Sheet that summarises the rules.

24 historical battles are included ready to play with background information, army lists, deployment and a brief description of the battle:

Lexington and Concord 1775, Bunker Hill 1775, Quebec 1775, Long Island 1776, White Plains 1776, Fort Washington 1776, Trenton 1776, Princeton 1777, Oriskany 1777, Bennington 1777, Brandywine 1777, First Saratoga 1777, Germantown 1777, Second Saratoga 1777, Monmouth 1778, Stono Ferry 1779, Newtown 1779, Savannah 1779, Camden 1780 AD, King’s Mountain 1780, Cowpens 1781, Guilford Courthouse 1781, Eutaw Springs 1881, Siege of Yorktown 1781.

This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet (click booklet and fit to page in printing options). 42 A5 pages.

—description from the publisher

In the Year of the Snake, LongPack presents the latest Designer Pack, showcasing our craftsmanship with a variety of materials and production techniques for designers.

This year’s pack features Snakescape, a thrilling 4-player game where dice rolls determine the fate of adventurers escaping an ancient temple. The blend of strategy and luck keeps each roll exciting as players navigate the temple’s dangers.

—description form the publisher

A memory game where you compete with other players to gather the biggest number of plant (Nabat in Arabic) seeds in the land of Palestine. 
Memorize the cards on the board, then choose a plant card from your hand, then flip a card on the board to pair it with your chosen card.

—description form the publisher

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Film Fatale: Scenario Pack
description:
Lights, camera, action!

It’s the golden age of silent filmmaking, with instant classics popping up on the silver screen left and right. But when an ambitious new producer unwittingly releases accursed horrors upon three different film sets, it’s up to our intrepid investigators to fix things before the credits roll on the entire production!

In Film Fatale, one to four investigators ham it up in Hollywood’s Lot C, where three troubled film sets are host to several bizarre and dangerous events. As spacetime warps and fiction becomes reality, you’ll find yourselves negotiating with alien Saturnites, fleeing from hungry dinosaurs, and warding off bloodthirsty vampires as you try to wrap up this thrilling mystery. The scenario can be played on its own or inserted into any campaign as a side-story.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
A detective-themed board game set in a dark and atmospheric environment, immersing player in the role of a detective tasked with solving brutal murder cases and other darke themed events. The game blends several interactive mechanics to create an engaging experience.

Players utilize action cards to perform various investigative tasks. Each action card features a QR code; scanning this code player make action on board, reveals narrative outcomes and crucial clues, guiding players toward new information presented in the paragraph-driven storybook. Through this unique system, players explore detailed location boards, uncover important item cards, and interact with witnesses by conducting interrogations.

Taking meticulous notes is also essential, as the game strongly emphasizes narrative depth and deduction. Keeping detailed records of clues and interactions allows players to piece together complex storylines, enhancing their investigative experience and enabling them to successfully solve the intricate mysteries presented in each case.

—description from the designer

Brutal Beatdown - Cursed is the fourth standalone release in the Brutal Beatdown series. You don’t need the first game Brutal Beatdown - Infestation to play. The game offers multiple play modes: Training Mode, Face-Off Mode, Tag Team Mode, Endurance Mode, Boss Mode and Onslaught Mode.

Brutal Beatdown - Cursed (like each iteration) comes with its own set of brutal fighters, unique items, thematic enemies and cool bonuses.

Delta Rummy is a fast-paced tactical game for 2–4 players.

Each round of Delta Rummy is made up of 3 steps: Bid, Buy, and Build.


     Bid: everyone picks a card from their hand for turn order, biggest number goes first.
     Buy: swap the card you bid with a card from the market. Last player gets all 3 remaining cards.
     Build: make or extend as many sets and runs as you can in front of you.


Once the deck runs out score points for sets, runs, and bonus points for having all 8 of a colour or number.

Highest score wins!

—description from the publisher

Luddite: Threads of Rebellion is a small expansion to the Luddite game. These print and play cards provide new victory conditions to add even more strategy and replayability into each game.

As well as being required to reach the requisite level of damage and escape the NED before the clock runs out, you now have 48 new unique victory conditions you can add into your games. These new cards will keep games fresh and interesting for many campaigns to come! They also allow you to up the difficulty of games to match your increasing expertise.

You’ve built a thriving city, now it’s time to kick back and enjoy Big City Vacation!

This expansion features two modules that can be played separately or together:

Big City - Add towering skyscrapers that take up two board slots! Each has unique requirements and scoring conditions, and they score every round, so grab them fast!

Vacation - Set sail with a new personal board and 90+ wooden tokens. Move your cruise ship across island chains by placing the right tokens, earning bonuses and points as you go!

Get ready for a bigger, bolder Micro Architects experience!

—description from the designer

Farms Race: Deluxe Upgrade Kit contains everything you need to play Farms Race with up to 5 players, plus an expansion's worth of content, higher quality components, and bonus goodies to build out your game.

Included:

     1 Additional Playable Species: Rabbits (1 Region of each Resource, 2 Resource Dice, 4 Combat Dice, 25 Resource Cards)
     8 Additional Mutation Cards
     6 Additional Spy Cards
     68 Wooden Human Worker, Victory Point, Nuke, and Turmoil Tokens
     Metal Current Player Coin
     Tuck Box for sleeved cards
     Canvas Dice Bag
     Comes in the Deluxe Edition box

Cuckoos love to hide and trick everyone! Get the animals in the right order to foil their plans!

Race to get your animals in the right order to claim the trophy. You'll need to listen carefully and act quickly to beat the competition.

Each player has 4 cards: 2 Cats and 2 Dogs in a face-down pile in front of them.

The active player for the turn names the 4 cards in any order, for example "Cat, Dog, Cat, Dog".

All players, including the active player, race to grab their cards, reorder them, place the cards down in that order, and grab the trophy card from the center. Play continues until one player wins two turns in a row.

Rumours of a group of British special forces Operatives known as 'the Increment' have been circulating for many years. Operating on behalf of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service, the missions undertaken are 'black ops' - deniable missions that would be refuted by the British government if compromised. These include a whole range of objectives where maximum discretion is required. E Squadron is the modern day successor to the Increment but no official confirmation of the group or any of its operations has been given.

In the game you will be controlling a few Operatives from E Squadron as they attempt daring missions. As you will discover, stealth is often the key tactic here, although once that advantage is lost, things may well become more hectic!

Set on a square grid, the game implements an easy system of line of sight as your Operatives and the Hostiles they face move around inside and outside buildings. Try to find cover and stay hidden or simply shoot your way out. You decide the approach. Explosives, climbing, overwatch - it's all here in this easy to learn and hard to master solitaire game.

The game includes the rules, suggested colour counters and 10 full coloured Mission Maps.

Replayability is provided by varying set up and using a different mix of E Squadron Operatives for each play through.

—description from the designer

Step into Rome, 150 AD — a world where danger, intrigue, and history collide. In Letters of the Word, you don the sandals of a determined scribe, risking everything to gather the sacred Letters and build the New Testament. But this is no ordinary quest. With every step, the chance of winning hangs in the balance. Your fate is unwritten. How will you shape history?

The open-world journey is yours to shape. You will navigate bustling markets to secure vital supplies, find shelter in secret safe houses to make copies, and risk daring encounters with Roman soldiers who seek to silence the Word. Wild beasts prowl the roads, unforeseen challenges emerge, and every choice sends ripples through your path, ensuring that no two games unfold the same way.

There are multiple starting points, each at a Gospel location, and countless paths to choose to take. Robbers, soldiers and lions lie in wait ; a roll of the die will determine your fate. Blessing Cards will aid your mission. Your supplies will run low and supply limits are in place, so choose wisely where to trade and stock up for your travels. Manna Cards can favor you or force you to reshape your strategy.

Letters of the Word delivers fast-paced gameplay fueled by teamwork, strategic planning, and high-stakes decisions. There’s no trivia, no memorization — just pure strategy. Whether you’re a lifelong believer, a curious explorer, or a seasoned board game strategist, this experience welcomes all. Victory isn’t won through knowledge — it’s earned through bold choices and sharp thinking.

—description from the designer

Enter Crystalline Cavern with your trusty Rock Hammer to collect rare crystals. Roll, Match, Collect!

Roll dice to match the die on the crystal card to collect crystals. Get extra crystals when the die on your hammer card matches the die on the crystal card.

The player that collects the most Crystals, wins.

—description from the designer

In a village cursed by a mysterious force, no one is safe, not even from themselves. When night falls, the boundary between innocent and monstrous blurs. Under the grip of the "Werewolf's Curse," even you might unknowingly become the very threat the town fears. With no game master, no elimination, and no waiting for night phases, players must rely on deduction, bluffing, and social cues to survive a single, tense round of suspicion and accusation.

Unaware Werewolf is a quick, hidden-role microgame for 3-7 players. Each participant receives two role cards, but only one is active at a time, and the player with the highest combined curse value becomes the werewolf, possibly without even knowing it. The game features asymmetric card powers, deduction through limited reveals, and a unique twist: you might be the villain and not even realize until it's too late. With variable setups, escalating tension, and a compact 5-10 minute runtime, Unaware Werewolf offers a fresh spin on the social genre.

Welcome to Orbit City! Play as a member of the futuristic Jetsons family — George, Jane, Judy, Elroy, or Rosie — and explore the towering spires of the city of tomorrow. Across three episodes (rounds), you'll traverse the city collecting items and experiences that correspond to your Demand Cards.

In each episode, players take turns based on their position on a linear track of locations. On your turn, you choose the distance you wish to travel and then reveal cards at your destination. The number of steps you take becomes your currency for acquiring these cards. The further you advance, the more money you can spend — but once you pass a location, it cannot be revisited!

The game features an optional Mini-Expansion with the dog Astro, introducing new powers and strategic choices, activated by a single-use Astro token during each episode.

—description from the publisher

Bailen 1808: A Decisive Victory, recreates this pivotal battle, an unexpected confrontation that marked a turning point in the struggle against Napoleonic domination.

The game shares the same system as our other Napoleonic titles, such as Waterloo 1815 and Austerlitz 1805. Units are represented by wooden blocks, which not only offer a unique visual appeal but also facilitate a clear visualization of movements on the battlefield.

—description from the designer

Bailén 1808: Una Victoria Decisiva, recrea esta trascendental batalla, un enfrentamiento inesperado que marcó un punto de inflexión en la lucha contra el dominio napoleónico.

El juego comparte el mismo sistema que otros títulos napoleónicos de nuestra editorial, como Waterloo 1815 y Austerlitz 1805. Las unidades se representan mediante bloques de madera, lo que no solo ofrece un atractivo visual único, sino que también facilita la clara visualización de los movimientos en el campo de batalla.

—description from the designer (Spanish)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Descend into the heart of darkness and carve your own path through a shifting underground maze in The Cabin! In this thrilling 1–2 player adventure, every tile you lay could reveal hidden treasures—or unleash ravenous monsters lurking in the shadows. Arm yourself with swords, ladders, lanterns, and even dynamite as you balance daring exploration against mounting peril and the ever-looming “Stirring Below” threat. Will you emerge with the greatest hoard of gems before the darkness swallows you whole? Brace yourself for a pulse-pounding blend of tile-laying strategy, tense decision-making, and heart-stopping surprises—no two expeditions through The Cabin are ever the same!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Trek: Captain's Chair – To Boldly Go
description:
In Star Trek: Captain's Chair, you go head-to-head against an opponent, each stepping into the shoes of an iconic leader from across the history of the Star Trek galaxy. Command ships, crew, and away teams, while you manage resources and alliances!

Star Trek: Captain's Chair – To Boldly Go is a new game that can be played on its own or combined with Star Trek: Captain's Chair to create even more captain competitions.

With To Boldly Go, you can play as James T. Kirk, Khan Noonien Singh, Philippa Georgiou from Star Trek: Discovery, Rebner, the Pakled Commander from Star Trek: Lower Decks, and more. Each of the six captains comes with a unique, asymmetric deck of cards that matches that leader's personality, abilities, allies, and assets. These decks can be expanded with cards available to both players, cards representing the people, items, planets, and events that characters encounter in iconic storylines. Each deck creates different strategic directions based on which common cards are available and what strategy your opponent deploys.

Prefer to fly solo? Take on the galaxy with a challenging single-player mode built to test your skill and strategy!

True to Star Trek, antagonism will not win every game. You will need to pursue diplomacy, exploration, and science, with different captains excelling at different paths. With hundreds of unique, deeply thematic cards, you'll want to explore the galaxy again and again. The chair is yours, captain!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Trek: Captain's Chair – Second Contact
description:
In Star Trek: Captain's Chair, you go head-to-head against an opponent, each stepping into the shoes of an iconic leader from across the history of the Star Trek galaxy. Command ships, crew, and away teams, while you manage resources and alliances!

Star Trek: Captain's Chair – Second Contact contains three new captains that can face off against those in the original game, against those in To Boldly Go, and against one another, but you'll need common cards from one of the base games. Two of the captains are Carol Freeman, Captain of the U.S.S. Cerritos, and Christopher Pike, Captain of the U.S.S. Enterprise, and you can also add Krulmuth-B as a starting location, opening a portal that crosses space and time!

The Aeldari fight to ensure the very survival of their dying empire – their star-spanning realm lies in ruins, and their gods are dead. Yet woe betide those who think them weak. Guided by the foresight of their peerless seers and marshalled by cunning generals, the warhosts of the Aeldari utilise advanced technology, and the natural speed and agility of their warriors, to achieve victory and hold their doom at bay.

Codex: Aeldari is your must-have guide to leading an army of these swift and deadly warriors, offering insight into their ways of war and the various Craftwords that make up the Aeldari race. This also includes the sinister Drukhari units who have sided with the Ynnari, and the murderous troupes of the Harlequins. You'll find loads of strange and esoteric artwork to inspire you, along with beautiful galleries of painted miniatures. This tome gives you the rules needed to wage war across the galaxy against myriad foes, whether in Combat Patrol, narrative Crusade campaigns, or matched play games of Warhammer 40,000.

Inside this 192-page hardback book, you'll find:

     Background material and lore for the different Aeldari Craftworlds, as well as the strange Harlequins, and macabre Ynnari
     Evocative artwork and photography that will inspire your own collection and unleash your creativity
     Rules for using Aeldari in games of Warhammer 40,000 with 66 datasheets, covering each unit
     Rules for running your own Guiding Fate narrative Crusade campaign, and for playing fast-paced Combat Patrol games


—description from the publisher

Across the centuries, the fate of the Inner Sphere changed time and again with the actions of one MechWarrior and one 'Mech fighting at the crux of history. There stories became legends passed down through the years, and now these legends and their 'Mechs stand revealed as never before.

Legends II profiles of the Inner Sphere's most famous (or notorious) MechWarriors, and their BattleMechs.

BattleTech Universe is an intensely detailed sourcebook providing an overview of the history and setting of the BattleTech Universe, providing details of the features, technology and factions of BattleTech stretching across all BattleTech eras to the time of the ilClan.

-description from the publisher

Angry Axolotl is a fast-paced, family-friendly card game where players race to ditch their cards in pairs before the angry axolotl catches them! It’s easy to learn, full of surprising twists, and loaded with personality—perfect for kids, adults, and everyone in between.

Inspired by the classic game Old Maid but with a chaotic action twist, Angry Axolotl adds exciting gameplay to the mix. On your turn, play a pair from your hand to trigger a special actions. But don’t get too comfortable, the last player holding the Angry Axolotl loses, and the rest escape the wrath of the Angry Axolotl!

The game includes 51 illustrated cards packed with personality, from mischievous axolotls to sneaky surprises. Each round is quick, interactive, and different from the last, making it perfect for game night, sleepovers, or anytime laughs are required.

Whether you're a casual player or a competitive card shark, Angry Axolotl offers light strategy, unpredictable moments, and a whole lot of axolotl fun.

—description from the designer

Strike the Bear is a strategic-level board game of the German invasion of Russia in World War II. The battle covers the critical early months of the war, from June 1941 to March 1942. The German code name for the campaign was Operation Barbarossa.

One player handles Nazi Germany while the other commands the Soviet Union. They attempt to reach victory by controlling objectives across the game map. To underscore the fluidity of the campaign, these objectives can and do change over the course of the war. The uncertainty of where and when forces must shift to achieve their territorial goals reflects the doubts and hesitations of high command.

At times, operations will consume operations points (OPs) for both sides. The non-active player will have to pay OPs for such things as Battle Continues and Reserves Commitment, and if unable to will fight unsupplied with detrimental effects. OPs are also used to recover disrupted units and acquire replacements. There are a finite number of available OPs each turn, and the player's objectives may change while rolling for the OPs in the turn.

At the end of play, players check to see if they have achieved victory based on objectives held or conquered. Unit losses do not measure victory by themselves.

Game Scale:
Game Turn: 3 week
Hex: 120 miles / 193 kilometers
Units: Corps to Groups

Game Inventory:
One 17 x 22" full color mapsheet
One dual-side printed countersheet (108 1/2" counters)
One 12-page Strike the Bear rulebook
One dual-side printed Player Aid Card

Solitaire Playability: High
Complexity Level: Medium
Players: 2 or more
Playing Time: 2-4 hours

Note: Players will need to supply 2d6 dice.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Suspeição, we find a crime scene with 4 pieces of evidence, and all players are suspects.

Each round, a new crime scene is prepared using randomly numbered cards, and a small board leaves only 4 of these numbers exposed, which will be the incriminating evidence for that round.

Each player receives 4 cards and, on your turn, you must use the actions of your cards well to try to find this evidence in the hands of the other players, while trying to keep some of this evidence hidden in your hand, if you have any, guaranteeing positive points for doing so.

At the end of each round, the incriminating evidence that remains revealed in front of each player will be converted into negative points. At the end of 3 rounds, the player who gets the most positive points will be the most "innocent", while the one with the most negative points will be the "prime suspect".

—description from the designer

In Wax Packs Unwraps, players are kids in 1960's America, collecting and showing off rare and valuable baseball cards of their favorite ball players. Players will purchase Packs of cards, crack them open, and discover what treasures lie inside. They then have to decide which cards to keep, which to trade to other players, and which to sell to the market for bubblegum (the currency in the game). Ultimately the player who best fills up their 3×3 card sleeve grid and completes their personal goals will be the coolest kid in town. Also, don't forget to get your favorite player to autograph your card for double the points!

—description form the designer

I Do My Own Stunts is a meeple drafting, rolling, placement, set collection game and it's the third installment in the "Scott Rogers signature" collection of games.

You play as a Hollywood Stunt Coordinator managing a team of stunt performers as they work together to create a thrilling, action-packed movie!

Draft your stunt performers - each with an unique ability - and "roll camera" as you roll your meeple stunt people to see what poses they perform. Place your stunt people on film scene cards of different genres (action/sci-fi/thriller/spy and more!) to complete action scenes!

Use your stunt performer's unique abilities to create dynamic combos and avoid tragic accidents! Didn't get the poses you wanted? Reshoot a scene by spending a film reel, but be careful that your meeples don't bounce off the airbag and get hurt otherwise they'll end up in the infirmary!

The player who has created the most spectacular movie from scene cards, matches the most genre scenes and has the least injured stunt performers wins the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: ロードス島戦記 戦乱の覇王 (Record of Lodoss War: Supreme Warrior King)
description:
In the war-torn realm of Lodoss, six monarchs rise amid chaos, each determined to rule the cursed island. As power shifts and kingdoms clash, cunning and conquest are your only allies. Will you build your empire with honor, or seize victory through ambition and betrayal?

Record of Lodoss War: Supreme Warrior King is a competitive mission-based card game where 4 to 6 players collect character and treasure cards to fulfill secret objectives and accumulate honor points. Tactical elements such as one-on-one duels, stealth infiltration, and reinforcements give each decision weight. The game can end either by surpassing 100 honor points or by assembling all five sacred artifacts, triggering an immediate win.

A two-player, asymmetric, abstract strategy game where one player controls eleven pieces spanning the entire lower row of a vertical grid board and which may only move directly upwards one spot at a time, may not move backwards, and cannot make any captures. They are trying to get any one of their pieces across the yellow line at the top of the board. The other player controls five pieces that just defend against the oncoming invasion. They have for black blocker pieces and one yellow striker. The blockers may move as a queen in chess does, as many spaces as they like in any direction. They cannot make captures, but they other player's pieces cannot move past them. The yellow striker moves as a king in chess does, one space in any direction, and will capture any of the opponent's pieces it lands on, pushing it through the grid of the board and out the other side. Red wins if they make it to the endzone with any of their pieces. The defenders win if they manage to do that by either eliminating all of the opponent's pieces or enough that their blockers stop the remaining pieces.

You are tool-using gorillas!
Use a special tool—a stick with a suction cup called a Petakibo—to grab cards featuring different fruits!

There are over 30 gameplay rules included, and if you come up with your own, you can send them to the designers—they might even become official!

This is a fast-paced, analog action card game that’s fun for both kids and adults... as long as the suction cups stick!

The objective of the game is not to win. The goal is to NOT BE THE FIRST LOSER.

Players play some mini-games within the game (simple action games and interesting word games) while following a few quirky rules. If they lose a game or break a rule, they pick a penalty card, each of which has two letters on it. If they pick up enough penalty cards that spell TH IS  IS  RI DI CU LO US !!!
they lose and the game stops.

The main deck to play with is the green game deck, which they'll draw from on their turn. Rules from the orange deck (1 or 2 or 3, players' choice)are opened prior to starting the game. These are community rules that everyone has to follow. Rules will change during the course of the game when players are prompted to change them. They might also be prompted to punish their opponent by giving them one of their red penalty cards. (It is important to note that players cannot have duplicates of penalty cards. If players draw a duplicate, they're free to give that card to anyone who doesn't have that particular card).

There is also a purple challenge deck, designed as an ice-breaker. It has funny, embarrassing challenges that players are asked to complete (there are prompts included in the game deck). If they decline, they draw a penalty card. This is an optional deck though; not necessary to play the base game.

A shorter, more intense version can be played by spelling just 
RI DI CU LO US instead.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ポートレートオブポッポ (The Portrait of a Pigeon)
description: Players are pigeons on the brink of death with their lives flashing before their eyes. However, a mistake by a god-like being causes everyone's memories to become jumbled and mixed up. Now, players must compete to gather the most valuable memories. The one who collects the most meaningful memories and crafts the best portrait of their life wins the game.
publishers: 東京なかよしデザイン (Tokyo Nakayoshi Design)

boardgame: 三国杀飞鸿印雪 (Sanguosha: Feihong Yin Xue)
description:
三国杀飞鸿印雪 is a special edition of the popular Chinese card game Sanguosha, released as part of its 15th-anniversary celebration. The game is deeply rooted in the historical Three Kingdoms era, featuring legendary generals, strategic battles, and hidden roles. This edition introduces new mechanics, exclusive generals, and special cards, enriching the tactical depth and variety of gameplay.

In terms of gameplay, players take on different roles—Lord, Loyalist, Rebel, or Traitor—each with unique objectives. The game revolves around card-based combat, where players use attack, defense, and strategy cards to outmaneuver opponents. Feihong Yin Xue introduces new skill sets, special mission-based abilities, and additional card decks, such as the extra card pile, which allows players to access unique effects.

The goal of the game depends on the player's assigned role. The Lord must survive and eliminate Rebels, the Loyalists must protect the Lord, the Rebels aim to overthrow the Lord, and the Traitor seeks to manipulate the battlefield for personal victory. This edition enhances the experience with new generals, exclusive weapons, and advanced mechanics, such as "智囊" (Strategic Pouch) and "使命技" (Mission Skills), which add layers of complexity and decision-making.

Overall, 三国杀飞鸿印雪 refines the classic Sanguosha formula with new tactical elements, expanded character abilities, and a fresh thematic twist, making it a must-play for fans of the series and strategy enthusiasts alike.

The Federation: Piracy expansion is made up of two modules: the Piracy module and the AI Assistant module. Both of them can be played together or separately. The purpose of these modules is to bring more dilemmas to players. They also allow for a new asymmetry decided by the player and not imposed.

The box also contains the Solo mode. The Solo mode can be played with the basic box and also with the 2 modules of the Expansion.

—description from the publisher

Don't be the player to make the big splash! In Don't Flush the Fish, take turns placing colorful fish onto the toilet paper, but here’s the catch! The more fish you add, the harder it gets to keep the toilet paper from tearing. Keep a steady hand or risk flushing all your hard work—and fish—down the toilet!

Scoop 'Em Up: Scoop up the wet fish from their fishbowl, and place them one by one on the toilet paper over the toilet bowl!

Don't Flush The Fish: Don't break through the toilet paper, or you'll cause all the fish to flush right out of the toilet and lose the game!

—description from the publisher

Age of Quarrel is a strategic 4X board game set in the shadow of a fallen medieval order. Long ago, the city of Middelalderen held the land together. It was a crossroads for merchants, a seat of learning, and a source of legitimacy. It did not collapse in a single catastrophe. It narrowed, hardened, and quietly pushed whole peoples beyond its walls. The city still matters, but the world around it has learned how to survive without permission.

Players lead rival factions competing to shape what comes next.

The game unfolds over three Years, each divided into four Seasons. In each of the Spring, Summer, and Autumn seasons, each player take five turns, choosing a Civic Action to drive your faction forward, or a Tactical Action to press their position on the map. The Winter Season is a time devoted to reflecting on your growing legacy, weathering the hardships of Winter, and planning for the coming year.

The map grows during play. Exploration expands the world outward from known clearings, and it can create opportunity just as easily as it creates problems. New terrain can change your plans immediately. It can also change what your opponents can reach, how quickly barbarians appear, and how reliable your supply will be later.

Military power matters, but it is not the only way to take ground or break an opponent’s hold. Levies, soldiers, and Priests each matter in different ways, and pressure creates conflict. Sometimes it’s an exposed settlement or an overextended line. Sometimes it’s converting rival units to your cause.

Between Seasons, the game resolves its economy as the clock that drives the world forward. Settlements produce resources based on the land around them, storage limits force hard choices, and prices at the Market shift as conditions change. If you stretch too far, the supply check can punish you, sometimes harshly, and the map remembers that mistake.

Barbarians are not a scripted nuisance. They are a living presence shaped by the world’s history and by the gaps players create. They grow in Winter, they threaten weak borders, and they can become the cost of ignoring what you can’t protect.

Legacy Points are earned throughout the game and in final scoring. You gain them for what you build, what you hold, what you achieve through force or influence, and for controlling Middelalderen. Winter scoring rewards stability, while end-of-game scoring reflects what your faction leaves behind: infrastructure, population, surviving armies, and resources converted into lasting legacy.

In Age of Quarrel, you are not restoring a golden age. You are inheriting its mechanisms. The game’s central question is not whether you can win but what kind of order your winning creates, and whether it is any better than what broke the world in the first place.

History will remember what endured.
Whether it deserved to is another matter.
