boardgame: 色情を持て余した男子高校生がクラスメイトの女子26人と何股まで隠せるかを真剣に競い始めた (A Sexually Frustrated High School Boy Seriously Competes to See How Many Secret Relationships He Can Hide with His 26 Female Classmates)
description:
You are a dangerously overconfident high school boy navigating a chaotic social minefield of love, lust, and lies. During school life, you can secretly date as many girls as you dare — but when Valentine’s Day comes, inquisitions begin. Will you keep your harem hidden or crash into scandal when your secrets unravel?

A Sexually Frustrated High School Boy Seriously Competes to See How Many Secret Relationships He Can Hide with His 26 Female Classmates is a deduction and risk-management, where you secretly choose which classmates to date and must endure interrogation rounds to protect your score. During the School Life Phase, you discreetly date girls (kept hidden) or declare them as friends (revealed). In the Valentine Phase, players interrogate one another based on revealed friends. If two or more relationships are exposed, you're purged — and all your dates become negative points. The game balances secrecy, bluffing, and calculated exposure.
designers: 谷津ユウイチロウ (Yuichiro Tanizu)

description:
In the world’s grandest casino, Grand Palace, six outrageous gamblers clash in a high-stakes battle of wit, nerve, and pride. From mob boss Smith to the unhinged clown Maggy, each brings their own ego and eccentricity to the table. Bet One! isn’t just about winning chips — it’s about outsmarting your rivals, reading their every bluff, and staking everything on your instincts in a battle where timing, deception, and guts reign supreme.

Bet One! is a bidding and bluffing game for 4–6 players where each gambler uses a limited set of numbered chips (1–9) to compete for scoring cards. Players simultaneously select a chip to commit to the round, with the highest unique bid winning the prize, but identical bids cancel each other out. With a limited supply of chips and the option to buy back used ones at a cost, strategic resource management and psychological insight are key to dominating the casino floor.
publishers: アクシア・クリエーションズ (Axia Creations)

description:
Test your knowledge and become a nerd quiz champion.
260 questions about games, movies, general knowledge, and popular culture.

Gotham City is under siege as its most dangerous villains Joker, Penguin, Riddler, and Mr. Freeze—form an unholy alliance. Only the Dynamic Duo, Batman and Robin, can stop them. But timing is everything: act too soon and fall into their trap; wait too long and the city falls.

Homem-Morcego: O Jogo de Cartas is a card game of timing, deduction, and hand management, where players must outwit their opponents by choosing the ideal moment to play their action cards. It’s a reimagining of the classic game Potato Man, featuring new components, team play rules, and thematic twists drawn from the Batman universe. In the team variant, coordination between Batman and Robin is key to defeating the villains before their plan succeeds.

Even the most mighty of dragons must start somewhere! In Drakelings, players run dragon hatcheries to prepare young drakes for their time at a prestigious dragon training school, not least among these being Drakewright University. Only the most daring hatcheries will gain the attention of Drakewright, so players must carefully choose the Drakelings they will raise, the caretakers they will employ, and the equipment they will use to gain victory points and secure their place among the greatest hatcheries.

Players will begin by moving a Drakeling or caretaker pawn along a 3 by 3 grid to select a card they can add to their hatchery. Equipment  will give immediate rewards, such as resources or victory points, while drakeling or caretaker cards have special abilities that will give larger rewards, but may only activate under special conditions or at the cost of resources.

Each drakeling has natural abilities in which they excel. By pairing a drakeling with a caretaker equipped to handle their natural talents, you main gain a handful of rewards such as meat to feed your dragons, crystals to pay your caretakers, or even additional cards to add to your hatchery. Carefully planning your pairings will allow you to chain together massive combos and rapidly build your hatchery into and bustling home for drakelings.

As the game progresses, players will gain victory points through successful pairings, special abilities, and achievements cards. Once the deck runs out, the player with most victory points will be declared the winner.

—description from the publisher

Magic brews enter Endhaven with a twelve-card Potion deck, five Adventure cards and two new Allies: Maple Verdant Forager and Helion Rising Titan. Potions allow players to skirt the rules by granting them powerful abilities.

The Emperor’s Children are the twisted Heretic Astartes sons of the Daemon Primarch, Fulgrim. They are prideful traitors who have fallen victim to their depraved obsessions. Upon the battlefields of the 41st Millennium, they indulge these obsessions in a horrifyingly violent fashion. Always on the move, they are driven by their compulsive need to experience new thrills and, in honour of Slaanesh, weave an exquisitely intricate and lurid tapestry of violence.

Codex: Emperor's Children is a vital book for collectors who enjoy setting the chosen of Slaanesh upon an unsuspecting galaxy. This impressive tome is packed with twisted artwork, alluring background material, stunning photography, and the rules you need to unleash your hedonistic warriors in games of Warhammer 40,000. You’ll find datasheets, Detachments, narrative Crusade rules, and a self-contained Combat Patrol section to help you fight tight, tactical games with your collection. This book will allow you to live out your obsessions no matter how you like to collect, paint, and play with your Emperor's Children.

Inside this 112-page hardback book, you'll find:
– Shocking lore and background material detailing the descent of the Emperor's Children
– Evocative artwork and photography that will inspire your collection and unleash your creativity
– Rules for using Emperor's Children and daemons of Slaanesh in games of Warhammer 40,000, with 22 datasheets, six Detachments, and two army-wide rules
– A set of rules to launch your own narrative Crusade, gather ingredients to create Combat Elixirs, and receive the Boons of Slaanesh
– A guide to painting your Combat Patrol and rules for fielding the Callous Blades in skirmish-sized games

A metric activity game. Manufactured by Damon.

"Coordimetric consists of two sequential games which combine two distinct educational objectives: to develop the concept of the number 10 to linear metric units and to develop basic concepts of coordinate geometry. The excitement and challenge of chance linked with educational benefits of learning metric measurement make COORDIMETRIC an extremely popular game."

Play summary: Play is driven by numbered cards, drawn two at a time, which move a player's marker to coordinate points on the game board. Based on the placement, players add a certain number of "millimetre" chips to their scorecard. As points are accrued ten millimetre chips are traded for a centimetre chip, centimetre chips are then traded for decimetre chips. The first player to collect a certain number of the highest level chips wins.

This drawing and guessing game, essentially identical to Pictionary, is for 2-4 teams of up to 5 players.

Players move a pencil figurine across a board with colored spaces, each representing a category: 1) Arts, Sports & Entertainment, 2) Geography & Natural Sciences, 3) Verbs, 4) Home & Food, and 5) Anything Goes.

In the first round, all teams simultaneously guess a word from the "Anything Goes" category within 3 minutes. On each subsequent turn, a player rolls two dice: a four-sided die to determine the number of spaces to move, and another die with two possible results: "I" (Individual Guess) or "TJ" ("Todos Juegan," Spanish for "All Play"). If "I" is rolled, the team advances by guessing correctly. If "TJ" is rolled, all teams guess together, and the first to answer correctly moves forward.

The first team to reach the finish space and correctly guess a word from a category chosen by the other teams wins. The game features common words, along with a mix of Mexican and 80s pop culture.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: The game purpose is to collect five different crocodiles. The game starts with 20 playing pieces placed face down, then each player takes 5 pieces and keeps them hidden from the opponent. During the game you will be exchanging your pieces for the pieces on the table or your opponent's pieces, or showing your pieces. The game consists of four matches. The match ends when you show five different crocodiles, and you get 5 points minus a point for each pair.

Through clever collection and tactical planning, gather the most valuable sets. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

The game is played over three rounds.

At the start of each round, reveal one market card in the center and deal 4 cards to each player.

On their turn, the active player must choose between two actions: placing a card; placing a die.

Passing is not allowed. If you run out of either cards or dice, you must choose the remaining action.

—description from the designer

Roll 6 dice, each featuring a mix of consonants and vowels, then flip the hourglass! Your mission? Create as many words as you can before time runs out.

Each word must use at least three of the letters on the dice, but the more letters you manage to work in, the more points you’ll score—one extra point for each additional letter.

But here’s the twist: if your opponent matches one of your words, it’s only worth half the points! And no repeating words from previous rounds—yours or anyone else’s, they’re all worth 0 points. After 16 fast-paced rounds of rolling, scrambling, and racing against the clock, the player with the most points claims victory.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Halo: Flashpoint – War Games Expansion Pack
description:
CUSTOM FIRETEAMS, NEW SPARTANS AND NEW WEAPONS

Complete your Spartan training and fine-tune your fireteam for tactical excellence with the War Games expansion pack.

With points values listed for every miniature and weapons combination released so far, build custom fireteams that go far beyond the base game’s team drafting mechanic.

Get started with four additional Spartans equipped with Mark V [B] armour and new loadouts, including the Concussion Rifle and more.

—description from the publisher

In Doçura ("Sweetness"), players take on the role of chefs competing to prove who is the best in town. They’ve parked their food trucks around the market square and are eager to see who will come out on top!

To win, they’ll need to gather ingredients from the market stalls and prepare cakes and smoothies according to their customers' orders. The chef who keeps their customers happiest will be crowned the competition’s winner!

Doçura is a children’s game with grown-up mechanics—simplified and intuitive, designed to match a child’s abilities. Rondel, worker placement, resource management, and fulfilling orders (contracts) are all incorporated in a fun and engaging way. A true "kids' eurogame," featuring adorable artwork, loads of fun, and easy-to-learn gameplay.

—description from the publisher

In Brinquedos Assombrados ("Spooky Toys"), a semi-cooperative children's game, a group of kids must retrieve their forgotten toys from the haunted mansion. The problem? The ghost haunting the mansion has grown attached to the toys and refuses to give them up!

To recover their toys, the kids must explore the mansion while avoiding the ghost. They roll the die and choose the best path to move their character tokens, collect toys, and escape the Ghost. But beware! He's always lurking nearby and might startle you, causing you to drop a toy and flee in terror to one of the house's entrances.

Knowing the children must be home by dinnertime, the Ghost will do everything possible to make time fly—so it won't have to deal with these pesky kids... at least for tonight.

Choose your side: play as one of the brave children or the mischievous Ghost. The side that ends up with the most toys by the end of the night wins!

—description from the publisher

You are a TANK. Your objective is to be the last one standing. Do this by using shooting cards, narrowly dodging Landmines, and betraying friends. Or you can go neutral, the boring route. The choice is yours.

TANKS is a card game in which everyone draws a four card hand and throughout the game players play cards to lower their opponent’s health and to build a strategy.  Players attack each other by playing “shooting cards” to lower the player's health. There are three different types of dice used; six sided, ten sided, and twenty sided. The six sided dice are for general rolls and to keep track of calculated shooting cards’ countdowns. The ten sided dice are used to keep track of player’s health. The twenty sided dice are used for general dice rolls and for deciding for which player to go first. The winner is the player to be the only one in the game with more than zero health.

—description from the publisher

Meow in a Row is a cute and strategic abstract game where players take turns placing cat tokens on the board, aiming to be the first to line up five in a row—horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. With simple rules and quick playtime, it's perfect for both casual players and lovers of classic connection games, now with an adorable feline twist.

—description from the publisher

Meow-CALA is a charming cat-themed twist on the classic game of Mancala. Players take turns picking up and sowing cat tokens around their board, aiming to collect the most in their store. While the core mechanics remain true to the timeless original, the cute artwork and playful theme bring a fresh and delightful energy to the strategic gameplay. Perfect for two players who enjoy clever, simple tactics with a feline flair.

—description from the publisher

Summer is here, so why not take a three-day trip to some of the most famous Greek islands? Explore, go hiking, admire the landscape and take photos, or simply rest and relax. Vacations to Greek Islands is a competitive roll and write, print and play game.

In Vacations to Greek Islands, players plan their route and visit different locations on a Greek island over the course of fourteen rounds. At each location, they can explore, hike, or admire the landscape. However, they only have three days to plan before their vacation ends, so they must choose their routes carefully to ensure they spend enough time at each location without sacrificing the opportunity to visit others. Of course, they can take pictures and rest to regain strength and plan a bigger route for the next day. Their goal is to have as much fun as possible and earn the most points by the end of the game.

The player with the most points at the end of the game wins and can boast about having the best vacation ever!

—description from the publisher

Welcome back to Karuba! 
The expedition begins – and you are not alone. As you uncover winding paths and search for forgotten temples, other teams move forward. The jungle is unpredictable, time is running out… and every decision counts!
The player who retrieves the most valuable treasures wins this exciting expedition race.

With 3 expansions: Unlock special actions and complete exciting missions! Or curse your competitors' paths and place obstacles in their way…


     Big box with 3 brand new expansions
     With game material for up to 6 players
     Entertaining: everyone plays at the same time!
     Adventure classic in a fresh design


—description from the publisher

description:
Firefighters on Duty: KS promos include all the stretch rewards generated during the KS campaign for Firefighters on Duty on 2023.

Drone common vehicle
Rapid responder common vehicle
Floods expansion
Rescue dogs expansion
Public panic module
Cat on a Tree module

High pressure nozzle equipment
Hoze extension equipment

The DJ flips the top card, scans its code, and the music kicks off on Spotify. While the music plays, everyone uses their vinyl disc to guess the song's release year. Best guess wins the card, and if the player can name the artist and song, they activate the card's special action.

Contents: 300 music cards, 60 karaoke cards, 8 date-setting vinyl discs, and a free Hitstory application for Android & iOS.

In Currency War, players play different countries in the time and space background of the two world wars.
Run your country's economy during the war and try to have the strongest financial resources to defeat other countries.

By combining real historical stories around the two world wars with the more abstract concept of economics, students can learn the concepts of international exchange rates and currency values ​​in economics more efficiently. The historical stories and fun aspects can also stimulate students' interest and drive their learning motivation, allowing "learning" to change from rigid teaching in black and white in textbooks to students playing board games independently and learning knowledge through board games.

Game Introduction:
Players play one of four countries (the United States, China, Germany, and Japan). Players use historical cards and purchase resources in each round to decide the outcome of the war, and even influence other players' actions, trying to disrupt other players' plans and maintain their own country's economic prosperity, thereby leading the war in the flames of World War II. Will history repeat itself or be rewritten?
In order to allow players to integrate into the current history, the names of historical events in each country in this game are all based on the names of the events given by the current powerful countries in that country, which may be different from the current names.

Game objectives:
When all the historical cards have been used up, the person with the highest value of money (the sum of the values ​​of all the currencies in hand) and the highest value of resources is the winner.

boardgame: Point of View: 1 Session – Clash Club
description:
Point of View: Clash Club is a fast-paced, cooperative puzzle game where communication is everything.

While chasing a fugitive, your team investigates the bizarre “Clash Club” – a surreal mix of techno, wellness, casino, and interior design. But the mission takes an unexpected twist...

Each player sees the same 3D scene from a different angle on their own fold-out map – and sharing visuals is not allowed. Only by talking and piecing together your unique perspectives can you solve the 60 puzzles and uncover the truth.

A compact, one-session game full of surprises, teamwork, and fresh “aha!” moments.

—description from the publisher

The Rebel Expansion introduces three new Archons, two fresh Map Sectors, Upgrade cards for adding to your power in between games, and rules for an engaging linked campaign, enriching your gaming experience.

Add new content to your gameplays and rediscover the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Indomitable Survivor: Poison Partisan Ritika
description:
Poison Partisan - Ritika is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Poison Partisan - Ritika hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Glossary card
1 x Aggregate Silk Gland Spidicules indomitable resource
1 x Poison Partisan pattern card
1 x Poison Partisan pattern gear card
1 x Install guide card
1 x Large matte art print
Learn more about indomitable survivors
For best experience, include Spidicules in your campaign.

Unsteady on its remaining legs, the monster's body sunk low against the stone faced ground. A greasy miasma emanated from the monster's battered body, choking her lungs and burning her eyes. Legions of newly hatched spderlings, converged voraciously on the phenomenal call of its progenitor. Their coarse legs scrabbled over Ritika's body, talons slicing her flesh in their fevered rush. The monster hesitated, rising above her ravenous brood. Ritika held fast to its spindly fingers. Throwing her weight over the monsters swaying body, she hacked at the exposed joint, toppling the creature for a final time. The swarming brood descended on the corpse, devouring its broken body. One by one she smashed and stabbed the spiderlings until the skittering dark grew still. With the last of her strength she yanked the florid organ from the corpse, retching its oily stench.

Cause trouble – 'cause causing trouble is fun!

Troublebot is a lean, mean card game of many small but meaningful decisions. Whenever it is your turn, you get to pick up a new shroombot. Then you play one of the two shroombots in your hand and perform its action.

While most effects help you take care of your seedlings, some will cause the other players trouble. If you have the most valuable seedlings in the end, you win.

—description from the publisher

The troublelicious heist game with just one loser!

YUM YUM Trouble Gum is a card shedding game. When it is your turn, you draw a new card and/or play a card that fits.

As soon as everyone and everything is in place, a heist can go down. At that point, be sure to have the right cards left in your hand or, better yet, none at all. Either way, let someone else take the fall!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Castle Combo: Out of the Oubliette!
description:
The prison gates have just slammed shut... 12 outlaws are now serving their questionable sentences in the kingdom’s dungeons! Will you free them in time for the fiesta?"

Out of the Oubliette! adds 12 new cards and a new rule: padlocks allow players to delay some card effects until a later turn, creating even greater combos!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Two games based on the Chettinad region in South India. The first game is inspired by the handmade Athangudi floor tiles from Chettinad. It is an abstract strategy tile laying game, where you compete in a tug of war by completing patterns. Lay traps to score higher, and aim for a knock-out victory! It can be played in two modes - easy & hard.

The second game is a resource management game with multiple paths to victory. The game simulates a typical village in Chettinad, with two mansions that need to be built with Athangudi tiles, and pillars of teak. The village temple seeks donations of terracota horses. Manage your money to be the first to complete either mansion, or donate 4 horses to the temple, or end up with 50 gold!

Both games are for 2 players.

—description from the publisher

A semi-cooperative campgain game based in 16th century Kerala, with missions involving the Calicut Zamorin, Portuguese and Arab traders. Fictional missions rooted in historical events.

—description from the publisher

A trick-taking card game based on the game 28. Netravati reimplements 28 into a resource gathering, puzzle forming game. Play in two teams of two, as you try to gather railway tracks and tunnels to lay the Konkan railway, to cross terrain such as mountains and forests.

Win tricks to secure tracks that you need, and bid the highest to get the chance to connect railway stations. Try to secure your objectives, as the other team tries to derail your plans!

—description from the publisher

Vosper Attack is a set WWII miniatures rules with is based on Torpedo Boat battles in the English Channel (1941-45), involving the British Vosper Motor Torpedo Boats and the German E-Boats.  The rules are simple and abstract, but it would be quite easy to add in other type of ships into the game.

Equipment you need is a number of small scale Ship Models (1/300 scale), Aircraft Models, Torpedo Markers, Dice (D6), Ship Cards, three sets of different Colour Counters, Position Cards, 30 degrees turn Template, Ruler and a pack of Playing Cards.

Based on authors previous game rules; Battle Trek and Mega Fighting War Machines.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Phoenix & Phoenix Unleashed
description:
Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Phoenix & Phoenix Unleashed brings an iconic story arc and character from the Marvel Universe to the table.

This character pack brings new mechanisms to the table, a dynamic set of miniatures for hobbyists, and a fan favorite for die-hard and casual X-Men fans.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In Orphan of Plight, you take the role of a teenager without family, shortly before the fall of modern civilization due to a global famine.
Orphan of Plight is a event-driven game. During the course of a playthrough, you'll resolve 12 event cards, each with choices to make. Most of them will present you with moral dilemmas: Will you help people who - just like you - are trying to survive? Or will you take advantage of their condition?

You win by resolving all event cards, signifying your arrival at the Orphanage.

Your character may suffer from hunger or wounds, things which have become usual after the fall, however, there's a third condition you'll encounter: Despair. You suffer despair by taking regrettable actions, however in the same way you can feel a little better by doing the right things.

You'll have to manage 2 resources: Food and Bullets. Your character must consume 1 food after resolving a card, while bullets can be used if things go awry... or for your personal gain.

Some cards may also allow you to gain upgrades: items or equipment which will aid in your journey.

Orphan of Plight has a small footprint, is quick to play (2-3 minutes) and can be taken anywhere, consisting of just 32 cards and 4 markers.

With its 24 Journey Cards, your experience will always be different. Additionally, you may also play using 16 cards instead of the usual 12 for a harder game.

—description from the designer

Abuelas con hachas ("Grannies with Axes") is a fun card and strategy game inspired by nostalgic summer memories in rural towns, where grandmothers used axes for all sorts of tasks.

In this game, players compete to collect axes and chickpeas, which they trade for granny cards, aiming to score the most points by the end of the game. With simple yet strategic mechanics, the game unfolds over three rounds, each divided into turns.

Players place cards from their hand into axe rows, using chickpeas to boost their position. This determines the pick order when axes are distributed at the end of each round. Collected axes can be exchanged for granny cards, which award points and require specific combinations of axes and chickpeas.

The strategy lies in managing your resources and positioning yourself ahead in the rows to grab the best cards. The “cutting in line” mechanic adds a competitive twist, allowing players to jump ahead if they have more chickpeas in hand. Axe and granny cards have different values and requirements, encouraging careful planning each turn.

At the end of the game, points are scored based on collected cards and remaining chickpeas, rewarding both strategic play and efficient use of resources.

Abuelas con hachas blends resource management, player interaction, and a uniquely thematic design. With easy-to-learn rules and quick gameplay, it’s a light game packed with strategic depth.

—description from the designer

In Get Gnomed!, be the first player to steal your opponents' lawn ornaments and claim victory in this wacky and whimsical board game. Mix strategy, stealth, and high fashion as you command your gnome army to become the backyard champs!

During a turn, players play cards and roll dice to fight their opponents and search their mushrooms for a lawn ornament.  But there are traps and bombs under mushrooms too, so you better watch out!

The first player to find a lawn ornament underneath one of their opponent's mushroom tokens wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Throwing Thunderbolts: A Wargamer's Guide to the War of the First Coalition, 1792-1797
description:
‘Peoples do not judge in the same way as courts of law… they throw thunderbolts!’ — Maximilien Robespierre.

Throwing Thunderbolts is a unique guide to wargaming the wars of the French Revolution that both the beginner and experienced wargamer, new to the period, will enjoy and find useful. 
This is a book for wargamers based on history rather than a history book. It will explain how the battles of the revolution can be recreated by some of the different wargame systems available at an appropriate scale. 
The focus of this volume is the War of the First Coalition, 1792–7, which involved more than one hundred and fifty battles and actions, all of which offer potential scenarios for different wargames. 
The armies of France, Britain, Spain, Austria, Prussia, United Provinces (the Netherlands), Sardinia Piedmont, Hannover, Hesse-Kassel, Hesse Darmstadt and the Two Sicilies (Naples) are all involved in this great conflict, involving action on all the borders of France as well as the internal counter-revolution. The navies of Britain and France also fought in the Atlantic, the Mediterranean and in the West and East Indies, where their armies also fought bloody campaigns. 
To capture the full benefits of this focus the Second Partition of Poland (1792–3), the Haitian Revolution (1791–1804) and the Third Anglo Mysore War (1790-1792) are excluded from the scope of this work. 
This is an exciting period for wargamers as the art of war was undergoing continual transformation which was reflected in the way the different armies fought, and this guide will bring this to the fore. 
A comprehensive review of the military history of the war provides the background complete with clear maps. 
Throwing Thunderbolts covers the full gamut of wargaming this period, selecting scales and rules and painting miniatures.
Throwing Thunderbolts includes nine ready-made scenarios, using popular, commercially available rulesets, covering the full range of the combatants and at different scales of game. Included in the scenarios are the first major battles of both Napoleon Bonaparte and the Duke of Wellington, at Toulon, 1793, and Boxtel, 1794 respectively. The Battle of Castiglione is fought in full at two different scales, and the armies of Prussia and Sardinia Piedmont feature strongly in two more scenarios. 
Two further scenarios are naval scenarios including a part of the Glorious First of June and a frigate action in the East Indies. 
Throwing Thunderbolts is essential reading for anyone interested in recreating the War of the First Coalition with miniatures on the tabletop. 
Throwing Thunderbolts is a unique book, no other publication deals with wargaming the War of the First Coalition so comprehensively.

boardgame: 聚光派—數感系列：形意黑熊 (Xingyi Formosan Black Bear)
description:
形意黑熊是聚光派的形意拳高手，出拳變化莫測，讓人眼花撩亂。形意拳的奧義是透過口訣的數字順序，前後對調、左右翻轉，使出連續招式。

現在換剛入聚光派修行的弟子，你來試試看，是否能成為形意拳的高手。

Xingyi Formosan Black Bear is a master of Xingyiquan (a form of Kung Fu) from the Juguang School. His punches are unpredictable and dazzling. The secret of Xingyiquan is to perform continuous moves by switching the numbers in the formula, turning left and right.

Now let's turn to the disciples who have just entered the Juguang School to practice. Come see if you have what it takes to become a master of Xingyiquan.

Gameplay: using a tetris pattern, find and group the numbers on the card within the matrix.

boardgame: 聚光派—數感系列：格鬥獼猴 (Gathering School—Number Series: Fighting Macaque)
description:
聚光派中有著一群熱愛格鬥的獼猴們，個個練就一身好本領。平時喜歡和門下師兄弟形意黑熊、閃電石虎和旋風藍鵲切磋武藝，展現各式絕活。但其實格鬥獼猴有著超強洞察力，能在瞬間，掌握格鬥勝負的關鍵！
　　現在換剛入聚光派修行的弟子，你來試試看，是否能成為洞察關鍵瞬間的格鬥高手。

There is a group of macaques who love fighting in Juguang Sect, and each of them has mastered a lot of skills. Usually they like to practice martial arts with their fellow disciples, Xingyi Formosan Black Bear, Lightning Stone Tiger and Whirlwind Blue Magpie, and show off their unique skills. But in fact, fighting macaques have super insight and can grasp the key to winning or losing in a fight in an instant! 
　　Now it's the turn of the disciples who have just entered Juguang Sect to practice. You come and try to see if you can become a master of fighting who can see through the key moment.

Gameplay: Roll the dice and find the picture that matches

boardgame: 聚光派—數感系列：閃電石虎 (Force School: Lightning Stone Tiger)
description:
The Lightning Stone Tiger moves very fast, comes and goes without a trace, and specializes in carrying out secret missions for the Juguang Sect. He is good at the combination of three types of boxing, defeating many masters and completing many difficult tasks.
　　Now the leader of the Juguang Sect has instructed that the new disciples can learn the secrets of the Lightning Stone Tiger and help the Juguang Sect to fulfill the important task of glorifying the sect in the future.

　　閃電石虎動神速，來無影去無蹤，專門執行聚光派的秘密任務。擅長三種拳法搭配的組合技，擊敗眾多高手，完成許多艱辛的任務。
　　如今聚光派掌門指示，希望新加入的弟子能習得閃電石虎的奧義，未來協助聚光派完成光大門派的重責。

boardgame: 聚光派—數感系列：旋風藍鵲 (Force School: Whirlwind Blue Magpie)
description:
Whirlwind Blue Magpie, who comes and goes like a gust of wind, is highly skilled in martial arts, quick in attack, and knowledgeable in many martial arts. He can quickly perform a variety of unique skills in a flash, defeating many masters who come to challenge the Juguang Sect.
　　But Whirlwind Blue Magpie has a mortal enemy - Thunder Tree Frog, who is as fast and skilled as Blue Magpie. The two often meet to fight but can never decide the winner. Finally, they decide to send their most proud disciples to decide the winner, bringing this long-lasting duel to an end.

　　旋風藍鵲，來去一陣風，武術高、出招快、知識淵博深黯許多武林絕學。能在電光火石間迅速施展多種絕技，擊敗許多前來聚光派挑戰的高手。
　　但旋風藍鵲有個死對頭—雷霆樹蛙，不論速度、招式都不亞於藍鵲，兩人常常相約比鬥但始終無法分出勝負，最後決定派出自己最得意的弟子一分勝負，為這場持續許久對決劃下句點。

This Titan expansion for Battle Monsters brings Mothra to the battlefield. Mothra can be played with any Battle Monsters faction.

The Queen of the Monsters has a unique suite of effects that can make her hard to plan against. She places web tiles that can pin troops in place, heal her allies, poison her enemies, and unleash one of the biggest attacks in the game with her giant stinger strike.

Battle Monsters: Mothra expansion includes:

     Gigantic Mothra figure
     Titan board
     Deluxe Titan dice
     Deluxe Titan base
     Web tiles
     Rage cards
     Initiative cards


—description from the publisher

Turn math into a delicious fast-paced dash for the win with this fine motor dumpling bonanza! Draw a number card, then pick and match two numbered dumplings that add or subtract to the number shown, but watch out for surprises—you might lose a turn, name your own number, and more! In addition to early math skills, kids also hone fine motor skills as they play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ホワイトランナー (White Runner)
description:
Set forth into a thrilling world of speed and snow! Compete against other cross-country skiing animals in the once in a century "White Runner" race to prove your agility, speed, and endurance. Play as the swift rabbit, clever fox, mighty bear, or athletic deer, using their unique skills to outmaneuver your opponents and win the race!

Each round is divided into two sequential phases:


     Skating Style Phase
     Skiing Phase


During the Skating Style Phase, players select one of their four skating style cards to play. The cards will dictate the player order, the amount of available dice, and the possible movement spaces for the next phase. They also modify the players' speed, potentially granting them more dice to use throughout the game.

In the Skiing Phase, players go in order dictated by the stamina cost of their skating style cards. Depending on the card played, players will roll a certain number of dice to earn their movement points. These movement points are spent to proceed in the race, fulfilling the requirements of each movement space ahead. However, players may only move as many spaces as their played skating style card allows.

Being in the lead isn't always beneficial though - jumping over other players counts as one move, so you can advance multiple spaces with just one move!
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Eldritch Rivalry is a competitive strategy game for 1–4 players, inspired by Lovecraftian myths, ancient gods, and dark cults.

Each player leads a unique cult in a ruthless race to earn the favor of the Great Old Ones. Over the course of 12 rounds, players move across the map and take up to three actions per turn — such as drawing or playing cards, completing missions, sacrificing acolytes, and buying from the market — while racing to complete shared objectives before their rivals to earn precious rewards.

Objectives are limited and can only be claimed by one player, adding urgency, player conflict, and area contention. In addition to competing for missions, players can also sacrifice their acolytes to perform powerful rituals that grant victory points and unique strategic advantages.

Games last around 90 minutes with 4 players. The game offers high replayability and evolving strategies thanks to 248 cards across 9 unique decks — far more than you'll use in a single session.

—description from the publisher

Reseller Must Be Destroyed is a bluffing and bidding game where players compete to collect valuable items while uncovering hidden scalpers. Each round, everyone secretly plays a card—highest number wins the item, unless there's a tie. The twist? Scalpers are lurking among the bids, and any item they win counts double. Spotting patterns, avoiding ties, and outplaying the scalpers are key to winning over nine tense rounds.

The Rookies is a cooperative game that needs curious, young eyes with a flair for adventure. Follow the threads of any story, observe the cards, match the right elements, and discover the end of each adventure. It’s up to you to unravel the 4 mysteries during a day with the Rookies!

This game includes text that immerses young players in amazing adventures. Parents, now’s the time to warm up your beautiful voices and bring the chosen story to life! A story unfolds over several turns, in which kids would be your smart guides to follow. Taking turns, according to their choices, players will visit locations and try to combine cards to form shapes that unlock new cards to advance the story until the End card is found.

—description from the publisher

Inspired by the landscape and wildlife of the British countryside, Meadowvale is a beautiful strategic tile-placement game.

Players build a shared countryside of woodlands, hedgerows, rivers and meadows, where animals behave according to their ecology: foxes hunt rabbits, owls sweep across open fields, and hedgehogs seek shelter along gardens and hedgerows. Through wildlife cards and habitat tokens, players enrich the landscape, creating thriving habitats that animals can inhabit and benefit from. Carefully position and move your animals, invest in species and habitats, compete for limited species tokens that unlock new ecological abilities, and anticipate your opponents as the countryside evolves.

Create balance in the countryside, claim your place among the wildlife, and become a Keeper of the Vale.

boardgame: Ich hab noch nie ... für Harry Potter-Fans
description:
Never have I ever ... - the popular classic game for Harry Potter fans
Now it's getting magical! Whether on the go, at home or as an exciting party game - this card game is guaranteed to never get boring. Funny, magical and a little bit wicked, over 60 magical statements are guaranteed to provide fun and variety at theme parties, birthdays and celebrations. The perfect game for laughing and finding out secrets about each other.

Train your mind in this race for the perfect time!

Mind Sprint is the game that blends meditation and speed. The athlete who gets closest to the time targets is the ultimate winner!

This game can be used for cognitive training for athletes! On your marks! Get set! Go!

How to Play

1. Race Start
The organizer signals the start: "On your marks, get set, GO!!"
Upon hearing "GO!!", players click on Start Race.
The pen must be positioned in any lane on the starting line of the chosen event (100m, 200m, and 400m).

2. During the Race
Players move the pen along the course on the sheet, simulating the race.
The pen must cross the finish line before clicking Stop Race.

3. Verification and Medals
Everyone clicks on View Final Time.
Whoever gets closest to the target time receives gold, the second closest receives silver, and the third closest receives bronze.
Medals are recorded on the scoreboard.

4. Winner
After the three races, whoever has the most medals wins. In a tie, the one with the smallest total difference between real times and target times wins.

—description from the designer

This card was a promo in the Spielbox issue 3/2025. The rules are in English, French and German.
The promo was also available in Plato Magazine 176.

GRUMPY GOAT

The game follows the original rules with the following addition: Shuffle the Grumpy Goat card into the deck.

The Grumpy Goat is a joker card and stands for the number 0 in any color. If you start with the Grumpy Goat, the value stands at 0.

When you play multiple cards, the Grumpy Goat must be in last position.

Example: If you play 2 and 7 of the same color and you use the Grumpy Goat, the value of your set is 720. If you have the Grumpy Goat in your hand at the end of a hand, you receive 5 points.

The escape box for Minecraft fans: stop the hacker attack!
In this unofficial co-operative adventure game, you take on the role of students in the real world. Your friend Vincent, aka the YouTuber ‘Wink’, has received the beta version of Minecraft Earth from a mysterious programmer. But it was a trap! Without intending to, Vincent spreads a mega virus throughout Paris, causing traffic lights, computers and vending machines to go crazy. Can you solve the eleven puzzles in 45 minutes and save the city before it is completely thrown into chaos by the evil hacker ‘Pharaoh’?

—description from the publisher

Build chains of bacilli and collect the most through smart steals—be the first to reach 15 points!

Mark the play area. Start with an 80x80 cm space (e.g., a table); this can be adjusted later depending on the group's temperament: the smaller the area, the more confrontation there will be during the game.

Each player chooses one color and takes 16 matching cards, shuffles them, draws four, and places the rest face down next to them to form their draw pile.

The youngest player (or the one closest to age 6) goes first.

On their turn, players must play a card from their hand into the play area, and then draw one card from their draw pile—unless they have no more cards left to draw.

—description from the designer

Collect the most valuable sets of dancers and enjoy the rhythm of the ringdance!

Shuffle the 48 dancer cards.

Each player should have 3 face-up cards in front of them.

It’s important that players sit facing each other so that each has one unowned card set on either side, forming a ring.

Choose a starting player. The game proceeds in clockwise order.

On your turn, always draw one card, then choose whether to play: the drawn card, or one of the three cards in front of you.

If you play a card from your display, place the newly drawn card in its place.

The played card is placed face up next to the draw pile, and you must perform the action shown on the card.

—description from the designer

DOWN: The exciting cave expedition
Should you continue abseiling or rather wait and hope for the big treasure find? That's the question in this captivating family game for ages 8 and up. The cave explorers embark on an exciting expedition into the depths and hunt for the best treasures. But who will collect the most points in the end and who will find nothing more than a boring hard hat? It's not just luck that decides your fate, tactics are also required!

—description from the publisher

The brand new Roll & Write by Rita Modl: Make tactical marks!
Luck meets strategy! With these dice, it's all about the size, because the three different sizes mean one to three crosses in the respective row of numbers. Points are awarded for the position in the row. The rules are quickly explained and the fun can begin immediately! A dice game that requires tactics - and that famous bit of luck! The two different game scenarios offer variety and extra fun: Compete against each other in an exciting competition - or go on a record hunt on your own! Who will use the dice tactically and achieve the highest score?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Der ultimative Spielblock: Kopf gegen Kopf – Die Wichtel sind los!
description:
Head to head - together or against each other?
Whether at home or on the go - four different games provide plenty of fun: who will win in "Which gnome is it?" and who will be the quickest to find the right keyword in the hidden object game? Together you can search for differences in cute pictures and find out how similar you think in "What do you think?".

—description from the publisher

LOL: The party game with guaranteed laughter!
Take on the challenge: Can you master the challenges, read out the jokes and keep to the rules without laughing? The one who doesn't laugh wins! But that's really difficult with these jokes and funny tasks. The game includes 5 different categories for extra variety: Jokes & Jokes, Actions, Crazy Rules, Who would rather? and Clear the Stage!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bicocca (1522) • Pavia (1525): Second expedition of François I to Italy
description:
To celebrate the 500th anniversary of the Battle of Pavia (February 24, 1525),  this game features  the entire 6th Italian War, beginning in 1521 and leading up to Pavia,. The strategic map will include part of southern France, invaded by the Imperials led by defector Charles de Bourbon, former constable of France, and notably the strategic city of Marseille, which has been under siege for 40 days.

There are different scenarios:
- La Bicocca: 1521-22
- Intermezzo : 1522-1523
- Marseille: 1523-24
- Pavia: 1524-25
- The 6th war of Italy: 1521-25

For those who own “Marignan 1515, la chevauchée de François 1er en Italie”, the map will be twice as large (85 x 60cm), with the same number of counters (216 in 15mm format) and a thick rules and scenario booklet in English and French. The game will be available in a ziplock format.

Number of Players: 2
Solo Playability: 4/10
Complexity: 6/10
Duration: 120 to 180 minutes
One Turn: One to two months (winter)
1 Strengh Point: Represents 500 to 2,000 men
Scale: One zone ≈ 50 km

—description from the publisher

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Players act as rival crime outfits in 1920s New York. Each crew is represented by face-down cards showing mob roles. You’ll manage a small, fixed tableau of three or four cards while the rest form a shared draw and discard pile.

On your turn you draw one card, then either play it to the discard area to trigger its printed action or swap it face-down with a card in your tableau, triggering the bumped card instead. Actions let you peek at hidden cards, swap crews, flip opponents’ cards face-up, block attacks with a Safe House, or eliminate a rival by flipping their $3 Capo with a Hitman.

A round ends when the deck empties or the required number of players are “whacked.” Surviving players reveal their crews and score only the $2 Bagmen and $3 Capos they kept hidden. Scores accumulate over rounds; the moment someone reaches $10 or more, they win.

—description from the designer

Bluffosaurus is a clever and fast-paced bluffing game where players compete to build the best sequence of dinosaurs—based not on what they see, but on what they think they know.
Each dinosaur card shows a charming prehistoric creature on the front and a set of numeric values on the back, representing stats like weight, speed, or lifespan. Players hold their cards so that only they can see the values; opponents can only see the image. Throughout the game, players place these dinos into a central row—left if they believe their dino’s value is lower than the others, right if it's higher—all without knowing any values except their own.
The twist? A scoring card defines the active category for the round, and only that stat matters. Can you accurately guess where your dino fits in the line-up? Or are you just hoping not to get called out?

After each dinosaur is played, the next player may choose to continue the sequence or challenge it. If a challenge is made, all dinosaurs in the row are revealed and checked for correct order. If the sequence is valid, the last player who played a dino earns the scoring card. If not, the challenger scores instead. Either way, the game continues with a new round and a new category until one player collects five scoring cards and claims victory.

Key Features:


     Unique combination of open images and hidden information
     Tense decision-making and psychological mind games
     Replayable with different stat combinations and bluffing dynamics
     Quick turns and constant engagement


Perfect for players who enjoy deduction, bluffing, and a little prehistoric mischief, Bluffosaurus is easy to learn, quick to play, and always surprising. Will your dino strategy stand the test of time, or will you get outbluffed?

—description from the publisher

Flails & Citadels is a new grimdark solo adventure compatible with Doom Pilgrim.

It uses the same game mechanics as  Doom Pilgrim, but offers more adventuring, with the focus on encounters and looting, compared to the original, which has a wider medieval setting scope, sometimes less appealing for a regular fan of fantasy games.

A new feature in Flails & Citadels is cards have almost always some effect. While in Doom Pilgrim, quite often you just pass a card with no change in your character sheet (for example if you come to a town with no gold in your purse), in Flails & Citadels, cards usually have some effect for every condition you are in at the moment, which makes the game much more actionable and balanced.

—description from the publisher

RUSE: ALTITUDE is an expansion that opens up a new mountainous territory, adding new objectives, new dice, and unique natural obstacles to heighten exploration and competition between foxes.

—description from the publisher

In this game, you compete as a Chai stall owner in a vibrant market. Choose your speciality Chai blend, serve customers strategically down the queue, and earn double points by showcasing your unique recipe. Utilise, "Waitlist" cards to skip customers in the queue, adding a unique twist.

The game ends when the customer deck runs out, and victory goes to the player with the most points, combining served Orders, Specialised Chai bonuses, and unused Waitlist cards. Get ready for a chai-sipping, strategy-packed adventure for a perfect fun family time!

A picture is worth a thousand words, but with TAKATCH TU?, images become perfect puzzles for those who can read between the lines.

Two, three, four, or more than five images and as many syllables to guess to form everyday words based on fun and graphic illustrations in every sense of the word: that's what awaits you with Takatchu tu?, the latest addition to our collection of party games for ages 18+.

boardgame: Five Leagues From The Borderlands Compendium
description:
In the perpetual gloom of the underworld’s massive vaults, strange creatures prey on those eking out a living in the ruins of ancient halls. Only the bravest of adventurers prowl the rubble, seeking forgotten riches and wondrous artifacts.

This Compendium gathers the first two expansions for Five Leagues from the Borderlands into a single volume, adding new characters, mechanics, enemies and aberrations to the core game. Additionally, this book features the new standalone Five Leagues game: Deep Below.

DEEP BELOW
In Deep Below, you take charge of a six-strong warband exploring the underworld’s collapsed keeps and claustrophobic caves. It is both a standalone game and a variant of Five Leagues from the Borderlands, tailored to the deep, dark environment, and integrated to allow characters to transfer between games for an expanded game universe. Deep Below streamlines the core mechanics, offering a full campaign experience with its own character progression.

Archetype-based character creation including Rune Magic, a new mystic art.
Build your underworld base using Settlements and Structures rules.
New origins: Grey Elves, Derzkan, Weaslings, and Windspite Clan Dwarves.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Five Leagues From the Borderland

description: Includes a new floor and two reputation tokens.

Coincoinche is a revisited version of belote coinchée. Players form two teams of two and bid on the number of points they think they can score. Be careful not to get "coinché"... or worse, "coincoinché"!

The game is played over several rounds, until a team reaches 2,000 points.
In each round, players bid on the number of points their team is capable of achieving. The winning team for the round is the one that makes the highest bid.
At the end of the round, their score is checked: if the bid reaches the number of points, they score their points plus the bid.
But if their score doesn't reach the bid, the opposing team wins all the points!

boardgame: I Love Hawker Food: The Card Game
description:
Dive into the vibrant world of Singapore's iconic Hawker Centres with I ❤️ Hawker Food - The Card Game! This engaging card game brings the delicious and diverse street food scene to your tabletop.

Packed with 5 distinct game modes, this game offers something for every taste and mood: from the lightning-fast reflexes and classic memory challenges, to strategic deduction, secret food guessing, and high-energy verbal charades. Each mode offers a unique twist on familiar mechanics, ensuring high replayability.

Featuring 30 beautifully illustrated food cards (representing 15 unique Singaporean dishes), I ❤️ Hawker Food is designed for 1-10 players, ages 3 and up, with quick playtime of 10-20 minutes. It's easy to learn, highly versatile, and a fun way to discover popular local dishes as you play.

Get ready for hungry laughs and endless fun in this compact, delicious game experience!

—description from the publisher

A clever memory game for the whole family, from the youngest ones. The game offers three game modes, which can be combined, so it also has challenges for older players. From simple matching to special abilities, and true to the world of folk tales players can also experience the struggle between good and evil. All this with illustrations from one of the greatest successes of the Pannónia Film Studio's Kecskemét Studio, the Hungarian Folktales series from the golden age of Hungarian cartoon production.

A Magyar népmesék memóriajáték remek szórakozást kínál az egész család számára. Több játékmód is választható, így minden korosztály megtalálja a neki való játékot. A kicsik játszva fejleszthetik memóriájukat a keresd a párját játékkal.

A nap-hold-csillag modul segítségével növelhető az egyes motívumokkal jelölt lapok száma. A haladók könnyedén használhatják a különleges képességeket, amelyeket az ikonok jelölik, valamint ahogyan a népmesékből, úgy innen sem hiányozhat a jó és a rossz küzdelme. Ebben a játékmódban a vitézek és a gonosztevők küzdhetnek meg. A zöld hátterű vitézek és a lila hátterű gonoszok csapnak össze egymással. Ha sikerül egyszerre egy zöld és egy lila hátterű lapot is felhúzni, akkor a vitéz nyert.

A különböző játékmódok kombinálhatóak egymással, így egy új, összetett és még izgalmasabb játékot kapunk.
A jateknet.hu leírása.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
SUMMATION™ is a fast-playing, turn-based card game where players use numbers, operators, and wild cards to form equations that match a target number (SUM OF X).

The game is designed to blend quick thinking, strategic decision-making, and mental calculation in a way that is easy to learn, fun for all ages, and highly replayable.

Each round begins by determining a SUM OF X — a number between 11 and 20 that becomes the target for all players.

Players start with seven cards and take turns either forming valid equations that reach the SUM OF X or drawing a card.

All equations must start with the Last Card from the discard pile (Last Card Rule™), and number cards placed side-by-side automatically sum thanks to the Auto-SUM™ mechanic.

SUMMATION™ is ideal for families, classrooms, and casual players who enjoy clever card play and math-based strategy without unnecessary complexity.

Easy to learn. Infinite ways to win.

At the turn of the 18th century, the night sky was an uncharted frontier. Under the guidance of astronomer Caroline Lucretia Herschel and her brother William Herschel, a new generation of scholars is called upon to chart stars, identify comets, and name unknown celestial bodies.

Herschels is presented in a tabloid-style newspaper with integrated rules and game board. A few additional components are required to play: a pencil, a pen, a ruler, and a standard deck of cards. Each round, two revealed cards determine the coordinate where the player may draw a line and which actions are available on the Celestial Map—such as discovering stars, planets, and comets, or defining constellations. Completed missions grant extra actions and allow players to name a Celestial Body. After 8 rounds, the player with the most Prestige Points wins.

—description from the designer

It's time for dinner, and the little pigs are feeling famished! Players are tasked with leading their squad of pigs across the mucky mud pit and into the barn, but the challenge ahead is far from simple, and the path is sure to become muddy!

The game dice allow players to move their piggy game pieces forwards or backwards. Black indicates a move forward. Red indicates a move backwards.
Landing on a pig of a different color allows the top pig to take control and change the direction of movement.

The game ends when a player has all his pigs in the barn.

—description from the publisher

In Memoriabô, children are invited to build robots. The parts are obtained through a memory-based mechanic—searching for matching pairs of robot parts on a grid of 16 face-down cards. When a player finds a pair (either identical cards or cards of the same color), they collect them and take them to their laboratory, where they’ll assemble their robots.

When a player completes an entire robot of the same color, they activate that robot’s bonus effect. There are 4 robot colors, each with a unique effect. Turns proceed this way, with each player taking their chance to find matching pairs and fitting them together to build their robots—until someone assembles their fourth robot or no more cards remain to replenish the grid. At this point, the endgame is triggered: everyone gets one final turn, and the player who has completed the most robots is declared the ultimate robot builder!

Memoriabô blends memory and puzzle mechanics, adding the excitement of combo actions when a robot is fully assembled in a single color. Beyond the basic mode, the game allows players to adjust the difficulty level using special effect cards, which can be included or excluded for endless variability in each game setup.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Soloventure: Forest of the Mad Queen
description:
In Forest of the Mad Queen you find yourself waking in the middle of a thick wood. With no idea how you arrived there, your only hope is to follow the meandering paths through the thicket for a sign of escape!

This solo-play game has been designed from the ground-up as a single player campaign with endless replayability and playthrough.

With Four Heroes to choose from and a procedurally-generated dungeon full of randomized encounters be sure to keep your wits about you as you explore the Forest of the Mad Queen!

—description from the publisher

Penny Poltergeist is a spooky 9 card Print-n-Play game. Solitaire.

You are locked in the house. You are not alone. Something else is here. You can feel it. The cold. An unnatural cold. And violence.

In Penny Poltergeist, you move from room to room investigating for clues. You have a candle. You are looking for a key. You must escape.

—description from the designer

The dinosaurs are on the loose and causing total chaos! To fix this, you must organize the best park for these adorable dinos, making sure dinosaurs of the same species stay together and don’t mix with others—or else they’ll start fights!

Dino Parque ("Dino Park") is a light and fun game where you roll dice, choose the best combination, and write down your results, trying to become the best Dino Park builder! A perfect game to introduce the "roll and write" mechanic to kids and new players.

Each round, you’ll pick a combination of dinosaurs and fences to organize your park. Your goal is to complete as many islands and/or dinosaur species as possible to earn points. At the end, the player with the most victory points wins as the ultimate Dino Park builder!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fantasy Civ Solo: Discovery and Doom
description:
Fantasy Civ Solo: Discovery and Doom is a print and play solo civilization style game where you’ll explore  fantasy landscape full of monsters, discover wonders hidden in the wilds, expand your civilization across the land, and seek to keep your lands safe by exterminating your foes.

At the start of the game, you will pick an aspiration for your civilization. If your civilization accomplishes the aspiration, you win the game! If, at any point, your civilization controls no cities, you lose the game. Fantasy Civ Solo: Discovery and Doom plays in three turns.

Civilization Turn. On this turn you'll collect resources, explore the map, buy upgrades or forts or cities, train military units, and move your military units to attack monsters. As you explore the map, you'll uncover more resources to collect, expand your military training options, and make epic discoveries. These discoveries can lead to all sorts of possibilities like allying with powerful entities, special actions to make heroes, or quests to complete.

Map Turn. On this turn, monsters will move around the map, and attack the forts or cities you have created for the civilization.

Doom Turn. On this turn you'll roll on the Doom Clock, and if it strikes, the Doom will arrive. The Doom is an extremely challenging "boss fight" for your civilization. If it arrives, good luck!

Gameplay follows this turn sequence - civilization turn, map turn, doom turn - until  your civilization achieves its aspiration and you win the game, or all of your cities fall and you lose the game.

While playing Fantasy Civ Solo, you can explore the map and make discoveries like:

-Giant Boars - which allow you to create mounted military units, that offer more health, speed, and tusk attacks
-Dwarf Mine - which you can trade with or recruit military units from to fight for your civilization
-Treasure Map - which, if you are able to journey to where x marks the spot, offer a promising reward
-Wizard's Tower - which can offer great power, at a cost
-The Resting Place of the Golden Idol - which challenges you with trials of great sacrifice - but the idol itself has great powers of claimed
-60 unique exploration results

You will also uncover monsters, including descriptions like:

-Brutish, clad in cold crude metal - but above all, smelly. They can be smelled from miles away, and the smell lingers...
-The creatures don't have skin, sinew, or muscle anymore. Only bones - bones that walk, run, scratch and tear.
-It is uncertain what they look like, for they wear rags and cloth made from the color of the night. They move in darkness, with the silence of shadows.
-At the center of a massive smoking crater is a dark, cracked stone. Movement can be seen coming out of it. A creature - near translucent - emerges, slowly oozing across the ground.
-30 unique monster results

Fantasy Civ Solo is a solo RPG with full exploration, combat, map movement, and resource rules. It includes a tight game loop that doesn't require any oracles or ambiguous situations. The gameplay of Fantasy Civ Solo is similar to a 4x or civilization style experience, with an emphasis on exploration.

Fantasy Civ Solo: Discovery and Doom includes:
-60 Unique Exploration Results
-30 Unique Monsters
-15 Different Upgrades
-6 Dooms
-6 Aspirations
-Game Map
-Civilization Play Sheet
-Civilization History Sheet

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet – Journey Together
description:
With deep trust and steady guidance, Trainers help bring out the best in their Pokémon. The bond they share empowers them to act as one in battle as they push their strength to the limit, including as Pokémon ex! Team up with N’s Zoroark ex, Iono’s Bellibolt ex, Lillie’s Clefairy ex, Hop’s Zacian ex, and more Trainer’s Pokémon, and discover the unstoppable power of friendship in the Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet—Journey Together expansion!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet – Destined Rivals
description:
There’s trouble brewing now that the nefarious Team Rocket has joined the battle. Be on the lookout for these shifty Team Rocket Admins and Giovanni, the boss himself—they’re ready to disrupt the game.

—description from the publisher

In a world where there are “kaiju,” monsters that look exactly like food, you, a staff member of a struggling lunch box shop, must create a monster lunch box that is more beautiful and more tightly packed than those of other staff members.

Every turn you take a card that gives you one or two different tiles. Place the tiles within the rules that change every game to score specific points and activating effects inside your lunch box. Space is limited and the game ends when all the tile cards are played or someone can't place a tile anymore. 
The highest score wins!

In Role to Reign, every player creates a completely unique character and sets out to take control of a changing, unpredictable world.

You’ll draw a Race, a Role, and a Weapon to start. These give you your starting stats, gear, and abilities. You’ll track your Strength, Presence, Mana, Armor, Movement, and HP on your character sheet. As you play, your stats grow through gear, companions, mounts, and cards.

To make it easier, we built an online character tracker that does all the math for you. As you collect cards or take damage, the tracker updates your stats in real time. It also calculates your Initiative and Attack values automatically.

Initiative is based on the average of your current Strength, Presence, Mana, HP, and Armor. Your Attack is your current Strength plus Presence. In combat, you'll roll a d10 and add it to that number. The tracker handles all of this for you, so you can focus on strategy instead of number crunching. It works on any device and even saves your progress if you're playing a longer session.

Explore a Living Map

The board is made of 30 modular hex tiles that change every game. Each tile has different terrain and interactable locations. You’ll build a new map every time you play, so no two games feel the same.

As you travel, you’ll run into:

Campfires – quick PvE fights for gold and rewards

Towers – tougher fights with better loot like weapons and armor

Cities – can be fought, claimed, and upgraded to give you passive gold and healing

Beacons – roll with Presence to try and earn powerful rewards

Anvils – roll with Strength for game-altering upgrades and riskier options

Stables – non-combat areas where you can buy mounts and companions to boost your stats

You’ll use your Movement stat to travel across terrain, and terrain costs vary. Some areas are harder to cross, and others can be used strategically for defense or control.

Combat: PvP and PvE

You’ll fight in two main ways, against locations (PvE) and against other players (PvP).

PvE combat uses initiative and a d10 roll modified by your stats. You’ll alternate attacks with the enemy until someone is defeated.

PvP combat skips the d10. Instead, you compare your attack stat to the defender’s armor. Each player can spend Strength or Presence to roll a bonus die to boost their roll.

You can fight anyone on your tile at any time. Defeating another player earns you some of their gold and cards. If they’ve died six times to other players, they’re eliminated, and that could be your win condition.

If you die from a location, no penalty, but you respawn at your Stronghold or highest-upgraded city with full stats. You don’t lose your gear. If you die to another player, you do gain a death, and lose a small amount of gold an equipment.

Five Ways to Win

This isn’t a game where you’re chasing one goal. There are five different victory conditions, and you only need one to win:

Control all Strongholds

Control all Cities (solo or with an ally)

Reach 2500 Gold

Complete 18 Quest Points

Be the Last Survivor (all other players have died to PvP six times)

This means you can play aggressive, passive, sneaky, economic, quest-focused, or adaptable depending on what your character is good at.

Turn Structure and End of Turn Cards

Each turn has seven phases: collect gold, play a card or use mana, move, take an action, trade or upgrade, finish movement and heal, then draw an End of Turn card.

Those End of Turn cards are a big part of what keeps everyone engaged. After every turn, you draw one. Some hit the board hard with global effects. Some target individual players. Some give loot or help reposition players. Some are just chaos. With over 150 cards in the deck, the world constantly shifts around you. Even when it’s not your turn, you’re still in it.

What makes Role to Reign Unique?

Characters are fast to build but deep to master

The online tracker keeps everything simple and automatic

Turns are structured to keep things moving, and involve other players during their "down time"

The board changes every game

PvP and PvE are both strategic and rewarding

Victory paths are flexible so you’re never locked in, can ally, and pivot your goal as your character evolves.

Gear, quests, companions, and cards all feed into how you play

Whether you want to build an empire, quest your way to victory, or crush everyone in your path, Role to Reign gives you the tools and the freedom to play it your way.

—description from the designer
Trivial Pursuit: Orsyp Edition is a version of the classic game that centres around itSMF ITIL v3.

Move the Firefox along the track of an independent board to collect additional bonus tiles.

—description from the designer

Advance on the Village track to unlock bonuses and hunting new tiles.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Super Boss Monster II: The Die is Cast
description:
The Dice Queen has arrived in Arcadia with her diceling minions, introducing an all-new mechanic to the hit dungeon-building card game: dice-powered rooms and effects!

Playable as a standalone or a sequel, Super Boss Monster 2: The Die is Cast brings you more dungeon-drafting, minion-placing, hero-luring gameplay. But now you'll need to master the power of dice! When you build certain rooms, you'll roll the dice to power them up by placing the dice in your dungeon. You can then manipulate those values with other Rooms, abilities, and Spells!

The Die is Cast and Super Boss Monster will never be the same! Super Boss Monster 2 is backward compatible with all existing Boss Monster and Super Boss Monster cards. As a standalone game, Super Boss Monster 2: The Die is Cast will contain everything that players need to play.

—description from the publisher

Crawlout: A Bag of Trouble is a solo dungeon crawler you can play in 20–30 minutes, armed with the PnP material you've printed, a bag, and your most trusted six 6-sided dice.

Players take on the role of a lone adventurer exploring randomized dungeons via the draw of tokens or cards from a bag. The game has easy-to-learn rules, fast-paced decision-making, and high replayability thanks to its modular setup.

Crawlout: A A Bag of Trouble uses a bag-draw system where you pull tokens or cards representing rooms, enemies, items, and events. Each draw progresses your dungeon crawl, with branching paths and unexpected encounters emerging organically from the randomized sequence. Combat and exploration are resolved with minimal bookkeeping—often just tracking stats with dice or a small player sheet.

It's time for a new generation to take over the dungeons! In Boss Monster Junior, little monsters take on the dungeon-building and hero-scaring adventures.

Collect coins to pay for rooms in your dungeon, draft heroes to visit your dungeon, and slay them to collect their treasures! Each treasure is worth 1 Victory Point, and whoever has the most of each treasure type gains 3 Victory Points at the end of the game.

As a standalone game, Boss Monster Junior contains everything needed for 2-4 players.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Adventure Is Nigh! Extras Pack
description:
This Extras Pack contains the material that was exclusive to the Collector’s Edition for the The Red Dragon Inn: Adventure Is Nigh! Kickstarter. If you missed the project, or only backed it for the base game, you can still snag everything RDI x AIN!

The Red Dragon Inn: Adventure is Nigh! Extras Pack is an expansion for The Red Dragon Inn, and a base game is required in order to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ultimate Dinosaur Fighting: Triassic Terror Expansion
description:
Triassic Terror adds 2 new dinosaurs to Ultimate Dinosaur Fighting. It includes the Dinosaur Reference Cards, dinosaur standees, VP tokens, as well as 10 new events, 10 new mutations and 10 new techs.

—description from the publihser

Welcome to the Market!
Experience the bustling food alley from three unique angles.
Shopper: Race to complete your grocery lists without missing a thing.
Sambal: Bargain for ingredients and cook up spicy dishes.
Stray: Lead a clan of street cats fighting for left overs and territory.
Fast setup, flexible complexity, and endless replayability. 
Ready to hit the market? Play all three games with the same set of components. 1 box, 3 games!

—description from the designer

This is an expansion to the party game from Piatnik played in 2-4 teams with at least 2 players each. It contains 330 new items.

The teams' meeples move over the board, the goal is to reach the finish. Depending on the category of field they are currently standing at, one player has to act (pantomime) or to describe or to paint a phrase given on a card and the other members of his team have to find out what that should be. If they manage to do so, their meeple moves forward.

(A) Game Setup

Place the Player Aid card in the middle of the table for all players to refer to. Flip the card as necessary to refer to the information on both sides anytime.

Shuffle the 16 Guardian cards face-down. Deal 3 cards face-down to each player. Players should pick up their cards with the backs facing them and the faces visible to others. Do not look at the faces on your own 3 cards. Place the remaining cards face-down spread out individually in the middle of the table to form the card area.

Decide on a starting player, preferably the last player to watch a fantasy movie.
You are ready to play Secret Guardians!

(B) Flow of the Game
Beginning from the starting player, each player takes a turn in order until one player has correctly declared a win to end the game. A player turn is described in detail below:

(C) Flow of a Turn
1. Draw a Card
First, the player draws one of the face-down cards in the card area. They may not draw a card the last player discarded. Make sure the backs are facing you!

2. Name a Guardian
Then, name one of the 4 guardians you think are on your card faces. The 4 guardians are: Garuda, Kirin, Merlion and Cheonyong. The other players must tell you honestly if the guardian you named is on at least one of your face-out cards. If you are correct, then go to step 3. If not, go to step 4.

Note about the cards: The backs (cities) denote the possibility of that guardian being found in that city.
For example, there is a 50% chance that there is a Merlion on the front of a card with a Singapore back.

3. Carry out an Action
If the player successfully names a guardian on their card faces, they may carry out one of the 3 actions below:

Flip a card
The player may choose one of their 4 cards to flip around such that the back is facing outward and the front is facing them. The player can now see the win condition. A card with the front facing the player is known as a flipped card.

Swap a card
The player may swap any one of their 4 cards with a card in the card area, or with a card in another player’s hand. They may NOT swap with a card that is flipped, or that was swapped in the previous turn.

Declare a Win
The player may declare a win by choosing a win condition on one of their flipped cards.
They then unflip all their cards such that all their card fronts are facing the other players.
If the win condition is met, the player immediately wins the game.

The win conditions are:

     Have 4 guardians of the same colour
     Have 4 guardians of different colours
     Have 2 pairs of guardians in different colours


4. Discard a Card
The player chooses one of their 4 cards to discard into the card area. They may not discard the card just unflipped (if any), or the one that was drawn this turn — they must place it in now.
It is now the next player’s turn clockwise!

(D) End of the Game
When one player has successfully declared a win, the game ends and that player is the winner!

boardgame: The Third World War: Finnish Airforce
description:
Issue 109 of Paper Wars

Allows the 100 aircraft units in Third World War to take losses in "squadrons" of 25 aircraft.
Includes combat tables for formations of less than 100 aircraft.
Includes counters to mark aircraft units with their squadron losses.
Includes a counter for the Finnish airforce (3 squadrons).

Königgrätz '66 is intended to be an easy game to learn and play but large enough so players will learn the details of the battlefield as well as the weapons, tactics, and maneuvers that succeeded or failed.
The game includes two main scenarios covering the initial action in the morning and the full day scenario. It will also include an introductory scenario, a retreat scenario, and a "what if" scenario that gives the Austrian player more freedom with his reserve corps.

The Three-Headed Dragon expansion is an additional card for the game Duo Regna, designed to make your matches even more challenging. The three-headed dragon counts as three dragons in the final scoring.

Shuffle the card together with the other dragon cards, mix well, and play as usual according to the original rules.

The lemurs have multiplied! The “Double Lemur” is a promotional card for the game Lemur, giving you the chance to get rid of a bad card or mess with other players!

RULES
