Shuffle the Double Lemur card with the other cards before starting the game. The player who draws the card decides when to play it on top of another Lemur and, upon playing it, chooses a card from their hand and gives it to the player on their left (or to any other player in the advanced variant).

boardgame: O Que O Quê: More Drawings
description:
The “More Drawings” expansion is an additional card for the game O Que O Quê.

Shuffle the card together with the others, mix well, and play as usual according to the original rules.

boardgame: Socotra's Hottest Day: Most Exciting Day of Socotra
description:
Socotra's Hottest Day is a Japanese Doujin made by the Great Sato Society Doujin Group.

The game is set in the Red Sun Black Cross world where Germany wins WW2, and Japan, Britain, and the USA band together to stop them.

This particular scenario takes place on November 8, 1948, on Socotra Island in the Indian Ocean. Japanese and British forces attack the Island, which is a German base for operations in the Indian Ocean, in order to assist the Americans who are dealing with an Invasion of the USA.  The Island is held by the 15th German Panzergrenadier Division.

The Japanese vanguard force was counterattacked by a superior force of German armor. They are now pressing down on the beaches, and the Allied forces make a counterattack of their own to stop the German advance.

The game consists of a folder, rules, map, and 198 counters.

The “More Fruits” expansion includes additional cards for the game Qui-Quitanda, featuring 3 new fruits to make your matches even tastier.

Shuffle the cards together with the others, mix well, and play as usual according to the original rules.

boardgame: Pathfinder Dice Conquest: Shadern Immolator Promo Card
description:
Promo Creature card: Shadern Immolator.

7 health, 1 attack 
Reveal : Draw an additional card.

When placing a die on this creature, you must immediately re-roll it.

Item-  Gloom Blade: Discard to change a die to any number.

boardgame: Dream State: A Dark and Troubling Board Game
description:
Dream State is a card-and-dice game in which the players are challenged to advance a Dream Marker through 3 dream states. As the dream progresses, players may (or may not) benefit from recurring dreams, stimuli from the physical world, and the horrors locked within their minds.

Once the game board is setup, the goal is for the player(s) to make it through all 3 Dream States to the End icon on the board; this represents the player awakening before dying in the dream.

Players will spend their turn doing the following: 
1. Moving the Marker 1 Eye-con on the game board.
2. Drawing a Dream Flow card, resolving it immediately, then discarding the card into the Recurring Dream deck. 
3. Draw a Stimuli Card and resolve it immediately IF one of the following is occurs:
3a. The Marker has just entered a new Dream Level OR
3b. The Marker has just landed on a 3 OR
3c. A card has prompted the Dreamer to do so.

The game includes 1 double-sided game board, 2 dream markers, 42 dream-flow cards, 12 stimuli cards, and 2 threshold dice.

Get ready to launch into fun with Spring the Coop! This hilarious and fast-paced action game is perfect for 2 to 4 players, ages 8 and up. Your mission? Spring all your chickens back into the nest! Players use mini trampolines to bounce quirky chicken characters – each with their own unique look. Successfully spring chickens into the coop to earn golden eggs … the player with the most eggs at the end of the game wins!

—description from the publisher

2 to 4 players collect clues across the span of 80 years while struggling to prove their unique theory about the Loveland Frogman phenomena. This historic-ish game offers actual events and places in the legendary Ohio river town. Be careful as your opposition isn't always the other witnesses (players), sometimes the townies, men in black, and historical disasters are the real opposition. Do you have what it takes to prove your theory or will you, like so many before you, get lost in speculation?

The goal is to collect 10 pieces of Evidence; the first player to do so immediately wins.

At the start of the game, each player will receive a Witness Card and Theory Card. These cards provide flavor and direction for the players investigation.

Players will travel to various locations on the game board by rolling dice against a target reflex value; they are trying to reach the locations shown on their Location Card.

On the players turn, the following will occur in order:

     Draw a Location Card if you don't already have one; resolve it once you arrive at the location.
     Roll to determine if you move; no movement roll required if the year on your drawn location card matches the year on your witness card.
     You may use a card from your play area. These cards are collecting in various ways during game play.  
     Draw a Fiver card immediately after you gain your 5th Evidence.

Don’t get burned! This fast-paced family game is pure chaos & laughs. Pass the spud before time’s up!

Keep the potato in play to avoid losing points in this sizzling game of HOT suspense! Feel the heat and keep your cool in this laugh-out-loud Family friendly game!

Keep the potato up or lose points! Last one standing wins!

—description from the publisher

A quick-play dice and card strategy game featuring the cunning and might of obscure and infamous lurking things.

Slumbering ancient things and elusive cryptids are waiting to be unleashed on the world. Your goal is to use your cards as buffers against your opponent's cards. Players are safe as long as they can draw random cryptid cards to whittle down those of the opponent. The first player to exhaust their last-standing monster card loses the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cryptid Hunter: Hands of Ill Fates Booster Deck
description:
This 17-card enhancement deck is made for use with our Cryptid Hunter card line, including Cryptid Hunter: The Elusive Card Game 2.0 AND Cryptid Hunter: All Alone.

This expansion adds in effects that last the entire game making the game more challenge. At the beginning of the game, 1 or 2 Fate cards are drawn and describe how they impact gameplay. Some of the cards simply make certain Cryptids more difficult to defeat while other cards add in conditional effects based on what they roll on their turn (e.g. the Alien fate card: 1. Any hunter rolling snake eyes is probed! Loss all leads, evidence, and items).

Can't get your friends together for game night? Heck with all of 'em! Now you can hunt cryptids all by yourself (ill advised to be certain). But you already have the core West Virginia game deck, Cryptid Hunter: The Elusive Card Game 2.0? No problem. This deck is not limited to the borders of West Virginia and has a whole different set of cryptids and cryptid hunters. Like the other decks, this one is also compatible with the Hand of Ill Fates booster deck.

There are three games modes each with different victory conditions:
Survivalist: Survive 3 Cryptid Encounters to win. 
Tenure: You must have 12 Fame Points after the last Lead from the deck is drawn and resolved. 
Endurance Hunter: Replenish exhausted decks with discarded cards. Survive to 25 Fame Points.

During setup, the player will take a random Cryptid Hunter card and decide which victory condition they are targeting. This card tells them their stats are that will be used for dice rolls; additionally, it contains their hand limit for the 3 types of cards in the game.  Shuffle the Lead, Evidence, Item, and Encounter decks separately, and place the dice nearby. Now you are ready for your Cryptid Hunt.

During gameplay, the player may perform one of the following actions on their turn:

     Draw a Leads: Draw a card from the Leads deck. 
     Discover Evidence: Discard 2 Leads Cards to draw an Evidence card. 
     Encounter a Cryptid: Discard 2 Evidence Cards to draw an Encounter card. Once a Encounter has been drawn, read and resolve the cards text.  
     Trade: Discard any 1 card for an Item card. 
        Note: If a player has hit their potential limit in the Leads, Evidence, or Items, they may not perform actions that would draw additional cards of that type or must ignore drawing cards if the result of a card text. 
    


Encounters could result in damage to the player. The hunter rotates their card 90 degrees to the right after each hit, and once the player has taken a 3rd damage, the hunter is dead, and the game is over. Some cards will heal the hunter allowing them to un-rotate their card 90 degrees of the left.

The game is over when the player has completed one of the three victory conditions or has died.

boardgame: Dream State: Crossover Booster Deck and Glow Board
description: Enhance your dreamy experience with the glow board. It comes with the 17-card booster deck allowing players to interact in the other dreamer's dream states. This enhancement deck includes 6 glow-cards, 1 instruction/intro card, and 10 holofoil cards to elevate your black-light experience. Even more immersive. Even more creepy.

24/7 - Rally is a clever and accessible cardgame for families and friends, played on a colorful 24-hour clock of circular layed out cards. 
Use dual-color hourcards to move pawns forward — but every move affects someone else too! Work together as a team or play individual, engage in the rally to reach midnight first. Easy to learn, full of surprises, and fun for players from age 10 and up. (or 7+ if you play without the special event cards)

—description from the designer

Take your Battleship fight underwater as you use dice to hunt for enemy submarines. Avoid mines, choose weapons and become the victor.

This game combines memory cards, dice, and dice launchers for a game that can be learned in 5 minutes. Each of the three games in this collection use the key mechanics of the original game and add a dice based twist.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

boardgame: Soldiers of Napoleon: Wars of the First Empire
description:
Refight the battles of the early Napoleonic Wars as France and her allies march in conquest of their First Empire, with Soldiers of Napoleon’s exciting card-driven action sequence, creating tabletop wargames with dramatic tension, difficult tactical command decisions and a strong narrative that will make the battles feel like the Napoleonic period.

Included are: fourteen army lists over four campaign theatres, for the wars of the Third, Fourth and Fifth Coalitions against France, 1805-09, and the invasion of Russia in 1812.


     The War of the Third Coalition, includes four army lists for the principal combatants: France’s Grande Armee against the alliance of Austria and Russia, plus the small British Expeditionary Force to southern Italy.
     The War of the Fourth Coalition, includes three army lists for the combatants of the campaign: France’s Grande Armee against the Prussians and Russians.
     The War of the Fifth Coalition, includes five army lists for the combatants of the various campaigns in Bavaria, Austria, Poland and the Netherlands: France’s Grande Armee, the Bavarian army and the Duchy of Warsaw against the Austrians and the British Expeditionary Force to the Netherlands.
     The Invasion of Russia, includes two large army lists for the combatants of the campaign: France’s mighty Grande Armee de la Russie against the Russians, including new unit profiles.
     March On!, is a narrative campaign system for use with any Soldiers of Napoleon theatre, as players command their own division through a series of linked battles and refight their war, until one side is forced to sue for peace. Featuring unit experience and attrition rules, campaign random events, great battles and siege assaults.
     A Siege Assault scenario and new units for the Army’s Central Reserve, rules for the siege train’s heavy guns and mortars.


You will need a copy of Soldiers of Napoleon Rulebook and Card Set to fully appreciate and use this supplement.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Soldiers of Napoleon: The Peninsular War
description:
Refight the many battles of the Peninsular War as France and her allies march in conquest of Portugal and Spain, with Soldiers of Napoleon’s exciting card-driven action sequence, creating tabletop wargames with dramatic tension, difficult tactical command decisions and a strong narrative that will make the battles feel like the Napoleonic period.

Included are: five army lists over seven years of fighting across Spain and Portugal, for the early years of the war, 1807-1811, and the later years, 1812-1814 as the alliance advance on, and then invade, France.


     The Early Peninsular War, includes three army lists for the principal combatants: France’s Army of Spain against the alliance of the British and Portuguese armies, plus the loyalist


Spanish army.

     The Late Peninsular War, includes two army lists for the combatants of the later campaign: France’s Army of Spain against the alliance of  the British, Portuguese and Spanish.
     Great Battles – Albuera, 1811, is a mini-campaign of five historical scenarios to refight the Battle of Albuera. Scenarios include: the French’s first feint towards Albuera village, the first and second assaults by the French V Corps on the southern flank at the southern knoll, a renewed assault on Albuera, and the finale, General Cole’s 4th Division’s counter-attack.
     New Rules and Unit Profiles, including new Unique Units for the armies, such as Spanish Guerrillas, new Commanders of Note and full unit profiles for all the army lists included.


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Enjoy a new take on a classic by playing Connect 4 horizontally and with dice! Roll four dice, pick one and place it on the board. With new actions like swap and flip, every game is unique and fun! Checkers meets Connect 4! Each of the three games in this collection use the key mechanics of the original game and add a dice based twist.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

description:
Scrabble meets Poker in this ultimate mash up! Two great games are combined into one with Texas Spell Em. Roll your dice to get 3 hole letters. Roll 5 dice for community letters. Use the eight dice to make the highest point word and then bet and bluff your way to victory! Each of the three games in this collection use the key mechanics of the original game and add a dice based twist.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

description:
The best liars know when to lie and, more importantly, when not to. In the game of Pinocchio, you must assess whether the sum of all cards drawn has exceeded 21. Everyone announces their numbers, but can you discern truth from deception? Lying may not always win, but honesty can be a dangerous game too.

In Pinocchio, you take turns drawing a card and announcing what number it is (you may lie). If you think the sum of everyone’s cards is more than 21, instead of drawing a card you may declare a challenge. If you are right, the player before you is cursed. Otherwise, you are cursed. The game is played until one player receives the 4th curse and gets transformed into a donkey. The player(s) with the fewest curses wins.

—description from the publisher

As the new homeowner of an old house in the middle of nowhere, you quickly discover that, even though there are no humans in sight, this region is filled with small strange creatures that you decide to call Squunchies. You become single-minded in attempting to document as many Squunchies as possible, catching them and using their resources to increase your home size and catch even more. Can you maximize your number of Squunchies, their combined happiness, and the size of your home?

Squunchies is a creature-collecting dice management game, inspired thematically by Pokémon and mechanically by One Deck Galaxy. It features dice manipulation and placement, resource management, and a unique overlapping card system where you must choose which abilities to cover up when placing new ones.

—description from the designer

Get ready to rev your engines in this thrilling card game where you and your friends race against the traffic light, betting on whether to speed through or slam on the brakes, while cunningly shifting the light’s color with "Accelerate," "Brake," or "Brake Check" cards to unleash the epic Ibaraki Dash—a daring solo "Go" on yellow that scores +2 points—bluffing and strategizing your way to be the first to hit 5 points and claim victory!

—description from the designer

Agents of Timekeepers Incorporated, we need you! The talented Miss Jane Austen’s reputation has been tarnished, and the ripples across the timeline are threatening to erase her works from history. Of course, some may see no harm in this, but it is a truth universally acknowledged that it would be of no use to live in a world where Mr. Darcy never existed!

Travel back in time to Regency-era Bath, England, where social standing means everything and scandal can be ruinous. You and your fellow agents must work together to unravel a mystery of intrigue and deceit and unmask the scoundrel who slanders Jane Austen’s name. Use your courage, wit, powers of persuasion, and tremendous sensibilities to solve the puzzles and, thereby, the crime. If you fail, history will be rewritten. But succeed, and the true timeline will be restored!

—description from the publisher

Do you have what it takes to work the high steel and win?

In Up-Struction, players take turns to roll the die to decide which action to take. Roll an I-Beam to add to your structure and reach for the sky. Roll a Stop Work Sign to block your opponent. Roll a Crossbar to turn your opponent's work into your work. As you build, your worker figurine climbs higher and higher. But beware, one beam placed out of balance and the whole thing could come toppling down.

How to play:

     Roll the die to decide which action to take.
     Roll I-Beam to add one of your I-Beams to the structure
     Roll a Stop Work Sign to block your opponent.
     Roll a Crossbeam to build off of your opponent's structure.
     Game ends when either one player runs out of I-Beams or the structure tumbles.
     Player whose worker figurine is placed the highest wins.
     If structure tumbles, player who did not cause it to tumble wins.


—description from the publisher

Stringbag lets you fly Fairey Swordfish torpedo bombers against axis warships. Stringbag is solely a solitaire game on the British strike against the Italian Navy at Taranto in 1941. All you supply is a standard deck of playing cards, and you will be ready to go.

When this chicken's gotta go, it's time to play Bingo! Use the funnel to insert poos into this crazy chicken and wind him up! Kids and parents alike can't help but giggle as he waddles along dropping his poos. At the end of each round, players mark their bingo cards on any corresponding space with a poo nugget on the game board. Got five in row? Shout Bingo!

—description from the publisher

Get ready to smack this vending machine and take back your snacks before it attacks! Spin the wheel and drop some treats into the top of the game unit. Then give it a whack and press down on top to see if your treats pop back out! But be careful – eventually the monsters will get tired of being smacked around and jump out! Whoever has the most points when the monsters attack wins.

How to play:

     The spinner tells you what to drop into the vending machine. 
     The snacks are getting stuck! Shake and tap the machine to get them down!
     Smack the top and collect the most snacks to win.
     But watch out for those monsters! They could pop out at any time. 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Race to Kepler: A Deckbuilding Space Adventure
description:
The 20 most populous world cities have launched into the cosmos, each racing to claim humanity's new home: Kepler-452b.

Rising temperatures have rendered our planet unlivable, but humanity always finds a way. In the twilight of Earth's habitability, this is humanity's greatest challenge yet.

In Race to Kepler, you'll lead one of these ambitious city-states in a high-stakes interstellar competition. Recruit elite crew members, bid for powerful Titans, and generate the energy needed to power your ship. Your mission: be the first to reach and colonize our species' last hope for survival.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Aetherial War Card Game: WOE Machine Expansion
description:
Meet the  WOE Machine, an unstoppable lifeform eliminator. Designed by Aegis Prime Industries, this technologically advanced prototype is a menacing sight to behold.

This Elite Boss for the Aetherial War Card Game introduces the first boss with multiple phases, as well as new mechanics, story, and an intense challenge for those brave enough to face it.

—description from the designer

A tricky game for in between or on the go with high replay value, new game mechanics, driven by 60 4-Dots-Relation tiles.

Who manages to generate the most points or beat their own high score.

Only those who make the right decision, with a little luck, win 4-Dots-Relation in the end.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Baron von Zom's Dice of the Undead: Extra Card Pack
description:
This set of 32 additional cards features the Baron’s most outrageous, even brainlesser rules and hilariously unpredictable, extremely undeadly events. You’ll laugh, you’ll curse, and you’ll love how they shake up the game. But beware—these cards might be a bit too much for sensitive minds, and those with fragile nerves might lose their sanity!

—description from the publisher

L'Ultima Battaglia: Volturno 1860 is a Tactical level two-player simulation board-wargame that aims to simulate the battle of Volturno (1860) during the Italian Risorgimento.
The game uses a rules system derived from Custoza, Fields of Dooom and Una Vittoria Inutile, Goito 1848 (Para Bellum VI) of the same designer.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: トリックオブザキラートマト (Trick of the Killer Tomatoes)
description: There are only two suits for taking tricks. Players have to avoid tricks, take only one suit, or take tricks as much as possible. There is no trump card for playing the same suit. Each trick with the same number will be directly knocked out and discarded. The remaining tricks with higher numbers of the leading color will be played until someone takes 10 cards or the hand is out of cards. The score is the number of cards of different colors multiplied by the number of cards taken. If the player takes ten cards, there is no deduction.

The Squirrel is hungry after hibernation and is on the hunt for his favorite foods! In this fun family bluffing game, players will trade fruit with the squirrel in exchange for valuable acorns. But here's the twist - if your friends think you're bluffing, they can call you out, and you risk losing your nuts! The goal is to capture as many acorns as possible from both the squirrel and the other players by being clever and strategic.

Players will need to decide when to bluff and when to play it safe, trying to outsmart the other players while keeping the squirrel satisfied. Who can collect the most acorns and claim victory? But be careful, because once you're caught bluffing, your chances of winning can slip away! Perfect for family game nights, this game is sure to bring laughter, strategy, and a bit of sneaky fun to the table.

—description from the publisher

Yes or No is the card game you can't say no to! Get ready for a fast and furious game of questions where the only rule is you can't say YES or NO! Take a card and begin asking questions - if your opponent says YES or NO, ding the bell and keep the card. If your opponent makes it through without saying YES or NO, they keep the card. Be the player with the most cards at the end of the game to win!

How to play:
Step 1. DRAW a card.
Step 2. ASK questions. 
Step 3. DING the bell! 
Step 4. Earn the most Cards and WIN!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Guinness: Pub Game Series – Epic Coaster Games
description:
Imagine your favorite Guinness posters. Then, imagine them in action playing some of your favorite pub games! 40 actual Guinness coasters, bearing more than 100 years of Guinness artwork, allow fans to celebrate their favorite stout in the most appropriate manner possible. Our instruction booklet contains rules for 12 different games – you can also make up your own! After all, good things come to those who wait.

Enjoy these games:

     Free Throw
     Coaster Catch
     Head Balancing
     Tight Squeeze
     Relay Rally
     Boss Toss
     Kangaroo Hug
     On the Money
     Waiter Run
     Long Toss
     Picture This
     Pyramid


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Изнанка: Пуговицы и печеньки (The Upside Down: Buttons and Cookies)
description:
Изнанка: Пуговицы и печеньки ("The Upside Down: Buttons and Cookies") is an expansion for The Upside Down, delving into the stories of two characters from the base game. Instead of personal objectives, you’ll encounter storybooks: five chapters filled with unexpected events, absurdity, and profound mysteries. Every choice unlocks new abilities and character forms, while the captivating diary entries of the Hunter and the Inspector will bring you closer to uncovering the true nature of the Upside Down.

Also this expansion includes two miniatures: the Hunter and the Inspector.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Изнанка: Кирки и склянки (The Upside Down: Picks and Vials)
description:
Изнанка: Кирки и склянки ("The Upside Down: Picks and Vials") is an expansion for The Upside Down that reveals the untold stories of two base game characters. Replace personal objectives with immersive storybooks—five chapters of bizarre events, dark secrets, and unpredictable choices. Shape your path by unlocking new abilities and forms. Madopa and Gemsneak await you in this twisted world where every decision matters!

Includes two detailed miniatures: Madopa and Gemsneak.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: World Traveler: Race Around the World
description:
World Traveler, the thrilling board game where the goal is simple—complete all four journeys first—but the journey is anything but easy! This game challenges players to navigate their way across four journeys using planes, cars, buses, and boats, all while managing their resources wisely.

Will your money last, or will unexpected expenses leave you scrambling for cash? You might have to press on with limited funds or be forced to stop & raise cash! Strategy, luck, and smart decision-making will determine who can successfully complete all four trips first!

Along the way, players will encounter travel dilemmas, unexpected setbacks, and exciting opportunities that add an element of suspense and unpredictability to every round.

Four Trips with Different Vehicles: Rent a premier vehicle before each leg to maximize your benefit or save your money and hope for luck!

Encounter Mischief: Along the way, players may encounter the opportunity to hold back their competitors, delivering a game-changing result!

Money Management: If players run out of money, they will be stuck until they raise more funds, so strategize your vehicle choices to maximize your impact!

Pack your bags and claim victory with World Traveler!

—description from the publisher

Game is based on the comic strip (also a radio series) The Nebbs by Sol Hess. Deck has 45 cards: 36 suit cards and 9 score cards. Suit cards come in 4 suits named for characters from the comic strip: Nebbs, Potts, Slider, and Max. Each has 9 cards numbered 1-9. Score cards are: Win 1/2/3/5, Lose 1/2/3/5, and one labeled "? - Value of card below". Place the score cards in a face-down stack. Deal each P 9 cards. (for 2-3 players, set the excess cards aside). Play is standard trick-taking. Nebbs are always trump. The winner of the first trick takes the top score card and puts it in front of them. The score cards are then turned face up. The winner of each subsequent trick takes the top score card. After all 9 tricks have been played, each player scores 1 point for each trick taken plus the value of the score cards they took (win cards are positive points, Lose cards are negative points. The value of the  "value of card below" score card is equal to the value of the score card below it (this should be recorded when the next score card is revealed. If it is the last score card in the stack, it scores nothing. Player with the most points after 9 hands wins.

Board (bottom of box) has 60 spaces w/space 60 in middle surrounded by 3 concentric tracks. Background is yellow and numbers are black. There are no illustrations or text of any kind on the board. Spinner has #s 1-6 and  Yellow Flag space. On turn, spin the spinner. If you spin the yellow flag, stay where you are. Else, move the # of spaces indicated. If you would land on another P, lose your turn. 1st to reach the North Pole (space 60) wins.

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Nickelodeon Avatar: The Last Airbender – Quick Play
description:
Dive into the magical world of Avatar with Trivial Pursuit: Avatar: The Last Airbender Edition! Challenge your friends and family with 600 questions that span the epic adventures and rich lore Avatar . Players will test their knowledge across six dynamic categories like Nations of the World, Characters, Elements and Bending, Avatar Lore, Plotlines, and the wild category “My Cabbages”. Packaged in a sleek, Avatar-themed wedge container for easy play at home or on the go, are you ready to master the elements of trivia? Find out now!

600 trivia questions across six unique categories:

     Nations of the World
     Characters
     Elements and Bending
     Avatar Lore
     Plotlines
     My Cabbages


—description from the publisher
family: TV Shows: Avatar The Last Airbender/Legend of Korra

description:
The Minor Details game is the party game for people who can’t draw (much). In this unpredictable, quick-playing game, players choose a Secret Word and draw one detail of it – not the whole picture! Then, they cover their drawing and pass their portfolio to the next player, who adds their own detail – without looking at the previous drawings. The last player to receive the portfolio uncovers the 3 sketches. Then players take turns trying to guess the Secret Word based on the other player’s drawings. Guess enough correctly to win!

NO DRAWING SKILLS NEEDED: No drawing talent? No problem. This fun game for adults and teens is all about the details (and not a player’s drawing skills).
PIECE TOGETHER THE BIGGER PICTURE: This is a drawing game with a catch – players can’t draw the whole picture! They try to guess the Secret Word using the other players’ drawings of only one feature of that word.
BAD DRAWINGS ARE PART OF THE FUN: Create loads of humorous moments with drawings so unforgettably bad they should be framed.
WIN AND LOSE AS A TEAM: In this cooperative game, players work together, trying to guess as many Secret Words correctly as possible to win. So there’s no stressful competition, just lots of laughs.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description: A mini-expansion with 2 new companions and 12 new mountains.

In a superstitious land, the Queen looks to the stars to know what future seasons will bring. The Royal Court turns to you, an expert in astronomy, to decipher the forecasts of the Queen's favourite Soothsayer. Find the Soothsayer's constellations in the night's sky and the Queen will surely reward you.

Starry Night is a paper-and-pencil game where player need to find 6 constellations that will lead your kingdom to prosperity. The more and brighter stars you find, the more reputation you gain.

On vacation? Is the road long? This Pocket Quiz is for you! An adventure that takes you from the city all the way to the lair of the Lazy Dragon! Together, answer sequences of questions, pass through all the stations of the Ultimate Adventure, and who knows—you might even win the Ultimate Merit Badge!

• Each round takes 5 minutes to play. The adventure takes hours to complete! • Question cards are compatible with any level • No setup required—just unfold the card and have fun! • A fully cooperative game so even the driver can play

THE PERFECT GAME FOR... • Entertaining all travelers in the car • Making your journey unforgettable

—description from the publisher

Asteroids are rich in rare metals, minerals, and volatile resources that make space a profitable venture. Build mines, defend your colony, and steal from your opponents in this space age gold rush!

—description from the designer

This set introduces species tiles from the Mexican Cloud Forest biome. This expansion is standalone but can also be incorporated into any other biome set of kCaloria.

kCaloria gives you the chance to build an expansive and intricate food chain that must overcome obstacles at every turn!

The objective of kCaloria is to play greater and more complex tiles in order to increase your final score. You will alternate placing tiles with your opponents and expand your respective food chains to dominate the table!

Note: Each player needs their own 60-tile set with KCalorie counters.

boardgame: Spy In The Bar (スパイ イン ザ バー)
description:
Find your spy partner at a bar and exchange secret information.
You are given the task of communicating with him through a "secret signal" that can only be made while holding a glass . Will you be able to complete the mission while the police are keeping a close eye on you?

Spy in the Bar is a slightly unusual party game for adults that can only be played while drinking alcohol or juice. If you find a partner who is showing off the same "secret signal" as you, give them a confidential information card. If you have managed to exchange cards with each other at the end of the game, you win! However, if the police find out that you are a pair, your victory is invalid...It's a game that is full of tension and laughter, where players communicate with each other using a "secret signal" with a glass in each hand.

boardgame: Midnight Cocktail (ミッドナイトカクテル)
description:
This is a pitch black bar that operates only by moonlight. There is a mysterious rumor that if you toast with someone who has the same cocktail as you, you will somehow be blessed with good fortune. However, there is almost no light inside the bar, so you can't see who is drinking what cocktail. So, can you guess the cocktails while interacting with the bar and toast with your "soulmate" who has the same cocktail as you ?

Midnight Cocktail is a light-hearted mystery game in which you aim to toast with someone who has the same cocktail card as you in a dark bar where you can't see the cards in your hand. Using "social cards" that have effects such as "peeking at the cards on either side of you" and "exchanging cards," you can deduce who has which cocktail, and when you find someone who has the same cocktail as you, propose a toast. It's a game that gets more exciting with each successful toast, and allows you to become friends with anyone.

boardgame: BARTENDOUBTS (Bartender Out: バーテンダウト)
description:
When the current bartender retires,a group of apprentice bartenders are gathered."We want the most skilled bartender among us to be the next master.

A truly top-notch bartender should be able to tell
if a cocktail is real without even tasting it ." Can you spot a real cocktail, earn the trust of the others, and become the new master?

Bartender Doubt is a thrilling and fun deception game between liars, where you must spot lies mixed into the cocktails and collect points as a bartender. Make a cocktail by combining the alcohol cards and ingredient cards in your hand, and hand it to someone saying, "Here's a gin and tonic." At this point, your opponent must figure out whether it is a real cocktail made according to the recipe, or a fake cocktail made with different ingredients. Overcome the fear of being eliminated if you make too many mistakes and aim for a high score.

In a far-away land, dwarven Brewmasters are competing to make the most delicious drinks for the upcoming Orctober BeerFest! Players take on the role of one of these Brewmasters, managing their brewery, farm and workers.

With just months until Orctober, players will have to buy and sell goods at the best prices, build and convert buildings, and hire more helpers. At the end of the game, the Brewmaster who was awarded the most Victory Points will win the Golden Stein at the BeerFest and win the game!

Fantasy Brewers is an action selection and resource management game. Players will take turns placing different sized workers at action spaces, queuing them up to await their turn to buy and sell goods, brew beer, distill whisky, extend the brewery and decorate for the festival!

—description from the designer

The Ground Between is a low-to-medium complexity Tactical-level wargame set on Western Front of World War 1. It covers infantry conflicts over no man’s land between France and Germany. Each game portrays a short engagement (playable in one hour) of Battle of Ypres around 1914 - 1915 in the Western Front.

The game features low counter count, one-sided, and large counters, which is ideal for Print & Play.

The combat system is specifically developed to reflect trench warfare: the emergence of machine guns that change modern warfare, the deadliness of close quarter combat, and the importance of troops’ morale as much as tactics in the battlefield.

—description from the designer

The Havoc Expansion, brings two new variants and a solo mode to XSCAPE.

The Chaos Weaver & Frost Bringer variants use a truncated board design that brings a new level of strategy to your game as they wreak havoc through the board.

The Frost Bringer moves in an unpredictable yet deliberate path, destroying everything in its wake and leaving a trail of ice behind. If the Frost Bringer rolls into you, you're out!

The Chaos Weaver weaves through the play area with seemingly erratic movements, leaving destruction in its wake. Anything caught in its path is eliminated.

In SOLO Mode, it’s you against the Reapers — secure the last remaining space to claim victory. Use the Ice Chips strategically to avoid getting trapped, and outmaneuver your unyielding foes.

The Havoc expansion is a limited release.

In T+ (T plus): Boardgame Anvil Adventure, players take on the role of blacksmiths competing in a lively festival to forge the most renowned weaponry. Using a thrilling push-your-luck mechanic, each blacksmith aims to enhance weapons through a process known as "ตีบวก" (enhancement), attempting to reach the legendary +10 level. Each success etches their name into blacksmithing history—literally—earning them reputation points symbolized by medals.

On each turn, a player may move their blacksmith pawn 0–2 spaces across a grid of equipment tiles, then attempt to enhance the weapon at their current location. Enhancing involves revealing cards from a personal deck—each “ติด” (success) raises the weapon's level by one, but revealing a “แตก” (failure) causes the entire attempt to fail, and might even reduce the weapon’s level if it's a critical failure. Special cards, such as "หยุด" (Stop) and penalty effects, add strategic risk to how far players push their luck.

Shared spaces on the board, like the sacred hot spring tile, allow players to refresh their deck or avoid the penalties of others' enhancements. The tension builds as players move tactically across the board, aiming to be present on the right weapons at the right time while dodging overpopulation and managing their hand.

The game ends when a player earns 7 medals by fully enhancing 7 weapons, thus being declared the most famous blacksmith of the festival. Alternatively, if the deck runs out or no more tiles can be placed, the player with the most medals wins. Colorful, fast-paced, and full of dramatic reversals, Anvil Adventure is a celebration of bold moves and calculated risks.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
An expansion to  18AL.

A seventh company - the Atlantic Coast Line - is added to the company rooster.

Some minor rule's changes are necessary for the new company.

Chase tricky animals, recover stolen loot & outsmart rivals in this fast-paced, push-your-luck board game for 1-5 players.

Bag & Snatch! is a fast-paced, strategic card game designed by David Spada and Nestore Mangone. Each match lasts around 15 minutes—perfect for quick, exciting sessions!
In this dynamic game, players use their cards to snatch tokens from the center of the table. These tokens are divided into four animal groups, each with a unique ability that can shift the tide of the game, disrupt opponents, and open new strategic opportunities.

Strategy is everything! Points are scored at the start of each turn based on how many tokens of the same type you’ve collected—the more you have, the more you earn! But beware: if a player depletes the central supply of an animal, everyone else must return their matching tokens, losing valuable points in the process.

To win, you’ll need to master the balance between hoarding and diversifying, knowing when to go all-in and when to play it safe. Will you take risks for a big payout or play the long game for steady gains? Bag & Snatch! is easy to learn but full of clever twists—dive in and outsmart your rivals!

—description from the publisher

In Dieren Dobbelen you put together your own zoo! Roll the dice to make the right combinations of animals. Fill your enclosures and try to get your zoo as full as possible to win.

—description form the publisher

In Dieren Dobbelen stel jij je eigen dierentuin samen! Rol met de dobbelstenen om de juiste combinaties van dieren te maken. Vul je verblijven en probeer je dierentuin zo vol mogelijk te krijgen om te winnen.

—description form the publisher (Dutch)

In Contubernium: Rome at War you take control of an 8-man squad of roman legionaries (the contubernium), as they are about to face an enemy army in battle. You will determine their fate by triggering dice-based abilities and playing action-cards to support your legionary in the front line. Each engagement is randomized through a number of selectable card decks (events, enemies, battlefields) to maximize replayability and the feeling of unpredictability on the battlefield.

Contubernium is not necessarily about defeating every enemy, it’s equally important to keep your contubernales alive throughout the battle. Rotate your frontline whenever exhaustion is straining your legionaries. Maintain your shield wall and face Rome's enemies head on. Legionaries, advance!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A bite-sized Poker deckbuilder that packs flavor, strategy, and dungeon-delving delight... served fresh in a lunchbox! Lead your rag-tag party of heroes into the Sporken Depths, where monsters are spicy, treasures are well-seasoned, and every hand you deal could be your last supper.  Whether solo or with friends, it’s always time to deal yourself a hero and chew through danger!

—description from the designer

Mana Blade is a stylish two-player fighting card game with deep and addictive and gameplay.

Pick from five unique playable characters, normal girls by day who can transform under the light of the moon into magical warriors known as "Enhancers".

Each Enhancer is easy to play, but can take tens or even hundreds of games to truly master. With five characters in the base game players can enjoy 10 highly nuanced match ups in true "fighting game" fashion.

How to Play:

The game is divided into rounds in a "first to two wins" format.  Turns are simultaneous, with players trying to out-read one another by choosing an attack from their hand to play face down and, if they wish, double down by spending Mana Crystals to power up their chosen attack for the turn.  Players reveal their cards.  Slash beats Breaker, Breaker beats Guard, and Guard beats Slash. If both players play the same type, the card with the fastest Hit Speed wins.  In the case of a tie, the player with the Advantage token wins.

Players can also play green Evade cards like "Duck" or "Sidestep", which win or lose completely depending on the attack angle of their opponent's attack (such as Straight, Vertical or Horizontal etc). If you guess right, you can counter attack, but guess wrong an you get hit for full damage.

The player with the winning card claims the Advantage Token (if they didn't already have it) and applies the hit icons listed on their winning card.  Some hit effects include:

Damage:  Reduce your opponent's life to 0 and you win!

Push: Push your opponent gradually towards the edge of the arena.  Push them out of the arena for a sudden death victory, or use the alternate side B arena to perform a "wall combo".

Stagger / Knockdown: Your opponent suffers a Hit Speed penalty next turn, allowing you to pressure them with slower, more powerful cards.

Launch: Smash your opponent off their feet and connect with a mid-air follow up attack. Push your luck by rolling the "combo die" in an attempt to combo into more powerful attack cards.

When you take damage you gain Mana Crystals.  These let to wager them for more damage next time you find yourself in an advantageous position. Gain 3 mana Crystals and you can spend them on a "Mana Burst", allowing you to ignore the results of combat and claim the Advantage token.

In Mana Blade you don't have to discard your cards, (although you can 'cycle' them out once a turn for the next card of your deck).  Instead try to build up an oppressive hand of attack cards and force your opponent to adapt or die.  Every card has a counter, and players only have 6 cards at once making it impossible to answer every situation perfectly.  Exploit your knowledge of the game, the cards, the characters, and your opponent's habits to claim ultimate victory.

—description from the publisher

Via Gladiola is a fast-paced ladder-climbing card game inspired by the legendary Nijmegen Four Days Marches. Over four rounds—one for each day—players try to finish the march with as few blisters as possible.

Each day starts at kilometer 0 and ends at 50. On your turn, you either "walk" by playing a higher card to the pile, or “stand still” and draw a penalty card. You can safely advance up to 5 km. Go 6–10 km ahead and you must draw 1 card; 11–15 km costs you 2. Anything more isn’t allowed—you’re pushing too hard.

On day 4 only, the 'gladiola' cards can make your life a little easier, as you Can play these to stand still without having to draw a penalty card. 
Finish your hand efficiently and avoid stacking up blister icons. After four days, the player with the fewest blisters wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 夺笋呀! (Panda Battle: Grab bamboo shoots)
description:
Thunder is about to strike! The bamboo shoots in the precious bamboo forest will be devastated. As the chiefs of various panda villages, players compete with each other for bamboo shoots and fight against thunder to protect them and prevent evil spirits from infiltrating.

Luck or clever construction? There are so many combinations waiting for you to discover!
Who is the final king of bamboo shoots?

What are you doing in the game?
Players dispatch various pandas to the bamboo forest area to collect bamboo shoots. When they move back to the village area, they immediately harvest a random bamboo shoot. Then place it on any field on the village board. When all the bamboo shoots are harvested, the game enters the Thunder phase.
Victory objective: All players' bamboo shoots will be destroyed by several thunderstorms, and the player with the most remaining will win.
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
游戏正在 新物集 众筹中。喜欢可以去点感兴趣！
背景：天雷即将来袭！竹林宝地的竹笋，将受到灭顶之灾。玩家作为各个熊猫村的族长，为保护竹笋，和防备邪祟混入其中，开始互相争夺竹笋，并对抗天雷。

运气还是巧妙构筑？超多组合等你发掘！
谁才是最后的夺笋之王？
玩家派遣各种熊猫，到竹林区采集竹笋，他们移动回村庄区时，立即收获1个随机的竹笋。然后放在村庄板的任意田块上。当所有竹笋全部被取完，游戏进入天雷阶段。

胜利目标：所有玩家的竹笋，经过数道天雷的破坏后，剩余最多的玩家获胜。

Expand your horizons!

The promotional card “Location 7” for the game Córdoba adds a new dynamic to the line of Locations right from the start of the game, and also changes how the game ends in case of a tie.

boardgame: Lux Nova: Master of Stained Glass
description:
The Master of Stained Glass expansion takes the competition in Lux Nova to a whole new level, giving players the chance to earn an exclusive trophy that could be the key to victory.

But only a true master will manage to hold onto it until the end of the game and secure a crucial advantage at the decisive moment.

The Master of Stained Glass cards feature unique illustrations and are distributed separately, although their rules are identical.

ムッジーナ！ (A Mujina) is a speed game from Japan. Players try to identify the correct animal faces fastest.

The game has a set of tiles and a set of matching player cards. Each show the faces of Tanuki, but the tiles are in colour and the cards are only in black ink. As everybody knows, Tanuki are animals native to Japan, but they are also special shape-shifting creatures. Here, they are trying to change into other animals, like pandas, tigers or bears.

The tiles are dealt out face up on the table in a mixed up way. The players are dealt 4 hand cards, and must grab the four tiles correctly matching their hand cards first.

But it is tricky, as the differences are quite small. Are the ears round or pointed, are there whiskers, is there a leaf on their head?? Check the eyes, check the nose, every feature must match.

On the back of each card and tile is a set of simple markers. If the two are matched, then the lines will match up exactly. This is a very fast way to show whether the right ones have been picked or not.

Players score scores for being first, and lose points for cards left in hand. After a few rounds, most points wins.
use summary

The players advance on the board and answer trivia questions about german culture, society and history. If answered correctly, the card is kept; whoever collects 17 cards the fastest, wins the game.

Nacheinander ziehen die Spieler auf dem Spielplan weiter und beantworten Fragen zur deutschen Kultur, Gesellschaft und Geschichte. Wird die Frage richtig beantwortet, behält der Spieler die Karte; wer zuerst 17 Karten sammelt, gewinnt das Spiel.

Live your best life in Adulthood, now with Pets! The Pet Projects expansion refreshes the hit life-sim game Adulthood. This shuffle-in set of over 40 new cards includes Pets, new Careers, Partners, Life Events, Values, and Solo Achievements.

Pets provide an alternative scoring path for the game; as long as you're able to invest Care in your pets, they'll pay off with Happiness points and other abilities. There’s also an all-new set of Careers with infinite activations, allowing you to activate those cards as many times as you have the time and energy to do so!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Broadland: An English River Cruise Game

description:
A group therapy ritual where 1-4 players summon and seal away their own personal demons. This fast-paced, simultaneous-action drafting-style game has each player populating their own summoning circle in a tableau-style puzzler, aiming to form complete seals and conquer their demons. Hefty and colossal Demons of increasingly larger card sizes can be summoned and sealed once their requirements are met, and each one gives all players unique benefits that can change the way you play the game. Courage cards gained over the course of play grant player-specific abilities, sins are built up and spent as currency to take bonus actions, seals are cracked and replaced as the game progresses. Build an engine, choose your path to victory and score the most over three rounds to show your demons who's boss.

An optional upgrade pack adds the option to play with 5 or 6 players, and all of the components will fit into the base game box!

—description from the designer

Fisketuren is a lighthearted and family-friendly card game full of luck, laughter, and underwater mischief.
Players take turns asking each other for specific fish types, trying to collect complete sets — while dodging old rubber boots and greedy sea crabs.

Inspired by classic "Go Fish" gameplay but with a twist, Fisketuren adds unique action cards, playful illustrations, and a pinch of strategy.
Easy to learn and quick to play, it's perfect for 2–5 players on a fishing trip around the table.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: そこまで絞るにはねむれない夜もあっただろ(You must have had sleepless nights to tighten your body so much)
description:
そこまで絞るにはねむれない夜もあっただろ ("You must have had sleepless nights to tighten your body so much") is a card game in which you shout at the macho of your heart at a bodybuilding competition.

It's no surprise that every player wants to root for Macho, but in this game, the player who can continue to support Macho longer than anyone else wins.

18Hedjaz is a strategic railroad game set in the Ottoman Empire in the late 19th century. It centers on the construction of the Hedjaz railroad from Damascus to Medina - a project with religious, military and geopolitical significance. Players take on the roles of investors and railroad directors, build routes, trade in shares and influence the development of the region. But resistance, for example from Lawrence of Arabia, threatens progress.

The game is based on the mechanics of the 18xx series:

     Switch between stock market rounds (share trading, dividends) and operating rounds (train operation, line construction).
     New elements: Hedjaz phase (route development in the Ottoman Empire) and Lawrence phase (sabotage, loss of income).


Special features:

     Unique faction abilities (e.g. money bonuses, espionage) that create asymmetrical starting conditions.
     Private companies that are auctioned off and offer tactical advantages.


18Hedjaz combines historical themes and economic strategy. It offers high replayability through diverse combinations of abilities, companies and geopolitical events. Whoever controls the railroad shapes the future of the Middle East - but must be prepared for disruptions and power games.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Journeys Afar, where infinite worlds filled with boundless possibilities are connected by a single road: the Ketsueki. Along this path, brave heroes venture forth, each journeying to distant, mysterious lands. On their travels, they explore sites of great importance, acquire powerful items, make offerings to benevolent spirits, sharpen their skills, battle fearsome ronin, and form lasting bonds. But in the end, only one will be remembered as a legend.

Journeys Afar: The Ketsueki is a sandbox game of epic adventure, where the journey matters more than the destination. Each player is a unique hero from a distinct world. Your world defines everything in the game: encounters, quests, threats, upgrades, and special rules that reshape every session into a new experience. As heroes travel across The Ketsueki, they impact the worlds they visit and discover who they truly are.

The game includes six fully-realized worlds:

    Memori, the Garden of Sleep — where dreams are born and the Ancients call home
    Shards of Hansha —  where a society of incredible artists devote their lives to crafting starglass
    Horobi, Twilight Unending — where the sun never rises, and darkness spreads unchecked
    Kakurega, Home to Beasts — where skilled Hunters track monstrous prey
    The Island Nation of Shima — where the Great Houses vie for the Sky Throne
    Unmei-Unmei, the Dancing Parade — where the flip of a coin can change fate itself
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Caravanserai: Anahita Mini Expansion, Solo Mode
description:
Everyday life in the caravanserai is quiet, but one day everything changes. Every week, the arbab, the prestigious owner of the place, arrives to inspect everything. But for the stable boys, this day is more about another visit: it's usually the day when Anahita, the arbab's beautiful daughter, joins her father. The little lady enjoys making friends with the boys, who don't hesitate to include her in their latest pranks.

—description from the publisher (translated)

This third expansion to Caravanserai brings two new characters into the caravanserai: the arbab and Anahita. The arbab works to make your life harder but Anahita is here to help you. The two appear in tandem, balancing out each other. The main goal of the game remains unchanged, but victory conditions can now be triggered by a position that changes from one game to another and not by reaching a set score.
The arbab, during his sojourn in the serai limits player options, but his charming daughter offsets this by using her special "favour buttons".
This expansion brings a significant change to game mechanics, so primarily it is recommended for seasoned "Caravanserai" players.
"Anahita" is compatible with the base game of "Caravanserai", and with both the "Dervish" and "Opium" expansions. Furthermore, Caravanserai is now playable in solo mode too.

People call those who can change the scales of value "Kamisama". Through wealth, faith, and fame; the Kamisama compete to become the most worshiped god.

Players take on the role of one of 5 gods. Generate the resource "mana" by playing ritual cards, which is then spent to build cities.

Ritual cards have powerful effects that can change the cost or point values of these cities, or even block players from building a city type. This is key to victory, since each god has a build bonus.

boardgame: Creature Caravan: Krax Trader Creature Promo Card
description:
Originally available during the The Secret Cabal Gaming Podcast is Outta Control in 2024 Kickstarter campaign.

A creature card.

Name: Krax Trader
Type: Folk, Deadly, Nocturnal
VP Banner: 1 per Deadly
Cost: 3 Bread 1 Coin
Ability: At any time, you may trade 2 Bread for 1 Card or 2 Cards for 1 Bag

Gem Jam is a sweet and strategic token placement game, where you’ll invite quirky friends to your table and share delicious Iced Gem Biscuits and Cookies to build lasting friendships.

Over 6 colorful rounds, players will draft friends, collect Iced Gem Biscuits, and place them carefully into lunch trays to fulfill pattern based cravings. Every friend has a unique taste, and satisfying their preferences will earn you Friendship Points. But watch out, once a treat is placed, it’s there to stay!

With shared objectives, spatial pattern matching, and clever token placement, Gem Jam serves up a light yet delightfully puzzly experience for 2–4 players.

Whether you're maximizing patterns or sneaking in bonus points with cookies, every round is a chance to grow your social circle and your score!

Will you become the most beloved host at the table?

—description from the designer

Fun card game for children: In the Pundo Garden, players collect pandas, protect them from thieves and slide down with them. Perfect for children aged 3 and up as a first board game.
Playful learning: promotes emotional development, social interaction and rule awareness. Children learn to win and lose through exciting game mechanics.
Simple game principle: draw cards, steal pandas, slide down or protect your animals with a caregiver card! Quick to understand, ideal as a first card game for toddlers.
Family game & gift idea: perfect for game evenings together. Compact & fun on the go. A great gift for Christmas, birthdays or as a souvenir.
FAVORITE TOYS FOR CHILDREN: Delights fans of Memory, Exploding Kittens & Throw Burrito. Ideal for 2-4 players, with cute design and lots of fun for young and old

—description from the designer

November 1983 — After practicing all year, it's time for the big roller disco competition. The best jam skaters from around the world have gathered with their finest skate gear under the glare of the mirrorball. Synth-pop booms from the speakers. Who will hit the right rolls and land the right moves to be crowned the champion of the roller disco?

Roller Disco is a fast-paced card game in which you play sequences of skate cards around a mirrorball with the objective of shedding your cards first. Combining ladder-climbing mechanisms with unique route-building, you must choose if you are "Movin' on up" or "Gettin' down" when you choose card ranks to use in your sequence. Wild jam cards will help improve your sequence and even change your route direction!

Roller Disco is quick to learn, but clever hand management is needed to carefully plan your routes and skate your way to victory.

—description from the designer

Fast response game: Who reacts the fastest? Collect cards by recognising the right ingredients and acting in a flash! Perfect for children and adults.

EASY GAME PRINCIPLE: Meet the three conditions of action – Pişti, Super Waffle or Waffle Party – and win! An ideal starter game for children aged 4 and up.

Promotes cognitive skills: Trains attention, reflexes & social interaction. Ideal as a memory alternative & for fans of fast card games.

PERFECT FOR GAME NIGHTS: Compact & entertaining for on the go or at home. For 2-4 players, suitable for families, parties & as a gift for children & adults.

Favourite toy for all: whether as a Christmas gift, souvenir or as an addition to card game collections – Waffle Party provides fun and excitement!

—description from the designer

Use our modern vices to fight your inner demons and advance your chance of winning.

Introducing a new card type for the game Side Effects — "Fun Drugs".  These new "Fun Drug" cards go on your Psyche just like other drugs, but they don't treat anything. Instead, they live to the side of your Disorders and aid you with special advantages throughout the game. But watch out! All Fun Drugs contain side effects.

The Legal Vice Card Pack comes with 3 of the world's most popular Fun Drugs: Coffee, Tobacco, and Alcohol.

—description from the publisher

A strategic card game for 2–6 players where each player takes on the role of a Pet, striving to be the last one standing amidst relentless enemy attacks.

This game is an original creation by the Canvas Ranger community, proudly developed in Indonesia. While it may draw inspiration from familiar mechanics, all core elements—rules, gameplay dynamics, and card designs—have been uniquely reimagined, resulting in a fresh and distinctive experience within the world of card games.

One day, all the stars vanished from the world.
Children, chosen by Astraios bring the light back into the world.

Players take turns placing a tile one at a time to connect the stars into an enclosed shape to create a nebula.  Players now work with 3 tiles in their hand, and all information is open to all players.  At the end of the game players only score points for stars in their constellations.

Astraios: Starlight is a new glow-in-the-dark edition with changes to the gameplay strategy.  Play competitively up to 3 players.  With 4 players, play in 2 teams to control the sky.

—description from the publisher

Secretly fart without the other players getting wind of it in this hilarious full-throttle game! Just play a matching animal to get rid of your cards, but be careful! Some are much sketchier than others. If someone smells a fart and it turns out to be you, it can cause a real stink. Will you be crowned Queen of the Farts or will you be left with a bad taste in your mouth?

—description from the back of the box

Space, the final frontier. Let’s see what’s out there! 

With a theme of Space Exploration, Quantum Tricks supports 3-5 players and plays similarly to many other trick-taking games, making it simple to learn. But there are a few twists which make it very difficult to master. Instead of just one Trick, you will be playing always three Tricks at any one time, requiring considerable hand management Strategy.

And, oh yes, you’re not actually trying to win the most Tricks.
Intrigued?

How to play:

In Quantum Trick, you will be playing 3 tricks simultaneously! The lead player is the player to the left of the dealer, and play continues clockwise around the table until every player has played all their cards. On your turn, you must play a single card next to your player identifier on one of the three Trick markers.

Your options are:

     Play into an existing Trick, in which case you must follow the Suit of the Trick. You may play a card to a Trick into which you have previously played.
     Start a new Trick in a currently empty slot. This will be either a new slot for the Round or one that previously contained a Trick that has now been resolved (see clear the spaceways). When starting a new Trick, you cannot play the same Suit as another Trick currently in play.


End of the game
The standard game lasts for a number of rounds equal to the number of players. After each round, the player with the most tricks receives negative points equal to the number of tricks they won, while all other players score positive points based on the number of tricks they collected. However, if there is a player with exactly 5 tricks they gain 5 VP and only that player will score points.
artists: United States National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

description:
Did you know the CIA had a direct influence on implementing ditactorships all over Latin America?
What happened in Latin America during the Cold War? Who where the main characters, events and how can you rewrite history.

Almost as a 'side-quest' of the acclaimed Twilight Struggle, Imperialismo: América puts one player in control of the United States aiming to 'free the Latin America from Communism', and facing another player in control of the Latin America, 'fighthing against the North-American imperialism'.

Dispute the influence on each of the 5 Blocks, playing specific historical cards, to gain points each round.
Activate your historical leaders, different combinations on each match, to improve your odds and manipulate the game on your favour.
And focus on creating Revolutions for Latin America or implementing Dictatorships in favour of the US to speed your your scoring. First player to 25 points win!

—description from the publisher

Become the architect of the 1889 World's Fair!
Like Gustave Eiffel or Ferdinand Dutert, step into the shoes of a visionary and design the iconic attraction of this global event. Relive the construction of this historic World's Fair, where Adolphe Alphand, its organizer, entrusts you with a major mission: to create the most spectacular venues and attractions in celebration of the centennial of the French Revolution.
It is in this very context that the Eiffel Tower is born, becoming the iconic symbol we know today. ?
A reedition of Roll Through the Ages, a game by Matt Leacock, the renowned designer of Pandemic.

The game is played over several rounds. In each round, all players take one turn. Once every player has completed their turn, the round ends and a new one begins.
On their turn, a player may perform all of the following actions, if possible (and/or required by the dice roll):


     Roll the dice, then collect resources and worker wages
     Pay the workers and resolve any disaster effects
     Recruit workers and/or build monuments
     Purchase one (and only one) development
     Discard excess resources (beyond six) and pass the dice


—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: Roll Through the Ages (Matt Leacock)

description:
Evergreen – Cherry Blossoms and Bamboo is a set of 2 modular expansions for Evergreen.  This set includes Cherry Trees, which increase the fertility of your Biomes, and Bamboos, which allow you to grow big Forests very quickly. Discover new forest-planning strategies to get the most points out of them!

—description from the publisher

Keyflower: Boat 8 is a village tile for the game Keyflower available at Spiel 2025.

Side A: Boat 8a - When upgrading a tile, gain a gold on the upgraded tile.

Side B: Boat 8b - Take one free action in your village at the end of summer, autumn and winter as if you have put a keyple (meeple) on it.  It does not matter if the tile is occupied.

—description from the publisher

In Bunny Burrows, you'll be competing to get to the other side of the burrow the fastest! Run to the other edge of the board while strategically blocking your fellow rabbits with rocks.

The intersecting paths give you numerous options to continue through the burrow. Each roll of the dice will allow you to move forward, backward, or place/remove a rock on the board to trip up your opponents!

—description from the publisher

Totemix is a flip-and-write strategy game with a unique bidding twist, set in the mystical White City deep in the jungle. The Monkey God is angry, and only the tallest carved totem can earn forgiveness. Players take on the role of village leaders trying to gather resources and build the most impressive totem to save their people.

Each round, players reveal a bidding card showing available resources (like stone, crystal, gold, ruby, or smoke). Then, they secretly bid a number of crystals from their hand. All players receive a shared reward from the card, and bidding more unlocks better materials. After each round, players mark collected resources on their dry-erase player board and use them to construct totem pieces.

The game is played over a series of rounds until one player builds a 9-level totem. In the advanced game mode, players can also upgrade their resource sources to improve future rewards, adding an engine-building layer.

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
The Star Bridge Commission has hired you to complete an exploration of the known solar system, six planets in all.

Players will be using color theory to explore the planets. The three primary planets correspond to Red, Yellow, and Blue which match the dice. Players can use the dice individually or pair the dice to create secondary colors: Purple, Orange, and Green.

Each planet will only allow certain dice ships to land though so exploring the planets can be tricky. Additionally, Star Bridges have to be created to allow that exploration. In order to explore the player will need to create those bridges with the dice matching planets they have been able to explore. The unused dice will create demand at a secondary planet or at two primary planets.

Players will play simultaneously utilizing the dice rolled. Clever selection of the dice will grand the players greater exploration possibilities. With the game only lasting 25 turns, each selection of the dice will be more and more tactical.

—description from the designer

Shadow Puppets is the very first Bolster Expansion for Doomlings — introducing 40 new cards and four haunting new species of Doomlings: Deeplings, Glitterlings, Moonlings, and Fuzelings.

☼ Deeplings Expansion (11 New Cards) - From somewhere behind the velvet curtain, the Deeplings emerge. These mysterious mischief-makers use the Suppress mechanic to conceal your cards—and your motives.

☼ Glitterlings Expansion (11 New Cards) - Forgotten toys with fractured smiles and glitter in their gears, the Glitterlings revel in randomness. Will you risk the fate of your Doomlings species on the draw?

☼ Moonlings Expansion (11 New Cards) - The Moonlings speak in riddles and dance in shadows. They’re tricksters, masters of blocking and hand-locking your rivals into silence. Lower your Gene Pool to unlock their powers—but beware: what they offer comes at a price.

☼ Fuzelings Expansion (4 New Cards) - Fuzelings combine the art and mechanics of Deeplings, Glitterlings, and Moonlings. They can help you get bigger bonuses from Drop of Life effects and trigger new combos!

     2 Age cards
     1 Catastrophe card
     1 Mystery Holofoil Collectible Card from the Shadow Puppets Collection
     5 divider cards


—description from the publisher (card counts were corrected)

boardgame: Dark Venture: Battle of the Ancients – Ghosts and Robots
description:
The Ghosts & Robots expansion contains two factions.

The Ghosts are hidden to all but their controlling player. They move in secret on a magnetic mini-map behind a screen and are only visible when they share a space with another unit. They move more quickly than other units, can pass through the walls of buildings, and have the power to possess and control units of other factions!

The Robots gather materials that are used to construct new bots and drones. Materials are also used to purchase hardware upgrades: new weapon-enabled limbs, processors, and advanced shielding. They can also research new technologies to increase their defensive and offensive options: cloaking tech, nuclear cores, napalm, and landmines.

The map can be used to link the map from the Core Game to the Forgone Castle map to create an epic 3-board map! With such a large play area, players will need to move their units further on each turn, so each faction will be provided with means to do so: the Dhargon have a raft to move along the river running from one edge of the Epic map to the other, the Orpal can create transport portals, the Varpen can mutate wings, and the Eggmen will be provided with bicycles.

The Vault is an autumn promotional tile for the game Keyflower, being released at Spiel 2025.

The Vault scores points for gold in a similar way to the way that the Barn (any resource), the Blacksmith (iron), the Stone Yard (stone) and the Timber Yard (wood) score points for their respective resources.

Points are scored for gold that is on the Vault tile at the end of winter.  3 points per gold on the tile if the tile has not been upgraded.  4 points if the tile has been upgraded.  The Vault upgrade cost is any two skill tiles.

—description from the publisher

Heir of Egypt is a fast-paced game pitting 2-6 players against one another in a battle of reflex and memory. Steal cards by slapping them from your opponents or use your more powerful cards to take them by force. The game ends when one player controls them all.

From two wonderfully imaginative or intriguing situations, the Master of Awesomeness crafts a fun and thought-provoking “what would you choose?” dilemma. The other players are then faced with a tough decision as they must each secretly select one of the two options presented. After everyone has made their choice, the answers are revealed. Players score a point if they chose the same option as the majority, encouraging them to think strategically about what others might pick. Meanwhile, the Master of Awesomeness earns points based on how well they managed to divide the group, making it a clever challenge to create dilemmas that are truly split-worthy. It’s a game of empathy, psychology, and a bit of chaos!

—description from the publisher

From two hilariously awful scenarios, the Master of Misery presents a “shitty or shittier” dilemma that no one wants to face—but everyone has to choose. The other players must each secretly pick the lesser of two evils. Once all choices are in, the results are revealed. Players score a point if they went with the majority, rewarding those who can think like the crowd (even if the crowd has terrible taste). Meanwhile, the Master of Misery earns points for splitting opinions, making it their job to craft dilemmas so dreadful they're perfectly divisive. It’s a game of grim choices, twisted logic, and uncomfortable laughs.

—description from the publisher

The City Of Zee Welcomes You!
You are one of the Z-Bots, who control Zee Cities' Nuclear Reactor. When not working, Z Bots play a fun game on the Reactors' conveyor belts, taking turns running belts to bump the other Z Bots oﬀ the conveyor belts for points. What Fun!!!

—description from the back of the box

Zee Basic Description:
Players will build a game environment, made of sets of cards, which are three belt cards long, called "Conveyors". Each belt card in a conveyor has one of three symbols filled with one of three colors.
Once built players put their playing piece (Z Bot/ Zeeple) on an empty rear belt.  Throughout the game players use Control cards, match some of the belt cards shapes/colors so as to move the playing pieces fully forward (off a conveyor), setting them aside for points, which the players decided on earlier or move themselves/teammate trying to avoid them being set aside.  
The above is a description is for 2=6 player games. Solo is similar.

—description from the designer

Captain's Crew is a tense, fast-paced, and easy-to-learn game with a pirate theme.

The game consists of 10 rounds, each driven by unique combat mechanisms. In every round, players decide whether to engage in battle with their pirates or strategically retreat. At the heart of the game is the clever use of pirate abilities and dice values that affect both opponents and the flow of the game.

The winner is the first player to successfully claim enough pirates and ships.

—description from the designer

Festive Foliage is a Christmas themed expansion to Botany. it adds a Festive Specimen deck, along with new items, characters, and a Christmas Tree garden feature.

Partake in the merriment and spread the joy of the season!

Tango with Tiles in a Matching Frenzy!

Tile Tango is a fast-paced game for up to 4 players where players speed, pattern recognition and spatial orientation skills are challenged.  Players compete to match their set of 9 tiles to the 3x3 grid shown on the flipped match card.  With a mix of colors and patterns, players must race against each other to be the first to correctly orient their tiles and slap the card to claim the card's points.

Has 3 different card difficulties.

A 1 or 2 player tennis board game that utilises a moving laser as the ball

You can select 1 or 2 player mode and varying degrees of speed

The game is then one of dexterity - you need to move your racquet to “hit the ball” by crossing the path of the laser - this then sends the laser back to the opposing player to try and do the same

If you miss the shot you lose the point as in regular tennis

Speed of the ball and length of game can be varied

In single player mode you are playing against a computer player

One can think of this as the electronic version of the dexterity game “Tennis Masters”.

Lie Hunt: Secret Teller is a conversation-driven betting game that encourages players to share personal stories from their own lives.
Each player selects a topic card and writes the title of a real (or fictional) anecdote they have.
As the stories are shared, players place bets on whether they believe each story is true or a lie.
Correct guesses earn players additional chips equal to their bet, while incorrect bets result in losing the chips wagered.

Using any kind of dice, 2 or more players will clash over a series of Fights to boost their Ego and prove they are the most powerful Psychic in the neighborhood.

Square Up- select dice and roll to go first.
