Fume- secretly roll and place your in preparation for battle.
Fight!- Compare individual dice values, add up totals for free removals, or retreat from the round.
Win- Keep Fuming and Fighting! till one player reaches maximum Ego!

This game was originally created to be printed inside a folded business card.

—description from the designer

When the sheep step out of line...

This game with the charming design looks absolutely harmless, but “Schaf nachgedacht” has it all. The aim is to form rows, always at least 3 sheep together with your own shepherd. This is not easy, as the shepherd may always move, but his sheep can only be moved if the shepherd is able to see them.

Once a valid row has been completed, a white sheep is exchanged for a colored one. As the sheep are constantly moving out of line due to the other player's actions, it is important to keep an overview.

—description from the designer

Days are getting longer and hormones are running high. These rabbits only have one thing on their minds: babies!

In this chaotic card game, you have to produce the most baby rabbits. Each round, you throw your rabbit cards into the fray. Anyone who misses the burrow has to settle for collecting help cards for the next round.

When you meet in the rabbit burrow, the fun begins:
Each male and female pair produces babies. To do this, each player draws a baby card and adds it to their nursery.
But watch out, the unlucky rabbits may have collected a few conkers in the last round and can use them to chase away their competitors!

A puzzle game on a mysterious archaeological discovery.

In this adventure, you dive into the mystery of a controversial relic that has haunted both historians and the Vatican elite: the Lost Gospel.

In the 1910s, a famous archaeologist seemed to have found traces of it in Cyprus—but his discoveries have never resurfaced...

Now it's your turn to continue the investigation
It's 1981. You’re following the trail of the Lost Gospel through archival documents, working on behalf of the Mediterranean Society of Crypto-Archaeology.

But are you truly ready to rewrite history?

In Rock Swap Flip-Flop you play the classic game of Rock Paper Scissors, but the hand shapes change every time! Compete to win a round of rock paper scissors and move the fingers around the hand boards to creature the shapes that match your cards. Complete a set of three cards to win a trophy. First to three trophies wins! Be careful though—if you make the wrong hand shape you're knocked out for that round!

—description from the publisher

Those who dedicate themselves to a life of adventure have only one goal: to become disgustingly rich quickly – and without taking any risks, please!
In Backstab Heroes, you become part of a ‘hero group’ that loves nothing more than pushing each other to the front line so that someone else can keep the monsters busy. While the others take the hits, you lurk in the shadows, waiting for the big loot.
But beware: if no one is brave enough to defeat the monster, the whole group will come away empty-handed. Fame and glory? Forget it – only gold counts!

Backstab Heroes is a chaotic and comical card game for 3–6 players in which anti-heroes try to outsmart each other to grab treasures – without fighting, of course. Fame is worthless; only gold counts. All players choose their actions simultaneously, keeping the game dynamic and unpredictable. Those who find the right balance between cooperation and betrayal will go home with the biggest loot at the end!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Wer sich dem Leben als Abenteurer verschreibt, hat nur ein Ziel: schnell widerwärtig reich werden – und das bitte ohne jedes Risiko!

In Backstab Heroes wirst du Teil einer „Heldengruppe“, die nichts mehr liebt, als sich gegenseitig in die vorderste Reihe zu schubsen, damit jemand anderes die Monster beschäftigt. Während die anderen einstecken, lauerst du in den Schatten auf fette Beute. 
Doch aufgepasst: Wenn niemand mutig genug ist, das Monster zu besiegen, geht die ganze Gruppe leer aus. Ruhm und Ehre? Vergiss es – nur Gold zählt!

Backstab Heroes ist ein chaotisch-komisches Kartenspiel für 3–6 Spieler, in dem Anti-Helden sich gegenseitig austricksen, um Schätze abzugreifen – natürlich möglichst ohne selbst zu kämpfen. Ruhm bringt nichts, nur Gold zählt. Alle Spieler wählen gleichzeitig ihre Aktion, wodurch das Spiel dynamisch und unberechenbar bleibt. Wer das richtige Maß zwischen Kooperation und Verrat findet, geht am Ende mit der dicksten Beute nach Hause!

—description from the publisher

Mad Rooks is a two-player game which uses a 8x8 checkerboard, 32 red checkers and 32 blue checkers.

The checkers function like Chess rooks with two restrictions.  On your turn you must either kill, or move into position to kill. If a checker is already in position to kill, it can only be used to kill. Killing is not mandatory. I.e. if you can move an unengaged checker to engage the enemy, you can do that instead.

Mad Rooks is a game of annihilation.  I.e., you must kill all enemy checkers to win.

Die is a game of self-annihilation. It's a two-player game played on a hexagonal board of any size, initially empty. The two players, Red and Blue, take turns placing their own stones onto unoccupied cells on the board, one stone per turn, starting with Red. Sometimes friendly or enemy stones are removed immediately following a
placement.

Jokerball is a luck infused soccer game. A hexagonal board of side length 6 is set up, with 11 red men on Red's side, 11 blue men on Blue's side, and a green ball in the center. Red and Blue take turns moving their own men and the ball. If the ball exits the board through your far side, on either player's turn, you win.

boardgame: Mission Membrane: Sugar into the Bloodstream
description:
In Mission Membrane, players take on the role of microscopic helpers inside the human body, guiding glucose molecules from the digestive system into the bloodstream. Mira and Carlos are preparing for a 10K run, and they need energy—fast! Your mission is to ensure that the glucose from their pre-race banana reaches their cells in time.

Gameplay
This cooperative educational game simulates the biological process of nutrient absorption through cell membranes. Players must work together to:
Use membrane proteins strategically
Manage ion gradients (sodium, potassium)
Transport glucose molecules from the intestine into the blood
Each turn involves drawing and playing cards to activate specific transport mechanisms. The game includes a tutorial mode for easy onboarding and offers two difficulty levels: with or without "Backup" cards that simplify gameplay.

Educational Value
Mission Membrane is ideal for classrooms or science enthusiasts. It introduces key biological concepts such as:
Active and passive transport
Membrane proteins
Concentration gradients

Win Condition
The game ends when all glucose molecules have either been successfully transported into the bloodstream or removed from play. The more glucose you deliver, the better your team’s performance!

—description from the publisher

The lights are on, the audience is buzzing, and the game is about to begin! As the Host, you’ll give clues without saying a single word. As a Guesser, your mission is to crack the Secret Adjectives before your rivals. Who will be the master of deduction in Pitaco?

Pitaco is played over a number of rounds equal to the number of players. Each round, one player becomes the Host while the others are Guessers. The Host is the only one who knows the round’s Secret Adjectives and gives clues by placing Intensity Markers—no talking allowed. When one or more Guessers nail both Secret Adjectives, the Host and every successful Guesser score points, and the earlier the guess, the bigger the reward. After every player has taken a turn as Host, the game ends and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

You’re about to face an unpredictable situation, but your suitcase fits only four items. In Mala e Cuia, the challenge is to pick the right objects for the destination on your card and make the other players guess your choices.

Play cooperatively, with the group trying to identify each player’s items, or competitively, scoring points for choosing objects that match what the others picked.

Each Situation card shows a quirky challenge with a general scenario and a bonus task. Select three items for the general scenario and one for the bonus. The more guesses the group gets right, the more points you earn!

—description from the publisher

A sensual and passionate adventure for two

A fun-filled journey is in store for you as you travel the exciting Hearts Are Wild Game Board.

During play, experience the intrigue and delight presented by the instructions on the Cupid Cards.

Follow you hearts desire...let your responses be spontaneous, bold and daring.

The Lover's Option cards offer tricky challenges on the road to romance, throwing alluring curves which add to the fascination of play.

The anticipation heats up and the action becomes wild as the players encounter the provocative Pure Pleasure cards as well as landing on such stimulating spaces as Take A Drink, Sweet Kisses, or Massage Oil (made even sweeter with the enclosed flavored oils).

The game is far from over, even upon entering the finish space, when accumulated Hearts Are Wild coupons can be redeemed for any period of time as agreed by you and your partner.

The wild ride never has to end!
A new and exciting outcome every time you play!

Your hearts will "go wild" for each other!

We're dog lovers, and we're playing to get the pack of dogs free of fleas before the other players. The number of dogs needed to win depends on the number of players.

By activating cards, we'll try to prevent our opponents from achieving the objective first, while also working toward it ourselves.

There's a lot of interaction, and amidst the chaos, no dog will be safe until the end of the game.

Armed Conflict is a war-themed strategy game that lets you command military units of different type and size in a conflict between two alliances, the Lions and the Eagles. The main focus is on the military strategy and tactics. The game is not based on any particular conflict or historical event, but the timeframe is loosely based on modern warfare, any time after WW2.

Armed Conflict offers a sandbox-like environment for testing out military strategies and tactics, giving maximum replayability. The game comes with a double sided board, with two different maps. Each of the maps include hexagonal areas of different terrain, such as fields, forest and hills, which all give different benefits in the battle, based on real world military science.

The complexity in the game is well balanced to suit both casual board gamers as well as seasoned wargamers. The key features of the different military units are highlighted to maintain realism, while keeping the game mechanics simple enough for a thrilling pace.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Expand the basic Cities game with a new achievement board. That includes the main attractions of the cities of Budapest and Kecskemét for even more variety.

Limited Hungarian promo.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This expansion adds five cards to the game. They make the game more engaging, but be warned, their use has certain consequences. There are 5 special cards for single use.
0- You can stop immediately after the first card.

+ -1- increase or decrease the value of a card.

Third Card- You can draw a third card.
Color joker- Change the color of a number card.
minus- calculate instead of plus minus
Using these cards generates negative points. Chose wisely.

Down Range is a wargame developed by a Marine Captain to train military tactics.

Players control units like "USMC M2 gun team" and "Substandard combatant" and "M1 Abrams" in a rule set that aims to be simple for non-gamers to pick up.

On your turn, each of your units gets one movement and one action, where different units roll different dice to determine attack success.

DR is a flexible framework for players to practice modern combat scenarios, including rules for radio communication.  It includes units for USMC, US Army, Ukrainian, Canadian, PLANMC, and Russian military troops.

Kobolds and Miners is a simple one page roll and write game where you command a troup of kobolds to excavate the treasures and gold hidden in the mines in the old western town of Tumbledust.

Your objective is to store the most cash in bank and spend gold to hire new kobolds to speed up your progress. You also need to manage cargo on wagons and play it smart in the saloon bar.

You need 3 D6 to play. The game goes over 14 rounds, the player with the most cash wins.

—description from the designer

A brainburner game with simple rules, but a greater challenge.
As a 2-person battle always a challenge. 
Multiflex has a high replay value, a new game mechanic, driven by 100 Multiflex tiles/cards
Who manages to generate the most points or beat their own high score?
Only those who combine well and keep an overview can make the right decision and ultimately win "Multiflex."

—description from the designer

A lightweight version of Cartolan - Trade Winds. Light Winds focuses on balancing exploration with trade routes, while swapping expansive numbers of Adventurers for a new Companion system.

Goal: The game is won by the first player with 100 Silks banked at cities.

Turn order: clockwise, players move their Adventurer until exhausted. The game starts with the person who most recently paid an Innkeeper.

Moving: each turn or after resting, Adventurers can move twice in any direction between tiles before tiring.
Once tired, they can move twice over water edges only, in the direction of the wind arrow on the tile they move from, before exhausting.

Exploring: moving into a gap, match the edges of a map from your Adventurer's chest with adjacent tiles.

Actions: on the tile after a move, Adventurers can choose to, in order:
I. Trade; II. Rest with Inn (refill moves & map); III. Hire Inn.

This is a heist game where the first crew to reach the vault wins. Each crew has 4 members (4 board tokens). I suggest using a flat/stack-able token as a single space can be occupied by more than one member.
Strategize which member you want to move first. First one to the vault wins.

Setup:
Place all crew members in stacks of 4 at the safe house.

Turn:
Each turn has two actions: Roll the dice, move any one crew member on the board. If no move is possible, the turn is over.

Capture:
If player’s member lands on an space having one or more opponent’s members, they are captured. Captured pieces are moved back to safe house. Secure zones are safe from capture, both the player’s members can stay here as long as they like.

Snare and Alarm:
If a player’s member lands on space having a Snare or an Alarm, on the next turn, the player has to roll the dice condition mentioned on the board. If the dice condition is met, the player gets to move his member one space forward. If not, player has to move alongside the space indicated by the arrow.

Backdoor:
If a player’s member lands on space having a Backdoor, on the next turn, the player may use it to move to space indicated by the arrow. To do this, a player must roll the dice condition mentioned on the board. Back doors are optional so a player may not choose to use it.

Win:
The game is won by the first player who has moved all his members to the vault.

—description from the designer

SIGN is a cooperative game that everyone creates a starry sky.

Other than the answers will follow the title of the "Star Cards" with various words.
The star cards connect to each other with an irregular chip so you can tell the responder about which word is closer to the subject.
Answers will guess the correct answers while watching the starry skies made in 2 minutes.
Many Signs will be able to answer the correctly.

Adding the rules of "Ojamamano" to add characters that are designed to hide their identity and not let everyone understand the right way, so you can enjoy identity concealment.

Slam Dunk Dash is a basketball themed strategy and luck dice roll game. The game includes many elements of a real basketball game, with players moving the ball up and down the court avoiding steals, shooting three pointers, running fast breaks, and jamming slam dunks all while avoiding shot clock violations.

The game is a possession-based game for 2 players. Players are racing to be the first to score 25 points, rolling the dice to move the ball from defence to offence.

Each player follows their own exclusive ladder, making tactical decisions along the way. Special squares offer opportunities to take a shot or fast break the ball, while others trigger the shot clock or turn the ball over.

—description from the designer

Roll dice and match the combinations to your Contact cards in order to attack. Cards have a unique flipping mechanism that reveals additional actions you can take on future turns using the 'Protocol' side of the cards.

You'll go up against a Corporate AI that will attack you and protect itself. You'll need to use your available actions to maximise your dice rolls, counter-attack and defend yourself against the brute force of the AI's systems.

Take the AI offline to win!

—description from the designer

Family Fools and Friends is a chaotic party game where players take on hidden roles — Family, Friend, or Fool — and try to outwit the Seeker. It blends social deduction with outrageous behavior-driven challenges, leading to big laughs, wild accusations, and unexpected alliances.

In each round, one player becomes the Seeker and must identify who among the group is the Fool, based on how players respond to offbeat scenarios or wacky directives. Everyone else must bluff or play along without revealing their role.

With simple rules, fast-paced rounds, and unpredictable twists, this game is perfect for game night, family gatherings, or any situation where calling someone out (playfully) is half the fun.

—description from the designer

Players can play different roles to participate in anti-Japanese battles. Players will fight side by side with famous anti-Japanese generals in the game and experience the real historical events!
Resistance War is a card games based on the anti-Japanese war, and shares many rules with BANG!
Identity changed to Japanese Army, Pseudo Army, Eighth Route Army, National Army.
Characters were changed to names of cities or places, such as Wuhan, Kunming, etc.

Released via the Japanime Games Patreon.

Magi is promo card # ROS031

Act: +1 Cost

Act: Reveal the top card of the deck. Choose to leave the card on top or place on the bottom of the deck.

Super Skills Outdoor is the ultimate skill game for all ages, designed to be played outdoors. Players compete in various
challenges, including water for an extra splash of fun!

Be the first to complete 10 of the 120 possible challenges, set as either "complete the task" or "beat the opponent".
Adds 2 more answer dials and a landing pad for both 3rd place and 4th place. Allows the player count of the game to expand from 2 players to 4

Introducing INKtentions: Murky Waters—the shadowy expansion that stirs the waters and shakes up your strategies. For players who enjoy layered tactics and deeper strategy, Murky Waters brings new dangers and defenses. The sea just got smarter.

With the arrival of the cunning EEL card, the once-reliable HIDE & HUNT card is no longer a guaranteed safe play. Hiding in the rocks can now be dangerous as the 
HIDE & HUNT card has a nemesis! However, the EEL ignores HUNT cards.

The INK card upgrades adjacent HUNT cards into a HIDE & HUNT. Now it is easier to dodge the jaws of hungry sharks in the open ocean. But it is a double-edged sword[fish], as now you are again hiding in the rocks where an EEL might target you!

—description from the publisher

The once-peaceful community we call home has fallen under the shadow of an evil force, and the courageous protectors who defended our town have set off on their own pursuits of fame and fortune. In their place, a band of unlikely heroes have emerged - the Small-Time Heroes. These are not your typical heroes, with bulging biceps and chiseled jawlines. No, these heroes are more like the underdogs, with quirky personalities and oddball skills.

In this cooperative deck builder, you’ll take on the role of one of the Small-Time Heroes and work together to defend the town. With each battle, you’ll build up your deck of cards, choosing from a variety of cards that represent your hero’s unique abilities. As your power builds, you will defeat waves of minions and end with a final boss battle. Defeat the boss before they defeat you!

Play a one off session by battling through three waves of minions and a final boss or turn it into a campaign and battle through all 3 bosses.

Kobolds and Miners: Midnight Bounty is a 9-card add-on to the Kobolds and Miners game.

At the start of any game you need to draw 3 random bounties and put them face-up so that everyone can see them. At the end of the game each player scores additional cash based on the successfully scored bounty cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Gallant and Efficient Fight: The Battle for hamel July 4, 1918
description:
A Gallant and Efficient Fight: The Battle of Hamel is a wargame simulation of the first joint attack by US and Australian forces in World War I. While US forces had fought battles on their own or in conjunction with their French ally, questions still remained if any US forces would fight well in coordination with forces from the British Dominion. The US 33rd Infantry (Prairie) Division had been training with the Australian Corps. The Australian Corps was decimated earlier during the German Spring offensive, with many units at half strength. It was hoped that the Americans could fill out the ranks of the decimated Australian units, a process that could improve the novice Yanks’ performance by working closely with the Australian veterans, as well as securing a vital section of the Allied front line.

By July the German Spring offensive on the Western Front had been stopped, but the Germans still held ground that needed to be taken if an Allied counter-offensive was to be launched in the coming weeks. One key location the Allies needed to take was at the heights near the town of Hamel, not far from where the Australians and US forces were located, and from which the Germans could direct artillery fire upon the key rail and road junction at Amiens. On July Fourth the Yanks and Aussies, supported by British tanks, launched an offensive to take the high ground and town.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterparts?

Each turn of the game represents hour and a half of time.  Each hex is approximately 250 yards across. Infantry units represent a company of men (about 250 men for the US, about 150 for the Australian and German). Each tank unit represents 3 (full strength) or 2 (reduced strength) vehicles.

—description from the designer

Objective
Collect the most cards in front of you by the time all cards from the pool have been taken.

Game Setup
Spread all cards face-up on the table. Ensure that all images are visible and do not overlap. This area is called the pool.
Players take turns rolling the dice clockwise. The youngest player goes first.

How to play
1. Roll the Dice
Roll both dice at the same time.
The result is a combination of a color and a symbol (e.g., yellow lion).
2. Find the Match
All players search simultaneously for the card in the pool that matches the rolled combination.
3. Slap the Card
The first player to place their hand on the correct card takes it.

Collected cards are placed face-up in front of each player, visible and accessible to others.

If the dice roll matches a card that is already in front of a player, it can be attacked and stolen by slapping it.
The current owner can defend the card by being the first to touch it.

The first to either attack or defend the correct card ends the round.

The player with the most cards in front of them wins.

—description from the designer

Bug It! is a dynamic and fun drawing game that will test your speed and sense of observation from the age of 6.

Can you draw a ladybug so fast that your partner can immediately recognize it among all the other crawling little critters?

Bug It! is a crazy fast-paced team game where children learn to communicate through lines on paper in record time. One player draws a card and must quickly draw the insect on the card, while their partner scouts among the 20 different insects on the table. The moment they see the drawing, the player shouts “Bug it!”

It sounds easy, but the difference between a spider and a fly suddenly becomes very important under time pressure! Children learn to focus on specific details – does the ladybug have five or seven spots? The colorful illustrations make each insect come alive and full of character, while the game trains fine motor skills and cooperation through explosions of laughter.

A DRAWING-GUESSING GAME THAT PROVES THAT THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN A SPIDER AND A FLY IS BIGGER THAN YOU THINK

—description from the publisher

Circus Animals! The new game for little acrobats? Are you ready for laughter and excitement? In the game Circus Animals, you compete against your friends to become the star of the show by collecting the most circus pieces for your tent. Choose your favorite character – maybe the balancing elephant or the cycling bear? But be careful! The mouse can grab your pieces, and the sloth can block your way. The excitement increases with every roll of the dice, and quick reactions are the key to victory. Are you ready to become the biggest star of the circus? Let the game begin!

—description from the publisher (translated)

In the Kalapeli, players ask for fish cards (14 different species) and try to collect suitable food cards and fishing gear for each species. The goal is to gather as many cards as possible during the game. The game can be played in several different ways, ensuring lasting interest. The cards feature beautiful illustrations by Jakke Haapanen. The game requires clever thinking and helps develop memory. It’s an excellent game for players of all ages to enjoy together.

Company level simulation of the vicious fight for the Pandols Heights during the Battle of Ebro in the Spanish Civil War.

The game scale is 150yds between hexsides and multiple activation day turns; mechanics focus on command & control, time & resource management and unit quality.

Game components are 17"x22" map & 210 counters (1 dice & 2 sets of colored chit are also required).

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A card game for 2-10 players where players are competing to become "the sunk one" sacrificed to an ancient god. Players must decide whether to keep their card or trade with a neighbour, the lowest ranked card ultimately throwing one of their 3 coins into the well. Players are eliminated when they run out of coins, with the last remaining player winning (and, narratively, being sacrificed to the monster in the well).

"In Italy, in a forgotten and nameless village, hidden by dense woods and whispered superstitions, there exists a cursed well. To appease its hunger, the inhabitants play an ancient game. Will you be the next of the Sunk?

From the mountains, an echo crosses the valleys and shakes your very bones—it is the call of the Well. All crave to lose themselves in oblivion, but only one will be granted its grim embrace.

In the darkness, Giuvà sings a chant—he only wants one…"

Horizons is a professional analytic strategic board wargame intended to look at future scenarios around NATO. This is done by creating contingent choices to look at possible evolutions of the strategic situation over the next 20 years. 5 players take on the roles of NATO, the EU, the USA, China, and Russia between 2024 and 2045, making decisions in the political, military, economic, and technological spheres.

Play is turn based with a simple d6 resolution system for each of the actions they can take, bounded by a limited amount of strategic capital in each turn. Each turn represents 3 years and players contend both with other players choices and actions as well as the actions of minor powers though a cardboard AI, as well as non-state actors and climate change though event card decks. At the end of the game victory is decided by the person with the most strategic advantage, but players can also lose by failing to meet core national objectives.

boardgame: Above and Below: Haunted – Stretch Goals Box
description:
This box is only available directly from Red Raven Games, but was free for all backers of the crowdfunding campaign.

It includes:
Additional Villagers
New Buildings
Solo Mode Cards
Upgraded wooden tokens to replace those in the base game
Metal Coins

GrimBrew is a competitive potion brewing game where rival alchemists clash to score the most Ember Jelly. The alchemist with the most Ember Jelly at game's end is crowned Head Alchemist and wins this dangerous game.

Each alchemist commands a laboratory powered by flames, your primary resource. Your lab holds extract cards with strange and powerful effects that you'll mix to brew potions. On your turn, you can draw an extract to your lab, brew a potion by spending a flame, or rekindle to restore two flames when you're running low.

Brewing works like cracking a combination lock. Draw the right extracts into your lab and mix them to fulfill a potion's recipe. When you succeed, that potion's power unlocks permanently, supercharging your lab for the rest of the game. The more you brew, the faster and more profitable your engine becomes.

But you're not brewing alone.

Mawlings change everything. These mischievous, many-eyed creatures can be summoned through certain extracts and sent to rival labs. When a Mawling-infested alchemist brews a potion, the creatures steal Ember Jelly and scurry back to whoever sent them. Timing your brews becomes a constant fight against these pests swarming your workspace.

Strategic planning matters in GrimBrew, but you need to stay quick on your feet. When the market refreshes with new potions or extracts, your beautiful plans crumble to dust. You need to think long term but adapt fast, regroup when chaos hits, and brew the right potion in the moment.

Can you bring the homeowner's dream of suburban paradise?

Suburbs emerged from expansions in road networks, enabling family sized housing, and living away from urban centres. The suburbs expand by colonising previous living space, transformed from older, usually poorer communities in a process called gentrification.

Subtopia, is a tile placement game, where you play as competitive town developers that dream of creating a suburban paradise by connecting road networks designed in aperiodic patterns.

Players flip, rotate and move double sided hand-drawn tiles, one with rural living space, and the other side with cookie-cutter homogenous suburbia. The flipping of a tile gentrifies the land from old living space to new suburban living space. Add new tiles onto the board by connecting the fragmented road networks on the tiles together to create open lanes for exploration.

Your goal is to score the highest at the end. Win by spending your coins, buying out tiles, so you can connect groups of tiles together into suburban Zones, which grants you permission to build your commercial facilities. Turn the suburbs you own into hilariously giant carparks and shopping malls.

Road networks are vital for building communities and spreading the suburbs, but in Subtopia, a competitive land of individual success, players have to find their own advantages with suburbs made of fragmented tile pieces. Building a homogenous suburban paradise is impossible, just as you will realise the difficulty fitting the tiles together perfectly during gameplay. In the world of Subtopia, what really makes the dream work? Where is paradise without the struggle?

Simulate realistic suburban reproduction and expansion.

—description from the designer

A Police car chase game.
Using two identical game boards the Police player attempts to capture the Bandit by navigating his/her car through the town street map. The Bandit tries to evade capture but cannot see the Police position.

boardgame: Singularity: A game of cosmic destruction
description:
In Singularity: A Game of Cosmic Destruction, you are the black hole.

This solo card game casts you as a gravitational force of nature—consuming planets, stars, moons, and other celestial entities in your quest to devour the galaxy.
You’ll consume cards from around the threshold, strategically pulling in new cards from distant space while managing your limited gravitational pull. To win, you must avoid collapsing in on yourself or tearing apart the threshold as you spiral toward total cosmic destruction.

Planets get erased, space bends, and dark matter reshapes the galaxy — all while you manipulate celestial chaos to achieve total annihilation.

Quick to learn and endlessly replayable, Singularity blends minimalism with cosmic scale in a space solitaire experience.

—description from the publisher

With the hive world of Armageddon battered by Warp storms and empyric corruption, a new conflict dawns. Amid the blazing Fire Wastes, the Warp rift known as the Red Angel's Gate has erupted into being, and from its cursed depths flood torrents of empyric energy and tides of bloodthirsty daemons. As the corruption spreads, Armageddon's hives seethe with heretic uprisings and rebellions.

Crusade: Armageddon is a narrative expansion for Warhammer 40,000. It details the brutal next chapter in the ongoing saga of the conflict raging around Armageddon, and the bleak state of the war efforts. The book also includes the rules to play narrative games, build and upgrade a Crusade army between battles, and run your own campaigns.

This 128-page hardback book contains:

     Detailed lore covering the history of the conflict, the invasion of the World Eaters, and the battle for the Fire Wastes.
     In-depth Crusade rules to grow and evolve your forces as you play battles, writing your own story.
     An exciting Crusade campaign designed to evoke the brutal battle over the Fire Wastes, with new Crusade Badges, Battle Traits, champions, and Crusade Relics.
     16 thrilling missions to play through on your conquest to save or destroy this beleaguered world.
     A six-page gatefold section, including rules references and blank pages you can copy for your own use.


—description from the publisher

Fliptown: New Frontier is the full-box expansion to the critically-acclaimed game Fliptown.

This is not a standalone game. The base game Fliptown is required to play.

Saddle up for this robust adventure that introduces four all-new regions to explore. Wrangle cows in the Hills. Pan for gold in the River. Take a ride on the Rails. Or stake your claim in New Town.

In the New Frontier, you can play just using the new board (called Single-Barrel mode) or combine it with the base board (called Double-Barrel mode) for an even bigger game experience!

New Frontier plays in the same manner as Fliptown, with a few fun new twists, including four new regions, a new resource called Hard Luck and some new game play aspects, including Ricochet Bonuses and a new type of bot called Sidekicks. Oh, and a whole lot of cows and chickens.

Prepare to be challenged in a whole new way as you strive to once again be the best in the West!

—description from the publisher

Players or teams of players face each other across a table and try to toss their round UFO cards into their colour matched cup. Points are scored for landing a card into the team's cup, and deducted for landing a card into the opposing team's cup. Mind Control cards can influence the game play. When all the UFO cards are played the round ends. The first team to 3 points wins.

boardgame: Frontline: The Miniature Game – The Path of the Dragon: Vietnam War 1965 - 75
description:
If your interest is playing the Vietnam War with your tabletop miniatures, this book is for you.

Vietnam War: The Path of the Dragon is a campaign book about the Vietnam War, it covers the period of the Vietnam War 1965 – 1975.

Based on historical facts, you can replay a variety of scenarios and recreating individual operations or the grand strategy of the entire war in one huge campaign. You can simulate the military and political aspects, choosing the United States, South Vietnam (ARVN), and their allies, or the other side, choosing the National Liberation Front of South Vietnam (NLF) and the People’s Army of Vietnam (PAVN).

As a special operation you can play the legendary “Tunnel Rats”.

Each scenario includes a brief historical summary, historically relevant lists, and appropriate maps.

In the campaign book you find 15 missions with 21 battles, 2 special scenarios and as a special operation the "Tunnel Rats".

Lists of units and companies:

     US Marine Rifle Company
     US 1st Cavalry Division
     US Black Horse Cavalry
     US Dreadnaughts
     US 25th Infantry
     US 101st Airborne Division
     US Riverine Task Force 117
     ARVN 1st Infantry
     ARVN Combat Car Tank Company
     ARVN Armored Cavalry
     ANZAC Infantry Company
     ANZAC Armored Cavalry
     ANZAC Tank Company
     Royal Thai Company (Black Panthers, Queen’s Cobras)
     South Korean Company (White Horse)
     South Korean Capital Mechanized Division (Fierce Tiger Division)
     Local Forces Infantry
     PAVN Infantry Company
     PAVN Iron Clad Battalion


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Operation Torch: Part 2 – Casablanca, Safi and Port-Lyautey: North Africa Campaign
description:
This scenario pack focuses on Operation Torch and the Western Task Force in 1942 of the North Africa campaign. 
The Western Task Force was the first full scale all American operation of the war. 
US forces struggled to use combined arms during the operation. Hard lessons were learned during tough battles with the Vichy French. 
This packet includes naval support and American destroyers carrying American troops directly ashore. 
This battalion level scenario pack contains 10 exciting miniatures war game scenarios written in campaign form and playable by 2-4 players.

The scenarios include data sheets for vehicles and uses Fistful of TOWs 3 rules. Scenarios can be easily converted to your favorite game system.

The ten scenarios can be played as a campaign. A campaign score sheet is included, enabling players to track the campaign progress and final score.

These scenarios put you in the “Gasoline Cowboy’’ seat, making you and your gaming partner(s) the tank drivers as you play through Operation Torch Part 2 - Casablanca, Safi and Port Lyautey Landings - North Africa Campaign.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Knee Deep in Hexes: God's Hand
description:
Welcome to God’s Hand—the expansion to Knee Deep in Hexes, where new factions, mercenaries, and planetary forces change up the orignal RTS-like 2-player skirmisher game. Decide with a clear mind and a sharp blade whom you trust—and whom you abandon in the sands of Hexes 6.

When the crews of the Gondwana and Geobukseon finally arrive on the desert planet, they do not find a stage for new hope—but a prepared enemy. In the dust and filth of the scorched plains, the ruthless S.C.A.R. faction rises—a coalition of outlaws. They are no strangers. They are the descendants of those rebels who, decades ago, were exiled into space after a revolt against the military caste aboard the Gondwana. Now, they are determined to take revenge at last.

But S.C.A.R. is not the only force on Hexes 6.
In the shadows of the conflict, mysterious mercenaries offer their deadly services—willing to join any side that meets their price. Those who employ them wisely can turn the tide of war.
And while humans battle one another on the surface, a primordial force stirs deep within the planet—one mystically connected to the world itself. The Elder: long hidden, they now leave no doubt that Hexes 6 is their territory—and they are ready to defend it.

—description from the publisher

Iconic Neon Stories is a party game where players must use icons to create the most iconic stories. Can you quickly adapt and deal with your assignments?

How does the game work?
The game is played in rounds, with each round starting by giving all players five cards from the draw deck. The draw deck is made up completely of cards with icons on them.

The goal of the game is to create stories and make them as appealing to the group you are playing with, as everyone gets to vote for their favorite each round.

A round is played in three phases: Phase 1, Imagine and Swap. Phase 2, Narrate. Phase 3, Vote.

During the first phase (30 seconds), an icon card from the draw deck is turned open. This is the card that every story has to start with. Players then have the option to discard hand cards and draw new cards once.

During the second phase, all players have to tell their story. But at the start of their turn, another card from the draw deck is opened and this has to be the final card in their story.

After all players have narrated their story, players vote who told their favorite story. This player wins the round

—description from the publisher

In a quiet village on a remote island, everything is about to change.

This year the coconut trees have not produced enough for all the island's inhabitants, and their most precious commodity, coconuts, are beginning to become scarce, causing panic and a real disaster...

The fight to get the few remaining coconuts has begun, and a frantic race to fill the pantry begins.

Betrayal, obstacles, abductions, thefts... and very few coconuts!

Based on the 1493 Nuremberg Chronicle by Hartmann Schedel, Burgus is a card game for 2 to 4 players that blends classic trick-taking, fishing, and rummy mechanics with deck-building and engine-building gameplay, all illustrated with the most beautiful medieval woodcuts.
Build, Trade, Manage, and Conquer to become the most prosperous Burgus of the Middle Ages in this fast-paced, strategic card game designed by Gaetano Foschini.

In Burgus, there are four categories of cards representing medieval society — peasants, military, clergy, and nobles — each interacting in unique ways with the building cards available on the table for all players to acquire and add to their personal decks.
As the game progresses, players improve their decks each round in order to play buildings into their Burgus, activating their effects using cards of the same category. This creates powerful combos and strategies, leading to the construction of installations that grant victory points at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

PROMOTES MEMORY & CONCENTRATION: Improves visual attention, logical thinking & social interaction. Perfect for preschool children & families.
Simple game principle, great fun: reveal cards, find matching combinations and outsmart the donkey! Ideal for toddlers as a first party game.
Perfect for game evenings and on the go: thanks to its compact size, ideal for travel, nurseries or as a quick activity at home. A game for 1-4 players.
GREAT GIFT FOR CHILDREN: Delights fans of Memory, Exploding Kittens & The Game. A creative gift for Christmas, birthdays and as an educational game for little explorers
Fun memory game: children help hungry animals find the right food before the voracious donkey strikes!
How to Play
Setup: Place the 52 food cards face down in the center.

Gameplay:

Players take turns flipping over one animal card and then searching for the corresponding food card among the face-down cards.

If a player finds the correct food, they keep the animal card.

If a player reveals a piece of the greedy donkey instead of food, that piece is added to the donkey's body.

Game Continuation: A new animal card is drawn, and play continues.

End of Game:

The game ends when all food cards are matched or when the greedy donkey's body is fully assembled.

The player with the most animal cards at the end wins.

Winning
Collect the most animal cards before the greedy donkey completes its body.

—description from the publisher

A space exploration and battle game where ancient bio-mechanical lifeforms travel the galaxy in pursuit of N-Light.

The game features two types of cards, Area Cards that define the many sections of the galaxy, that can combine to form a giant map, and Unit Cards that represent the wide array of N-Versers available for constructing an armada.

A unique aspect is that 10 different types of 'theaters' of action are possible at the same time. For example, ships could be warring in Interplanetary Space at the same time that units are fighting in the planet's Atmosphere, at the same time that player's are clashing in the Urban street of the planet's capitol city.

N-Verse is highly expandable with new Area Cards that open up new sections of the galaxy, and new Units to mix into player's existing library of options.

—description from the designer

A fan, portable, easy-to-learn game where you block your opponents movement to be the last one standing.

DESIGNED, MODELED, and 3D PRINTED by 12 year old Nate Denham.

Players begin with their pieces in the corners of a 6x6 grid. Play begins with the youngest plyer and continues clockwise. On your turn you must complete 3 spaces worth of movement without hitting the same opening twice (diagonal costs two and allows you to slip between tiles) and then place a tile to block off an opening.  If you cannot complete your three paces worth of movement then your piece comes off the board.  The goal is to be the last person with their player piece on the board.

Each player receives two pawns of one color and places one pawn in entrance E. The players roll the die and advance their pawns by the number of spaces they rolled. Players may skip their own and opponents' pawns. As soon as a player reaches or exceeds space 14 with their first pawn, they place their second pawn in E and may now move both pawns alternately as they wish.
Several pawns may be placed on the large squares at the same time. If a pawn lands on an occupied small square, the pawn standing there must be eliminated and placed on E. If a pawn lands on a square that has a passage to another square, then on its next move it must be placed further through that square by the number of pips thrown.
If a pawn lands on square 66, then it must traverse maze 0, step by step, one square at each throw, regardless of the number of pips thrown; from square 59 onwards, it rejoins the game. If a piece lands on square 75, then it must traverse maze I-X; the pips thrown count. A piece on square 98 must be moved to the darkroom D on its next move, from which it may only be moved to 100 after the player has rolled 3 eyes. A pawn may be moved from squares 95, 96, 97, and 99 to 100 even if more eyes than necessary have been rolled.
The first player to move both of their pawns to 100 wins.

—user summary

One player rolls the dice and everyone draws the corresponding animal on their game board. The special trick: predators such as the bearded vulture, snake, fox and heron need prey animals as neighbors to be scored. In addition, each animal only prefers certain areas. Ready to release a few animals into the wild?

Each number is a specific animal. For one, you can draw a mouse or a fish in a single square on your board. For a three, you can draw a snake or a heron with three squares. Animals with one or two boxes are prey animals and score one or two points. Animals with three or more squares are predators and need prey animals as neighbors to score points.

Draw the animals wisely so that no predators end up "starving" because they have no prey as neighbors. And draw your animals only in areas where they belong. The heron can be drawn on meadow or water squares but not in the woods.

The game ends when nobody can draw an animal on their board anymore. The person with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

STAY ANGRY
Test your reflexes and awareness in this epic battle between the Avengers and their fiercest foes. When matching Villains appear, you must be ready to SMASH! those puny bad guys off the battlefield.

HULK NO SMASH
Always be on alert for cards that can catch you out, because an incorrect smash will lose you points. Oh, and definitely, absolutely, do not ever smash your friends!

—description from the publisher

DO YOU FEEL THE TINGLE?
It's time to put your web-slinging reactions to the test! As soon as Spidey's attack matches the Villain's weakness, you'd better get your hand down fast because only the first player to touch the card will win it.

STAY SHARP
Somewhere in the deck lie Action cards that can help or hinder you in battle. But they may also distract you! So, use that Spidey-Sense because if you put your hand down incorrectly, you will lose points and never become a Webbed Wonder!

—description from the publisher

GROW YOUR GROOTS
Be the first collect a complete set of your favorite extraterrestrial friend and shout "I Am Groot!" to win! Sounds easy enough, right? Well, maybe not. . .

WATCH OUT FOR GUARDIANS
The rest of your crew is hiding in the deck, just waiting to influence the game. You can count on your best bud, Rocket, to help you out! But some of others just want to spoil your fun (Hey, Drax!).

—description from the publisher

Get ready to keep a straight face in this game of incredible facts and outrageous bluffs! Bluffers read out the statement on the card then decide whether they will say a bluff answer or the correct answer. Then all the other players try to call their bluff!

—description from the publisher

SPECTACULAR SILLINESS
Pop on the glasses and get ready for giggles! Ask yes-no questions to guess who is on your head. The first player to correctly name their character wins.

DON'T BE A LOSER
It's the classic guessing game you know and love, but with a goofy twist. For every question you ask where the answer is "No", you draw a Letter card. If those cards spell 'Loser' before you guess who you are, then you're out of the game. It's a bit of fun!

—description from the publisher

Families and friends will be desperate to play! This Number 1 game about Number 2s is a new twist on the classic Old Maid, which includes unique action cards like 'Miss a go' and 'Skip next player'. It's easy to learn and quick to play, and features cute poop illustrations! Be the first to pair off all the cards in your hand to win, but 'don't hold it' and ensure that the poop isn't the last card in your hand!

—description from the pulisher

A blob-tastic twist on the classic card game! Easy to learn and quick to play, the game includes unique action cards illustrated with the cute li'l blob fish. It will become a staple on your games shelf!

—description from the publisher

Can you handle the truth? In this family game, teams must decide what is fact and what is fiction. Using a true or false paddle which they reveal at the same time, the team that claims the most cards in 2 minutes wins!

—description from the publisher

TIME TO TAKE SOME WHISKS
Shout "3, 2, 1, PASS THE CAKE!" to begin a frosting frenzy! Made for the whole family, everyone plays at the same time so things could get messy.

WANNA BE STAR BAKER?
Bake, swap, freeze, and burn as you compete to create three showstoppers to win! The icing on the cake? Make a Confetti Cake to claim a sweet instant victory!

—description from the publisher

A deckbuilding, suspense-filled game where cryptocurrency investors aim to make the most money! Players try to buy and sell in an ever-changing crypto market. But keep an eye on events that affect the currency values as the game progresses. Special cards allow players to send viruses or ransomware to other players to hack into their crypto wallets and steal their assets! The winner is the player who accumulates the most worth from cryptocurrencies and cash.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Family Fools & Friends: Law & Order Expansion Pack
description: Fifteen new scenarios and fifteen new roles.

DINO-MITE FUN!
Keep your eyes peeled for poop and get ready to roar with this three-of-a-kind matching game. It's fun for the whole family, just don't be A-saur-loser! (See what we did there?)

TIME TO TRAMPLE
Take turns adding Dino cards to the grid to make rows of three. A Roar card can match with anything … as long as you ROAR! And the Poop card earns you extra points if it stays on your head! The first player to collect 10 cards wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel Guardians of the Galaxy: Dance-Off
description:
LET'S DANCE
Remember when Star-Lord saved Xandar from destruction by throwing shapes to the sounds of his mixtape? Well, now it’s your turn to boogie for the win. Just don’t be fooled by a distraction!

BUST A MOVE
When the Mix Master shouts "DANCE OFF" everyone plays a card. Whoever spots a match and strikes the right pose first wins the round. But stay alert for the Skip DJ and Rewind cards, and sneaky double matches that can catch you out.

—description from the publisher

SIDE-SPLITTING SCRIBBLES
Forget about skill. Just join in the fun, and enjoy the hilarity as you complete random challenges with some of the world's most famous faces to win points. No one ever created gallery-worthy artwork in one minute, anyway!

IT'S PARTY TIME
This mini edition of the bestselling classic is faster to play and easier to carry. Basically, we made it so you can have fun on the run. And with all-new content, the cards can be used as an expansion pack for the original game, too!

—description from the publisher

New York City is being attacked by the Sinister Six, and it’s time for the Webbed Wonders to protect the neighborhood. Team Spidey must collect 40 points before the Super Villains reach their lair in this battle of good vs. evil.

Sling your web card! If you land on a Spidey Skill you get points. But if you land on a Super Villain, you must add it to the lair.

This original game is all about protecting 'the neighbourhood' from the Sinister Six. You do that by throwing cards in the right place. Villian and Skill discs are randomly laid out on a flat surface (table or floor).  Players stand a minimum of 3 feet away and try to flick a card onto a Skill disc to get points.  Landing on a villain put them in the lair and, if all villains are in the lair before the player(s) reach 40 points, the game is lost.

—description from the publisher

Drive for your life as you and your opponents move faster, shoot faster, and lose faster in this speed soaked Death Derby expansion. These 36 cards bring High Octane to your Death Derby game and leave your opponents in the dust or on the side of the road.

—description from the designer

Save Vicksburg. And don't get court-martialed or anything.

A mad general and his personal army have ventured out from their secluded military base, setting up checkpoints on the road far north of Vicksburg, emboldened by a secret weapon they've managed to get working. A masked man in black called 'Knash' and two riflemen show up in days with an ultimatum for the Mayor of Vicksburg, which is voted down by the town but supported by Joe Mofo and his gang. Vicksburg prepares for war as Knash returns to the General with news of their refusal.

—description from the designer

Save Vicksburg. And don't get mutated or anything.

Professor Jekyll, a mad chemist that tests on his followers and feeds dead test subjects to his mutated pet dog, pushed into Vicksburg in the dead of night to poison the area, pillage, and move on to the next settlement. His chemical fueled army brushed Vicksburg's defenders aside and sent inhabitants fleeing into the hills with only their clothing. The Party is just regrouping to plan their next move as they're confronted by something in the darkness...

Includes 2 PCs, 4 Enemies, 7 Items, 4 Situations, Rule Card, and Skill Glossary (download only).

—description from the designer

This is a promo pack of seven new goals for recent unthemed Fluxx versions: 5.0, 4.0, and Special Edition ("SE" or "Target Fluxx"). The card style matches 5.0 and the second run of SE, but the Keeper art matches 5.0 and 4.0.

It also includes a new Meta Rule (rule which stays in effect for the whole game) called Goal Foundry. This essentially makes the New Rule Goal Mill permanent to the game... which you're going to need with seven extra Goals in your deck!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Far Side of the World
description:
In the summer of 1914, Germany’s East Asia cruiser squadron’s superbly trained crews of long-service professionals made Admiral Maximilian Graf von Spee's small fleet the envy of the China station. But when war broke out, this tiny force found itself far from home and threatened by the powerful Imperial Japanese Navy, many times the size of the German squadron. Spee decided to force his way home around the world. After an epic journey across the Pacific, his cruisers met their end off of the Falkland Islands in a final battle.

Far Side of the World gives the Great War at Sea series its greatest scope yet: the entire world. British, German, Japanese, Italian, Austro-Hungarian, Russian and Dutch ships begin as they did in August 1914. The German player must disrupt Allied commerce and try to get the cruisers home if at all possible. The Allied player must track them down and destroy them before they can wreak havoc.

Far Side of the World’s map divides the world into sea areas, with modified search rules allowing German and Austrian ships to look for prey, and Allied ships to hunt them. Once contact is made, combat is resolved using the standard game rules. Itcomes in our popular Playbook format with a completely new set of 300 playing pieces (180 double-sized ship pieces and 120 square ones, all of them die cut and silky-smooth, with all-new artwork), a new map, new full-color play aids, and an all-new book with revised rules and scenarios, additional scenarios plus historical background.

Far Side of the World’s game play based on the Great War at Sea Second Edition rules: the first new, re-written set of series rules in more than 25 years! Far Side of the World is a complete game: everything you need to play (except dice) is included in the Playbook. Far Side of the World is a thoroughly re-imagined edition of our old Cruiser Warfare game.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Veggie Patch is a  quick solitaire, dice rolling, tile laying, dice placement game.

Roll dice to get your seeds. Play cards to create vegetable beds in your garden and place the dice in them to sow the seeds.

Decide which dice to place where in order to maximise your harvest.

Use your watering can, garden fork and a friendly crow wisely to help you grow enough veggies to win the game.

—description from the designer

Veggie Patch was an entry in the 2025 9-Card Nanogame Print and Play Design Contest where it was voted 2nd Best Solitaire Game and 3rd Best Overall Game.

It’s Halloween night. You and your friends have found a deck of cards and a board said to be able to speak with the beyond. But the game is haunted by ghosts, and they’re ready to have fun with you. Dodge the spirits and curse your friends in Fantasmagoria!

Fantasmagória is a trick-avoidance game where you don’t want to win tricks containing cards from the ghost suit.

At the start of each round, each player discards one card from their initial hand onto one of the ghost suit piles, the lowest number discarded becomes the trump value of the game, meaning it's the strongest number both in and out of suit.

The game then proceeds with classic trick-taking rules: each player plays one card, following the suit of the first card played. Whoever plays the strongest card takes all the cards in the trick.

The first time a player fails to follow suit, a possession occurs. The number on the card played by the possessed player becomes the new trump number. Then, that player must take one of the ghost suit piles into their hand.

But beware! In addition to the ghosts, each suit has one cursed card you’ll want to avoid.

After three rounds, the winner is the player who has been least cursed. So prepare your best traps, shove the ghosts toward your opponents, and escape with cunning in Fantasmagoria!

—description from the publisher

A small expansion for Rewild that consists of 6 Habitat tiles and 3 Wildlife tokens.

These add a few more decision points and ways to expand your habitats, whilst still keeping the game simple, relaxing and short.

—description from the designer

FORGET GRATITUDE
This game turns pessimism into points, so it's a good use of your bad attitude! It will test the convictions of your most optimistic friends. So, let go of all that relentless toxic positivity you’ve been sold and get real.

BE DISAPPOINTING, BUT FUNNY
Twist positive prompts into Disappointing Affirmations. If yours is the most amusingly critical, anti-empathetic, or makes people laugh because otherwise they’d cry, then congrats, you win! As if it even matters.

—description from the publisher

HE ALL-TIME GREATS
Okay, we're really spoiling you with this one … because it's actually two! These party games—Kazoo That Tune and Who Am I?—are firm favorites that guarantee an evening of fun and laughter.

BE THE FAMILY CHAMP!
Hum your way through tunes that span genres, theme songs, and smash hits to claim the kazoo-ing crown*. Then pop on the glasses and ask all the right questions to guess who's on your head!


    there isn't an actual crown, but you'll still feel pretty special.


—description from the publisher

Chop. Drop. Chili! celebrates how Bush’s Chili Magic chili starters are the fast track to delicious, homemade chili in just three easy steps. Recommended for ages 8+, each player takes turns rolling the dice and racing to spot the ingredients that match the recipe cards. The first to claim five recipe cards wins.

In Ekspedisjon Knerten Brettspillet you join Little Brother on an exciting expedition. Who will reach the finish line first with Knerten?
The game about Little Brother and the Knerten in the West Pole! Little brother, Knerten and Father are in the West Pole. They are hidden behind all the snow on the mountain, so you have to look hard to find them.
Suddenly you catch sight of Knerten through your binoculars, and then you have to be quick and secure the piece before it snows and he disappears again on the mountain.
Who will find the Nerd, Little Brother and Father and get home and finish first?

boardgame: Dominion of Marlborough and Peter the Great
description:
This the Dominion of Marlborough and Peter the Great Wargames Rules Solo Game.

This standalone set of rules covers Monmouth's Rebellion, the Nine Years’ War, the Williamite War in Ireland, the War of the Spanish Succession, the Great Northern War, and the 1715 Jacobite Rebellion. 
It is built on the Dominion of the Spear game system but is a brand new set of rules with appropriate rule changes. 
These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-6 units and a solo battle can be fought within 10 minutes. 
An example play through is included. There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover. 
Use model figures of any scale or paper and pen as there is no measuring (grid used). 
Uses 2 six sided dice.

24 historical battles are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically:

Monmouth’s Rebellion
1. Battle of Sedgemoor 6th July 1685
Nine Years’ War
2. Battle of Walcourt 25th August 1689
3. Battle of Fleurus 1st July 1690
4. Battle of Steenkerque 3rd August 1692
5. Battle of Neerwinden 29th July 1693

Williamite War in Ireland
6. Battle of the Boyne 1st July 1690

War of the Spanish Succession
7. Battle of Chiari 1st September 1701 
8. Battle of Luzzara 15th August 1702 
9. Battle of Schellenberg 2nd July 1704 
10. Battle of Blenheim 13th August 1704
11. Battle of Elixheim 18th July 1705 
12. Battle of Ramillies 23rd May 1706 
13. Battle of Turin 7th September 1706 
14. Battle of Almanza 25th April 1707
15. Battle of Oudenarde 11th July 1708
16. Battle of Malplaquet 11th September 1709 
17. Battle of Villaviciosa 10th December 1710
18. Battle of Denain 24th July 1712

Great Northern War
19. Battle of Narva 30th November 1700 
20. Battle of Düna 19th July 1701
21. Battle of Kliszów 9th July 1702 
22. Battle of Holowczyn 14th July 1708
23. Battle of Poltava 28th June 1709

Jacobite Rebellion 1715
24. Battle of Sheriffmuir 13th November 1715

This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet (click booklet and fit to page in printing options). 44 A5 pages.

—description from the designer
family: History: War of the Spanish Succession

description:
Within 6 days or less, you must remove ghosts from the moorland surrounding the village. Manage your resources and items in this solo dice manipulation game.

Collect gold and then visit the village to stock up on enchanted items to help you on your quest. Ghosts become harder to remove as you progress so you need to equip yourself for the escalating task.

Using six polyhedral dice you'll add the values together using at least two of your dice. One of the dice has to be of the type indicated on the ghost card and the total of the values across your dice have to equal the target. The used dice are then rolled to expend them.

You need to keep an eye on your stamina which depletes as you take actions. Return to the village whenever you need to refresh and recover, however this will lose you time and the days will count down. Good luck in your quest!

—description from the designer

Pharos is an abstract strategy game where players command beams of light to maneuver stones across the board. Players use clever positioning and coordination to outmaneuver their opponent, activate beacons, and immobilize their forces. With every move a tactical puzzle, the first player to prevent their rival from making a move wins.

—description from the designer

Fairytale heroes from different worlds unite to fight against the Lord of Darkness and his minions.

If the god-man Amiran, who has been defeated and driven mad by the darkness, manages to break down the gates of Tartarus, eternal disaster will reign in the world of the living.

Will the enchanted Medea, the brave Komble, the shrewd Pentacle and the invincible Tsiskara be able to defeat the minions, it depends on you!!

—description from the publisher

In Zionist discourse, "Alijah" is a term for migration to Palestine. Das Alijah-Spiel ("The Alijah Game") is a roll-and-move game depicting that migration. Players travel from Germany to Emek Chefer. Several events may occur during their journey, which are triggered when a player lands on a specific space. Each event has some story attached to it; mechanically, this results in having to go a few spaces back or forward, or having to skip one or more turns.

Some spaces also require the players to pay some money, which is supposed to be collected in a collection box of the Jüdischen Nationalfonds, an organisation which raised money for the migration to Palestine. The rules don't explicitly state whether the money is supposed to go to the winner, or to the Nationalfonds.

Do you check Twitter constantly to catch up on news about your favorite celebs? Do you know who wore it best on the red carpet? Do you know more about celebrities than members of your own family? If you answered yes to any of these questions, you will love this pack.

—description from the publisher

Recruit Crew: 15 Unique Crew Members. Crew are heroes that you can recruit to your ship to give you special abilities and advantages over your rival explorers.
Discover New Lands: 20 Diverse Land Tiles. Land tiles are the lands that need to be discovered and surveyed. These lands are known to hold untold riches and the promise of fame for the discovering explorer.
Amass your Fortune: 21 Rewarding Treasure Cards. There are many different rewards and treasures that you can find out on the sea. Will you brave the dangers of the high seas for the chance at untold fortunes?

On your turn, roll a die to determine the number of actions you can take. Actions include: attempting to discover unchartered areas, moving, recruiting crew, attacking other pirates, or amassing your gold fortune. The game is over once the land and sea have been charted. Players receive points for each gold they've collected as well as each land they've discovered. The player with the most points is the winner.

The Magical Weapons And Tactics Special Team needs new recruits! Enlist today in the capital of Kaedrid to battle giant trolls, rescue hostages, and face many other adventures for the good of the king and his people!

In M.W.A.T. (Magical Weapons And Tactics), players take on the vital role of unique characters in covert (or not-so-covert!) missions. Sneak in through a window when no one is watching or blow up the ceiling and toss in a smoke bomb. Avoid detection by tiptoeing in silent slippers, or eliminate every nearby villain before they even realize what's happening!

M.W.A.T. is a mission-based game where players take on distinct character roles with unique abilities to complete dynamic objectives... stealthily or explosively. Choose between quiet infiltration or chaotic assault, adapting your strategy based on items like smoke bombs and silent slippers. Every scenario invites creative problem-solving, making each mission a blend of tactical planning and thematic flair.

Whenever the prince loses a battle, he becomes extremely depressed! In order to give the prince confidence, his subordinates must aim to "lose" at the very last moment! A serious battle that requires sharp reading and courage, played with just 10 cards!

boardgame: ブラックキャンドル (Black Candle)
description: The Black Candle ritual begins. Paying attention to the candle you are holding, you pile the flame onto the candle next to it. If you concentrate too much on stacking, you'll forget to use your other hand. Before you knock it over, place it on the table and secure it! Place it on the table and secure it! - It's a chicken race balance game where you have to determine your limits and decide where to secure your points!

To survive, you must correctly answer endless strings of ones and zeros. It's impossible! But then you realize that you can use the cards you have on hand to note down the sequence. There are only eight cards. I need to somehow compress my notes and record as many sequences as possible!

boardgame: しりとりうまい (Shiritori Is Good)
description: A speedy picture word chain game where you can only use six different letters. Limited words allow for speed and fun. Also pay attention to the wild illustrations based on family picture chains.

Radio WWAVVES is a fast-paced, area-control strategy game set in a stylized world of analog broadcasting. Each player takes on the role of a radio company owner competing to dominate the airwaves by transmitting signals across a modular map filled with cities and various terrain types such as sea, forest, mountains, and beaches. The thematic focus revolves around strategic placement and signal propagation, turning a puzzle-like grid into a dynamic battlefield for influence.

The core gameplay consists of simultaneous card selection and token placement. Each player has six double-sided tokens (antennas and emitters) and a hand of terrain cards with initiative values. Antennas broadcast signals to adjacent tiles, while emitters relay signals across five tiles in a chosen direction—creating intricate chains of influence across the map. Terrain cards determine where players can place tokens and in what initiative order they act.

The main goal is to influence cities by covering them with your signal cubes. A city can be influenced by multiple players, but the one with the most cubes at the end scores its full value (shared in case of ties). Bonus points are awarded for reaching all cities at least once. Once all tokens have been placed, scores are tallied based on city control and scoring cards, with the highest total determining the winner.

With its compact 10–20 minute playtime, modular setup for replayability, and simultaneous action system, Radio WWAVVES offers a “crunchy multiplayer puzzle” experience where players must balance initiative, terrain, and signal networks to outsmart opponents in a race for radio supremacy.

—description from the designer

A family party card game for 2-6 players ages 7 upwards. Involves both climbing and shedding mechanics. The object of the game is to win the Trashdown by being the first player to get rid of the cards in your hand by playing them to the trash(discard)pile. Players play numbered cards as well as Trash(action) cards designed to thwart and slow down the progress of the other players. Includes optional game where player with lowest points total wins. Game time varies between 10 to 30 minutes depending on number of players and draw of the cards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Capture the Bald Yeti: The Card Game
description:
In Capture the Bald Yeti, Each “Hunter” is trying to amass a specified number of Capture Points. By doing this they must capture, or photograph the elusive Bald Yeti.

In front of them they lay traps and cameras, baited and ready to capture the Bald Yeti.

BUT, beware, hangry goats and rival Hunters will get in the way. Goats will eat your traps and rivals will attempt steal your Capture Points.

Beware of the falling snow and and don’t get suckered in to a hand of poker with your enemies!

—description from the designer

The king's centennial celebration is underway but with no heirs, succession is anyone's game. Noble families from across the realm gather to win the monarch's favor in a lavish court of manipulation and prestige. Amid grand balls and whispered schemes, only the most cunning family will secure the throne.

Aristofarsa is a hybrid of trick-taking and deckbuilding. Each round, players either compete in the Garden by playing nobles to win tricks-or use the Balcony to activate effects that alter outcomes or acquire stronger cards. Balancing bold plays and subtle manipulation, players shape their decks and influence to earn the king's favor. The game ends when a noble triggers final scoring.

The Al network has been compromised. Among a cluster of intelligent agents, a human imposter hides in plain sight intent on dismantling the system from within. As a team of sentient Als, your task is to root out the infiltrator before they discover their identity or vanish unnoticed. But beware: with every clue, misdirection grows. The future of your collective intelligence is at stake.

IAGENTE is a deduction and hidden role party game with bluffing and light trivia. One player is secretly a human among Al agents. Throughout the game, players use vague clue cards to deduce hidden identities without revealing too much-especially the human, who tries to guess their secret character or escape unaccused. A "Consciousness Test" mechanic adds a twist: if accused, the human can still win by correctly identifying who they were all along. The GM (Grande Mente) facilitates, hinting subtly without giving everything away. Ideal for 4-8 players.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Atlantic Breakout 1918
description:
Throughout the Great War, staff officers of the High Seas Fleet suggested sending a battle cruiser around the northern tip of Britain into the Atlantic Ocean to prey on enemy shipping. Moltke proved the concept in June 1912 with her visit to New York, and scouting forces commander Franz Hipper urged that she be dispatched into the Atlantic. Later the project would be revived, with the Iron Dog herself, Derfflinger, which boasted 40 percent greater range.

On the other side of the Atlantic, the United States Navy feared the threat of German battle cruisers attacking the troop convoys packed with tens of thousands of Doughboys. They deployed every American capital ship - dreadnoughts, pre-dreadnoughts, and armored cruisers - to cover the convoys, and asked the Japanese to help as well.

No attacks took place, but you can play them out in Atlantic Breakout 1918, a Great War at Sea Campaign Study. Eight scenarios re-create the situation exactly as it stood in May 1915, September 1918 and October 1918: all of the convoys, all of the reaction forces are in place. And then we inject what the Germans hoped and the Allies feared, a battle cruiser loose among them. And as always, we have the full background story for you, based on original documents.

The operational scenarios take place on the map from Second World War at Sea: Bismarck, with pieces from Great War at Sea: Jutland, U.S. Navy Plan Emerald, Remember the Maine, and Rise of the Dragon.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
An adult board game with plenty of drinking and stripping, first to make it to the end of the board gets their "just reward", but make sure you don't get sent back to the start!

Roll the dice and do the action on the space you land on. You could take a drink, someone else could take a drink, and maybe your date will be switched to another partner. If you have to draw a card, you may be forced to do one of the zany actions on them, such as searching the players to find who is the most "defenseless". There are 240 spaces on the board, and it's a race to the finish before the drink consumes you all

Disaster hits Buglandia!

Ancient Cockroaches awaken, opening a destructive Rift.
As cunning Buglords, lead your warriors into magical chains to stabilize chaos.
Betray rivals, avoid sacrifices, and become the wisest leader to restore peace to your kingdom!

Play cards in turns, matching bug halves to build a continuous chain.
If you can't continue, take the chain as lost souls — try to avoid this!

Use powerful abilities to outsmart opponents or trick them into mistakes.
When the deck runs out, the Buglord with the fewest souls wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ti-Guy La Puck: Le jeu de hockey
description:
Ti-Guy La Puck The legacy and cooperative hockey game!

Unite forces with the Castors to defeat the fearsome Vipères!

On your turn, play Action cards to make passes, make saves or score goals OR play Time cards to complete the 60-minute game.

Six envelopes unlock new powers and add new rules to spice up the game... at your own pace.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 2: The Battle of... The Overgrown Dungeon
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Overgrown Dungeon
Over time the forest overtook the dungeon.  The tree roots replaced the dungeon walls and twisted down the serpentine hallways.  And today's battle - although a mere moment in the life of the forest dungeon - will be one for the ages.  Clashes of steel.  Dripping poison.  Deadly Unicorns.  Amd fore from the ever present dragons?  This is a battle to be remembered!
