boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 3: The Battle of... The Burning Lighthouse
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Burning Lighthouse
On the great lava sea there are boats that sail across the molten stone as if it were salt water.  A lone lighthouse protects these ships, and it is for control of that lighthouse the factions are fighting for.  This battle will be a blend of blood and fire!

description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Sunken Pyramid
The pyramid was once more than a hundred meters tall!  After sinking into the swamp, it's only a meter and a half.  So it's not much to look at, but it's at a very strategic point between two cities so everybody is fighting for it.  Heck, even the Yetis came to claim the miniature landmark.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 5: The Battle of... The Upside-Down Monastery
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Upside-Down Monastery
Everything is slightly wrong in this cursed church.  First off, everything is upside.  And, once you enter, you realize what's good is bad, what's right is wrong, and your cat is now a dog.  It is a unforgiving place for a battle, and perhaps that's why only the vile and wicked factions showed up to cross swords.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 6: The Battle of... The Crossing at Two-Peaks
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Crossing at Two-Peaks
The great bridge spans the two peaks, and nobody can agree on who built it.  Was it the giants and their massive strength?  Was it the crafty dwarves?  Or was it a lone Yeti who had too much time on his hands.  Each faction wants credit for the bridge, and now a battle will determine who gets to write its history

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 7: The Battle of... The Hole in the Farmlands
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Hole in the Farmlands
A farmer was going about his day as normal when a pit opened up in the middle of his farm.  What was inside was a horde of zombies, rising from the ground and ready to conquer the land.  Factions from all around joined in the battle - and the winner would control the fertile lands.  The farmer moved to the beach.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 8: The Battle of... The Floating Pyramid
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Floating Pyramid
Legends are told in ancient taverns about the floating pyramid.  They say it was enchanted by an old wizard who lived in the desert.  Truthfully, it was built by a dwarf who did a terrible job at reading the directions.  But, the pyramid is filled with gold so everybody is fighting for it.  The wizards want to keep the legend, the dwarves want credit for building it, and the dragons fly above to take all the treasure they can.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 9: The Battle of... The Tower of Trees
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Tower of Trees
The gnomes feel extra alone in the battle for the tower of trees.  The largest trees in the realm were uprooted and twisted together to form the giant structure.  Only the largest creatures dare to fight for it.  The gnomes will spend their time trying not to get stepped on.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 10: The Battle of... The Brewer's Hut
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Brewer's Hut
Nobody knows what is inside the brewer's famous beverage, but there is certainly magic in the ancient grains he uses.  A battle surrounds the travel between the kingdom's magical factions, and the winner will get to roll home all the barrels of beer they wish.  But, did the battle start because of the magical brew?  Or, was it just a coinless orc who couldn't pay his bar tab?

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 11: The Battle of... The Broken Islands
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The XXXXX
The castle came from the sky.  One day, the giant structure of stone and mortar appeared just above the clouds and plummeted into the shallow waters.  The castle shattered upon impact, forming islands of ruin.  The elves and orcs watched the episode from the shoreline and charged ahead.  They were surprised to find the castle already infested with zombies and werewolves.  Did the castle come from distant horror lands?  Only the seers know, but they are fighting, too!

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 12: The Battle of... The Melting Fortress
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Melting Fortress
This battle is for a stone fortress covered in ice.  The frostbeasts are fighting to keep the fortress encased in ice,, while the demons are trying violently to melt it and take it for their owns.  Others wage war, too, looking for an opportunistic time to take ahold and claim victory.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 13: The Battle of... The Seaside Torch
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Seaside Torch
The lighthouse is the tallest in the entire realm.  It stands over three hundred meters tall, with a maze of staircases leading up to its ten meter flame.  Other, smaller lighthouses jut from the side of the tower with their own giant flames.  This battle is waged up and down the stairs, as the factions fight for control of the critical control point.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 14: The Battle of... The Castle at Hightop
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Castle at Hightop
Watch the edges!  The castle at hightop only has a meter of room around the walls before the shee cliffs start.  Each faction fighter must be very careful as they trying to claim control over the castle.  A single misstep could mean death!  And, it doesn't help that the ice kings and queens are leaving ice along the edges.  An added complication is the tricksters and shapeshifters - it's hard decide who to trust!  What a dangerous situation!

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 15: The Battle of... The Inn at the Top of the World
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Inn at the Top of the World
Before the fighting started, the inn would get a few travelers a week.  It is, after all, set atop the highest peak in all the realm.  But, with the ability to see for miles and miles around, the factions know that this was valuable real estate!  Now the factions are fighting over the tiny, two-bedroom inn.  The looming Shadow faction couldn't even fit inside!



Referenced in the Claim Anniversary Edition rulebook, Claim Scenario Cards are used to tell a story and set up a battle.Sold directly from White Goblin Games, this scenario contains a short narrative along with preselected factions & locations from multiple products within the Claim family, requiring all items listed under "Expands" to use*.

A table displaying all such information for this and other Claim Scenario Cards is available for viewing in the Claim Scenario Card List.

*Claim Anniversary Edition is NOT required.  However, it contains Claim 2 and so it can be used in its place.

F1 Racing Simulation is a dice-driven lap-by-lap F1 simulation. The unique feature of this simulation is that each driver does not race on a traditional track or board, but on their own individual card on your tabletop!

Available seasons: 1951, 1960s (1963-1966), 1976, 1981, 1986, 1990, Late 1990s (1995-2000), 2003, 2008, 2024 and 2025.

Please do not purchase our simulation if you are looking for a game where you control a car or influence the outcome through strategic decisions. Although each race includes a few, sometimes important, decisions, the race itself runs on its own. This is a simulation - like watching a race on TV, where your favourite drivers compete realistically against each other on various tracks!

Each driver has five fixed driving attributes and one flexible one. These are activated depending on the track and race progress, triggering an unpredictable race event. Everything that defines this racing series is represented in this simulation.

During the race, individual drivers are occasionally presented with a choice between two options — for example, during the start phase, pit stops, or overtaking maneuvers. These choices are few, but can sometimes be strategically decisive. However, there is also the option to not intervene in the race at all and simply watch the race unfold.

Qualifying and Pre-Race need about 10 minutes, the race about 40 minutes. If you're playing the game for the first time, it might take twice as long.

The game includes all drivers and tracks from the 2024 season. With seven different drivers taking multiple wins and four teams winning races, the grid saw renewed competitiveness.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 16: The Battle of... The Church of Ice
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Church of Ice
Today's agenda is the ice of kings and queens versus the phoenix fire!  Their relentless fighting has disturbed the undead, cause them to rise from the catacombs and fight for their own territory.  The poisoners will fight, but hang back.  After all, many of the undead became that way from their poisonous vials!



Referenced in the Claim Anniversary Edition rulebook, Claim Scenario Cards are used to tell a story and set up a battle.Sold directly from White Goblin Games, this scenario contains a short narrative along with preselected factions & locations from multiple products within the Claim family, requiring all items listed under "Expands" to use*.

*Claim Anniversary Edition is NOT required.  However, it contains Claim 1 and so it can be used in its place.

boardgame: Claim Scenario Card 17: The Battle of... The Volcanic Castle
description:
Narrative from Scenario Card:
The Battle of... The Volcanic Castle
There is no shadow in a field made of fire, so there is no hiding from the creatures of flame.  Still, they were surprised to see the brave Ice Kings and Ice Queens appear to battle in such an unforgiving lands.  Could the Ice Kings and Queens conquer the lands before their fortitude melts?



Referenced in the Claim Anniversary Edition rulebook, Claim Scenario Cards are used to tell a story and set up a battle.Sold directly from White Goblin Games, this scenario contains a short narrative along with preselected factions & locations from multiple products within the Claim family, requiring all items listed under "Expands" to use.

A table displaying all such information for this and other Claim Scenario Cards is available for viewing in the Claim Scenario Card List.

Witches' Revel is an expandable card game for 2 players, where two witches fill the field with spells that shift, smash and blow opposing spells right out of the sky… until one of the witches taps out, and then whoever has filled the field with the best spells wins the game. It's a game that condenses competitive card game tropes in a smaller game package that's all about sequencing.

To play, players bring a witch card and 25-card spell decks; the game is played in short turns, where a player will draw a card, play a card (or skip that play, drawing an extra card in the process), and then use the Powers of their cards on the field before passing.

Spell cards are played in one of the five spaces the field is divided in, each able to hold only 1 spell for each player, and the offensive spells cannot be normally removed from a space once played, meaning that the tempo of these 1-play turns and the availability of space left of the board become the main resources a player must manage to effectively play the game.

Players can also improve their spells by stitching one atop another, making both cards into a larger, more threatening spell. Larger spells overpower (discard) smaller ones, stopping the opponent from winning the space or forcing them to change their plans as a key piece of their synergy is removed. However, this, and other plays through the game, can sap a witch's Stamina, which does not replenish. When a player ends the turn with no Stamina, the opponent will get one more turn to try and turn the tide, getting to make bonus plays on that final turn depending on the Stamina they saved, and then the player who has the most powerful spells in a majority of spaces wins the game!

—description from the designer

Lone Wolf the Skirmish Game lets players recreate the epic battles set in the world of Magnamund.  Wave 1 celebrates the 40th anniversary of the Giak Attack Box Set - including the likes of Lone Wolf, Crown Prince Pelathar and his retinue Sommlending Army against the might Dark Forces of Helgedad! - The dreaded Gourgaz commands his Giak units along with the fearsome Drakkarim.

Combining tactical round & turn based gameplay, unit cards for stats and special moves along with custom dice and hex battle map.

Players elect how many unit points to build their side (1 for infantry / 3 for Brutes or Leaders) Combat dice are rolled to see which player/s go in what order.  Round proceeds with players taking it in turns to move (hexes) and attack by selecting a target - close and ranged combat dependant on the unit abilities (using movement points and/or dice & combat dice to attack and/or defend) Players can also choose to bank certain dice rolls for later use.

The game concludes when the last player's unit is standing for the win.

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Cards Against Humanity: Science Pack 2 is a 30 card Cards Against Humanity expansion that supports the annual Science Ambassador Scholarship for undergraduate women and non-binary students in science, technology, engineering, or math.

As of June 2025, this pack is available for purchase at Cards Against Humanity's online store packaged with the Contact party game.

Unfold the mat, grab the sausage roll, and get ready for fun with LadBaby’s hilarious game! Perfect for families, teams take turns rolling a quirky foam sausage roll down a runway, aiming to land as close to the end as possible without going off. Points are scored based on where it lands, and each spot involves completing silly sausage roll-related challenges. From singing with the sausage roll as a microphone to balancing it on one leg, the game guarantees laughs and fun for all ages. The team with the longest sausage roll at the end wins!

—description from the publisher
This promotional pack is produced by Devir and contains 10 cards with 20 new words in Portuguese.

Created by Fabio Tola and Fel Barros, Offline Fun: The Game brings a very familiar setting for those who attend DOFF: a group of friends goes on a trip through the event, collecting games of their favorite type — but be careful: they have to fit in the backpack!

They can also take part in panels, paint miniatures, or join a tournament… as long as they’re careful not to get separated from each other.

The game recreates the experience of a board game enthusiast at the Diversão Offline event. In it, players take on the role of visitors trying to make the most of their time at the convention.

Each player has a character with specific attributes and a hand of cards that simulate activities such as trying out new games, producing content, forming partnerships, or simply having fun.

Over a number of rounds (representing event days), players will move across different spaces on the board, perform actions, interact with other players, and strategically play their cards to accumulate Prestige Points (PP).

At the end of the game, the player with the most Prestige Points is declared the winner — the most prestigious visitor of the event!

Phenlin hasn't slept since he read the scroll. Visions tear at his slumber as his mind tries in vain to make sense from the non-sensical. But they are all coming true, these visions of his.......

Dungeonrunner is a solitaire card game inspired by classic pen and paper fantasy RPGs. The player takes a hero on a quest through their deck of 54 cards. Each adventure deck is filled with danger and peril as the player guides the hero through hazardous traps, countless monsters, and dreaded curses.

Each turn, you play a card from the adventure deck. It may be a monster, trap, event, or item. Once that card is resolved by combat or your decisions, you draw the next card. If you can make it to the quest card for the given scenario, the hero completes the adventure!

Your ship has been attacked! Fight off the scoundrels, capture mercenaries to turn them into allies, and travel across the galaxy in search of the missing parts to repair your ship and make it out of this adventure unscathed.

Using dice, create addition and subtraction equations whose results will allow you to land on different planets and discover what lies hidden there.

2 to 6 players find themselves in a flea market looking to complete sets of 3 types of objects: toys, clothes and tech. But beware of the dangers of thrifting your items may come with mold or bugs and sometimes there’s a thief around the corner.

In each of the 3 rounds of the game you set up 20 cards in one of the 3 configurations suggested in the rules and begin a open draft, after the cards are gone each player count their points and begin a new round (but keep your items from the previous rounds)

The player with the best score wins.

—description from the designer

Cistude is an abstract game for 2 players aged 10 and over, for games lasting from 10 to 30 minutes.

You are 2 naturalists in charge of the census of European CISTUDES, a freshwater turtle protected species, of the Mer Rouge pond in Brenne (Indre, France).

Be the first to spot a specimen, or the one who counts the most of specimens once you’ve explored all the banks of the pond.

A specimen is drawn using 4 two-colors hexagons recto 
verso (2/3 of one color an 1/3 of the other and the complementary pattern on the other side).

The game is divided into in 2 phases : in the first phase player will create a pavage with 32 hexagons. If in this phase a player create a turtle he will win by sudden death. In a second phase player will modify the pavage to draw the maximum of turtle. Players with the highest number of turtle win the game.

—description from the designer

You are a group of wanderers who have entered the Grid of Shadows, a mysterious and enchanted realm where bluffing, sabotage, and deception rule.

To escape this treacherous grid you take turns placing cards face-down on a shared grid, boldly declaring what they’ve played… or bluffing through their teeth. Some tell the truth. Others don't. The only certainty? Sooner or later, someone will call "Lights!" — forcing a dramatic reveal that could punish the bluffer or backfire on the accuser.

Every card carries a unique ability. Some abilities activate if you’re the first to play to a pile, others if you’re the last — timing is critical. When the draw pile is empty, the game ends, and players total the value of the cards left in their hands.

—description from the publisher

Arcane Engines is a racing game designed by Dragonmount Press,  set in a fantasy world where teams of elves, dwarves, gnomes, humans, orcs, goblins and potato people race each other in high speed arcane chariots for glory.

Players take up the roles of a crew aboard a magical chariot pulled by an arcane engine. They have to face different traps and unique hazards such as small enemies and bosses on the different racecourses, such as a snowy-mountain peak, deep dwarven mine, magical elven forest, racing colosseum and more, while they gather treasures and race for the finish line.

The game can be played by 1-7 players. It’s competitive by default, but can be played as a co-op game and it has both one- and two-handed solo modes. There are 2 playstyles: race or championship. A race takes ~20-35 minutes. A championship takes 120+ minutes. Races are won by whoever gets 1st place, but championships are won by the player who collects the most Gold Pieces.

—description from the publisher

Rubble Town is a card-based game designed to be played solo in ~20 minutes, probably over a beer and competing with friends and mates to get the best score. The aim of the game is to introduce players to some of the key features of urban combat in an engaging way.

Rubble Town allow players to explore the different trade-offs of urban combat, such as deciding how much they will rely on using heavy weapons to help ensure success – but at the cost of increasing the rubblization of the town, or trading time to avoid casualties. The random street and event cards will provide different challenges on each turn, and introduce players to an array of urban issues including combined arms, mouse-holing, direct and indirect fire support, civilians and the media.

Rubble Town comes in WW2 and Modern versions, the latter allowing you to use UAVs, UGVs, LAWs and other more modern equipment.

—description from the designer

Rock Girls is a fast-paced party card game themed around rock bands, designed for 2 to 4 players.
In the game, players take on the role of band leaders, drawing cards, playing them, and positioning musicians on stage to cleverly trigger unique skill effects and compete for the highest score. Each round is filled with tactical decisions and a touch of luck, with endless possibilities from different musician combinations.

boardgame: CookieRun: Braverse Trading Card – Brave Beginning
description:
CookieRun Braverse Trading Card Game is a trading card game based on the Korean mobile game IP CookieRun.
In this 2-player game, players use a 60-cards deck composed of Cookie cards, Item cards, Trap cards and Stage cards to compete against their opponent. The goal is to defeat your opponent's cookies. Each cookie card has a Level (1, 2, or 3) and a number of Health Points (usually 1 to 6). The cookie will faint if their HP is reduced to 0, and placed in their owner's Break area. When a player has cookies in their Break area whose combined levels are equal to 10 or more, they lose the game

This set includes 159 regular + 44 hidden cards total (63 Common, 45 Uncommon, 25 Rare, 17 Super Rare, 7 Ultra Rare).

Each pack contains 5 cards out of a possible 159. A single pack is not enough to play the game. This pack may contain duplicates, and you are not guaranteed to get the card(s) you want.

Wür.D.E. is short for "Would you rather..." but it also stands for the word "would," because in this game, players are placed in two extreme situations: sexy, dangerous, embarrassing, and disgusting. They must choose one. The other players then discuss and decide on a situation. Whoever is right wins a point. Then it's the next player's turn. The game ends after a certain number of rounds.
If necessary, you can immediately put various things into action to support your decision. You can also specify the situations.
A party game for all ages.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Chef Class
description: Make the most of the many consumables in The Elder Scrolls with the Chef Class! A new class for your adventurer to take on that utilizes food in new and exciting ways. This pack features a Chef Class card, a new legendary consumable, and several new side quests that focus on food.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description:
FRYSK is a game in which 1 to 6 players create their own version of the northern Frisian province of the Netherlands. Gameplay is family-friendy, and about optimizing the puzzle, as each type of landscape tile scores victory points in different ways. You develop a landscape by turning over a tile, which generates income and offers the opportunity to construct buildings for even more victory points. Currency in this game is water, accentuating the importance of the waterways and lakes in the landscape.

While the game mainly aims to entertain, the types of landscape and their development are grounded in history. FRYSK is the native language of the region the the game will be release bi-lingual, in Dutch and Frisian (and online English rules).

The game will be available in retail selectively and at 42 participating museums which each offer a unique scenario card that expands the game play (and even can be combined together). A 43rd scenario card will be broadly distributed, which makes use of of some the of the extra tiles included in the game.

FRYSK is in the same line as GRUNN. While it similarly is about making your own tableau with tiles, collected from a central market, it has a few subtle differences that change the puzzle and includes more unique tiles linked to new scenario cards.

—description from the designer

Hank Maartens, the leader of a local renaissance festival, was found murdered and half-burned in the iron smith’s forge. The police have a suspect in custody but Artemis Troy, a volunteer at the faire, believes something else is afoot. She needs your help in uncovering hidden conspiracies and catch the real killer before they escape.

boardgame: Heroes of the Shire: Fire & Ice
description:
Heroes of the Shire is a turn-based combat game where players cast spells, use abilities, and learn combos to defeat their enemies. With 20 characters, 12 bosses, and over 800 spells and abilities, no two games will ever be the same.

There are 2 main game modes: Arena & Scenario.

Arena Mode: Players battle it out in the arena to become victorious. There are many different arena modes, with the most common being free-for-all and teams.

Scenario Mode: A cooperative mode where players travel through a themed scenario, defeating bosses and trash mobs via an adventure tile system. One pre-selected player (Villain) will defend the enemies, by taking actions for each enemy unit. Each scenario contains 3 epic boss battles which can last anywhere from 1.5 to 4 hours. Players can adjust the difficulty of a boss at any stage in the game, and players can join or leave a game as they see fit. Bosses' stats can be modified even mid-battle. A saved game sheet is available for those lengthy adventures that can't be completed in one evening.

—description from the designer

This Fire & Ice box contains the following 10 heroes: Cleric, Hunter, Paladin, Warlock, War Drummer, Berserker, Runesmith, Illusionist, Alchemist and Musketeer.

boardgame: Heroes of the Shire: Earth & Water
description:
Heroes of the Shire is a turn-based combat game where players cast spells, use abilities, and learn combos to defeat their enemies. With 20 characters, 12 bosses, and over 800 spells and abilities, no two games will ever be the same.

There are 2 main game modes: Arena & Scenario.

Arena Mode: Players battle it out in the arena to become victorious. There are many different arena modes, with the most common being free-for-all and teams.

A saved game sheet is available for those lengthy adventures that can't be completed in one evening.

—description from the designer

This Earth & Water box contains the following 10 heroes: Druid, Assassin, Witch Doctor, Blood Mage, Warrior, Dragon Knight, Shaman, Engineer, Scholar and Bard.

description:
Heroes of the Shire is a turn-based combat game where players cast spells, use abilities, and learn combos to defeat their enemies. With 20 characters, 12 bosses, and over 800 spells and abilities, no two games will ever be the same.

There are 2 main game modes: Arena & Scenario.

Arena Mode: Players battle it out in the arena to become victorious. There are many different arena modes, with the most common being free-for-all and teams.

Scenario Mode: A cooperative mode where players travel through a themed scenario, defeating bosses and trash mobs via an adventure tile system. One pre-selected player (Villain) will defend the enemies, by taking actions for each enemy unit. Each scenario contains 3 epic boss battles which can last anywhere from 1.5 to 4 hours. Players can adjust the difficulty of a boss at any stage in the game, and players can join or leave a game as they see fit. Bosses' stats can be modified even mid-battle. A saved game sheet is available for those lengthy adventures that can't be completed in one evening.

—description from the designer

The base Light & Shadow box contains the following 6 heroes: Beastmaster, Clairvoyant, Hydromancer, Light Mage, Mercenary, and Shadow Knight.

4 additional heroes were added as an addon in the Gamefound campaign: Enchantress, Mad Scientist, Necromancer and Swashbuckler.

Combine your little cards to score the most points. Take advantage of special little cards to boost your play area.

At the end of the game, whoever has the most points wins the game to free up the cards.

Cartitas (Little Cards) is a game that you can play with its base mode or with one of the modules. You can combine modules for great replayability. The challenges change when you add or combine modules.

On your turn: play a card from your hand to your play area, if you meet the card's condition. Play special cards to score extra points.

Build your play area to score the most points at the end of the game.

Chain of Command 2 is final edition of the Award winning WWII Platoon level rule set, the first edition of which won the Wargames Illustrated Best Set of Wargame Rules Award consistently over its thirteen year lifespan. The Second edition build on the original to provide all the command challenges of the original but with a more dynamic structure, meaning that you are always in the heart of the action.

Chain of Command’s unique command and control system combines with the Patrol Phase to place players on the empty battlefield and allows them to make plausible command choices as they lead their force forward to, we hope victory.    
As one reknowned playtester said, “This is Chain of command on Steroids. I love it!”

At 114 pages, the rules include a scenario generation system and Army Lists for Britain, Germany, the United States and Soviet Union. from 1944 to 1945, allowing the gamer to select support depending on the scenario being played, so you can pit two well matched forces against each other for a fun and challenging gaming experience. The rules are designed to work with the existing Chain if Command supplements, campaigns and Handbooks.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Chain of Command: The Far East 1941-45
description:
The book is a 120-page handbook that provides comprehensive coverage of the campaigns in Malaya, Hong Kong, Burma and the Dutch East Indies. 
Included is an historical overview of the campaigns, as well as a whole raft of new rules to cover the events and terrain in the Far Eastern Theatre.

The handbook includes nearly fifty individual army lists with support lists for each one. As in the 1940 handbook, a comprehensive guide to the period is provided. In addition, this handbook offers a comprehensive range of national characteristics and rules for jungle training that allow the gamer to reflect the tactics of the period.

The lists are as follows:

Britain, Dominions and Empire


     British Infantry Platoon 1941-42
     British Garrison Platoon 1941-42
     Indian Army Platoon 1941-42
     Indian ‘Milked’ Platoon 1941-42
     Australian Rifle Platoon 1941-42
     Australian Blackforce Platoon, Java 1942
     Canadian Infantry Platoon 1941 (Hong Kong)
     Hong Kong Volunteer Defence Corps Platoon, 1941
     Malaya Regiment Platoon 1941-42
     Burma Rifles Platoon 1941-42
     Volunteer Defence Corps 1941-42
     Indian Army Platoon 1943
     British Infantry Platoon 1943
     “Commando” Platoon 1943-44
     Chindit Platoon 1943
     Burma Rifles Platoon 1943
     British Infantry Platoon 1944-45
     Indian Army Platoon 1944-45
     Indian Motor Rifles Platoon 1944-45
     Indian Parachute Platoon 1944-45
     Tribal Levies 1944-45
     African Infantry Platoon 1944-45
     Assam Rifles Platoon 1943-45
     Chindit Platoon 1944
     Chindit Reconnaissance Platoon 1944
     Army Command Section 1944-45
     Royal Marine Commando Platoon 1944-45
     RAF Regiment Flight 1944-45
     Armour Support List


Dutch East Indies


     KNIL Field Force Platoon
     KNIL Garrison Platoon
     KNIL Marechaussee Platoon
     KNIL Cavalry Platoon
     Cavalry Reconnaissance Troop
     Marine Platoon


Japanese Empire


     Infantry Platoon 1941-42
     Cavalry Reconnaissance Platoon 1941-43
     Motorised Reconnaissance Platoon 1941-43
     Special Naval Landing Party 1941-43
     Army Parachute Platoon 1942
     SNLF Parachute Platoon 1942
     Infantry Platoon 1943
     Infantry Platoon 1944-45
     Commando Platoon 1945
     Line of Communication Guard Platoon 1943-45
     Line of Communication Mobile Platoon 1943-45
     Indian National Army Platoon 1944-45


Alongside these lists we have nearly fifty support option lists giving support choices for a huge number of unit types, so if you have an Indian Infantry Platoon, you can select support from other friendly Empire units. All in all, the gamer can choose an incredible range of interesting and varied troop-types and then choose supports from friendly forces and allies for forces of almost limitless variety. But that’s not all! Each nation has a full Arsenal list for weapons and AFVs as well as comprehensive notes to cover their support options.

Finally, we have four new scenarios to introduce the gamer to this new and exciting theatre of war: The Roadblock, The Road North, Demolition Derby and Fight for the Chaung.

—description from the designer

boardgame: So You Want to Be an American?
description:
So you want to be an American? You'll have to navigate the U.S. immigration system first, with all of its twists, turns, and obstacles. Each player's path through the game is their own; there is no competition between players to get to the end. Everyone has the same goal: to become a U.S. citizen. Learn what it takes to get there, and decide for yourself: Does this system work?

This game is designed for people 12 and up, and is specifically recommended for those with birthright or inherited U.S. citizenship. It is a reflection of the system in real life, in the probability of success, time spent on the journey (1 turn = 1 year), unanticipated events, and real citizenship test questions. The game has been vetted by people who have experienced the U.S. immigration system firsthand within the last 30 years. It is a primer on how the legal immigration system works, and promotes empathy for the ways in which different identity factors can affect how challenging and how long of a process different people have to go through to become citizens. The game is designed to be played multiple times, with different starting identity characteristics each time.

So You Want to Be an American? is for 1-5 players, and is ideal for families, classrooms, or educational programs seeking to inspire civic engagement.

—description from the designer

Autumn has arrived, and colorful leaves litter the ground. As a collector, this is your favorite time of year. Gather the halves of leaves, and assemble them in your albums. Create the most impressive collection to become the envy of your peers!

Folia Folio is a set-collection game that uses the structure of a may-follow trick-taking game. Players form albums by connecting left and right sides of matching leaves and matching numbers. The longer your chain of matches in one album, the more points you score.

Hone your skills with this unique immersive golf game! Apex Golf combines precision and strategy, in the same way as a real golf course, the Royal Sandmark Golf Club. With its stunning graphics, its challenges, and the skills you will have to earn, each stroke will impact the final outcome, just like in a real golf game. Golf enthusiasts will find a familiar and rewarding experience, while newcomers can explore the world of golf in an immersive way.

—description from the publisher

A card-playing game based on the classic "Sevens", where players seek to complete runs of up to 5 colours in numerical order from their hand. Once a player exhausts their hand, the round ends, and players count the points remaining in their hand of unplayed cards. The winner is the player with the fewest points after 10 rounds.

This expansion expands the base game's maximum players from 4 to 6.

The box contains 2 additional player boards, 40+ additional tiles, additional currency, and all the other components to allow the base board game to be played with up to 6 players. There is also a rulebook which updates some of the rules to enable playing with 6 players.

This also works with all other expansion packs.

boardgame: Computer Junkyard: Lightning Strike Expansion Pack
description:
Uh-oh! A major lightning storm has just rolled in!
If you draw a lightning bolt there's a good chance something on your computer is about to be blown up. Unless, of course, you bought a surge protector!

This expansion includes 7 new power supply tiles, 6 lightning bolt tiles, 5 surge protector tiles, a 10-sided die, and a rule book.

This expansion is designed to work with the 6-player expansion.

boardgame: Computer Junkyard: Peripheral Hardware Expansion Pack
description:
Need a printer? Or how about an old modem?
Players will need to install an extra peripheral to their computer in order to win!

This expansion includes 8 interface cards, 7 peripheral cards, and a rule book.

This expansion is designed to work with the 6-player expansion.

boardgame: Computer Junkyard: Golden Bugs Expansion Pack
description:
This kit adds a fun variety to the game play.
There are a set of variations included to incorporate the golden bugs, but the only limit is your imagination!

This expansion contains 12 golden computer bugs and a rule book.

This expansion is designed to work with the 6-player expansion.

The Junior expansion uses the core mechanics of the base game, with a simplified set of rules, and some kid-friendly tiles. Designed for 8 - 13 year olds, a fun booklet is included to help the younger audience learn more about what's inside a basic computer.

This expansion contains 10 computer ladybug tiles, 6 kid-friendly named software tiles, 1 computer booklet, and a rule book.

This expansion is designed to work with the 6-player expansion.

This expansion includes 3 unique software tiles ("Plant Hunters", "Kickstarter: the Game", and "Check-Os"), only available to Kickstarter backers.

This expansion is designed to work with the 6-player expansion.

Tour de Colligo is where you’ll experience the thrill of competitive cycling! Just like in real-world races, teamwork, strategy, and stamina are key to victory.

Whether you're a seasoned cyclist or have never touched a bike, this game is easy to pick up. Depending on your experience, this game can last 60-90 minutes, and with adjustable 3D challenge zones, each playthrough offers new strategies and replayability.

Your goal is to guide your team of four cyclists to the finish line and earn points for their finishing position on the podium. The player whose team has the highest overall combined points wins!

You'll need smart strategies to win, but you are also encouraged to work with your opponents in order to progress quicker! Expect to form alliances... or experience betrayals.

—description from the designer

Epiko Valley immerses 2–4 players in a mythic world drawn from Indian legends, where four distinct castles—Jamvanta, Kishkindha, Jatayu, and Naga—await reconstruction. Richly illustrated terrains like Winter, Magic, Heaven, and Hell set the stage for strategic decisions tied to cultural lore, with Yama, the god of death and justice, ever-present to challenge players’ choices.

On each turn, players draw a strategy card, roll dice to move their avatar, and resolve the action on the tile landed upon—gaining gold, collecting life elements, or facing hazards. Simultaneously, Yama advances, creating tension that forces careful balancing of risk and reward. Collecting the five elemental tokens grants extra lives, while gold management and clever card play enable progress or defensive maneuvers. The interplay of terrain paths, resource decisions, and unpredictable card effects ensures every session unfolds differently.

Victory is achieved by completing your chosen castle puzzle or outlasting all opponents. Players must weave together tactical movement, resource management, and timely use of strategy cards to build their fortress while evading Yama’s pursuit. With its fusion of cultural storytelling and dynamic mechanics, Epiko Valley offers an engaging journey where every decision shapes a unique legend.

—description from the designer

A very special edition to celebrate our 15 year anniversary as a board game publisher. For 14 years, White Goblin Games has published the best card, dice and board games and we provide 'Happy Faces Everywhere'.

Met deze bijzondere editie van Qwixx vieren we ons 15-jarig bestaan als spellenuitgever. Al
15 jaar brengt White Goblin Games de leukste kaart-, dobbel- en bordspellen op de markt en
zorgen we voor ‘Happy Faces Everywhere‘!

This mini-expansion adds 4 new buildings (PR 1-4):

     3 Passive Building tiles
     1 Income Building tile


Changes in setup: Before resolving Player Setup, add the new Starting Buildings to the respective stack.

Tianxia: New Ships promo adds 2 ship tiles and 3 demand tiles to deepen strategic options. Shuffle them into your game setup for fresh challenges and variety.

This mini-expansion adds:

     2 Ship tiles
     3 Demand tiles

Druid Path is a solo monster-taming card game where you step into the role of an ancient Druid, wielding the primal forces of nature to tame Beastborgs—cyber-organic creatures forged from magic and machine. Your mission: stop the Darachs, rogue Druids corrupted by forbidden technology who seek to control these powerful beings for their own dark ends.

How to Play:

Choose your Path, select your Druid, and face off against powerful Darachs. You have just 10 turns to defeat them before corruption consumes everything.

Each turn, move along your Path to gain stamina and encounter new Beastborgs, magical items, or threats. To tame a Beastborg, collect the right gemstones and roll the dice to meet their tame value. Use item cards to boost your chances—but fail, and the Darach takes control of them, activating their dangerous shadow abilities.

Throughout your journey, you'll collect magical items and deploy robot allies to support your strategy. But beware—every item has a shadow side the Darach can twist and use against you.

With limited stamina and time, you'll need to carefully manage resources, create powerful combos, and make every move count. Can you restore balance before the darkness takes hold?

—description from the designer

The quickest bird rules the roost in this fast-flying card game! Race to get rid of all your numbered bird food cards before the other players. Fend off sneaky squirrels, chase down wiggly worms, and build cozy birdhouses—while keeping up with the frantic, free-for-all fun! It’s energetic, it’s wild, and it’s every bird for themselves.

Play is simultaneous. Players aim to get rid of their cards by playing a card that is one number higher than the one on the discard pile... or by playing an identical number and shouting 'down' to change to descending numbers. But when a player plays a special action card, everyone must stop and do the action before play continues. The first player to successfully complete the action restarts play with any card. Get all your cards out to win.

—description from the designer

After countless sprints, late-night deploys, and one too many retros, tech workers finally have a way to process their burnout: by weaponizing it.

In PLAI, each player becomes the founder of a tech startup trying to outlast their rivals in a brutal landscape of buzzwords, funding rounds, market events, and hostile takeovers. Players must fund their operations, steal other companies' assets, and defend their shaky roadmap with every card in the deck. If your startup collapses, don’t worry—you’ll join the Open Source Project, where defeated founders can still band together to take down the last startup standing.

Built on a 106 card deck, PLAI features fast-paced rounds for 2 to 5 players. Each turn, players either draw Funding cards to grow their resources, play a card or launch a hostile takeover in that mirrors the absurdity of real-world tech culture.

Playable in under 30 minutes, PLAI is a game for developers, founders, and product teams who’ve lived through sprint hell, priority changes, and side project drama—and who are finally ready to laugh about it.

—description from the designer

By adding Módulo Caóticas (Chaotic module), cards will appear that will complicate the game.

If you like a bit of chaos in your games, this is the module for your playgroup.

You can add "chaotic" cards along with other modules.

Each chaotic card has 3 effects depending on whether it is the first, second, or third chaotic card revealed.
This card is a wild card. You can use it as any of the four colors: yellow, green, purple, or light blue. You can also use it as any of the four types of cards: marked, lucky, magic, or special.

Hey you! Yes, you!

Are you confident enough to claim that you know all each other's hobbies and embarrassing stories? Each other's dreams and each other's fears? We bet you don't! Play this game and gasp in suprise, roll on the floor laughing and have a great night that brings you closer together. Whether you play it with your new friends, old friends, colleagues or well-known family members, we guarantee that you will learn something new about each other.

In this game, you take turns placing 3 cards with characteristics or experiences in front of you, after which the rest have to guess which ones apply to you. After you reveal what's true and what is not, the other players will be curious about the stories about why! Whoever can best deduce what the other people are truly like, wins. Will that be you?

—description from the publisher

Pssst jij daar!

Durf jij te zeggen dat je al elkaars hobby's en gênante verhalen kent? Elkaars dromen en elkaars angsten? Wedden van niet! Speel dit spel en schreeuw van verbazing, lach je rot en heb een mooie avond. Of je het nu speelt met je nieuwe vrienden, oude vrienden, collega's of alom bekende familieleden, wij garanderen je dat je iets nieuws leert over elkaar.

In het spel leg je omstebeurten 3 kaarten met eigenschappen of ervaringen voor je neer, waarna de rest moet raden welke op jou van toepassing zijn. Iedereen is daarna nieuwsgierig naar het verhaal erachter! Wie het beste de anderen kan inschatten, wint. Zal jij dat zijn?

—description from the publisher (Dutch)

10 new images, created by the Scottish artist Tracy Hall,
to be added to those present in the Kaleidos box, for an even more exciting game experience !

Team Quack is a cooperative game where sharp observation and clever hints are your keys to victory!

Work together to convey secret images using subtle clues and fun noises. Each round, you'll try to gauge how clear your hints are and predict how easily your teammates can guess your secret card.
Be careful, as too many wrong guesses will cost you valuable lives. Aim for the medal playing as many rounds as possible!

1. Choose image cards that describe your Secret Card best. 
Draft the image cards you don't use.

2. Share with your team how obvious your pick is!

3.Can you match the Secret Cards to the correct players…or will you guess wrong and lose a life token?

—description from the publisher

In a distant realm where fairies once flourished, darkness has dimmed the magic of the world. Only the power of enchanted crystals can restore the forgotten beauty of the Fairy Garden.

In Jardim Encantado ("Enchanted Garden"), players compete to collect and use colorful crystals to explore magical garden locations, gaining points and unlocking bonuses. Each turn, players play cards in trick-style challenges to acquire the best crystals for their tableau. These crystals are then spent to acquire garden cards of increasing power and value, which provide bonuses for future turns and contribute to endgame scoring.

As the game progresses, players race to unlock powerful Fairy cards by meeting specific conditions, gaining significant exploration points. The game ends when a player reaches 12 points or when the third Fairy is claimed. The player with the most exploration points is declared the guardian of the restored garden.

With clever hand management, timing, and tactical choices, Jardim Encantado offers an elegant blend of trick-taking, tableau building, and set collection, all wrapped in a whimsical fantasy theme.

—description from the designer

The cult reflex game meets an iconic universe! Jungle Speed Donkey Kong is a fast-paced, hilarious twist on the classic game of observation, with a banana-filled mechanic that brings Donkey Kong’s chaotic energy to the table.

Players flip cards simultaneously. When two identical symbols appear, a duel begins! If the banana totem is on the barrel, grab the big banana and remove it.If the banana totem is off, grab a small banana and toss it into the barrel. First to react wins the round!

—description from the publisher

A rare version of the classic board game Cluedo, made in Greece by ΕΠΑ toy company. The game was released in the 70s and the location that the action takes place is the city of Athens.

It can be played from 3-6 players. Players move to different areas of Athens trying to solve the mystery of: who done it, with what and in which area of the city. 
Players are dealt character, weapon, and location cards after the top card from each card type is secretly placed in the confidential file in the middle of the board.

Players must move to an area and then make a suggestion against a character saying they did it in that area with a specific weapon. The player to the left must show one of any cards mentioned if in that player's hand. Through deductive reasoning each player must figure out which character, weapon, and location are in the secret file. To do this, each player must uncover what cards are in other players hands by making more and more suggestions.

Once a player knows what cards the other players are holding, they will know what cards are in the secret file, and then make an accusation. If correct, the player wins, but if incorrect, the player must return the cards to the file without revealing them and may no longer make suggestions or accusations.

Bug - The Old Army Game

The game consists of two dice with "body" "eye" "tail" "head" "feelers" and "leg" on the sides.  Players roll both dice to complete a full "bug" which consists of 17 parts - 1 head, 1 body, 1 tail, 2 feelers, 2 eyes, and 10 legs.  Each piece of the bug is worth 17 points.  Whoever completes their bug first wins the difference between 17 and whatever the other players lack.  Play 5 games and winner is player with highest total.

Game comes like a book, with the 2 wooden dice hidden in the spine and the pages of the book are score sheets with 4 columns and 5 rows.

Game of skill for two or more players.

Players place a die in a bucket at the top of a pole that has a target on the bottom.  They then shoot a marble at the target to drop the die onto the playing board.  The player then receives points equal to the total of the die and the number of the square the die fell into.

If die does not fall, player only receives points for number on the die.

Winner is player with highest score.

Solitri is a cooperative card game for 2~4 players. On your turn, you either play a card or discard a card, and then draw a card. Players will win if the following 3 conditions are met: there are 3 rows of 1~8, all the tokens have been removed, all the objective cards are completed. Players will lose if the discard pile overflows or the above-mentioned conditions are not fulfilled when a player wants to draw a card but cannot.

—description from the designer

Kvass is a domino trick-taking game for 2-3 players inspired by the game 1000.

Players bid to determine how many points they think they can take in a round, bidding between 100 and 400 points.

The game also features a marriage system which will change the trump suit mid-game.

To play you need one standard set of dominoes.

—description from the designer
Mini-extension contains 10 special cards that you can use to devastate or support other players.
A mini extension contains 40 character slices so that eliminated players can still be part of the game.

Allows up to 12 players to enjoy the game.

A huge addition to the base version of the game, introducing 55 game cards, 6 dice, and 4 heroes (Hero, Thermos, Coco, and Glutton) who eat regular Tootheads for breakfast.

In 2012 the state of Colorado voted to make the recreational sale of marijuana legal. While for a few years Colorado had made the sale of marijuana available for medical customers, now EVERYONE can buy weed!

YOU ARE GOING INTO BUSINESS! You'll need to buy a place to grow marijuana, and rent a storefront to sell your product. What kind of marijuana do you plan on growing? Are you going to grow low grade flower to appeal to low priced demand, or are you going to grow top shelf, kind bud? Oh, you should probably get some bongs and edibles, people need ways to smoke your weed, and a good many costumers like to eat it in candy bar form.

Can you navigate through the many costs of running a business in this new industry? Will you price your product to outperform your competitors? Will you be the richest tycoon in this new marijuana age?

Gameplay: Rocky Mountain High is played in turns, each turn will have many player actions. Throughout a game a player is managing their store, while also appealing to customers and the shop's safety. Customers have demands for cheaper prices, marijuana grades, supplies and more. A dispensary owner can build an edge by investing in better locations, growing more plants, having wider array of strains, stronger advertising and more. There is a balance to making a profit in this new marijuana industry. The player with the most money wins.

—description from the designer

Bringing legends to the present to fight for the future

Time Rift is a hybrid, collectible card, miniatures, and tabletop tactics game featuring champions from throughout world history. Customizable, simple to learn, and complex to master. Takes 5 cards and 5 minutes to jump right into the action!

Everything you need to be history's greatest Champion

An all-new CCG that delivers a complete 2-Player game in every booster pack! Mix and match powerful abilities and devastating card combinations to turn your Champion into the ultimate fighting machine.

Intense, Strategic Combat

Movement and facing are integral to the fight. Choose loadout cards to equip your Champion. Maneuver into the perfect position to take advantage of your opponent’s weaknesses. Reveal new cards each round to power up and exploit the rules of the game!

A Complete 2-player game in every booster pack!

No breaking the bank to build a competitive deck. No more cracking a pack, praying for even a single card of value. Time Rift offers a fully playable experience with every purchase.

Learn in minutes and play endlessly!

Time Rift is simple to learn but complex to master. Mix and match powerful abilities and devastating card combinations to turn your Champion into the ultimate fighting machine. Learn in minutes and play endlessly!

A Commitment to Artists

Trivium is committed to fully support the creative community, paying premium prices for premium artwork and No A.I. Time Rift includes and supports the industry’s best illustrators.

—description from the publisher

In Galactic Duel, players lead rival teams of astronauts on a high-stakes mission to explore and study a newly discovered galaxy. Each planet offers unique opportunities—and challenges. On your turn, you'll send astronauts to planets and activate their powerful abilities to gain an edge. As planets fill up, only the most strategic team will thrive. Once all astronauts have been deployed, the final scores are tallied. The player with the most victory points is declared the winner. Will your crew outwit the competition and make history among the stars?

—description from the designer

The goal of the game is to move your elephant across the game board farther than the other players. To do this, you need to come up with associations for your cards and guess other players' cards based on their associations. The winner is the one who best recognizes other players' cards while giving associations that are recognizable but not too obvious. The idea is that at least one player understands which card you chose, but at least one player does not guess it.

Проверено временем, что два лучших варианта классно провести вечер — настольные игры или просмотр фильма. Мы решили соединить эти два занятия в одно и выпустить «Имаджинариум: Кино». С каждой карточки вам будут улыбаться персонажи любимых произведений.

Одни персонажи вам будут знакомы, вторые заставят посмотреть фильм со своим участием, третьи выдавят скупую слезу. Погружайтесь в мир воображения и хороших фильмов.

Players start with a hand of action cards and take turns drawing from the Road deck, trying to collect Experience cards and their matching Gear. If a Gear card doesn't match, it's placed face-down in an expanding grid that players will draw from after the Road deck is exhausted, with the goal being to collect all the Gear for three Experiences.

Aether Reign is a tactical squad-based game where each player takes control of an asymmetrical alien faction to battle for dominance over Aether.

Each player begins by selecting one of several available factions, which they will use to overpower their competitors and prove themselves the most dominant life form in the universe.

The board and faction upgrades are randomized every match for a unique experience each game.

Players interact by collecting Aether, upgrading their units, fighting other squads, and destroying enemy bases. By entering the Aether Rift, players can bend reality to their faction's needs with powerful distortions and permanent upgrades. With powerful ultimate abilities, the tides of battle can be shifted in a single turn.

Each faction has its own special objective, which can be used to bring them closer to victory. The last player with dominance remaining controls the Aether Rift and wins the game.

—description from the designer

Players begin with a hand of Item cards, with the rest of the deck placed in the center of the table and surrounded by Camper cards to start a grid. Taking turns, players draw from the Item deck, trying to match the things each Camper wants-if they are unable to do so, they're "fired" (removed from the game) as camp counselor. Otherwise, when the Item deck is exhausted the person who has collected the most Campers wins.

A Movement deck is shuffled to one side and a 10x5 grid of Ocean and Fish cards is randomly laid out, with one player choosing to be Sharks and the other Fish. Players take turns drawing one card from the Movement deck to determine how many fish/sharks they can move that turn. The goal of the Fish is to get to a Cover (coral reef) card and collected by the fish player, its former spot being replenished from the Ocean deck. The Sharks' goal is to eat the fish by landing on them, collecting them as well. When all fish are removed from the grid, the person who has collected the most wins.
This expansion adds ten new cards to the deck, introducing shovel cards that can be used to remove trees from the grid, and two new tree types for greater variety.

The drinking game known as Tablero had its genesis at a party attended by members of the worldwide medieval recreation group, the Society for Creative Anachronism (SCA) in 1985, or anno societatis 19, as time is reckoned in the Knowne World.

One person who was very keen on the history and playing of board games, Baron Sir Gerhard Kendal of Westmoreland, introduced a gambling game played with coins to the group; El Tablero de Jesús, said to have been played by 15th century monks. A couple of folks at the aforementioned party, known as Umeyama Taniko and Guido da Gucci, devised a similar game with shot glasses of beer instead of coins, and Tablero da Gucci was born. From those humble beginnings, the denizens of the SCA crowd-built the rules of the new game, now often referred to simply as Tablero. Sometime later, Baron Steffano da Gucci published a pamphlet offering a codification of the rules and some of the variants, but house rules and further variations not only abound but have always been encouraged and considered part of the fun.

What follows is a simplified set of our version of the rules.

A Tablero set consists of seven shot glasses, two six-sided dice, and a board with a 7x7 grid of a size appropriate to the shot glasses.

The standard Stake for a game is two bottles or cans per Player of some kind of alcoholic beverage, usually beer and NOT hard liquor.

The object of the game is to drink your Opponent’s beverage. Players must arrange 5 or more shot glasses in a continuous, unbroken line on any Row (the lines of squares horizontal to the players) but NOT on either Baseline (the two Rows closest to the Players). This allows them to Take a Pull i.e. drink the beer in those 5+ shots.

The Player rolls the two 6-sided dice and moves any two of the seven shot glasses, one for each die, the corresponding number of squares up or down the Columns (the lines of squares perpendicular to the players).

The Player must make both their moves even if this puts them at a disadvantage.

Dice rolls cannot be combined to move one shot.

The Player continues to roll the dice and move shot glasses until they 
a) line up enough shot glasses to Take a Pull, 
b) lose Control of the dice as a result of rolling a Pass Number (7 or 11), or 
c) are unable to move one or more of their shot glasses the number of spaces indicated by the dice. (Eg: the roll is a 6 and there are no shot glasses on either baseline, so no move is possible for that number.) They move neither shots, and pass the dice to their Opponent.

Before play a bystander is asked to roll the dice and this number needs to be remembered as The Queen’s Number.

Whenever the Queen’s Number is rolled, the Player who rolled it should choose any shot glass on the table, raise it, declare “Let’s Hear It For The Queen!” and drink the shot.

The empty glass is set on the Opponent’s baseline on the Column it came from, and the Opponent fills it. Play then proceeds as before, without re-rolling the dice.

If a Player rolls the Queen’s Number but does not notice and moves a shot glass (the Opponent should wait for them to let go of the shot before challenging the move) their Opponent may call Muggins on it by declaring “Let’s Hear It For The Queen!”. The shot glass that was moved in error should go back to where it was before the Queen’s Number was rolled and the Opponent gets to choose any shot and drink it. The emptied shot then goes on the Baseline of the active Player and they must fill it. The active Player retains control of the dice and continues with their turn.

You get to Take a Pull (i.e. drink the lined-up shot glasses) when you line up 5 or more shot glasses in a continuous, unbroken line on a Row (so, no gaps in the line of shots).

Note that at least one shot glass must be added to an assembled line of shots on the turn that the Pull is declared and taken.

Pulls may not be assembled on the baselines.

When a Player has arranged enough shots in a line for a Pull, they may drink them. In fact, the Player must drink at least half of the shots in the Pull. The other half of the shot glasses in the Pull they may optionally hand out to their Opponent, who is likely thirsty at this point, and/or to the Spectators that generally agglomerate to games of Tablero.
The Player must have finished both their moves before being able to Take a Pull.

After a Pull, the now empty shot glasses are placed on the Opponent’s baseline and the Opponent must refill them. Provided they fill all the empty shot glasses, the Opponent now gets control of the dice, becoming the active Player.

Should a Player be unable to fill a shot glass that is required of them (they have run out of beer) then they lose the game.
There is a fair amount more to the game but this represents the basics.

boardgame: Borealis: Arctic Expeditions – Society for Polar Inquiry Promo Pack
description:
A bundle of 3 additional scoring objectives, a new end-game scoring card, plus upgraded deluxe components - a metal first player token and 12 plastic society crests.

—description from the publisher

Small promo deck of 4 new award winning movies to be added to deck of the base game.

Small change on the position of the Final Showing card (Page 4 of the rules).


     2 players: 14 Movie cards at the bottom of the deck.
     3-4 players: 9 Movie cards.

Add chaos to your Cozy Cat Cafe by adding mice to other players cafes, or even your own. Mice give negative points but some customers actually require you to have mice, get the balance just right to create THE Coziest Cafe.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Citizens of the Spark: Transmission Pack
description: This Kickstarter exclusive pack adds 10 cards each for 3 new Citizens - Advocate, Pillager and Warden - as well as additional dividers and randomizer cards for storage and setup, and 3 new Scyntilla cards for solo play.

boardgame: X-ODUS: Rise of the Corruption (Second Edition)
description:
X-ODUS: Rise of the Corruption – Second Edition is a cooperative sci-fi game that blends tactical exploration, asymmetric ship abilities, and dynamic threats into a rich, replayable experience. Explore a modular galaxy, reveal hidden systems, and uncover powerful signals left behind by a forgotten civilization. Face enemies that adapt and escalate. Manage time, navigate danger, and cooperate to survive.

Each ship offers a distinct role — some scouts, some brawlers, some support — all essential to the fleet. You’ll unlock upgrades, manage resources, and react to the unexpected, all while racing against an enemy that never stops advancing. The new edition features streamlined mechanics, faster setup, new visuals, and deeper narrative immersion.

In X-ODUS: Rise of the Corruption, players take on the role of starship captains united by a critical mission: to recover the Precursor Keys. These powerful sources of energy, left behind by the ancient Precursors, are the only force strong enough to seal the Main Rift and stop the spread of the Corruption that threatens to consume the galaxy.

To win the game, Ships must find and activate all the Precursor Keys on the Main Rift and defeat any Avatars that may reach the Main Rift after the final Key is activated.

If the Corruption tracker reaches the Rise of the Corruption before the Ships seal the Main Rift, or if the all your Ships are
defeated, the galaxy will fall to darkness, and the game is lost.

—description from the publisher

The cat gang has staged a rebellion against their boss Nala, staging a coup that has ousted the big cat from power. 
Now the factions are fighting to get the most cats and take power from the legendary gang. 
Will you be able to dominate these beasts?

Use the actions from the cats to gather point cards and remove the cards from other players. Get 30 cat points to begin your own gang.

The Dog Army is in big trouble, respect and order have been disturbed at the canine base!
Someone has been leaving poop in the training yard, which as everyone knows is a major flaw that tarnishes the dignity of the Guardians of Etheria, according to the code of conduct, all poop must be collected.
This whole mess needs a solution, now you must prove your honor, showing that you are not the culprit.

Use the actions of the dogs from the army playing cards to distribute poop cards to other players and earn points being the player with less poop and securing your honor.

boardgame: Paper Scissors Rock: The Card Game
description:
Paper Scissors Rock is a quick-to-learn, dynamic card game that mixes the classic Rock-Paper-Scissors showdown with clever card play. The goal is simple: outplay your opponents and be the first to get rid of all your cards!

Enjoy the crafty combos, surprise reversals with the Rock-Paper-Scissors suit system, and the thrill of slamming down your last card. Paper Scissors Rock brings a classic throw-down to the tabletop in a fresh, fun way.

HOW TO PLAY:
Players begin by being dealt 12 cards each, with the remaining cards becoming the draw deck. Started from the left of the dealer, players take turns by playing a card from their hand to the stack, each time beating the previous card with either: a card of the same suit (rock on rock) & higher value, or a card of a stronger suit (rock on scissors) with at least the same value or higher.
If a player is unable to beat the current card on the stack, they draw a card and sit out of the round. When all players are out of the round and it is back to the player who played the last card on the stack. That player wins the round, the stack is discarded, and the round winner begins a new round with any card from their hand and all players are back in.
The first player to play all of their cards from their hand wins the game.

BONUS RULE:
Players can play duplicate cards as doubles. All subsequent players will now also need to play doubles for the rest of the round. Doubles still follow the same ruling of singles for suits and values.

—description from the designer

Splentia is a medium-weight board game that blends puzzly tile placement with engine-building elements.
As gem artisans, players compete to become the most renowned in the kingdom.
Expand your mining sites and grow your team of workers to complete intricate gemworks and earn VP.


     Each player simultaneously draws Miner Tokens and places them on their own Mining Sites. If a token can no longer be placed, the player goes Bust. Players may also choose to stop drawing at any time before going Bust.
     Players gain Gems based on where their Miner Tokens were placed.
     Combine Gems to complete Blueprint Boards (puzzles). Completing a board grants rewards such as gaining new Miners, unlocking Mining Sites, and earning Victory Points (VP).


Repeat these steps until a player reaches 15 VP.
Then, check Achievement Cards (personal goals) and determine the final score.
The player with the most VP wins the game.

—description from the designer
designers: Shiki Hasekawa (ハセカワ シキ)

description:
A yatzee game - Let the animal dice roll … With this funny dice game variant, even younger ones score points by collecting birds, rabbits, frogs & Co. 
Each player can roll the dice up to three times per round and tries to meet as many requirements as possible through clever combinations. 
The result can be entered immediately on the supplied block. Who manages to roll five identical motifs and
get the maximum number of points? And who gets a bonus?
Fun for the whole family – also ideal for on the go

—description from the publisher

boardgame: C'Era Una Volta: Storie di Cavalieri, Mari e Magie
description:
Add to your stories the heroism of Knights, the adventure of the Seven Seas, and the mystery of the Fairy Realm!

In C'Era Una Volta - Storie di Cavalieri, Mari e Magie, you’ll continue to tell your story using more than a hundred new cards divided into three major themes: the brave deeds of knights for their ladies, the exotic legends of pirates and sea monsters, and the incredible magic of classic fairy tales.

The cards in this expansion can be mixed with the main deck to add even more variety, magic, and adventure to your storytelling.

This box set includes the three expansions Knightly Tales, Seafaring Tales, and Enchanting Tales, fully translated into Italian.

—description from the publisher

In Einstein: The Game, you answer questions, whether individually or in teams, in eleven categories — logical thinking, problem solving, mathematical and literary skills — over three hundred puzzle cards to win key spaces on the game board, both blocking the other team and trying to get four of your markers in a row.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Avalanche, Exodus, & Lady Mastermind
description: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Avalanche, Exodus, & Lady Mastermind includes a variety of Brotherhood of Mutant characters to expand and showcase more X-Men villains and characters.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Get ready to relive the golden age of arcades with Streets of Revenge! Inspired by the frantic action and unmistakable spirit of 80s beat 'em up arcade games, this micro-game plunges you directly into adrenaline-fueled street fights.

With just a handful of cards and a few dice, every game of Streets of Revenge is an explosion of nostalgia and quick strategy. Forget complicated rules and long setups; here, the action is instant. Choose your hero, master your moves, and face hordes of enemies to clear the streets of thugs and reach the dreaded Final Boss. It's time to roll the dice and unleash your arcade fury!

In this game, you'll use dice to represent both your hero and the enemies, where the dice score indicates health points. You'll face enemies of different levels, each distinguished by their color and possessing unique abilities.

Your hero will have special skills and upgrades as you move across the scenario map. During your adventure, you'll be able to break reward crates (containing weapons, health, credits, among other things) and shoot explosive barrels to inflict additional damage on enemies. Your goal is to defeat all enemies to finally confront the stage bosses, each with their own distinct rewards and abilities.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Criminal Syndicate Affiliation Pack
description: Marvel: Crisis Protocol - Criminal Syndicate Affiliation Pack contains four villains in five forms — Kingpin, Bullseye, M.O.D.O.K., and Hood — previously released in individual packages or paired with a hero, giving you a quarter of villains to challenge your heroes in Marvel: Crisis Protocol.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Outer Rim Outlaws
description:
Once the galaxy's most formidable bounty hunters have received a contract, they will stop at nothing to accomplish their mission. Equipped with iconic beskar armor and a deadly arsenal of weapons, their unmatched prowess puts their enemies to the test.

Star Wars: Legion – Outer Rim Outlaws give syou two such bounty hunters to add to your game. Boba Fett (Daimyo) strikes fear into the hearts of his enemies and ensures no one escapes his grasp. Joined by Din Djarin, known as The Mandalorian, and his counterpart Grogu, a foundling with a strong connection to the Force, this trio of characters brings a wide variety of iconic abilities to the battlefield.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – IG-Series Assassin Droids
description:
Assassin droids accomplish their missions with ruthless efficiency, no matter the cost. IG-11 and IG-88 are available for hire for the Galactic Empire or Rebellion, without any qualm about their targets.

Bring a variety of tactical options to the battle with Star Wars: Legion – IG-Series Assassin Droids. These iconic assassin droids can take down multiple targets or hunt their target with precision. IG-11 and IG-88 provide lethal skills for whomever they fight for, giving them an edge against their enemies on the battlefield.

Learning with fun: Zoono combines mathematics with fun. Players recognise the biggest animal, count numbers & win cards. Perfect for ages 3 and up as their first math card game.
PROMOTES NUMBER & SIZE UNDERSTANDING: Children learn numbers from 1 to 10, compare sizes & train their visual attention - a creative educational game for preschool children.
Easy rules, quick rounds: Whoever recognizes the biggest animal first, collects the most cards and wins! A fun game for little explorers and their families.
Perfect for game evenings and on the go: thanks to its compact size, Zoono is ideal for travel, daycare and home. Promotes social interaction & team spirit - suitable for 2-4 players.
GREAT GIFT FOR CHILDREN: Delights fans of Memory, Exploding Kittens, Avocado Smash & The Game. An ideal gift for Christmas, birthdays and as a souvenir for little maths fans.

Zoono is a fast-paced reaction and concentration game where players flip cards at the same time and quickly identify the tallest animal shown. Some cards introduce number-based actions that encourage rhythmic counting and turn-taking. With each round, children develop key skills such as visual attention, number-size comparison, and social interaction. The game ends when all cards are played, and the player with the most collected cards wins.

—description from the publisher

Colourful and fun children's game: Wiggly Wild brings fun and learning together. Players place cards that match the colour or animal - an exciting card game for children and the whole family.
PROMOTES COGNITIVE SKILLS: Improves color & animal recognition, strategic thinking & social interaction. Ideal for children over 3 years as a playful learning experience.
Easy rules, fast game: play tactically, get rid of your cards & win! With special action cards such as the Chameleon, Sloth & Lemur, it remains exciting and unpredictable.
Perfect for game evenings and on the go: compact design makes it the ideal companion for travel, family celebrations and as a souvenir. Suitable for 2-4 players, easy to learn & always entertaining.
Great gift idea for children: delights fans of memory, exploding kittens, throw burrito & avocado smash. An original board game for Christmas, birthdays or as a surprise!

In Wiggly Wild, players take turns matching cards by color or animal type to be the first to play all their cards. Action cards like the Chameleon, Sloth, and Lemur introduce twists—changing colors, skipping turns, or forcing opponents to draw extra cards. With every move, children practice strategic thinking, color and shape recognition, and turn-taking in a playful, competitive setting. The first player to empty their hand wins, but don't forget to shout "Last card!"—or draw a penalty card!

—description from the publisher

A colorful trick taking card game where you work with your friends to beat them.

—description from the back of the box

In Shady Hues, players partner up to build up hoards of their favorite three colors in this exciting trick-taking game. If your partner is just dead weight, change the rules and pair up with someone else. If your cards aren’t good enough, change the rules to win on your own terms.

This game is played in 10 minute rounds, where we learn what tit-for-tat really means.

The object of the game; you and your partner win tricks but split winnings. Get ahead by manipulating the rules to specialize in three suits. Accumulate as many cards as possible in those suits while trying to avoid the rest.

—description from the designer

At the end of the 19th century, thousands of immigrants arrived in São Paulo in search of a new beginning. Now it's your turn to follow that path. Work, build, and thrive in a strategic game where time and effort are your most valuable resources. Gather your family, face challenges, and turn this new land into a home. The city grows with each round — and with it, the legacy of your Brava Gente.
