Brava Gente is a game of Worker Placement, Area Control, Set Collection, and Tile Placement. Over the course of 12 rounds, players must decide which jobs to take on and adapt as new opportunities arise. Shape your future by gaining experience, opening businesses, and bringing family members to help you build your life in this ever-changing city.
At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Fun concentration game: Findzy is a fun card game that challenges children aged 4 and up to track colours and recognise the common animal in a flash!
Promotes Visual Perception & Attention: Supports fast thinking, concentration & social interaction - ideal for preschool children & families.
Simple rules, maximum fun: identify the right colour and find the common animal to collect cards! Perfect board game for children and parents.
Perfect for game evenings and on the go: compact design makes Findzy the ideal companion for travel, nurseries and at home. An educational game for 2-4 players.
Great gift idea for children: delights fans of memory, exploding kittens and avocado smash. A creative gift for Christmas, birthdays and as an educational game for little explorers.

Playever Findzy is a fast-paced visual attention game where players race to spot matching colors and identify the one animal shared between two same-colored cards. As cards are revealed in turn, players must stay alert and think quickly. The first to find and name the matching animal wins all the face-up cards. The game ends when all cards are played, and the player with the most cards wins. Designed to strengthen focus, visual scanning, and rule-based thinking through playful competition.

In Playever Mixdit, players race to match five familiar animals with the correct items shown on a central card. The twist? The objects change position, shape, and color on every card, requiring sharp observation and quick thinking. The first player to correctly place all items on their board shouts “Mixdiit!” and wins the round. Designed to boost visual agility, color and shape recognition, and reaction speed, Mixdit can also be played in a slower, non-competitive mode for younger players building visual matching skills.

—description from the designer

Attention and concentration: playfully promotes attention, visual perception and reaction speed. A fast, fun educational game for children aged 3 and up.
Bilingual instructions: Includes detailed instruction manual in German and English for easy and understandable use.
Family game: exciting fun for the whole family. Whether with friends or parents - Playever Mixdit inspires young and old and strengthens social skills.
Quick rounds and easy rules: easy to understand and start playing. Players must combine animal cards with matching objects - whoever is done first shouts "Mixdit!" and wins.

Objective:
Be the fastest player to correctly match all five animals on your board with the items shown on the central card. The player who collects the most cards by the end of the game wins.

Setup:

Each player takes one animal board and 5 matching animal tokens (Panda, Monkey, Elephant, Tiger, Raccoon) in the same color.

The deck of object cards is shuffled and placed face-down in the center.

Game Play:

At the beginning of each round, the top card from the deck is flipped and placed in the center. This card shows all five animals holding different items — but be careful! The positions, shapes, and colors of the items change in every round.

All players quickly scan the central card and try to match each animal on their board with the correct item as shown on the card.

Players place the tokens representing the items onto the animals on their personal board as fast as possible.

The first player to complete their board correctly shouts "Mixdiit!" and pauses the game.

The other players verify the placement. If all matches are correct, that player wins the round and takes the central card as a point.

If the player made a mistake, they sit out the rest of the round, and the remaining players continue until someone finishes correctly.

Winning the Game:

Once all central cards have been used, the game ends.

The player with the most collected cards (round wins) is the winner.

Alternative Play for Younger Children:

For a calmer experience, let each player draw their own card and complete matching without time pressure or competition. This supports early visual recognition and independent play.

—description from the designer

Falcon’s Formula Racing is a dynamic racing board game where the ultimate goal is simple: cross the finish line first and become the champion.

The game features 6 teams, each with 2 drivers. At the start, each player selects a team and controls both of its drivers throughout the game. The action kicks off with a qualifying session, divided into two rounds: Q1 and Q2. Players receive qualifying cards and must decide which card to use in the first round and which to save for the second. The slower half of the grid is eliminated after Q1, while Q2 determines the starting grid for the race. Players can also choose to run extra laps during qualifying to improve their time, but doing so wears down their tires before the race even begins.

The race itself takes place over 4 laps. Before the start, players must choose a tire compound for each of their drivers, with different tires offering varying performance and durability — adding a strong strategic element to the game. Players are allowed up to 3 pit stops during the race, enabling them to swap worn tires for fresh sets — another key part of long-term race strategy.

Throughout the race, dynamic events like weather changes, driving mistakes, technical issues, or track limit violations keep players sharp. Rainy conditions drastically alter gameplay and can shake up the leaderboard at any moment. Other thematic race elements like yellow flags, safety car or driver penalties bring the authentic feel of real motorsport into the game.

If fewer than 6 players are present, the grid is filled with AI-controlled drivers. These bots behave like real players — they make pit stops, execute overtakes, and participate in all race events, ensuring a full and competitive racing experience even in solo or with fewer players.

—description from the designer

The classic party game of stumping your friends with clever clues and periodically screaming "CONTACT!"

Team up against your friends one at a time and outwit them with clever innuendo and secret hints.

Includes hundreds of cards with thousands of perfectly-curated words.

—description from the publisher

The clue giver reveals the first letter of the word to be guessed.  Each player then comes up with words starting with that letter, but only shouts out a description ("A type of fish!")  If another player thinks they know the word, they both say it at the same time; if they're wrong, everyone keeps playing, but if they're right, the clue giver reveals the next letter of the word.  Play continues until someone guesses the word.

Irány a Balaton! is a summer-themed board game focused on color and shape recognition, where players splash around with Little Bear and his friends to find the right water toys. Players roll a color die, then follow the seaweed trails on fish task cards to determine which color to look for on the cards – only the color of the water equipment matters, not the animal or its clothing. If the matching color isn’t present, players must slap the mosquito. The game can be adapted for different age groups and includes increasingly challenging rule variations (e.g., using more fish cards, or rolling both the color and shape dice so that both the color and the type of water toy matter). The player who collects the most cards wins!

S.O.B. Most Wanted is a fast-paced, laugh-out-loud party game that blends charades, sabotage, and strategic duels. Perfect for 3+ adults who love chaos, betrayal, and yelling at their closest friends — all before the timer runs out.

How to Play
2 Rounds. 1 Wild Showdown.

Bandit Round:
One player acts out a Bandit card while the others guess.
The first player to guess correctly receives the card — but wait, it could be a sneaky S.O.B. card (−1 point) or a Bandit card (+1 point)! Keep it face down and add it to your stash.

Duel Round:
Got a Bandit card? Shout "DUEL!" to pause the clock and challenge another player with a Bandit card.
Both of you act out prompts head-to-head. First one guessed wins the Duel and gets to steal a card from their opponent.

Sheriff Twist:
Steal the Sheriff card in a Duel and you can pass your S.O.B.s to other players face-up. Nasty move. We love it.

After the Duel, the timer resumes and the next Bandit round begins.

End Game:
When the timer’s up, count your Bandit points. Most Bandits = Big Winner.

—description from the publisher

This is a Monopoly-Like game, based on the city of Salina Kansas in the USA. It is part of Late for the Sky's "I {Heart} ..." series of 'Monopoly' clones. With street names and various locations around the city listed as properties. There are pewter molded player tokens, the houses in 'Monopoly' are represented by a "City Block", and the Hotels are represented by a "Key to the City" pieces. Their "Chance" and "Community Chest", cards have been replaced by "Big Fun" and "Contingency" cards. While the "Property" cards are slightly different. Two versions were released, the first in 2019, and the second in 2023, with different properties names, board, card text, and new box. Most other content, such as the illustrations on the cards, is identical as the original version.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: Поколения: Промокарта "Значок Премии" (Heredity: GMA Badge Promo Card)
description:
Поколения: Промокарта "Значок Премии" ("Heredity: GMA Badge Promo Card") is a goody Item card. The card is dedicated to the Geek Media Awards, and it was given to the guests at the 2024 awards ceremony.

The front side of the card (971):
Name: Значок Премии ("Award Badge")
Value: 3
Illustration: GMA 2024 Badge with bullet hole
Text: IF you become the target of a ranged attack, THEN "Flip this card".

The back side of the card (971b):
Narrative section:
You see a shot, and the bullet is flying toward you. Everything seems to slow down, and you have time to think that this is the end. Suddenly, you feel a sharp pain in your chest, but it's not as severe as you expected. You can tell by the sound of metal in your ears that the shot has ricocheted.

Instruction section:

     You have to select another character in your zone. He becomes the target of this attack. If you're alone in the zone, no one else gets hurt.
     Draw a Karma сard. IF the Karma сard has a "Defeat condition" symbol in its bottom left corner, THEN "burn" ("Award Badge"), ELSE another dent appears on the commemorative badge from an event before the Fall. "Flip this card".



Only one goody can be added to the Play Characters' inventory at the beginning of each chapter. If you have more than one, you can play with a different goodie for each chapter, but never more than one in the game at the same time.

Once a goody is in the game, it becomes part of your inventory and is saved like the rest of your Objects.

If you lose a goody, you can't add it again to your inventory during the campaign.

Tsar Dušan is dead. His empire, crumbling.

Your lord? Uroš the Weak. Your loyalty? A facade. Pursue your ambitions, fulfill the Deeds, and claim his power.

On your turn, you’ll play a card as an Estate (resource generator), a Building (conversion), or a Deed (objective). Use Estates to gather resources, funnel them through Buildings to forge Ambitions - Military, Diplomacy, or Trade - then spend those to fulfill Deeds that bring you ever closer to the throne.
And when all else fails, you can always sell your… cards… for coin.

Remember: Only Unity Saves the Serbs, as long as you are the Unity.

Note: 2 copies needed for 4+ player games. Semi simultaneous play at higher player counts.

—description from the designer

Each player takes a set of 5 blocks (labeled A-E) with 2 symbols on each side. Choose a difficulty level (1-5) and place the corresponding cards face down in the middle of the play area. Flip over the first card and all players simultaneously attempt to match the pattern on the card using their blocks. The first person to complete the pattern rings the bell and keeps the card. The player with the most cards at the end is the winner.

Two Suits is a simple trick taking card game. The goal is to get as many points as possible through either taking tricks or trying to take no tricks at all.
Two Suits was designed and created with the following goals in mind:

     Easy to learn but difficult to win
     A trick taking game that had no restrictions on what card you can play (no following suit)
     Everyone is encouraged to take tricks
     Bidding “No Tricks” is the riskiest play (sluffing is the hardest thing to do)
     Is a “My first trick taking game” experience
     It is played quickly (less than 45 minutes)
     Great for large groups (up to 16 players!) and scales well
     It is fun!


—description from the publisher

A quick 2-8 player game. Players have a hand of cards and a draw pile. You may play up to four cards each turn on top of the other cards in a 3 x 3 grid anywhere that the creates a row or column of all cards being the same number, the same color, or a run of three consecutive numbers (eq, 2-3-4). If a player play a card to make two matches at once, they may put a card from their hand directly into another player's draw pile.

Great 72 is not a classic historical wargame.

You won’t find scenarios for recreating specific battles or strictly defined army lists here. 
Instead, this is a set of flexible rules that allows you to use historical 1/72 miniatures to create exciting tactical battles and enjoy a simple, dynamic game. 
Whether you prefer historical settings or want to immerse your miniatures in a fantasy world, Great 72 has you covered.

As a dedicated fan of 1/72 miniatures, I created this game to breathe new life into these figures, often left gathering dust on shelves.

Great 72 — is a set of simple rules for miniatures wargaming, meaning battles with miniature soldiers.
This book will allow you to assemble an army and conduct a tabletop battle in any historical era – from Antiquity and the Middle Ages to the Napoleonic Wars, as well as fantasy battles featuring wizards, monsters, and legendary heroes.

The core of the game is based on miniatures in the 1/72 scale, which gives Great 72 its name. 
To avoid unbalanced crossovers, the game is divided into three distinct ages:

     Sword Age(Antiquity and the Middle Ages)
     Gunpowder Age (from the Renaissance to the Napoleonic Wars)
     Fantasy Age (the world of magic and legends)


The basic rules are the same for all ages, but each age has its own unique features and unit rules.

Bring the Italian Wars of the Fifteenth and Sixteenth Centuries to life on the Pike & Shotte tabletop!

Fought between the Holy Roman Empire, Spain, France, and a whole host of Italian states, the Italian Wars would see a pivotal turning point in the history of warfare, marking the transition from the ‘bills and bows’ of medieval combat to the arquebus and pike. Packed with incredibly colourful characters, perhaps no forces better epitomise the period than the Landsknechts and Swiss. These legendary mercenaries waged war against each other across Italy and beyond, ushering in the new era of battlefield tactics. Death & the Landsknecht lets you bring these famous battles, and more, to the Pike & Shotte battlefield.

Accompanying the book is the exclusive special figure, Richard de la Pole. A scion of English nobility following the Wars of the Roses, de la Pole would be central to the final Yorkist challenge to Henry VII's authority. Forced to leave England in the wake of many of his co-conspirators' and family members' executions, he would become an ally of King Francis I of France. He was in the process of arranging a French-backed invasion of England when peace broke out, but remained with Francis as the King went to fight in Italy. De la Pole would be killed at Pavia in 1525.

Depicted in the thick of the fighting, sword and broken pike in hand, the special edition Richard de la Pole miniature is a fantastic opportunity for painters to show off their skills, and a great addition to any Italian Wars collection.

Assemble the finest forces your paymasters can hire, and prepare for the Italian Wars! The era of the Condottieri and their great mercenary armies is upon us!

—description from the publisher

Geng Kecebong: Splash & Snatch is a light push-your-luck party game for 2 to 5 players where every decision drips with risk and reward. On your turn, choose to draw animal tokens from a hidden bag or go home to secure your catch. The more you push, the more tadpoles you might find... unless a crab ruins everything.

Every token has a different effect:


     Tadpoles give you points
     Fish let you draw again
     Crabs make you lose everything you haven’t stored!
     Eels, Frogs, and Leeches offer clever ways to manipulate the game


But that’s not all — New Rule cards are revealed mid-game to twist the rules and force players to rethink their strategies. And with unique once-per-game character abilities, every round feels different.

Play over three rounds. Be the first to reach 15 points or have the most tadpoles stored at the end to become the new leader of the Geng Kecebong!

Simple to learn, fast to play, and hilariously chaotic — Splash & Snatch turns muddy mayhem into memorable moments.

—description from the designer

Kamikaze is a chaotic and hilarious party card game where the rules change constantly – and so does your dignity.

Players take turns drawing from a deck of 60 unique cards across three categories:

Action cards that trigger immediate, often ridiculous effects

Rule cards that alter how the game is played until they’re removed

Chaos cards that shake everything up in unpredictable ways

At any point, you can refuse to follow the instructions on a card – as long as you finish your drink.

Kamikaze is designed for 2–10 players, takes 15–30 minutes, and is perfect for parties, pre-parties, and late-night breakdowns. Just don’t expect to stay in control for long.

—description from the designer

Chaos Mode is the first variant of the original  card game, One To Ten! Protect yourself with Avalanches, predict and eliminate with Earthquakes, search and memorise with Lightning, rotate with Tornados, steal and replace with Tsunamis, filter with Volcanos, destroy with Meteors, store and hide cards, and draw more cards per turn.

—description from the publisher

Secret Recipe: Kitchen Mischief Expansion introduces events and mishaps that happen when you share the kitchen with family and friends. Some bring you points, some introduce an obstacle, shake up the rules, or give you unexpected advantages.

The expansion includes:

     23 event cards with immediate effect,
     13 event cards with lasting effect,
     4 wooden fire tokens,
     1 rulebook.


—description from the publisher

Deco Dica is a super versatile game that can be played in 5 different ways, where players are challenged to work on their Creativity, Persuasion, Improvisation, Strategy and Memory.

The game consists on using cards with objects that must be associated to create stories and convince the others that you are the most creative one.

In a world torn apart by celestial catastrophe, remnants of ancient faith and forgotten power collide in a desperate struggle. Vestige Saga: Fallen Grail is a competitive, team-based tactical board game set during the final four nights before the collapse of a doomed world. Two teams of two players each are drawn into a secretive ritual involving divine blessings and fateful curses—only one side can claim the final grace of the fallen goddess.

Each match begins with players drawing hidden Prayer Cards, secretly determining who among them carries the Blessing. To win, a team must identify and eliminate the opposing blessed target—turning combat into a mind game of deception, deduction, and precision execution. Alternatively, in free-for-all Duel and 2P Duel modes, only one warrior will survive to tell the tale.

Choose from eight asymmetrical characters, each with unique weapons, skill boards, and special mechanics—from summoning skeletal followers to manipulating teleport gates. Positioning, line-of-sight, and status effects such as Burn, Freeze, and Curse are vital to combat. A dynamic modular map ensures every battle feels fresh and tactical.

Time is running out. As the world crumbles, life thresholds dwindle, maps collapse, and divine relics vanish. Will you seize the last shard of salvation—or fall with the Grail?

—description from the publisher

Crowns is a tactical area control card game inspired by various traditional card games, autochess, and tile placement games.

The game requires two players, 20 minutes, a standard deck of 52 cards, and enough table space to place 5x5 rows of cards on it.

The objective of the game is to win by controlling the majority of squares on the board.

The deck of cards is split between black and red, and the players take turn claiming squares while trying to lock down their opponents side of the board.

—description from the designer

Draw tiles from the bag and fill your rack. You and your opponent build a river and the fun of map building begins. Start with a road and connect your town with a ferry crossing. After some turns there is a good chance conflict will begin. Recruit your troops, command your Captains. Defend your territory and conquer your opponent’s.

The game is simple in its design concept and execution. But the replay variety and possibilities are endless between the two variables of the tiles that you draw from the bag and the dice combat resolution. No two games will ever be the same.

The joy of the game comes from literally discovering the game itself. It takes a long time to master the game and to have any sense of confidence in knowing where tiles are to be played and what kind of combat to be waged. The game is challenging beautiful to look at, exciting and a lot of fun. The game also allows plenty of opportunity for table talk and social engagement regarding such variables as tile drawing and dice rolling.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Civil War Expansion
description:
Civil War explodes onto the scene of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! There are no villains this time around; instead, this expansion sees players facing off against heroic leaders fighting for their ideals. On the side of registration, Iron Man and Captain Marvel lead the charge in support of the Superhero Registration Act, while Captain America and Spider-Woman lead the resistance that opposes it. Each of these leaders comes with a fully customizable scenario, with a wide range of main schemes and modular encounter sets to build your own unique Civil War experience. Joining the roster of playable heroes are Tigra and Hulkling, both of whom come with pre-built and ready-to-play decks. Finally, the expansion also comes with rules for a brand-new, optional competitive mode in which players battle to be the first to take down the opposing team’s leader.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

boardgame: CookieRun: Braverse Trading Card Game – Starter Deck Red
description:
Prefer an aggressive playstyle that pressures your opponent?
The Red Starter Deck features cards that boost damage.

It includes various Items and Stages that increase attack power, allowing you to overpower your opponent from the early game.
The deck contains Trap cards that can neutralize or counter enemy attacks!

It’s packed with powerful cookie cards capable of dealing heavy damage to the opponent’s Battle Area.

Ideal for players who like to strike fast, hard, and without hesitation to dominate the game.

This product is for one player. Two players and two starter decks are required to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CookieRun: Braverse TCG – Starter Deck Yellow
description:
A deck that grows stronger as more cookies stack in the Break Area!

The Yellow Starter Deck uses unique tactics to manipulate the Break Area, turning unfavorable situations into opportunities.
Some cards activate when a certain level is reached in the Break Area, rewarding players who take calculated risks and aim for a comeback.

The core strategy is combining low-level cookies with cards that reduce Break Area levels.
It also includes Trap cards that can neutralize or counter opponent attacks.

This is not the end — it’s where your real strategy begins.

This product is for one player. Two players and two starter decks are required to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CookieRun: Braverse TCG – Starter Deck Green
description:
Build up resources early and unleash a powerful late-game strategy!

The Green Starter Deck excels at boosting resources and leveraging them for powerful plays.
Quickly expand your Support Area, then reduce it at key moments to block attacks or land finishing blows to overwhelm your opponent.

Mastering the Support Area is the key to dominating the game with the Green deck.

This product is for one player. Two players and two starter decks are required to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CookieRun: Braverse TCG – Starter Deck Blue
description:
Draw many cards and use a wide variety of effects to achieve victory!

The Blue Starter Deck specializes in expanding and managing your hand through continuous card drawing.
It enables powerful combos to inflict major damage by raising and lowering the number of cards in hand.

This deck requires foresight and careful planning — victory goes to those who stay one step ahead.

This product is for one player. Two players and two starter decks are required to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CookieRun: Braverse TCG – Starter Deck Purple
description:
Erase memory. Rewrite the battlefield.

The Purple Starter Deck is a disruption-focused deck that relies on psychological tactics and effects that send Cookies and Stages to the Trash.

Use trash-based effects to break your opponent’s rhythm and build your own unique path to victory.
Purple is the color of those who weaponize confusion.

This product is for one player. Two players and two starter decks are required to play.

—description from the publisher

Terra do Sol is a worker placement and area control board game set in the historical period of the Cangaço, a socio-political movement marked by banditry and resistance in the Brazilian Northeast during the early 20th century. Players take on the role of leaders of cangaceiro bands, each with asymmetric powers, trying to survive the harsh drylands, outmaneuver rivals, and build fame in the hinterlands while being hunted by government forces.

The game unfolds over a series of rounds where players send out their cabras (band members) to take actions, such as attacking towns, trading at the market and recruiting new cangaceiros. Players must carefully manage resources like water and ammunition, endure seasonal droughts, and build relationships with local coiteiros (sympathizers) to gain advantages.

Each leader earns points in unique ways and must balance aggression with survival. There's no direct combact in the game, players  At the end of the game, the player with the most fame points is crowned the legendary leader of the Cangaço.

There's no direct combat between players. All confrontations happen through strategic positioning, area denial, and competition for shared objectives.
The game is played over a fixed number of rounds, each consisting of three phases:

Allocation Phase: Players take turns placing their cabras (meeples) to select actions for the round, such as attacking localities, visiting the market, getting resources or recruiting.

Action Phase: In initiative order, players resolve their actions.

End of Round: Players deal with seasonal effects (like droughts), refill the board, and prepare for the next round.

Throughout the game, players gain fame points by conquering cities, fulfilling orders, recruiting cangaceiros, progressing on the coiteiro track, and using their leader’s unique scoring condition. The player with the most fame at the end of the final round wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Richthofen: The Flying Circus, 1917 is a solitaire wargame covering fighting in the air over the Western Front in France throughout 1917 during the formation of the famous Flying Circus.  You have the opportunity to play the role of the famous Red Baron -- Manfred von Richthofen flying either an Albatross D-II, or a Fokker Dr.1 triplane.  You may fly other German fighter pilots and planes as well, with each pilot and plane individually rated.  The game system handles the Allied side.  Play takes place over a period representing one week, with you aiming to dominate the skies over the battlefield.

The game demonstrates the importance of good strategy and decision making, while acknowledging the part that fortune played in the life of a World War I fighter pilot as well. The system is highly playable and creates a solid narrative, with game time around an hour.

Richthofen: The Flying Circus, 1917 uses the Eindekker: Fokker Scourge, 1915 game system, modified to reflect the changing nature of 1917 air combat and technological advances.  If you have played Eindekker, you will immediately know how to play this game.  Two Scenarios are included, Spring 1917 and Summer 1917. In addition, an Advanced Game is included that provides modifications and different airplane characteristics, along with an Advanced Game Random Events Table.

Within the pages of this game book is contained all the rules, counters, ratings, game displays, game logs, daily record sheets, "pencil-mode" instructions (for playing without components), advanced game rules and ratings, and histroical commentary and aircraft profiles section... everything you need to play the game apart from two dice and a standard deck of playing cards.

The game system was designed by Bob Flood.  In this Volume 2 of the series, Gary Graber has extended the system to cover later war aerial combat and missions.

As mentioned, the game system can be played in two ways. You may scan and print the game components, and play it as a regular solitaire board game. Alternatively, you can play it as a “roll & write” game in pencil-mode, where all you need (besides dice) are a pencil and the pencil-mode game logs and map, where you keep track of game events on the provided logs, and movement of your plane on the map, erasing and reentering its position as play unfolds. Either method may be used, depending on your own preference and playing circumstances.

If you have enjoyed Eindekker, you are sure to appreciate and enjoy what Richthofen has to offer.  Original Game Design:  Bob Flood.  Richthofen & Series Editor: Gary Graber.  Publisher: Minden Games.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: An expansion for Hide the Body.  Adds enough cards to the game to take the player count from 2-6 to up to 10 players.

In Bravura, you draft skill cards and train tirelessly, striving for a place on the grand stage. Grace alone won't carry you—you’ll need timing, artistry, and the will to outshine (or support) your rivals.
The players need to collect sets to improve their dance skills during the training phase and during the Casting phase these skills let them get the roles of the ballet. The better roles gain more Fame points.
The spotlight awaits...

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In SWUP5, anything goes:
Collect color sets, play sneaky special cards, and drive your opponents crazy!
What starts out harmless quickly turns into a wild ride full of tactics, timing, and nasty twists.
Every card can save you – or ruin everything.
Only those who strike at the right moment and combine wisely will claim victory.

SWUP5 is totally bonkers, brutally chaotic, and delightfully unpredictable.
One thing’s for sure – nothing is for sure!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gunsen: The Battle for Toshi Ranbo
description:
In Gunsen: The Battle for Toshi Ranbo, Rokugan teeters on the brink of upheaval as the Great Clans—the Crab, Crane, Dragon, Lion, Phoenix, Scorpion, and Unicorn—clash for control of the legendary fortress, Toshi Ranbo. Once a strategic gem on the borderlands, the castle now stands at the heart of a war-torn battlefield, its banners contested by rival warlords seeking glory and vengeance. The air hums with the tension of drawn steel and elemental magic, as ancient shrines smolder and village alliances shift like the wind.

In this strategy game, players take on the role of ambitious daimyō representing one of the seven Great Clans: recruiting samurai heroes, drafting powerful orders, and building their clan's military might and strategic influence. Through tactical maneuvers and clever engine-building, they strive to fulfill seven victory objectives—from capturing and holding the coveted castle to completing secret missions and claiming sacred locations. Only one clan will write its legend in the blood-soaked chronicles of Toshi Ranbo.

—description from the publisher
family: Setting: Legend of the Five Rings

description:
Iron Knee is the newest character for Paperback Adventures. As a martial arts expert, she uses Stances and Combos to defeat her foes.

The Iron Knee Box contains:
1 Character tray
4 Status tokens
1 Stance token
7 Oversized cards (enemy and character)
90 Poker-sized cards with sleeves

Spice up your party playing this hilarious game. In the process of playing poker, every player participates in fun activities, such as, doing a charade or pantomime, reading someone's palm, doing a no-no, giving a tender bear hug to all players of the opposite sex, sensuously tickling another player, etc. Two or more can play.

boardgame: Twilight Imperium: Fourth Edition – Twilight Codex Volume IV: Liberation
description:
The Codex is a web-based publication that highlights rules updates and showcases new content for Twilight Imperium: Fourth Edition and its Prophecy of Kings expansion.

This volume includes:

3 Alternative Faction Leaders
2 Alternative Faction Technologies
4 New Galactic Event Cards
1 New Hero card 'F.S.S Orlando'
2 New Legendary Planet Cards
1 New Public Objective Dual-faced Stage I & II
3 New Relic Cards
1 New Scenario 'LIBERATION OF ORDINIAN'

This also Incluses:

Codex 4.5
2 Alternative Basic Technologies

The Mecatol Report
Material written en designed for the GENESYS Roleplaying Game and specifically to complement the Embers of the Imperium sourcebook. It provides additional information about the Keleres Headquarters, the enviroment of Mecatol Rex (both inside and outside of Mecatol City), and a short adventure summary that can be fleshed out into a full adventure. It is also for anyone who wants to know more about Mecatol Rex and the Keleres.
In Não Pode, your challenge is to get your team to guess the word on the card — without using any of the five forbidden words.

Simuhackrum is a cyberpunk themed game, where a solo Hacker must draw tiles to build out a network path toward five randomized, secret servers, trying to find the Secure Vault before running out of time, accrue too much noise, or run out of brain matter.

Along the way, the Hacker encounters obstacles in the form of noise beacons, locked gates, static ICE (intrusion countermeasure electronics), or sentient ICE.  They may also find valuable data (aka credits) that can be used to purchase software mods that help bypass said obstacles.  This game utilizes a press-your-luck and resource management mechanic.

In addition, the Simuhackrum may also be adapted to use in tabletop RPGs (TTRPGs) in lieu of hacking mechanics, which are effectively just rolls against a spreadsheet of challenges. Imagine building out a network on the same battlemat where the rest of your team is dealing with corporate security!

—description from the designer

boardgame: One King! A Guide to Wargaming Argyll's & Monmouth's Rebellion of 1685
description:
One King! is the perfect companion to the Monmouth Rebellion of 1685 covering every encounter and unit in England, and for the first time in Scotland. Its sits alongside the book Fighting for Liberty as the ideal companion and provides the information needed to bring the Monmouth Rebellion to life on the tabletop.

One King! includes fast play campaign rules that uses the strategies, maps, deployments, and military objectives allowing the enthusiast or wargamer to follow in the footsteps of the commanders - even change the course of history over a coffee.

The battlefield plans give all the information needed to fight out all the encounters as accurately as possible. While the orders of battle and unit sheets will guide the tabletop wargamer in the recreation of their miniature armies or navies!

One King! has everything for the wargamer or military enthusiast, even the chance to change the course of history and crown the one true King James II & VII.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: First To Worst: On The Go Edition
description:
In this fun collaborative game, players try to get into each others' heads by guessing how fellow players rank a variety of topics in priority order of 1 through 5. Match more right than wrong and victory is yours!

—description from the publisher

The On The Go edition contains 100 new topics, that are different from the 290 topics in the regular edition. It can be played on its own, or combined with the regular edition.

In a magical and exciting world, there is a community of small creatures called Clickies. These endearing and friendly creatures are known for their boundless energy and their passion for teamwork. Each Clickie has a special skill: some are expert builders, designing colorful shelters and creative structures, while others are masters at sorting and organizing colors, creating dazzling visual displays.
The Clickies live in an environment full of challenges, from organizing their home in the Rainbow Forest to dealing with the playful wind that scatters everything in its path. Through the manipulation of colored cubes and the power of friendship, these little creatures show that together, they can overcome any obstacle. With every project they take on, the Clickies teach valuable lessons about work, creativity, and the importance of caring for their world. This is a story full of adventure, laughter, and magic, where each Clickie shines in their own way. Shall we play?

Objective
Be the first player to collect 5 cards by completing the challenges faster than the other players.

Three mode games:

Speedy Clicky (Pink Challenge Cards)
On the count of three, all players must simultaneously create the object shown on the challenge card, following the template and matching the shape and color of the polycubes exactly.
The first player to complete the object must quickly hit the bell. The round will stop, and the other players must raise their hands while the object is checked. If the object is correct, the player wins the card. If the shape or color combination is incorrect, the player loses one of their cards (if they have any), and a new round begins with a new challenge card.

Creative Clicky (Beige Challenge Cards)
On the count of three, all players must simultaneously create the object shown on the challenge card, using the number of cubes indicated on the card. In this mode, the figures should resemble the proposed illustration as closely as possible. The first player to complete the challenge and hit the bell stops the round, and the other players must raise their hands.
Then, the object is checked. If correct, the player wins the card. If the shape isn’t correct (other players cannot identify the shape), the player loses one of their cards (if they have any), and a new round begins.
At the end of each round, all players must return the cubes to the center of the table.

Observant Clicky
In this game mode, creativity and observation take center stage.
The challenge cards from the Creative Clicky mode (beige cards) will be used.
Each round, one player is the creator, and the other players are the observers.
The creator will place 4 face-up cards on the table and begin building one of them using the cubes. At any moment, the other players can pick up the card they believe the creator is building. Once a player picks the correct card, the creator stops and confirms it. The observant player who guessed correctly wins the card as a prize. Then, the player to the left of the creator becomes the new creator for the next round.

Singularities is a game of bluff and strategy.
Each player embodies a robotic AI that directs the votes of corporatists or activates their special ability to secretly support their faction.
Phase 1 (influence): Vote for a faction
Phase 2 (digital chaos): Attack or defend to establish dominance
Scoring and victory: Votes are revealed, secret objectives are analyzed, and influence points are tallied.
The player with the most influence allows their faction to control the Cosmic Council.

Protect your flock of sheep from the cunning wolves sneaking around the field! After 12 rounds, the player with the most victory points wins.

One Game, two sides: Play the simpler winter side or the more complex summer side – it’s entirely up to you!

Winter Side:
Calupum in the truest sense of the word: Here, you only need to watch out for the wolves — and, of course, your fellow players! Play Calupum in winter mode as an introduction or for a quick game in between.

Summer Side:
More components, more possibilities, more Calupum! The Summer side is significantly more complex. Here, you’ll encounter not only wolves and sheep, but also objectives, tunnels, dog cards, and much more! In summer mode, Bobtail the sheepdog and the shepherdess come to your aid. Use the dog cards for extra advantages and collect even more points with the objective cards.

—description from the publisher

Step into the captivating world of Infinite Legacy, where every decision counts and your fortune is only as secure as your strategy. This isn’t just a board game—it’s a living, breathing economy built on trade, resource management, and the will to conquer. In this one-of-a-kind experience, players become rulers of their own territories, setting their own prices, hiring mercenaries, and negotiating with (or betraying) others for power. But the land holds more than just opportunity—it hides deadly monsters and ancient secrets. As you explore, you’ll uncover hidden mines that drastically boost your resource production and grant you access to rare materials only found through combat. With a revolutionary movement system for troops and exploration, no two games play the same, and no strategy goes unchallenged. Whether you build an empire through trade, warfare, or cunning diplomacy, this game rewards bold moves and smart thinking.

This isn’t just a board game—it’s a full-scale adventure packed into an epic 2-foot by 4-foot battlefield. Designed for strategy lovers, fantasy fans, and tabletop warriors alike, this game delivers an immersive experience straight out of the box. At the heart of the game is a massive illustrated map—your war-torn world, your kingdom, your opportunity to rise or fall. Players will command powerful factions, gather resources, forge alliances (or betray them), and wage war for dominance.

Pre-Orders will be available November, 2025
Official Release Date set for Valentines Day 2026

—description from the designer

You have finally found the coordinates of the legendary Jurassic Valley, where the remains of the giants of the past lie! Find out where to dig, bring the bones to light and reconstruct the skeleton of the T- Rex or the Velociraptor...
...ready to set off on the trail of the ancient dinosaurs?

A Jurassic adventure on the trails of the mysterious dinosaurs. For 2-4 Players.

Move your archaeologist on the squares of the valley and discover the ideal place to dig. Roll the dice and draw precious fossils from the bag trying to recognize by touch which dinosaur they belong to. If you are a skilled paleontologist you can assemble the skeletons and enrich your museum with the largest collection of dinosaurs.

Bloody Lane is a solitaire wargame available on Amazon. The player controls the defending Confederate soldiers holding Bloody Lane, while the advancing Federals are controlled by an AI bot detailed in the following rules. The player rolls six-sided dice to determine the results of chosen actions, which affect the situation on the Battle Sheet (the graphic representation of the area in which the defense of Bloody Lane took place). As the Union assault intensifies, the player will have to make increasingly tough decisions to ensure victory.


     You command five Confederate Regiments defending Bloody Lane. Each historical Confederate Regiment is represented by a Unit counter that occupies a Defensive Position.
     Union Regiments attack in four Waves, each getting stronger. Each historical Union Regiment is represented by a Unit counter and enters an Attack Track. That counter then advances from one Attack Position to the next Attack Position along that Attack Track. 
     You must choose and resolve appropriate actions to defeat each Attack Wave. 
     As both Confederate and Union counters suffer Hits, their Cohesion is reduced, making each Unit less able to carry out the assigned action. If a Confederate counter ever has its Cohesion reduced to zero, that Unit is Shattered and cannot select any action other than Rally to the Flag.
     To resolve an action, make a Cohesion check. Roll 1d6, apply any modifiers to the Cohesion of the Unit and compare the result of the die roll to the current Cohesion of the Unit. If the roll is equal to or less than the (modified) Cohesion, the action is resolved successfully. 
     In addition to resolving actions, the Union will also resolve four Events each turn. Events simulate off-board musket and artillery fire, attacks by sharp shooters, smoke that obscures the battlefield, and attempts to reform Union units. The severity of Union Events is determined by the Difficulty Level selected and are detailed on the Player Sheet. 
     While battling each Attack Wave, you must carefully manage the Supply Line to ensure Orders, Ammunition and Reinforcements arrive in a timely manner. Skilled use of Confederate Artillery will help you achieve this goal. 
     If a Union counter enters a Defensive Position occupied by a Shattered Confederate Unit, Bloody Lane is captured and the game ends in defeat for the player. 


—description from the publisher

Goal Rush is a quick and exciting board game where two players race up their ladders, roll the dice, and make tactical plays to outscore their opponent before the clock runs out! With every roll, players move backward and forward toward goal-scoring chances, double their momentum with speed boosts, and watch the game tick away on the time track.

—description from the designer

This game is a strategic, poo powered card game. Perfect for families that poo!

With 108 hilarious poo-based cards, you'll need to battle it out to get the most "Poo Points" before the deck runs out.

Use your "Action Cards" to help gain the advantage. Strategy is everything!

WARNING: You must have a sense of humour to play this game...

—description from the back of the box

Funocracy is a party game where creativity and quick thinking come together to create hilarious moments.

A theme is revealed, and in just 25 seconds, the player must give an answer that starts with one of the three available letters. The more creative or funny the answer, the better! But there's a catch: the player only scores if the rest of the players vote their answer as NICE!

Each letter card is worth 1 to 3 points, and the goal is to score the most points over three rounds.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: 2025 Roll to Move Goody Box
description: A limited edition goody box available on the web store during Roll-to-Move 2025. This is solely for Shadows of Brimstone.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Carrion Trench Feeders Enemy Pack
description:
Scouring the battlefields and bombed-out trenches of Trederra, these burrowing insect-like creatures tunnel through the rock and debris in great swarms to feed on the bodies of the fallen soldiers that litter No Man's Land. With vicious mandibles and a hard segmented shell, they are drawn to feed by the stench of the carrion dead. Two rows of sharp, hook-like talons line the underbelly of the Trench Feeders, allowing them to pierce the flesh and bone of those victims they find that still draw breath, preventing their weakened prey from escaping.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to add the Trederra-themed Carrion Trench Feeders to your games of Shadows of Brimstone! There are 12 small Carrion Trench Feeder models included, as well as the large Enemy Record Sheet, and all of the Threat and OtherWorld Threat Cards needed to add them to your games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Gates of Valhalla – Oathbreaker Axemen Enemy Pack
description:
Cowards, traitors, and backstabbers, these long-dead viking warriors broke their sacred oaths of loyalty and fellowship in life, often for the promise of riches or power. Betraying their allies and kinsmen to torment and death, the Oathbreaker Axemen have been cursed to roam the world for their treachery and dishonor. Now they are little more than rotting husks with rusted chain armor and chipped axes, still carrying the burdens of their broken oaths.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to add the Oathbreaker Axemen to your games of Shadows of Brimstone! There are 6 Oathbreaker Axemen models included, as well as the large Enemy Record Sheet, a deck of Broken Oath cards, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add them to your games, including an Epic Threat of a Legendary band of Oathbreakers - Hel's Reavers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Swarm Pack #4 – Stranglers and Night Terrors
description:
An Enemy Swarm Pack is a new themed set of cards focusing on fighting swarms of a particular Enemy Type, as well as a new set of Spawners for that Enemy Type. In this case, the pack focuses on fighting swarms of Stranglers and Night Terrors, and a mind-bending Tear in Reality that spawn new Stranglers and Night Terrors during a Fight.

Also includes Enemy Record Sheets for OtherWorld variants of Stranglers and Night Terrors, with the stats and modifiers worked into the Enemy Record Sheet itself, as well as new themed Elite Charts and Brutal sides for these variants. This set also includes a new Mission themed for swarms of demons from the Void, Torn from the Void!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Forbidden Fortress Feudal Village – Daily Event Pack #1 Supplement
description:
Every day in Feudal Japan is an adventure. Especially in the region under the shadow of the mountain. As dark clouds form overhead and dragons circle the skies, you never know what new excitement the day will bring. Will the Dark Web of the Spider Queens ensnare the village, a terrifying Water Spirit emerge from the local well, or will Fireworks burst in the air in celebration? Oni Nobu Slavers may even raid the village looking for poor souls to work their Dark Stone mines.

This 15-card Game Supplement for the Forbidden Fortress Feudal Village Expansion includes a brand new set of Feudal Village Daily Event cards to liven up your Town Stays. These cards can be added to the Feudal Village Event deck to nearly double the number of cards, adding fantastic variety to your Town Stays between every Adventure.

boardgame: Forbidden Fortress Legendary Spectres Enemy Supplement #1
description:
Legendary Spectres are the shades of once great Heroes from throughout history. As cold and lifeless shadows of their former glory, they are now filled with little more than hate and a burning desire for revenge. Often plagued with Haunted Memories of their old lives, they repeat their most powerful fears and desires, endlessly searching for something that will bring them a peace that is forever beyond their reach.

This set adds new large Enemy Record Sheets for 2 of the nightmarish Legendary Spectres – Constable Ludlow and Valentina De La Rosa, making them full Enemy mini-bosses. It also includes a set of new Threat cards that combine various Forest of the Dead themed Enemies together, and even Epic Threats to face off against a more dangerous version of each of these Legendary Spectres with their ghostly hordes.

This game supplement enhances the Forest of the Dead OtherWorld Expansion as well as the Resin Enemy Figures for Constable Ludlow and Valentina De La Rosa. That OtherWorld Expansion and those thermal plastic Miniatures are NOT INCLUDED, but sold separately.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dark Stone Magazine Issue 2 Promos
description: The second issue of Dark Stone Magazine is 68 pages filled with articles and features focusing on your favorite games from Flying Frog Productions. This issue also includes six exclusive game cards that are associated with many of the features and articles in the magazine.

boardgame: 勇闖舊城記 (Tales of the Old Fort)
description:
This is a cooperative game about building the walls and the city of Zuoying, a fort in the south of Taiwan near Kaohsiung. When certain buildings are constructed, events are triggered and can hurt the players if they are not well prepared. The game ends when the deck runs out or when you reach the end of four eras. You score points for your achievements during the game to determine how well you have fared.

The advanced game introduces secret characters and turns this game into a competitive game. Every player draws a character card, and each character scores based on a different criteria.

Game like top-trumps played with monsters in the cards: you choose a feature of the monsters (most horns, eyes, arms or legs). All the players show the topmost card in their pile and the winner takes it all.

You are the CEO of a tech-innovation startup. No money, no product, not even a pitch deck—only passion, vision, and a few comrades at your side. From a tiny office to the global stage, you must fight through trade wars, capital winters, and fierce competition.

In Tech Frontiers, a 1–4 player semi-asymmetric card-driven game, you will lead your team in one of four industries—Chips, AI, Robotics, or Medicine. Raise funds, build products, win markets… from garage to IPO!

How You Win Earn CEO wealth by filing patents, scaling product lines, capturing market share, and landing bigger funding rounds—all while guarding your voting control from dilution.

Key Systems Global Event roll-and-reveal opens each round, sparking trade embargoes, deepening the capital freeze, or jolting IPO valuations.

Multi-use cards fuel the five core actions—Research, Production, Sales, HR, Fundraising—forcing razor-sharp choices between breakthrough science, blitz marketing, and runway-saving layoffs.

Asymmetric Founder Powers and Synergy tokens let you string compound combos: one lab success can snowball into a full-stack ecosystem if timed just right.

Push financing too far and a hostile board shackles your vision; play it too safe and a cash-flow crunch will gut your roadmap. Talent raids, down-round negotiations, and side-channel sales keep table talk deliciously tense.

The game ends when any player takes their company public or after eight rounds—whichever hits first. Highest CEO net worth wins, but every founder who steers their start-up through the storm earns bragging rights.

The venture is yours… but it will be anything but easy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Empire of Grass: The Conquests of Genghis Khan
description:
Empire of Grass: The Conquests of Gengis Khan is a Solitaire Card-Wargame of the Mongol invasion of Eurasia in the twelfth century under Genghis (Chinggis) Khan.

You play the Great Khan, commanding his armies as he conquers territories, recruits other nomads into his army, and either garrisons or razes cities.

The map of your conquests is made through placing cards which represent the regions of Eurasia that the Mongols conquered (or tried to). As you expand you have to take care as neighbouring kingdoms may appear to challenge you and your allies can desert or even rebel.

An optional game allows for an uncertain world with the cards randomised.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A narrative-driven skirmish game set in the Viking Age and early medieval period, where every battle tells a story.

"Protect the village. Or burn it to the ground."

Will you stand your ground, defending your people, treasures, and livestock from invading raiders? Or will you storm the enemy’s lands, loot their riches, and set fire to everything in your path? Pillage throws you into the heart of the action—climb, swim, fight, and make every decision count.

Sequence of play
A game of Pillage is divided into game turns, themselves divided into 5 phases:

     Initiative phase
     Movement phase
     Shooting phase
     Close combat phase
     Fire phase


These phases are alternated: the player who has the initiative at the start of the turn will therefore play each phase first (player A does his movement phase, player B his movement phase, player A his shooting phase, player B his shooting phase, etc.).
This avoids the pitfall of endless game turns waiting for your opponent to complete all his actions.

Factions:
7 factions are available directly in the rulebook: Vikings, Anglo-Saxons, Franks, Normans, Bretons, Irish/Picts/Scots and Welsh!
Other factions are already available as free downloads from the Pillage website: the Rus/Varègues, the Magyars and the Byzantines!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Cuckoos love to hide and trick everyone! Get the animals in the right order to foil their plans!

Race to get your animals in the right order to claim the trophy. You'll need to listen carefully and act quickly to beat the competition.

Each player has 4 cards: 2 Sharks and 2 Frogs in a face-down pile in front of them.

The active player for the turn names the 4 cards in any order, for example "Shark, Frog, Shark, Frog".

All players, including the active player, race to grab their cards, reorder them, place the cards down in that order, and grab the trophy card from the center. Play continues until one player wins two turns in a row.

Impulse Dice Game begins by shuffling your 10 character cards. Then, your opponent shuffles your cards and returns them to you.  
Players decide who goes first, and each player draws 3 cards from their deck.  
Set the *Skill Crest* and *Action Crest* markers to *0*.  
Set the *Impulse Level Crest* marker to *3*.  
Each player starts with:  

     *0 Skill Crests and Action Crests
     3 Impulse Level Crests


Objective:
Impulse Dice Game is a card and dice game where the goal is to either defeat 3 of your opponent’s characters or destroy their 3 towers. 
you can play characters with Impulse Level crest
And his abilities with Skill Crests and move, atack and defend with Action Crests

—description from the designer

boardgame: Red Letter Yellow Letter: More Things Promo Pack
description: A promo mini-expansion featuring 25 new "Things" cards.

Kiburi is a 2-6 player strategic card game themed around lions with bluff, bravery & the wild! How will you become king of the jungle?

Using poker type mechanics, players build a pride of lions and complete in a high-stakes contest for dominance. Will you hunt with precision, deceive your rivals, or call upon the wild to tip the balance?

Kiburi is a quick to learn and play game where players have multiple paths to victory, and multiple ways to disrupt their rivals.

—description from the publisher

A social, semi-cooperative game for 3-5 players of negotiation, gambling, combo-building and reactions.  Players seek to earn victory points through loot cards they acquire using actions or by breaking the vault.  Actions and vaultbreaking both require skill tokens, which players generate by combining training cards.  Each time the vault breaks and loot cards are taken, the difficulty increases, but so do the rewards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: A Strong War: The Conflict for North America
description:
The title, A Strong War, refers to the type of war the Abenaki Nation threatened to unleash on the land-grabbing British in 1753. The sparks of war, ignited in 1754 near Fort Duquesne by an obscure colonel of Virginia militia, George Washington, spread quickly; soon, flames engulfed the entire globe as England and France vied for control of empire.  Over the next 5 years, Regular regiments from the French and British armies, American and French-Canadian provincial units, and Native warriors all fought in a chaotic and violent series of campaigns and frontier raids that culminated in the British conquest of French Canada and the defeat of the Native Nations (most of which had sided with the French).
A Strong War brings this war to life in a simple, fast-playing game.  The map (covering the region stretching from Louisbourg to Alexandria, and from New York to Lake Erie) uses point-to-point connections to highlight the key locations that were targeted during the war.  Using wooden cubes to represent the different types of forces used (French and British Regulars, British Colonials, French-Canadian Marines, French Bush Rangers, and Native American warriors), each player has only a few pieces (maximum 13 for the British and 10 for the French) to use each turn (one turn = one year, so there are 6 turns/years total). 
The heart of the game is the unique combat system where players can deploy forces to a chosen battle and then commit them one by one; or they can call off the battle if it goes badly and save some of those forces for use later in the year.  The types of forces committed also play a critical role in combat – the British want to mass their Regulars but the French want to bring in a mixed force to take advantage of the different skill sets each provides.  Thus, tension is created in trying to decide if/when (or where) it is best to commit one’s forces: do you avoid combat completely; do you call off a combat that is going badly; or, do you go all in and commit your entire force?  But if you lose a battle, initiative then swings to your opponent who may then launch an offensive. 
Each player has several paths to victory, leading to a “different” game each time, and forcing players to choose between different strategies each turn.  Finally, it is a quick game, taking just a couple of minutes to set up, and usually taking less than an hour to play to completion.  The game also plays well solitaire, though there is no dedicated solitaire system.

—description from the publisher

Operational Level Urbanised Campaigning (OPUC) is a modern operational level game which aims to investigate the interplay between rural and urban combat. Operations are over a large area (100km+), with the player in the role of a Division or Corps commander, juggling issues of civilians, logistic and national and international opinion, alongside trying manage the fight on the ground. In order to stop urban fights just becoming a +1 on combat, major urban areas are represented as side maps, so you may well have several urban fights taking place at the same time as an evolving rural fight.

OPUC typically uses a 15km hex grid and Battalion manoeuvre units. There are no capability cards, but each player has a menu of actions that their Command Points can be spent on. Players have to manage a logistic budget, and urban operations typically use up far more of that than the rural fighting. But moving away from MSRs or beyond your supply points also increases logistics demands - so what’s the right approach?

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 59 wins the game.

—user summary

Meet your new friend, Jasper Flowers. Jasper is a clock aficionado and former jewelry repair shop employee. Convicted of murder and dismemberment, Jasper is currently incarcerated at Listening Friends of America's Bozeman Facility. Jasper's former boss believes he is innocent and longs to see him walk free, while the head of LFoA is convinced Jasper is guilty - and that he is plotting something much worse.

—description from the publisher

Experience the thrill of Transformation through six gripping episodes!

Your correspondence continues with the nefarious John William James. But, the game has changed. Can you resist becoming just another pawn in his wicked game?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Listening Friends of America program.

Hunt a Killer: Awakening's storyline puts you (or you and your friends) in the shoes of a volunteer for Listening Friends of America, an organization dedicated to providing a listening ear to isolated communities across U.S. by assigning volunteers to those cut off from society in nursing homes, prisons, mental institutions, etc. As a listening Friends of America volunteer, your job is simple: take the time out of your day to read letters sent to you from your assigned friend. Meet your new friend, John William James. Convicted of murder, John is currently incarcerated at Listening Friends of America's Boseman, Montana Facility. Is he truly capable of the crimes he's been committed for, and is his journey over simply because he is "locked away"?

—description from the publisher

Concealed is a print and play solitaire card game that you can enjoy at your own pace, whether you're just looking to unwind or you're in the mood for a bit of a brain teaser.

Before the game starts draw 1 card and that will be your concealed card for the game.

The goal is to fulfill the prophecy of that card by drawing and placing cards around your board every turn until you fill up the whole board except for the 2 cards that together create the concealed card.

—description from the designer

AbduCOWS is a fast-paced game!

With each dice roll, you'll need to quickly find the correct combination between the characteristics of the 3 dice and the cow card on the table.

But that’s not all!

The fourth die is the action die, and you must perform the indicated action before placing your hand on the correct monster tile. Whoever manages to do this 6 times wins the game! But be careful—sometimes the dice can trick you and get in your way!

—description from the designer

Come one, come all—it’s time for the most anticipated event of the year at the Bubbling Charms School!

We’re talking about the Misturebimbola Cup (yes, that’s a real name at the School)!

In this tournament, spellcasting students from all over the enchanted world gather for a legendary challenge: to create the most amazing, speedy, and powerful mixtures ever seen.

But don’t be fooled: among bubbling cauldrons and very strange ingredients, you’ll need sharp memory and eagle eyes—because things don’t always go as planned...

Your rivals will also be ready to cast pesky spells on you, making your eyes tremble endlessly or giving you an uncontrollable urge to make funny faces, among other bits of magical madness!

Are you ready to do whatever it takes to win the Misturebimbola Cup?
Then take a deep breath, mix with courage (maybe with a dash of Mandrake)... and let the Mixing begin!

—description from the designer

In Obriady Kola Roku ("Rituals of the Wheel of the Year"), you become a Keeper of Traditions, guiding the rhythm of time through ancient Ukrainian celebrations. From singing spring Vesnianky to lighting Kupalo fires in summer, from preparing harvest feasts to invoking protective forces in winter — each ritual card shifts the energy of the season and reshapes your path to victory.

Your goal is to complete a harmonious cycle of rituals before the year turns. On your turn, choose from four actions: draw from the ritual deck, play a card to strengthen your cycle, disrupt others with events, or activate unique effects.

Ritual types, Obriad (rite), Mystical, Music, Food, Fire, and Aura, create synergies and rewards for those who plan ahead. But beware: unbalanced rituals or unresolved omens can disturb your progress.

With a modular market, fast-paced turns, and dynamic spirit tokens, no two games play the same. Strategic, symbolic, and culturally rich, Obriady Kola Roku is a 2–5 player set collection game that plays in 25 minutes — and once the year ends, you can’t wait to start another.

Get ready for an exciting journey across Europe. But beware! No journey is smooth sailing. Unexpected twists and turns, challenging obstacles and nerve-wracking decisions await you. Play strategically, stay flexible and use every opportunity to get ahead. The motto is: Be faster than the rest, reach the finish line first and win THE RACE!

In your turn you take the following steps:


     Spin the wheel of fortune
     Throw the dices to move forwards
     Draw an event card every time you reach a new city

Wild Hunt Festival: Anniversary Edition is a remaster of Kradia: Wild Hunt Festival. WHF is a cooperative JRPG boss-battling board game. In the game, players are tasked with forming a party of adventurers and combining their powers to defeat a series of bosses. As a cooperative fantasy-themed game, players must synergize their abilities and employ effective combat strategies via the utilization of items and their characters' unique class abilities to defeat a myriad of bosses.

Each character in Wild Hunt Festival has an assigned role (or class) such as attacker, healer, defender, etc. These characters possess unique strengths, weaknesses, and class-specific skills that all must be leveraged throughout each battle and in conjunction with one another to overcome the game's challenging monsters.

There are three different game modes in Wild Hunt Festival. Story Mode offers a campaign-like progression system where players start at level 1 and take on a series of bosses, leveling up between each fight. In Challenge Mode, players start at level 4 (max level) and fight a single super-charged boss. And introduced during the original KS campaign, Nightmare Mode. In Nightmare Mode, players will be tested to their strategic limits against some of the game's most threatening foes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wild Hunt Festival: Dankai Survey Brigade
description:
Wild Hunt Festival: Dankai Survey Brigade is an all-new standalone expansion to Wild Hunt Festival. Entirely compatible with both the Anniversary Edition and the original K:WHF, this game features content developed by Liner Note and Wild Hunt Studios.

The main feature of this game is the entirely new rogue-like Dungeon Mode. Players will traverse a map of survey cards. At each destination, the gamebook will have an event that takes place corresponding to the location on the card, and players will need to make decisions in order to gather powerful new relics and continue pushing towards the final boss fight.

Find the relics, defeat the bosses, and solve the mystery behind the Golden City of Tillia!

—description from the publisher

Take care of whatever is terrorizing the farmers. And don't get ate or anything.

A creature people call 'The Lurker' has moved into the forest around Vicksburg, leading a motley band of animals in raids against farmers and travelers. The rumor is that it can control the forest dwellers and is either a demon or military experiment. The Mayor of Vicksburg doesn't care what it is and only wants it gone so townsfolk can return to a normal life.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Wild Hunt Festival Side Stories: An Aria of Light and Shadow
description:
Wild Hunt Festival's first mini expansion, An Aria of Light and Shadow, features two new characters, two new items, a new Ikuna, and a story route of four bosses.

Players will take the role of Rui and Haruno (or any combination of heroes from compatible titles) to take down the bosses that stand in their way! These two new characters each feature unique personal mechanics that expand the strategic nature of Wild Hunt Festival.

—description from the publisher

Wild Hunt Festival: Chronicles is a big-box release that encapsulates the 2025 crowdfunding campaign to revive the Wild Hunt Festival series for new and returning players!

Wild Hunt Festival: Chronicles contains all three of the games on offer from the Kickstarter campaign: WHF Anniversary Edition, WHF: Dankai Survey Brigade, and WHF Side Stories: An Aria of Light and Shadow.

The Chronicles box is one storage solution featuring all the content from each of the three games on offer.

—description from the publisher

Which animals sleep the most?
Which are the most visited museums in the world?
Which artists have won the most Grammys?

In É Top!? Superinteressante, a new Top 10 category is revealed each round, and you have to guess something that’s on the list. But you don’t win the game just by knowing the answers! Challenge the guesses you believe are wrong to earn points.

Just be careful — if you challenge incorrectly, you’ll be the one losing...

The Vivarium Expansion isn't just an add-on — it's a transformative evolution of Machina Arcana, deepening the horror and strategy in ways never seen before. Each Vivarium scenario weaves its own dark tale. The stories expand beyond the icy mountain, unveiling haunted ruins, forsaken domains, and realms lost in time and space.


    It incorporates new decks into your game to transform every session with immersive and unpredictable challenges. 
    Every map tile now holds a surprise — encounters, evolving terrain, and environmental shifts — making each step into the unknown more immersive and unpredictable. 
    A comic-book-inspired storytelling system replaces the traditional chapter structure, making the story more immersive, easier to follow, and filled with branching choices that shape your journey. 
    New explorers, arcane relics, sinister events, and chilling encounters breathe fresh life into Machina Arcana, ensuring that every journey is shrouded in mystery and peril. 


Vivarium is fully compatible with all previous scenarios and editions of Machina Arcana.

— Description from the publisher

An outrageous, foul-mouthed, abuse-hurling, raucous, goblin name-guessing party game for up to 10 truly unhinged players.
Stiltskin is a goblin-soaked celebration of hilarity, absurdity, and chaotic revelry. Each player secretly crafts a grotesque goblin name using a shared pool of word cards. Then the chaos begins. Taking turns, players hurl accusations—outlandish guesses packed with abuse—trying to deduce the names of their rivals. Get it right, and your victim is silenced. Get it wrong, and you might just give yourself away. But beware: laugh, and you’ll be shamed. And if you laugh again… your secrets might start to slip.
Be the last Goblin whose name remains a mystery.

—description from the designer
Adds rules for the axe-wielding Rus, cavalry-mounted Magyars and mercenary-using Byzantine factions.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
PDF to add rules for flocks of sheep and beehives to the game.

Both the flock of sheep and swarm of bees are natural hazards that players can use to help themselves or hinder the opposition.

boardgame: Plastic Grenades: Rules for Generic Army Men at the Squad Level
description:
Plastic Grenades is a light wargame about skirmish actions with  plastic soldiers and vehicles at the squad level and below, which sets it apart from other rulesets of the type out there. The focus is on the individual soldiers, the challenges and risks they have to face, and their heroic, sometimes unbelievable deeds in combat.

The game is not intended to be realistic at all, though it was designed to take into account and reward the good use of military concepts and tactics such as combined arms, cover, flanking, massing of fire, momentum, morale, overwatch, range, suppression, etc.

We could fairly say that these rules intend to capture the essence of those military concepts at the firefight level, within the scope and constraints of the base game system.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: UMATAKA: A Life of Jamon Era Flame Shaped Pottery and Crown Shaped Pottery expansion
description: This expansion set introduces pottery that provides special points and pottery that gives players special abilities.

boardgame: The Dredge:  A Game about Gold Prospecting
description:
The Dredge: A Game About Gold Prospecting is about life in the Sierra Mountains as you pan for gold. Each turn will start with the Town card, where you will read it, and then pick up a Gold card and read it. Please hold on to your cards so the total amount of gold can be added together to figure out the winner.

—description from the designer

Finding yourself in an unbelievable situation straight out of a horror movie, survive a nightmare where any mistake you make will be your last. Search for keys to locked doors, solve strange puzzles and collect useful items to fend off the dangerous grasps of the undead. One thing is for certain; death is waiting around every corner.

GAIDEN - is a dungeon crawler survival thriller aimed to be challenging, portable and replay-able. Escape several locations ranging from a cruise ship on the ocean to a city being overrun by the undead, and many other locations that recall a harrowing tale.

Use the Aim & Shoot mechanic to take down enemies, no need to roll dice and rely on luck to survive, well maybe a little luck.

—description from the designer

To win in this surreal deckbuilding game players buy supernatural poker cards, burn cards to thin their decks and bend the rules using a plethora of jokers.

The game is structured like a traditional poker game, players bet chips on their poker hands by raising or calling the bet, or fold to get a free new card. Players also get to permanently remove one card from their deck per hand by doing the burn action. After each hand is played all players that didn't win or fold get to buy new special playing cards and jokers, increasing their chances of winning in later rounds.

As the game progresses players will become increasingly powerful and asymmetric in their abilities. The player who has the most poker chips after twelve rounds wins the game.

—description from the designer

Statue is a tactical 2-player abstract strategy game where players battle for control using dice as warriors. Each player begins with 8 dice housed in the central Statue of Zeus.
