On their turn, players either place, stride, or diminish—placing new dice, moving existing ones based on their face value, or forcing risky plays by lowering their own dice.

Dice move orthogonally and gain power as they move—cycling from 1 to 6. Landing on an opponent’s die removes it from the board. Strategic movement and timing are key, as the goal is simple: reduce your opponent to fewer than three dice on the board to claim victory.

—description from the designer
This complete box solution includes the deluxe version of the core game and both gameplay expansions. In addition, this collection includes 3 breaking news promo cards.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
A lightweight game with a smooth learning curve and satisfying mechanics - relaxing yet rewarding. A game where you roll dice, color terrain, and draw quirky little creatures that hatch, evolve, and gradually ramp up the challenge as the world takes shape.

Print and play roll and write board game Alien Biosphere welcomes you with three affordable starter creatures and a whole new planet to colonize. At first, you go with the flow - rolling dice, expanding peacefully. But soon, a resource shortage hits or a sudden cataclysm shatters your calm. That’s when you realize: to survive, you’ll have to use strategy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: MicroMacro: Kids – Crazy City Park: Silenced
description:
Print & play bonus case which can be downloaded from the publisher's website. The English version of this case is titled "Silenced." The German version of this case is titled “Auf Leise Weise.”

—user summary

Confusion at the concert of the famous rocker Tessy Tennessy! None of the speakers on stage are working! A technical defect or is there more to it? Who has a motive?

—description from the publisher (English)

Verwirrung beim Konzert des berühmten Rockers Tessy Tennessy! Keiner der Lautsprecher auf der Bühne funktioniert! Ein technischer Defekt oder steckt mehr dahinter?
Wer hat ein Motiv?

—description from the publisher (German)
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Age of Galaxy: Kickstarter Promo Faction cards
description:
Contain 5 Promo faction cards exclusive in the Kickstarter version. 
The 5 factions are:

Faction Name: Kollektiv
Ideology: Science
Faction Bonus: 2 Credits
Abilities:
1.When you research you may pay 1 Influcence for 2 Cruisers and 1 Discovery
2. Advanced Technology: Gain 1 Prestige for every of your Planet Types

Home Planet: Volcanic
Discard Bonus: 1 Discovery

Faction Name: Guliee
Ideology: Industrialism
Faction Bonus: 1 Credit 
Abilities:
1. anytime: Pay 1 Discovery to gain 2 Credits
2. production: Gain 1 Discovery for each 3 Productivity you have.  
Home Planet: Arctic
Discard Bonus: 3 Credits

Faction Name: Ikhno'pede
Ideology: Diplomacy
Faction Bonus: 3 Cruiser, 2 Credits 
Abilities:
1. Researching Techs that gain you Strength cost you 2 Credits less
2. When you Nominate you may pay one Productivity to gain 3 Cruisers and 1 Prestige.  
Home Planet: Desert
Discard Bonus: 1 Influence

Faction Name: Pholtar
Ideology: Culturalism
Faction Bonus: 1 Credit 
Abilities:
1. When you play this Faction gain 1 Credit and 1 Discovery for each of your colonized planets. 
2. Advanced Technology: Gain 3 Prestige and as many times as you want: Pay 1 Influence to gain 1 Prestige. 
Home Planet: Oceanic
Discard Bonus: 1 Prestige

Faction Name: Yoji'ma
Ideology:  Militarism
Faction Bonus: 2 Cruisers and 1 Credit 
Abilities:
1. Your Warm and Desert colonized Planets are always protected.
2. When you Colonize, you may pay 2 Cruisers to gain 1 Relic and 1 Prestige  
Home Planet: Warm
Discard Bonus: 1 Cruiser

boardgame: Age of Galaxy (Second Edition): Preorder Promo Faction cards
description:
This promo was added to preorders of the game and contains 5 promo factions.

So far only one faction as been revealed, but it looked like they belong all to one of the 5 different Ideologies:

Faction Name: Trogs
Ideology: Industrial
Faction Bonus: 3 Credits
Abilities: 
1. When you develop you may instead place a third disc on one of your already developed Planets. It now counts for 2 developed Planets. 
2. Advanced Technology: Gain 2 Victory points and 2 Innovations.  
Home planet: Desert
Discard Bonus: 1 Influence

boardgame: Sanctuary: The Game of Cat Rescue
description:
CATS.  They’re adorable, they’re playful, they’re our little fur babies. And now you get to rescue them! Players take charge of sanctuaries, gathering resources and taking cats out of the city shelter. They then match those adorable pets with adopters who are searching for their unique traits. 
A turn consists of three phases.

In the first phase, each player first taking three actions. These actions include pulling cats from the city shelter, drawing more goals or subsidies cards, gathering food to feed the cats, building out their sanctuaries, etc.

In the second phase, each player tries to adopt up to two cats to adopters. Each adopter has an adoption number which must be reached. The player rolls a d4 and adds the cat's adoption bonus, as well as +1 for every trait symbol it has which matches a trait symbol the adopter has. If the total is the adoption number or higher, the adoption succeeds and the player gains victory points.

In the third phase, the players check to see if the city shelter is overcrowded and any cats inside are put down, they can trade cats among each other, they feed their cats, and they roll to see how many more cats go into the city shelter and how many more adopters are drawn.

The game continues for 4-6 turns, depending on the number of players.  At the end of the last turn, players see who has accomplished several achievements. The rescuers who find the most homes for cats, and who achieve other goals, will win the game. But if city shelter fills up beyond capacity, everyone loses.

—description from the publisher

On June 5, 1944, resistance fighters listening to radio broadcasts in France heard: “This is London. First, some personal messages: ‘You wound my heart with a monotonous languor...’ That's it, it's D-Day!
In Limousin, the networks sprang into action: Destruction of locomotives, railways, viaducts, attacks on command posts, destruction of telephone lines. ... On June 8, the occupation forces' high command ordered the 2nd SS Division Das Reich to leave Montauban and move up through Limousin to “break up” the maquis, which were becoming alarmingly active. It must be ready to join the Normandy front on orders. By June 4, the Breton maquis had already begun gathering and sabotage operations. On the night of June 5, 36 French SAS soldiers parachuted into Brittany. Their mission: organize the resistance, pin down 85,000 Germans in their garrisons, and seal off Brittany. No German troops must be allowed to reach the Normandy front. During these crucial days that determined the success or failure of D-Day, will you be brave and tactical enough in your battles to slow down or even stop the advance of the 2nd SS Division Das Reich, wear down its offensive potential, and limit its atrocities? In the continuation of the scenarios, and in order to play the last game in the Liberation series (the liberation of Paris), will you be courageous and tactically astute in your battles? In these two scenarios, the fighting reaches its peak. The final victory or defeat of the Allied forces depends solely on your decisions and your intelligence in the face of an occupier who will put its last forces into a fight to the death.

—description from the designer

February 1944, German and Vichy forces realize that the Resistance is recruiting on a large scale and preparing for a widespread uprising. The Savoie maquis decide to put the Montagnard plan into action as the Allied landings approach. This plan involves direct confrontation with the occupying forces and tying down as many enemy troops as possible by applying the “silo maquis” concept. In this fight to the death, will you be able to prevent the German and Vichy armies from encircling your positions while supplying your natural fortress where you have set up your command post? Can you hold out long enough to defeat the inevitable encirclement and, with your victory points, win “the battle of the airwaves” and restore pride to the French resistance?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Lietuva: Tai ką turi žinoti kiekvienas lietuvis!
description:
What do everyone has to know about their country? Get to know or check your knowledge about longest rivers, best sportspeople, most important history's twists and turns and many more things!
New, interesting questions and new gameplay won't let you get bored, but also will give a lot of useful information!
In the game there are 800 questions divided to six categories with multiple choice answers.

By cleverly placing and moving colored round tiles, players recreate the patterns shown on the pattern cards. Each successfully recreated pattern is worth between 1 and 3 points. The other players try to prevent the player from completing the pattern.

The first player to reach 10 points wins.

boardgame: Quiz-med: Mehr als 3000 Fragen und Antworten aus allen Gebieten der Medizin
description:
Players move their pawn accross the board and answer questions from six areas of medical knowledge.
If the question is answered correctly, the player receives the card. If the question is answered incorrectly, the player sitting to their left receives the card and may answer it.
 When a player has three cards from one area of knowledge, they can exchange them for the corresponding colored ring. The player with six colored rings can move to the finish space and answer the final question. If they answer the question correctly, they win.

A space dice game about exploring and colonizing the Solar System, in which players take resources (dice) from the galactic fabric itself and use them to research new technologies, send deep space probes, move colony rockets, build habitats, and influence others.

—description from the designer

Eimel are funny wooden playing stones with a different colours, pattern and faces. 
With different ways of playing children in the age of Kindergarden (4-6 years) can learn and experience the different logically attitudes, immprove their counting, faculty of speech and social attributes.
Different types of game:

     building Houses (colours), Streets and villages (pattern)by attribute cards.
     building rows of Eimel by colour
     sorting smal Eimel to big ones by colour
     dicard the eimal from your drawn attribute card
     guessing game: which Eimal has a hidden paper under it
     Eimel Lotto
     draw an Eimel and sort it to a tree 
     memo with hidden Eimel
     passing a street when the Eimel has the correct attributes

boardgame: EXIT: The Game – Kids: The Great Bee-scape
description:
On her first flight away from the hive on a pollen collection trip, little Betty Bee is carried far away by a strong gust of wind to a distant flower meadow. To survive the return flight, she needs to fortify herself with delicious flower pollen, but it is guarded by clever creatures that present her with tricky riddles.

In EXIT: The Game – Kids: The Great Bee-scape, you get to lend a bee a helping hand to help her fly home.

Super Space Battle Frigate Assault (SSBFA) is a game management style game where you will have to manage the space frigate's shields, reactor core and weapon systems while taking fire from enemy ships and fighters and navigating through minefields and asteroids. Your mission is to hunt down the enemy dreadnaught and destroy it before it reaches an ally space station by commanding a space assault frigate.

This solo game is played using a card-deck which consist of a movement deck and a space frigate card with a set of weapon and crew cards which player's will be commanding.
Each turn the player will have to draw a card from the movement deck and resolve the event before commanding your crew to repair your ship or attack your main target. Attacking is resolve through rolling dice and adding the value of your weapon strength to beat the enemy's armor value. Your ship can suffer damage by way of events drawn by the movement deck or by the main enemy returning fire.

This game was originally entered into the 2019 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.

—description from the designer

The Messiah is back in town!

Everyone wants to sup with Jesus but there are only 12 empty seats at the table. Who will get the chance to witness another awesome miracle or listen to his latest parable?

In this trick-taking game of jostling for seats and rubbing shoulders with holy people, players send out guests in 1s, 2s, or 3s to determine who gets a seat at the supper table. Score the most points by wisely choosing who sits inside, but more importantly, who stays in your hand when the game ends.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hocus & Pocus Minus: Tempête et champignons
description:
Hocus & Pocus is a comic combined with a game, one title in a series subtitled "The comic in which you are the hero". You embody a character in a immersive story in which your choices guide your progress. You have a character sheet akin to those used in role-playing games that tracks your possessions, your special abilities, your coins, and your victory points. In general, gameplay consists in following the comic's panels — which contain clues, riddles and traps — and making choices about where to go and what to do, while using your visual perception to collect clues and be smart enough to resolve the riddles. As in any game, you can lose — sometimes really badly!

Play the role of Hocus and Pocus, one to accomplish the mystery of the mushrooms and the other to look for Dorothy.

À l’École des Fabulins, nos deux amis Hocus et Pocus doivent chacun accomplir une mission : Pocus ira au Pays des Mille et une Nuits enquêter sur de mystérieux champignons et Hocus partira en quête de Dorothy, au Royaume des nuages…

Deux aventures passionnantes mais périlleuses ! Heureusement, petit lecteur, petite lectrice, Hocus et Pocus peuvent compter sur toi et sur leurs fabulins pour les aider ! C’est toi le héros, l’héroïne !

Choisis tout.e seul.e tes actions et tes chemins en suivant les symboles et les onglets colorés, car c’est toi le héros, l’héroïne !

Dolmen is a cooperative game of logic and spatial management, where players take on the roles of druids and shamanesses working to rebuild a stone circle and contain a growing lunar curse.

Each turn, a player plays a card that determines how many steps the Sun Herald moves along a circular track. The landing space indicates the available actions for placing or moving wooden blocks within the play area. Each card also shows a 3D block configuration—an objective that must be completed before the Moon phase is triggered.

When the Sun Herald crosses certain thresholds, the Moon advances. Its movement depends on the number and value of unresolved objectives. If the Moon advances too quickly, a Spell card is revealed, introducing new complications.

The game offers adjustable difficulty through a system of Curse cards, which introduce lasting conditions. Only a few are used each game, ensuring variety from one session to the next. Dolmen is designed to be accessible to family groups as well.

—description from the publisher

2 Hedgehog cards for Sowy. Hedgehog is the 4th special card type. 
The hedgehog offers an alternative place to place owl lands, other than the journey. Lands placed on the hedgehog can be stacked, but a new land always has to have a higher value than the one currently on top. At the end of the round, all lands are placed in the discard pile, they score no points. A second hedgehog can be placed onto an existing hedgehog pile. The same rules apply.

Promo set for the Polish edition of Ecosystem (Ekosystem) with 10 cards: 5 Mermaid and 5 Waterfall cards.

Mermaid: allows for placing 2 land cards face down at the same time.
Waterfall: counts as stream with length 2.

Egyptian mythology indicates that each deceased was brought before Osiris in a trial in the Duat. During this process Anubis weighed the heart of the deceased in a scale, in one pan he placed the heart and in the other a feather. If the heart was lighter than the feather, the sentence was favorable and the deceased was assured of eternal life. If it was heavier than the feather, which implied impurity, it meant the definitive end of life.

In this game you must roll the dice and draw the mythical symbols on the mural of judgment, overcome the challenge and appear before Anubis with your score to know the fate of your soul.

Anubis is a game developed for 12th Roll & Write Game Design Contest with a duration of 10 to 15 minutes.

In this game you must roll the dice and draw the mythical symbols on the mural of judgment, overcome the challenge and appear before Anubis with your score to know the fate of your soul.

boardgame: Skifildol Italian Brainrot Trading Card Game
description:
A card game based on the Italian Brainrot memes that were all the rage in 2025. Rules, binders, and card packs were sold in Italian newsstands. Each card shows an illustration from the meme gallery. Cards are divided into Characters and Help cards. Character cards have a value in Follia ("Insanity") and possibly bonuses in attack and/or defense. Help cards describe various effects.

The players create decks with 17 Character cards and 3 Help cards. Players alternate being the attacker and the defender. Players draw a card at the time; if it is a Character, they'll use it in the upcoming duel; if it is a Help card, they keep it and continue drawing until they get a Character.

The attacker and the defender play the Character they have in hand. Next, first the attacker and then the defender can activate effects and/or use Help cards. Whoever has the highest Follia score at the end wins the round and places their Character at the bottom of their deck, while the loser discards their Character out of the game.

The player who eliminates all of the opponent's Characters wins the game.

Justice & Evil is a card game for 1 to 6 players, where the strength of Justice and Evil are tracked on tracks.  Each turn, players choose cards from their deck and reveal them, adjusting the tracks accordingly.

To win, a player needs to be on the team that wins when the game ends - generally by one of the tracks reaching the win position.

When players reveal cards, if they match another player, those players flip over any cards remaining in their hands - switching allegiances to the other team.

boardgame: The Old Ones of El Dorado
description:
In the shadows of forgotten epochs, before cultists whispered secrets in and around Arkham, there lay the hidden city of El Dorado. Contrary to tales of gold, El Dorado’s true treasure was immortality, bestowed upon it by the Old Ones.

In The Old Ones of El Dorado, you seek immortality by any means necessary in this mythical city. Together, you and your rivals are constructing a pyramid to Cthulhu. But within your personal temple, you worship your own Elder God. To fulfil your mission, you'll dispatch followers to labour in the Physical Realm and seek forbidden knowledge in the Other-Lands, all whilst striving to cling to your sanity.

The Old Ones of El Dorado is a hand management and drafting game in which the cards form a closed economy of actions. You can play a card into one of four action rows and perform both the action of the space and the action on top of your card.

However, the next player may choose to play into the same row, if there’s space, gaining them the action on their card, your card, and the action associated with that row. Each card offers two actions to choose from, but once played, the orientation becomes fixed.

Alternatively, you may decide the time is right to collect the cards from an action row. Performing the alternate actions shown at the bottom of these cards, you'll then gather them back into your hand, ready to use on a later turn. If it just so happens that a rival house has only just added to this row, then paranoia and insanity awaits them. Every creeping madness token gained in this way will block part of your resource limited storage space.

Throughout The Old Ones of El Dorado, you'll gather resources to construct temples, craft sacred masks, and raise shrines. You’ll make dark sacrifices to the Old Ones and send cultists into the village to obtain knowledge and goods. These efforts will earn you points and bring you closer to immortality as promised to you by the Old Ones of El Dorado... but is it a blessing or a curse?

—description from the publisher

Moneysharks is a strategic, understandable and action-packed card game. It combines a quick setup with tactical bidding & stitches. In 45 minutes of fun, you can show your friends who is the biggest money shark.

The Multiple Lot Fixed Placement Auctions can make Moneysharks a very strategic card game.
Shared incomes is a cooperative element and with it and the 3 finical crises give Moneysharks further strategic twists.

—description from the publisher

In Yukidarumatozantai, players are building up a snowy mountain, aiming to earn a certain number of points - which are granted to climbers who reach certain levels.

The building is reminiscent of building a house of cards, and each turn, a player takes scaffolding cards from a supply and one mountain card from their hand, adding them to the mountain the group is building. Each of the mountain cards have a special effect, such as moving one of your climbers or exchanging hands of cards.

There are specific rules around where you can add mountain cards - related to where your climbers currently are.

It comes with "rescue chopsticks" in case your climber meeples become unreachable by your hands.
designers: Tsukii Yosuke (つきい　ようすけ)

description:
Mysterious Island: The Card Game is a tense, survival adventure for 1–6 players.

Explore, craft, and survive to complete your Escape Objective before the decks run out or your friends perish. In Cooperative Mode, work together to escape. In Competitive Mode, only one player escapes the island!

Player Turns & Actions

The game progresses through a series of phases: Day Phase, Player Phase, Night Phase, Player Phase. The Day and Night decks act as a timer. When these decks run out the island explodes in a volcanic eruption.

Day Phase
-Reveal a Day Card.
-Apply its effect to ALL players.

Player Phase
One at a time, each player takes 2 actions during their turn. The 5 available actions are:

Craft
Explore    
Rest
Move
Campfire

Night Phase
Reveal a Night Card.
Apply its effect to ALL players.

Escape Objectives
Escape Objectives, which are drawn and start of the game, lists the required Craft cards in order to build each objective. Building an Escape Objective is done at the last Island Location...unless you discover secrets on the island! In competitive mode, these escape objectives should be kept secret!

End-Game Scenario
Before players attempt to build their Escape Objective, check the number of DANGEROUS cards in the pool. (Cooperative and Competitive!)
• If 4 or more DANGEROUS cards are unresolved, the escape fails.
• Players must resolve at least 2 of the DANGEROUS cards before another escape attempt.

Win & Loss Conditions

Cooperative Mode

Win – The Escape Objective has been built.
Lose – All players dead, or the Day & Night decks are depleted.

Competitive Mode

Win – First player to craft and activate their personal Escape Objective.
Lose – All players dead or Day & Night decks depleted before escape.

—description from the designer

Hunter vs Prey thrusts you into a brutal alien war already in motion—and you’re right in the middle of it. Stranded deep inside an icy, labyrinthine alien temple, you control one of five human explorers (3 male, 2 female) desperately navigating the ruins of a battlefield between ancient alien species. The original Prey—terrifying bioengineered creatures—are trapped in the temple. But now, a new faction arrives: the Hunters. Their mission is clear—eliminate the Prey—but the appearance of humans presents a new and tempting target. In this war, the enemy of my enemy is my friend… until they’re not.

Designed for 1–5 players with full solo/co-op and competitive modes, Hunter vs Prey blends stealth, survival, and tactical combat across a multi-level modular map. The environment itself evolves each round—chamber doors randomly open and close based on card draws, locking players in or out of critical zones. Secret passages, underground vents, and high-level access areas become available only to those with the right gear and skill upgrades. You’ll have to adapt quickly: one round you’re the hunter—next round, you’re the prey.

Players can upgrade their characters by unlocking powerful new weapons, stealth tech, or alien-enhanced abilities—just like in a survival horror video game. Each character has a hidden objective that may conflict with the team’s mission—or the others’ lives. Stealth is a weapon, especially in the hands of the Hunters, who can appear from nowhere with deadly precision. Meanwhile, the Prey can mutate, ambush, and outmaneuver—especially when the temple itself seems to favor them.

The climax builds toward The Mission: reaching the Queen’s Chamber, a massive circular arena with enormous temple gates that only open once the uniquely designed rotating ring board—physically attached to the main map—is unlocked. Fail to stop the Queen’s escape, and she will annihilate all of mankind. Every session of Hunter vs Prey is cinematic, suspenseful, and dangerously unpredictable. Choose your side carefully… and remember, the temple chooses no favorites.

—description from the publisher

PORT, STARBOARD … OR off BOARD?!

With the power of their own memories, 2-5 sailors defy all dangers and salvage valuable treasures from the depths of the Memorian Sea.
All sailors on board! Captain Memo has the helm of the ship firmly in his hands. And because everyone takes over his role from time to time, his course manoeuvres may be best remembered, but not always in a good way.
Treasures lie dormant in the sea between reefs and giant squid. If the captain succeeds in lifting five of them per voyage, the sailors are happy too. However, if his nautical manoeuvres become too tricky for them, clever sailors leave the ship – or even call for mutiny, only to do much better as captain themselves … or not …

—description from the publisher

Red Alert: New World Order is a strategic, alternate-WW2 history board game that captures the explosive, over-the-top warfare of classic real-time strategy games. Players command powerful factions, build sprawling bases, manage resources, and unleash powerful units and structures — from tank divisions to Tesla-powered weapons — in a race for rare earth minerals with shifting alliances.

Red Alert: New World Order blends territory control, base construction, and tech-tree advancement into a deeply tactical tabletop experience. You’ll harvest resources, deploy, tanks, and air units, and upgrade your arsenal with experimental super-weapons. Whether you choose to align with the Allied Nations or the Soviet Bloc, your path to victory lies in striking the balance between defense, expansion, and overwhelming force.

Combat is fast-paced and cinematic, with streamlined mechanics that replicate the feel of real-time strategy while offering deep strategic choices. Every battle is dynamic—your opponents may deploy devastating bombardment form the Aurora Bomber, launch an Iron Shield defensive grid structure, or electrify an entire battlefield with advanced Tesla tech. Commanders must adapt quickly, or face annihilation.

With European classic battlefield maps, cities, snow terrain, naval assault maps and more, the gigantic maps, asymmetric factions, faction-specific abilities, and scenario-driven campaigns, Red Alert: Mew World Order delivers a nostalgic-yet-modern board game experience. It’s a tribute to the golden era of RTS games — now reborn on the tabletop with explosive detail and alternate-history flair.

—description from the publisher

Elemental Puzzle is the story of Ikit and his group of young Shaman friends, with the mission of capturing the 6
Elemental Spirits escaped in the Klimat Archipelago, facing endless challenges and summoning Spirit Cards with Extraordinary Powers!
To complicate their task there will be the crazy Dr. Toxic and the powerful thousand-year-old witch S-TAR! Prepare your Spirit Cards and use the PUZZLE Skill to create combinations of Unique and Incredible Creatures! Shout like Ikit: Go! Go! Go! Puzzle!!!

Elemental Puzzle is a Trading Card Game for ages 12 and up, for games from 2 up to 4 players, lasting approximately 30-60 minutes. Each player begins by shuffling his Deck of at least 30 cards and the order of play is chosen, for example by rolling a die. Each player draws 10 cards and then places 9 of them face down on his Game Field. At the beginning of each turn, if possible, you must draw a card. The Spirit Cards you have on the Field represent your respective Life Points, which can decrease as well as increase, if you play new Spirits on the Field. When you reduce the number of opponent Spirits to 0, by attacking with your Spirits or activating their Skills, you win the Game!

boardgame: Vista, Nariz y Boca: El Juego del Vino
description:
Use your senses to discover the characteristics of other players' varietals before you're eliminated from the game. With Vista, Nariz y Boca (Sight, Nose, and Mouth), you can show off everything you know about Argentine wines.

The game features 20 cards of red and 20 white grape varieties produced in our country. It's an ideal tool for learning more about our national beverage. You'll also discover 60 cards with the characteristics of each grape variety, all related to the sensory spectrum stimulated when tasting a glass: the hue of the colors and brightness; the aroma of flowers, fruits, spices, or nuts presented to the nose; the tannins, acidity, astringency, and finish.

Drinking wine is also sharing it. Toasting is simultaneously giving a toast, offering what we make to someone else.

Vista, Nariz y Boca is more than a game about Argentine wines. It's a celebration of love, friendship, and laughter.

Build collections of mates, bombillas, and thermoses and become the best cebador!

El Juego del Mate is the official game of the National Mate Festival. It consists of 46 cards representing elements of the mate world, such as bombillas, mates, and kettles. It also has five jokers whose illustrations refer to the ways we like to drink mate, whether bitter, sweet, with herbs, tereré, or cooked. Finally, the deck is completed by five special cards that represent the ideal step-by-step process for preparing a good mate.

To play, you should know that the mechanics fall within the drafting family: you will receive eight cards and must leave one in your place at the table, passing the remaining seven to the player on your left. Consequently, you will receive seven cards. We repeat the sequence, leaving one card and passing six, which also gives us six. We repeat until everyone has eight cards in their area of ​​the table. At that point, we present the combinations we've made: the best combinations earn us higher scores.

Mate is a game for the whole family and can be enjoyed with friends, with quick 15-minute games for two to five players ages eight and up.

Now that you know how to play, we're ready to start drinking mate: LET THE ROUND BEGIN!

Siege of the Forgotten is a cooperative boss raid experience set in the Alpha Clash universe. Designed for 1–5 players, this standalone game pits you and your allies against the relentless forces of the Discarded, culminating in an epic battle with the monstrous Lord Krung. Whether you're playing solo or as a team, every decision counts in this high-stakes, cinematic showdown.

Players take on the role of unique Contenders—each with their own deck and playstyle—working together to complete objectives, manage escalating threats, and survive a gauntlet of devastating boss effects. The encounter unfolds in phases, with dynamic boss flips, evolving mechanics, and a boss deck that adapts and intensifies as the game progresses.

Built on the foundation of the Alpha Clash Trading Card Game, Siege of the Forgotten is fully self-contained. However, longtime fans will find rich lore connections, exclusive cards, and PvE-ready content that deepens the narrative arc of the franchise.

Whether you're new to Alpha Clash or a returning player seeking a new way to engage with the universe, Siege of the Forgotten delivers a tense, strategic, and deeply thematic cooperative experience that rewards teamwork, timing, and tactical play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pst! oder Nur immer langsam voran.
description:
The players take turns to roll and move.

The game was probably published about 1890.

Card & Coin: Metal Edition is a beautiful set of multi-use playing cards and 101 metal coins of a variety of denominations. It contains a deck of 54 standard playing cards with additional elements to expand the possible games you can play. It includes rules for 4 1-4 player games.

Kin Kado: A modern twist on poker. Over 7 rounds, 1-4 players will Sell and Buy cards from their hand in order to build a winning poker hand. There is no player elimination and players can always participate in each round!

Twinsy: A trick-taking 'duel'ing game for 1-4 players. Players will race to complete 1 of 3 objectives first; Fill the garden with the most pieces, race to the other side of the shape track, or collect a set of 5 cards. The actions players can take on a turn are based on the cards they played during the trick, manage to play a pair of the same suit to inch closer to the finish line.

Ranchin: A trick-taking space game, where players are ship wranglers, trying to park the most valuable ships into their supply. With each trick, players take a major or minor action to manipulate the board of coins. Players will Build, Move, Fight, and Park ships for 3 rounds. Whoever wrangled the most ships is the winner. This game heavily uses the rotated side of the multi-use cards. It's quite the brain burner.

Bizmess: A trick-taking market manipulation game, where players take on the role as portfolio managers trying to make their clients the most cash. The most convincing (winner) of each trick gets to flip a switch in the market and change the price of the stocks with cards that were played that round. In the end, only stocks the public is interested in is worth anything. The best capitalist is the winner. If no one wins, the market crashes.

While Card & Coin includes these 4 games, the possibilities are endless and a digital living rulebook is included that is expanded over time.

—description from the designer

The MIRU Hex Kit is a physical manifestation of the solo game series: MIRU. Set in 2125, after an accident with a robot that kills your brother, you set out on a long and harrowing journey to kill the god responsible, an AI that took over the world a century prior. Spanning across 3 books, you'll adventure through an unknown world and face its horrors as you defend your people and yourself from an all powerful god and her giant machines.

The components in this box facilitate the play for the MIRU series, but was also designed to be a general ttrpg set of components, with over 80 terrain hex tiles and nearly 200 tokens representing items and enemies for your players to interact with physically on the table.

Besont is a two player placement game based on the nim principle where players place generic pieces ('gold coins') on the board.

Taking turns players lay 1 - 5 coins along any straight line, they don't even have to be connected. The winner is the one who lays the last coin on the board, the 15th coin. It's not about making lines, it's about setting traps.

The theme is tied up with Cornish history. The game board is the Cornish coat of arms made up from the fifteen gold coins Cornwall had to pay for the Duke of Cornwall's release.

Coming back to the game mechanic nim principal has been adapted to include diagonals and instead of taking away, you lay down.

Misty Woods Escape is a family-friendly competitive board game for 2–4 players, set in an enchanted forest that doesn’t want you to leave.

Each player controls a brave animal adventurer exploring shifting paths, dodging carnivorous plants, and collecting glowing crystals needed to activate the ancient portals hidden deep within the Misty Woods.

On your turn, you’ll reveal new forest tiles, race to claim crystals, and play powerful event cards—some helpful, some designed to sabotage your rivals. Watch out for shifting fog and living trees that rotate paths or block your way entirely.

As the forest comes alive, time is running out—will you be the first to escape, or be lost forever in the woods.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The King Fun Game: Burger King Kids Meal Board Game
description: The King Fun Game created in 1978 by the Burger King Company to be given away with kids meals. Remove the perforated game pieces and top to spin and move the pieces to reach the king to win.

This HUGE shiny d10 is used to keep track of your level, but that's not all; it also includes a set of cards to use with it!

When you use this Level Die to track your level in any game of Munchkin, you may also play these cards. Each card is, of course, themed to the number of your level, from 1-9. The cards are not shuffled into the deck – in fact, they have unique backs to help keep them separate.

Did we mention this die is REALLY BIG?

Be very careful where you roll this die. It weighs over a pound and measures 80mm across! And no, really, it shouldn't be used as a weapon. Unless you're in a dungeon . . .

Alice and her sister, Lorina, have received a hastily scrawled note, likely from the White Rabbit, about the need for help in Wonderland. They’ll set off on a journey to rescue all manner of characters from the tale, find pieces of Alice’s story, an occasional tool, and a not so trustworthy cat, because after all, we’re all mad here!  Help Alice and Lorina navigate Wonderland collecting story elements and finding denizens all in the hopes of landing the best score. Also avoiding the wrath of the Red Queen, she’s still mad.

Players will select 1 card a turn from the tea cup shaped selection of cards. Will the Tea Party and its participants score the most? Maybe the Royal Court is the way to go? Each game 3 of the 18 cards will be scoring cards for the rest of the cards in the Tea Cup layout.

The game is played in 3 rounds.  After the 3rd round, the player with the highest score, wins.

Play with 1 or 2 players, or print a second copy of the game for a 3-4 player game

Alice in a Wonderland Rescue can even be played with other games in the Rescue series including Safe and Hound and Otters to the Rescue!

—description from the designer

Ghost Kids Full House is a card game where each player will add a card from their hand to the end of a central tableau on their turn. Each of the cards represent a child going trick or treating on Halloween, and when a player adds their card, they flip over any matching children in the row - each card has a different child on the back.

Optionally, a player can also use the effect of the child they played, such as flipping over a card of their choice from the row.

Then, if there are multiple of any children in the row, the player collects them. If a player ever has a "full house" in front of them, the round ends and scores are calculated - based on such things as having children that match the leader and variety of different children.

The game ends once a player has earned a certain number of points and the player with the most points wins.
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
In Sushi Fight, players are thematically at a conveyor sushi restaurant, competing to eat delish dishes.

A selection of sushi cards are dealt out to the table and then players simultaneously choose an action from their hand - things such as drawing a certain number from the market, or "eating" the cards they've previously acquired.

The game ends when the deck of sushi cards is exhausted and then players calculate the points earned from collecting certain combinations of sushi. The person with the most points wins.

boardgame: Final Score Soccer World Cup Edition
description:
Everything needed to play is included.  There are no additional game parts to buy!

As the name implies, this quick play game provides you with a final score for a soccer match. Allowing you to rapidly play matches and watch standings unfold before your eyes. However, there is more to the game than just generating scores. There is plenty of excitement and drama along the way, keeping the game play exciting and compelling. You will have late in the match goals, equalizers and fantastic saves.

Each team is rated individually for Offense, Defense, and Pressure situations at home and on visiting turf. This is not just a home adjustment. Each team is literally rated individually for home and away. This gives you a very accurate portrayal of the squad's true abilities. And while there will surely be some surprises along the way, since this is a game, the accuracy of the game will astound you.

The History of the World Cup contains every World Cup competition from 1930 to 2018.  Not only do you get all of the World Cup teams, you also get every team that competed in a final qualifying group.  In some seasons you get every team that competed in a qualifying match!  This is a comprehensive history of the World Cup Competition.

We have also included normalized ratings for every team, so you can play out the Ultimate Tournament to find out which World Cup squad was the best of All-Time!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Guerra y Progreso: Roma vs Cartago
description:
In this fast-paced asymmetric card duel, you’ll play as either Rome—the unstoppable, expanding republic—or the powerful merchant city of Carthage. Develop unique technologies and advance by exploiting your lands, but beware—War can break out unexpectedly at any moment!

Will you relive the glory of the Roman Empire, or rewrite history by leading Carthage to victory? The course of history is in your hands!

War and Progress: Rome vs Carthage is an asymmetric two-player game featuring unique cards for each side. These cards are gradually revealed during the game as players invest in developing their civilization—or, if they prefer, they can focus on scoring as many Glory Points (Victory Points) as quickly as possible.

But if a player spends their resources at the wrong moment, they may find themselves without troops when their opponent eventually plays the War card.

—description from the publisher (translated)

En este rápido duelo de cartas asimétrico podrás jugar con Roma, la imparable república en expansión o con la poderosa ciudad mercante de Cartago. Desarrolla tecnologías únicas y progresa explotando tus tierras, pero cuidado ¡pues la Guerra siempre puede desatarse por sorpresa!
¿Revivirás la gloria del Imperio Romano o cambiarás la historia liderando Cartago hacia la victoria? ¡La historia está en tus manos!
Guerra y Progreso: Roma vs Cartago, es un juego asimétrico para dos jugadores, con cartas únicas para cada lado que se irán revelando a lo largo de la partida si el jugador invierte en Desarrollar su civilización, o si quiere, invertirá en ganar lo más pronto posible el mayor número de puntos de Gloria (Puntos de Victoria). 
Pero si el jugador gasta sus recursos en el momento inadecuado, podrá verse sin tropas para cuando su oponente juegue eventualmente la carta de Guerra.

—description from the publisher

In The Crossword Factory you’ll compete against your friends to make the best crossword. Each round you'll all secretly pick a letter and then announce it to the table. Then you have to add your letter and your two neighbors' letters to your crossword grid.

The game book contains 100 player sheets with unique challenges, clues and setups to try.  All you need to play are some friends and some pencils. So…ready to make a crossword?

—description from the designer

After centuries of slumber, dragons have awakened and returned to the realm.

Rival guilds now seek to reassemble the ancient dragon scrolls, legendary texts said to contain the long-lost secrets of taming these powerful beasts. With only partial sets in their possession and no way to succeed alone, guilds must exchange fragments to complete their scrolls and claim mastery.

Choose carefully. The right piece could bring you closer to control. The wrong one might lead your opponent astray… exactly as you intended.

In Dragon Scrolls, players compete over three rounds to build the most powerful scroll. At the start of each round, players are dealt a hand of three scroll fragments. All players simultaneously select one fragment to draft to their own scroll and one fragment to pass, face down, to both of their neighbors.

Finally, players must flip one of the fragments passed to them face up, choose to keep it, or push-their-luck and discard it to draft the remaining fragment!

—description from the designer

The Fire Department needs your help putting out fires around town! Pick a color and pump the firetruck for your color water droplets. Then rush to the nearest house card to extinguish your fires! Is it a 1, 2, or 3 alarm fire? Hurry and run back to the firetruck for more water droplets and save the day! Be the first firefighter to douse all their flames and win!

How to play:
Collect your droplets, find the house cards that match, and place the correct number on each card - the first player to douse all their flames wins!

—description from the publisher

STICK IT TO THE COMPETITION: Take the classic strategy game to a whole new level! The tabletop board is made of special sticky material that instantly grips and allows the included balls to stick to the board, making gameplay more exciting and unpredictable.

REUSABLE AND WASHABLE: The best part? The board is washable and reusable, so you can play over and over again without worrying about making a mess.

HOW TO PLAY: It's like a classic game you love, but with a cool twist. You'll be bouncing balls on the table onto a sticky target to make your moves. Try to get three in a row while blocking your buddy's shots.

—description from the publisher

A two-player fantasy war game where two competing war bands go head to head to ravage the most loot. Player's secretly give orders to their three war bands and reveal them simultaneously and then enact those orders. However, each order counter has a number of war bands that can take orders that turn so players have to strategize between giving single order type to multiple units or multiple orders to a single war band. Players fight it out in this fantasy setting and the first to seven loot points win!

Available as a free Print-n-Play from the designer or as a part of the first issue of the One Page Game Jam magazine.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This mini-expansion card pack adds the Axolotl character and a new mechanic: the Big Red Button to FlipToons.

Is your cast not living up to their celebrity? Smash your button to re-do your Flip and try for a lineup with better on-screen chemistry.

Once the button is smashed, it's broken! (Flip it over to the broken side.) Maybe you'll be lucky enough at the market to hire the Axolotl mechanic who can fix your button right up.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Converge: Keepers of Order – Solo Mode
description:
The officious Directors that oversee the details of daily life on Malvios are bound by rigid plans and archaic regulations. Can you utilize these calculating administrators to achieve your goals?

Keepers of Order is a solo expansion for Converge which works with any combination of skillsets. With only 5 turns to play, it’s a race to achieve goals and earn stars before time runs out! On every turn, one of the opponent’s cards will be revealed, serving as both one of their faction members and a shared goal. Play 2 cards each turn to counter their steady advance to victory.

Each of the solo cards features an ability on one side and a goal on the other. At the start of the game, pair 3 ability cards with 3 face-up goals. You may use the ability at any time as one of your actions, or complete the paired goal to gain the ability to use as an additional action when you complete the paired goal, giving you an extra chance to turn the tides in your favor. By managing the artifact and shrewdly using your abilities, you might just save the day.

In Swider, each player is a spider trying to complete their web before the other players.

Specifically, players are building out a grid of cards in front of themselves, such that it is in numerical order, spiraling out from the center. Generally a person may play one card on their turn, but certain conditions - such as playing a card that is +/- 1 from an adjacent card, or cards that share the same one's digit - allowing playing more than 1.

When a player's web reaches a certain level, they can score one of their scoring cards, and the first player to complete a certain number of them triggers the end of the game. The person with the most points wins.
designers: イワムラ タクヤ (Takuya Iwamura)
artists: イワムラ タクヤ (Takuya Iwamura)
publishers: キューハチドッグ (Que Hachi Dog)

description:
In The Craftsmice, players draw gems out of a bag to attempt to acquire certain tool cards. The gems are in three colors, and within each color, there are three different shapes.

Players can spend or store some of these gems to acquire cards that will allow them to modify future gem draws or cards which will grant points.

The game ends when only a certain number of gems are left in the bag. Players earn points for certain cards completed and leftover gems. The person with the most points wins.

This fun and imaginative game calls on players' creativity to sell hilarious and unlikely items.

One player is the Buyer. The rest of the players are Sellers.
The Buyer draws a Sale Card, which gives Sellers all the instructions for their pitch (find a name, a price, a slogan...).

Sellers have got each a different card of an article to sell.
According to the Sale Card, they have 1, 2 or 3 minutes to prepare their best pitch.

Then, they pitch (one after another) their article, being the best convincing Seller they can. They may talk, sing, mime...

The Buyer will choose the Seller he found amazing and give him/her the Sale Card. The Seller wins the number of points on the card.
Next round: one of the Sellers becomes the Buyer!

A humorous game to let go and laugh out loud, which can also be played in teams.

—description from the publisher

In Ad Astra: Mineral Race, players are rival factions racing to colonize and control a newly discovered planet rich in rare minerals. Set in a high-stakes sci-fi universe, this Euro-style RTS board game offers streamlined rules where gameplay is key, and every choice matters.

Ad Astra blends timing decisions with accessible mechanics, giving players a wealth of options without overwhelming complexity. The modular board, evolving production systems, and variable setups ensure no two games are ever the same.

Immerse yourself in a thematic sci-fi setting that draws you into its story — from the moment your ships land to your final push for planetary dominance.

Will your faction rise above the stars — or be left in the dust?

—description from the designer

Mixology Party is a real-time trading game where downtime doesn't exist. In this game, players try to stock their bar and craft as many cocktails as they can. But the catch is that while the active player is gathering ingredients and mixing cocktails, the rest of the players are allowed to trade freely, so the active players would need to finish their turn as quickly as possible to avoid missing out on the juicy deals.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Elemental Puzzle TCG: Terrestrial Blaze – Starter Deck
description:
With the "Terrestrial Blaze" Starter Deck you can gather the Energy of the Earth's Core and Blast Away all your opponents!!!
In this Starter Deck you can find the Spirit Cards of Ikit, the main character of Elemental Puzzle, like Money Monkey, Drag-Onsen and his Ace Spirit, the unpredictable Tiki Skate!

boardgame: Sword of Fire: The Battle of Lutter August 27, 1626
description:
After having passed most of the summer season waiting upon events, King Christian IV of Denmark decided to stir himself and join his Protestant army to combat the Catholic League forces under the command of General Johan Tzerclaes, Count of Tilly. Christian’s force was weakened from the start after dispatching several detachments to garrison captured cities and giving a portion of this command to General Ernst von Mansfield to attack another Catholic force under the command of General Wallenstein. When Mansfield was defeated at the Battle of Dessau Bridge, King Christian decided to retire back to his base at Wolfenbüttel, but the King’s movement was slow and his orders at times confusing and contradictory. When the opposing armies joined battle, Tilly outnumbered his Protestant opponent. What ensued was one of the more sanguine and violent battles of a war that had already become infamous for its levels of violence and destruction. Can you do as well or better?

Each combat unit represents from 250 to 1500 soldiers, and it is rated, from left to right, for combat morale and movement. Each infantry strength point is around 150 men, each cavalry strength point around 80 men. Each leader is rated for Command (indicating how many activation chits that leader has) and Movement. Each hex is about 75 meters across. Each turn represents a half hour of time.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Elemental Puzzle TCG: Oceanic Tornado – Starter Deck
description:
With "Oceanic Tornado" you can ride the Rough Waves of the Sea and reach Victory Island, thanks to a Roaring Strategy!!!
In this Starter Deck you can find the Spirit Cards of Kitoi, the main rival character of Elemental Puzzle, like Basketball Fish, Rock Rocket and his Ace Spirit, the Guardian of Sea Treasures, Lock Nessie!

—description from the publisher

Alibi is a hilariously crude, role-playing card game of quick wits and clever ad libs for 3+ player. This is the perfect party game for a rowdy night in or after a crazy night out. You make the stories; where the game goes is entirely up to you! What is your Alibi?

One player is The Detective; they are the judge, jury, and executioner. They are also the scorekeeper. The Detective's job is to interrogate each suspect and dissect their alibis to determine who is most guilty. The remaining players are the suspects. The suspects are trying to make sure they don't have the most points of guilt at the end of the game. The player with the most points of guilt loses, the others win.

boardgame: Elemental Puzzle TCG: Power of Nature – Booster Pack
description:
With the "Power of Nature" Booster Pack you can awaken the Ancient Elements and shape your future with the Unbeatable Forces of Nature!
In this Booster Pack you can find the Spirit Cards used by Ikit, Kitoi, Rikat and Kawait, the 4 main Heroes of Elemental Puzzle, like Torture Totem, Leviathank, Samurough and Chimerafro!
All these 4 Multi PUZZLE Spirits are very Powerful, each with an Original Form of 4!

—description from the publisher

Mision Escorpion ("Scorpion Mission"/"Sting Bot") is a fast-paced, real-time dexterity game for 2 players, designed for children ages 4 and up. Players complete in a tense reflex duel: the "Swiper" player attempts to sneakily turn the turn on the mechanical Robo Scorpion's back, while the "Stinger" controls the scorpion’s sting via a manual trigger.

The objective is to collect 10 coins by successfully turning the dial without being "stung." If the scorpion’s tail strikes your hand, you lose coins and your turn ends. A stamped red dot serves as a playful but tactile penalty, reinforcing the risk-and-reward dynamic. The game requires no batteries and features simple physical components, including a die, plastic coins, and a Robo Scorpion with a functional snapping tail mechanism.

Gameplay highlights include:

Opposed reflex timing: One player reacts while the other tries to misdirect.

Manual trap activation: The sting is not automated but controlled by the opponent, adding human tension.

Light antagonism: The theme and design emphasize dramatic showdowns with a comic tone.

Its simple ruleset and bold tactile design make it especially engaging for younger players seeking a slapstick-style showdown.

Similar to:

Loopin' Louie - both use reactive mechanisms with a humorous "trap" twist.

Crocodile Dentist - shares the surprise/tension of a physical snap as a loss condition.

Pie Face! - uses opponent-triggered tension and comic payoff in a head-to-head format.

Saddle up for a Wild West adventure as your match cute kitties based on their cowboy attributes. In this cooperative game, kids turn over kitty cards and look for matching boots, bandanas or hats. For any match you find, you get a cowboy cookie. If you don't find any matches, you lose that cat to the Scratching Post. If you can fill your cookie jar with 10 cowboy cookies before you lose 3 cats to the Scratching Post, you win!

In RISK G.I. JOE: Special Missions, 2-4 players each join one of two factions — G.I. Joe or Cobra — and seek to complete missions or resolve plots in an effort to score the most objective points (OP) after three rounds of play.

One of five master scenarios guides the action, leading to conflicts erupting in hotspots around the world. The game includes multiple unit types — GIs, Skystrikers, Wolverines, Cobra Troopers, Rattlers, and H.I.S.S. — with variable movement, initiative, and battle bonuses, and units defeated in battle are only temporarily lost, cycling back into play over time. Action cards create new avenues for moving and gaining units not present in the traditional game, and scenario and objective cards will influence player strategy.

boardgame: Command at Sea: 5th edition – War at Sea 1926-1955
description:
Command at Sea (CaS) is an award-winning World War II tactical naval rules set. 
It can simulate almost every naval operation of  World War II with accurate and consistent results. Victory depends as much on the tactics and planning of the players as on the ships and equipment under their command.

CaS emphasizes technical and historical accuracy using a streamlined, easy to follow rules structure that keeps the action fast-paced and fun. Combat resolution is built around a single die roll philosophy that keeps the players focused on the battle and not on the mechanics of the rules.

Command at Sea allows the player to experience the kind of decisions that a ship or battle group commander must make when he fights a sea battle during World War II. Victory depends on making the right decision at the right time. CaS is more than just a game, it is an accurate simulation. The models that make up the heart of CaS are based on historical technical data and extensive operational analysis. Tactics that were successful in actual battles will also work in CaS.

Some key points about the CaS game system are:

     Detailed but simple gunnery system
     Elegant torpedo rules including a lead angle solver
     Accurate armor model including torpedo protection systems
     Realistic damage results with varying degrees of severity for fire and  flooding
     Historically accurate modeling of sensors (radar, HFDF, sonar & visual)
     Innovative aircraft rules which facilitate rapid resolution of mass air attacks
     Simplified procedure for depth charge attacks
     Sidebars provide historical background and a description of some of the matematical models used to calculate game values.


Our goal in designing Command at Sea was to make World War II naval combat as exciting and fast as possible, but without compromising historical accuracy or losing the tactical "feel" of the game. We believe we did just that, but we invite you the players to "assume the watch" and judge for yourselves just how well we hit the target.

—description from the publisher

Simpan Baik-Baik (loosely, “Save It Carefully”) is a light economic board game themed around personal finance and savings. Set in contemporary Malaysia, players take on the roles of individuals navigating life’s financial ups and downs—making career choices, earning income, saving for retirement, and handling life’s unpredictable challenges.

The game simulates a financial journey through career decisions (Corporate Employee or Self-Employed) and life events represented by career, bitter & sweet, and savings spaces on the board. Players manage their earnings by collecting salaries, contributing to EPF savings, and reacting to event cards that introduce windfalls or setbacks.

Each decision—whether to further your education, buy a property, switch jobs, or continue working—affects your financial health. Players build their savings and EPF contributions while aiming to collect dividends and retirement bonuses. The game culminates at the retirement space, where players tally their cash, EPF savings, dividends, and retirement bonus.

The objective is to retire with the highest total wealth, balancing career growth, saving discipline, and responding wisely to life’s uncertainties. Rather than hoarding cash, the game rewards setting and achieving financial milestones along the way.

Simpan Baik-Baik is a family-friendly game (playtime: 30–45 minutes), designed to make financial literacy fun and relatable. Its simplified mechanics mirror real-world financial planning, helping players understand the importance of savings, income management, and adapting to life's surprises.

boardgame: Citizens of the Spark: Rise & Fall Mini-Campaign
description:
Rise and Fall is a mini-campaign for Citizens of the Spark, in which players compete to collect the most prestige over five games.

Prestige is awarded to players at the conclusion of each game.
based on the total sparks for each player

If the winning player has not previously won a game in the
campaign, they are also awarded bonus prestige.

After five games, total each player’s prestige. The player(s) with the most prestige has been most

instrumental in rebuilding Fera and is declared the winner!

The Station Brakes Family Game gives your family the opportunity to enjoy each other's company and learn faith-building truths. Begin your journey in the blue SUV and roll the die. Answer the Mental Mirage trivia, accomplish the Can-Do Canyon challenge or lasso the DVD Clip Dispenser to continue your trip. The first person to make it to Art's Stop, Shop and Roll wins!

—description from the back of the box

600 Questions covering Classic Rock.
Includes 100 question cards, custom die, and rules.

A mouse family has moves to a new town. The mice are still getting used to a new home, but they already invited all the neighbors to a housewarming party. What should they eat? Luckily, there is a pizzeria right above the mouse house, where tasty morsels fall off the tables every now and then. But be careful - a pet raccoon zipping around the pizzeria tends to drag all the food it can get its paws on into its pantry. so, keep your eyes open: explore the mouse house and stock enough treats so that no one leaves the mousewarming party hungry!

How to Play:

Drop the food die into the special 3D pizzeria. Where did the food crumbs fall?
Move one of the mice. Pick up the fallen food and deliver it to a kitchen, or the thrifty raccoon will snatch it from under your nose!
Feed all the guests before the raccoon stocks up its pantry.

—description from the publisher

Game set-up: Shuffle all the cards and form a draw pile in the centre of the table. The first player is chosen at random.
How to play: Each player, in turn, completes the following steps: 
They MUST draw a card from the pile and place it, face up, in the centre of the table. If a card of the same sport has already been drawn, the new card is placed on top. When a flag card is drawn, it is placed on the table, face up. 
Then, they MAY, if they wish, take all the cards of the same sport (the entire column) from the centre of the table. The sport chosen may be different from the card they have just drawn. When a player takes a column of cards, they gather them into a pile in front of themself. Each player can only collect 1 sport per round. 
End of the round: A round comes to an end as soon as the number of flag cards in the centre of the table matches the number of players. Each player then counts their collected cards and records their score. Then, shuffle all the cards to start a new round.
End of the game: The game is played over 3 rounds. At the end of the 3 rounds, the player who has accumulated the most cards in total wins the game.

boardgame: Life in Reterra: Local Game Shop Promo Card
description:
A promotional size 2 building, replaces Restaurant

Ability

Purple
Points - 0
When built, choose a relic type and place 1 inhabitant on any empty space next to each relic of that type in your community.

Orange
Points - 1
When built, place 1 relic token next to each Local Game Shop in your community, including this one.

In Balkanika: Safira's Escape, you don't just play the story; you collaborate to build it from the ground up, encounter by encounter. This player-driven, multi-layered narrative system enables players to shape the unfolding world, the destiny of their party, and the individual transformation of their characters through various choices and locations. The story culminates in a uniquely personal conclusion that only your party could create.

—description from the publisher

A small solo PnP Card game mostly played in hand. Your are a human computer navigator trying to bring a ship through a hyperspace jump. Dissolve all disruptions using Focus and Prescience before reaching the final beacon or the ship is lost and all souls aboard it.

It uses a 30 card deck which you go through twice using resources and support cards and decide how to solve all disruptions and turning or flipping them to their safe sides and taking into account that some disruptions can be safe but flip back to unsafe states at the end of the game if not certain prerequisites are fullfilled.

—description from the designer

Deep in the heart of a bustling warehouse, communications have gone horribly wrong. One of the team has accidentally sat on their radio… and now everyone is getting a real-time audio tour of their posterior. With chaos erupting over the airwaves, only one mission remains: Find the Butt Radio. Or hide it well.

In this chaotic and clever trick-taking game, one player discovers they’re the Butt Radio Culprit — but they’re not about to confess. Over two escalating phases, players battle it out with a shifting hierarchy of suits and a must-follow trick format.

In Phase One, players race through rounds of trick-taking to earn advantages: the Accused gains pockets to hide the Butt Radio (represented by face-down cards), while others earn guesses to uncover it.

In Phase Two, the hunt begins. Guesses are made, cards are swapped, and tension mounts with every trick. If the radio is found, the warehouse's efficiency is saved. If not? Well… I guess we have to shout to each other now!

Butt Radio was born at a board game convention after a real-life rogue transmission caused announcements through the halls. It was designed and tested amidst fits of laughter and cries of "WHO SAT ON THE RADIO?" at the convention and refined afterwards, this game turns an unforgettable moment into an unforgettable play experience.

Perfect for 4 players who love deduction, deception, and just the right amount of cheek.

—description from the designer

This fast-paced card game raises the tension with every turn. The cards have one light and one dark side, but the two sides represent different cards (colour, value, etc.)
All the players advance towards the objective announced at the start of the game. 
The Flip card turns everything upside down: everyone turns over their deck. The game goes on, and you have to adapt quickly!
Action cards add spice to every moment: bombs, boomerangs, jokers, etc. 
When it comes to counting, the player who has achieved his objective perfectly has no penalties. If the objective is missed or exceeded, penalties apply. 
It's a tactical, bluffing card game that's perfect for setting the mood.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Use Up All Your Sick Days
description:
Work on a day like today? No way. Time to call in sick. Hopefully no one else had the same idea. It's a hilarious climbing shedding game fun for the whole family!

Use Up All Your Sick Days is simple. There is no turn order and you need to play a card with the next day or same worker currently on top of the discard pile. But if go to play at the same time as someone else, you both leave the card and draw a 2 cards as a punishment. (You also can't play after yourself, and you could play a card face down as a doctor's note, making it wild.)

Play all your cards to lose a tired worker token and draw back to 7 cards.  The first half of the players to get rid of their tired worker tokens win!

Solo/co-op rules are for 1-6 players and are much more puzzly! You play HR organizing the worker's schedule. Co-op limits communication, but you you can spend pizza tokens to flag where you want to play later. Solo has cozy mode with no automa and challenging mode with an easy to run automa.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Labour movement / Workers rights / Unionising

description:
It's a beautiful day at the petting zoo for a breakout!

Every turn, the next player takes the deck and flips a card into one of the sanctuaries on the table. While it's not your turn, you can grab a pile. You can only have so many sanctuaries, and you only score animals you have the right sanctuary for, so move fast! Will you collect amazing alpacas, bountiful bunnies, or happy little goats?

—description from the publisher

Fly dragons to deliver mail, ferry friends, move supplies, and explore the wilds! Adventure awaits!

Dragon Mail is card game. When your a dealer, place one card from the top of the deck on a location of your choice. While you're not the dealer, visit a location to fill up your dragon with cards and score matching cards. You have limited moves the entire game, so plan carefully, but also move fast before another player grabs the perfect shipment!

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Floating islands in the sky
Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Ever wanted to open your own trendy cafe? Your dominoes have ingredients on one side and recipes on the other, and both sides have arrows you point to yourself to score points at the end of the game (in addition to a favorite ingredient if any are left on the table). The challenge is to protect your own ingredients while you cover other's ingredients with your recipes.

The game also comes with dozens of optional barista cards, granting asymmetric powers and alternative scoring conditions. Draft 2 at the start of the game and unlock some awesome broken combos!

It's time to bake and brew your way to the best little vegan cafe in town!

—description from the publisher

Join Unicorn, Mari Lwyd, Kelpie, Pegasus, and dozens of other horses from myths.

Merge gems into creatures, creatures into magic ponies, and ponies into legends, and ride them up the mountain of mystical dreams!

Merge Mountain is a family weight resource management. Cards grant powers and move you tracks on the mountains. When a player makes a big enough herd or reaches the top, the game ends and points are counted.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Indomitable Survivor: Tyrant Slayer Scarlet
description:
Tyrant Slayer - Scarlet is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Tyrant Slayer - Scarlet hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Serpentine Tailbone Dragon King indomitable resource
1 x Tyrant Slayer pattern card
1 x Tyrant Slayer pattern gear card
1 x Install guide card
1 x Large matte art print
For best experience, include Dragon King in your campaign.

It’s a fishy feeding frenzy. In this shark bowling game, players go head-to-head sending a very hungry shark at each other’s fish pins, trying to knock over as many as possible.​ First, set up your pins. Wind-up the shark by pulling it back until its mouth is wide open, then send it zooming towards your opponent’s fish pins. If the shark’s mouth chomps down on a fish, you get an extra turn. Collect all the pins to win. Mats provide additional play options for repeatable play.

—description from the publisher

Fate of War invites you to step into the sandals of ancient commanders and lead your forces through the epic battles of the Second Punic War — from the hills of Cannae to the gates of Rome.

This tactically rich historical wargame for 2–4 players blends deep strategy with streamlined gameplay. Whether you’re a seasoned general or a newcomer to historical games, Fate of War offers a satisfying challenge that’s both accessible and full of meaningful decisions.

Each scenario captures the essence of ancient combat — with varied terrain, distinct unit types, and the real tactical dilemmas faced by Hannibal and his Roman adversaries. The adaptable scale (typically 1:2,000) means that your forces may represent entire formations or small cohorts, depending on the battle.

With a mix of smart card play, tactical maneuvering, and just the right dash of luck, every round keeps you on your toes. If you’re looking for a game that brings ancient history to life while keeping the focus on fun, fast-paced strategy, Fate of War is ready to test your mettle.

—description from the publisher

STACCS is a mind-bending card game of matching and stacking. The 60-card hexagonal deck features classic suits and numbers, plus wilds and zeroes. The cards are designed to look like cubes, creating a trippy optical illusion. Players take turns matching cards to build one shared, three-dimensional STACC. First one out of cards wins!

There are 3 cardinal rules that govern STACCS and all its Play Modes:
- Matching suits STACC on the TOP surface of a card.
- Matching numbers STACC on the SIDE of a card.
- Matching face cards STACC on the FACE of a card.

Special cards (O, J, Q, K, A) add a little chaos with blocks, attacks, counters, and extra turns — but Wilds are the real game-changers: they let you call your suit and steer the STACC in a brand new direction. When you’re down to one card, make sure to let other players know by saying “UH OH!”

There are also extra game modes including:

     SOLOSTACCS - the solitaire mode for 1 player
     WILD OUT - a collaborative four-STACC game
     UH OH! PARTY - increase the total players to 10 by combining 2 DECCS


—description from the designer

¡ADUANAS! The trick-taking game where you DON’T want to win the tricks.

Players take turns winning tricks to divide up the luggage before passing through customs checks in different countries.

Can you keep all your money safe while your fellow travelers go bankrupt exploring the world?

You'll also learn the names of some of the world's most valuable gemstones, along with various countries and their flags.

Whoever has the most money left at the end of the journey wins!

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Pick - Plant - Pluck! Will you try to dial up high heat for big points? Or turn and burn for a quick, spicy score?!

In the game of Pepper!, Players will need to balance speed and strategy, in order to pick and plant the perfect patch of puzzling peppers!

Perfect patches are scored by spelling "PEPPER" with cards you've collected. But each adjacent card must share the same color or quantity!

Each turn players can: PICK (draw), PLANT (play), or PLUCK (steal). The more pristine the patch, the spicier the score!

The first player to reach the top of the scale wins!

—description from the publisher

Tokyo S.O.S. Kaiju Attack! is a pulse-pounding cooperative game, where your all-star team must defeat a rampaging Kaiju before it destroys all of Japan. 80's anime vibes collide with the battles of classic Kaiju cinema!

Each player chooses from a variety of unique heroes with their own special abilities. You'll use action cards to disrupt the Kaiju's movement and attacks, complete daring missions, and deploy military units.

Be warned, though, the Kaiju has its own deck of movement and attack cards. As it stomps through Japan, it will be oozing radiation, hunting down nuclear reactors, and destroying your valuable tanks and military bases.

If that wasn't bad enough, the rest of the world is watching and it may turn the entire country into collateral damage if the threat level gets too high!

—description from the publisher

In a time where city-states and corporations rule the world, you have been chosen to lead. As mayor, strategically deploy your workforce and companies to exploit resources and grow your capital. But beware: only the one to outsmart the competition can build the most influential city.

This is a area control, resource collection and city-building game that is all about pure strategy and tactics. There are no random factors, luck, or hidden information (unless you use optional variants). Your skill in accumulating victory points will determine the winner.

—description from the designer

SAME DiffeRent is a hilarious and thought-provoking party game where players try to think alike—or completely differently.

Each round, teams respond to playful or challenging prompts by writing down answers in secret. Match all your teammates with the same answer and earn big points. Give all different answers? You’ll still score, but less. Anything in between? Nada.

Designed for deep laughs, surprising insights, and unexpected connections, SAME DiffeRent is perfect for game nights, group bonding, or sparking conversation. Includes a mix of light-hearted, serious, and 18+ questions. First team to 31 points wins—but really, everyone wins with this much fun.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Badge of Dishonor — a satirical, fast-paced card game where the goal isn’t to be the best, but the worst! Play as hilariously incompetent ICE-inspired agents who fumble, overreact, and embarrass themselves in front of everyone. Each game is packed with outrageous characters, ridiculous situations, and gut-busting humor perfect for parties and game nights.

What is Badge of Dishonor?
It’s a collectible, standalone card game where players compete to rack up the most Disgrace Points by failing spectacularly. From chasing innocent bystanders for “suspicious sandwiches” to interrogating piñatas for “illicit contents,” you’ll laugh at every misstep of your incompetent agents.

How Does It Play?
