Draw a Situation Card filled with over-the-top scenarios.

Compare stats from your Agent Card to see if you fail (and earn disgrace) or narrowly avoid humiliation.

The more you fail, the more points you earn — first to the target Disgrace score wins at being the biggest Buffoon!

Two Ways to Play:

Quick Mode: Just draw and fail — perfect for lighthearted parties.

Stat-Based Mode: Compare agent stats against situation difficulties for hilarious strategy.

Why You'll Love It:
Irreverent humor — perfect for fans of satirical games like Exploding Kittens or Joking Hazard.
Unique collectible cards — every agent and situation is a one-of-a-kind parody.
Fast, flexible gameplay — great for 2 to 6 players, ages 15 and up.

Get ready to embarrass your friends, sabotage your rivals, and laugh until it hurts. Grab your copy of Badge of Dishonor today and see who becomes the most disgraced agent of all!

—description from the designer

Set of 4 promotional cards for the game Ancient Knowledge on the theme of Czechia, containing for example the Celtic head of Mšecké Žehrovice or the Zuran hill

All 4 of them are shuffled into the the standard deck.

Monolith is solo sci-fi skirmish game about plundering the depths of an immense arcology known as The Citadel and battling the minions of the cruel sentient intelligence imprisoned within. You control a team of three Remnants as they emerge from the wasteland to scour the city for relics, and lead their crusade to destroy the immortal machine once and for all.

The game offers eighteen unique scenarios, nine different enemy types and three levels of increasing difficulty presented in a campaign that never unfolds the same way twice. Along your journey you will recover Fragments of the past to craft and upgrade your weapons, armour and cybernetic augmentations while resisting Corruption and attempting to complete objectives that will cause The Citadel's Collapse.

It's designed for solo play, with quick resolutions, dynamic initiative and special abilities that can turn the tide of battle. It's miniatures agnostic, so any kind of sci-fi, cyberpunk or grimdark future collection will do, in terrain of any kind of urban hellscape.

Have no fear. The Citadel must fall.

—description from the designer

Splash Showdown is a fast-paced dueling card game where every move you make matters!

In Splash Showdown, players engage in an exciting battle where every decision counts. Each card you play costs "Water," a unique resource that serves as both your life points and your only resource. Manage your Water wisely to outsmart your opponent and claim victory!

The game features colorful artwork that captures the essence of sunny days by the pool, beach outings, and refreshing water fun. Every card is beautifully designed to enhance your gaming experience, making each match feel like a mini-vacation.

You must balance your Water usage carefully! Will you spend it all on powerful cards to attack your opponent or save some for defense? Each round brings new challenges and opportunities, ensuring no two games are ever the same.

—description from the publisher

These 2 tiles replace the two game tiles that have no stars and they offer an advanced variant of the game.

When you obtain one of these 2 characters, you can look at the 3 Character tiles set aside at the beginning of the game and decide if you want to exchange it for one of them.

—description from the publisher

"The world is all but done for. A plague unlike anything else ravages everyone and everything. Monsters wander closer and closer to the once peaceful villages. Heroes are nowhere to be found. You may not be of legend, but who else will make a change?"

Questbound tells the story of a dying world, conquered by plague and the monsters it creates. You've seen the toll it has taken on the earth, the town, the people. You have suffered, those you love have suffered, and you want that suffering to end. Or, at least subside a little. Picking up your pack, you set across the blighted landscape, facing enemies and encountering obstacles along the way.

Will you become a story of Legend? Or will you simply go quiet into the good night?

Questbound is a thematic 1-2 player game with euro-like resource management aspects paired with an adventure RPG of survival.

Each player takes on the role of a small town professional who is looking to make change in their world. Keeping track of your supplies, herbs, and lanterns, while garnering reputation with those that populate the dying world, you will adventure as far as you can and see how much fame you gather along the way.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Foxes' Quest: A Co-operative Family Adventure
description:
Join forces in Foxes' Quest to save Snuggle Haven! The evil Grumble Foot has captured the Parrot of Peace and unleashed Predators across the land. Work together as four brave foxes, each with special powers and unique abilities, to gather resources, battle Predators, and journey to the Mountain of Destiny where the Parrot of Peace is being kept.

Combine your strength in the Battle Field to defeat enemies, but beware: a loss sends you to the Prison of Defeat, where only the Slightly Blue Tree’s magic can heal you. With only three chances to defeat each Predator, strategy is key! Under the shelter of the Slightly Blue Tree, trade resources with teammates and strategize for the journey ahead.

Build your health to 20 points to unlock the secret tunnel in the Cave of Dreams that leads to the Unknown Forest to confront Grumble Foot. Once Grumble Foot is defeated, reach the Mountain of Destiny to free the Parrot with a final code-breaking challenge.

Will you save Snuggle Haven? Gather your team for a thrilling, fun-filled quest!

—description from the designer

Play as rival Police Departments trying to stop an active serial killer. Race other players and the FBI to be the first to collect enough Evidence to win before the Suspect garners too much Attention and disappears forever.

—description from the designer
Quiz game combined with Alias like word explanation game. Game sets in Seinäjoki area and questions are about the City of Seinäjoki.

Agile&Co casts players as video game studio managers who grow their companies by balancing agile practices (like retrospectives and clean code) against strategic decisions. Through shared action selection and hand management, they deploy products, earn revenue, and adopt improvements—all while juggling limited resources. Agile Maturity cards permanently boost actions (e.g., extra Development phases), while Agile Values scale points for long-term investment.

Each turn, players pick from five activities (Conference, Development, Deployment, Retrospective, Coach). Strategic depth comes from timing choices, managing card costs, and adapting to opponents. After 48 activities, the highest-scoring studio wins, with points earned from deployed products, agile upgrades, and stacked values.

The game blends light strategy with thematic depth, mirroring real-world agile challenges in a competitive yet accessible format.

Elementra is a fast-paced, 2–4 player card game where players embody one of the four primal elements —  Earth,  Fire,  Water, or  Air.

At the start of each round, you're randomly assigned an Element. Your mission? Race to collect three matching Element Cards from draw piles and be the first to call out “Elementra!” to end the round.

But it’s not just about collecting it's about outsmarting. Use powerful action cards, chaotic Misfit abilities, and game-shaking Supremo Cards to twist the tides in your favor.

Expect surprise attacks like Tornadoes and Tsunamis, sneaky sabotages, and shifting elements that force you to adapt your strategy every round.

player with the highest total points at the end of round reigns as the true master of the elements.

Fight the nastiest monsters from prehistoric times! You no longer need a giant meteorite to make the dinosaurs extinct - not when you've got a horde of Munchkins craving a level-up!

Just make sure you don't fall into prehysteria while you're sipping a dino colada on your raptor steed with your nine-times-wise assistant paleontologist hireling, taking down enemies like the plush dinosaur or the poposaurus. You can make history here, even in the other included expansion. YOUR version of history!

This edition contains the two expansions Munchkin 9: Jurassic Snark and Munchkin 10: Time Warp. It is not a base game. To play it, you need the Munchkin base game.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Rails and Cargo: Think and Write
description:
Rails and Cargo is a solitaire train game played using just two sheets of paper. All you need to get started is one regular pencil and four colored pencils or crayons.

As the railway planner and operator, you’ll rebuild tracks, construct new factories, produce valuable goods, and deliver them to their intended destinations.
There’s no luck involved in the gameplay - dice are used only during setup to create variety in each session.

—description from the designer

A humorous party game set in a fantasy world, where each player tries to recover the One Doll, by undertaking missions, casting powerful and hilarious curses, eating snacks and making your opponents' life miserable.

The game features map movement on a small board (each location with a mission to solve), dice rolling for getting resources to cast curses, a hand of snacks to eat and curses cards to cast on your opponents, character special powers for each player and a small Voodoo Doll.

Whoever is in possession of the One Doll when the end game conditions are met, wins the game!

In Your Turn, a 4 player game is played in teams, but a 3 player game is played individually. The cards in the game have a color, a number, and a directional arrow.

The card play is similar to games like Uno, where you must match the color or number of the previous play. However, turn order is determined by which player the arrow on the card points to - that player will take the next turn. There are also rules around communication and playing multiple cards at the same time.

A round ends when either a player has played all of their cards, a player has too many cards in their hand, or a special game end card is drawn from the deck. Players lose points based upon the cards remaining in their hand.

The game lasts until a team or player has lost a certain amount of points and then the team or player with the most points wins.

boardgame: 行列の指揮者 (Gyōretsu No Shikisha)
description:
In 行列の指揮者, roughly translated as Streetcar Conductor, the players move their streetcar around a rondel, picking up and dropping off passengers.

Each station has a specific color, and passenger cards of that color will get on or off at that station - however! Each station only loads and unloads from the front or rear door, and passengers can only board or depart the streetcar at that door.  The passengers will also, generally, not rearrange, so only matching colored cards at the right door will depart.

After a player attempts to unload passengers, passengers waiting to board may be allowed on - in the right order and through the proper door - though the streetcars have limited capacity.

Afterwards, two additional passenger cards are placed at stations.

The game ends when the deck is exhausted, and the player who has delivered the most passengers wins.

Thornshire’s Queen has decided that she shall invite the Messenger's Guild to assist in the recreation of The capital city of Ferrum’s Ford.

As the Messengers Guild, it is your duty to ensure that the Queen is always well informed of all that happens within her realm.

Like the other Guilds in the base game, the Messenger's Guild has unique goals and scoring conditions.

The Messenger's Guild can be used to expand Guildlands to a 7 player game.

—description from the publisher

A new duo of pirates inspired by the designers of Celestia and Celestia Duo, Aaron Weissblum and Bruno Cathala. It introduces 2 exclusive effects: Pursuit, which forbids players to speak, and Boarding, which allow to fix a mast.

A new Speakeasy in the shape of a fun board game store or café. It features 2 Standard sides (no Regular one).

This promo card comes either with no printed name, or with the name of a partnering FLGS.

FATA is an educational and strategic card game designed to challenge players’ observation and logical thinking skills. Using a deck of colorful number cards, players take turns placing cards on the table to build interconnected patterns, much like a mathematical crossword.

The goal is to create valid chains of numbers and colors by applying simple mathematical relationships. Each move requires careful planning and analysis, rewarding creativity and foresight.

With its intuitive rules and short playtime, FATA is ideal for families, educators, and anyone looking to combine fun with cognitive development. The game supports 2 to 6 players and plays in about 20 minutes, making it a versatile choice for classrooms or casual game nights.

The goal is to be the player with the highest score after an indeterminate number of rounds.
To begin, deal 4 cards to each player and place the remaining cards face down in the center to form a draw deck. Flip over the top card; this will be the reference card to start the game.

On their turn, a player has 2 options:


     Add 2, 3, or 4 cards to the table, forming a single interconnected line like a crossword. The condition is that for each characteristic of the cards (color, element, number), they must be either all the same or all different. Then, draw from the deck the same number of cards that were played.
     Pass their turn. In this case, they may also exchange any number of cards from their hand (from 0 to 4).


At the end of each turn, players score points equal to the numbers on the cards involved (both those played from the player's hand and those already on the table).

The game ends when all cards in the draw deck have been used and one player has emptied their hand (doubling the score from their final move).
Each player then totals their points, and the player with the highest score wins.

Hoaxite is all the rage right now across the galaxy. So far, 4 varieties have been discovered but nobody is entirely sure what it's even good for.
Some say a small spoonful of it lets you glimpse into the future, others claim it works wonders to prevent tooth decay. Or maybe it's just crystallised space ogre snot?
Whatever it is, a thriving black market for the stuff has developed in the Scumpit Nebula and you are here to get in on the action.

Jump across the orbital space stations to acquire these crystals and manipulate their value in your favour (or against your opponents') to head off with the most precious load of cargo.


     Smugglonauts was an entry in the 2024 1-Card Print and Play Design Contest


—description from the designer

A cooperative game for 3 players who represent staff sections in the fictional US Army 91st DSSB (Divisional Sustainment Support Battalion). They work together to prepare and send off daily supply convoys to divisional Brigade Combat Teams on the “front line”. Essentially a time management and planning game, with simple processes – features include an endless time track (a mechanic stolen from Bruno Faidutti’s very weird Red November) and roles and choices that put demands on the players as the situation continues to change and crises arise.

The game has simple components – two pages of tracks and charts, some small player mats, 60 markers and a set of coloured cubes to represent supplies (a set of supply markers is provided if you don’t have cubes).

Most civilian-market wargames have detailed procedures for movement and combat, with the logistics processes handwaved away. For a long time I have wanted to design a game that approached the inverse of this. It is not meant to be a simulation so much as a vehicle for delivering some insights to staff and combat arms officers on the unending challenges of life in the Quartermaster Corps.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In the wake of the Great Gun's reverberations, alien growths are discovered among the foundations of the great city of Hive Fissilicus. Bionically augmented Tech-Priests lead Battleclades of their cyborg servants beneath the hive city to halt the spread, but awaken monstrous aliens, leading to furious fighting.

This expansion includes two multipart plastic and push-fit kill teams, a set of Tyranid-themed terrain features, a horde of non-player operatives for solo and cooperative play, rules, datacards, and accessories for your games of Kill Team. It continues the ongoing narrative on Volkus, and introduces fresh ways to play, more operatives, and strategies – it's an ideal way to add even more variety to your games.

This boxed set contains:


     80-page softcover Typhon Dossier: All the background, artwork, and rules for both of the two kill teams in the box, plus an overview of the planet Volkus, the Tyranid infestation underneath Hive Fissilicus, and the kill teams clashing amidst the growths. This book includes two new Mission Packs – one for solo or cooperative play, and another to add Tyranid non-player operatives for both teams to contend with.



     Battleclade Kill Team: The Adeptus Mechanicus let their tech thralls loose to cleanse the infestation, led by a Technoarcheologist and a Servitor Underseer. These 10x multipart plastic and push-fit miniatures include a variety of weapons and cosmetic options, as well as an Adeptus Mechanicus Transfer Sheet. You'll find all the bits you need to build specialists like the Technomedic Servitor, Breacher Servitor, and Auto-Proxy Servitor.



     Raveners Kill Team: Hulking, multi-limbed xenos creatures that attack by bursting from underground tunnels. These 5x multipart plastic miniatures can be built as Ravener Warriors, and specialist bits are included to build a Ravener Prime, Wrecker, Tremorscythe, Felltalon, and Venomspitter.



     Hormagaunts and Ripper Swarm: This set of multipart plastic miniatures builds 10x small but vicious Tyranid Hormagaunts, along with 1x Ripper Swarm, to use as non-player operatives in solo play and cooperative games.



     Killzone Upgrade: Tyranid Infestation: This terrain set includes 2x gestation sacs, 2x shardspewer growths, 2x capillary towers, 1x brood nest, 2x underbrood mounds, 2x capillary roots, 2x spore vents, and one piece that builds either 1x brood nest or 1x spore chimney. This set is designed to work with the Typhon Mission Pack within the Typhon Dossier.



     Battleclade Datacards: 32x Datacards covering operative rules, abilities, faction rules, ploys, faction equipment, universal equipment, and more.



     Ravener Datacards: 33x Datacards covering operative rules, abilities, faction rules, ploys, faction equipment, universal equipment, and more.



     Typhon Card Pack: Includes 10x Typhon Event Cards, and 6x non-player operative datacards



     Combined Token Sheet: Easily track abilities, objectives, equipment, and points throughout your games with team-themed tokens. The sheet contains 78x tokens – 39x double-sided cardboard tokens for the Battleclade kill team, and 39x double-sided cardboard tokens for the Raveners kill team.


—description from the publisher

Terra Indomita is a dynamic deck-building strategy board game that blends the tactical depth of real-time strategy (RTS) games with the strategic card play of trading card games (TCGs). Choose your faction—or combine factions—to build a customized deck. Deploy your forces, outmaneuver your rivals, and dominate the battlefield as you strive to conquer your enemies and emerge victorious.

—description from the designer

Based on the 1974 film Black Christmas.

Christmas is the most wonderful time of the year… to die! The sorority sisters of Pi Kappa Sigma are being harassed by disturbing phone calls from a mysterious stranger, and now he’s upping the ante as he decks the halls with bloody corpses. In order to survive the season, the young ladies must help the police trace the killer’s calls and find out where he’s hiding before it’s too late. Danger lurks around every corner as players work together to keep the killer on the line for as long as possible… while trying to stay alive!

Players choose one of seven characters from the classic horror film and cooperatively navigate their sorority house, uncovering special events and collecting items as they go. Their ultimate aim? To identify the 7-digit phone number of the killer.

Players roll 7 dice, allocating their face-up values to different actions, each of these actions having their own dice requirements in order to be executed.

Actions include “trace” (securing one digit of the 7-digit killer’s phone number) requiring four-of-a-kind dice faces to be allocated, “peek” (enabling event cards to be revealed) requiring 3 dice faces of a value greater than six to be placed, “unicorn” (allowing a dice face to be changed to any other value that the player wishes for) requiring 3 consecutively numbered dice faces to be allocated and “grab” (allowing an item token to be taken from a room) requiring a matching pair of dice faces to be allocated.

Players may also trade and have unique character abilities to help them survive this ordeal.

Create your perfect day with the fluffiest little genetic experiment in the Galaxy! Move around the board with one of 4 brightly colored Disney Stitch tokens, each in a different funny pose. Buy, sell, and trade Stitch's favorite activities, and explore his island home with Ohana and 626 cards.

Includes gameboard, 4 tokens, 18 title deed cards, 16 Ohana cards, 16 626 cards, 32 beach huts, 12 lighthouses, 2 dice, money pack, and game guide.

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The English Civil Wars – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
A 64 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - The English Civil Wars, includes six complete army lists to enable players to build Early Royalist, Early Parliamentarian, Scottish Covenanter, Montrose Scottish, Late Royalist and New Model armies.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The New Kingdom – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
A 58 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - The New Kingdom, includes seven complete army lists to enable players to build New Kingdom Egypt, The Hittites, The Sea People, The Mittani, The Nubians, The Libyans or The Israelites armies.

—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars in the Ancient Near East
Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

description:
Which is bigger: the number of rats in New York City, or the number of pounds of gold dissolved in normal seawater worldwide?

120 well-researched, cited, and sourced facts about numbers ranging from the tens of thousands to the trillions, all about something interesting. You don't need to know the answer. You just need to know better than your friends, and be gutsy enough to gamble on your answer!

It's trivia for people who don't like trivia, and a math game for people who don't like math.

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The Punic Wars – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
A 64 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - The Punic Wars, includes seven complete army lists to enable players to build Polybian Roman, Early Carthaginian, Late Carthaginian, Ancient Spanish, Numidians, Gallic Tribes or Ligurians armies.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The Rise of Macedon – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
A 62 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - The Rise of Macedon, includes five complete army lists to enable players to build Early Macedonian, Late Greek City States, Late Achaemenid Persian, Macedonian Empire, or Ancient Indians armies.

—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great
Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

description:
A casual memory game inspired by Japan’s popular instant noodles.

Players line up three “Procedure Cards” showing noodle-making steps, memorize them, and turn them face down. They must recite all the steps in the correct order. If they succeed, they add a new step and pass the challenge to the next player. If they make a mistake, they must take the incorrect card and all remaining cards after it as penalty points.

The game continues until the deck runs out, and the player with the fewest penalty cards wins.

Memorizing these unique and funny noodle-making steps makes this game perfect for parties and family gatherings. Cooperative and time attack rules offer even more ways to play.
publishers: Studium Mundi (スタジオムンディ)

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: Sub-Roman Britain – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
A 54 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - Sub-Roman Britain, includes five complete army lists to enable players to build The Romano British, British and Welsh Kingdoms, Early Saxons, The Scots-Irish, or The Picts armies.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The Greco-Persian & Peloponnesian Wars – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
A 52 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - The Greco-Persian & Peloponnesian Wars, includes five complete army lists to enable players to build Greek City States, Spartan, Early Achaemenid Persian, Delian League, or Peloponnesian League armies.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: Caesars Campaigns – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
A 64 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - Caesars Campaigns, includes seven complete army lists to enable players to build Caesarian Roman, Early Germanic, Gallic Tribes, Ancient Britons, Late Ptolemaic Egyptian, Late Pontic and Numidian armies, along with four pages of full colour pictures, showcasing example units and commanders from those army lists.

—description from the publisher

On Bloody Ground - El Cid & The Reconquista, includes six complete army lists to enable players to build Christian Spanish, Almoravids, Al-Andalus, Late Christian Spanish, Almohads or Late Andalusian armies.

—description from the publisher

description:
Culture Cue is an experience-sharing board game designed to connect people through storytelling. It was created as a way for players to communicate with others by sharing their experiences. Designed with a familiar game style and mechanics this board game can easily bring different people of different backgrounds and generations to enjoy a fun-filled time on game night.

By sharing stories and experiences the players are rewarded by being able to move forward a certain number of spaces. Players can choose not to share and have to move back spaces. The first player to move their tokens to Generational Enlightenment, the final circle, wins the game!

—description from the designer

The next installment in the series of Escape Room card games in a small box...

When you wake up with a pounding head, you know neither who you are nor where you are. The surroundings, an abandoned old hotel in the mountains, are unfamiliar to you, the door looked. How did you get here? And what yare you doing here?

You've barely freed yourself from the room when shots suddenly echo through the old walls. Someone is trying to kill you! A wild escape through the dilapidated building begins...

Will you succeed in defying the killer's treacherous attacks and escape with your life?

—description from the back of the box

Players take the roles of major stakeholders in city planning and collectively planning a new neighborhood. Even though they are working together, each of them has their own secret agenda (i.e., buildings they want to see built). In four rounds, each player is going to propose two projects, then the others will vote on those projects. In a second phase, players can contribute resources to approved projects to realize them. Only realized projects count at game end towards your objectives.

This game has been developed in cooperation with Lookout Games for an exhibition at Deutsches Architekturmuseum from Jun 27 to Nov 2 called "Building Cities Today?".

A Print-to-Play Board Game of Faith, Firearms, and Imperial Destiny

Command history in the making with Faith and Fire: The Battle of Chaldiran, a gripping board game that thrusts you into the cataclysmic clash between the Ottoman and Safavid Empires on August 23rd, 1514. Relive this decisive confrontation where gunpowder met zeal, tactics met ambition, and the fate of the Middle East was irrevocably altered.

Command of Sultan Selim I's well-drilled Ottoman forces, masters of artillery and fire discipline, or rally the fervent cavalry of Shah Ismail’s Safavid army, driven by spiritual fire and bold maneuver. Every choice will echo across the battlefield in a war that set the tone for a century of imperial rivalry.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Décorum: Best of the Digital Scenarios Season 1
description: Contains an additional 20 2 player scenarios for the base game. Picked by the developers from submissions on the online version of the game
Contains an additional two 2 player scenarios and eight acrylic gift tokens for use with them. The Gift tokens can take the place of any furniture item, complexifying these two bonus scenarios

Card game for girls' nights, bachelorette parties or Galentines celebrations, made to connect women. Contains 240 cards across 6 categories of questions and challenges. Prepare to laugh, bond, and overshare!

Draw a card and follow its instructions. Collect the most cards to win!

Keep this if...: you meet the condition.
Give this to...: another girl who you feel fits the description best.
Challenge: complete it to win a card.
Point at: on three, everyone points at the girl who fits the description best.
Popular opinion: on three, everyone gives thumbs up or down.
Best response: everyone answers, best response wins.

Read a vocabulary card and find the word that completes the sentence. Answer correctly and start herding your animals. Be the first player to put all 7 animals in your pen and win!

—description from the back of the box
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Battle Card Brawlers is a duel game in a fantasy setting. Each player has a general on the board (a 5x9 grid) with 2 attack and 25 health and the goal is destroy the opponents general, while keeping your own general alive.

Each player has a deck of 60 cards consisting of minions, spells and artifacts. There are starter decks, but with the huge amount of available cards a ton of different decks can be built.

Minions are summoned onto the board and all friendly units can move and attack on every turn after they are summoned. Moving and positioning units to guard others and to play around effects and spells of your opponent makes it a very strategic game. With most of the action happening on the board, it is also highly interactive.

At the start you have a mulligan phase with 3 cards. Each turn (including the first) two cards are drawn. Once per turn a card can be added to the mana pool to pay for mana costs of other cards - there exist no extra mana cards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 50 Árboles de España y Europa: Naturaleza con Juan y Migas
description:
Juan and Migas help you identify 50 trees you can find on your daily adventures.


     Fun questions
     Detailed illustrations
     Practical descriptions
     Lots of interesting facts


Perfect for nature lovers!

This is a flexible educational game where players explore tiles showing different products or elements of nature. Each tile comes with engaging questions that can be answered individually, in teams, or as a family. Players can decide how to keep score, for example by awarding points for correct answers or simply playing for fun and learning. The game encourages curiosity, discussion, and discovery, letting everyone choose their preferred way to play.

—description from the publisher

Stones & Stars Rummy is a “Gin Rummy” playing card game with some elements of 500 Rum, and other Gin Rummys as well as several new concepts.  The objective is for each player to Meld enough cards to obtain a total Deadwood card value equal to or under the Shifting Knock Number (SKN) in order to “go out” at end of round thereby scoring your opponent’s Deadwood points. More layers have been added to the game by giving each card extra attributes (Gems and Planets) that may allow them to form additional unique Melds.

—description from the publisher

A solo bookgame where Risk meets the Apocalypse.

The world is ashes. The factions that remain aren’t fighting for power—they’re fighting for survival.

Doomfront is a fast-paced, 15-turn solo book game(s) played in about 30 minutes.You can play over 20 times or more if you have a good printer..lol.You can put the game down, carry it with you and pick up right where you left off with no set-up or clean-up.  Inspired by the global conquest of Risk but laced with the creeping dread and sudden chaos of Terror, this game drops you into a brutal post-apocalyptic battlefield. You command the last remnants of Phoenix, humanity’s final hope, against two relentless AI factions: The Melted and The Howlers.

All you need is five dice or a dice app, a pen, and the will to push through.

Each turn, you’ll take bold actions, respond to emergent threats, and adapt to a world that doesn’t care if you win. The map changes. The enemy evolves. And your decisions—attack, retreat, infiltrate, or hold—are the only thing keeping hope alive.

No setup. No cleanup. Just brutal, strategic gameplay in a world that’s already lost everything.

The ashes are yours. If you can survive.

—description from the designer
Think you know it all? Well think again! This game will leave you scratching your head over even the most obvious answers. All the questions seem simple, but do you really know the answers?

The legendary lost Ghost Train heading to Santa Fe with the Lost Dutchman’s gold has appeared!

Navigate your way through the haunted train and avoid the Lawmen that patrol the cars. Collect the gold and escape before it’s too late and you disappear into the night as part of the Ghost Train forever!

—description from the publisher

Plays like a simplified 2D version of ‘Colt Express’. Six random train car cards are laid out in two rows of three. The top row must have the cards with the US Marshal stars on them. Gold tokens are placed nearby, face down so the values cannot be seen. Lawmen tokens are placed on the Marshal stars. The active player may move up to two spaces in any direction except diagonally. If the player lands on either a blue or red space, they draw from the Haunted Deck and do the action that matches the color they are on. White squares are action spaces and players must move in the direction of the arrow. Two players may not occupy the same space, so the active player will push the other player in the same direction the active player was moving. A player cannot be pushed through a wall or off the train. If the pushed player lands on an action space, they must immediately take that action. Only the active player may collect gold by landing on a gold space. The player selects a gold token, but keeps the value hidden from the other players.

After every turn the two Lawmen will move one space down the middle of the cars. If they land on a player’s token, that player immediately loses all their gold. If a Lawman lands on an action space, they immediately take that action, unless it would cause them to remain on that space or move illegally. Lawmen can move through walls, but cannot enter the Escape Car or collect gold.

The Escape card is the last card in the Haunted Deck. When it is revealed, it is centered above the two top train cars, and play continues. The first player to leave the Escape Car receives three points. After that, all other players will have one turn to attempt to also escape, and will receive one point for doing so. Once everyone has had their last turn, the players who escaped will reveal their gold tokens, and the values are added to their escape points. The player with the most points wins.

Pac-Man, before or after Star Wars?

In turns, players try to locate one of their cards in the timeline. The first player to have no cards left in their hand wins. But be careful... if you make a mistake, you draw!

Knowledge, deduction, and a little luck are key to winning. But the mechanics are based on learning by mistake. "I never lose. I either win or I learn." -Nelson Mandela.
Chroni is an educational and evolving game with a whole collection of themes to mix and match. So, David Bowie, before or after Rocky?

The first edition of Got what it bakes/Van sütnivalód? is being produced in Hungarian, and is currently in manufacturing.
If there is sufficient international interest, an English-language edition may be published in the future.

Van sütnivalód? = Got What It Bakes?  is a thematic, strategy-driven board game for 1–4 players, where everyone steps into the oven-warmed shoes of a passionate artisan baker. Across three intense and flavorful rounds, you’ll gather ingredients, manage your time and resources, and bake delicious recipes to earn points — all while balancing the unpredictability of dice rolls and the satisfaction of strategic planning.

Theme & Story

The game is inspired by the world of home baking, especially the renewed love for sourdough and pastry-making that blossomed during the COVID era. Each player runs their own cozy bakery, starting from scratch with just a little time, a few coins, and a handful of recipes. As you bake more and invest in your kitchen, your bakery grows into a well-oiled oven of efficiency and creativity.

Your sourdough starter is alive. The timer is ticking. Customers are hungry. Will you bake it now?

Core Gameplay Mechanics

Draft & Write Mechanism: This is a “roll & write” style game with a twist: instead of simply rolling dice, you draft them from shared pools. Each die you take influences which ingredients you collect, and how you proceed with your recipes.

Resource Management: Players must manage two core resources — Time and Money — along with a supply of ingredients. Baking requires careful planning, and sometimes even improvisation under pressure.

Recipe Fulfillment: Each round, you’ll select and fulfill sweet or savory recipes, using dice to collect ingredients and choosing whether to spend time or gamble with a risk roll to bake under pressure (via the “Bake It Now!” action).

Upgrades & Refreshers: Permanent kitchen upgrades (like a Mixer or Dough Board) and single-use refreshers (like an Iced Coffee) allow players to manipulate dice, earn bonuses, or gain extra resources — adding layers of strategy and engine-building potential.

Multiple Game Modes:

Solo Mode: Beat score thresholds and complete challenges alone.

Competitive Mode: Secret goal cards drive each player’s unique scoring strategy.

Cooperative Mode: Shared objectives require teamwork, donations, and planning — but everyone wins or loses together.

Strategic Depth

While luck plays a role through dice rolls and card draws, Got What It Bakes? is a highly tactical game that rewards efficiency, foresight, and adaptive thinking. Key strategic choices include:

- Choosing when to spend time vs. risking a rushed bake

- Managing leftover ingredients in your pantry for later turns

- Deciding when to invest in upgrades and which refreshers to buy

- Timing your baking and maximizing recipe points within only 3 rounds

What Makes It Unique

Deeply Thematic Experience: From the use of real recipe types to the inclusion of time-pressure decisions that mimic real kitchen stress, the game immerses players in the baking process both narratively and mechanically.

Tight, Replayable Structure: With only 3 rounds and fast-paced turns, the game stays engaging and easy to replay, especially with different player counts and modes.

Creative Visual Design: The game’s charming illustrations and clear iconography help communicate rules intuitively, even to younger or newer players.

Customizable Difficulty: The game includes multiple difficulty settings and optional simplified modes for children or casual players, making it family-friendly while still challenging for strategists.

Van sütnivalód? = Schlau gebacken! ist ein thematisches, strategieorientiertes Brettspiel für 1–4 Spieler, in dem alle in die ofenwarmen Schuhe eines leidenschaftlichen Bäckermeisters schlüpfen. Über drei intensive, geschmackvolle Runden hinweg sammelt ihr Zutaten, verwaltet Zeit und Ressourcen und backt köstliche Rezepte, um Siegpunkte zu erzielen – stets im Spannungsfeld zwischen dem Zufall der Würfel und der Genugtuung cleverer Planung.

Thema & Geschichte

Das Spiel ist inspiriert von der Welt des Heimbackens, insbesondere von der wiederentdeckten Liebe zu Sauerteig und Gebäck während der COVID-Zeit. Jeder Spieler betreibt seine eigene gemütliche Bäckerei, beginnt mit wenig Zeit, ein paar Münzen und einigen Rezepten. Mit jedem gelungenen Backwerk und jeder Investition wächst die kleine Backstube zu einer effizienten, kreativen Backwerkstatt heran.

Dein Sauerteigstarter lebt. Die Uhr tickt. Die Kunden sind hungrig. Wirst du jetzt backen?

Zentrale Spielmechanismen

Draft & Write-Mechanismus: Ein „Roll & Write“ mit Twist – statt nur zu würfeln, draftet ihr Würfel aus einem gemeinsamen Pool. Jeder gewählte Würfel bestimmt, welche Zutaten ihr erhaltet und wie ihr eure Rezepte voranbringt.

Ressourcenmanagement: Spieler verwalten zwei Kernressourcen – Zeit und Geld – sowie ihre Vorräte an Zutaten. Backen erfordert Planung und manchmal Improvisation unter Druck.

Rezept-Erfüllung: In jeder Runde wählt und erfüllt ihr süße oder herzhafte Rezepte. Mit Würfeln sammelt ihr Zutaten und entscheidet, ob ihr Zeit investiert oder das Risiko eines Eilbackens eingeht.

Upgrades & Erfrischer: Dauerhafte Küchenhilfen (wie Mixer oder Teigbrett) und einmalige Erfrischungen (wie Eiskaffee) erlauben das Modifizieren von Würfeln, Boni oder Extraressourcen – und bringen strategische Tiefe sowie Engine-Building ins Spiel.

Spielmodi

Solo: Punkteschwellen knacken und Herausforderungen allein meistern.

Kompetitiv: Geheime Zielkarten treiben individuelle Strategien voran.

Kooperativ: Gemeinsame Ziele erfordern Teamarbeit, Spenden und Planung – alle gewinnen oder verlieren zusammen.

Strategische Tiefe

Obwohl Würfelglück und Kartenziehen eine Rolle spielen, ist Schlau gebacken! ein taktisches Spiel, das Effizienz, Voraussicht und Anpassungsfähigkeit belohnt. Zentrale Entscheidungen:

Wann investiere ich Zeit vs. wann riskiere ich ein Eilbacken?

Welche Zutaten lagere ich für spätere Züge ein?

Wann lohnt sich ein Upgrade, wann ein Erfrischer?

Wie maximiere ich meine Punkte in nur 3 Runden?

Was das Spiel einzigartig macht

Starkes Thema: Echte Rezeptarten und Zeitdruckentscheidungen spiegeln die Backrealität wider – sowohl narrativ als auch mechanisch.

Kompakt & Wiederspielbar: Nur 3 Runden, schnelle Züge, hohe Spannung – ideal für verschiedene Spielerzahlen und Modi.

Visuelles Design: Charmante Illustrationen und klare Symbolik machen die Regeln intuitiv erfassbar – auch für jüngere Spieler.

Anpassbarer Schwierigkeitsgrad: Mit verschiedenen Levels und vereinfachten Modi bleibt das Spiel familienfreundlich, ohne strategische Spieler zu unterfordern.

Van sütnivalód?
A „Van sütnivalód?” három fordulón át tartó, pörgős stratégiai társasjáték, ahol minden játékos egy lelkes pékmestert alakít. A játék elején célkártyát és süteményrecepteket választasz, majd fordulóról fordulóra hozzávalókat gyűjtesz kockadobással, vásárlással és ügyes tervezéssel. Ezután jöhet a sütés: ha van elég időd, biztos kézzel dolgozhatsz, de ha szorít az idő, kockázatot kell vállalnod – és a végeredmény nem mindig garantált. Bár a játékban jelen van a szerencsefaktor, a kockadobásokat különféle fejlesztésekkel és akciókkal aktívan befolyásolhatod, így a siker leginkább a jó döntéseken múlik. A kész finomságokat eladhatod, fejlesztheted a konyhádat, és új recepteket is szerezhetsz, hogy a következő körökben még hatékonyabb legyél. Vigyázz: ha túl sok mindent akarsz egyszerre, a süti könnyen a szemetesben landol! A játék végén a győzelmi pontok döntik el, ki volt a legügyesebb – szólóban, versengve vagy kooperatív módban, közösen célokat teljesítve.

A játék, amiben a nyersanyaggyűjtés, a dobsz és írsz mechanizmus kockadraftolással és szettgyűjtéssel párosul. Emellett a zsebpénz és a szabadidő helyes beosztása is kihívást jelenthet, míg a fejlesztésekkel járó jutalmak is pikantériát adnak a játékmenet közbeni tervezésnek.

—description from publisher

A strategy game for two players in which you take the role of one of 2 twin sisters, competing to be the one who inherits the throne. A fun and deep game, easy to learn, hard to master, block your opponent and find the tempo to claim your place as the Queen on the field.

The game is played on two boards,a rondel that the players use to select and block actions, and grind in which each player starts with 2 guards and one princess. If you attack the opponent princess or place yours in the start position of your opponent princess you are the winner. To achieve this select your actions carefully in the rondel to use your cards to move and attack when the moment is right.

—description from the designer

CUNO is a lightly competitive tile-placement game where players strategically match and connect rings to score points. Every move matters. Plan ahead, block opponents, and set yourself up for high-scoring plays. With simple rules and deeper strategy, CUNO is easy to learn and fun for friends and families alike.

Perfect for 2-4 players, the game features dynamic tile placement, and evolving board patterns. Whether you're making quick connections or setting up powerful combos, CUNO rewards smart decisions and tactical thinking.

—description from the designer

Spring is when the young arrive. When discarding to add Food, add 1 Egg token to the Watering Hole in addition to the Plant tokens. Place the Egg nearest you to show it’s your Egg.

An Egg can only be eaten by a species with the Opportunistic trait. When an Opportunistic species forages, it may eat the normal amount of Plants, or it may eat 1 Egg.

Each scored Egg is worth 1 point at the end of the game. If 1 of your Eggs is in the Watering Hole at the start of your Feeding turn, you may discard it to add 1 Population to 1 of your species.

Strattegee is a tactical board game of modern warfare that reimagines the timeless strategy of chess with a bold, 3D battlefield and no reliance on luck. Designed for those who crave pure, skill-based competition, the game unfolds across land and air, challenging players to command infantry, artillery, and air units with precision and foresight.

In Strattegee, every unit has unique movement and attack capabilities that mirror real-world military roles. Ground units capture territory and block advancement, while air units strike from above, creating dynamic vertical and horizontal threats. Ammunition is limited, resources are visible to both players, and every decision counts—there are no dice, no hidden information, and zero chance elements.

The gameplay is built for both 1v1 and 2v2 formats, offering tightly contested duels or large-scale coordinated assaults. Players win by eliminating the opponent’s Command Piece — a mission that demands strategic depth, forward planning, and creative problem-solving. With quick setup, high replayability, and elegant mechanics, Strattegee is easy to learn but difficult to master.

Whether you’re a chess lover, a war gamer, or a strategist at heart, Strattegee offers an immersive test of battlefield command where only the most skilled tactician survives.

—description from the designer

The old golddigger Gulliver Guldborg has died and left his goldmine full of gold. It is now up to you brave people to collect the gold. The player that collect the most gold, lives happy after!
Guldfeber ("Gold Fever") is a fun children and family game, where both young and old can play and compete.
The game takes under 5 mins to learn and still has a lot of surprises, so two games are never the same.
In the game you must collect as much gold from the gold mine and bring it home - but watch out for the other players that will try to steal your gold!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Guldgraveren Gulliver Guldborg er død og har efterladt sin mine fyldt med guld. Det er nu op til nogle modige mennesker at få hentet guldet op fra minen.
Den, der får hentet mest guld kan leve lykkeligt resten af livet.

Guldfeber er det sjoveste børne- og familiespil, hvor både små og store kan deltage og kæmpe mod hinanden.
Spillet tager under 5 minutter at lære og indeholder alligevel så mange overraskelser, at ikke to spil udvikler sig ens.

I spillet gælder det om at få hentet guld og komme hjem med dette – men pas på at dine modspillere ikke stjæler guldet fra dig.

—description from the publisher

A fast-paced, head-to-head card game of strategy, sabotage, and street control. Compete to out-earn your rival by recruiting powerful gangsters, activating combo abilities, and locking down the block.

Gang Gang blends simple rules with deep strategic choices, perfect for competitive friends or casual gang bosses.

Draft from a constantly changing “street market” of cards.

Boost your income by stacking gangsters of the same color and triggering passive bonuses.

Activate powerful one-time Boss abilities when your crew is fully aligned.

Use your Jail Token to lock up an opponent’s key gangster for 2 turns — cutting off their abilities and cash flow.

To win, control a full matching gang for a full turn — or out-cash your rival when the game deck runs out!

—description from the publisher

Trading card game based on the Italian TV show "Gormiti: The New Era". Cards use stills from the show and original art as illustrations.

Cards are divided into Warriors, Weapons, Moves, Arenas, Special Effects, and Gormiti. Each player chooses a Warrior card to represent them in the game, and they can play their other Warriors as supporting characters. Gormiti are particularly powerful supporters who can be summoned only if their specific guardian is in play. After playing cards, the active player can declare attacks against the characters of the opponent.

The purpose is to play characters and upgrades to defeat the main Warrior of the opponent.

Pairagon is a 2-player memory game that can be played using a standard deck of playing cards.

The objective is to be the first to reveal all five pairs that are face down in the middle in a single turn. You use the cards Ace to five, Ace having the value of 1. Each turn a player reveals two cards, continues if they matched until either all cards are revealed or non-matching cards are turned up.

The twist comes that if a non-matching pair was revealed, you add their numbers, turn all cards face down and count to the card in the position of the added number from your the left. You then shift that card to the left of the middle row. Now try again. There are also some variants to make the game easier or harder.
Swiss-Quiz is an electronic quiz game about Switzerland.

boardgame: The Grinchmas Journey Board Game: How the Grinch Stole Christmas!
description:
Follow the magic of Christmas and journey to Who-ville!
Help the disgruntled Grinch and his friends as you travel towards the music and cheer of the holidays.
Be ready to compete for presents as you travel across the land. Use all your cunning and skills as you steal gifts and disrupt the festivities. Who will be the first one to reach Who-ville and learn the true meaning of Christmas? Tis the season to be jolly and make sure you are not left out in the cold!

Join The Grinch, Max and Cindy-Lou Who in their journey to Who-ville. Adventure awaits jam-packed with your favorite schemes and escapades of the green grump!

HOW TO PLAY:

PLAYING THE GAME

     Active player rolls the die and moves their Character Piece according to the roll.
     Game board actions are played out, whether it be moving up or down Ladders, picking up a Grinch Card or swapping character placement. (When you land on a Grinch Space, pick up a Grinch Card and follow the instructions.)
     In the event the active player lands on a square that another player is occupying, they have been entered into a Grinch-Off.


GRINCH-OFF GAME RULES
A Grinch-Off requires both affected players to roll the die. The highest roll determines the winner and the losing player is sent backwards on the gameboard the difference between the rolls. For example, if the Cindy player rolls a 7 and the Max player rolls a 4, the Cindy player is sent backwards 3 squares. If that space is again occupied, another Grinch-Off will take place between those players.

Please Note:

     A Grinch-Off only takes place after all board actions have been played out and the active player has reached their final destination of that round. For example, if the player lands on an occupied square that also has a Ladder, the player must first move up or down the Ladder; therefore, they may no longer be occupying the same space.
     Board actions don't apply after a Grinch-Off round and the losing player has been sent back.


FINISHING THE GAME
In order to determine the winner, the first 2 players to reach the Finish square on the game board compete in a final Grinch-Off. This Grinch-Off will be the best 2 out of 3. Each player will take turns rolling the die: the highest roll wins the round. Any ties go to the first player to reach the Finish Square. Any Grinch Cards collected throughout the game can not be used to affect the outcome.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: How the Grinch Stole Christmas

description:
In Call My Bluff, one player creates a secret five-letter word, then the other player takes turns guessing letters in the word as well as whether they're in the proper location, similar to Mastermind.

But while one player is guessing, the word creator is secretly listing words that can be created from the letters in the goal word. After guessing that word, the guesser then has a set amount of time in which to create a word list of their own. Whoever has the longer list wins.

What's My Word? is a word-guessing game for up to four players. Each player creates a secret word, then tries to guess the word of the player on their left, while judging the guess of the player on their right.

After a player guesses, you tell them only the number of letters in the guess that are letters in the goal word.

DEDUCO is a hidden-role social deduction game centered around the conflict between two factions: Civilians and Assassins. Each player takes on a secret identity, playing as a character with unique abilities, and must use deduction, bluffing, and strategy to achieve their faction's objective. The setting creates an atmosphere of suspicion and tension, where players must carefully read each other’s actions and intentions.

Gameplay:
The game alternates between two phases: Interrogation and Protection, and Action. During the Interrogation phase, players ask closed questions to try and uncover others' identities, with answers given secretly using Yes/No tokens. Then, all players vote to place one player under protection, shielding them from attacks in the upcoming Action phase. In the Action phase, each player secretly selects an ability from their character sheet and executes it. Some abilities deal damage, others heal or defend, and some can only be used once per game.

Goal:
The goal of the Assassins is to eliminate the Civilian by inflicting 6 wounds. The Civilians must eliminate the Assassin by doing the same. The game ends when a player reaches 6 wounds. If that player is the Assassin, the Civilians win. If it’s the Civilian, the Assassins win. If it’s another role, the faction with fewer total wounds is declared the winner. In case of a tie, victory goes to the faction opposite that of the eliminated player.

Conclusion:
DEDUCO offers fast-paced, intense gameplay driven by hidden roles, strategic decisions, and psychological insight. With variable roles and actions, each session unfolds differently, making deduction, timing, and bluffing key elements for success.

—description from the publisher

A party game where everyone is affecting the movement of dinosaurs who are roaming the group and devouring those who do not feed them. Players simultaneously have to choose between three cards to either move the dinosaurs to the right, left, or to feed the dinosaur. Once everyone has played a card face down, all players reveal and show their selected card. Dinosaurs move and devour! Be the last one standing to win.

—description from the publisher

Players roll a dice to determine which color scoop they get, they may keep rolling to get more scoops. However, if they roll the same color twice they lose all their scoops for the turn! Once they have their scoops, they have to balance them on top of each other to make the highest stack! Two game modes allow players to have simple or complex scoring methods to fit the desired experience.

—description from the publisher
This shimmerkin splatterkin promo came with a purchase of the First Sketch Visionary Supply Kit. It's immune to a foe's talents when dry and can change a splatterkin's colorway.

Designed by the host of Ben Trading Cards Youtube channel, one of the main promoters of the game, the original print run of the cards will have the Ben Trading logo on them. The Speedrobo run removes the logo, obviously, and it came with the First Sketch visionary Supply Kit. This dark splatterkin can reveal cards on the drying rack or technique cards.

One of the first Inkkins introduced into the game, it came with the First Sketch Visionary Supply Kit. There is also a full card art variant. When defeated, Skibidee removes two inspiration from the opposing visionary.
This shimmerkin splatterkin came with the Color Theory booster set. It protects the splatterkins on your canvas and receives bonus inspiration for all the other shimmerkins you have in play.

Play Veto Revolution, a fun family shedding card game with a bold camo color theme, perfect for game night with the family and friends! Channel the spirit of a revolutionary leader, outsmart your opponents by strategically discarding, playing multiple cards, and using your VETO card to avoid picking up. Beware of action and wild cards that can change the game dynamics. Be the first to liberate your hand and dominate the game! Deck includes 111 custom cards, QR code card and instruction sheet.

Veto, a game full of Victory, Excitement and Thrilling Outcomes!

This game can be played with 2-4 players. Have a larger group? Purchase another deck and shuffle together for endless fun!

Veto Revolution is an easy shedding-type card game spiced up with special functions of action and wild cards. Players try to get rid of all their cards first, stacking equal or higher-ranking ones on each other, unless the action card has been played to make low cards win! Play a VETO card to stop from having to pick up cards when a player matches your card. Don't be the last one with cards left at the end!

The Deal – Shuffle the deck and distribute the cards in any number of piles to form draw piles. Each player selects cards from any pile to create Ground piles. The number of Ground piles, ranging from 1-4, may be decided at the beginning of the game. These piles shall remain face down and must have 4 cards in each. Each player then selects 4 cards to create their hand, which a player will look at and play from first. Combine remaining undrawn cards into one pile, face down, and form the draw pile.

Game play – To decide what player starts the game, each player turns over a top card from one of their Ground piles. In case of a tie for the highest value, turn over the top card of another Ground pile until the highest card is revealed. The player that turned the highest valued card will start by discarding any card from their hand (not from the Ground piles) to create a discard pile. Do not draw a card from the draw pile. Ground piles cannot be played from until a player’s hand cards have all been played. Play will continue clockwise. The next player must play a card equal to/higher than the last played card, or a wild card, following the instructions on any action cards. If the player does not have a valid card to play, they must pick up the entire discard pile and add the cards to their hand. If a player matches the last played card, the previous player must draw 3 cards from the draw pile, unless they can play a VETO card to stop the pick-up. Once a player has played all the cards from their hand, they may begin to play from the Ground piles. When the top card of a Ground pile is played, do not turn over the next card. These cards remain face down and are played blind. If the card is played and is not a valid play, the player must pick up the discard pile to create hand cards.

Winning – A game may end when either one player is the first to play all their cards or until there is only one loser - the last player with cards remaining.

—description from the designer

Before Speedrobo took up Splatterkins, Ms. Faust self-published through the imprint Psyterra Games through her Etsy shop. It got enough of a buzz that Ben Trading Cards began promoting it and held sort of a raffle through his Youtube channel and Patreon page in 2022 for this card, which has a blue border. When Speedrobo reprinted it they gave it the gray border of the monochrome colorway and changed its special ability.

Blue Mikami ability: Conceal: Mikami cannot be attacked during the turn it becomes the Showcase. Your opponent is unable to attack you directly as long as Mikami is the showcase.

Gray Mikami ability: Blizzard: Players cannot place any Splatterkins on the Drying Rack while Mikami is the Showcase.

The meeting is already in session.

Twelve seats. Two agendas. One Cabinet that cannot afford to crack.

The Executive is consolidating power, and the hand-picked Cabinet is the vehicle. Most members are loyal. A few are not. None of them will say so out loud.

In The Cabinet, players are secretly divided between Majoritarians who serve the Executive's agenda and Skeptics who are quietly working to derail it. Bills get passed or amended by secret vote. Subcommittees get nominated and challenged. Alliances form, suspicions surface, and someone at this table is not who they claim to be.

The Skeptics have to resist without getting caught. The Majoritarians have to win without knowing who to trust. The Executive has the power and is watching everyone.

A political hidden identity game of deception, deduction, and betrayal for 5 to 12 players.

—description from the publisher

An abstract strategy game of control as winter draws in.

As the forest prepares for winter, two players guide opposing herds of deer through the woods. This is a of positioning, timing and of gentle shifts that build pressure until nothing more can move.

The Wintering is an elegant, nature-inspired strategy game for two players. Tactile, quiet, and deeply strategic, it unfolds in three distinct phases, each with its own character.

In The Advance, space is open and choices are plentiful. In The Turning, pieces shift in function and the board begins to tighten. And in The Wintering, players face a final test of timing before there are no remaining moves.

The goal is not to eliminate, but to endure. When your opponent can no longer act, winter has arrived and you have won.

With simple rules and rich decision space, The Wintering rewards foresight, timing, and spatial intuition. As winter deepens, so too does the game, until no more paths remain.

—description from the designer

"Already Sushi" is a two-player card game.
Step into the role of a suave sushi chef and serve sushi with hidden wasabi—without getting caught!

Eating sushi earns you points, but eating wasabi will cost you.
Get hit with wasabi three times and... it's Already Sushi!
Instant defeat—so be careful!

Overgrown is a 2-4 player, simple area control game great for friends and family.

Players take on the roles of plants competing over space in the garden.  Each turn, they can choose to:


     Spread: players place a new Root on the board adjacent to a Root they already have.  This allows them to take more space in the garden.
     Grow: players place a new Stem on a Root or another Stem they have on the board, growing their plants to try and make them bloom.  Each root can only have up to three Stems.
     Wilt: players that have two full plants adjacent to each other (one Root and three Stems on each space) can wilt one of them (removing the Root and Stems from the board) to bloom the other.  This is one of the two ways to achieve a bloom.
     Absorb: Each time any player Spreads or Grows, they put a corresponding Water (for Spread) or Sun (for Grow) Token into the Token Bag.  On their turn, a player may choose to Absorb instead of taking the other actions.  This allows them to reach into the Token Bag and draw 4 Tokens.  Each Token they draw allows them to add to the board: 1 Stem for each Water Token; 1 Root for each Sun Token; 1 Bloom if 3 of the same type of Token are drawn.  However, if the player draws a Cloud Token, they lose their turn and put all Tokens back into the Token Bag.  If the Pest Token is drawn, the player's tallest plant is devoured by the Pest.


When a player Blooms, they place one of their flowers on top of a full stack of Stems.  All other pieces in the adjoining 8 spaces are removed from the board as a result.

The first player to bloom 3 flowers wins!

—description from the designer

Dive into the excitement of Veto Wild Waters, the card game that's a perfect match for a splashing good time with family and friends. Outsmart opponents by discarding wisely, playing multiple cards and using your VETO card to dodge the currents of trouble. But beware: wild waves of the Hand Cleaner and action-packed cards like the mighty playful dolphins can turn the tides at any moment! Don't be the last fish in the sea holding cards! Get yours today and prepare to make waves at your next game night.

Combine decks for more than 4 players.

Veto is an easy shedding-type card game spiced up with special functions of action and wild cards. Players try to get rid of all their cards first, stacking equal or higher-ranking ones on each other, unless the action card has been played to make low cards win! Play a VETO card to stop from having to pick up cards when a player matches your card. Don't be the last one with cards left at the end!

The number of Ground piles, ranging from 1-4, may be decided at the beginning of the game. These piles shall remain face down and must have 4 cards in each. Each player then selects 4 cards to create their hand, which a player will look at and play from first. Combine remaining undrawn cards into one pile, face down, and form the draw pile.

Game play: To decide what player starts the game, each player turns over a top card from one of their Ground piles. In case of a tie for the highest value, turn over the top card of another Ground pile until the highest card is revealed. The player that turned the highest valued card will start by discarding any card from their hand (not from the Ground piles) to create a discard pile. Do not draw a card from the draw pile. Ground piles cannot be played from until a player’s hand cards have all been played. Play will continue clockwise. The next player must play a card equal to/higher than the last played card, or a wild card, following the instructions on any action cards. If the player does not have a valid card to play, they must pick up the entire discard pile and add the cards to their hand. If a player matches the last played card, the previous player must draw 3 cards from the draw pile, unless they can play a VETO card to stop the pick-up. Once a player has played all the cards from their hand, they may begin to play from the Ground piles. When the top card of a Ground pile is played, do not turn over the next card. These cards remain face down and are played blind. If the card is played and is not a valid play, the player must pick up the discard pile to create hand cards.

Winning: A game may end when either one player is the first to play all their cards or until there is only one loser - the last player with cards remaining.

—description from the publisher

Discover Snakes and Hawks Deluxe, a trick-taking card game with a twist! Designed for 2-6 players ages 7 and up, this game combines strategy, partnership, and unpredictability. Featuring vibrant custom cards and unique gameplay mechanics, it’s perfect for family game nights or gatherings with friends. Snakes and Hawks Deluxe includes score keeping boards and player tokens.

What Makes It Unique?
Unlike traditional trick-taking games, partnerships in Snakes and Hawks rotate with every deal. The dealer gets to choose their partner, creating ever-changing dynamics and strategies as alliances shift with each round. While teamwork is key during the hand, only one player can claim ultimate victory by having the lowest score at the game’s end.

The Goal? Keep Your Score Low! Avoid penalty cards, capture powerful Hawks to deduct points, and aim to collect all four Snakes for a massive scoring bonus. The game ends when someone reaches 200 points or falls below -100 points. Be the player with the lowest score to win!

Why You'll Love It:
Designed with vibrant artwork and an easy-to-understand rule set, Snakes and Hawks brings the whole family together for thrilling gameplay. Its compact size makes it perfect for travel, parties, or casual gatherings.

Scoring Highlights:
Each penalty-colored card adds 1 point. Hawks subtract 3 points. Snakes are 10 points each (15 for the penalty colored Snake) Collecting all 4 Snakes rewards a -50 point bonus. End the game when a player reaches 200 or -100 points. Lowest score wins!

—description from the publisher

This expansion introduces 2 new playable characters, Yeti and Death Worm, and a new mechanic that allows you to use Cabins in The Wilderness.

-Cryptids-
Like all the other Cryptids in the game, the characters in this Expansion come equipped with their own unique Cryptid Powers. You can activate these abilities once per round by discarding a card from your hand. 
Yeti - Befriend: Attract 1 Human to the Site of each of your Hiding Spots
Deah Worm - Burrow: Extract 2 Humans and Extract up to 2 Humans from another Site.

-Cabins-
The new feature of Yeti or Not adds Cabins into the rest area with special bonuses.

This Print 'n Play expansion was the result of Social Stretch Goals during the Twisted Crypitds Kickstarter campiagn. It adds DROP BEAR, KRAKEN, and KELPIE to your Cryptid lineup! The pdf includes Sighting cards, tokens, and player markers.

Three Dead Strangers is a card game with light social deduction elements. You play one of four characters invited to a mansion where three murders were committed. Your goal is to pin the murders on the other players while avoiding being blamed for the murders yourself!

Three Dead Strangers utilizes domino-shaped cards each representing a character’s motive, means, or opportunity to have committed a murder, played in an overlapping manner, creating a simple-to-understand yet surprisingly deep-to-play game.

Players keep their character a secret, but once all three elements (motive, means, and opportunity) are in place for one of the characters, it's time to let the accusations fly as to which player you think was behind the heinous crime. The fun doesn't stop when a player has been successfully accused of being a murderer, as nobody is eliminated until the game is over, and the game isn't over until all three murders have been solved. Maybe one player killed all three dead strangers, or maybe each victim was done in by a different player. Nobody will know until the cards have been played!

With an average gameplay time of 15 to 20 minutes, Three Dead Strangers is an amuse-bouche of a game, intended to be an appetizer to your main course of an evening of gaming.

—description from the publisher

Banjo-Dungeon is an indie board game from Japan designed for two players. You control a knight venturing into an arena with the goal of snatching your opponent's treasure.

The game begins with an empty board. Each turn, players place two tiles on the field. There are two types of tiles: Wall Tiles to construct walls and pathways, and Magic Circle Tiles to summon dragons or golems to aid you in battle. Players have a limited supply of mana, represented by candles along the edge of the board. If you use 3 mana, the candle moves 3 spaces. When you run out of mana, you can't use it. Mana is used for character skills, summoning minions onto the field, and casting summon spells for your monsters.

Movement and combat are similar to games like Final Fantasy Tactics, with units having movement ranges, attack ranges, passive skills, and active skills that require mana to use. The game concludes when you reach your opponent's treasure chest, at which point you win!

boardgame: マンション・オブ・ザ・ヴァンパイア (Mansion of the Vampire)
description:
In Mansion of the Vampire, players are invited to spend a night in the opulent home of a wealthy, eccentric host. But this is no ordinary soiree – a bloodthirsty vampire, disguised as one of the guests, lurks among you!

As a human, your goal is simple yet perilous: fulfill your unique character's victory conditions and survive until dawn. Cooperation is your greatest weapon, as humans must band together to outwit the lurking threat.

For the vampire, the path to victory lies in cunning deception or brutal confrontation. Will you patiently blend in with the humans, waiting for the perfect moment to strike? Or will you reveal your true nature early, embracing a numbers game to overwhelm your unsuspecting prey?

Gameplay Overview:

Each turn, players secretly select a movement card, revealing their destination simultaneously. The enigmatic Master of the House also moves, guided by a revealed movement card, adding an unpredictable element to the night, some the unique characters may need to interact with the master to achieve the victorious condition. As the game progresses, players may need to introduce themselves when entering the same room, reveal their true vampire identity (if they are one), or utilize their character's unique abilities. Additionally, players can steal or acquire valuable items from various rooms of the mansion to fulfill certain character-specific victory conditions, such as  the thief or the businessman. The clock inexorably ticks forward with each passing turn, with a strict limit of 12 turns before dawn breaks. Will you escape the night alive and achieve your goals, or become another victim of the vampire's chilling embrace?

boardgame: La Quete des Gemmes de l'eternite
description:
La Quete des Gemmes de l'eternite ("The Quest for the Eternity Gems") is a solitaire gamebook that immerses you in a fantasy world.

Play as a team of intrepid heroes tasked with an epic quest in the heart of the magical kingdom of Agrila. Once peaceful, these lands are now threatened by an evil force that only the Eternity Gems can contain. But time is running out: these sacred artifacts must be recovered before darkness engulfs the world.

Each hero on the team possesses unique skills and plays a crucial role in this adventure. It's up to you to harness their talents to overcome perilous challenges and face hordes of fearsome creatures that will try to hinder your progress.

This book doesn't just tell a story: it offers you a true gaming experience—it's a board game in a book. Dice and strategy will be your best allies. The game system automatically manages monsters and events, leaving you free to focus on your decisions and tactics.

With the exception of the six-sided dice, everything you need is in this book:

Detailed game rules,

Ready-to-cut tokens and cards,

A rich and immersive campaign that evolves throughout the adventures.

Prepare for epic challenges, hone your strategic thinking, and lead your heroes to victory in The Quest for the Gems of Eternity!

—description from the designer

As the day of the equinox approached, crowds gathered in the heart of the Green Valley to witness the annual Trial of the Forest - an ancient ritual to select from among the bravest daredevils a new Guardian, a protector against the darkness lurking in the shadows.
Players collect resources, play cards, fight opponents and embark on dangerous expeditions. In the course of the game, the cards played build the player's history by defining his deeds.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Nevermind The Distraction: Oil & Water Distraction Pack
description:
Add variety to any edition of Nevermind The Distraction with this Oil & Water themed Distraction Pack. Pairs perfectly with the Abstract Edition of the base game!

—description from the publisher

Kairos is a fast-paced historical strategy game where players compete to earn the most points by the end of the game. The game ends when any of the players reach one of the four victory conditions.
