Points are awarded for a variety of accomplishments during the game, including capturing capital cities, liberating allies, progressing through Eras, and more.

At the start of the game, players draft from the available civilizations and settle in a location on the map. From there, players expand their borders, engage in tactical battles, and grow their capital. Combat is resolved through dice rolls, influenced by terrain and unit types. Players will accumulate gold and access strategic resources to progress their civilization and recruit additional soldiers.

Throughout the game, many players will capture other players' capital cities. When this happens, the captured civilization will fight to regain control of the city, and the occupier will be rewarded for each turn they control the city. Other players may step in and neutralize the conflict. Capturing and liberating cities from other players is the primary path to victory in Kairos.

At the end of the game, the player with the most victory points wins, regardless of which player reached the victory condition that ended the game. Kairos has been carefully designed to play quickly and encourage replayability.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fleets of the Second Great War: La Royale, The French Navy
description:
The Second Great War is our alternative-history setting, in which U.S. President Woodrow Wilson’s attempts to negotiate peace in late 1916 (which actually happened) are successful and bring the Great War to a close two years earlier than in our history. But not everyone is satisfied with this peace, and a generation later France, Russia and Italy renew the war against the Central Powers.

Our Second Great War at Sea games and books bring this setting to the Second World War at Sea series of historical wargames. The situation’s different, and the technology’s different, too: airships dominate the skies, and battleships dominate the seas.

La Royale is a sourcebook for the Second Great War setting, all about the French Navy in the Second Great War at Sea. We talk about the new ships added to France’s fleet – and there are a lot of them – in The Cruel Sea, but also in upcoming expansions. And we talk about the warships France really did build and deploy in the real World War II.

La Royale is a sequel to Fleets: Imperial Germany and Fleets: Imperial Russia. Just like in those books, we tell you about the ships, and also show you their schematics, just like in the warship guidebooks of the real world. It’s all part of the world-building, to make your games that much more fun.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Find the matching pairs and build animals!
Spread the differently shaped tiles on the table and mix them up. Take turns trying to find two pieces that form an animal. If you find the bunny, a cute wooden bunny figure hops your way – and might just help you win the game!


     A memory game that also develops fine motor skills
     Includes an adorable wooden bunny!
     The game adapts to the players’ skill level


—description from the publisher

Goal
Finish off your post-apocalyptic neighbors to command the new local power center.

Gameplay
Take charge of 1 of 8 Factions from four Groups, generate Resources, build up your offenses, and take charge of your corner of the world.

The World
It has been decades since the end of the Resource wars in 2126 and the collapse of the 'Old Nations', but New Yellowknife still has nearly 200 years before their world domination. This period of time that will one day be known as 'The Disturbance' is the start of rebuilding efforts. Everyone with big ambitions wants to be the next regional mover and shaker or tyrannical King, creating a checkerboard of settlements that blaze onto the post-apocalyptic scene and mostly fade to obscurity in short order.

—description from the designer

Come up with words that contain all the letters of the same color! Make a wish for the gifts you want to receive: anything at all... but only from the letters dealt on the table!

The word-building board game "Best Present" is the perfect gift for children and adults alike, for everyone who loves creating words from available letters. It’s almost like Scrabble or Words with Friends, but with simple rules, a compact box, and just one deck of colorful letter cards — convenient to take on the go or to play quickly with kids.

All hands on deck! On the horizon, two mysterious drifting ships have locked in a fierce boarding battle… Through your spyglass, you don’t see a single sailor on board, but it looks like the ships are full of treasure and… skeletons? Caramba! Skeletons won’t scare you, but other pirate sloops are already racing toward your find at full sail... Very soon, several pirate boats will be docking from different sides of the floating treasure island. Throw the skeletons and rival pirates overboard and collect the treasure — before the ships sink!

Turn by turn, reveal tiles on the ship-shaped game board, find pistols, bombs, barrels, and tins of sprats! Attack enemy pirates and toss them overboard! And if all your pirates end up overboard — try to climb back onto the ship on the wave and wash away the careless pirates still on board!

Dude-L-Bug uses a spinner with images of various dominoes on the outer edge.  Players have a score card with 4 boxes, each with 9 spots to record your spin.

Players spin the arrow and if it stops on a spot with a domino, the players write those two numbers in one of the blocks on the score sheet (i.e. domino with 4 and 2 pips could be recorded as either 4/2 or 2/4, with the first number written above the line in a box spot and the second number written below the line.

Other space on spinner board include Gain 10, Lose 5, Lose Turn, Choose Any Number, and the Dude-L-Bug (with gives an automatic 40 points)

Play continues to next players until one of the player's score boxes is filled up.  Then the box is scored as follows:


     Sum of row or column adds up to multiple of 5
     Horizontal "run" 10 points
     Vertical "run" 30 points
     Horizontal "straights" 10 points
     Vertical "straights" 25 points


In cases where straights or runs add up to a multiple of 5 there are additional points given.

Play continues until all 4 boxes have been scored.  Highest total wins.

In the year of our Lord 1589, the northern Rhineland is swept by a tide of savage persecutions and hasty summary trials for heresy, witchcraft, and lycanthropy. The County Tribunal, led by the controversial Prior Erastinuz Wetzel, launches numerous proceedings, accusing — primarily young women — of these crimes.

During one of these trials, the young Magdalena Roth, under pressure from the devout matrons of Blutfurt (The Pure), is accused of being a werewolf and sentenced to burn. Her execution is set for the evening of the second day of November, but Powerwolf — sons of the Legends of the Night and the Armenia Savage Army, defenders of the "Real Truth" — are determined to stop this injustice and break the bloody cycle unleashed by the Priory's fanaticism and its most feared servant: the relentless witch hunter Sebastian Rabenklaue, known as the Devourer of Heretics! Can you lead the Powerwolf in a desperate quest to save an innocent girl from a false accusation and a terrible fate?

1589 is a co-operative game in which players must lead the Powerwolf in a desperate quest to save an innocent girl from a false accusation and a terrible fate before the hour of Vespers (that is, within eight game turns). They will gather evidence of the Prior's fanaticism, act from the shadows to sabotage the execution — by extinguishing torches or stealing bundles — lure the witch hunter away from the pyre by drawing him into the forest, and sway the Pure to return to their homes, convincing them of the girl's innocence.

But beware as both the Pure and the witch hunter are deeply suspicious. Any false step may reveal the Powerwolf as accomplices to the accused...and earn them a place at the stake!

—description from the publisher

Denny from the Deep is a game similar to "Cootie" or other "Bug" type games, except this one uses an octopus as the creature you assemble.

There are 4 octopi bodies, each with eight legs, one nose two eyes, and on pair of eye glasses.

A plastic spinner is spun and the symbol on the spinner indicates which part is added to the octopus.

Winner is the first person to complete their Denny.

Likely published between 1950 and 1957.

boardgame: A Battle Through History: Heroes expansion 1
description:
This is an Expansion for the game A Battle through History An adventure with Sabaton.

4 new Heroes cards to be added in your battles through history! ... and 2 Special dice for the official Sabaton’s game A Battle Through History

In A Battle through History each player dresses the part of a time-traveling storyteller (a “Sabaton”), whose journey through historical Eras enables them to relive epic battles alongside legendary troops and heroes. Players can both face “the story” (represented by cards on the main board). He challenges the other players, deploying archers, tanks, airplanes, Vikings, and any other troops that they were able to “conquer” during their travels through the Eras. Moreover, by collecting precious Relics, they can enrich their knowledge and thus gain the points needed to win the game.

Whoever collects the greatest Units, Heroes, and Relics during their travels wins the game and can therefore sing of their heroic deeds!

Are you ready to be a real Sabaton?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vengeance: The Fall of the Soviet Union, AD 2025
description:
Vengeance is a hex & counter quadrigame, set in an alternate timeline in which NATO, China, Iran, and other nations ally to invade and conquer a resurgent Soviet Union in 2025.

Vengeance recalls fondly-remembered, continent-spanning alt-history strategic games of the 1970s, but at an operational scale offering fast-moving gameplay, with special rules to model key elements of 21st Century warfare.

Vengeance puts you in the role of a theater commander, making multidimensional decisions – not just where to maneuver brigades and divisions, but how to counter insurgents, prioritize logistics and airpower, and employ special operations forces, diplomacy, and economic “soft power” to shape the battlefield.

—description from the publisher

The goal is to accumulate the fewest negative points or fists where each card has a number of fists at the top.

Rule 1 “ascending order”: The values of each hand must always increase from left to right. Since each card appears twice, it is possible to place the same card or number to the right of the previous one.

Rule 2 “figures”: In each of the 4 hands, only cards of the same figure should be placed.

Rule 3 “full hand”: A hand is complete with 5 cards. The player who needs to place the sixth card must take the 5 cards currently on the hand/raw. Their card will become the first card in that hand.

Rule 4 “low card”: If a player plays a card with a value lower than the cards on the table, they must take the cards from the corresponding hand and figure. Their card will become the first card in that hand.

Rule 5 “negative points”: All the cards a player takes from the center of the table during the current round must be placed face down in front of them to count the negative points at the end of the round.
Important note: The cards taken cannot be played or taken again.

This is a solo "Pencil and Paper" game that allows players to relive the battle of Waterloo in 60 minutes or less. Players take on the role of Napoleon at his last major battle. Each turn the player will choose one of several available actions, activating the different historical formations at the battle. After selecting their 1 action per turn, they will roll dice to determine the "AI" opponents move. After resolving the AI action, the player will roll to determine the outcome of their action. Some actions are tied to the historical events, while others allow for some ahistorical movements and attacks. Each turn losses will be accrued through the different actions until the player either achieves the win conditions or is defeated. There are several win and loss conditions, but generally the player needs to defeat a certain number of Allied formations while not losing several key units themselves.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Igloo Battles, players are tribes of people living in the cold Northern lands vying for seats around the campfire. Players take turns placing one of their cards on one of the 4 campfire locations on the map until everyone runs out of cards. Then you score who controls the most areas to determine the winner! Game plays in less than 10 minutes and is fun for all ages!

—description from the designer

A quick game of balance, where you need to hand the bats and don't let them fall.
Players hang bats one by one and try to balance them so that one side is not heavier than the other. However, bats come in different sizes. Players must spin the wheel and hang the bat of the chosen color. Whoever hangs or is hanging the bat and all of them fall, loses the game.

•••

Um jogo rápido de equilíbrio, onde você precisa pendurar os morcegos e não deixar cair!

Os jogadores vao pendurando morcegos um a um e tentando equilibra-los para que um lado nao fique mais pesado que o outro. entretanto, morcegos tem diferentes tamanhos, os jogadores devem girar a roleta e pendurar o morcego da cor sorteada. aquele que pendurar ou estiver pendurando o morcego e todos cairem, perde o jogo.

Take on the role of servers at the Rout 66 Diner.
Your goal is to serve the most food and guests possible all while sabotaging your opponents!

Diner rush is a table management game where players need to decide when they should take orders, deliver orders, or clear tables all while dealing with other players messing with there plans or messing with the other players!

The goal of the game is to score the most Star rating (SR)
by serving guest food before a set number of critics (a guest card type) are in the discard pile.

Each player will have a table section board containg 5 tables where they are dealt face down guest cards. 
A communal board contains both the kitchen section and the counter section. 
There are 3 decks of card.

They are:

Guest cards: Guest cards have a SR and some have effects when you take there order.

Food cards: food cards have a SR and cooking timer value. 
When ever a food card is put in to the kitchen place a cooking timer on it equal to its stated value unless a card effect states otherwise.
cooking timers go down by 1 each round.
once a food has no cooking timer on it immediately is moved to the counter.

Rush! cards: These cards are used to interact with the board and other players. A player may only ever have a total of 5 rush cards in there hand. 
There is no cost to using Rush! cards. There is no limit on the amount of Rush! cards a player can use during a round.
Rush! cards can only be played when:

     At the start of a round.
     The start of a players turn
     When ever a player declares they are taking a player action.
     When ever another rush card is played
     At the end of the last players turn


At the start of the round a new guest card is placed face down in each players table section if there is an open table.

Then each players takes there own separate turn.
On a players turn they can take up to two actions and they may take the same action twice 
There is a total of 3 actions available to the player. 
These actions are:

1.) Take an order: A player selects a face down guest in there table section and flips them over. If the guest has any reveal effects the player follows them. If there is no effect the player then takes the top card of the food deck and puts it in to the kitchen. This is the guests order. The player puts a cooking counter on this food equal to what is displayed.

2.) Deliver an order: A player selects a food on the counter that belongs to a guest in there table section. They then place this food on top of that guest card as the guest now has there order.

3.) Clear a Table: A player selects a table in there section that has both a guest and food card on it. The places both cards in to the discard pile and then scores the displayed value of SR on both the guest card and food card.

At the end of the round after all players have had there turn all cooking timers are moved down by 1. If a food has no cooking timer on it ,immediately place that food card on the counter.

Then each player is dealt a new Rush! card.

The game will enter its end once a set number of critics are in the discard pile. Once the third critic enters the discard pile, players finish up the current round and will have one more additional round before play is stopped.

If at the end of the game a player has food on the kitchen or counter that player losses points equal to the foods SR.

Then the player with the highest SR wins!

—description from the publisher

Drachen—Reconnaissance at Verdun, 1916 is Volume II in Volko’s Recon series that began with Coast Watchers. Similar use of hidden position blocks and chit pull maximize secrecy. Over 2 to 4 turns, you can play out the reconnaissance struggle ahead of each historical offensive within this iconic trench battle.

Drachen offers 8 main scenarios and a solo learning situation spanning German and French offensives within the titanic 10-month Battle of Verdun. Asset cards add to the character of each stage of the battle and illustrate each side’s innovations in reconnaissance, aerial warfare, firepower, and protection. The game pits 2 players in a contest of planning, detection, and deception. It will also include instructions for solitaire play.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In the epic and no-holds-barred game of Heavy Knuckles, fearless warriors assemble their haphazard mechs using an array of unique component cards. Each hero begins by crafting their mech's Core with Pilot and Chassis Cards. Arm Cards drawn from the deck are strategically  placed and reinforced around the Core, ready for battle!

During each turn, players roll the Action Dice to unleash heroic actions like Rebuilding, Swapping Parts, Sabotaging, and of course, ATTACKING! The ultimate goal is to obliterate the opponent's Core Cards with unique combinations of Shoulder and Fist Attacks while skillfully blocking incoming strikes. Victory is achieved when a player demolishes all of their opponent's Core Cards, leaving their mech in a glorious heap of scrap metal.

Prepare for strategic and hysterical mech battles that will have you cheering for more!

—description from the designer

You're out for an evening together and enjoying a few drinks. You buy something from a guy who calls himself C-Man and then.... Black Out. You and your friends wake up with a fresh tattoo and one less friend! Where's Sam?

In this cooperative mystery game packed with humor and mini-games, it's up to you to figure out what bizarre things happened last night. Create a timeline and find your friend Sam again!

—description from the publisher

Cha-ching! This Monopoly Grab and Go game is a compact version of the Monopoly game, featuring the same classic Monopoly gameplay in a travel-size format! Like the classic Monopoly game, it’s all about buying, selling, and trading properties to win. Chance or Community cards can be a game changer. Will they make a player richer—or send someone to Jail? This portable travel game for 2 to 4 players is a great family game for adults, teens, and kids ages 8 and up. Plus, the mobile size is just right taking nearly anywhere for on-the-run fun!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

boardgame: The Adventurers: The Temple of Chac
description:
The Adventurers: The Temple of Chac is a exciting, push-your-luck adventure game where players take on the roles of rival treasure hunters exploring a dangerous, ancient temple deep in the Guatemalan jungle. Dedicated to Chac, the Mayan god of rain and thunder, this temple is filled with priceless artifacts...and deadly traps.

Players explore the temple by rolling dice, to gather treasures and unique artifacts dedicated to different Mayan gods. They must navigate hazards like lava rooms with hidden trap tiles, shifting crushing walls, rapids that can sweep them away, and a massive rolling boulder that steadily advances, threatening to trap them inside forever. The more treasure they carry, the fewer actions they can perform, making greed very dangerous.

The goal is to escape the temple alive with the most valuable collection of treasures. Only those who can balance risk and reward, avoid injuries, and outsmart both their rivals and the temple’s traps will come out as the greatest adventurer.

—description from the publisher

This new edition of The Adventurers: The Temple of Chac is part of a 3-game collection that uses the same system and has complete compatibility of adventurers and their starting gear.


     An entirely new Action System where players do not only roll their own dice, but assign them to specific actions.
     A new encumbrance system that still penalizes players for being too greedy, but never results in them completely skipping their turn.
     Zotz’s Cave tunnel, dedicated to the God of the bats. Adventurers can find Zotz’s treasures when searching but may also encounter vampire bats and large, venomous spiders that have made their home in the cave.
     The removal of character death/player elimination from the core rules except at the very end of the game.
     A new lockpicking system that uses bag pulls instead of dice rolls.
     The addition of being able to steal treasures from the opposing adventurers.
     A new scoring system that accounts for a type of set collection as well as for wounds and injuries.

boardgame: Dot and Dash: Over and Out
description:
Dot and Dash: Over and Out is a competitive card game where players take on the roles of radio operators communicating with Dot the frog and Dash the tortoise, racing to send the strongest signals using melds of cards that match Morse code patterns.

Each round, players are dealt a hand of 8 cards and a 4–6 letter word is chosen as the target transmission. For each letter in the word, players must construct melds (combinations of cards like sets, runs, or assortments) that match the Morse code pattern for that letter—● for dots and ▬ for dashes. The strongest meld that matches the morse code letter earns a point.

Gameplay continues letter by letter through the word, with players refreshing their hands and competing for each point.  After three full words are played, the player with the most points wins—unless there’s a tie, in which case it’s one last signal showdown.

With its mix of tactical meld-building, signal interpretation, and forward planning and charming character flair, Dot and Dash: Over and Out delivers a unique blend of logic and rivalry in every round.

—description from the designer

After You, Betobetosan, A Trick-Taking Game of Creeping Dread for 4 Players

Every night, on quiet streets lit only by flickering lanterns, something follows. You hear it—step, step… step, step…—always just behind you, never in sight. It is Betobetosan, a yokai known in Japanese folklore for silently trailing behind night-time travelers with soft, sticky footsteps.

In After You, Betobetosan, you and your friends play as children walking home late at night, trying not to panic as Betobetosan draws closer. One player becomes the unsettling yokai, using limited information and shifting suits to win tricks and spread fear. The other players—Children—must stay one step ahead, using clever cardplay, illuminated safe zones, and their one precious "After You, Betobeto-san" card to avoid gaining Fear tokens.

But be careful. If you match Betobetosan’s move exactly, you don’t just lose the trick—you’ve locked eyes with the yokai. And once you do, fear creeps in faster than footsteps in the dark.

Will you brave the night and outsmart Betobetosan… or be the next one left trembling on the roadside?

—description from the designer

boardgame: 50 Minerales y Rocas: Naturaleza con Juan y Migas
description:
Juan and Migas help you identify 50 minerals and rocks you can encounter on your daily adventures.


     Fun questions
     Detailed illustrations
     Practical descriptions
     Lots of fascinating facts


Perfect for nature lovers!

This is a flexible educational game where players explore tiles featuring different minerals and rocks. Each tile includes engaging questions that can be answered individually, in teams, or as a family. Players can decide how to keep score, for example by awarding points for correct answers or simply playing for fun and learning. The game encourages curiosity, discussion, and discovery, letting everyone choose their preferred way to play.

—description from the publisher

Cuckoos love to hide and trick everyone! Get the animals in the right order to foil their plans!

Race to get your animals in the right order to claim the trophy. You'll need to listen carefully and act quickly to beat the competition.

Each player has 4 cards: 2 Skunks and 2 Raccoons in a face-down pile in front of them.

The active player for the turn names the 4 cards in any order, for example "Skunk, Raccoon, Skunk, Raccoon".

Both players, including the active player, race to grab their cards, reorder them, place the cards down in that order, and grab the trophy card from the center. Play continues until one player wins two turns in a row.

boardgame: 50 Aves de España y Europa: Naturaleza con Juan y Migas
description:
Juan and Migas help you identify 50 birds of Spain and Europe you can find on your daily adventures.


     Fun questions
     Detailed illustrations
     Practical descriptions
     Lots of interesting facts


Perfect for nature lovers!

This is a flexible educational game where players explore tiles showing different birds and other elements of nature. Each tile comes with engaging questions that can be answered individually, in teams, or as a family. Players can decide how to keep score, for example by awarding points for correct answers or simply playing for fun and learning. The game encourages curiosity, discussion, and discovery, letting everyone choose their preferred way to play.

—description from the publisher

In Dumpster Divers players will choose between a wide variety of critter families (raccoons, bears, pigeons, bin chickens, skunks, household pets, and perhaps a mysterious bigfoot) that are ripping open a trash bag, and competing with all the other families over the various rubbish scraps that come out of that bin. Each family will score points depending how full they are and how their dietary needs (public and private objectives) were provided for.

Players will use a sort of “I split you choose, bidding system” to try and get as many scraps as their greedy paws can get away with. On their turn players will “grab” some of what is available to all players trying to get away with all their coveted loot. Then each other player must decide if to further split the grabbed scraps or pass them by and focus on the remaining scraps still in the bin. Stay near the bin longer and you might get more food, but your family will have to settle for the dregs, but beware your family is hungry, they will not say no to any food even if it is against what the doctor prescribed.

—description from the designer

The MIND MGMT Playing cards feature all new art by Matt Kindt on every single card. Not only are these cards beautiful, but each card has a different ability on them. If you choose to play with abilities, then it will energize any existing card game out there! Want to add some zip to Hearts, Euchre, Cribbage, Rummy and more? Activate those abilities that will add some fun and unexpected twists to your old favourites. Not interested in the extra abilities? Simply ignore the text on the cards and they can be used as a regular deck of cards. Well, as regular as Matt Kindt would allow!

The Joker Card in this deck can be used in games of Mind MGMT: The Psychic Espionage “Game.” giving the ability: 'Recruiter discards to move through 1 mayhem token. They do not have to indicate which one.'

Flip out with Wingin’ It, the fast-paced family game that will send feathers flying! Designed for 2–5 players aged 8 and up, this explosive push-your-luck game is all about tactics, strategy, daring, and a bit of bird-brained bravery. Stack up your Chicks, whack the launcher, and let your birds fly!

Each turn, players use the unique bird token launcher to flip their baby chick tokens, hoping they’ll land as fully grown birds. The more you flip, the more tokens you can win, but push your luck too far and reveal identical baby birds, and you’ll forfeit your go!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Peaky Blinders: A Rogue Trivia Game
description:
Think you know everything about Tommy Shelby and the Peaky Blinders? Prove it in Peaky Blinders: A Rogue Trivia Game — the fast-talking, sharp-betting quiz game based on the hit TV show. Test your knowledge, bluff your way through, and outwit your rivals in this gritty game of gangland trivia.

Players answer questions about the world of Peaky Blinders, throw their answers into the mix, then place bets to fight for the chance to choose first. Pick the right answer, earn points, but be warned, picking wrong can cost you dearly.

It’s not just what you know…it’s how you play the game.

—description from the publisher

Become a master merchant in Traders of Muziris, a dynamic print-and-play game set in the bustling port city of ancient Kerala.

Over nine rounds, players navigate a colorful board of shops and harbors, spending action points to move, buy exotic goods like pepper and silk, and sell them to ships waiting in port. Completing ship contracts unlocks blueprints, gemstones, and flags that boost your prestige and end-game points. But beware—the crowd pawn can block key shops, forcing you to rethink your trading routes and timing.

With resource management, clever movement, and escalating rewards, Traders of Muziris recreates the thrill of the spice trade, letting you chart your path to becoming the most successful trader on the Malabar Coast.

—description from the designer

In Tadoba: Wildlife Safari, players venture into the dense jungles of India’s famed Tadoba Tiger Reserve, rolling dice to traverse a vibrant hex-based map filled with varied terrains like forests, hills, and reservoirs.

As they move, players reveal hidden Spotting Tokens that might lead to critter sightings or false alarms, creating moments of surprise and tension. Collected critter cards — birds, reptiles, and mammals — are carefully arranged into the Field Guide’s zones like Photography, Jungle Diaries, Observers, or secret Wild Habitats, each scoring differently and offering unique bonuses.

With clever movement choices, risk management, and set collection, Tadoba delivers an immersive wildlife adventure packed into an accessible print-and-play format.

—description from the designer

A two player card game where the players place animals and use their abilities and favored food to try and claim food cards. The basic version has players playing off the top of their deck and fighting over 2 food cards per round. There is an advanced version as well where players will have a small hand of cards and will be fighting over 4 food cards per round greatly increasing the animal interactions. The game is very youth friendly yet still advanced enough for veteran gamers. The cards along with their art were fully designed and printed locally in Pennsylvania.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Host Your Own Escape Room: Japan Edition
description: A night at an exclusive movie premiere in the heart of Tokyo takes a deadly turn when a dark force awakens from the cursed ground. You'll need to work together to solve the puzzles and prove your virtue before time runs out.

The cards are everything! Work together with up to 6 classes, scale the ever-changing tower, crawl dangerous rooms to find treasure, and defeat the dragon boss at the top. Design classes your way, tons of mechanic variety, and humorous flavor text!

Dungeon crawl the 6 random classic-style floors of the tower, defeating monsters and enduring traps to gain treasure, increase stats and equipment, and become strong enough to win.

Over 850 cards that consist of equipment, items, potions, abilities, monsters, traps, chests, events, jobs, races, and so much more! The cards each have their own rules and determine the order of events within the gameplay.


     1-6 players (4-6 classes)
     30-60 minute playtime (per floor, Legacy Gameplay)
     Ages 10+
     Game contains: 850+ game cards, 1 Floor Tracker, 1 Turn Tracker, 2 Status Effect Trackers, and 16 Meeples


—description from the designer

Note: All artwork is currently placeholder.

Almost 20 years ago, in the idyllic Swedish island of Mörkö, two murders took place. It's still unknown who the murderer is. It is now up to you if this murder case will ever be solved or forever rest in the unknown.

You get the mission to reopen the case. Find the killer by reviewing old case files, photos, maps, interviews, articles from the paper and more. There are clues that you might need to go online to comprehend, review alibies and so on.

The story is written based on what real criminal investigations look like, and has been revised by lawyers and police officers to feel more authentic.

Works great to play solo or in a party of crime-solving friends. Difficulty 3/5.

boardgame: Unsolved Mysteries: Mordet på Villa Sommen
description:
On a cold winter evening, an exclusive showing of Villa Sommen turns into a bloody tragedy when real estate agent Johanna Espe is found brutally murdered and mutilated in the mysterious house. The object has a dark history, and the investigation turns into a tangle of unresolved conflicts, burning passion, lies and fatal mistakes.

You get the opportunity to solve the case. Find the killer by reviewing old case files, photos, interviews, articles from the paper and more. There are clues that you might need to go online to solve.

Works great to play solo or in a party of crime-solving friends. Difficulty 3/5.
Expand your Say my Name with 50 new cards.
Expand your Say my Name with 50 new cards.

Expansion to Skull King including:
19 new play cards
3 reference cards
4 additional blank cards

The new cards add both additional suited cards that follow the standard game rules for Skull King as well as new special cards that add new abilities to the play.

This expansion is compatible with versions of Skull King that include the White Whale card in the base game.

boardgame: History Dad: The Second World War
description:
Welcome to the front!

History Dad: The Second World War is a set of miniatures wargame rules for you:

Whether you are an old hand or brand new to historical wargaming we have you covered with everything you could need to play out exciting battles in the greatest conflict of human history:

     A straight forward game system with minimal use of dice modifiers. You can focus on outflanking the enemy tanks and suppressing their troops, instead of trying to memorise a lot of numbers.
     A game that is friendly to being played whatever way you want to. Whether you want to create historical scenarios, play pick up games with army lists, play campaign games, play with friends or solo, it is all provided for. 
     Easy to adapt both historical encounters as well as scenarios from other skirmish games.


The rules are aimed at the platoon level with a typical force having 20-40 infantry and whatever supporting units you like such as a tank or two. You could potentially play quite large games but we suggest starting out smaller.

All figures are based individually with each figure representing exactly one soldier.

You may have questions at this point:

Who are the creators?

This is a straight up Nordic Weasel Games production so the writing and design is all Ivan Sørensen, with editing and proof reading assistance by Traci Morrissette (making this the first family project for NWG).

What are the basic mechanics?

Alternating activations with officers being able to activate multiple units, straight forward combat with delayed resolution, a bit more detail for vehicles (where lucky shots and hit locations make an appearance), suppression, concealed units and so forth. As a bonus both close assault and indirect fire rules do not require a correspondence course to understand.

What are some clever bits from the rules?

Casualty resolution is delayed so you don’t know how effective your fire was until the unit goes next. This applies to tanks as well which will often go “silent” when hit and you will have to decide if you give them another round or pick another target.

What all is included?

The “scenario rules” include mechanics for things like engineering and stealth missions, there are 7 scenarios to play, platoon information for the four “big” late-war powers (US, UK, German, Soviet), stats for 25 mid and late war vehicles, tables for rolling random forces, campaign rules, a points system (with the formula documented so you can set up your own vehicles quite easily) and solo play guidelines.

What scale I can play in? 

Any you like! As long as the figures are based individually you are good to go.

Practically speaking you will probably be using figures from 15mm and upwards.

Can I use an army built for another skirmish game?

Absolutely. Again, as long as they are individually based you are fine.

What if I don’t know anything about World War 2?

Do not fear! The rule book contains a host of explanations of various concepts of war and every chapter features War Explainers to help you understand what is going on and how the rules relate (or differ) from real, historical combat.

Will there be updates?

Yes. As is always the case when a game is released it turns out that there’s a typo that escaped detection, a rule that could be explained better or a scenario that needs tweaking. There will be updates forthcoming on a regular schedule.

For this reason we do suggest that you wait a few days to print out your copy.

Will there be more scenarios or expansions?

We are already discussing scenario books covering Poland 39 and Norway 40 in greater detail.

Will there be other conflicts covered?

Great War (1914-1918) will almost certainly be covered though possibly as a stand alone book. If there is interest and sales are sufficient, I would like to cover Korea later as well.

What exactly is a History Dad?

A History Dad lives within many of us. They find tanks and artillery guns cool, they think a lot about the Ardennes and they probably have “Never was so much owed by so many to so few” as their email signature message. In short, it is a short hand for someone who always had a fascination with and interest in the war but who perhaps never quite took the step into painting model soldiers and playing battles. Perhaps it all looked a bit intimidating. Perhaps they wanted to be able to finish the game in time to get the grill going.

Well, this is their time. Whether they are a mom, dad, sibling or just an interested party, the spirit of the History Dad is alive and well.

—description from the publisher

EFS is a light party game about voting, as aliens, on all the stuff found on your first visit to Earth. You picked up a lot of stuff and possibly a human or two who may be posing as aliens amongst you.

Each turn, one of the players (the leader for this turn) picks a topic and everyone votes on it using the E F S cards. The letters stand for Epic, Funky, and Smart, until the group redefines what they stand for. Like voting on Earth, the MAJORITY wins, but only if all of the letters E, F, and S appear in the vote. If only two of the three letters appear, the MINORITY wins. If everyone votes using the same letter, the leader loses a point. Ties make whatever the leader voted for the winning vote. Remember, you are voting as aliens, so you don't have to vote how you think. You can also discuss and lie about how you will vote.

Players gain a point for each winning vote they cast. The player with he most points after 2 rounds wins. Unless... there are humans; They win as a team if undetected. Unless... a player correctly accuses another player of being human. Accusing costs 3 points, but if correct, you win the game!

The game was designed with inclusivity and creativity in mind. Instead of prescribing topics, the language-independent cards are full of illustrations to help inspire players to come up with funny topics. The voting words (Epic, Funky, Smart) are customizable to suit any group. This 30-minute game is for 4-10 players, work colleagues, friends, families, creative comedians, drinking buddies, nerds, aliens, and deduction-style, light party game lovers.

There are two variants included in the game for you to discover. EFS is available as a Forever Free Print and Play Game with English and Japanese rules.

—description from the designer
A 2-player game with different victory condition in each game.
Add 6 new species to the Oikos forest. With these 6 birds native to the Brazilian fauna, new mechanics and interactions can be added in order to increase the game's playability.

Add 6 new species to the Oikos forest. With these 6 reptiles native to the Brazilian fauna, new mechanics and interactions can be added in order to increase the game's playability.

Components:
6 species boards
1 heat marker
30 wooden pieces:
2 Alligator
3 Anaconda
4 Tortoise
5 Coral
7 Iguana
9 Lizard-of-the-Backlands

Promotional type of crowdfunding on MeepleStarter, adds the Capybara specie.

Components:
1 species board
5 wooden capybara meeples

Add 11 new species to the Oikos Forest and scenarios, threats and goal cards. Additional animals released during the MeepleStarter crowdfunding campaign, new mechanics and interactions can be added to greatly increase the game's playability.

Build interactive worlds for limitless tabletop gaming with SOURCE.

SOURCE is a modular tabletop gaming system and open-source platform created by Adventure Together Games. It allows players to build and customize tabletop maps and environments for various games, including RPGs like Dungeons & Dragons and Warhammer 40K, as well as their own custom adventures. The platform is designed to be scalable, durable, and immersive, with a focus on affordability and community-driven content creation.

Modular System:
SOURCE uses a system of hexes and tiles that can be combined to create diverse and dynamic map layouts.

Open Source:
The platform is open source, meaning anyone can design and publish their own games, rulesets, and assets for use on SOURCE.

Scalable and Immersive:
SOURCE enables the creation of large, detailed game environments that can be easily set up and torn down. 
 Community Driven:
SOURCE is designed to foster a community where players and creators can share their content and collaborate on new experiences.

BUILD. AIM. SWING. ROLL. SCORE.
A Golf Simulation Like No Other
Combine dice-rolling strategy with customizable courses for endless fun. Aim you shot, pick your club, and curve your ball across diverse terrain. Sink your ball in the fewest strokes to claim victory!

Accessible, Customizable, and Replayable.
Build, rebuild, and imagine your courses for unique challenges every game.

A 2P head-to-head game, where each player utilises Gods of the Greek pantheon to outmanoeuvre each other. Players take turns playing cards to their 3x3 grids, then resolving all the effects of their face up cards to influence the game state.

After each player has played their 7 cards, their grids are scored according to the number of connections their face up Gods are showing. Combine this with any points scored from card effects throughout the game, and the player with the highest score wins.

This game is available exclusively as a free print and play.

—description from the designer

Put all the potato chips in the bag. Then, start taking them out one by one. If you pull out the wrong chip, all the others will jump out too! Get your friends together and have a round of games to see who can go the longest without letting a chip jump out!

...

Coloque todas as batatinhas fritas dentro do saquinho. Depois, comece a tirar uma a uma. Se você puxar a batatinha errada, todas as outras pulam também! Junte os amigos e faça rodadas para ver quem consegue ficar mais tempo sem deixar a batatinha pular!

Divirta-se tentando achar e pegar as imagens corretas, usando as "mãozinhas" com ventosas.

No final depois de cada um dos rounds, quando todas as imagens ja foram encontradas e coletadas, o jogador que pegou mais cartas iguais as que vao sendo reveladas, vence!

...

Have fun trying to find and pick up the right images, using the little hands with suction cups.

At the end of several rounds, when all the cards have been found and collected, the player who has picked up the most cards matching those that are revealed in each round, wins!

Build. Battle. Bot your way to victory.

In Rows & Bots, every move matters in your race to get four robots in a row on a dynamic 4x4 grid. Draw cards, trade parts, assemble your ultimate bots, and strategically move them into position — or crash the competition in head-to-head battles!

With simple rules and fast-paced turns, this clever game of cards and strategy is perfect for players ages 12 and up. Whether you're planning the perfect combo or disrupting your opponent’s lineup, Rows & Bots is packed with tactical choices and fun surprises every round.

Build smart. Battle bold. Line them up to win!

—description from the designer

Reboot Food is cooperative educational game that plays like an escape room. Each page is a fully illustrated environment with puzzles hidden everywhere. Through teamwork and critical thinking, you'll piece together solutions to create a sustainable future.

How To Play
Work together to solve riddles and puzzles on each page. Each page has three puzzles. Solve the puzzles in any order, but don't turn the page until all the puzzles are completed. Pay close attention to the details on each page.

Everything you need to solve a puzzle is visible on the page; no folding or cutting is required. Each puzzle has a numerical code as its answer. Once you're sure of your answer, you may check it.

End Of The Game
If you solve the final puzzle, stop the stopwatch and celebrate with your team. You have rebooted the food system!

Scoring
Your total time determines your score. Are you Unbeetable, Strawbest or Pumpkintastic?

—description from the designer

Pātaahua is a bilingual strategy game set in pre-colonial Aotearoa. You get to play as the rangatira (chief) of a hapū (sub tribe), tasked with building the most magnificent pā (fortified village) in the land. You’ll send your people to gather resources, build constructions (like whare, waka and defences), make artefacts (like tools, korowai and weapons) and gain the deep knowledge of tohunga.

Your goal is to have the most mana (i.e. the most points) by the end of the game. Along the way, players need to gather, trade and spend their resources wisely to be sure they can feed their people and show manaaki to other hapū. Central to the game are the cultural values of kaitiakitanga (environmental guardianship), manaakitanga (hospitality) and mana tangata (power and status).

—description from the publisher

In Horticolor, you'll have to create the most beautiful garden.
This garden will be made up of plants, trees, various insects and the small animals associated with them.
During the 3 rounds, using the resource dice, you will try to validate your garden cards, but be careful: the values and colours of the dice must match those of your garden cards. What's more, some cards have synergies between them, such as bees and flowering plants.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Amelia Davies et la Quête du Graal
description: In this cooperative puzzle / treasure hunt game, players work together to solve riddles in over 50 documents in order to find the Holy Grail.

A quiz game with a stack of cards each with six questions about the 2000's.

At the beginning of the game, the players decide how many points to play to.

On a player's turn, he rolls the die and the player to his left asks him the corresponding question. If he answers correctly, he gets the card as a point. If his answer is wrong, the other players may hit the bell to answer the question and get the card and the point if correct.

The game ends when a player has the agreed number of points.

Breaking Demon’s Contract is a dark, emotional cooperative card game that draws players into a world where friendship is the only weapon against inner torment. One of your own—the Possessed—is being consumed by a hidden Demon. To save them, players must build trust, confront emotional wounds, and uncover the truth buried within.

Gameplay
Designed for 2–4 players, the game revolves around strategic card play, resource management, and team coordination. Players gain Friendship Points to unlock the ability to Talk It Out—a critical moment that reveals the Demon. Once revealed, the Demon becomes more powerful, triggering effects and escalating tension. Each round is framed by a Diurnal Cycle, representing limited time before the Possessed is lost for good.

Goal
Players must work together to heal the Possessed, endure unpredictable events, and deal damage to the Demon using action cards. Victory comes only if the Demon is defeated before time runs out. Fail, and the Possessed is lost to darkness.

Tone & Feel
While rich in theme, the game emphasizes teamwork, planning, and clever card play over storytelling. Expect tension, tough decisions, and satisfying cooperation—not a narrative, but an experience shaped by your choices.

—description from the publisher

At the dawn of time, the first spirits were born - gentle and tireless in their mission to spread life across the barren land of Pangaea. As players, you enter this mythical age, growing your trees and performing rituals to awaken Gaia’s dormant energy and summon elemental creatures to roam and nurture the growing continent.

In the Dawn of Pangaea, you and your fellow players act as the first spirits of Pangaea. On your turn, you must choose 2 of the 5 actions that are available to you e.g. planting seeds, growing trees and expanding your forest. Anytime during your turn, you may also play blessing or favor tokens, that you have collected during the game. These tokens will also allow you to summon the ancient guardians with their powerful abilities. Play Ritual cards to score points for growing your trees across different biomes or for attracting creatures that require a specific weather condition. With every point that you gain from these rituals, you climb higher up the sacred Mountain.

The first player who reaches the top of the mountain, triggers the end of the game and initiates the final scoring. The player with the most points wins the game and gains the high blessing of Gaia.

—description from the publisher

The second mini-expansion to Wild Cards. It consists of 1 new leader card: the Penguin leader.

You receive the Penguin leader whenever you buy an animal with cards of its natural habitat. The Penguin leader will give a higher reward for paying in natural habitat cards as long as the player owns the card.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Saurozoic Warriors Arena, the gladiatorial Dino battling board game! Players compete as DINO-GLADIATORS in a brutal contest of brute strength and cunning strategy; the last Dino standing wins!

During each game, players use cards and dice rolls to defeat other gladiators and win the crowd's favor.  However, The arena is built upon an active volcano.  Each eruption affects all players and changes the game.  The game ends when the last crowd card is drawn. Each player gets once last turn. The winner is the only one gladiator left standing. But, if more than 1 gladiator survives, the final volcanic eruption eliminates all remaining gladiators.

—description from the publisher

Highway Lights is a reimplementation of the designer's 2019 release "Run et Run". The game is a race along a track where some spaces show positive numbers and some spaces show negative numbers.

A number of cards are revealed face up, and in turn order, the players draft one of the cards. These cards are then executed in numerical order - rather than turn order. Players may occupy the same space, but are stacked. If a player would move off of a space, they carry any player pieces on top of them with them through the move.

If the space a player ends on shows a number, positive or negative, that is a movement value applied only to the top-most player token that was moved.

There are some other wrinkles, but the player who reaches the goal first wins.

Bird Brains is a memory game about just 7 cards. Each card shows a color and a pattern - and a different color and pattern on the rear.

On a player's turn, they flip over 2 cards, aiming to have the color or pattern match. If they do, the turn proceeds to the next player. If they do not, the player takes the chicken token.

If a player needs to take the chicken token and already has it, they lose.

Grab your favorite pen, pencil, or quill—it’s time to dive into a fast-paced, ever-changing dungeon crawl that fits in your backpack and plays just about anywhere.

In Dungeon Run: Pocket, you’ll battle monsters, dodge traps, and grab treasure while mapping your own path through a randomized dungeon layout. No two runs are ever the same. Choose your moves wisely—when your health hits zero, the goblins haul you out on a stretcher (and they don’t do refunds).

Perfect for solo adventurers and coffee-table crawlers alike.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Windog, on your turn, you declare a color and an animal, marking it off. The other players then mark either a window of the same color or a window with the same type of animal.

Players earn points for marking off a certain number of each animal and for completing bingo lines.

The game ends when a player completes a specific number of lines and the person with the most points wins.

Eastern Front, Summer 1944: Soviet forces launch a major offensive, codenamed BAGRATION, which destroys a large portion of the German army positioned in the central sector. The Soviets advance as far as the Vistula River, reaching the outskirts of Warsaw. General Model, by rallying some relatively intact forces and counterattacking from various directions, manages to halt the offensive, at least temporarily. All this occurs as Warsaw rises up, and, a little further south in Magnuszew, other Soviet forces succeed in crossing to the western side of the Vistula, creating a dangerous bridgehead...

The game consists of ten turns in total.
Each turn represents one day of real time. Each hex represents a distance of about two kilometers from side to side. Units vary from brigades to battalions, most of them armored, mechanized or motorized.

Complexity is rated 2 out of 5 and an average game duration is about 4-5 hours.

GAME COMPONENTS

    1 standard box - 30x22x3.5 cm
    2 identical standard maps - 56x86 cm (22x34 inches)
    2 counter sheets, 14.5 mm counters - 176 counters per countersheet
    1 color rulebook (16 pages in English, 16 pages in Italian)
    5 A4 size cards
        1 table card in English and Italian
        1 setup and reinforcement card in English and Italian
        1 German events description card in English and Italian
        1 Soviet events description card in English and Italian
        1 German/Soviet tank guide
    
    2 dice


(from publisher's website, corrected by BGG user)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Elden Ring: Machinations of the Witch
description:
Elden Ring is a big box cooperative fantasy campaign game, where each session is playable in 90-120 minutes and core box campaigns will span 30-40 hours, increasing to 90 hours with the expansions. Diceless and with strong elements of deck-building, combat is both deadly and personal. As your Tarnished character levels up, you must carefully build your attack and effect decks to best optimize with your equipment.

Explore the Lands Between and discover its mysteries on a map of detailed hex tiles, drawn from a stack that evolves with each scenario. Go toe-to-toe against great enemies on individualised combat grids, each designed to challenge players with unique obstacles. Transport yourself into a gripping narrative, journeying through the hidden corners of the Lakes of Liurnia.

Machinations of the Witch brings brand new gameplay elements to the foundation laid by previous Elden Ring boxes. Face off against enemies wielding powerful sorceries, attempting to counter them with quick thinking and careful timing. Explore the Lakes of Liurnia, scouring the world around you for secrets to give you an edge over your foes.

Elden Ring: The Board Game is a big box cooperative fantasy campaign game, where each session is playable in 90-120 minutes and core box campaigns will span 30-40 hours, increasing to 90 hours with the expansions. Diceless and with strong elements of deck-building, combat is both deadly and personal. As your Tarnished character levels up, you must carefully build your attack and effect decks to best optimize with your equipment.

Explore the Lands Between and discover its mysteries on a map of detailed hex tiles, drawn from a stack that evolves with each scenario. Go toe-to-toe against great enemies on individualised combat grids, each designed to challenge players with unique obstacles. Transport yourself into a gripping narrative, journeying through the decaying ruins of Caelid.

Dominion of Rot brings brand new gameplay elements to the foundation laid by previous Elden Ring boxes. Cleanse areas of Caelid to push back the infection of the Scarlet Rot during exploration scenarios, and finish battles decisively to stop enemies empowered by the infection from growing too strong. Be wary of the rot building up on your Tarnished too, or your attribute deck can become infected with cards that could put you in danger!

After a hard day escaping dungeons and facing dragons, there’s nothing like returning to town and getting hammered while playing cards at the finest tavern in the region: HeartsDrunk!

Become a legend at the tavern by mastering the art of drinking—stay on your feet each round in this high-proof card game!

Based on the classic game of Hearts, HeartsDrunk is a pocket-sized drinking game for 4 players. It combines trick-taking mechanics with special magical drink cards that can be purchased at the tavern each round. Players also have a skill tree—each skill more ridiculous than the last—which can be upgraded by spending XP.

In the end, the goal is to stay sober at the table. Each Heart card collected costs the player life points (and a shot of their drink, of course). When a player’s life hits zero, they must buy the next round of drinks and are out of the game. The player with the most life points left at the end is crowned the grand winner of HeartsDrunk!

—description from the publisher

Not So Fast! is a must follow trick-taking game with a bicycle racing theme. Each player will be assigned a player color, and that player color will determine what their personal trump will be.

Each round, players will be dealt 8 cards. Players will draw an additional 4 cards from an Exchange deck and may choose to exchange 0-4 of those cards with the cards they were dealt. For each card exchanged, players must “Down Shift” their bicycles (pip down the value on their 10-sided Gear Die).

After 8 hands of trick-taking are completed, players will score whatever the current value is on their Gear Die for each trick won. Each player will also have a chance to go “Off Track” (declare that they will win zero tricks) as long as they don’t exchange any cards.

—description from the designer

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms or Rolling Realms Redux with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Vantage.

To play this realm, you mark cards in your grid and you activate those cards by using their numbers in other realms. Can you build up the perfect grid in synergy with the other realms to earn 6 stars?

—description from the publisher

Sibelius’ compositions drew Finland’s borderlines into the world map, in an age when the state of Finland had yet to attain its independence. How did this semi-orphan son of a young widow grow into a revered maestro, who rubbed shoulders with kings and presidents? And what is synesthesia, and how did it affect the composer’s life? In this game, you can relive Sibelius’ childhood in Hämeenlinna and his final years in Ainola in Järvenpää, join him in his studies abroad, take part in both his solitary and family travels around the world, and witness the creation of a Finnish identity for a fledgling nation. The players’ movements around the game board are dictated by dice-throws and governed by a set of rules, which are supplemented by a history section.

This game was published for the 100th anniversary of Finnish independence in 2017.

This is educational roll and move game about Helsinki. Players are rolling the dice and moving across the streets of Helsinki. There are 32 places of interest and if players happen to stumble on them, then they must answer some questions. If the player knows the right answer, he/she might get some kind of reward (extra throw of dice, get further). If the player's answer is not correct, he/she might get punished (miss a turn, go backwards). First one to finish is the winner.

This is educational roll and move game about Suomenlinna a sea fortress off the coast of Helsinki. Players are rolling the dice and moving across the streets and alleys of Suomenlinna. There are 29 places of interest and if players happen to stumble on them, then they must answer some questions. If the player knows the right answer, he/she might get some kind of reward (extra throw of dice, get further). If the player's answer is not correct, he/she might get punished (miss a turn, go backwards). First one to finish is the winner.

Prove you're the best dinosaur collector and have fun. Add up your points to win. Enjoy collecting the most dinosaurs. Travel the world, completing the challenges in this board game, and defeat your opponents. Roll the die to move around the world, collect help cards to use on your turn, and capture dinosaurs to score points and avoid being eaten. The player with the most points wins.

Demuestra ser el mejor recolector y diviértete. Suma tus puntos para ganar. Disfruta recolectar el mayor número de dinosaurios. Recorre el mundo cumpliendo los retos de este juego de mesa y vence a tus oponentes. Lanza el dado para moverte por el mundo, obten cartas de ayuda para utilizar en tu turno, captura dinosarios para ganar puntos y evitar ser devorado. El jugador con mas puntos sera el ganador.

This is educational roll and move game about History of Porvoo City (Borgoa Vetus in Latin). Players are rolling the dice and moving across the streets of old Porvoo. There are 28 places of interest and if players happen to stumble on them, then something good or bad happens (extra throw of dice, get further, miss a turn, go backwards). First one to finish is the winner.

Love all things space? Get ready for the ultimate Space guessing game! Divide yourselves into teams, or play one on one. Ask up to 10 questions to guess what's on the game card! Is it a planet? Does it emit its own light? Think hard, ask intelligent questions, use your clue cards wisely, and be the first player to collect 7 game cards to win!

—description from the publisher

The greatest musical recognition game! Spot The Intro is the crazily addictive audio quiz game in which teams compete to recognize songs from just 10 second clips! Played using your smartphone, it's a memory jogging mix of fun and nostalgia for anyone with a passion for music. Featuring hits from the last six decades, and utilizing your favorite music-streaming service, there's 1,200 great tip-of-the-tongue tracks for every generation to try and guess! Grab your phone... it's time to get the party started!

—description from the publisher

Team goes forward by answering the questions correctly. For every correct answer team can hit the timer with plastic hammer and it gives team more time to list more answers. Ten is the maximum number of answers in the round. First team to reach the goal, is the winner.

Camp Grizzly is a semi-cooperative survival horror game in which you and your group take on the roles of camp counselors being stalked by Otis, a homicidal maniac in a bear mask. To win, players must collect a set of objectives to trigger (and survive!) one of four available Finales. Along the way, the counselors will find items and weapons to help them survive and fight back, encounter cameos and plot twists, bump into errant campers in need of help, and maybe even sneak off for some foolin' around.

Rife with Easter eggs and steeped in lore, Camp Grizzly is a love letter to the horror genre that creates unforgettable moments and stories at the table, and we can't wait to get this new edition into your hands!

Camp Grizzly Second Edition will include the full game and all previous expansions, and is being created in collaboration with the original designer and artist. Here is what's new:


     Double-sided game board with an all-new camp layout
     Great quality components and cards
     A few rules tweaks including adjustments based on player count
     Optional rules to remove player elimination
     Updated art & graphics


—description from the publisher

A Bible-based Trading Card Game for 2-4 players where the first player to fully illuminate their Lamp (scoreboard) wins! Play cards with titles and concepts drawn directly from the bible. Use the proper spiritual trait tokens as resources to play each card. Don't forget to build, trade, and collect! It's a game and more.
Space Base: Velocity is a micro expansion that features 12 new ship cards to add to your Level 3 deck giving you instant rewards for Deploying them, extra turns, and turbo charging your dice rolls!

boardgame: Ready Set Bet: Let It Ride
description:
Let it Ride adds 5 new Exotic Finish and 14 new VIP cards to add to your games of Ready Set Bet.

There are 10 new Bonus Bet chips showcasing new VIP bets like Bifectas and Exactas! Included are 2 Promotional VIP cards that can only be played with a live House player calling the action as they alter the race in interesting new ways. The Exotic Finish card, Tight Race,  from the original game has also been updated, changing the requirement a bit for more action.

There are two versions of Let it Ride, matching the design of either the retail version or the High Roller edition of Ready Set Bet.

—description from the designer

As a Philanthropist, you're volunteering as chairperson of a conservation fair to benefit the endangered Lemur Leaf Frog. It's almost time to open the fair to guests, but none of the vendors have arrived yet. Can you finish setting up in time for the event to go off without a hitch?

Endangered Rescue: Lemur Leaf Frog is part of the 18Escape line of games, each of which pack a full escape room game experience into a tiny package! As a group, you will solve a series of puzzles to unravel a mystery.

Puzzles may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the companion website (compatible with all smart phones and computers) and it will check whether you are correct. If you are, the website will tell you which card or cards to take next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game, but can pass it on to another group for their use.

This entry in the series takes about an hour to solve, and is rated 1 out of 3 in difficulty.

—description from the publisher

Welcome back, witches! Some new witches have set up shop in town, and for better or worse, their familiars seem to have made a home for themselves on your workbench! New arcana has been unearthed to add different powers to every game.

Whirling Witchcraft: Fantastic Familiars introduces a new cast of witches to the world of Whirling Witchcraft. These witches offer a more challenging, but unique, experience. Some come with their own familiar, a magical animal that will visit each witch and cause chaos on their workbench. Whirling Witchcraft: Fantastic Familiars also gives you a set of 9 arcana cards, where any combination of the 3 different symbols will be played each game.

—description from the publisher

Atomic Assault Multiplayer are rules for miniature combat in space for 2+ players.  
Our playtesting was conducted with 4-12 players.  
Spaceship combat on 2" hexmat 6' x 4' or 8' x 6' works very well.  
The rules include color asteroids to cut out or photocopy and glue to card stock.

Turn sequence:

     Write movement orders
     Move ships
     Plot shooting
     Take damage received
     Check for criticals


All ships start with six shields, one per hex side.  
Burn through a shield and extra damage goes internal to the hull and more important parts of the ship.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood on the Clocktower: The Carousel – Experimental Character Collection
description:
The Carousel' is an Experimental Token Set - a collection of physical tokens including 72 Experimental Characters for the social deduction game Blood on the Clocktower.

This character pack is designed for those committed to playing BOTC with custom scripts and experimental characters, taking their social deduction gaming experience to the next level.

This includes all Experimental Characters included in the Kickstarter edition (with any updated abilities), but does not include any characters from the base game.

Requires a copy of Blood on the Clocktower, and custom scripts.

In this fantasy adventure game you will play as a one of four royal heroes, called Mageineers because of their ability to merge magic with machine. A Warlord has attacked your land of Galmenni and set fire to the capital. With your parents the king and queen gone, your tutor Dobern leads you to the only place he believes you have any hope - the Junkyard.

In the Junkyard you will draw cards for making a Juggernaut, a mech powered by your magic, to fight the mechanized minions and the Warlord. But being made from salvaged junk, you will need to travel across the land completing quests, upgrading your Juggernaut, meeting the locals, and fighting beasts, to grow strong enough to face the Warlord's Junk Golems, before the final confrontation with the Warlord.

However the Warlord card will secretly go to you or another player, becoming their Acolyte, which will make for a final confrontation of all players verse one.

—description from the designer

The zoo animals are on the loose and need to be pulled back in.

Use multi-functional cards to fulfill goals and capture the animals in this 18-card cooperative wallet game.

Go Extinct! Megafauna is an all-new edition of the award-winning game Go Extinct! that features Australia's unique and charismatic extinct megafauna with original illustrations by palaeoartist Nellie Pease. Play a round of Go Extinct! Megafauna to meet the enormous reptiles and mammals of Australia's recent past and learn some of their Indigenous names.
This Fast-paced game is like Go Fish, with players competing to collect related sets of animals (known as clades). Players can ask for their favourite animals by name or ask for deeper categories of animals, such as any mammal or marsupial. This winning strategy teaches students how to identify common ancestors and read an evolutionary tree, one of the most important scientific diagrams out there!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Railroad Tiles: Hospital & Local Market Promo Tiles
description:
Expand your Railroad Tiles experience with 2 additional sets of Objective tiles!

Build the Hospital surrounded by Towns, and make sure the Local Market is well connected to your Highways!

Included as part of the base game 2024 Kickstarter Campaign's exclusives.

Take control of one team in this foosball-like game of hockey and best your opponent!
Control your players via sticks coming out of the rear of the table. Twist, move and shoot to try to get past your opponent's goalie.

Get ready to test your speed with the Whac-A-Mole Match-A-Mole Game. A fast-paced game that's a total blast. Spot the match to the mole tile and "smack" it. The first player to get 5 moles wins!

How to play:

     Players take turns flipping over their top card and placing it in front of them, on top of any cards they may have previously flipped.
     As soon as you see a card or combination of cards that match the mole tile, whack the mole tile with your mole smacker!
     The first player to stick their mole smacker to the mole tile and pick it up, gets to keep it. If it's not sticking, keep smacking!
     Flip over a new mole tile and continue playing. Pay attention!


There might already be a match showing. If there is, smack it!
If you accidentally pick up a mole tile that does not match any of the face-up cards, you must put the tile back and set your mole smacker on the table. You can't use it again until another player collects a tile.

     Keep playing until someone gets 5 mole tiles.



     If the “X” card is showing on anyone's pile, no one is allowed to use their mole smacker-even if a matching card is showing! They'll have to wait until the X card is covered when the next card is flipped over.
     When a player flips over the “!!!” card, all players race to smack the mole tile, whether there is a match or not. This only happens once, as soon as the card is revealed.


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description:
A tactical card game for 2 to 6 prospectors who try to dig as much gold as possible from the shared mine in 4 rounds using their equipment. However, diggers are not nice to each other and stealing the opponents gold will be a common thing!

—description from the publisher

A poker game for 2-6 players in which the players have to use their card luck to win as much money as possible. A combination of traditional poker with a 3D cargo ship to match the poker cards.

—description from the publisher

A dice and auction game for 3-4 players in which the players have to make the most of their dice luck to win as many towers as possible. Players may use all or some dice again to push their luck - or fail.

—description from the publisher

A tactical dice game for 3-5 players in which they try to get their wagons to the front of the train as secretly as possible. As the other player do not know your wagon, make them guess wrong so your wagons reaches the aim first!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Aquatica: Stubborn Crab & Huntress Promo Cards
description:
Promo cards for Aquatica base game.

Stubborn crab lets you buy a location and recruit a character.
The Huntress lets you raise 2 and conquer a location.

As it is time to prepare for bed, a group of friends discuss their plans. It seems a being of the nightmare realm will come again to steal their toys, and only by setting traps of their own do they have a chance of getting even.

Playthings is a sensory guessing game where players take turns as the Night Terror, hiding toys in rooms on a house map, while the rest ‘sleep’ with their eyes closed. They await daybreak and a chance to guess where their toys have gone. However, they had the chance to gain slivers of information to assist them— such as sliding their fingers under the mat to feel vibrations, or placing jingling bells as sound traps But the true choice is simply whether or not to peek and know for certain. However, even as the players score points individually and cooperatively for finding their toys, the Night Terror scores for their failures, and the ability to guess who did or didn't peek.

A ridiculous and unique ritual of the senses, Playthings asks if players are smart enough to deduce from very little, or are brave enough to feign ignorance as they watched the Night Terror commit its deeds. Will you peek?

—description from the publisher

From the box front:

"Top Agent is a top thrilling contra espionage game for all ages, and an exciting family entertainment. Where is the Top Agent? Who will expose him? How many men do you lose in the tough game? The game setup is ever new and the solution unpredictable."

Sixty-eight chits are distributed face-down on an 8x9 grid. Ten of these are bombs, while the rest are foreign agents of varying value, including the Top Agent 'Nelson Strax' himself! Players each start from one of the empty corners with one of their own four agents (pawns) and move on the board by rolling a d6 and turning over chits for points. Finding a bomb causes the pawn to 'die' immediately and the player can start with a new one from their corner; losing all of their agents means the player is out of the rest of the game, but can keep the points collected so far.

Once 'Nelson Strax' - worth 5 points, the highest value agent in the game - has been found the game turns into a race, with each player trying to get back to their home base as fast as possible for a small points bonus by the order of arrival. Most total points at the end wins.

This game is likely from the late 1960's as the main publisher went defunct in 1969 (although the brand may have been used for some time after) and it's also the time that FinnGame was active.

4go takes place on a 4x4 board upon which players take turns placing randomly drawn pieces. The pieces each have 2 colors, one on each side, and players may choose which color to place face up.

The object of the game is to create a 4-in-a-row of their own color (warm or cool) or the neutral "void" color. Players may substitute "wild" colors for any of the other colors when forming their 4-in-a-row.

Players may also stack pieces onto single pieces that have already been placed on the board, provided the colors that touch "match."

The game can also be played in an "advanced" mode where players attempt to score points by forming multiple 4-in-a-rows, scoring a point for each piece of their color involved in the 4-in-a-row. In this mode, pieces can also be moved to adjacent spaces, so long as stacking rules continue to be respected.

—description from the sculptor
