Hercules is a promo champion for backers, who support the Micro Hero: Hercules Kickstarter.

The card needs a starter kit/ advanced deck to be playable.

Hercules
Mana Cost: 8
Attack: 8, Defense: 5

The special abilities are:
Rage
Beserk

NEW 2-IN-1 PUZZLE BOARD GAMES: This board game includes 2 ways to play: a magnet game and a connect 5 pieces game. This strategic board game not only keeps children away from electronic devices but also promotes brain development and builds concentration
MAGNETIC GAME CHESS: This magnet game includes 36 magnetic pieces for 2-4 players. The goal is simple: The first player to get rid of their pieces wins! When magnetic pieces attract each other, everyone laughs heartily, and you will feel that it is all worth it
CONNECT 5 PIECES: This board game includes 120 black and white plastic pieces for 2 players. The goal is simple: The first player to connect 5 pieces in a straight line wins! When someone achieves this, everyone cheers about it, enhancing the bond between people
ROTATOR ADDS FUN: Players can decide whether to use the rotator and in which direction to turn it.

—description from the publisher

Asteros: Lords of Debris is a sci-fi strategy game where rival private companies - equipped with only a few ships and limited resources - mine asteroids to bankroll an emerging off-world colony. Across four interlocking zones - the asteroid belt, an orbital station, trade routes, and a bustling spaceport - you’ll refine ore, manufacture components, expand shared infrastructure, and compete for contracts. A dynamic event engine keeps gravity hazards and market prices in flux, making every round a test of timing, movement, and adaptability. Profits and reputation crown the victor, yet the harsh frontier forces competitors to rely on one another - blending fierce economic rivalry with unavoidable cooperation. At the end of the game, the player with the most reputation points is declared the winner.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A set of scenarios specifically designed for strategic solo play - scenarios that would highlight the strengths and weaknesses of each class and force players to really get under the hood and learn to play each class masterfully.

—description from the publisher

22 Modern Games for the Classic Tarot

A book of 22 modern card games inspired by the Major Arcana from acclaimed designer D. Brad Talton Jr, plus 5 new pocket-sized decks.

The games include: Lottery, Disappearing Act, Crowns, Tango, Hot Pursuit, The Lion's Share, and more! Some games play up to 8 players, and some are solo.

—description from the publisher

This is educational roll and move game about Sibelius, the most famous Finnish Composer. Players are rolling the dice and moving through places in Europe following the carrier of Sibelius. There are 28 places of interest and if players happen to stumble on them, then they must answer some questions. If the player knows the right answer, he/she might get some kind of reward (extra throw of dice, get further). If the player's answer is not correct, he/she might get punished (miss a turn, go backwards). First one to finish is the winner.

In a land devoured by fire and fanaticism, not every ally wears the face of a hero.

This expansion introduces Burgomaster Erik Ziegler, known as der Schachspieler — the Chess Player — a shadowy manipulator who may lend his aid to the Powerwolf… but always at a steep cost.
Can you trust a man who plays both sides of the flame?

Inside, you’ll find 10 Burgomaster cards, 5 new Powerwolf Action cards, and 5 Heretic Wolf cards — each adding new layers of strategy and tension to your battle for Magdalena’s salvation.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dominion of Napoleon Bonaparte: Fight Battles of the Napoleonic Wars – Solo Game
description:
This standalone set of rules covers the French Revolutionary Wars and the Napoleonic Wars. It is built on the Dominion of the Spear game system but is a brand new set of rules with appropriate rule changes (including a pre-battle artillery bombardment and increased use of the unreliable attribute). These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-6 units and a solo battle can be fought within 10 minutes. An example play through is included. There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover. Use model figures of any scale or paper and pen as there is no measuring (grid used). Uses 2 six sided dice.

25 historical battles are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically:

Montenotte 1796, Lodi 1796, Rivoli 1797, Pyramids 1798, Marengo 1800, Austerlitz 1805, Jena 1806, Auerstedt 1806, Eylau 1807, Friedland 1807, Bailén 1808, Roliça 1808, Vimeiro 1808, Corunna 1809, Wagram 1809, Talavera 1809, Salamanca 1812, Borodino 1812, Berezina 1812, Vitoria 1813, Leipzig 1813, Hanau 1813, Quatre Bras 1815, Ligny 1815, Waterloo 1815.

—description from the publisher

Before the flames. Before the Priory. There was Vargamor — the first to awaken the blood of man and wolf, the mother of all wolves.

Her voice still echoes in the dark forest, calling her children to stand against mankind’s relentless hunt.

This expansion includes 17 Mission cards that aims to protect the pack’s cubs and empower the heroes with new tools to stop the execution of the innocent Magdalena.

—description from the publisher

Position Your Cards Fast… Just Don’t Be Last!

A really fun, original card game in which players start with four square cards that contain a total of 16 icons. Race your opponent(s) to position your 16 icon images, so that only nine of them are visible in a 3x3 grid, perfectly matching the nine icons that appear on the challenge card. Score extra points for finishing first and for keeping your icons connected. Underground Gridzy includes 60 regular challenges and ten ‘expert’ challenges.

Doodle Disaster is a party drawing game that perfectly showcases how quickly and hysterically communication can break down.

Players take turns as either the Doodle Dictator or a Doodler. As the Doodle Dictator, you explain how to draw a picture only you can see. You can only describe the picture using lines and shapes. The Doodlers keep their drawings hidden and must remain silent!

After two-minutes, the Doodlers guess the picture. Did the Doodlers create recognizable works-of-art or Doodle Disasters?

—description from the publisher

Hockey 110 is as exciting as ice hockey and as strategic as chess. You'll experience the same game situations as a real game.

Checking, risky passes, one-time shots, and spectacular saves are all integral parts of this game.

For each attempted play, you know the exact chance of success expressed as a percentage.

No cards to draw to influence a sequence of plays; it's your efforts on the ice that will dictate the outcome of the game.

Set in the word of Quincy's Tavern, this is a strategy card game where you place cards from your hand to complete patterns as you compete to place all player tokens first.

Players set up for the game by selecting one of four factions (The Gryphon Watch, The Phoenix Menage, The Dragon Synod or They Kitsune Conclave) and taking all of the related tokens, seals, and player mat. Next the cross-roads tile is placed in the middle of the play area in front of all players and becomes the game board. Five cards are then dealt to everyone and the remainder of the cards are set to the side with four more cards being drawn and laid face up for all to see.

A turn consists of four actions. Action one, is playing two cards from your hand off of the cross roads tile. You can place a card over a matching tile or over a tile it is superior too (Gryphons cover Phoenixes, Phoenixes cover Dragons, Dragons cover Kitsune, and Kitsune cover Gryohons similarly rock-paper-scissors). Action two, check to see if you have created a spirit guardian pattern that matches a pattern on your player mat. If you have, you place one of your faction tokens on any tile in that pattern thus claiming it. Action three, place your seal tokens on the board. This seal depending on which side you place face up will change a tile it is placed on to the face of the seal you placed on it. Lastly you replenish your hand by taking two more cards either from the cards that are face up or from the deck.

Each player takes their turn completing all four actions in clockwise order until there is a winner. The game is won when a player has placed all faction tokens on the game board.

Board game on the battle of Poltava using the Husaria system.

The battle took place on July 8, 1709 near Poltava in modern-day Ukraine between the Swedish army under the command of King Charles XII and the Russians led by Tsar Peter the Great. The Swedes attacked the Russian positions in four columns. Despite initial successes, they were later held back and their right-hand column was decimated. Soon afterwards, the Russian forces seized the initiative and went on the attack. The battle ended with the defeat of the Swedes, most of whom were either killed or taken prisoner.

A stand alone tabletop game of Elvish Duelling set upon the very cusp of a civil war. A war which will tear the Crystal Empire apart and see Crown Prince Mordred replace his mother the Empress Morgana upon the Peacock Throne. A war which, when it comes, will see Wylde Magicke usurped from its paramount place in Urop and the power of Black Powder take the Elves and then all of Valon by storm.

Tensions run high between the Imperial Elves and the new Garde followers of Mordred. The old ways are crumbling and power is up for grabs.

Duelling is a matter of personal honour and the ultimate test of skill. 
Will you prevail? 
Now take up your sword and assume a guarding stance!

—description from the publisher

Growing Grounds is a chill yet strategic roll & write, print & play game where you cultivate a mysterious plant on a forgotten island.

Using dice to grow stems, add leaves, and bloom flowers, you'll unlock bonuses, connect with insects, and complete different goals — all while telling your story of growth and discovery.

With simple mechanics, solo and multiplayer modes, and a relaxing theme, Growing Grounds offers a thoughtful, replayable experience.

—description from the designer

boardgame: CAPTCHA'D: Prove You're Not a Robot
description:
CAPTCHA'D: Prove You're Not a Robot - Cranium for the Artificial Intelligence Generation! A thematic party game with word game mechanics: Taboo, Pictionary, & Balderdash with an AI twist: the first boardgame to use ChatGPT. Use real AI and your wits to escape the five-level rotating boards.

THE PREMISE

You’re robots proving your sentience by escaping a Turing Test lab made of 5 rotating levels that can open or block paths each turn. Think creatively as four color-coded CAPTCHA’D rooms challenge you to give clues to a real AI: make AI give the correct answer before the other players by writing, drawing, & bluffing your way through. Careful: Special GLITCH cards allow players to alter the lab or reprogram abilities each turn. The first one to escape the lab will have proven they are truly more than a machine and wins!

FOUR FUN AI CHALLENGES!

GREEN: Guess which player used AI to answer the question on the card.

BLUE: Make AI guess the word on the card without using the banned words.

RED: Write the best prompt to make your AI image match the one on the card.

YELLOW: Make AI guess what’s in your drawing.

—description from the designer

A box consisting of micro expansions/promo cards for 5 games set in the Kinfire Universe:

Kinfire Chronicles: Night's Fall - 1 Silver-Diamond treasure pack (8 cards)
Kinfire Council - 1 Set of threat, research, and decree cards (9 cards)
Kinfire Delve: Vainglory's Grotto - 1 New Well Master card
Kinfire Delve: Scorn's Stockade - 1 New Well Master card
Kinfire Delve: Callous' Lab - 1 New Well Master card

FUGU! The Sushi Strategy Game is a strategy and resource-management game where players race to be the first to complete sushi recipes using ingredients like rice, soy sauce, avocado, and seaweed. The central mechanic revolves around collecting fish each turn either from the Bank (safe) or the Pond (risky), where players can draw multiple fish but risk losing everything if they draw the dreaded Fugu card.

Fish are spent to buy random resources, wildcards (which provide special powers like stealing, switching recipes, or selecting specific ingredients), or specific resources. Collecting and managing these resources lets players complete recipes and win. Drawing fish from the Pond can be a gamble, but the reward is faster progress and potential passive income if players save enough fish.

The Pro Game version adds complexity: players can complete others’ recipes to steal their fish, earn stars to win, and interact with upgraded rules for wildcards, resource discards, and passive income bonuses. With wild swings, sneaky plays, and shared information, FUGU balances strategic planning with push-your-luck tension, all wrapped in a whimsical sushi theme.

—description from the designer

The holiday season at the “Möwenruh” beach campsite is over. Faded garden gnomes watch over empty pitches and the smell of cold barbecue hangs in the air. But the permanent campers are hanging in there - with weather-beaten awnings and smouldering neighborhood disputes, they are defying the dreary autumn.
Everyone knows everyone here. And everyone knows Horst Hartmann, the uncrowned king of the site. He knows everything, sees everything and has an opinion on everything. But today the top dog has fallen silent – forever.

Deadly Dinner – Mord im Vorzelt is a classical murder mystery game: Enjoy a dinner (or just some snacks) with your family and/or friends, while all of you take on the role of a murder suspect. One of you has committed the crime, but you all have secrets you would rather keep. Find out the truth without giving yourself away. Your goal is to solve the murder – or get away with the bloody deed.

A game of Deadly Dinner consists of three rounds, in which each player gets new secret information about the crime. Players must discuss their information and clues as a group in order to identify the perpetrator in the end. To prepare, each player should receive their character's starting information a few days before playing the game.

—description from the publisher

The young wizard apprentices are learning how to brew potions. But the ingredients are hidden in the dark cellar of the wizardry school. And the light goes out from time to time. So be fast and memorize what you can to find the ingredients even if you can't see them. A wonderful memo games not only for kids.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Rise of Nerath: A Mythralis Chronicle
description:
The Rise of Nerath: A Mythralis Chronicle is an epic fantasy board game that merges deep narrative exploration, strategic battles, and breathtaking miniatures into a cohesive, immersive experience.

Set in the ancient, magic-infused continent of Mythralis, players lead unique factions in a world where gods walk among mortals, ancient creatures rise, and the lines between power and faith blur. Every decision leaves a mark on the world, every battle shapes the dawn of a new era.

Multiple Game Modes

Narrative Campaign: Embark on a story-driven journey through interconnected quests where choices and victories impact your path, shaping your faction’s legacy across different scenarios.

Dungeon Crawling: Explore dungeons using modular double-sided tiles with traps, treasures, bosses, and evolving challenges in cooperative or solo play.

Divine Battle: Engage in fast-paced, competitive tactical battles on the main board, combining card-driven action, dice, and modular terrain.

Solo & Coop: Fully supported solo play and cooperative modes where players face the game’s AI and narrative challenges together.

Deep Customization & Progression

Manage your party of 3 characters per player, equipping weapons, artifacts, and relics that grant unique abilities and status effects during combat.

Advance along skill trees using Glory Points earned through quests, conquest, and building temples to your chosen deity, unlocking powerful abilities and special actions.

Summon creatures, build temples for your deity, and conquer strategic areas to expand your influence on the map.

Engaging Systems

Status Effects System: Inflict or suffer poison, burning, paralysis, and many other effects, tracked visually through custom tokens, adding depth and tension to each encounter.

Action Card Management: Each equipment grants specific action cards, enabling tailored combat strategies and deck optimization as your party evolves.

Dynamic Quest System: Experience branching paths, side quests, and evolving objectives that ensure each game session is unique.

Premium Miniatures & Art
The game features a collection of highly detailed miniatures representing heroes, creatures, and gods, bringing the world of Mythralis vividly to life on your table. Paired with modular tiles, custom tokens, and beautifully illustrated cards, each session becomes a visual and strategic spectacle.

Whether you seek deep narrative campaigns, tense dungeon-crawling challenges, or competitive skirmishes, The Rise of Nerath offers a complete gaming experience for 1–6 players, with rich replayability, strategic depth, and an evolving world where every game becomes your legend.

—description from the publisher

description: Courses & Clubs is a 1-4 player, narrative, golf Simulator/RPG (Role Playing Game) that takes place on a imaginary golf course. You can create your player or play as one of many legendary, fictional and real golfers!  In Courses & Clubs you are a golfer playing through a round of golf (18 holes) trying to achieve the lowest score possible!  You can also gain XP to improve your golfing skills!  Simple to play but detailed in it's percentages, Courses & Clubs brings golf to the tabletop unlike any before!

Dive into the hilarious and explosive world of Grizzy and the Lemmings!

Play as the Lemmings with your friends, to snatch food, right under Grizzy’s nose. Pick your Lemming, roll, and split the dice. Collect food, and enjoy the bonus spaces !
Avoid Grizzy : If he catches you, he takes back some of his food. If your pantry is full, help the other Lemmings, to win, all together, before the Ranger arrives !

Gameplay steps:

     Roll the dice and move: Distribute the dice and move your Lemming to the corresponding space to collect food.
     Gather food and enjoy bonuses: Bonus spaces let you play again or directly choose an item for your pantry.
     Evade Grizzy: The final die determines how far Grizzy moves, based on the card drawn. If he catches a Lemming, that Lemming loses some of its food.
     Win together: Once your pantry is full, help the other Lemmings so everyone wins before the Ranger arrives.


Join forces: Fill your pantries together before the Ranger spoils the fun. Remember, teamwork is the key to victory!

Plongez dans l’univers hilarant et explosif de Grizzy et les Lemmings !

Unissez vos forces : Remplissez vos garde-mangers ensemble avant que le Ranger ne vienne gâcher la fête. Attention, la coopération est la clé de la victoire !

Is your level already over 9000?
Is there a ghost in the machine? What impact has The X-Files had on our society? boardgame: Adventure in a Box: Sturmfels Akademie – Der Turm der Bettler
description:
Welcome to the Stormrock Academy!
Begin your training at the most famous training center in the Shardlands! In the fantastic world of Torsten Weitze's novels of the same name, you will face countless dangers to prove yourself and earn enough money to continue your training. Are you ready to enter the Tower of Beggars and prove your strength?
Take your fate into your own hands and experience a turbulent school year at Stormrock Academy, where you will have to face numerous dangers in order to collect enough coins for another year.

Willkommen an der Sturmfels-Akademie!
Beginne deine Ausbildung an der berühmtesten Lehrstätte der Splitterlande! In der fantastischen Welt der gleichnamigen Romane von Torsten Weitze stellst du dich unzähligen Gefahren, um dich zu beweisen und genug Geld zu verdienen, um deine Ausbildung fortzusetzen. Bist du bereit den Turm der Bettler zu betreten und deine Stärke zu beweisen?
Nimm dein Schicksal in die Hand und erlebe ein turbulentes Schuljahr an der Sturmfels-Akademie, in dem du dich zahlreichen Gefahren stellen musst, um genug Münzen für ein weiteres Jahr zu sammeln.

When the humans are asleep, the city belongs to the cats!
In the port city of Havena, not all cats are gray at night, but they wear boots, hats, shirts, weapons and have crazy adventures. Throw yourself into this turmoil and enter the competition for the most beautiful cat in town!
Help a cat in love win the favor of the most beautiful cat in town! Compete in a garbage fight, dance around and win the cat's heart. Only those who outdo their competitors and pay attention to what the cat loves can earn enough favor to help their ally win their great love.

Wenn die Menschen schlafen, gehört die Stadt den Katzen!
In der Hafenstadt Havena sind nachts nicht alle Katzen grau, sondern sie tragen Stiefel, Hüte, Hemden, Waffen und erleben verrückte Abenteuer. Stürze dich in diesen Tumult und stelle dich dem Wettstreit um die Gunst des schönsten Kater der Stadt!
Hilf einer verliebten Katze dabei, die Gunst des schönsten Katers der Stadt zu erlangen! Behaupte dich in einer Müllschlacht, tanze um die Wette und erobere so das Herz des Katers. boardgame: Knitting Circle: Special Requests Promo Cards
description: This mini-expansion includes 12 unique double-sided Request Cards! With the addition of these additional cards, players will have new ways to play that create new spatial challenges and add completely new gameplay elements!

Experience the thrill of the beautiful game with Roll-n-Go Soccer! This fast-paced board game puts you in control of your own soccer team, challenging you to outmaneuver your opponent and score 5 goals for victory. Using simple dice rolls, determine the outcome of every pass, tackle, and shot on goal, bringing the excitement of real-world soccer tactics right to your tabletop.

Easy to learn and quick to play, Roll-n-Go Soccer lets you dive straight into the action. Strategically position your 7 players and utilize your powerful Striker to create scoring opportunities. Will you build up with short, precise passes, or launch a long ball downfield? Defend your goal with your agile goalie and anticipate your opponent's moves to intercept crucial passes.

Roll the dice to execute pinpoint passes, challenge for possession, and unleash thrilling shots on goal. Feel the tension rise as the goalie attempts a crucial save! With elements like offsides, corner kicks, and even penalty shootouts, Roll-n-Go Soccer captures the key strategic decisions and unpredictable moments that make soccer so captivating. Gather your friends, choose your side, and get ready to Roll-n-Go your way to soccer glory!

description: With the new edition reducing the game from 5 rounds to 4 and adding two extra starting spectacles, we’ve created the perfect design space for a smooth and exciting 6-player experience.

Watch, flip, rotate… and race to complete the pattern before anyone else!

In Buzzle, all players compete to be the first to recreate the insect pattern shown on the central card. Sounds easy? Think again. You'll need to position your insects correctly, matching their orientation, sometimes flipping or rotating them—and even challenging your instincts with special rules and surprise effects.

When you think you’ve got it right, slap your hand on the central card and prove it! If you’re correct, you earn the card as a point and move one step closer to victory. If you’re wrong, you’ll have to wait while the others keep playing.

Some pattern cards hide tricky visual challenges or extra twists, so stay sharp, keep your eyes focused, and be the first to complete three patterns to win the game.

—description from the designer

Observa, gira, voltea… ¡y corre a completar el patrón antes que los demás!

En Buzzle, todos los jugadores compiten por ser los primeros en reproducir el patrón de insectos mostrado en la carta central. ¿Parece fácil? Piénsalo otra vez: tendrás que colocar tus insectos en la posición y orientación correctas, a veces girándolos, volteándolos o incluso desafiando tu intuición con reglas especiales y efectos sorpresa.

Cuando creas tenerlo todo listo, pon tu mano en la carta central y ¡demuestra que no te has equivocado! Si aciertas, ganas la carta como punto y te acercas un paso más a la victoria. Si fallas, tendrás que esperar mientras los demás siguen jugando.

Algunas cartas pueden ocultar trampas visuales o retos extra, así que mantén la cabeza fría, afina la vista y sé el primero en completar tres patrones para ganar la partida.

In Collies & Sheep, players take turns recruiting adorable dog breeds—each dressed as a playful collie—who will help them gather as many sheep as possible before the game ends.

All dogs help you gather sheep, but some have special abilities that can give you an advantage. Along the way, you’ll also play Action Cards to hinder other players: steal their sheep, block their moves, or protect your own flock from sneaky tricks.

The game also features a solo mode, where you face off against the odds of drawing Coyote Cards—dangerous predators that threaten to scatter your flock before you can win.

With simple rules, charming artwork, and quick turns, Collies & Sheep offers a cozy, interactive experience full of laughter, strategy, and a little bit of playful mischief.

Feel the thrill and enjoy the mystery from a escape game in your own home! As in the case in many Escape Rooms across the world, you are “locked in” in this game and the object is to find the codes to “escape” within 60 minutes by solving puzzles in riddles.

Secret Agent - Operation Zekestan
The new self-proclaimed country of Zekestan is holding the world in an iron grip. Their power display and threats about their secret weapon are downright frightening. The way in which the new president came to power appears to be only a foretaste of what his sick mind has planned!

An Egyptian-themed trick taking game made for the TTUTcon event held in Layton, Utah, in August 2025.

This 3-player, low-luck trick-taking game is played in just 18 total tricks. In Round I, player play a must-follow trick-taking game with their 9 cards. They either earn points (for a first-place finish in a trick), the ability to score second place in Round II (for second-place finishes), or the ability to change the rules of the game in Round II (for third-place finishes).

In Round II, first-place finishes get 2 points, second place finishes get points if they previously marked second place in that trick, and third place gets nothing.

Most points wins.

However beware they may have counter cards which deflect damage back to you. It's everyone for themselves. On your turn you can only choose to play a card or reload (draw a card). An abstracted regimental level game about the British (and Sepoy) battles against the Sikhs and their potential Afghan and Kashmiri allies in the Punjab.
Bi-monthly turns in the less scorching fall-winter-spring months.
Point to point operational map with optional hexagonal tactical battle maps, including Kangra Fort - never previously taken, even by Alexander the Great - which my GGG Granduncle Alexander Jack subdued by getting 18-pounder cannon pulled up by elephants into overlooking mountains - see the watercolor painting he had commissioned.
(Alexander and youngest brother Andrew just ill-timedly visiting from Australia would both die in the 1857 Sepoy Rebellion Cawnpore Massacre.  An English general was in command - they were Scots, the sons of William and Grace Jack of King's University, Aberdeen.)
Simple, standard combat odds battle resolution with differing Recoveries from Disruption, reflecting differing combat resiliencies. Infantry, cavalry, artillery pieces, including Sikh irregulars.
Either commander may opt for negotiated surrender to end the 1st Sikh War, as historically, but with losses somewhat cumulative.
Game ends as soon as Sikhs lose all major cities and Kangra Fort or British lose Delhi.

boardgame: Victory or Death: A Wargamers Guide to the American Revolution, 1775-1782
description:
This wargamer’s guide provides a comprehensive overview of the American War of Independence (or American Revolution), 1775–1782. The guide includes a series of maps showing the location of major battles by year with accompanying summaries of those battles and of the major campaigns of the war, and additionally it includes details of many lesser-known engagements. The book also provides information on, and organisations of not only American Continentals, French regular units and British regular units but also on American militia, the British Army’s German Auxiliaries, and the British provincial and loyalist units, and will thus enable wargamers to focus their efforts on specific campaigns or battles.

Orders of battle for selected campaigns and specific battles are provided. Uniform guides, including uniform colours and facings for each of the participants are included along with descriptions of the armies’ colours. The guide provides information on rules for the period and an overview of various miniatures available in a range of scales. Several tabletop wargaming scenarios, accompanied by maps, orders of battle and painting guides are also included in the book; additionally, information for several historical campaign scenarios are given, showing how campaigns can be used to add a new dimension to wargaming and to generate tabletop battles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Acting Out: A Game of Terrible Actors And Ridiculous Guesses
description:
Acting Out is a party game that takes the basic concept of charades and reimagines it with a competitive twist involving whiteboard guesses and bluffing. The game takes place in a casual setting where one player at a time becomes the actor, drawing a prompt card and attempting to act it out using only gestures—no words or noises. The other players serve as guessers, each writing down on whiteboards what they believe the prompt was, aiming to either guess correctly or create a plausible enough answer to mislead others.

Each round unfolds in two phases: performance and guessing. During the performance, the actor interprets a prompt, such as “using a lightsaber to trim your nose hairs” or "frying bacon on a boat in a hurricane". Guessers then write down their interpretations of the performance in secret. After all guesses are submitted to the performer, they are read aloud, including the real prompt, and players vote on which one they believe is correct. Points are awarded based on correct guesses and successful deception.

The game’s humor and challenge come from the intentionally absurd prompts and the limited communication method. Without spoken clues, players are forced to exaggerate or invent physical gestures to convey often complex or nonsensical scenarios. This creates an environment where even wrong answers can be convincing—or at least entertaining enough to be mistaken for the real thing.

Acting Out is structured but flexible, making it well-suited for informal play. The whiteboard guessing mechanic distinguishes it from traditional charades by rewarding both accurate interpretation and clever misdirection. While the gameplay is easy to grasp, the variety of prompts and the social dynamics between players keep it unpredictable.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Waking the Bear: A Guide to Wargaming the Great Northern War and Turkish Campaigns 1700-1721
description:
The Great Northern War (GNW) has seen a resurgence in recent years within the wargames community, however, there has not been a general (non-rules specific) guide to the conflict until know.

This book gives the reader a general understanding of the historical background to the conflict and to the main combatants. In addition to Russia and Sweden, the armies of Denmark, Hanover & Holstein, Ottoman Empire, Saxony, Poland, Prussia are covered, as well as the involvement of the Cossack nations.

The book looks at naval landings, siege warfare as well as the more traditional battles, with this focus on lesser known battles instead of the more traditional battles of Narva and Poltava. Battle reports primarily are focused on small evening games utilising a small number of units and therefore achievable by the majority of wargamers. Options are included to turn a number of these into larger multi-player games.

While the majority of the GNW occurred in and around the Baltic states, the book also covers events on Russia’s southern borders with the Ottoman Empire and there are gaming suggestions for the continuation of the conflict on Russia’s eastern border with China.

Utilising almost 100 unit and individual figure photographs from several renowned collections, the book contains a large amount of detail readily accessible to the reader, while incorporating the latest research into the period from internationally renowned academics, historians, and wargamers.

—description from the publisher

Turn up the volume of your Idol A Live experience with 3 brand new idols to manage! This expansion brings new items, events, fans, and, naturally, songs to increase the replayability of SUSABI GAMES' idol manager simulator.

A new mechanic, the [Energy Drink] item card, is implemented alongside one of the new idols. A new type of fan, the [Metal Fan] can also join the audience - they can only be recruited by one particular idol's team, making them a roadblock for the other player!

The wish you hold becomes your shining Stella!

Sherlock Holmes – BloodStorm is a cooperative dungeon crawler set in a dark, horror-infused world where Sherlock Holmes, Dr. Watson, and their allies investigate brutal murders and occult conspiracies. Players will explore modular maps, solve challenging puzzles, face horrifying creatures, and manage scarce resources in a campaign of 10 linked scenarios.

Using action point allocation, variable player powers, and environmental puzzles inspired by classic survival horror games, players will uncover secrets and battle cultists and alchemically-mutated horrors while trying to seal a dimensional breach threatening to engulf the world in a bloody storm.

Play solo or with up to 5 players, experiencing branching narratives and immersive exploration that will challenge your deduction skills and your nerves alike.

—description from the designer

Reports of a group of rebels gathering in the woods outside your city are causing quite the stir. They claim to be fighting for the poor, but let’s be real – who needs them when you’ve got your own benevolent rule? Besides, these rebels ruin your fashion image, with their penchant for green tights and capes. Maybe it’s time to send in the royal guard to deal with this little revolt. Oh, and then some breakfast.

This expansion for Kingdom Legacy offers 5 rounds of gameplay and 30 new cards. It is recommended to have plenty of sword production to take on this challenge, as many of the cards are enemies.

Purge 8 (focus on 2 wood production cards) and 2 permanent cards. The focus means you must choose 2 wood production cards until 2 such cards have been chosen, then the purge continues without focus.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Elder Space is a solo dark space exploration game where you play as an investigator sent to the most remote corners of the grimdark universe through spatio-temporal rifts.

Every turn you choose from three cards, seeing images only: one card to discard, one to put on the bottom of the deck, one to play. You make your choices, gathering points of Failure on your journey, which you will weigh against points of Success to see if your people will take you back home, or if they leave you far from your galaxy as an expendable, a failed experiment. As you learn to remember which image hides what danger or benefit, the game changes from exploration more into planning and strategizing.

The game consists of 90 tarot-sized cards, from which you take 60 random ones for each session. Pen and paper is also needed to track the character's progress, acquired statuses, and equipment.

The mechanics are a variation on those of Doom Pilgrim.

A game where Yatzy meets Bingo!
Do you have a knack for dice games like Yatzy? Do you love the thrill of calling out numbers in Bingo and claiming the perfect spot? Then you're in luck — Yingo blends the best of both classics into one exciting game of chance and strategy!
Carefully place your chips to rack up points while blocking your opponents from taking over the board — but don’t forget to trust the dice and roll your way to victory! Smart positioning meets a bit of luck in this fast-paced, interactive showdown.

—description from the publisher

We've officially made contact with an extraterrestrial civilisation!
However, the peaceful talks are interrupted when Doctor Moritz steals a gift meant for the aliens. To avoid a major scandal, Spy Guy - an intergalactic detective of great fame - sets off in pursuit of the devious doctor. Equipped with a modern device capable of producing sound even in the vacuum of space, Spy Guy is determined to track down the villain!
Spy Guy is a cooperative game that requires a keen eye and close teamwork from players. Search for clues across multiple cosmic boards. The more clues you find, the faster you will catch up with Doctor Moritz and help Spy Guy reclaim the stolen gift.

—description from the publisher

The theme of the game centers around "earthlings" depicted on illustrated cards, each with different characters and colored backgrounds. Players race to identify and grab the correct earthlings based on clues presented on task cards.

Each round, a task card is revealed that describes certain types of earthlings (like "Cat" or "Farmer") and indicates what color they must be. All players simultaneously try to find the correct matching earthling cards among a spread and cover them with their hands. The first player to fully cover the correct card claims it. If a player covers the wrong card, they must return one of their previously collected cards.

Some task cards are special "Piu-piu" challenges, where players must simultaneously cover two matching cards from a specific category (like "Pets" or "People") with both hands while shouting "Piu-piu!" Doing this correctly allows them to take both cards and forces other players to lose one. The game ends when all task cards have been used, and the winner is the player who has collected the most earthling cards.

MultiMorbid is a competitive board game in the strategy and territory conquest niche. It takes place inside the body of a genderless adult individual called the Host. Their lifestyle, which is considerably negligent toward their physical integrity and often irresponsible when exposed to risky situations and false information, has resulted in them becoming infected with several diseases simultaneously. These diseases are represented by players, ranging from three to six at a time. Each player's objective is to survive within the body, fighting among themselves for the space they need to multiply and fulfill their natural purpose: to dominate the organism and earn the honor of killing the Host before any other disease!

Eternal Legacy Soccer is a moderately complex semi-statistical replay gaming on Soccer. In each set you find a different number of team data card (one for each team), with team data for the replay and individual team rosters (with 20-25 players listed). The game permits to replay an entire game through its highlights that you will resolve individually (so, around 10-15 actions for half of the game). There is a basic rulebook and also an advanced one (suggested) that adds a lot of things, like injuries, fatigue, individual scorers and assistmen, random events and weather effects.

As the name implies, most of the sets will have a sort of "fictional" best of for each team for the period covered by the set. First sets released are Scotland Premiership 70s, World Cup 30s, Mitropa Cup 1927/40 and European Championship 1960/1980

Welcome to Waters Edge: Northern Lights! This new edition plays in a similar way to Waters Edge but with northerly twists! It offers a brand new deck of cards full of beautiful new artwork and a whole snowy environment to tackle Waters Edge challenges in.

In addition to building your town, you will also build fjords which offer a new way to bring fish to your northern people. Ravens swoop past, ice caves offer a place for fermenting your catch while nordic shell houses provide a safe retreat from those roaming yetis searching for their own fishy treats...

Players will now also fish at night under the lights of the Aurora Borealis (northern lights) but only when you've paid your fishy dues to the gods who unlock powers in return. Only four of the eight powers will be up for grabs during any game which keeps it interesting each time you return to play, especially when the cards only reveal their power on selection!

For those who haven't played the original game, Waters Edge: Northern Lights will follow similar game dynamics in that it is a tactical card game for 2-4 players. Games last between 20 - 45 minutes and are suitable for those aged 10 upwards (or 6+ with a little more support!). The objective of the game is to gain as many points as possible in the timescale available. Points are gained by catching fish and expanding the town.

Waters Edge: Northern Lights is a standalone game in the Waters Edge series!

—description from the designer

In this one-sheet print-and-play game, your mission is to travel through space, planet by planet, and make it home.
The game is extremely short-setup included, it takes about a minute to play. But beware: failure can strike at any second.

The Cosmocadence - the rhythm of the universe - guides your journey. Its pace is set by the players: fast for a quick, intense challenge, or slow for a more forgiving ride.

Each player, in turn, must clap in rhythm to move forward to the next planet. Stay in sync, or risk being lost in space.

boardgame: Murder Mystery at the Movie Premiere
description:
Host your own murder mystery Evening

Deadly deeds are taking place at an A-list Movie premiere in the Hollywood Hills. Can you and your guests work out whodunnit? You'll find all you need to throw a star-studded mystery dinner party from the comfort of your own home ... 3 alternative endings for multiple game nights!

This expansion for Mycelia brings in new game mechanics to elevate the strategic depth of the original game as well as the option for a fifth player and 35 new mushroom cards depicting fungi endemic to North America.

Inspired by the symbiotic relationship between trees and fungi in nature, the new mechanics incorporate saplings and “Grandfather” Trees. As part of your SPORE action you can choose to invest one of your spores into a sapling and in return you are given a chance for a symbiosis bonus. Once three spores have been invested into a sapling it is eligible to ‘AGE’ (a new action added in this expansion) into a Grandfather tree. These can be used tactically to syphon spores into new locations and are also worth 5 points each.

—description from the publisher

Colossus is an abstract strategy game of shifting gravity and positioning.

Enclose your opponent’s stones to eliminate them. But beware: stepping on a direction field awakens the Hand of the Colossus, tilting the board and sending all pieces sliding.

Master movement, control the chaos, and outlast your rival before you're reduced to fewer than four stones.

—description from the designer

4 card mini expansion with Polish National Football team players, added exclusively to the Polish/Ukrainian pre-order of the game. 
Includes 3 Star players and 1 Goalkeeper card.

Like its owner, mystery writer Rosemary Clark, Winterwood Mansion is known for being the source of numerous enigmas.  Filled with hidden passageways, secret entrances, and lots of quirky design choices with no apparent purpose, Winterwood Mansion was built in the 1920s by railroad mogul and amateur detective, Clyde Winterwood. Although Rosemary Clark has lived in the house for more than thirty years, she is certain there are still things about the house she hasn't discovered yet.  Nothing drives that point home more than the hidden room she discovers behind one wall of the cellar.  Even more shocking is the long-dead body she discovers there belonging to a local reporter who went missing twenty years ago.  Rosemary Clark is known for many things, her razor-sharp memory being one of them, and she doesn't remember ever meeting this woman nor inviting her to Winterwood.  How did she get here?  Who killed her?  The answers may be found in a notebook tucked into one of the woman's pockets.  On it is a list of 7 names, each of which the reporter seems to be investigating.  Rosemary knows every name on the list and immediately hatches a plan to solve this mystery.  It's time to throw a dinner party like the one thrown in 2019, and the guests this time will be the names on that dead woman's list.  It's another dark and stormy night, and it's time to catch a killer who thought they had gotten away with murder.

Difficulty Rating - 7/10

—description from the publisher

QR Code reader and internet access required.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Rivals – Superman vs. Lex Luthor
description:
Superman and Lex Luthor clash over the Metropolis skyline. Build more powerful combos with your deck to be the first to take down your rival!

Standalone game and compatible with other games in DC Deck-Building Game series.

KEY FEATURES

Heroes, Villains, Equipment, Super Powers, and Locations pulled straight from Superman comics!
    Each time your Character is defeated, they gain more amazing abilities and become a greater challenge to defeat!
    Confrontation cards give you bonuses when you try to take down your opponent!
    Block cards make it harder for your opponent to defeat you

—description from the publisher

Potato Tomato is a party puzzle game for the whole family. The rules are simple: Use your nine playing cards to follow the given pattern as quickly as possible. You have to pay close attention to the correct arrangement so as not to mix up the colors and vegetables. If you're too slow, your fellow players will snatch the cards right out from under your nose. You win the round by placing all the cards correctly and then calling out "Potato Tomato." First player to win six rounds, wins the game.

Monkey Bingo is a simplified bingo game for children.

Players have a board with a 3x3 grid of animals on it.

The caller slides the monkey to push a animal tile out of the stacker, each tile has two animals on it.

If the player board contains an animal on the released tile, the player covers it with a token.

When the player's board is completely covered they call 'Monkey Bingo!' to win the game.

In a desolate future, the space passenger ship Electric Sheep crash-lands on the war-ravaged wasteland planet Replicant. Among the survivors is the very person responsible for the crash-secretly hoarding the only working communicator. Food is scarce, trust is thinner. Will you work together to endure and unmask the traitor, or succumb to paranoia and starvation?

WHO'S. FOODS. FOO! is a hidden role survival game where players navigate suspicion and scarcity. Each round includes five phases: Exploration, Sharing, Suspicion, Distribution, and Consumption. One or more players are secretly saboteurs, while the rest play unique survivor roles like the Glutton or the Hot-Blooded. Survival requires negotiating food distribution and uncovering deception, all while maintaining enough resources to stay alive. The traitor wins by surviving longer than all other players.

Inspired by the elegance of prime numbers and mathematical logic, Primzahl turns the beauty of numbers into a tactical duel. Players compete with colorful cards in search of ideal combinations - such as assembling a hand with all cards of the same color while balancing the tension between playing strong cards and preserving valuable combinations.

Primzahl is a ladder climbing game with a hand-building objective. Players take turns playing cards in ascending order according to specific patterns (like singles or pairs), but the goal isn't to empty your hand - it's to form a secret target pattern, such as all cards of the same color or a specific numerical sequence. The gameplay balances tactical timing and bluffing, as every card you play might sabotage your own progress.

Disney Magic Meets Classic Fun: TABOO: Disney is the perfect blend of Disney magic and the classic party game you love. Gather your friends and family for a lively, laughter-filled competition as you race against the timer to describe Disney-related words without using the "taboo" clues.

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes 2nd Battle of Tiamat
description:
The 2nd Battle of Tiamat is a scenario for use with the Tsukuda Legend of the Galactic Heroes game system.

This is a doujin dealing with the 2nd Battle of Tiamat, one of the largest and most decisive battles of the LOTGH universe. This pitted the Empire forces, which also included the father of Gregor von Muckenberger (from the main series), against the Alliance "730 Mafia" headed by Bruce Ashbey (who would be killed). It was a battle that was shown in the later Geiden series of episodes (episodes 111+).

This is a doujin scenario that needs to be assembled. When assembled, the game includes 197 counters, and several pages of rules.  To play, you will need at least one of the main games from the Tsukuda series: Lippstadt, Iserlohn, Lost Complete Victory, or Vermilion.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description: Guess the Fake is an all inclusive interactive family quiz game originating in France for 2 - 5 players. There are 1,000 preloaded questions, asked by a built in host, where you answer which ones are fact or fake? You'll win one point for each correct answer you make, but lose two for an incorrect answer! Plus, each round has its own rules and little unique surprises! This clever electronic game keeps track of all players answers and the scores. No need for any question cards, paper or scoreboard.

The Across The Board Wooden Tabletop Baseball Game is a classic game that can be played with family and friends. To play, move the first batter to home plate and roll the dice. Then, locate the dice combination on one of the two outfield billboards and move your players accordingly.

This is like regular baseball except less physicality.  As you roll your dice, you look at the faces and proceed on.  These are listed on the board for easy reference.  Proceed with your batters until you have 3 outs against you, then it is your opponents turn to roll the dice, just like in regular baseball.  The winner is the one with the most points.

Based on the popular UK-TV quiz show, LINGO is the addictive, simple to play word game for the whole family. With only the first letter given, players take turns to solve a series of mystery words (LINGOs) against the clock, by writing their answers on the large wipe-clean game unit. Each letter guessed correctly is highlighted as follows – green if the letter is in the correct position, yellow if it is part of the LINGO, but in the wrong position or red if the LINGO doesn’t contain that letter at all. Solve a LINGO correctly and bank the money and try to solve the much longer end of round Puzzlewords but run out of time and your opponents can steal. How quickly can you LINGO when the pressure is on?

Outlaw Poker is a fast-paced card game that brings the excitement of the Wild West to your table! With simple rules and engaging gameplay, it's perfect for game nights with friends or family.

The aim of Outlaw Poker is to score fewer points than your fellow outlaws by the end of the game. Lower your score by pairing up cards and discarding high-value ones.

Special 'Outlaw Cards' that provide players with special abilities throughout the game, adding just the right amount of chaos, alliances, and rivalries.

—description from the designer

The 2025 ELL deck game is Bankabell.

A 1-2 player word game about using the trickier letters, buying cards, banking cards, and trying to make sets of numbers.

It'll be publicly available on 1st August.

To start the game, make a market of cards, draft 5 out of 10 specific cards, then each turn:

MAKE A NEW WORD
CHECK CARD LIMIT
BANK 
BUY NEW CARDS

Finally, in a solo game you discard 2 from the market. In a 2p game, it's now the other person's turn.

The end-game is triggered when the market runs out.

Score 10pts for each full set of numbers (1,2,3,4,5), 5pts for each 'partial set' of 4 different numbers, and 1pt for each other card.

—description from the designer

The eternal cycle, from Sun to plant, from plant to prey, from prey to predator, and so on, as the cycle continues eternally.

The goal of the game is to create large sets of cards to score points and win the game when the deck runs dry, however you may also gamble to win the game regardless of score, or lose if you can't get what you need to win the gamble.

Each turn you may take one of three actions, draw and discard, take and give, or gamble. At anytime during your turn you may make exactly one set of cards from those in your hand, that either being a straight of cards in numerical order or a match of cards with each number being the same.

—description from the designer

GALIMAH

/ɡɑːˈliːmɑː/

n. (process) Surviving the apocalypse by becoming a god through overcoming the inner conflict between good and evil.

This is a game about survival in the end times.
Convert followers, perform sacred deeds, erect holy sites and walk the razor’s edge between good and evil to ascend as a new god, thus saving yourself and your followers.

Players take turns in sequence, performing 2 actions each turn.
Every major move counts as an action: playing a card, gaining resources, drawing a card, converting a follower, activating a card’s ability, evolving yourself, or claiming the favor of a god.

Most actions exhaust the cards or abilities used, creating natural pauses. Players can spend both actions on a "power nap" to remove 1 exhaustion from each of their cards.

The first player to gain the favor of at least 4 gods ascends to godhood and wins.

Throughout the game, players shape their path as righteous, corrupt, or balanced through their actions. Each time they gain Karma, they advance on the corresponding Karma track and draw a Karma card, triggering an immediate, unpredictable reward or penalty that reflects their chosen path.

—description from the designer

In Evil Genie, players combine Good Cards and Evil Cards to create dilemmas and guess the Wisher's choice to score points. The Wisher must decide whether to accept the twisted wish, while others predict their decision. Correct guesses earn points, making each round a test of intuition and persuasion.

As happens every Christmas, Santa Claus is running short on little helpers to prepare the presents. And who better to turn into the perfect elf than a goblin stripped of its wickedness? So, in the hope that someone will fall for the trap, Santa sends gifts to the goblin village — including one containing the feared Absorb Malice. Goblins, being curious and mischievous creatures, love opening gifts… but they know danger could be lurking inside. Whoever opens the Absorb Malice loses their wicked essence and risks turning into an elf. Any goblin who becomes a "half-elf" has until the final Event to make things right: by collecting the right cursed gifts, they can brew the Enema of Malice and steal wickedness back from another goblin. At the end of the game, any goblin who hasn't reclaimed their wickedness will be shipped off straight to Santa's workshop… let's hope they enjoy singing Christmas carols for eternity.

Goblins Hate Xmas! It’s a card-game. Each turn, the first player draws an EVENT card, then everyone draws SABOTAGE cards until they have three. Players use TRAP cards to give face-down GIFTS to opponents, ACTION cards to steal, destroy, or move cards, and INSTANT cards for immediate effects. You can defend with ACTION or INSTANT cards even when it's not your turn. After sabotage, the EVENT card is revealed and resolved, then all gifts are opened. Normal goblins discard opened gifts, but half-elves can keep the gifts needed to perform the Enema of Malice.

If you find ABSORB MALICE in a gift, you become a half-elf. Half-elves must collect the right cursed gifts to brew the Enema of Malice - a ritual that lets them steal wickedness back from another goblin, transforming back into a goblin while turning their target into a half-elf.

To Win:  Be a full goblin when all EVENT cards are exhausted (midnight strikes). To Lose:  Remain a half-elf at game's end and get shipped to Santa's workshop. The tension:  normal goblins avoid gifts, but half-elves desperately need them for redemption!

—description from the designer

The coastal towns of the Inner Sea are loaded with valuable diamonds and you and your crew are out to take advantage of the current supply. Competition among couriers, however, has resulted in increased illegal transportation of the rare jewels and rumors of smuggling have caused the Capital City to dispatch its navy ships in response. Now you must navigate the waters more carefully in your pursuit of fortune.

Players use their allotted number of actions to gather four different types of diamonds while also avoiding pesky navy ships. Available actions include sailing around the board, battling other players, commandeering new vessels, smuggling diamonds, equipping weapons, and completing quests. Diamonds are gained by rolling dice and are kept hidden from other players until any player accumulates seven of one type, which immediately triggers the game end.

End-game scoring is determined using highest-lowest scoring, which means a player's lowest diamond-type total is the most important. Don’t forget to keep track of your competitors’ cache or the game may end before you are ready.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Battle of Amritsar
description:
This game simulates the Battle of Amristar.  In 796 UC, the Alliance invaded Empire territory with a fleet of over 100,000 ships.  Reinhard decided to pull back and take all available supplies with him, forcing the Alliance to give up its supplies to the liberated colonies.  However, this backfired as supplies became scarce.  This forced the Alliance to retreat, and Reinhard counterattacked.  After several engagements, the Allied forces regrouped for a last stand at Amritsar.

This is a Japanese Doujin (fan-made) game that was available only at game shows in Japan.  It was not made for the general public. You would place your order in advance and then pick it up at the show. Because of this, many of these types of games are hard to get.

This game takes place in the LOTGH Universe.  The game is based on Game Journal's "All Quiet on the Western Front".

The game comes with rules, card manifest, a counter sheet, and a map.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Nature Expedition is a series of card games for children and families. Each box contains 50 cards with detailed illustrations of each animal, plus a complete profile, fun facts, and quizzes to test everyone's knowledge. A true find for the curious! Each player takes a turn using their cards to question their opponent as if it were a trivia game. The winner is the person who answers correctly the most times.

Investigate dinosaur remains and learn all about them! This is a true find for those who are passionate about these magnificent creatures!

Expedição da Natureza é uma série de card games para crianças e famílias e cada caixa contém 50 cartas com ilustrações detalhadas de cada animal, além de um perfil completo, curiosidades divertidas e quizzes para testar o conhecimento de todos. Um verdadeiro achado para os curiosos de plantão! Cada um na sua vez usa as cartas para questionar seu oponente como se fosse uma trivia. Vence quem responder corretamente mais vezes.

Investigue os vestígios dos dinossauros e aprenda tudo sobre eles! Este é um verdadeiro achado para aqueles que são apaixonados por essas criaturas magníficas!

Each box comes with 50 cards featuring detailed illustrations of each tree, plus a complete tree profile, fun facts, and quizzes to test everyone's knowledge! Each player takes a turn using the cards to quiz their opponent as if it were a trivia game. The winner is the person who answers the most correctly.

This is the ideal companion for all tree lovers! And also for those who want to brush up on their knowledge or teach younger ones.

Cada caixa vem com 50 cartas com ilustrações detalhadas de cada árvore, além de um perfil completo das árvores, curiosidades divertidas e quizzes para testar o conhecimento de todos! Cada um na sua vez usa as cartas para questionar seu oponente como se fosse uma trivia. Vence quem responder corretamente mais vezes.

Esse é o companheiro ideal para todos que amam árvores! E também para aqueles que querem estar com os conhecimentos em dia ou ensinar os mais novos.

The rules are designed for 15 mm (1:100 scale) scale models but there is no reason they won't work at other scales as well.

The rules cover almost all aspects of WW2 land combat including infantry, guns, artillery, armour, recce, beach landings, defences, a wide range of terrain types, destruction of buildings, air support and much more.

These comprehensive rules have simple mechanisms that will allow you to getting playing quickly and steadily realise the potential strategic and tactical options presented to you for game play.

Included with the rules is:

     The rules
     A quick reference sheet
     Points values for a wide range of WW2 weapon systems (British, German, Japanese, Italian, Soviet and USA)
     Stats information for those weapon systems needed to play the game
     A small getting started scenario to help you learn the game
     BPM standard games for casual club games or competition style games
     A paper copy of the markers needed to play the game


The standard game system includes 42 different game scenarios (6 standard game types and 7 standard scenarios giving 42 possible combinations).

Ideal for competitions games, scenario games, historical re-fights, small and large games.

Army lists and historical scenarios are available separately.  In this initial release of the rules a set of 5 historical scenarios are available and Generic army lists for the end war period (September 1943 onwards).

More army lists, Historical scenarios and Weapons systems for other nations are planned for the future.

Plastic printed markers for the game to improve your game experience are also available.

—description from the publisher

Ark Nova: 3Dition is an official big box accessory expansion for Ark Nova that allows you to replace the cardboard tiles and buildings with 3D plastic components. This set includes:


    240 colored 3D building pieces
    71 acrylic tokens
    82 metal coins including x-tokens
    4 deluxe slide-in player boards
    40 action-card tiles
    2 big boxes to store everything including the Ark Nova base game and the Marine Worlds expansion components


In terms of new game material, Ark Nova: 3Dition contains printed versions of the altered mapboards originally playable only on Board Game Arena, as well as a physical version of the "Arcade" building card, along with a 3D building tile.

boardgame: Death Card: Scenarios for Vietnam Wargames
description:
Death Card Scenarios provides 20 tabletop game scenarios for Death Card, a set of Vietnam wargaming rules that uses playing cards instead of dice. 
These scenarios can also be used with other Vietnam War rules in various figure scales.

The scenarios are set from the years 1963 to 1978 and involve jungle, rural and urban settings, with the protagonists including units of the armed forces of South Vietnam, the VC and NVA, US, Australia and New Zealand. The scenarios provided encompass guerrilla, infantry and armoured operations, an airborne assault, and air strikes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tin Cans & Battlewagons: One Page World War II Naval Battles
description:
General Quarters! 
 This is a game of World War II naval combat.

To play you will need miniatures for battleships, cruisers and destroyers; a hexagon grid mat; and dice (D4, D6, D8, D10, D12). Each hex is 5 NM and each turn is about 20 minutes.

These rules are designed for quick play and fit on one page. While the rules are streamlined, extensive research and balancing has gone into the vital statistics for warships which provides insights into the tradeoffs made by various navies in the WW2 era. They assume some knowledge about World War 2 abbreviations (such as "USN" for United States Navy, or "BB" for Battleship).

This particular volume focuses on surface engagements.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 'Orrible Little Men: Large-Skirmish Wargaming through the Ages
description:
'Orrible Little Men is a set of large-skirmish wargame rules that allow the players to field armies from various periods of history, as well as fantasy armies.

The rules are token-light and come with a series of helpful pages at the back of the book including a quick reference sheet, a tactical guide for new players and a complete list of units.

The core rules are 15 pages long, with the rest of the document dedicated to the thematic historical and fantasy settings, the 20 scenarios, running campaigns and solo play.

Description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Stellar Revenant: A Pocket Scifi Tale
description: Stellar Revenant is a Sci-fi adventure book structured as a branching narrative made by the creator of What Lies Beneath and Pocket Scifi: A Cyborg Adventure. The game is a single-player experience. The gameplay uses playing cards to resolve encounters. The player's character gains new abilities by obtaining items. The gameplay will also feature skill checks. There are story elements within the game that are inspired by anime like Cowboy Bebop and Final Fantasy 7. The game features anime-style artwork. Finally, the game is a spiritual sequel to Pocket Scifi: A Cyborg Adventure.

Welcome to SLAM-15.

A set of science fiction miniatures rules optimised for 15mm figures, though usable in other scales as well.

Features include:

     Individually based figures operating in squads (the most common way 15mm players organise their troops.
     Easy to use multiple vehicles on the table.
     Rules are designed to reduce fiddly features and allow you to easily command 5+ units on each side. 
     Alternating activations (with bonus activations from commanders), suppression and reaction fire.
     Points system with a number of unit profiles and weapons (and more to come after the alpha test is concluded).
     Ideally suited for pick up play at a store, club or at the kitchen table.


The rules are set up to have a tactical feel, in keeping with the style of most 15mm figures on the market (tending towards hard scifi with some space opera).

It is my hope that these rules will help give the 15mm scifi community a rallying point and that over time, the rules can be supported by "ready to use" stats for popular figure ranges in those scales.

This release is an Alpha version, allowing players to get a look at the rules and try them out at their table. Feedback will be incorporated into the full game. Anyone buying the alpha version will receive a discount off the full version so you are not risking anything.

Note that these rules are aimed at larger figure counts and pick up games with a semi-hard scifi vibe. Players who prefer a more hero driven space fantasy vibe should pick up Mystic Space instead. Players with an interest in more GM driven scenario play should consider Five Parsecs Tactics.

Description from the designer

Theme
Set in a not-quite-dystopian future of space colonization, The Solar Republic imagines humanity expanding across the Solar System. Players assume the roles of ambitious pioneers and settlers competing to claim territory on planets and moons. The richly illustrated cards feature planetary and lunar suits, evoking astronomy and sci-fi lore.

Gameplay
The game blends familiar trick-taking mechanics—similar to Euchre and Spades—with unique space-themed elements. Using a 52-card deck (five suits plus two wild cards), players play tricks to win rounds. Cards are ranked by celestial importance: the Sun and Asteroid Belt as highest, followed by large planets, small planets, dwarf planets, and moons 
thesolarrepublic.com.

Goal
Players aim to "Demonstrate your Prowess, Explore the Unknown, Conquer the Solar System" by winning the most rounds/tricks. Each trick represents a strategic claim on territory, and by collecting them, players build their dominion in the Solar Republic.

This game offers a fast-paced, strategic experience for 2 or 4 players, with enough thematic depth and familiar mechanics to appeal to both trick-taking fans and space enthusiasts.

Howdy there, Deadly Stranger...

Bantam West (2nd Edition) is a competitive open-world roleplaying game for 2 to 4 players. Step into the boots of our Deadly Strangers, immortal outlaws, as you explore the Western town of Gallow Springs and the surrounding Wilderness, Wetlands, Mountains, and Plains. Live life however you please as you work, fight, build, trade, and burn to become the Most Notorious!

You’ll harvest resources to craft powerful goods, acquire deadly weapons and gear, level your character’s skills and endeavors, navigate the terrain’s hazards, build cabins and recruit a posse to assert your influence. Watch for events and encounters across the terrain that might bring riches–or ruin. In the end, only one Deadly Stranger can come out on top!

2nd Edition walks back into the saloon with a fresh shave, polished iron, and a brand new pair of boots. Since the release of 1st Edition in 2023, our team has scoured the internet to gather and analyze input from all players we could possibly find. Building on this foundation, Ike and the team refined the game into its most accessible, exciting, and replayable form yet!

Updates include:


     Rewritten rulebook (with professional Co-Author)
     UI/UX Overhaul
     Combat System Overhaul
     To-Do List Overhaul
     Event System Overhaul
     4 Additional Endeavors
     More Variation in the Characters
     12 New “Classic” Cowboy Guns


…and much more!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Res Arcana: Mystical Menagerie Promo Place of Power
description:
This Place of Power was released as print and play file for Res Arcana in multiple languages. With one change, it was later included in "Res Arcana: Perlae Imperii" expansion.

Mystical Menagerie
Place of Power
Cost: 4 Calm (blue) and 4 Life (green) essences
Ability: Creature artifacts cost you one fewer essence (not Gold)
Powers:

     turn Creature + xCalm + at least 1 card in your deck -> draw 1 card
     x3Calm -> 1 Calm on this


VPs: 1 per Creature + 1 per Calm on this

CHANGE (Perlae Imperii), 2nd power: x7Calm -> 2 Calm on this
Two small roll-and-write games for mental arithmetic with plus and minus in the small number range. Both games are great to play alone and just as good with others in a competition.

SUSHI OMAKASE: A Fast-Paced Sushi Card Game
Welcome to Sushi Omakase Chef! Compete against fellow chefs to create the most delicious sushi dishes. Strategize, steal ingredients, and defend your recipes to become the ultimate Sushi Omakase Master Chef!??

Why You'll Love It!

     Easy to Learn, Fun to Master – Simple rules with strategic depth!
     Fast-Paced & Interactive – Engage in exciting card game with friends.
     Perfect for Parties & Game Nights – Play anytime, anywhere!
     Vibrant & Minimalist Design – High-contrast artwork and clean visuals.


Objective
To complete as many sushi recipes as possible. The chef with the highest score becomes the Sushi Master! So get rolling, strategize wisely, and serve up the best sushi before your rivals beat you to it!

—description from the designer

Space Missions: Operation Paperclip is an expansion that adds even more excitement to the thrilling space race game Space Missions.

Each player can adapt their team of specialists to the current requirements and continuously improve them from round to round.

You are no longer just competing for the missions, but also for the experts.

—description from the publisher

UNO inspired by characters from the Marvel comic book series The Amazing Spider-Man.

A special "Wild Web Swing" wild card lets you skip any player with a card of the chose color.

The new 2025 edition offers two fast-paced dice race games with 10 dice in 10 different colors. Quickly place the correct dice for the options on your challenge cards so that you are the first to create a chain of 10 to 12 dice.
The 2 games in the box involve very different levels:

     The Colours Game is all about logic and speed
     The Stages Game offers even more challenges: You also have to do mental arithmetic and be very good at tactics.


Both games feature traps for you in the form of Chicanes on the challenge cards, so it is very likely that you are making mistakes that will cost you points.

This new edition of 2025 also includes completely new challenge cards.

—description from the publisher

In this game for couples, the players move across the game board after rolling the dice. Depending on the symbol on the space, the corresponding card is read aloud. These cards contain questions or tasks.
The first player to reach the finish space wins two destination cards. The second player receives a destination card if they have collected more than 25 cards.

Players try to land their bumblebee on flowers, depending on the color shown on the dice. If a player successfully lands their bumblebee, they turn the next card of their stack and complete the task shown in the next round. Then the task card is completed.

The player who first reveals all task cards in their stack and then lands their bumblebee on a flower wins.

Players develop a city worth living in following 8 steps and 4 phases. They form buildings with modelling clay, and explain their city concept to the game master.

Each player has five points to distribute to other players. They assess the planning concept of the other cities in terms of sustainability and positive influence on the future.

The player with the most points wins. As this game is a training game, all results will be discussed and reflected in the group.

The Hillview map pack is the second map for Scribble City, and introduces the following:


     Use bus stops to create bus routes! Move your bus around your city to sightsee buildings and features.
     Hills make building placements trickier, but the fantastic view makes them prime real estate!
     Score plans to build unique Hillview Landmarks! Be the first to get better rewards!
     Triggered rewards are a new reward type that can be stored to be used at the most beneficial moment!


To play Scribble City, you need three things: A city deck, a map pack, and colored pencils.

You can mix and match city decks and map packs for different gameplay experiences. Each city deck and map pack comes with their own unique mechanics and features, offering a variety of ways to play.

—description from the publisher

Discover the unique speciality of the city of Mannheim (called ‘MONNEM’ by the inhabitants). Instead of street names, there are squares in the city centre that are named with letters and numbers. During the game, you occupy these squares until you are the first to occupy three neighbouring squares with your tokens.

—description from the designer

In this world-first folding quiz, two teams use the FaltoMeter to make estimates on quiz questions. The game is played for winning points.
Don't worry: you don't have to know the exact answers to the quiz questions, just make good guesses and score.

Monchhichi attracts the kids to come to the beach! Every child has a sand castle in his / her colour. 
The kids take turns rolling the dice and then move their Monchhichi token accordingly. Only if the dice shows 1, the child puts a sea urchin to his / her castle, and if the dice shows 6, the child takes a starfish to his / her castle. When the token is on a field of the same colour as the sand castle of the kid, the kid gets 2 mussels. When the token is placed on a field of a different colour, the child puts one mussel on this field.
If there are no more mussels on the island, the child takes the mussels from the coloured fields instead of putting them there from the island. 
The game ends, when all starfishes and sea urchins are in the sand castles. The kids are calculating their victory points: Mussel: 1 point, starfish: 2 points, sea urchin: -1 point.
The players place their pawns on "1". Each player casts the dice, the first one to reach the cheese on "56" is the winner.

boardgame: Unten am Fluss: Die Hasen sind los!
description:
Every player tries to collect as much carrots as possible. The kids therefore take turns in rolling the dice and moving their little rabbits accordingly.
If the rabbit ends on a carrot field, the child takes a card and compares the number of carrots on the card and on the field: If both are the same, the child gets the card. 
The game ends, when a player crosses the finish line for the second time. The kids are then calculating the number of carrots they collected.

Who is this game for?
Do you devour detective novels in one sitting, binge crime series, explore escape rooms, or always guess the killer before anyone else?
Then it’s time to finally step into the role of the detective yourself!

Masters of Mystery is a game for all of you who enjoy intricate plots, hidden motives, and that thrilling moment when everything finally clicks into place.

What’s included?
Inside the case file, you’ll find all the evidence you need to solve the mystery:


     Suspect statements and photographs
     Forensic reports
     Letters, notes, documents
     Newspaper clippings
     Receipts
     Crime scene map
     Hints (if needed)


A sealed wax envelope containing the solution

How does it work?
Masters of Mystery is a cooperative crime-solving game that offers fun, immersion, and the feeling of being a real detective.
The goal is simple: uncover who committed the crime.

The game begins with an introduction that sets the stage—time, place, and suspects.
Then, players work together to examine the evidence, take notes, connect the dots, and develop theories.
You can go through the materials in any order, and each new clue might reshape your understanding of the case! If you get stuck, there are hint envelopes carefully crafted to nudge you in the right direction.
