Finally, the group presents their conclusion: Who did it, how, and why?
Then you open the wax-sealed envelope to reveal the truth!

All you need is a folder full of clues—and a bit of detective instinct.

Build your train & fill its cars with passengers as it chugs through the hillsides. Pick up folks along the way & ferry them to safety. But beware, for zombies are lurking in the shadows. If they enter a train car on a stop, they can infect those around them. Doctors with antidotes can help, but only if you manage to find one.

More trouble can come in the form of crowded hordes, derailed train cars, & even a "King Zombie."

Guide your trains to the safety of the last station, where the player with the most survivors on board will win. However, a final hidden surprise awaits under each train car - one that could make or break your final mission.

This combat adventure utilizes a standard deck of playing cards.

—description from the designer

WINING:
It involves purchasing cards that represent CASTAFIORE’s sheet music, in order to build collections:
The ship's wheel and the captain's old rum; the professor TOURNESOL’s watch and umbrella; and the canes of DUPONT and DUPOND.
Form series to win prizes and, at the right moment, trade your cards for Tintins.

PREPARATION:
In the middle of the table, put the following:

     The deck of 104 cards, with the figure upside down
     A roulette, that you set the numbers of Tintins need to win the game ( you set it from 30 to 50), decided between players.
     One players in the Banker, that is responsible to to give cards to the players, and also in the begin give 15 Tintins to each player.


THE GAME:
The first player is the bank, each player take a turn rotating to the left. Each player turn need to draw a card from the main deck.

What happen if you draw:

     CASTAFIORE, CAPTIAN, TOURNESOL or DUPONT/DUPOND card - you purchase it, paying to to the bank the price and its yours, but the player is not obligated to buy it. Leave on the side of the deck face up and pass the turn.
     1 TINTIN card - The banker pays him the TINTIN, and the card is placed next to the deck face up.
     RECEIVE or PAY card - the players follow the card instructions and place the card next to the deck face up
     TINTIN or MILOU card, this card is free, works like a Joker, that can replace any card from the game. 


You can exchange, buy or sell cards between players in order to complete a series, and then exchange that series for Tintins to the bank.
If the deck run out of cards, you need to use the upside up cards that are next to the deck, shuffle and use them as the new deck.

boardgame: Band of Brothers: Design Your Own Scenario
description:
This manual outlines supplementary rules for the "Band of Brothers" wargame series, enabling players to create custom scenarios.

It details a five-step design process, including selecting game parameters like theater and date, defining the battlefield layout, choosing from asymmetric (Clear, Penetrate, Seize) or symmetric (Skirmish) encounter types, and establishing victory conditions often determined through a bidding system.

The rules also provide historical unit organizations for various nationalities and time periods to ensure realistic force composition, alongside examples and player aids to facilitate scenario creation.

boardgame: Boots on the Ground: A Game of Epic Ground Battles
description:
This is the fourth game in the series of seven that I am creating that are based in the world of Tibasolite.  
This game is designed for large land battles, and occasionally space battles, and it is suitable for either 1/288 scale models or 1/144 scale models.

This game will work in conjunction with the other games that I have produced:  Galactic Storm, Space Confrontation and Telton.

Included in this rule book are:-

     The rules for playing the basic game (which works in a similar way to Epic Space Marine, but the combat is grittier)
     Advanced rules that can be used once the players are familiar with the basic rules and game play.
     Additional skills that can be used for certain characters in the game.
     A list of Tibasolite Magic Spells.
     Conversion charts and points calculations if someone wants to create their own race for the game.


One thing that isn't included is the Army Lists for the 21 races that exist in the World of Tibasolite, but these will be produced over the course of this year, time permitting.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Conflict Rising: A Game of Skirmish Battles
description:
Have you ever wondered what a game that incorporated rules from 40K Rogue Trader and Laser Squad would be like?  
Well, wonder no more, as Conflict Brooding is that game.

Conflict Rising is a Skirmish Battle game, which is designed to be played with campaigns (though standard battles can be played with the game too.  
How it works is a player or players will have a force that will have to undertake several missions following a narrative.  
These campaigns can also be played solo play, and rules for this will be explained in the campaign books themselves.

The game itself uses D4s, D6s, D8s, D10s, D12s and occasionally D20s, though there will be times when a D3 or D5 will be required (that can be done with a D6 or D10 respectively, and the score being divided by 2).

The basic rules take up about 20 pages, and when players are accustomed to these, they can start using the more advanced rules.

The game also has rules for vehicles, planes, boats, motorcycles, walkers, robots, mechanaughts, mechtitans and spaceships; all with their own special damage tables.  Of course, these don't feature that much in the game, but once players have done several scenarios or battles, that can try their hand at playing some games with these.

When it comes to creating a force for a battle game, the rule book has a simple way of doing this.  The players will select a race from 21 different races.  The information about these races is brief, but in the near future, I will have created army lists for these races.

Once a player has chosen the troops that they wants in their force, they can choose what weapons, equipment and armour each trooper will have.
Weapons can be chosen from a range of: 93 small arms,
31 grenades and bombs, 9 launchers, 49 support weapons, 49 close combat weapons

There is also a vast array of equipment (64 pieces) and 59 kinds of armour to choose from.

The game also has 81 different kinds of magical jewellery, 18 wands and staffs and 10 kinds of magic jewel.

Finally, troopers can be given special skills of which there are 21 to choose from.  These are Assassin, Mechanic, Navigator, Pilot (that allows special arial manoeuvers), Spiritual Psychics (with 48 Abilities), Raw Magic Magician (with 26 different spell outcomes), Tibasolite Magic Wizard (with 362 different spells), Herbalist (with inumerable kinds of potions), Scroll Reader, Doculator, Medic, Animal Handler, Martial Artist, Ranger, Rider, Thief, Machine Operative, Vehicle Operative, Black Smith, Elemental Fighter and Shape Changer.

There are also some kinds of vehicle to choose from, but more information about these will be given in a race's own army list.

There are also 31 super heavy weapons that can only be mounted on vehicles and spaceships, and 23 spaceship weapons that are too big to be mounted on the largest of vehicles.

finally, there are 18 field support weapons that can be used in the game.

Of course, with all these options, players may find the choice a bit daunting, however, included with this rule book is the campaign "The Subsistencers" that will allow players to get used to the game as it has limited weapons and equipment, and information about these weapons is displayed in the campaign PDF.  Also, when players buy the various army lists for the different races, they will have their own particular weapons that they use, making it easier to create a force if players want to play a battle game or create their own scenarios.

On the Way! is a solitaire Cold War tactical combat RPG in which players take on the role of a single tank commander belonging to one of four nations and their associated units (East Germany (7th Panzer Division), the Soviet Union (79th Guards Tank Division), the United States (4th Armored Division), or West Germany (5th Panzer Division)), starting within a 200-mile radius of the city of Fulda near the divide between East and West Germany, and set against the backdrop of a hypothesized breakout of WWIII at the tail end of 1968 after months of diplomatic tension and military build-up. Inspired by the legendary Avalon Hill classic, Patton’s Best, On the Way! intends to capture much of the former’s outstanding realism, abstraction, and excellent campaign in its own unique way, making it a fresh and original game. A few select differences and additions from the original Patton’s Best system are: Rules for dawn, dusk, and night combat, as well as night vision devices (infrared, thermal, etc.) and other Cold War gadgets like advanced two-plane stabilizers and auxiliary driver and/or gunner controls; a comprehensive selection of period-appropriate ammunition, including APFSDS (Armor Piercing Fin Stabilized Discarding Sabot) and ATGM’s (Anti-Tank Guided Missiles); four playable tanks representing the most common types that would be available to each respective nation and unit in late 1968 (the M60A1 (US), Leopard 1 (West Germany), T-55A (East Germany), and T-62 (USSR)); a detailed tank damage system, accounting for non-KO penetrations, hits by kinetic energy and chemical energy projectiles, as well as detailed crew wounds and a brand new bail-out minigame to resolve the abandoning of your tank turn-by-turn; condensed and specific objectives with particular mission end conditions to specifically avoid mental fatigue and cut out tedium; and much, much more! Additionally, the game has been intentionally designed with modularity in mind in order to account for potential expansions set in later decades (i.e. the 1970’s and 1980’s) with the introduction of advanced systems like thermal technology and explosive reactive armor (ERA), as well as support for WWII campaigns.

Over a period not exceeding more than a month and a half (when it is assumed that the war will either be halted by ceasefire or escalate into a global nuclear war of heinous “mutually assured destruction”, depending on the actual course of your campaign, which changes with each playthrough), your tank commander and his crew will need to fight tactically and soundly to ensure the survival of both themselves and the tank. While your crew fights engagements as they appear to them through their hatches and periscopes, the rest of the war rages around them (represented as a dynamically changing “victory perception” with weekly campaign events), with your outfit’s successes and/or failures potentially having a small but palpable impact on the result of the campaign, potentially earning your nation an early victory, or perhaps a terrible defeat. Combat missions and their associated objectives, as well as enemy units, weather, the time of day, and so on, are all generated through their respective generation tables. Combat missions are resolved on a week-by-week basis, with up to seven combat missions occurring per week until the end of the campaign. In that time, you and your crew may increase their ratings (an abstraction of veterancy – their skills as combat soldiers), suffer casualties using detailed wound generation tables, gain nation-appropriate decorations and promotions, suffer from fatigue, and more. Ultimately, though, surviving the first dramatic, fast-paced, violent weeks of WWIII’s conventional ground war of maneuver is the main goal of your tank commander; after the war on the ground comes to a halt, the rest is out of your hands.

On the Way! includes a comprehensive rulebook and tables (including combat mission, time, and weather generation, combat resolution, friendly and enemy unit actions, to-hit and to-kill tables, random events, detailed bail-out action resolution and wound recovery, and more); a campaign track sheet for recording your tank commander’s and your crew’s ranks, names, ratings, and fatigue, as well as victory points for destroyed friendly and enemy units, tracking ammunition and fuel, captured and lost objectives, and so on; a bail-out mat for resolving the pulse-pounding turn-by-turn escape of your crew from your tank, including pulling severely wounded crewmen from open hatches, escaping from rapidly-building flames inside of the tank, and dodging incoming enemy fire; a strategic movement map to track the movement of your tank and the friendly combined arms task force that it is attached to across a broader, abstracted visualization of Central Europe, complete with rules for time passage, weather changes, and fuel consumption; an abstracted tactical combat mat, representing a radius no larger than 3,500 meters from the center (your tank) in any one direction, where your tank and crew will fight engagements against the specific enemy units that are the most threatening to you that your task force is engaged with; a combat calendar for keeping track of the general course of the war from November 1968 up to January 1969, combat mission types and resistance generation, as well as your faction’s (NATO or the Warsaw Pact) victory perception; 5 large double-sided enemy vehicle unit type cards, 1 large double-sided enemy infantry unit type card, and 2 large double-sided player tank cards; and 2 counter sheets.

On the Way! requires two six-sided (d6) dice to play, and may be (but is not required) enhanced by taking notes and keeping a log on a separate sheet of paper, or digitally with any free word-like program, as your tank commander's story develops.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Art Society: Friends in High Places
description:
Out of all the peers that you try to impress with your impressive collection, there are a few that you consider the cream of the crop. Impressing them gives you the social and political edge you need to truly be the talk of town. There are friends, but then there are “friends in high places”, and you’ll do what you can to stay in their favor, and use it to bless your own pursuits.

In Friend in High Places, while building your collection, you will try to impress one, or all of three influential people in the local art scene; The Art Curator, the Socialite and the Art Critic.

Impressing the Art Curator will give you access to one powerful favor that can completely change the course of your game. The Socialite will present a series of smaller trends for all of you to compete to follow, the first to complete them gets an earlier choice on which rewards to reap. Keeping an eye on the timing of the Art Critic’s publications, and making sure your wall matches whatever the critics currently think is style can really make you the talk of town.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
AWRY: The Forest, a pocket sized horror card game for 1 to 4 players, created by James Ginn.

Everything has gone awry... I don’t know how to explain what happened... This was supposed to be a scientific expedition, but it turned into a nightmare. Last night something monstrous attacked us. We found Dolph hanging from the branches, mutilated beyond recognition. There was blood everywhere. It was a clear message...

Whatever did this was huge and ruthless. And that’s not all! Tech is useless here. Our GPS, radio, watches...none of them work. BUT weirder...even the map is wrong- it’s like the forest is alive and shifting around us.   We have to get out of here!

We ran with only the essentials. I’m writing this journal as a last resort, in case we don’t make it. Maybe someone will find it and learn from our mistakes.

If that's you, it means I’m gone and you are in mortal peril. Pay attention to every word. And may your God, luck and all the wisdom of the world be with you.

The goal of the games is to escape the forest. Your individual
progress is represented by forest cards which can be obtained during the game.

Each round is divided into three phases;
Dawn: Players draw 4 cards.
Day: Players interact with each other.
Dusk: Players resolve any threat cards,

There are nine types of cards: Navigation, Shelter, Provisions,
Threat, Stress, Junk, Weapon, Cursed and Illumination.

During the round, it is crucial players obtain Shelter and Provisions. Players lose 1 Life Token for each one they do not spend during the Dusk phase.

As well as Shelter and Provisions, Players can spend Navigation cards to draw from the Forest deck. This is how you obtain Forest cards. Each player has their own row of forest cards to track their progress and location. The game ends once all players have either escaped with their lives or perished.

—description from the designer

Дроноводи ("Dronovody") (meaning Drone-drivers in Ukrainian) is the world's first table-top game about drones. It transfers players into the world of the modern battlefield, where drones have become an integral part of tactics and strategy.

Players take on the role of unit commanders who manage crews, implement new technologies, destroy enemy troops and equipment using various types of drones. The game emphasises the need to take care of your personnel, the importance of medical and technical equipment in war, and the culture of civil volunteers.

Developed and created by Igrolad Studio, founded by veterans of the Russian-Ukrainian war. The authors of Дроноводи have real combat experience, including with the drones portrayed in the game, thus adding tactical plausibility and elements that reflect real challenges at the frontline.

The game features more than 50 authentic illustrations, including a catalog of enemy vehicles and Ukrainian UAVs.

A 18-card wallet compilation of 2 Button Shy games, delivered at ShyCon Seattle 2025:


     Emerald City (9 cards)
     Cuckoo Shuffle: krakens & bigfoots (9 cards)

boardgame: I Spy Flip & Find: Travel Card Game
description:
Flip the cards to find a pair. Shout "MATCH!" out loud into the air! I SPY Flip & Find is a quick, engaging card game that enhances cognitive skills through fun challenges. Featuring a travel friendly tin, vibrant illustrations, and educational benefits, it's ideal for game night with the kids, sleepovers, and travel. This game is designed for fast-paced fun, sharpening reflexes and observation skills. Find the most matches and win!

How to play:

     Take turns flipping cards to find a matching pair
     Find a matching pair? Shout "MATCH" & touch the match pad
     Keep playing to discover all the matching pairs
     Get the most matches to win the game


—description from the publisher

Cuckoos love to hide and trick everyone! Get the animals in the right order to foil their plans!

Race to get your animals in the right order to claim the trophy. You'll need to listen carefully and act quickly to beat the competition.

Each player has 4 cards: 2 Krakens and 2 Bigfoots in a face-down pile in front of them.

The active player for the turn names the 4 cards in any order, for example "Kraken, Bigfoot, Kraken, Bigfoot".

All players, including the active player, race to grab their cards, reorder them, place the cards down in that order, and grab the trophy card from the center. Play continues until one player wins two turns in a row.

A head-to-head sports game, where players will place cards face down on the Tilt (the barrier) in a fast-paced Joust. Players become the King or Queen of the Country they choose. They will move their Knight & Horse (cards) down the Tilt, where they will attempt to strike their opponent (any card opposite side of the Tilt) to win. With a variety of game-modes to play from, there is a mode for the entire family/friends including a solo mode.

The box contains cards for three games being played simultaneously, for a total of six players.

The game encourages Tournaments to help stores bring in more customers, give prizes to the winners and bring more players into the board game hobby.

The objective is to be declared the King's (or Queen's) Champion by defeating all the Countries' Knights.

—description from the designer

HIEM is a mini-expansion for the game GARDO. Use new check markers (privilege chips) to support the land your opponent is aiming for! In addition, the rules of GARDO have been brushed up, evolving into a more competitive game!
designers: Charlie Hishakaku (チャーリー飛車角)
publishers: Board Game Production Committee (ボドゲ製作委員会)

description:
Division M has arrived in the neighborhood!

The Agent Avenue: Division M expansion introduces a new agent type to Agent Avenue, with these special agents creating a danger zone around your home space. Also included are fifteen unique black market cards, each adding fresh twists and unpredictable opportunities to every game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In this game you two player deckbuilding battler game you take on the role of a Mechromancer, where you will build a Mechrobot, power it with Spirits, and use it to smash your opponent!

Inspired by classic anime of the early 2000's, this game captures the nostalgia and energy of your favorite fight scenes from games like Medabots and Shaman King!

Mechrobots are assembled by drafting parts at the beginning of the game, each consisting of two arms, a head, and set of legs.

Each turn players will acquire Spirit Points (SP) that they will spend to add new moves to their starting deck of 10 basic Actions and Reactions, or to apply unique Spirit Overlays to their bot!

Spirit Overlays are transparent cards placed on top of your bot part cards that permanently change the stats of your bot for the rest of the battle!

On your turn you will play actions to gain more SP, deal damage to each of your opponent's bot parts in the hopes of destroying each one and revealing the Soul Skeleton underneath! Once each of the four bot parts have been destroyed the match is over as there's nothing left to hold the Soul Skeleton to the mortal plane.

—description from the designer

Rocket Science is a realistic game based on the real world aerospace industry. In this game you must work together as engineers, scientists, analysts, and more to gather resources, increase character attributes, manufacture and test rocket components, and build a booster for the space administration deadline.

Rocket Science will test your ability to make decisions and create synergies with your teammates to optimize resource collection, perform actions, and ultimately assemble the final rocket all while the game plays against you using realistic event cards.

Accomplish your tasks and prove to the world that you have what it takes to be a rocket scientist!

—description from the designer

Introducing Northwest: Campfire & Solo Expansion - the brand-new expansion that adds two exciting new ways to play, each offering a unique twist on your journey through the Pacific Northwest.

Telling stories around a campfire is part truth, part myth—and the Tale Cubes bring that spirit into the game. These cubes let players embellish or create parts of their Journal for a new layer of strategy and storytelling.

For those adventurers who prefer to take on the challenges of Northwest alone, we introduce the Solo Summits mode! Prepare for a strategic and rewarding journey as you tackle the trails alone.

—description from the publisher

Ristiin rastiin is Disney themed roll and move game in an 8*8 grid. Every player's pawns starts at their own corners and try to get all of their four pawns to opposite corner to win. On your turn you roll the dice and move towards your destination. Diagonal movement is not allowed.

Corner squares have pictures of four Disney characters (Donald, Minnie, Mickey and Daisy) and two safe squares (for all players) per character in a diagonal. There are also squares with some other Disney characters (Goofy, Pluto, Uncle Scrooge and Huey, Dewey & Louie). When you end your movement with these Disney Characters you pick up a card from corresponding deck and read what happens (They might be good or bad for you.)

When you end your movement to non-character square nothing happens unless there is some other players pawn there. In that case you move that pawn back to starting it's starting square.

—user summary

Inspired by a biblical story, 10 Pragas do Egito ("Ten Plagues of Egypt") casts players as governors of provinces along the banks of the Nile River in ancient Egypt.

The objective of the game is to deal with the losses and misfortunes caused by the ten plagues sent by God to the Pharaoh, who refuses to free the Hebrew people from slavery.

The core mechanic of the game is dice selection, and each round has three phases.

In phase 1, players use the dice to collect tokens that will develop their provinces, move their boats along the Nile River, accumulate income, gain favor with the Pharaoh, recover tokens from their discard piles, and free the Hebrews.

In phase 2 there is a dispute for new territories that will expand the borders of each province, bringing a series of benefits that can be immediate bonuses, permanent special actions or points at the end of the game.

In phase 3, the round's plague finally occurs, revealed on the board from the beginning. Each player must, in addition to paying tribute to the Pharaoh, manage the losses caused by the revealed plague.

10 Pragas do Egito is a game with a medium level of complexity, offering an immersive and challenging experience. As the rounds progress, minimizing the effects of the plagues becomes more difficult. This requires planning and makes each chosen action a crucial decision for victory.

During the summer of 1943, across the vast steppes of the Soviet Union, one of the largest and most decisive tank battles in the history took place: the Battle of Kursk. Pitting the armored might of the Third Reich against the mechanized forces of the Red Army, their campaign marked a turning point in World War II.
The Eastern Front became a massive proving ground for armored warfare. German tanks like the Panzer IV, Panther, and the feared Tiger I aimed to break through Soviet lines with a powerful blitzkrieg offensive. But the Soviets, relying on their dependable T-34s and newly deployed anti-tank guns, had learned from earlier campaigns and built deep defensive lines, minefields, ambush points, and strategic fortifications.
Solitank is a solitaire card game with a two-player variant, in which German and Soviet tanks from World War II face off with the goal of destroying the enemy and conquering territory. You can choose which faction to play as: German or Soviet.
Each faction includes three unique AI Commanders, offering different scenarios and increasing levels of difficulty. These six Commanders simulate tactics and provide a scalable challenge
Solitank is a Print and Play card game available in two visual styles: a red-and- black version designed for vertical layout, and a full-color version designed for horizontal layout. Players may choose to print either version-or both, if they wish.

—description from the designer

Welcome to earth, human! We wish you the best luck on your search for Enlightenment. Be wary, for the high-dimensional spiritual forest around you is full of life, including predators and parasites. Navigate your way to Self-Actualization, choosing your practices, relationships and guides wisely.

This is an existential strategy card game where you manage your limited time to "Learn Lessons" (draft cards), complete needs on your personal player board, as well as fulfill secret life quests that you reveal at end of game.

You begin life with some needs already met: air, physical health, and immune health. You begin lacking many more: Control, Clothing, and Shelter, etc. On your personal board you see your needs arranged in a triangle with rows. Each unmet need has a number and a color representing their cost in Gold, Awareness (red), or Virtue (black).

Each of your cards represents how you can spend your time: Time is represented in blue, and an arrow shows how you can spend that time to gain another resource.

The game is unveiled iteratively in 4 un-boxings, called the 4 Incarnations. Incarnation 1 introduces the basic concepts, Events, Quests, Lessons (Careers, Guides and Practices). Incarnation 2 introduces Relationships (a type of Lesson), the Psychosis Event and Lessons that carry a Psychosis Risk. Incarnation 3 introduces mechanics for long-term planning, and Incarnation 4 introduces the concept of Self-Actualization and Spiritual Alignments

—description from the designer

boardgame: カードストライカー(Card Striker)
description:
ボールを進めてゴールを目指そう！
ドリブルで突破か、それともパスか？
相手のカードを読んでボールを奪え！
際どい角度から、いちかばちかのシュート！
カードで楽しむ2人対戦のサッカーゲーム！

Move forward and aim for the goal! Dececid between dribbling and making a pass. Read your opponent's card and steal the ball! Take a risk and shoot from a tight angle! Have fun with this 2-player soccer card game!

You wake up suddenly on the sand…
You had a clear destination in mind, but the sea betrayed you. The ship you traveled on is now scattered wreckage on an unknown shore. One by one, you awaken, dazed, with the salty taste of the sea still on your tongue and a veil of mist shrouding everything around you. Only four of you remain. You soon realize you’re not alone…

1. What Makes It Unique?
Saltwind is a brutal, story-driven skirmish game unlike anything you've played before. With scenario elements revealed turn by turn, and the game mode shifting from cooperative to free-for-all mid-session, every playthrough is unpredictable and tense.
Characters don’t have hit points, instead, their core stats (Strength, Weakness, Intelligence, and Madness) fully define how they act, survive, and die.

2. What to Expect on the Table
Stat-based survival system: No HP. Your stats are your lifeline, when one hits the limit, you die.
Fluid character state: Strength, Madness, Intelligence, and Weakness constantly shift during play.
Gruesome injury tables: Damage isn’t generic, each wound is detailed and permanent.
Persistent status effects: Bleeding, poison, corrosion and more add constant pressure.
Dynamic scenario system: Missions evolve as you play, often turning allies into enemies.
Over 1200 possible scenario combinations ensure endless replayability.
Scavenge, craft, heal: Patch yourself up mid-battle, or die trying.
Permanent death and item persistence: Dead characters leave behind loot for future survivors.
Single, universal master table handles everything from combat to actions, simple yet brutal.

3. What You Need to Know
Model count: 4 per player.
Miniature scale: 28–32 mm.
Board size: 60x60 cm recommended.
Game length: 45–90 minutes.
Players: Any, solo, coop, semi-coop, or full free-for-all).
Components required: standard dice, status tokens, character trackers.

4. Modularity & Expandability
Compatible with most generic post-apoc/fantasy miniatures.
Designed for easy scenario expansion.
Support Web app available on Alessar Games Official Website.
Perfect for narrative-driven players and fans of punishing, emergent gameplay.

—description from the publisher

Fuga! is a bicycle racing game that is played with a pen, paper  and a few standard decks of playing cards. The rules and all other game components are available for free online. Players can print these off or keep a computer or tablet handy, whichever is preferred.

Fuga! is designed to replicate the real strategy and tactics of professional bike racing—but without all the long, dull training kilometers or excruciating pain. It aims to appeal to both those who are new to the sport and full-blown couch director sportifs. Courses in the game take the group of contenders through a variety of terrains, such as steep climbs, technical descents, gruesome cobbles, and flat expanses–all of which suit the different types of riders that players are able to choose from. The game especially emphasizes the interpersonal component of bike racing, as riders joining a fuga—Spanish for “breakaway”—will have to cooperate with competitors in order to keep a larger group of rivals behind known as  the peloton—French for “main group”—at bay. Riders in the peloton, for their part, will often have to share with other teams the burden of chasing the fuga back or else miss out on their chance for victory. In the words of a Global Cycling Network commentator, “To become enemies, you must first become friends.”

Fuga! is also about taking calculated risks, staying true to the saying that pro cycling is “poker at 60 kilometers per hour.” In Fuga!, if you play your cards right, bluff your opponents when you need to, and choose the perfect moment to go all-in, you’ll be the one raising your arms in glorious victory while  your opponents are left shaking their heads and banging their fists in anger. Whether it’s by winning a bunch sprint, or a sprint of a small group, or pulling off a fuga de la fuga—breakaway from the breakaway—to go solo, the winner of this game will have demonstrated both brains and nerve to emerge victorious.

—description from the designer

Players create and manage their own corner stores in the big city, competing to attract quirky customers. Every potential shopper is unique. Learn their behaviors and try to cater to the finicky populace!

The goal is to earn enough $$$ to win the week! (there are 3 weeks played in the standard game). Bonus points are awarded for having a packed store and special card plays.

Action Cards inject some wild extra surprises into the chaotic flow. Players can also earn Promotional Signs that help attract specific customers, and obstacles that hinder the foot traffic.

Shop 'Til Ya' Drop is a humourous take on retail management!

—description from the designer

Shout Seven is a multiplayer strategy game for 2 - 4 players. The goal is to put 7 of your pieces in a row solely by moving your opponents pieces out of the way. You control your own pieces as well as your opponents.

Line up 7 of your pieces whilst your opponents are moving your pieces on their turn.  You can start anywhere on the board. You can only go where your opponents let you. You can push your opponents off the board.

If a stalemate occurs, victory goes to the player who has pushed the most pieces off the board.

Are you in the mood for a fun and exciting weekend? Or maybe one that’s more reflective and laid-back? In Finde, you’ll have to use all your skills to enjoy a weekend full of adventures.

In Finde, each player lives through a weekend by managing cards to improve their key skills — Feeling, Thinking, Sharing, and Enjoying — while keeping their Energy in check. The game unfolds over four phases: on Friday, players choose their starting traits that shape their personal play area. On Saturday Day and Saturday Night, players play cards to boost themselves or challenge others by swapping traits and activating special effects. Watch out for overdoing it — too many excesses can lead to addictions, which will limit the skills you need to win. Finally, Sunday is scoring time, where points are tallied based on secret goals, card effects (like statuses and instant or global effects), and penalties for players with too much Energy. The player with the highest score at the end of Sunday takes the win!

The game introduces concepts related to leisure styles, drugs, behavioral addictions, and warning signs that might indicate someone is developing an addiction. The goal is for players to become aware of the factors that can encourage or prevent the development of addictive behaviors.

Finde is a card game developed by the PlaytheWay label from the SIAD association and funded by the Government of Spain’s delegation for the National Plan on Drugs.

—description from the designer

Nestled between the Sound and the Cascades, Seattle's iconic skyline draws travelers looking for urban adventures or escaping into the great outdoors.

Make clever swaps to have the best collection of Wildlife and Trees when someone decides to Build! Each player has 2 cards with Wildlife and Trees on one side and a pair of City goals on the other. On their turn they either swap one of their Wildlife cards with the shared City goal in the center or they Build, forcing all players to reveal their hands and score their Wildlife according to the current City goals. Score points for meeting the primary goal and break ties using the secondary goal. Play to 7 points to determine who has the most scenic city!

Do all people feel the same way? Step into other people’s shoes and have fun exploring the strange and diverse emotions they choose in response to life’s stimuli. Input! is a card game about emotional intelligence where you’ll win if you manage to control and rationalize more limiting thoughts than anyone else.

In INPUT!, players receive limiting thoughts that they must transform into rational thoughts by guessing emotions through empathy with the active player in each round. Each turn, one player takes on the role of the protagonist: they listen to a situation (INPUT) proposed by the player to their right, secretly choose three emotions that situation evokes for them, and the other players try to guess them in several phases, receiving clues from the protagonist. Whoever guesses correctly earns points to convert limiting thoughts into rational ones. The player who manages to transform the most thoughts wins.

The game also includes a cooperative mode where everyone guesses at the same time and earns points together as a team, transforming the limiting thoughts from the common deck into rational thoughts. After the final round, players receive feedback based on their performance as a team.

This game aims to encourage debate and reflection about thinking styles and the different types of emotions associated with them. The goal is for players to expand their emotional vocabulary, understand, develop, and practice empathy, and learn strategies to regulate their emotions and manage disproportionate reactions.

Input! is a card game developed by the PlaytheWay label from the SIAD association and funded by the Government Delegation for the National Plan on Drugs.

—description from the designer

Flight is a module that can be added to the core game Nature to alter the experience. This module is for players who prefer to avoid conflict.

Find Safety in the Skies!
Ignoring a predator ‘til the last possible moment, leaping from the ground in defiance of gravity and soaring high into the sky: this is the stuff of dreams. Flight adds flying species to the Nature ecosystem, 5 new traits, and a safe haven where birds can migrate to grow their population in peace.

Abundance awaits those gifted with wings.

The Nature Game System
Nature allows you to combine modules to tailor the game for the players at the table.  Add Nature: Flight for people who prefer to avoid conflict, Nature: Jurassic for a more aggressive game, or Nature: Arctic Tundra for a survival game on the edge of the livable world.  There are 5 modules and 2 mini-modules that can be combined to create 96 different ecosystems. After the Summer 2026 releases there will be over 700 combinations to explore!


Nature Digital: Learn to play with an interactive tutorial on the free-to-try Nature app (iOS, Android, and Steam).
https://www.naturedigitalgame.com/


A Living Game System:
NorthStar Studio will release the Climate module in 2026, followed by one to two new modules per year for the next decade.
Vote for future modules here:
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/308/blogpost/166360/future-nature-modules-poll


Designer Diary
Dominic Crapuchettes discusses the inspiration behind Nature, which began during his time as a professional Magic player.
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/1/blogpost/179068/designer-diary-the-evolution-of-nature
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Bandit is a fast-paced and addictive card game that blends memory, bluffing, and quick reflexes. Players try to outwit their opponents and lower their score by uncovering, swapping, or discarding cards—sometimes their own, sometimes their rivals’. The twist? You only know two of your four hidden cards, and the rest of the game is about manipulating the unknown.

Each player starts with a 2x2 grid of face-down cards and a vague idea of their own score. On your turn, you can draw a card from the deck or the discard pile, then either use its power, swap it with one of your hidden cards, or discard it. Powers range from peeking at a card to swapping full hands or messing up your opponent’s layout. The goal is simple: have the lowest total score at the end of the round.

At any moment, if you have a matching card in value with the top discard, you can slam it down as an immediate discard. Be the fastest, and you’ll get rid of a card. Fail, and you’ll pay the price—penalty cards add to your total!

When a player thinks they have the lowest score, they declare “Bandit!” and trigger the final round. But timing is everything—call too early, and a +10 point penalty awaits. Call it right, and you earn –10 points. The game ends when someone reaches 100 points, and the player with the lowest total wins.

—description from the publisher

The let’s get merry CARD GAME

Think fast and talk faster with Making Spirits Bright, the merriest game of the season! In this merry match-up, players race to match the ""I Have A..."" drink cards in their hands to the ""Who Has A..."" drink cards in the center pile. The first player to get rid of all of the drinks in their hand wins!

Get ready for festive fun in the season's most spirited game that's perfect for holiday gatherings! Ready to jingle and mingle? Play this fast-paced drink-matching game and uncover your new favorite holiday cocktail! Think you can keep up? Let's find out!

—description from publisher

A golf game where you will play through nine "holes".
On your turn, you will decide which club to use and then roll a die which in combination will determine how far you hit the ball. This will then sometimes trigger local rules (many of these involve golf rules questions), and if you land on the green, you will switch to the putting green, where the game transforms into a dexterity game and you have to manually putt the ball using a little putting iron.

Someone has rearranged time! Players must to use their time machines to place key historical events back into the proper order. Players will learn about important and fun dates in world history such as which was created earlier, pizza or the Mona Lisa? And which came first, use of fire or use of the wheel? With 14 Action Cards, the game is different every time!

The Scholastic Time Machine Travel Game encourages fun with history and reinforces historical timelines. Use the included real Red Reveal Decoder to decipher the true date on each card. This decoder makes discovering the answer even more exciting!

EDUCATIONAL: Learn about important and fun dates in world history. Encourages learning history and reinforces historical time lines.
FUN WITH FACT: Players learn about important and fun dates in world history.
SCHOOL SKILLS: Encourages fun with history and reinforces historical time lines.

—description from the publisher

The Very Hungry Caterpillar Bakeshop is an engaging and educational game for preschoolers. Inspired by Eric Carle's beloved classic, this game brings the joy of baking into a playful setting where young children can develop their memory and matching skills.

Players take turns flipping over colorful Bake Shop Cards, racing to ring the Bell when they find a match. Each match includes the number of baked goods on the Card, adding a layered learning aspect to the game for a parent/guardian and child.

STEP INTO THE WORLD OF ERIC CARLE: Perfect for little bakers, this game combines memory and fun with images from “At The Bakeshop” book. Be the player with the most matches to claim your sweet victory!
PLAY & LEARN SYSTEM: This game reinforces Coordination using fine motor skills as kids pick up cards and ring the bell, as well as Mathematics as they engage their memory and counting skills.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: World of Eric Carle (University Games)

description:
Welcome to Chocoland, a world made entirely of sweets! Its capital, Candy Utopia, is a lively and colorful city with chocolate streets, caramel pavement, and houses made of soft marshmallow. The city is divided into several districts, each managed by the ingenious Gummy Bears, who work alongside skilled workers from various confectionery enterprises:


     The expert district builders of CaraFuel
     The masters of cutting-edge technology from JellyTech
     The tireless party spirits of BubbleFun
     The reliable digital specialists of MarshMail


The most eagerly awaited moment of the year is the Great Sweets Festival, a competition that lasts three days and puts the city's districts to the test. The Gummy Bears compete to improve their districts, enriching them with sweet activities and ever-expanding blocks. Each day of the festival represents a stage of improvement, and at the end of each stage, a piñata full of bonuses opens up: resources, decorations, and upgrades that are useful for the districts.
At the end of the festival, the Gummy Bears score their improvements and bonuses.

In more detail, a game of Candy Utopia is played over three days, each consisting of three turns per player. During the game, players build their districts by placing ChocoMix tiles made up of various types of terrain. They place workers to actions — called "activities" — and each activity moves a player on one of five thematic tracks, unlocking progressive bonuses, active powers, extra turns, or enhanced card/tile draws. Each activity is placed following distance and positioning rules, and their placement directly impacts future activities.

Cards can be played for instant effects, used to complete objectives, or saved to score end-game points; players will also compete over piñata cards, which are shared goals related to district building or card management and which award bonus points at game's end.

Play HITSTER Rock with the best rock music of all time!

Dive into this exclusive collection of legendary rock songs and classics. The first player to put 10 songs in chronological order, is crowned the undisputed HITSTER of rock music.

boardgame: Hunt A Killer x Sam and Colby: The Haunting at Wicker Ridge
description:
Sam and Colby have been working with the Scientific Organization for Unexplained Phenomena, SOUP, to try to identify and banish a demon haunting the Wicker Ridge house, but when strange forces drive them out, they turn to you for help.

Be the Paranormal Investigator, banish the demon and bring peace to Wicker Ridge.

Tired of dull nights? We know how to get your blood pumping! Wrap your hands around Hunt A Killer x Sam And Colby: The Haunting At Wicker Ridge. It's got screams, it's got spirits, and you'll definitely make some questionable decisions. So, you know, an upgrade You're gonna be grave-deep in a paranormal investigation. There’s mystery, there’s unexplainable activity, and a demon. It’s the perfect activity for date night, friend night, or after you've finished binging a true crime series. This isn't your average party game. It’s a dark, twisted story puzzle wrapped in a haunted mystery. This game is great for people who actually enjoy using their brains and don't mind chasing down a demon. You're the detective now. Try not to die. Inside the box are realistic clues, creepiness, and the overwhelming sense that something is watching you. Outside the box: still in your living room with the greatest feeling of solving a mystery. This game is ideal for anyone who wants to feel like they've stepped into a haunted true crime documentary. It’s creepy. It’s smart. It's a Haunting At Wicker Ridge. It's the ideal game of choice for anybody who's really into horror games, group games, party games for adults, likes discovering suspicious souls, and ultimately wants to start some drama in the most haunted way possible. Invite your friends, light a candle (preferably black), and banish an evil presence. If you can make it out alive, case closed.

An Immersive Paranormal Experience: Perfect for fans of the supernatural and problem-solving enthusiasts alike, this whodunit lets you play ghost hunter with realistic evidence.

You Are A Paranormal Detective: Sam and Colby need a hand with the unsolved mysteries at Wicker Ridge. Here's the mission: investigate, banish a demon, and don't get possessed.

Haunted House In A Box: Use realistic clues and evidence like a ghost comm box (hear the ghosts, ooooh), a planchette, and a forgotten journal to help fight a devilish entity and survive the haunting.

Exclusive Collaboration: Hunt A Killer teamed up with YouTubers Sam and Colby in this mystery game of paranormal proportions!

Difficulty: 1/5

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Old School Tactical: Volume 5 – Battle for France 1940
description:
Old School Tactical: Battle for France 1940 is a tactical boardgame that puts you in command during one of World War II's most pivotal early campaigns. Fast, deadly and unforgiving, it captures the chaos of blitzkrieg warfare as German forces surge through France and the Low Countries.

Players control squads, tanks and support units across richly detailed maps, making split-second decisions that echo the real challenges of small-unit combat. From desperate rearguard actions to armored spearheads, every scenario is a test of strategy, timing, and nerve.

boardgame: Danger Forward: The Battle of Gela, July 1943
description:
Danger Forward is a complete game covering the Battle of Gela that took place between 10-12 July, 1943 in Sicily. Following the initial landings of Operation Husky, the Italians and Germans were determined to throw the American forces back into the sea. They launched a vigorous attack that nearly succeeded, shelling the beaches with tank fire. The American ground forces, supported by the Royal Navy and US Navy offshore, fought hard to prevent defeat. Not resting on their laurels, the Allied forces had to push inland to secure ports and airfields to ultimately take the island. Taking Sicily quickly led to the invasion of Italy and ultimately the downfall of Mussolini. All of that hung in the balance based on the results of the fighting at Gela.

The game showcases the unique forces involved at Gela, including the infamous Hermann Göring Panzer Division, the US 1st Infantry Division ("Big Red One"), US Rangers, and the Italian Livorno Division that acquitted itself well. The terrain depicts the unique Sicilian landscape, from the invasion beaches, the rough mountains inland, and the ubiquitous olive orchards that dotted the island.

Danger Forward is ideal for gameplay: a single map, a handful of formations, and a three turn game. Yet the situation and game variables allow for replayability. Veteran players will see dynamic situations as challenging for each side, and new players will learn the ropes of how to perfect strategy and tactics. The maps and counters use the larger format as in Arracourt. It's ideal for setting up and completing in a single game session.

Scenarios:

The Battle of Gela (Campaign)
Cent Beach (Intro Scenario)
Dime Beach (Small Scenario)
American Husky (Historical Ground Entries)
Bitter Victory (All Historical Setup)

—description from the publisher

Alien Agendas is family friendly and takes inspiration for the Ancient Astronaut Hypothesis and cultural icons like the X-Files.

In this rummy style card game, you are investigators trying desperately to prove that various extraterrestrial species have plans for humanity and the planet on which we reside.

Collect and agenda card and its components to make a rummy and collect the points. More points for proving more complicated agendas, but be wary as your fellow investigators might steal an agenda and its points with the help of the "Men in Black" or "Project:Blue Book".

—description from the publisher

In this strategic race for survival, sneaky rabbits wield lethal carrots in calculated attacks - from the back! Roll the die and move around the board trying to reach other players from behind to remove them from the game. Pick up a carrot to add to your roll or hop in the rabbit hole to save yourself - temporarily. Watch your back. Avoid attack. The last rabbit standing wins the game.

Train like a Super Hero!
Join forces with many of Marvel's popular characters like Spider-Man, Captain America, Black Widow, and Iron Man as you complete training missions!

This officially licensed Marvel game uses motion detection technology to recognize movements as users crawl, fight and jump to victory during training missions that immerse them in the worlds of their favorite Marvel Super Heroes.

HOW TO PLAY:

     Listen to the advice from the iconic heroes of the Marvel Universe you will meet on your journey.
     Move all around to accomplish your missions: crawl, fight, jump, the artificial intelligence of your Super Hero Trainer will guide you!
     Includes motion detection technology that will recognize your movements.
     Keep track of your 30 training missions through your Hero Map.


CONTAINS:

     1 electronic Marvel Hero Trainer with motion detection technology that recognizes movements such as “JUMP, TURN AROUND, and SUPER PUNCH” - Built with smart motion sensors and adaptive difficulty, this interactive toy encourages kids to stay active while developing motor skills, coordination, and confidence through physical play.
     10 interchangeable Hero Tokens that activate SFX through built-in speaker and LED lights - Each token unlocks unique sound effects and lights, creating an immersive superhero training experience that keeps kids engaged as they interact with their favorite Marvel characters.
     1 instruction manual to assist with initial setup, tutorial, and regular play - Easy-to-follow guide ensures quick setup and smooth gameplay, making it perfect for kids to enjoy independently or with parents’ help during the learning phase.
     1 Hero Map to keep track of progress of the 30 training missions - Kids can visually monitor their achievements and progress through each mission, promoting a sense of accomplishment and goal-setting as they advance in their superhero journey.
     1 Hero Display to store the Super Hero Trainer and Hero Tokens - A dedicated display stand helps keep the training set organized and doubles as a showcase for collectible tokens, making clean-up easy and adding a cool touch to any play area.


—description from the publisher

Victory condition: stay in the green and answer as many questions as possible to collect money and become the Maitre du Midi

Each player draws a card and read it to the player to their left. (questions are organized in difficulty level, so it's important to go in order)
When a Mystery Star is revealed, the player can take a stab at identifying the image. If they get it right they received EUR10000, if wrong, they are out of the game.

If after 5 tries nobody has identified the pictures, whoever has the most money wins.

—description from user

Welcome to the Kingdom of Joustania, where rivalry, magic, and mayhem collide!

Centuries ago, Jordano the Unkind and Lunchius the Hungry quarreled over cider quality before the kingdom’s grandest tourney. Jordano, the cider’s creator, swore it was the finest, but Lunchius refused to agree. Their rivalry extended to the jousting field—despite neither being particularly skilled—and has been passed down through generations.

In a bid to settle the feud, one knight (history is unclear which) enlisted a wizard to grow a magical apple tree. Its fruit was meant to produce the perfect cider and enhance a knight’s prowess. But the wizard’s skills were... questionable. The apples sometimes grant strength, sometimes fail entirely, and sometimes cause wildly unpredictable effects. Now, it’s tradition—every knight (and horse) must eat an apple before each joust, proving their worth in a game of fate and folly.

Drunk Jousting is a chaotic, whimsical tabletop game where dice rolls determine whether you charge heroically or somersault off your horse in spectacular failure. The game is miniature-agnostic, so you can use any knights you have—or grab the two exclusive minis designed by the incredible Jaycee Fairclough. As the creative force behind Red Nebular Miniatures, Wasteman, Dungeon Degenerates Minis, and H+H Toy Company, Jaycee brings her signature magic to the game’s STLs and resin minis. While her designs remain top secret for now, expect top-tier quality and unmatched style.

Prepare for glory, disaster, and cider-fueled chaos—let the jousting begin!

—description from the publisher

Drunk Cowboy is a tabletop skirmish game played with dice and luck. Roll to move, roll to shoot and with a little luck you’ll be the last greenhorn standing.

How do you play? Good question! Roll a die, if it’s a 2-5 move that many inches in the direction the 1 is facing, and if you roll a 1 or 6, you get to take a shot at your opponent! If you hit, there’s a hit chart. 5 wounds and your character is out of the game.

I wrote Drunk Cowboy over the course of a few months and launched version 1 at Portland Zine Fest 2023.  It was a huge hit and I had a ton of fun demoing the game all weekend. Striving to make it better, a friend and I played it far too much and we reworked the dice mechanics to make it far more dynamic. Version 2 is twice the fun and I’m very excited to be relaunching it as part of ZineQuest 2024. I’ve been creating zines since the late 80’s, and my college degree was in traditional book binding techniques.

—description from the designer

Chilena is a fast-paced soccer-themed card game that captures the drama, chaos, and glory of the beautiful game — all in under 5 minutes. Inspired by the iconic bicycle kick (or “chilena”) and the untamed spirit of grassroots fútbol, the game blends tactical play with footballing flair. Ideal for cafés, pubs, locker rooms, or under folding tables at family barbecues.

Players take turns attacking and defending using their own 30-card decks—one "Home" and one "Away." Attackers build up combinations with Pass and Dribble cards to unlock Shots or even the fabled Chilena itself. Defenders counter with Tackles, Saves, or controversial Offsides. If a player can’t make a move, they must substitute — refreshing their hand while ceding momentum. Scoring a goal flips a card face-down to keep score. Matches can end in regulation, go to Extra Time, or even culminate in a Penalty Shootout using the decks themselves.

The objective is simple: score more goals than your opponent by the final whistle. But how you score — and how you stop your rival — requires cunning, risk-taking, and a touch of theatricality. Whether you win by tiki-taka precision or win ugly with a last-minute Rebound, Chilena rewards improvisation and boldness — just like the real thing.
publishers: Born Classic Card Game Design LLC

description:
You are the unspoken architect, the voice behind every turning point. From the first handprint pressed onto a cave wall to the code behind each digital invention, you will guide your civilisation through the great arcs of History. Which breakthroughs to embrace, which revolutions to ignite, which details to keep, which moments to cast in light... and which to leave in shadow - you will decide. This will be your version of our shared past, and our shared future. Will your choices echo through the ages?

Ashes & Amber is a competitive civilisation game built around a brand-new mechanic: players build stacks of cards representing their cities.

The game spans five eras — Prehistory, Antiquity, Middle Ages, Age of Discovery, and Modern Era. You will struggle through the catastrophe that strikes at the end of each era, and you will thrive by earning powerful accomplishments.

Players will use five different decks, one per era, for a total of over 500 unique cards. Each player also selects a unique personal deck with asymmetric mechanics, which they will develop throughout the game.

Player interaction is indirect, driven by shared objectives to complete both during the game and at its end.

—description from the designer

Pikavisa (Speed Quiz) is trivia game for two teams. Winner is the team, which reaches the end of the track first. There are two switching roles for the teams: Asking and Answering.

The Asking team first looks through the clues and secretly tries to guess on which clue the Answering team knows the right answer.

Then they ask the question from the answering team. There are eight categories of questions (nature, geography, history, sport, art, entertainment, music or miscellaneous). And there are four levels of clues (10, 7, 5 or 3 points). Team can guess once for every level and sand timer is running all the time. If the guess is correct, then the team gets to move as many spaces as they get points.

If the Asking team guessed the right clue level, they will get the same amount of points. If the guess was that Answering team don't know and that happens the Asking team gets 6 points.

When you happen to land on certain spaces on the board, there are special rules for answering the question. On Speed spaces you can guess as many times as you want, as long as you have time in sand timer.  On Double answer spaces you can guess twice on every level. On Double point spaces you get your points doubled.

A version of Cat and Chocolate with extraordinary scenarios. Each turn, the active player draws an event card depicting a certain crisis they must handle - Dinosaur attack! Broken time machine! Or the sinking of the Titanic! The player then chooses one to three item cards from their hand and explains how they use these items to get out of the situation.

Other players then vote on whether or not the active player succeeds; if a majority of players agree, the player keeps the event card. At the beginning of the game, players are secretly divided between Team Cat and Team Chocolate. The game is over when a special "End!" event card is drawn, at which point players reveal their team allegiance and count up the number of event cards they've won for their side. The team with the most event cards is the winner!

This game is a solitaire simulation in which the player commands a Japanese Army infantry Regiment (the fictional 236th Infantry Regiment) fighting through the China front.
The player takes the role of the regimental commander (Rentaichō), issuing orders to subordinate battalion commanders or directly leading regimental companies, aiming to accomplish missions.

However, unlike typical wargames, your subordinates and units won’t always obey with a simple “yes, sir.”
Ordering forced marches, attacks, or charges will increase the dissatisfaction of battalion commanders, who may sometimes ignore your commands.
On the other hand, forcing orders too much causes the regimental commander’s authority to decline, which also negatively impacts the operation.
Besides completing missions, you must carefully manage smooth organizational relations—a “realistic” simulation.

Defeat conditions are fixed (the regimental commander’s authority drops to zero and he is replaced), but there are no conventional “victory conditions.”
There are 10 missions in total, and based on your conduct during gameplay, a narrative system determines the commander’s fate in the late war and postwar period.

The map is created using cards, so the setup changes each game.
Chinese army actions are also resolved by cards, allowing for a quick and dynamic gameplay experience.

You stand at the threshold of Knowhere, a place where forgotten and forbidden knowledge is kept sealed. You are a Knowbody, a foreigner to this realm. You came here seeking answers to save your world, carrying a small bag of five Knowstones to guide your journey.

The path to the entrance is guarded by twelve wise beings known as the Knowbles. Only by passing each of their trials and collecting twelve Knowstones in a single run will you be granted temporary access to its secrets.

No one here succeeds at first. Each failure offers insight. Each run brings experience. Return, try again, and grow stronger. In time, you may overcome the trials and uncover the answers you seek.

Going Knowhere is a solo fantasy roguelite game played with a standard pack of cards and a single character progression pamphlet.

Pass Knowble trials, collect Knowstones, and unlock permanent character upgrades across multiple runs to gain entry into Knowhere and uncover the answers you seek.

10 Tail Tangle is the ultimate race to build the longest tail while backstabbing and collecting sets to outsmart your friends!

Players take turns using 3 action points to draw and play cards, ending their turn by placing a tail into their chain. The first player to gain 10 tails wins!

Players gain advantages over each other by working towards tail sets. Getting 3 of the same tail is a set and lets you have its ability effect. Allowing players to win earlier, get extra action points, draw from discard or even gain immunity to being stolen from.

—description from the designer

Word Race is a race to make words from letter tiles as they are turned over — and to expand your words or steal your opponents by adding one or more letters.

There's a pool of letter tiles, which players turn over one at a time. All players then try to spot words that can be made with the available letters, shouting out a word to claim it. You can make a word using just letters from the central pool, by adding one or more letters from the pool to a word that's previously been claimed, re-arranging its letters as needed. This can be done either to expand one of your own words or to one of your opponents', in which case you take the word off them.

The challenge involves coming up with words quickly as soon as letters are turned over, thinking of how existing words can be expanded with additional letters, and forming words that are hard for other players to steal.

Get the family together for this super challenge! Caiu Perdeu has many ways to play! The traditional game mode involves building a tower with 18 floors, each floor with three wooden blocks, removing pieces from the lower floors and placing them on top of the tower, thus making it much taller. But be careful! Each piece removed from the base of the tower makes it more unstable and may fall!

Junte a família para este super desafio! Caiu Perdeu tem muitas formas de ser jogado! O modo tradicional do jogo, consistem montar a torre em 18 andares, cada andar com 3 blocos de madeiras, ir retirando as peças dos andares de baixo e as colocando na parte de cima da torre, assim fazendo ela ficar bem maior. Mas cuidado! A cada peça tirada da base da torre, faz ela ficar mais instável e pode cair!

With the Crowdfunding Revolution expansion, you can launch your games through crowdfunding campaigns for powerful bonuses and prestige.

Invest in marketing, upgrade your staff with unique new abilities, and outsmart your rivals in a race to become the most successful publisher in the business.

Includes:
Marketing: Boost your reach and hype your games through a new marketing mechanism.

     Crowdfunding: Secure funding and unlock powerful benefits before your game even hits the shelves.
     Worker Upgrades: Train your team for specialized roles and gain powerful new abilities.
     Neoprene Mat: The Marketing mat slots neatly next to the game board, adding even more depth to the gameplay.
     New boxes: 10 new miniature board game boxes!

You are the courier to the royal family and must deliver a critical message to important family members. To get the favor of the King and Queen you must deliver this message in the right order to all important family members!

In this solo game, you move a courier token on a 3X3 grid of cards. Each card depicts a member of the royal family and you move from card to card based on the chess moves of the family members

The object of this game is to visit each member exactly once and then get an optimum score by delivering the message in the correct order starting with the most important people!

—description from the designer

Stop the Grinch from stealing Christmas. Join Cindy-Lou and the residents of Who-ville as they discover the true meaning of Christmas. In Clue: The Grinch, you will be trying to figure out which Who caught the Grinch stealing Christmas, What item they saw the Grinch stuffing up the chimney, and Where the item was taken from.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

boardgame: VEXEN: Adaptable Dark Fantasy Gaming System
description:
Do you have what it takes to survive? 
This ghastly lethal world wants nothing more than to see you defeated and utterly crushed by your hated enemies. 
Here, no one cares about your pathetic, uncontrollable screams... there's no crying in Vexen.

Well now, what could be better than exploring this loathsome place with others of your ilk? 
This is a dark fantasy adventure game that ends when the characters complete a quest and fulfill the requirements needed to survive. 
With Vexen, you'll experience a fast and easy game system designed for seasoned and new players alike.

No matter if you've played fantasy tabletop games before or not, you'll have no problem effortlessly learning this system. 
The rules can be freely modified and are quick-playing and intuitive, offering many chances for creative input, strategy, tactics, and problem-solving. 
This game is wickedly adaptable! 
Also, you can play with or without a Game Master (GM), creating your own scenarios or adapting published scenarios to these rules.

Here’s all you get: 

     Complete Player Character Creation System With 6 Races & 8 Classes 
     100 Ready-to-Use Monsters of All Species 
     47 Pregenerated Player Characters 
     Weather & Terrain Generation 
     Monster Tracking & Damage 
     Easy Combat Resolution 
     30 Different Weapons 
     Magik & Spellcasting 
     Combat Templates 
     17 Types of Armor 
     Unique Gameplay 
     QuickPlay Cards 
     Diabolical Traps 
     Optional Rules 
     Game Tokens 
     Player Logs 
     Objectives 
     Treasures. 


Yes, you read that right. 
All that in Vexen? 
Hell yeah! What more could you want? You Want Action?

Welcome Home.

—description from the publisher

Gather friends to weave tales of magical creatures and heroic deeds across 48 unique quests with 12 thrilling finales!

You will use your words to alter the story's shape and complete your quest. Each adventure is unique so venture into danger with word and wit to your heart's desire. Synergize your words, make friends, fight enemies, collect gold, and don't lose heart as you make your way to the grand finale!

During each story of the adventure, a player will be the Story Master. The Story Master is a neutral party member who asks the rest of the party for word cards. Once the Story Master has all 10 cards they will proceed to read the story card. The outcome of each event is decided by the Story Master based on the words (or numbers) given. After all 9 story cards are completed the Finale Card is drawn and that will conclude your quest...at least until next time!

Add your own flair when reading and lean into the weird and wacky. Create characters and names, and maybe even your own lore as you go through your campaign. Woven is as big as you make it!

—description from the publisher

Jom Burger is a dice drafting game in which you compete to make the most delicious burger. Dice represent ingredients, and pips on the dice are the point values. Every round players take turns claiming one ingredient in player order, and then again in reverse player order. You can score extra points for being first to complete a basic burger (i.e. 2 buns, 1 patty, 1 cheese, 1 lettuce, 1 egg), and/or a deluxe burger (2 of each ingredient). You only need at most 2 of each ingredient. If none are left on the table, you don't take anything.

—description from the publisher

Many card games are about who gets the best cards — Just get an Ace, and boom, you win: all luck, no brain power! Low-ranking cards like 2,3 often have no impact to the outcome of game. GALO breaks these paradigms by making the ranking circular: weaker card(s) are given the special power to defeat ONLY the stronger card(s) of the game

Game has 30 playing cards: 15 BLUE & 15 YELLOW cards. 
Each color has 10 Number cards, 4 POWER Cards and a "?" card
Strength of cards is listed below (in the increasing order):
WHITEHOLE* < MOON** < “?” < 1..10 < SUN** < BLACKHOLE*

     Numbered cards have STARS (+1 Point each) & VOLCANOES (-1 Point each)
     “?” is multi-purpose card: make it as 15 playing card, OR convert to a brand-new Power Card
     POWER Cards SUN&MOON, BLACKHOLE&WHITEHOLE are the real game changers.While they don’t carry any points, their powers will help you win the game.


BLACKHOLE: (Card text: Pass Strongest Card)
Opponent(s) has to either defeat it, OR Play their Strongest card. Can be defeated only by WHITEHOLE
SUN: (Card text: Pass Maximum STAR card)
Opponent(s) has to either defeat it, OR Play their maximum STAR card. Can be defeated only by MOON and BLACKHOLE
LUNAR MOON: (Card text: SUN IS SCARED OF ME…HAHAHA)
Second Weakest card of the game, but it has the special Power to defeat SUN (just like MOON causes Solar eclipse).
LUNAR WHITEHOLE: (Card text: BLACKHOLE IS SCARED OF ME…HAHAHA)
Weakest card of the game, but it has the special Power to defeat BLACKHOLE

ESSENTIAL RULES:

     Make separate deck for each color. Each color cards to be distributed equally among players.
     Start the game by playing hand card or face-up card (BLUE or YELLOW). All other players have to play same color card
     Player with stronger card wins; keeps the trick aside. Starts next round.
     After playing face-up card, turn over the facedown card under it
     Game continues until players have played all their cards
     SCORING: #Rounds won + #Stars – #Volcanoes (Tie-breaker: Last Round). Player with highest score is the winner. In case of tie, player who has won last round of the game will be the winner.
     For 6 players, 2 GALO boxes will be required. Both blue and yellow decks will have 30 cards each. If strongest card in a round is played by two players, the one who played it first will be the winner


GALO has Plug & Play Design that lets you start simple and level up as you go. New to cards? No problem — start without any power cards. Later, add one pair, and finally ALL of them!

—description from the designer

The MEST Narrative miniatures rules system is designed to be a flexible light-weight set of features that can work with numerous genres such as Swords & Sandals, Medieval, Swashbuckler, Wild West, Horror, Victorian Adventure, Pulp Heroes, Modern Battles, Future War, Sci-Fi, or Superheroes.

With these rules two or more players can simulate the last exciting minutes of derring-do, harrowing deeds, and heart-stopping acts of courage of any small-scale engagement pulled from any number of genres; from penny novels, serials, and otherwise. Will the dastardly villains win? Will the heroes prevail? Who lives? Who dies? The answers to these questions and more await your decision!

—description from the designer

Challenge your friends in head-to-head tactical battles! This expansion adds new game modes, PvP arenas, and exclusive PvP-only skills and character builds.

Draft two heroes, load them with a fresh set of PvP-only skills and items, then clash in quick 2-on-2 battles. Every win earns a Win Token and bumps both heroes up a level, letting you grab even more Tag-exclusive upgrades before the next round. Same shard and positioning system you know from the campaign, new cards, and a race-to-the-top ladder for pure bragging rights. One full tournament plays in about an hour.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Unknown (fan expansion for Terraforming Mars)
description:
This free to play fan-made expansion for Jacob Fryxelius' masterpiece Terraforming Mars is my attempt to combine my favorite game with my favorite sci-fi universe.

The core idea behind the expansion is to treat the planet — whether it’s Mars or LV-425 — as an unknown world waiting to be explored. You begin terraforming the surface with no idea what lies beneath, thanks to the hidden tile mechanics. This element makes colonization strategies harder to plan, as the bonuses from placing tiles are not always known in advance. However, this uncertainty can shift the dynamic — powerful bonuses can still be uncovered, and triggered events may impact your opponents. The new standard action “Dropship” lets you scout areas in advance, giving you a better overview… and maybe the edge you need to win. You’ll also get the chance to interact with iconic characters from the Alien universe — including Newt, Xenos and even the Xeno Queen herself. Each one comes with their own customizable event lists. You decide whether the planet is a nightmare hunting ground for Xenos, a paradise rich in resources — or something in between.

Best of all, there’s no need to buy additional game boards. You can customize your own map every game — it’s all up to you. Make the experience your own.

The new mechanics don’t increase the game’s complexity — instead, they introduce a sense of mystery and exploration, bringing a more immersive colonization experience to life. Ocean and Mons regions are still clearly marked, so all core placement rules from the base game and official expansions remain fully intact.

I truly hope you enjoy this expansion and have an epic time with your game group.
And remember: In space, no one can hear you scream.

description from the designer

boardgame: Shards of Infinity: Cień Zbawienia + Poza Horyzont
description: Two expansions in one box: Shadow of Salvation and Into the Horizon.

Play your own story! You are "Story Hunters - Detectives for literal investigations". Whenever there is  a mysterious incident connected to literature, you will have to get to work: Explore locations, question people, find useful items and use all of that to uncover the mystery.

In this case you have been summoned to Transylvania. A man called Professor Van Helsing has gone missing there and scary things appear to be happening...

In three chapters with a playing time of about 30 - 45 minutes each, players play their way through a deck of cards. By exploring locations on the map, solving puzzles and using the powerful books of secrets, players can gain helpful items as well as attributes to help with future missions - and access new cards to continue in the story. A chapter ends, when players have achieved their goals (meaning: been able to access the epilogue card). The game has a few different endings, depending on the players' performance.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Catchup! is a fast-paced card game where players race to get rid of the cards in their hand. Open up the tomato case and place a card into each half. Race to drop your number cards in order, either up or down. All players play at the same time, so the game can change in an instant. The first player to discard all their cards wins!
