Can you think fast or will your opponents act faster?

Ready to pack it up? All components load up easily into the tomato-shaped travel case. It's the card game you'll relish!

HOW TO PLAY:

     Race to discard all of your cards!
     You can only hold 5 cards at a time!
     Cards must be discarded in order – up or down. 
     Wild cards can change the whole game!


—description from the publisher
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
The Starfinder Infinity Deck includes:

     A deck of 65 high-quality cards with holographic foil, made up of 10 numbered cards in each of 5 suits and 15 special face cards, including stars, moons, planets, starship captains, and a black hole
     A rulebook with 4 different games playable with the deck


Even seasoned spacefarers need a break sometimes, and those in the Starfinder galaxy often turn to the Starfinder Infinity Deck for some light and fun entertainment.

The original rules for this ubiquitous deck of 65 cards, found in virtually every cantina and corner store in the Pact Worlds, were lost to the Gap, an infamous period of missing memory. Since then, countless alien species, cultures, and worlds have invented their own rules using this versatile deck of 65 suited playing cards.

The Starfinder Infinity Deck comes with rules for four games: a high-stakes gambling game, a card-collecting competition in which you'll salvage an abandoned starship, a tense two-player game that pits smuggler versus security, and a planet-hopping race to complete jobs-for-hire. Pick the perfect pairing for your players and pass the time in the world of Starfinder at your table at home.

—description from the publisher

Get ready for bluffing and partnership with a card game that will test your wits.
In Blefadores S/A, each round is an opportunity to deceive or be honest. Each player receives 2 cards and the sum of their values determines their starting score.
The catch? You can tell the truth about your cards or bluff, because you want to partner up with other players, since your final score for the round is your starting score plus the score of the player you partnered up with in the round.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Noite Zumbi is a trick-taking game where players must jointly survive the nights of a zombie apocalypse.
The cards won in each trick bring weapons and food, but also zombies, which must be collectively eliminated during the night.
Each hand of cards represents a night and at the end of the night (a.k.a. hand) the players check if they have survived or if they have been turned into zombies and lost the game.

A shedding game in which you play cards from your hand according to specific rules and aim to win by playing all your cards as quickly as possible. Use the power chips you can earn by passing and manipulate your rank to overcome unfavorable situations.

Natural Disasters is a module that can be added to the core game Nature to alter the experience. This module is for players who thrive on chaos, uncertainty, and memorable stories.

Behold the Power of Mother Nature!
Everyone wants to control their own destiny, but sometimes Mother Nature has other plans. She may darken the sky with a terrible hurricane, blow lava through the top of a mountain, or shake the earth to its core with a gigantic meteor! Natural disasters remind us of our insignificance — that no matter how hard we fight, some things will always remain outside our control.

Natural Disasters introduces beautiful oversized disaster cards and 10 comet tokens. At round's end, reveal a disaster that remains in play for 1–5 rounds. These scenario cards create new strategic puzzles each round for people who like to think on their feet.

The Nature Game System
Nature allows you to combine modules to tailor the game for the players at the table.  Add Natural Disasters to ramp up the chaos, Nature: Flight for people who prefer to avoid conflict, Nature: Amazon Rainforest for bluffing and deduction, or Nature: Arctic Tundra for a challenging survival game on the edge of the habitable world.  There are 5 modules and 2 mini-modules that can be combined to create 96 different ecosystems. After the 2026 releases of the Climate module and 2 new mini-modules, there will be over 700 combinations to explore!


Nature Digital: Learn to play with an interactive tutorial on the free-to-try Nature app (iOS, Android, and Steam).
https://www.naturedigitalgame.com/


A Living Game System:
NorthStar Studio will release the Climate module in 2026, followed by one to two new modules per year for the next decade.
Vote for future modules here:
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/308/blogpost/166360/future-nature-modules-poll


Designer Diary
Dominic Crapuchettes discusses the inspiration behind Nature, which began during his time as a professional Magic player.
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/1/blogpost/179068/designer-diary-the-evolution-of-nature

Arctic Tundra is a module that can be added to the core game Nature to alter the experience. This module creates a survival game for players who want a challenge.

Frozen, Windy, and Barren!
The deep loneliness of this desolate land resonates in the mournful howls of Arctic wolves as they follow migrating caribou across the frozen tundra. The ground is permanently frozen, denying trees the chance to take root. Instead, resilient mosses, lichens, and small shrubs cling to survival — nothing tall enough to stop the relentless, ever-strengthening winds. In this unforgiving environment, the bitter cold and wind are always in your face, determined to push back those without the resolve to persevere.

Arctic Tundra adds a frozen watering hole, a deck of 5 traits, and a Cold die to determine the harshness of the coming winter.

The Nature Game System
Nature allows you to combine modules to tailor the game for the players at the table. Add Nature: Arctic Tundra to experience life on the edge of the livable world, Nature: Flight for people who prefer to avoid conflict, or Nature: Jurassic for a more aggressive game. There are 5 modules and 2 mini-modules that can be combined to create 96 different ecosystems. After the Summer 2026 releases there will be over 700 combinations to explore!


Nature Digital: Learn to play with an interactive tutorial on the free-to-try Nature app (iOS, Android, and Steam).
https://www.naturedigitalgame.com/


A Living Game System:
NorthStar Studio will release the Climate module in 2026, followed by one to two new modules per year for the next decade.
Vote for future modules here:
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/308/blogpost/166360/future-nature-modules-poll


Designer Diary
Dominic Crapuchettes discusses the inspiration behind Nature, which began during his time as a professional Magic player.
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/1/blogpost/179068/designer-diary-the-evolution-of-nature

Amazon Rainforest is a module for the core game Nature that rewards bluffing, deduction, and perfectly timed reveals.

The Amazon Has Secrets!
Mysteries hide behind every leaf of the canopy - poisonous dart frogs, jaguars that can crack turtle shells, and monkeys in search of exotic fruit. This module adds a tropical watering hole, fruit tokens, poison resistance tokens, and 5 new traits, along with a major rule change: traits are no longer revealed at the end of the Adapting Phase. Instead, players may reveal a trait at any time to gain its benefit. Face-down traits provide no benefit.

The Nature Game System
Nature allows you to combine modules to tailor the game for the players at the table. Add Nature: Amazon Rainforest for players who enjoy bluffing and deduction, Nature: Flight for reduced conflict, or Nature: Jurassic for a more aggressive experience.  There are 5 modules and 2 mini-modules that can be combined to create 96 different ecosystems. After the Summer 2026 releases there will be over 700 combinations to explore!


Nature Digital: Learn to play with an interactive tutorial on the free-to-try Nature app (iOS, Android, and Steam).
https://www.naturedigitalgame.com/


A Living Game System:
NorthStar Studio will release the Climate module in 2026, followed by one to two new modules per year for the next decade.
Vote for future modules here:
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/308/blogpost/166360/future-nature-modules-poll


Designer Diary
Dominic Crapuchettes discusses the inspiration behind Nature, which began during his time as a professional Magic player.
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/1/blogpost/179068/designer-diary-the-evolution-of-nature

Honor & Infamy: Battlegrounds is a fast-paced tactical print & play game for two players. Players take on the roles of different fantasy classes to battle each other using different abilities with the goal of reducing their opponent's health to zero.

Choose your character, pick up a D6 and start to roll. With each roll you can further unlock your characters powers and start to fight your opponent. The first to reduce the opponents 20 lifepoints to zero wins!

Герои II ("Heroes 2: Write & Conquer") is a comprehensive strategy game in a handheld format. Explore territories, build castles, gather troops and search for powerful artifacts.

This is the second box in the Heroes game series, featuring 6 unique factions. The factions from it can be mixed with the factions from the Heroes: Write & Conquer.

The game lasts for 16 rounds.

At the beginning of a round, the first player chooses four actions out of five that all players in that round will perform. The first player writes the numbers 1, 2, 3 and 4 in the boxes of the selected actions — one number per box. The number determines the order in which the action is performed and its level. The higher the number, the later the action is played and the higher its level.
There are 3 types of actions in the game:

Wood, iron and gold gathering allow you to add as much of that resource to your inventory, as the level of the action is. Resources are needed for building and recruitment.

Building and recruitment action allows you to build structures and hire units of a corresponding level. Structures give you unique effects, while units are needed to fight with other players, monsters, or to be able to claim fortresses and artifacts.

Finally, movement allows you to move to other sectors based on the amount of movements points you have in this round. After all players finished their movement, different types of fights can be triggered. Players can fight each other if they happened to be at the same sector, or they can claim a fortress or an artifact of that sector. They can also fight monsters for reward. In order to do that, they’ll be spending combat units and compare their strength.

At the end of the game, players will count their victory points for the structures built, fortresses and artifacts claimed, resources gathered and monsters defeated.
The player with the most VPs wins.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
Iron Scales: Sky Dominion is a fast-paced tactical skirmish game set in a post-apocalyptic world where dragons exist in a modern era. Players take control of enhanced dragons mounted each equipped with missile pods, machine guns, and other brutal technologies as they battle for aerial dominance in a ruined world.

Combat unfolds on a hex-based map where positioning, timing, and prediction are everything. Each round begins with players secretly planning their movements using custom dragon maneuver cards. Once revealed, all dragons move simultaneously, potentially colliding or positioning themselves for devastating attacks. Weapons have unique ranges and arcs of fire, and each dragon-rider duo brings a powerful special ability to the fray.

Gameplay is quick and deadly, with a mix of light tactical planning and explosive cinematic moments. Whether you’re weaving through clouds to flank a rival, or locking on for a critical blast from a distance, every choice matters and every mistake can be fatal. Damage is tracked through a secret card-based system that adds tension and surprise, with some hits disabling wings, weapons, or control systems.

Designed for 2–6 players, Sky Dominion supports both free-for-all and team-based modes, offering high replayability with a streamlined ruleset. It’s accessible enough for newcomers, yet layered with enough strategic depth to keep veterans of skirmish games coming back for more.

description from the designer

boardgame: Super Auto Pets Card Battle: Classic Pack
description:
In Super Auto Pets Card Battle, players build teams of cute pets with unique abilities, then match them against a friend's team in a short and sweet auto battle.

Each game is played over six rounds, beginning with players receiving coins to spend on pet and food cards. After shopping, the players arrange their teams in secret and chooses their placement for battle. The round ends with players revealing their stacks and playing cards one at a time in auto battle style, knocking out pets and resolving abilities to eliminate the opposing team.

After six rounds, whoever has the most trophies wins the game! Super Auto Pets Card Battle is a fast and furious dueling game that is easy to learn and filled with tons of variety. Each Super Auto Pets set is a standalone game for two players, with new collections of pets, food, and crazy abilities. Mix sets for zany combinations or to let more players jump in on the action!

—description from the publisher

Get ready Miners!

A rival corporation has begun prospecting operations on Hoxxes IV, there are deadly new biomes to explore and disgusting bugs to kill!

An expansion for Deep Rock Galactic: The Board Game, includes new enemies such as the Patrol Bot and Burst Turret from the video game, and a new boss transplanted from the video game -- The Caretaker. Along with new tools and several new missions.

Ao longo do tempo, surgiram mais de 400 raças de cães em todo o mundo, com uma grande variedade de tamanho, estrutura física, pelagem e traços. Mas uma coisa é comum a todos: a capacidade impressionante de se adaptar a nós, humanos, de serem leais e nos acompanharem, seja aonde for.

Nas cartas contidas nessa caixa, serão apresentadas 50 raças de cães - desde o pequeno Chihuahua, passando pelo versátil Pastor-alemão, até o enorme Dogue Alemão. Para cada raça, há uma descrição das características mais importantes e várias informações extras superinteressantes. E para aqueles que gostam de adivinhar, temos o quiz com uma série de perguntas.

Over time, more than 400 dog breeds have emerged worldwide, with a wide variety of sizes, physical structures, coats, and traits. But one thing is common to all: the impressive ability to adapt to us humans, to be loyal, and to accompany us wherever we go.

The cards contained in this box will introduce you to 50 dog breeds—from the tiny Chihuahua, to the versatile German Shepherd, to the enormous Great Dane. For each breed, there's a description of the most important characteristics and several super-interesting extra pieces of information. And for those who like to guess, we have a quiz with a series of questions.

Welcome to the merciless world of Lying, Cheating Raccoons, where sneaky strategy and quick thinking will determine the ultimate trash bandit! In this fast-paced bluffing game, players roll their dice, hide their results, and then decide whether to tell the truth or lie their way to victory. Will you keep it safe, bluff, or challenge your opponents? Stay one step ahead of the competition, collect trash tokens, and prove you’re the craftiest raccoon in the group. But beware—call out the wrong bluff, and you might just lose it all! Are you ready to roll the dice and see how much you can get away with? Add Lying, Cheating Racoons to your collection of family board games and card games for a sneaky, good time!


     A FAST-PACED GAME OF BLUFFING, STRATEGY, AND TRICKERY: Lying, Cheating Raccoons challenges players to outsmart their opponents using deception, distraction, and a bit of luck! Bluff about your die roll, steal tokens, and keep your true intentions hidden as you fight to become the raccoon with the most delicious trash treats. 



     HIDE YOUR ROLL, MAKE YOUR CALL, AND KEEP A POKER FACE: Each player secretly rolls their raccoon die under their garbage can and decides whether to tell the truth or try to sneak their action of choice. Other players must choose whether to accept their move or call their bluff—get caught lying, and you face consequences!



     CHALLENGE YOUR OPPONENTS, STEAL TOKENS, AND CLAIM VICTORY! Players can attempt to take trash tokens from their left, right, or any opponent of their choice. But opponents can challenge the action. If your move is challenged, and your challenger is correct, you must forfeit your tokens.



     FUN, EASY TO LEARN, AND REPLAYABLE: Designed for fast rounds and endless replayability, Lying, Cheating Raccoons is perfect for family game nights, parties, after school, or any time you want an entertaining family party game. 



     PERFECT FOR FANS OF LIAR’S DICE AND LEFT, RIGHT CENTER: If you love bluffing games like Liar’s Dice and Luck Games like LCR, you’ll enjoy the mischievous, competitive nature of Lying, Cheating Raccoons. It’s a must-have for players who love deception, strategy, and cheating! 


—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray

description:
Hieron is a cooperative game in which players work together to win the favor of the 12 Gods of Olympus.
Each deity has their own preferences, accepting different offerings at their temples and, throughout the game, Heroes appear to help with the missions.

Each turn the player must play an offering card in one of the Gods' temples that has the same number or type as the last card played. If they don't, they pass the turn or play a hero who changes some of the rules of the game.
When a temple is completed, the player who finished it makes a demand, asking another player for a type of card, who reveals the highest card of the type requested, if they have one, making it visible to everyone.
Communication between players is extremely limited and this type of revelation can make the game easier.
To win, the team must complete all 12 temples, using the strategy efficiently.

The adventurers have left and the monsters are bored. Time to reform!
Turn this decaying den into the most fearsome dungeon ever!

In the game, each player builds their own floor of the dungeon, creating environments made up of rooms of the same color, aligned with Your Maleficence's requests. To score points, the rooms must be adjacent to the requests, respecting the indicated format and with bonuses for fulfilling the color of the request. 
The game has 6 rounds, divided into 2 phases. In the design phase, players choose open cards in the center of the table. In the renovation phase, they simultaneously place the chosen cards on their own floor, connecting them orthogonally to cards already played.
At the end, with 12 cards in their deck, each player will be able to relocate one of them before the final score.

Caos na Cozinha is an agility game to play with children.

4 players are divided into teams of 2 in which one is the waiter and the other the cook. Each waiter receives several orders which must be communicated to the cook. The cook must, as quickly as possible, pick up the EVA ingredients with plastic tongs and place them in the pans, creating the dishes and drinks that correspond to the order.
When a dish is ready, the waiter must remove the ingredients from the pot and place them on a plate. As soon as all the dishes in an order have been completed, the waiter rings a bell and receives a new order.
At the end of a predetermined time (usually 8 minutes), the team adds up the score of all the completed orders.
The game also has a variant for playing with older people, in which the cook works blindfolded.

The best French players have come together in this special MONOPOLY French Football Federation edition!
Buy your favorite footballers, invest in stadiums, and build stands to become the most powerful supporter of the FFF.
Monopoly FFF: a board game where the goal is to be the last player standing.

Les meilleurs joueurs français se sont réunis dans cette édition spéciale MONOPOLY Fédération Française de Football ! 
Achetez vos footballeurs favoris, investissez dans des stades et construisez des gradins pour devenir le supporter le plus puissant de la FFF. 
Monopoly FFF : jeu de société où le but est d'être le dernier joueur à ne pas faire faillite.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Spearhead – Sand & Bone Gaming Pack
description:
Spearhead is an exciting way for both new and experienced players to enjoy Warhammer Age of Sigmar. In this game mode, you'll fight small-scale battles that are fast, tactical, and different every time.

This pack contains cards, terrain, a gameboard, and supplement book for playing Spearhead games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar. The gameboard is 30” by 22.4” – the specified size for Spearhead games – and features pre-printed objective markers for your armies to fight over. The six pieces of terrain are ideal for adding extra dimensions to your games, creating opportunities for cover and ambush. Also included are themed twist cards that ensure games are close right until the final dice roll, and battle tactics cards, which dictate how to score victory points and offer powerful one-off effects.

Contents:


     1x 24-page Spearhead: Sand and Bone book: Includes rules to get you started with games of Spearhead, from choosing your army, to the Battleplan: Sand and Bone, plus rules for the Cities of Sigmar: Fusil-Platoon, Seraphon: Sunblooded Prowlers, Ossiarch Bonereapers: Mortisan Elite, and Ogor Mawtribes: Scrapglutt Spearheads, and more.



     1x Double-sided game board: This game board features four printed objectives on one side, and five on the other, and is designed for use in your Spearhead games. It depicts the barren Shyishan landscapes of Ossia – Sand, on one side, and Dolorum – Bone, on the other.



     6x Plastic pieces of terrain: This terrain depicts the morbid architecture found in the Realm of Death. Two pieces grant special abilities to nearby units.



     36x Spearhead cards:These cards contain twists and battle tactics ready for you to shuffle and draw during your Spearhead games.


A copy of the Warhammer Age of Sigmar Core Book to make full use of the contents of this set.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to 11th century Iberia! In this historical game, take on the role of a king or an emir and compete to build the greatest kingdom on the peninsula through territory expansion, constructing buildings and wonders, and by attracting a diverse court. As you expand and grow your kingdom, the population faiths of your kingdom will change depending on the lands you conquer, the buildings you build, and the courtiers you play. Be sure to maintain balance between the faiths of your kingdom and your ruler's political stance or suffer the consequences through lost income or victory points.

To play Iberia, a player's turn takes place over four steps.  

     Step 1, players collect maravedi coins (income).  
     Step 2, players use their maravedi to take actions.  Some of these actions include, constructing buildings and wonders, training soldiers, building castles, reforming politics, or upgrading mobility. During this step players may also play, and gain the benefits of, any courtier cards they have acquired.
     Step 3, players move their army to consolidate or take new lands.
     Step 4, if a player moves into region they do not occupy, they immediately enter battle and move to subdue the region.  If the player wins and subdues the population, they gain income, and their populations rise on their kingdom board.


Once all four steps are complete, play passes to the next player. Game play continues over a series of turns until on player raises the required number of flags or a player acquires two wonders.

—description from the designer

Soccer Duel includes 84 tiles. 80 show a fourth of either 1 or 2 soccer balls of 6 diff colors/patterns. Other 4 are 3 15-second "yellow card" tiles and 1 30-second "red card" tile. 2-Player game: each player draws 6 tiles and places them facedown in front of themselves. They then pick 1 to play faceup to their tableau as a starting tile. Mix the rest facedown and place between the 2 players. Players then simultaneously race to build their tableaus by placing tiles in them. Players may use one te faceup tiles in front of them or draw one of the facedown tiles in the middle. Tiles must be placed adjacent to existing tiles and must be placed so that any soccer ball quadrants on adjacent tiles line up and match in terms of color/pattern. If you can't play a tile, discard it to your box/ If you draw a "yellow card" or "red card" tile, you must wait the indicated amount of time (15 or 30 seconds) before you being playing again. Then discard the tile and draw another. After playing or discarding a tile, draw another from the pile. Once all tiles have been played, players swap their discard boxes then use their opponent's discarded tiles to fill out their tableau. Player who makes the most completed soccer balls wins.

—user summary

Discover India includes 36 cards (1 for each Indian state) with various facts about the state (exports, major cities, rivers, capital, etc.) Each player has a pawn and a set of 36 tokens (1 for each state). Board has 36 spaces (1 for each state), each showing a clue for that state (e.g. "Visit the wettest State"). On turn, roll 1d6 and move. If you land on a space without a token, you must guess the state from the clue. If correct, place your token for that state on the space. If not, lose your "next chance to roll the dice". If you land on a space with a token already on it, the next player takes the card for that state and asks you a question related to that state. If correct, take the card. Game ends when all spaces have tokens and all cards have been taken. Score 10 points for each of your tokens on the board and 20 for each card you took. High score wins.

—user summary

Combat! Tunisia & Sicily is a stand-alone solitaire game of man-to-man combat in which the player commands an American or Italian squad. Although the 11 scenarios in the game center around the fighting in Tunisia and Sicily, full-war Orders of Battle are included for the Americans and Italians. With this module then, gamers can play scenarios involving the Americans or the Italians from any year of the war. The included Random Scenario Generator and Campaign Game allow for an amazing gaming system which is self-contained, or can be combined with Combat! Eastern Front to create an even more expansive Combat! experience

Other new features found in this game include:

     Advanced Street Fighting rules such as flat roofs, sprinting through alleys, balconies, and more.
     National Traits for both sides that model their fighting traditions, formations, and equipment.
     New Weapons for both sides, including the M1 Carbine and the Italian armaments.
     New Terrain rules for River, Ford, Bridge, Cactus, and Scrub, plus Weather rules.
     Defenses such as Pillboxes, Strongpoints, Minefields, and Wire.


Competitive Play rules for head-to-head battles!

—description from the publisher

Chrono 1 is a car racing family board game for 2 to 4 players from ages 8 and up that takes around 20 minutes.
The board is constructed by randomly choosing cards, the more cards you choose, the longer the race will be.
To advance on a card you have three choice :

     Pay fuel
     Memorize a value, different for each player, written on the back of the card
     Push a real car model, provided in the box, to a precise position on a track.


Using, wisely, theses three ways to advance, will bring you the victory.

boardgame: A Little Selfish: Space Mini Edition
description:
Devise a devious strategy to reach safety while simultaneously preserving your oxygen supply and sabotaging your fellow astronauts. Be the first back to the ship or, well, you suffocate and die.

This is a mini edition of the bestselling classic. It's a faster game that can be played over a smaller surface area. We made this so you can be selfish wherever you go. You're welcome.

-description from publisher's website


A compact edition of Selfish: Space Edition in a smaller box with fewer components and supporting 4 players (instead of 5 with the full size edition).Includes: 4 Astronaut cards, 36 Oxygen cards, 23 Space cards, 14 Action cards, 2 Reference cards, 1 Spaceship card, Mission Guide, instructions.

Hellcat Tankers requires you to manage the Crew of a single World War II M18 Hellcat Tank in and around the Battle of Arracourt in France, 1944. The Battle lasted for 12 days from 18 September to 29 September. Each day your Crew and their Tank may or may not find themselves engaged in combat with the enemy, but you will certainly see plenty of action over the course of the full battle. Whilst this game is not a ‘narrative driven’ game as such, it has elements of those games, and with a little imagination it will provide memorable stories. There may be lucky escapes, notable bravery and manoeuvres to be proud of. There may also be times when your Crew simply needs to Bail out and survive to face another day. Some events are randomly generated to help add to the story, but you will usually have at least some degree of control over the level of risk to take and how vulnerable you leave your Tank to these events.

The Hellcat was a quick and relatively powerful tank destroyer. However, its armour left quite a lot to be desired. With this in mind, tactics often required crews to spot the enemy first, and use speed to surprise them and escape before any return fire came their way.

The game involves:
Armour ratings, firepower ratings, Tigers, Panthers, Panzer IVs, Pumas, infantry, movement, firing, MGs, ambushing, defensive manoeuvres, spotting, loading, repairs, fires, damage, snipers, mines, mechanical problems, artillery, Stukas, StuG IIIs, civilians, reinforcements, bailing out, buildings, trees, roads, hedgerows, water and so on.

How the game works
This is a solitaire hex and counter type wargame. You play the game, and the enemy is controlled by the game. You will need at least 6, but preferably a dozen or so, standard six-sided dice. 12 full colour Battle maps and suggested counters are provided (to be stuck to thin card and cut out).

The game has very good replayability due to the random nature of enemy appearance and actions, as well as end of turn events.

—description from the designer

Since ancient times, the Dreamlands have been the refuge of the dreamers: a vast dream world where the mythical city of Kadath lies hidden. The dreamer Randolph Carter penetrates into these lands in search of the lost city, while the Crawling Chaos Nyarlathotep does everything possible to divert him, sowing chaos in his path. Will you protect the purity of the dream, or drag it into the eternal abyss?

Kadath, is a game where each player places tiles in a 5x5 square grid, trying to form groups of four tiles from the same side to create enclaves on the board, whether they belong to the Dreamer or Chaos. The first player to create 5 enclaves and place their 5 enclave tokens  on the playing area wins.

Bamse's friends Nalle-Maja, Skalman, Farmor, and Lille Skutt are going on adventures, visiting the Pyramids, Atlantis, the Moon, and Loch Ness. They need assistance packing their own stuff as well as the stuff that is important to get to the place they are going.

They also need to get the right transportation (boat, submarine, helicopter or space rocket).

The trip can begin when the suitcase is packed, but they also need to get Bamse to come with them. Whoever gets to their destination first, wins.

Roll the dice and place the two yellow start/end pieces on the corresponding locations. Play solo or race an opponent to make the connection between the two positions and fill up the gameboard with the remaining puzzle pieces. The Genius Connection challenges you to outsmart your opponent in this head-to-head race. Enjoy endless random dice challenges or choose a difficulty level ranging from starter to expert from the challenge cards included.

It's hot in Vegas. Not just the temperature, or the action at the casinos, but tempers are flaring up across the city and alliances are being formed. Your goal was to win over the right people, but your rival is going for them as well. From the bunkers to the rooftops to the media, you'll need everyone you can to win this war.

Objective
Win over the right people in a city-wide conflict by winning battles at different settings using a standard deck of playing cards.

Components
9 Setting Cards (game-specific)
1 Standard Deck of Playing Cards (no jokers)

Setup
Choose 3 setting cards and place them in a row between the players.
Remove jokers from the deck.
Shuffle and deal the deck evenly between both players (face-down piles).
Players do not look at their cards.

Gameplay
Each round, players:
Draw 1 card from their deck.
Secretly assign it face-down to one of the 3 settings.
Repeat until each player has placed 1 card at each setting.
Reveal cards at each setting (left to right).
Determine the winner at each setting based on its specific rule.
The winner takes all cards played to that setting.

War (Tiebreaker)
If there's a tie at a setting:
Each player draws 3 cards face-down.
Then reveal the next top card of their deck.
That card is judged by the setting’s rule.
Winner takes all cards played to that setting.

Special Setting Effects
Some settings may affect the next turn.
Rotate the setting card to face the impacted player as a reminder.

Game End
If a player runs out of cards, the game ends immediately.
The other player is declared the winner.

Get ready for a pancake-flipping, card-grabbing food frenzy! In Me Want Pancakes! from Gamewright, players shout out their desired food, toss matching cards into the center, and try to grab the set if at least four of the same food appear. The first player to collect 10 cards of the same color wins! With cute illustrations, simple rules, and fast-paced action, this game delivers joy-filled moments and competitive fun for the whole family. Can you stack up enough dishes—or will someone else beat you to delicious victory?


     QUICK-PLAYING CARD GAME FILLED WITH FOOD-THEMED FUN: Each turn, a player calls out a food from their hand, and any matching cards from other players must be placed in the center. If at least four of the same food appear, the caller gets to collect them as points! With fast turns, unpredictable card reveals, and plenty of excitement, every round becomes a deliciously fun race to victory.



     EASY TO LEARN WITH SIMPLE RULES FOR KIDS: Me Want Pancakes! is designed for quick gameplay with minimal setup, making it an ideal card game for kids and adults alike. Players of all ages can jump in and start playing within minutes, ensuring an enjoyable experience for families, friends, and even newbie gamers.



     ADORABLY DELICIOUS ILLUSTRATIONS: Each card features charming and colorful food illustrations, bringing personality to the game. Whether it’s smiling pancakes, cheesy pizzas, or sweet cupcakes, the vibrant visuals make Me Want Pancakes! just as fun to look at as it is to play.



     PORTABLE AND IDEAL FOR TRAVEL, GAME NIGHTS, AND FAMILY FUN: With its compact size and easy-to-carry box, Me Want Pancakes! is great for playing at home or on the go. Whether you're heading to a family gathering, birthday party, vacation, or just looking for a quick and entertaining card game, it fits conveniently into any travel bag or game shelf.



     PERFECT CHOICE FOR FAMILIES AND FANS OF FAST-PACED GAMES: Perfect for kids, parents, and multi-generational family game nights, Me Want Pancakes! is an excellent choice for those who enjoy quick, action-packed games that feature a mix of luck and strategy. It also makes a great birthday, holiday, or special occasion gift for kids ages 5+.


—description from the publisher

Tayog is an abstract strategy board game for two opposing players who control bird pieces namely Manok, Bibe, Lawin, and Agila in an 8x8 checker board, competing for being the first player to successfully do one of any of the following objectives: Capture all of their opponents exposed Manok pieces, have at least eight Manok pieces in their reserve during their turn, or make their opponent unable to do any valid move during their turn.

After the setting up phase, players alternatingly take turns choosing to either Rally, Perch or Capture to try to achieve any of the aforementioned goals. Rally means to either place a Manok at an empty spot adjacent to another Manok or stack a non-Manok piece from the reserve on top of an exposed allied piece exactly one rank lower. Perch simply means to move a Manok piece to an adjacent empty square or a non-Manok piece using its valid directional movement on top of another allied piece that is one rank lower. Lastly, to Capture is to take an opponents piece of the same rank or one rank lower by occupying the space it was originally on and effectively taking all the pieces underneath it, flipping it and keeping it as a reserve so that it can be used as a piece to be Rallied later in the game.

Manok or chicken is the lowest ranked piece in the game and can only move to any of its eight adjacent squares. Bibe or duck is a rank higher above the Manok and can move as many spaces as it wants horizontally and vertically as long as there is a Bibe or Manok to capture and perch on to. One rank higher is the Lawin or Hawk, and it can move as many spaces diagonally and similarly can only perch or capture a Lawin or Bibe. The highest piece which is an Agila or Eagle can move as many spaces horizontally, vertically, and diagonally but can only capture an Agila or a Hawk.

Additionally, players may not move a piece they have just used and must put an Exhaustion Marker on top of it after every turn. The said piece may still be Perched, Captured, or Rallied by another piece since only the bird piece used cannot be moved on two consecutive turns. Lastly, non-Manok pieces may not pass through an opponents piece if its rank is higher but may do so over their allied pieces.

Meripeli ("Sea game") is spin and move game where you gather food, and then head to breeding-ground. First animal to reach it's breeding-ground with enough food wins the game. You get the food from the random tiles you collect from the spaces you reach on the board. Some of the tiles have questions on them, if you answer correctly, you get to take another tile.

The animals in the game are Salmon, Pike, Otter and Seal. The Fishes need only 8 points worth of food and the Mammals need 60 points worth of food. All the food in the game are fishes. The Fish players can only eat small fishes (2 points). The Mammal players can eat also the bigger fishes (20 or 30 points).

When a Mammal and a Fish land on the same space, the Mammal player can try to catch the Fish by spinning a wheel (1-6). If Mammal gets 4 or 6, the catch is successful and Mammal gets 2 points worth of food. The Fish goes back to it's starting place and it doesn't loose the collected food points. If the catch fails, then Mammal player has to pass next two rounds. Both Fishes can only be caught once during the game.

boardgame: Hextend: The Circuitous Game of Hex Connections
description:
With visceral tactical and tactile gameplay, 2-6 players take turns drawing wooden hex pieces, placing each piece at the end of their HEXtend trail snaking from their corner of the HEXboard toward the center hex.

Players can also place pieces on their opponents’ trails to impede their advancement. An intense battle ensues as players strive to reach the center and claim victory.

Alliances, truces and vendetta’s constantly shift as players use “co-opetition” to balance cooperative, mutually beneficial gameplay along with their own competitive advancement. The first player to the center hex wins!

BENEFITS:


     Learn to play in 30 seconds
     Simple premise with profound strategic gameplay
     Satisfying tactile experience
     2-6 players
     Prompts “co-oppetition” with ever-changing player alliances
     Strategic mix of competitive and cooperative gameplay
     Solid-wood game components with precision thermal print


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Decimate: The Speedy Multi-Player Solitaire Game
description:
Learn it quickly. Play it even quicker. 
The more you play, the quicker it gets.

With constant, frenetic, fast-paced action, 2-4 players must successfully redistribute their Decimate Pile into the 16 Build Piles of zero-to-10 cards in four colored suits onto the huge 20x20” gameboard.

Any player can also play from any of their four “on-deck” cards as well, all while also trying to track what they’re opponents are playing. Players combine hand-eye coordination and dexterity to play their cards more quickly than their opponents.

The player who depletes their Decimate Pile first ends the round, but all players get points based on how many cards they played into the Build Piles, so anyone can win. Players track their points on the nifty, tactile 10x2” deluxe wooden scoreboard. Whichever player reaches 100 points first, wins!

All four full-poker-sized decks are made of award-winning, high-quality, waterproof, tear- proof DuraDeckTM material, allowing high-intensity hand-to-hand combat card-playing. With the perfect combination of quick-brain-thinking and highly-dexterous, hectic, real-world, tactile gameplay, it’s fast-paced fun for everyone!

BENEFITS:

     Frenetic, fast-paced fun 
     Gameplay tests players’ quick thinking ability and dexterity 
     Improves hand-eye coordination 
     Satisfying tactile experience 
     Quality waterproof & tear-proof DuraDeckTM playing cards 
     Quality hardwood cribbage board 
     2-4 players


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blocked & Loaded: The Quickdraw Game of Guts and Glory
description:
Real World Old School Fun!

In the classic tradition of Rock Paper Scissors and the card game War, Blocked & Loaded gives kids a fun, simple game that they learn quickly and want to play again and again.

The gameplay prompts human interactivity and observation of each others’ strategies and “tells” to predict how each other will play round-to-round.

It’s fundamentally a game of “chicken,” where players have to exhibit guts and grit to put their opponents on edge before their opponents outwit them!

The winner of each round is honored by being presented a Shield of Commendation. Whoever has the most Shields after 10 rounds wins the game!

—descriptio from the publisher

boardgame: Zip Zilch Zero: Go to Zero to Become a Hero
description:
Space Age Card Game Makes Math Amusing!

Fans of the original Star Wars trilogy will recall Han Solo playing Sabacc with Lando Calrissian on the Millennium Falcon. Zip Zilch Zero is similar in that the object of the game is to get as close to zero as possible by the end of each round.

The deck is comprised of only two suits—red and black. The red cards are negative integers and the black cards are positive integers. Both suits have two sets of cards ranging from zero to 10. There are also a few zero value cards in the deck.

GAMEPLAY: Players draw and trade cards over several rounds, and by the end of each round, each player’s hand must consist of at least two cards, and whoever’s hand of cards totals closest to zero by the end of the round wins! 
Seven game modes make the game map to different kids’ ages and stages.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Deep Rock Galactic: Horrors of Hoxes
The Rank Game: After Dark Edition
description:
“The Gamification of Conversation” for Adults Only

For college students, young professionals & other party-game-lovers
    Gets people off their phones and interacting irl
    Easy to play ––Learn in 30 seconts
    Play a 3 minute round or play for hours
    Works with 2 or 30+ people
    Quality game components in bright colors
    Double-sided cards for 360-degree viewing

The super-fun ADULT party game about YOU, your life and your people. Rank your favorite things on the 160 topic cards and opponents must guess your order to win points. Get to know each other better or play ice-breakler mode with a new group of peeps.

This SPECIAL18+ ADULT EDITION includes all the main game components and The Rank Game's 4 most popular category packs for adults: Risky Business, Girls Night, Guys Night, and Relationship Goals. It also includes a BONUS SNEAK PEAK of 12 full rounds of Storyastic's new party game, Really Good Really Bad!

GAMEPLAY IS EASY! The "Ranker" writes their preferred order of the four items listed on the played Topic Card. Other players must guess the Ranker's preferred order. Every right item is a point and the player or team with the most points after 12 rounds wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Really Good Really Bad: The Get to Know You Guess & Rate Game
description:
The YES & NO and 
GET-TO-KNOW YOU
game where you
LOVE & HATE
and
GUESS & RATE

In the tradition of the award-winning, best-selling The Rank Game comes another super-fun party game about WHAT YOU & YOUR PEOPLE LOVE (or HATE). You rate your favorite (or least favorite) things on the 378 topic cards and your pals have to guess your ratings to win points.

Whether you’ve known each other for a long time or you’re just breaking the ice, you’ll feel seen and included while having an absolute blast!

GAMEPLAY IS EASY! The "Rater" draws a card and writes on their scorecard whether they rate the topic as “Really Good,” “Good,” “Meh,” “Bad,” or “Really Bad.” Other players must guess the Rater's rating. Every right item is a point and the player or team with the most points after 12 rounds wins!

4 GAME MODES: Ice Breaker; Spotlight; Teams of Two (like the Newlywed Game); Two Big Teams (like Family Feud).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Brandish: The Fun & Funny Fake Your Friends Game
description:
Remember the best-selling game Balderdash, where players guess the true meaning of arcane words or make up their own definition to get other players to vote for their “fictionary” definition? Brandish features the same exciting, creative gameplay but features real company logos which players likely will not recognize.

Drawing a card featuring an arcane logo, players must guess the brand or make up a brand name and state their brand promise (what the company does). Some players just go for goofy jokes, and that’s all part of the fun.

Authentic brand or slight of hand? Players get points for every vote for their fictitious brand, and whoever has the most votes after 10 rounds wins! Fact or Fiction, It’s Fun and Addicting!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: KnitPic: The Fast & Furious Photo Mashup Game
description:
The fast and furious photo mashup game

Players must “knit” pics to other pics in a way that their opponents agree is a good match. Three different game modes gives customers three fun games for the price of one.

(1) NITPICK MODE is an easy Apples-to-Apples-like gameplay where players submit cards to the judge who picks the best match. Play your picture card most associated with the Nit Picker’s (judge) card. The Nitpicker picks their favorite and that player wins the round!

(2) NITPIC MODE lets any player quickly play one of their 10 pics to the previous pic before their opponents do, building a collaborative “daisy chain” of photos without getting challenged by their opponents who yell, “NITPIC! That’s not a legit knit to the pic!” But if the challenger loses the challenge, they lose points.

(3) KNITPIC MODE features a more relaxed gameplay where players build out a grid of matching pics in the style of dominoes. This mode is a similar vibe to building puzzles and can be played competitively or collaboratively. It’s a Sunday afternoon with the game on the TV vibe, and a cozy fire in the fireplace.

—description from the publisher

The Speakeasy Upgrade Pack adds new content and upgraded components to your copy of Speakeasy. Originally offered during the Kickstarter campaign, this upgrade pack adds variety and expands the game with new player powers, goals, solo content, and quality-of-life upgrades.

Cosmetic Upgrades:

     20 Wooden Leverage tokens
     1 Embroidered Cloth bag
     4 City Docks Reference cards


Extra Gameplay Content

     14 Solo Achievement cards
     18 Once per Act Power cards
     24 Once per Game Power cards
     12 Variable Power cards
     16 Public Goal tiles


The upgrade pack was included in the Kickstarter version of the game, but is also available as a separate item.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You can hit the slots without ever leaving the comforts of home with this reimagined, age-inclusive game from Ageless Innovation. Made in partnership with Hasbro, Yahtzee Slots takes the beloved classic and adds casino twists that are perfect for the next family game night.

Forget rolling the dice. Trade your dice cups for the Yahtzee-themed slot machine, which rolls the dice for you and lets you win more from your turns. By loading the dice and pulling the lever, players can test their luck to receive special bonuses, including the chance to get an instant Yahtzee!

This reimagined twist features three ways to play, including Yahtzee Slots, classic Yahtzee, and a fun and quick game called Dice Drop. Yahtzee Slots comes with an oversized score sheet, a rulebook, and a game log for players to keep track of their past game nights.

YAHTZEE MEETS SLOT MACHINE MAGIC! Ageless Games Yahtzee Slots combines classic dice-rolling fun with exciting casino-style action! Spin the tabletop slot machine for bonus rolls, extra Yahtzees, and surprise rewards—all from the comfort of home. Perfect for seniors who love dice games, slots, and family game nights alike!

SENIOR-FRIENDLY DESIGN: Designed with older adults in mind! Featuring large print scorecards, easy-to-read rules, and simple gameplay mechanics. An engaging game that keeps cognitive skills sharp while delivering endless entertainment for elderly players.

3 WAYS TO PLAY—ENDLESS FUN! Enjoy classic Yahtzee, the new Slot Machine version, or the quick-play Dice Drop game. With options for different types of play, it's cognitively ideal for seniors, family gatherings, or solo play. The included Friends & Family Game Log helps preserve precious memories!

—description from publisher
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray

description:
A yokocho is an alleyway in Japan that’s lined with tiny bars, restaurants, and shops.  They’re a popular part of Japanese nightlife.

In this game, you help eight friends hit all the bars in a yokocho and have a great time.  You use standard playing cards to represent the bars, the patrons, and the drinks.  Have the patrons invite one another to the bars, and draw cards from the drink deck to see whether they're enjoying themselves or need to step outside for some fresh air.  Use 8mm cubes to keep track of which bars they've visited and how happy they are.

Have the perfect night out, but don't take too long.  The spirits are visiting the bars, too.  And once they're done bar-hopping, it's closing time!

—description from the designer

UNSPEAKABLE FUN FOR OLDER ADULTS: Taboo Charades brings classic word guessing fun with a senior-friendly twist! Perfect for older adults, this simple card game encourages players to act, guess, and laugh together. With nostalgic words from the '60s and '70s, it’s easy to relate to, making it a hit for seniors seeking engaging and lively group activities.

EASY-TO-READ, LARGE PRINT CARDS: Designed with aging eyes in mind, Taboo Charades features bigger, bolder text for effortless reading. Whether you're guessing or acting, the larger print ensures that everyone can stay focused on the fun without straining their vision, making it ideal for seniors with low vision.

GET ACTIVE WITH IMPROV: Who says games should just be sitting around? Taboo Charades adds a unique twist by including charades cards that get older adults moving and acting—no talking allowed! It’s a perfect way to add light physical activity and laughter to any game night, encouraging elderly players to stay active and engaged.

CUSTOMIZABLE & NOSTALGIC FUN: With relatable words from beloved decades, Taboo Charades offers a customizable experience that seniors can make their own. Add personal words or family inside jokes to make the game more meaningful. It's multigenerational fun that brings families together, from teens to grandparents, for timeless memory-boosting entertainment.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: Okay, Duckies, Now Let's Get In Formation
description:
You’re a mallard who dreams of being Madonna—A canvasback who yearns to be the next Celine Dion—A blue-winged teal looking to make it big like Beyoncé. To get there, you must learn all the dance formations of a true diva. But your only dance partners are rubber duckies! Rustle up your feathers, get in formation, and become the next Mariah Duck-arey!

In Okay, Duckies, Now Let’s Get In Formation, players take turns adding rubber duckies to a bathtub aiming to arrange the duckies into delightful formations. This crunchy spatial puzzle for two players will have you searching for patterns of duckies on an ever changing board.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Edge of the Knife
description:
In the corpse of Embergard, glory and honour bow to the need for victory. A knife in the back here, a throttling grip from the darkness there – inglorious reversals of fortune are not merely rife, but the order of the day.

Edge of the Knife is a complete and ready-to-play Rivals deck, usable by any warband in your games of Warhammer Underworlds . Just open the pack, shuffle the cards, and you're good to go! The deck is classed under the Flex playstyle, which combines two or more playstyles, encouraging a varied approach to play, and would work well with a Flex-style warband. Use these cards on their own, or mix and match them with another deck to create a custom Nemesis format deck.

This deck contains a 33-card universal Rivals deck, consisting of:

     1x Plot Card
     12x Objective Cards
     10x Ploy Cards
     10x Upgrade Cards


Each card measures 63mm x 88mm.

—description from the publisher

The super fun sight word game - teaches words that are essential to reading. Jump into action! Each card in Kangaroo Cravings includes a fun, multi-sensory action to build mastery with high-frequency sight words.

This game can be played in cooperative or competitive mode along the map where each plays will lead a kangaroo down the map toward the pizza through each successful read of sight word and the action on the card drawn from the deck. The cooperative mode is won by getting at least 1 kangaroo to the pizza before the raccoon who moves according to the action number on the card drawn.

The cards are divided into 3 decks ("level of difficulties"), each with different color backs. Irregular sounds are marked with red letters. There are one of 9 multi-sensory actions to promote long term memory learning.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: Duel for Middle-Earth – Allies
description:
The Lord of the Rings: Duel for Middle-Earth – Allies features various characters from J.R.R. Tolkien's universe who will come to the aid of either the Fellowship or Sauron.

Collect power tokens in each chapter and use them to call upon your allies and benefit from their effects.

All three turtles must work together to achieve the goal of floating in the water before the "Bright Sun" puzzle is complete. Individual players share the turtles; no one gets their own.

Roll the die to decide player action - move the turtle 1-3 steps, scoop and add water to the rising sea board, or add a puzzle piece to the "Bright Sun" puzzle.

The host or hostess places out cards around the house and the guests are encouraged to seek them out and answer as many clues as they can find.
There are 30 clue cards. Each answer will be connected with a well-known game or sport. Example: Word = Boundary: Answer = cricket.
First to get a correct complete card wins or, if no winner is claimed, the guest with most correct answers wins.

24 cards are placed around the house at a party. Each guest is given an answer card and they are encouraged to look around and write down their answer on the answer card. Each card around the house features a jumble of letters which can be rearranged to form the name of a flower or plant.
First to answer all 24 correctly wins. If no such winner, the player with the most correct answer wins.

Each guest is given a card to write down their answers 24 questions with feature clues to various plants you might see in a garden.
First guest to get all 24 correct wins or, in the event no-one gets all the answers, the guest with the most correct answers wins.
Published by the UK Chemist Boots.

boardgame: Frosthaven: Curse of the Lost Cabal (Promo Scenario)
description:
This is a digital, single-scenario promo item for Frosthaven.

The scenario was available as part of the 2023 Secret Cabal Kickstarter campaign.

boardgame: Rock Paper Scissors: Crystal Ball Variant
description:
Each player receives a card with two sides: "Same" and "Different". Before playing a round of Rock, Paper, Scissors, players secretly choose a side of the card to indicate their prediction—whether both players will throw the same hand or different ones. After concealing their prediction, they play Rock, Paper, Scissors with their free hand. Once the round concludes, players reveal their predictions.

Points are awarded in two ways: 1 point for winning the Rock, Paper, Scissors round, and additional points for a correct prediction — 1 point for predicting "Different" correctly, and 2 points for predicting "Same" correctly.

The objective is to accumulate the most points over a series of rounds.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Convention Promo Pack 2025
description: DC Deck-Building Game: Convention Promo Pack 2025 is a four-card pack available at Gen Con 2025 that contains Superman-related characters: Supergirl, Krypto, Brainiac, and General Zod.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Tim Drake/Tech Detective
description: DC Deck-Building Game: Tim Drake/Tech Detective is a set of two cards first available at Gen Con 2025 that could be obtained by participating in a DC DBG event.

CHOMP YOUR WAY TO VICTORY with multiplayer PAC-MAN action! Relive the arcade magic in this fast-paced, battle to master the maze in this officially PAC-MAN& Bandai Namco Entertainment licensed adventure! Each player controls their very own PAC-MAN, gobbling up PAC-DOTs, Fruit, and POWER PELLETS while dodging Ghosts and other rival PAC-MAN players. But beware, players also control the Ghosts, chasing down opponents and forcing them to lose lives! Activate power-ups, flip Ghosts to TURN-TO-BLUE mode, and rack up the highest score to become the ULTIMATE CHOMPION! Will your PAC-MAN claim the top spot? Add this beloved arcade classic to your collection of family board games for ultimate nostalgia!


     CLASSIC PAC-MAN FUN WITH A COMPETITIVE TWIST: Experience the nostalgia of the legendary arcade game in an exciting tabletop board game format! Players race their PAC-MAN through the maze, collecting PAC-DOTS and fruit while dodging Ghosts and rival PAC-MAN players. Outsmart opponents, plan your route, and earn points in this thrilling multiplayer showdown.


 

     MULTIPLAYER ACTION FOR 2-4 PLAYERS: Every player controls their own PAC-MAN! Quick decision-making and strategy are crucial as players race to collect PAC-DOTs, power up, and fight to stay alive! Designed for two to four players, it's an ideal choice for retro gaming fans and families looking for the next addition to their board game collection! 



     DODGE, CHOMP, OR CONTROL GHOSTS: This is not just a race to eat the most PAC-DOTs, players also strategically control the Ghosts! Carefully move BLINKY, PINKY, INKY, and CLYDE to block paths, chase down rival PAC-MAN players, and force them to lose valuable 1UP tokens, all while trying to escape! 



     POWER-UP FOR POINTS AND GAME-CHANGING MOVES: Enhance gameplay with power-up cards that give you extra movement, teleportation, or the ability to eat Ghosts. Each power-up changes the game, offering new ways to challenge opponents and climb to the top of the scoreboard! Every match plays differently, making for endless nostalgic, arcade-inspired fun! 



     CALLING ALL PAC-MAN FANS, RETRO GAMERS, AND ARCADE CLASSIC COLLECTORS: As an officially licensed PAC-MAN product, this game is a must-have for fans of the iconic franchise. Whether you grew up playing PAC-MAN in the arcade or are introducing your family or friends to the fun, Ultimate PAC-MAN delivers a nostalgic experience with a modern twist, making it a great addition to any games collection.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Rank Game On-the-Go: Culture Vulture
description:
NOTE: This pack works without the main game because it contains topic cards + rule book, score pad and card holder integrated into the plastic gift box. It's also an expansion to base game.Every card in this pack is only in this pack (except 3 of these appear in The Rank Game Greatest Hits).

Glam up The Rank Game with the "Culture Vulture" category deck of 50 topic cards about entertainment, celeb culture, pop culture, politics...all crazes hot and hip.

BONUS! Also includes 4 bonus surprise topic cards from another mystery category!

"A fun new game about your interests"
-FOXTV

description:
Play on-the-go with 54 of the best topic cards curated from 18 decks of The Rank Game!

With the massive success of The Rank Game, so many people asked for a "Greatest Hits" package that they can take on the go. This gave birth to, literally, The Rank Game Greatest Hits On-the-Go, which has three topic cards from each of the 18 decks of Topic Cards available for The Rank Game. (We also re-made every expansion pack into a standalone pack so you can take any Category Pack with you On-the-Go!)

This pack comes with the double-sided topic cards along with the full rule book and score pad PLUS the clear jewel box also has an integrated card holder you use during game play, so everyone can play together.

"A fun new game about your people's interest!"
-FOXTV

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Rank Game On-the-Go: Holly Jolly Christmas
description:
Share very merry Christmas cheer from Thanksgiving to New Years!

This On-The-Go pack WORKS ON IT'S OWN or as an expansion to The Rank Game main/base game, the super-fun party game about YOU and your people–and your likes and dislikes.

Play The Rank Game this holiday season with your multi-generational group of family and friends, with 54 topic cards all dialed into the holly jolly things you do together in this most wonderful time of the year!

This pack comes with the double-sided topic cards along with the full rule book and score pad PLUS the clear jewel box also has an integrated card holder you use during game play, so everyone can play together.

WOODEN FAMILY FUN BALANCING GAME
The Penguin Stack Up Wooden Balancing game is a fun take on a classic block stacking game. This game from Mudpuppy is fun for all ages, 4-99 years, and features a sturdy wooden penguin base and 40 colorful wooden sticks for stacking.

HOW TO PLAY:

     Take a stick and stack it on the top. But be careful - don’t let the penguin lose its balance!
     This game is a great way to foster fine motor skills, manual dexterity, patience and focus, as well as decision making.


—description from the publisher

Hot diggity dog!

Warm up your paws and get ready to race and roll with the Wiener Dog Race Game! In this easy-to-learn "WIENER-TAKES-ALL" dice game, you FETCH and ROLL two dice from the bag, then add the best one to your dog to stretch them out. Collect yard cards to lengthen your lead and win! Includes 4 adorable dice-collecting dogs.

How to play:

     Fetch dice from the bag
     Roll & collect to stretch your dog
     Collect yard cards to win!


—description from the publisher

This dynamic game requires creativity, excellent vocabulary, and reflexes! Your task is to form a word from the available letters as quickly as possible – but be warned! It won't be easy, as the letter layout changes with each round, and additional dice can help… or make the task more difficult!

How to play?


     In each round, 5 random letter cards are placed on the table.
     The player rolls two basic letter dice.
     The task for all participants is to quickly create a word that uses as many of the available letters as possible – those from the cards and the dice.
     Want to spice up the gameplay? You can add one of two special dice:
     Easy – containing frequently used letters that help form words.
     Hard – with letters like "ś," "ć," or "ź," which require more skill and vocabulary!


The first person to come up with a correct word and shout "I've got a word!" wins a point!

Immerse yourself in a prehistoric world with Dinofarma, where little paleontologists care for adorable dinosaurs. Whoever collects the most dinosaurs on their farm wins!

Game rules: 21 tiles depicting colorful dinosaurs are spread out on the table. One player rolls two dice, each with a different color on each side. The goal of all participants is to find and capture the dinosaur tile of the color indicated by the dice as quickly as possible.

Dinofarma guarantees great fun for the whole family, regardless of age!

Tokyo Highway Driver's License is a stand-alone speed matching game for players who are familiar with the rules of Tokyo Highway, as well as for those looking to learn the rules of Tokyo Highway. Test your abilities in recognizing the roads and car placements in this quiz-style game which consists of 20 cards. Can you solve the answer the quickest?

Shuffle the highway cards to randomly create the road conditions. Players need to examine the highway layout, and be the fastest to answer correctly to earns points. Simply flip over a new card to reveal the next layout prompt! The player with the most points after several rounds is the winner.

—description form the designer

You are a dwarf looking looking for the legendary gemstones in The Living Mountain, a feared mining place where old tunnels close regularly but a huge reward awaits.

Components

     Tokens of any kinds, can be cubes, maples, gemstones...
         5 for gemstones
         3 for game levels
         1 for shields
         5 for health points
         2 for bombs
    


Gameplay overview:

After shuffling the deck - flipping some cards in the process, since all cards are two sided, the player has to draw a card each round and place it vertically, horizonally or diagonally in a way that it'll match the last placed card.

Since the four general direction - horizontal and vertical - are associated with one or zero token on each card, you can for example: gather a gemstone, or in worst case, face a danger and lose health.

While going through the deck again and again - basically going deeper in the mountain - your goal is to find 5 gemstones before either dying or reaching the end of level 3 - going through 3 times the whole deck.

—description from the designer

Hello fellow Human! In a world infiltrated with AI characters, prove you are the true Human by getting rid of as much AI as (humanly) possible. The player left with the most Humans at the end of the game is the winne... err, true Human.

Am I Human Game tasks players to gather sets of AI characters, to get rid of them! Get out of here AI goop! Only then can you save our fellow humans. But, the generative goop can also generate some awesome combos, depending on which card of the AI set is being played. You can even save Superhumans if you build a whole set of AI.

The Goal: To bank as many Human cards as possible. The catch? You can only bank Human cards if there are no other cards left in your hand. Play actions and AI suits to clear your hand. You can play a row of 3 or more AI cards on the table or add AI cards to an existing row.

End Game: When the draw pile is depleted, each player will have one more turn to clear their hand to bank humans and earn Super Humans. The player with the most banked Human cards at the end of the game is the winner.


    No AI was used or abused in the making of this game, only free-range, ethically-treated humans were utilised in the making of this product.

The Radiation Game is a simple game of trying to stay alive. You take turns picking up cards, occasionally taking radiation points, and using action cards for some exciting twists. Once you’ve built up 10 radiation points, you have taken too much radiation damage and can no longer win, but any future radiation cards you pick up, gets passed onto the person with the least radiation! The last person to not reach the deadly 10 radiation points, wins!

Maneuver through a series of levels in the forest, trying to gather food, weapons, & lost coins. But watch out for the monsters of the woods, for they can derail your plans. Survive your trek with a sacred discovery in hand, & you win!

All you need is a standard deck of playing cards.

Deal yourself an inventory of health, coins, & weapons to start. Flip cards from the draw pile to enter new levels in the wilderness. If you encounter certain numbers, bad guys appear. Defeat them with weapons from your inventory, or give up some health & run away. Certain flipped cards represent coins for you to collect on that level. Levels that match top cards in your inventory allow you to collect more of those items. Pairs of drawn cards in a row can mean you're at a crossroads, which can be good (warp world) or bad (pitfall). Other combinations of successive level numbers can bring you huge weapons & special items.

Survive by playing through the entire deck without losing all of your health. The final card is the final boss. You must beat him by 3 points.

If you win, you also receive special reputable titles as badges of honor for special items collected on your trek.

—description from the designer

Ship Hapens is a thrilling cooperative board game where kids and parents join forces as space explorers repairing a ship in distress.

Roll the dice, wander ship compartments, collect tools and fix crises appearing all over the ship. Collaborate with other players and make it to the end of the galaxy, while preventing the ship from exploding.

With simple, yet exciting gameplay, clear rules and artwork approved by kids it's a kids friendly fun for the whole family.

Can your brave crew survive the journey to another galaxy?

—description from the designer

Civolution: Acceptance Letter is a small expansion pack for Civolution that features 3 Extra Credits. Extra Credits are small add-ons that can be used with the base game either individually or mixed together.

Extra Credit A: 6 starting attribute chips
At the start of the game, each player drafts a starting attribute chip, which is installed in their console and gives them an individual starting benefit.

Extra Credit B: 6 creature chips
Each creature belongs to one territory type and can be tamed by performing a hunting action in that territory type. Once tamed, the creature chip is installed in one’s console. As with most other cards and chips, creature chips must go in a column of their own. Each creature likes to eat the resources of its territory, which you can feed to them in the feeding phase in exchange for benefits.

Extra Credit C: 15 level-4 module tiles
For each of the 15 main modules, there is one powerful additional tile for level 4. At the beginning of the game, each player drafts three of them. Accordingly, during the game, they can upgrade each of these three modules up to level 4 if they manage to.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The game is played using a special twelve-sided die, containing several three digit numbers on the sides. The player who rolled the die chooses one digit of the rolled number, and everyone must write down that number on any square on their 6*6-grid. There is also a NUMERO- and a X-side on the die. When NUMERO is rolled the player who rolled it, can choose any number from 0 to 9 for everybody to write down. If X-side is rolled the player looses their turn. After the roll the die goes to the next player.

When the sheets are filled, every players counts the lines with at least three of the same consecutive numbers (diagonals are allowed). For every line you get as many points as there are numbers on the line. Winner is the player who got most points.

THE SWEETEST CARD GAME
The Great Intergalactic Pastries-Eating Contest has begun! Every year, creatures from across the galaxy gather for an epic challenge: who can devour the most pastries and claim the ultimate title of champion?
But beware... it won’t be that easy! Along the way, you’ll face sneaky traps, spoiled buns, cunning opponents, and the dreaded Monster Chefs, who will stop at nothing to sabotage your victory.
Are you ready for the challenge?

Setup
Deal 3 cards to each player and place the deck in the center.

HOW TO PLAY
Players take turns playing a card and placing their buns in one of the three decks in the center of the table. If the total value of a deck reaches 12 or more, that player takes it!
Use Monster Chefs to gain an advantage with their special powers!
If you play a bun with the number 2, you get another turn! First.

Pile rules:
Each pile can only have buns of the same color.
Spoiled buns are special, they can go on any pile.
The feast comes to an end!
When the main deck runs out, players play their remaining cards, and the game ends. Then, they add up the points from their buns and subtract the points from spoiled bun.
The player with the most points is the champion!

In Hollywood Dream, players enter the glamorous and competitive world of filmmaking. You'll acquire scripts, recruit actors, secure special effects and equipment, and race to release award-winning movies. Strategic planning, timing, and adaptability are key.  The gameplay will feel familiar to fans of Viticulture. Drawing inspiration from that title, Hollywood Dream introduces fresh mechanics and new layers of decision-making aimed at enhancing the worker placement experience.

At the beginning of the game, players choose two out of four unique investors — similar in spirit to Terraforming Mars' Prelude cards — each offering different starting resources and a preferred film genre. Matching your released films to those genre preferences will earn you bonus victory points.

The game revolves around acquiring and completing high-value Movie Scripts, utilizing auctions, managing bonuses, and aiming for Academy Awards in Rounds 3 and 5. Multiple strategic paths are available — whether you're pushing ahead with risky loans (which carry end-game penalties) or playing it safe and steady, there's room for various play styles.

—description from the designer

Haribo - Save the Gummi Bears

No Chance for the Gummi biter

When the Gummi Bears meet to paddle at the Pirate Lake, not a single eye remains dry. They form groups and happily anticipate the paddling fun. But suddenly the cheeky Gummi Biter appears -
and for some it all starts again. For only those who are lucky and react lightning fast will be the first to get their rubber boats onto the water.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

This is a set collection game. First you have to collect four card of the same color, when that is done, you put them on the side. After that you collect set of three cards from another color. Then two cards and then one card of the remaining color. First player who collects the all four sets, wins the game.

On your turn you take one card from the draw pile and then you play one card from your hand of four cards in to table. You can only play cards with the color of the set you are collecting at the moment (or Gummi Biter card). If you can't play a card then you have to discard one, then it's the next players turn.

There are two kinds of Gummi Biter cards in the deck the one color Gummi Biter and the multi color Gummi Biter. When you play a Gummi Biter card you have to shout 'Gummi Biter', then other players have to react fast and the last one not to slap the Gummi Biter looses their set in progress. The already made sets are save. If played Gummi Biter is multi colored, then everybody must take part in the slapping. If it's one colored, then only the players who have that colored set in making takes part. If you make a mistake with color you also loose your set.

boardgame: Shovel Knight: Dungeon Duels – Fish Head
description: Fish Head is a new playable character for Shovel Knight: Dungeon Duels. This pack comes with the Fish Head model, Fish Head standee, 1 boss AI deck, and 1 hero/boss card.

A word-explaining card game inspired by Alias. It's a team game where one team member must get the other team member to say the word that is written on card by using sentences to describe the word. In Poli-Seli all the words are somehow related to politics in Finland (i.a. politicians, parties, legislation terms, history and so on). The player has one minute time to explain as many words as he/she can and then it's the next teams turn to explain and guess. First team to reach agreed amount of words wins the game.

The Glasgow Train Robbery is a co-operative game for two players inspired by one of the most infamous crimes in British history. Set in 1960s Scotland, you and your partner take on the roles of two key members of a criminal crew determined to intercept and rob a high-value train — without getting caught.

Over the course of the game, you'll work together to plan the heist, gather the right equipment, mislead the authorities, and finally execute the robbery in a high-stakes finale. Communication is limited, tension is high, and every decision counts. Will you manage to synchronize your actions, stay ahead of the investigation, and escape with the loot? Or will a single misstep derail the whole operation?

Designed for tight cooperation and replayability, The Glasgow Train Robbery challenges players with unexpected twists and a constantly shifting landscape of risks and rewards. The game is played over an undetermined number of turns, ending successfully if you manage to complete all five plan cards before the train reaches London, with the Coordinator needing to complete the first four, and the Operator the final one. Fail to complete the plan in time or leave too many clues behind, then the police will catch you and you'll lose your freedom...and the game.

The Glasgow Train Robbery is the first title in a new series of games inspired by the most legendary heists in history. Each game will transport players to a different time and place, challenging them to work together under pressure to pull off high-stakes robberies — from daring museum thefts to notorious bank jobs.
