With a focus on historical flavor, immersive gameplay, and tight teamwork, this series invites players to step into the shoes of infamous criminals to see whether they can outsmart the law.

—description from the publisher

Players embark on a thrilling journey to build and then dominate a map. Settle hexagonal tiles reminiscent of Catan and strategically expand your territories like in Carcassonne, and engage in intense battles like in Risk to conquer rival kingdoms.

Every turn, you can deploy a land tile, a soldier, or both. It's like Carcassonne but with more complicated tiles and the added threat of land stealing.

Tiles can be placed in any orientation, but they must connect to at least one edge of an already placed tile. Soldiers can be placed on any land with the aim of ruling within a boundary. Until a boundary is closed, the player receives no points.

The goal is to accumulate Bitcoin from controlling as many acres of land as possible. Players earn 1 Bitcoin for every 6 acres of land their soldiers secure. (For example if a soldier is in a completed boundary of 12 acres, you get 2 Bitcoin. If it’s 18 acres, you get 3 Bitcoin. Total acres are rounded down to the nearest 6.)

You may contest an opponent's land by placing your soldier on it. Then the opponent must either deploy another soldier to secure their land back or roll dice to have combat. The number on the dice represents bullets, the higher number wins.

If you win a contested battle, the opponent loses the bitcoin for that land and must pay you Bitcoin equal to the value of the contested land.

The game ends when the first player has placed all their soldiers or tiles. The player with the most Bitcoin wins.

—description from the designer

THE SERIES
The Fast Clash Series is a new line of games designed to be played quickly, replayed often, and always enjoyed. Streamlined rules with no downtime let players jump straight into the action. Each title focuses on early 20th-century (1900–1925) battles at tactical, operational, or strategic scale. Components are high-quality, with maps and game elements that are intuitive, visually appealing, and satisfying to handle—everything is crafted for a smooth, intense, and widely accessible gaming experience.

THE GAME
Verdun 1916 simulates the World War I battle between French and German forces, fought from February 21 to December 15, 1916.

COMPONENTS
The game includes a large map of the Verdun battlefield, two High Command player aids (one for each side, featuring loss and morale trackers), 30 position cards to be placed on the map, and 48 action cards. Units can be represented in two ways: with wooden blocks (short or long—blue for France, green for Germany) or using a counter sheet of 81 die-cut tokens. Two six-sided dice are also included.

SCALE
Infantry: A small block represents either a battalion (standard or Stosstruppen) or a group of 60 artillery pieces. A large block represents a regiment. Each time track increment equals approximately 10 days. The map covers an area of roughly 25 x 30 km.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A flourishing coral reef holds a bounty of new experiences for an intrepid ROVE, though navigating its obstacles requires careful planning.

This 6-card Mission Pack expansion for Aqua ROVE takes players through a coral reef. However, movement is much more limited, forcing playing to work around the reef’s restrictions. For a greater challenge, activate Endurance Mode and push yourself to complete 12 full missions!

Shuffle the 6 expansion cards into the starting deck, then set up and play as normal. Each of the new cards features more restricted movement options.

All Mission Packs also include Endurance Mode. When playing in this mode, set up with a hand of 5 cards. Complete up to 12 Missions, rather than the standard 7, with higher ranking for completing each additional mission.

The Twilight Stone has been stolen, and the chief needs your help to find out who stole it and where it is! Follow the clues, crack the codes, and solve the mystery- one case at a time! Only the cleverest detectives can solve the mystery - are you up for the challenge? This is a fun family cooperative game that is great for kids and families ages 8 and up.

     WHO STOLE THE TWILIGHT STONE? Help the chief solve the mystery in this fun and collaborative mystery game where you play as the detective.
     DECODE PUZZLES: Junior Detective Mysteries includes 8 evidence envelopes to unbox. In each envelope you will uncover detective tools and clues. Work collaboratively to decode the puzzles and solve the mystery - one case at a time!  
     EXPLORE CLUEVILLE WITH YOUR MAP: Solve puzzles to unlock new areas of Clueville. Travel to new locations to solve puzzles and uncover clues. 
     UNBOX NEW TOOLS: Each envelope includes a tool that will help you collect evidence and connect clues. Unbox tools and use them throughout the game to solve the mystery.
     COOPERATIVE ONE-TIME SOLVE GAME: In this single-play strategy game, work together to decode puzzles, uncover hidden secrets and solve the case. Play collaboratively with others or solo!
     FAMILY GAME NIGHT: Mystery games make great family gifts that are perfect for Family Game Night. Cooperative mystery board games for kids and families who love indoor games and collaborative play.


—description from the publisher

Chinese version of Beijing Daxing Airport, which is limited to crowdfunding.

Contains one postcard with two additional missions (Yellow and Black).

boardgame: Deep Rock Galactic: The Board Game – Shockwyrm Mini Expansion
description:
Watch out for the Shockwyrm!

The Shockwyrm is a never seen before enemy joining the boardgame version of Deep Rock Galactic.
This nasty piece of work shoots out of the ground, knocking over everything on its path. Carefull, its shock attacks might stun you!
It attacks both at range and up close, so nowhere is safe to hide!

This expansion comes with its own set of custom action event cards.

The miniature is 7,5 cm tall (3.0 inches).

Steaming broccoli isn't as easy as it looks!

Time it just right and cook up a perfect place of green goodness. Just be careful that you don't botch the broccoli and have to start all over!

In Don't Botch the Broccoli, players have an identical hand of broccoli cards numbered -1 to 4 and simultaneously play one card each turn. Any players who played the same number on their broccoli card move their card to their steamer. Then whichever player played the lowest remaining card gets to cook their broccoli (move the card to their score pile), along with any cards in their steamer from previous turns. Any other players' cards are botched, and must return to their hand along with any cards in their steamer.

To win, you've got to find the right time to play each card so that you can cook up a big pile of broccoli.

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

boardgame: Cosmic Horror: Supernatural Horror in Miniature
description:
The stars are right, the end is nigh.

Inspired by the works of H. P. Lovecraft, Cosmic Horror takes Spectre Operations players back to the weird world of Lovecraft's 1930's America.

Cosmic Horror introduces rules for Sanity, Insanity, strange environmental effects and multiple variations for your Eldritch Horrors - as well as new weapons and investigator abilities to combat them.

—description from the back of the book

In Dragon's Hoard, you are a master thief, and have your eyes on the ultimate treasure: A Dragon's Hoard. But even the slightest bit of noise can cause the sleeping to wake up. Will you escape with a full bag of treasure, or will you be running away empty handed?

Dragon's Hoard is a Solitaire, 10 minute tile laying game with an interesting twist. Unlike most tile-laying games where you are confined to a grid, Dragon's Hoard allows you to place cards anywhere, in any direction, with only a few restrictions. You could place a card halfway across the table if you really wanted to.

The thief is trying to score various symbols on cards. Gems simply score one point if they are uncovered. Diamonds score 4 points, but they are much larger and more difficult to keep uncovered. Glyphs score a scaling amount of points, but only score if they are connected. Slimes take away points. And noise is the trickiest symbol of all - having two of them showing at the end of the game causes the player to lose.

—description from the designer

This short addendum details additional rules for Cosmic Horror, focusing on combat, weapons, and the summoning of entities, as well as new character and creature profiles.

COMBAT MECHANICS: It outlines Assault Actions, including momentum usage, Awareness Tests to avoid attacks, and Counter Assault Reactions.

WEAPONS: It provides profiles for various melee and ranged weapons with special rules like Bludgeoning, Armour Piercing, Slashing, and Automatic.

SHUB-NIGGURATH: It includes rules for summoning the Avatar of Shub-Niggurath, different forms (Manifest, Faded, Whisper), and special abilities like “The Black Goat” (spawning fragments) and “Uncontrolled Growth.” The summoning ritual is 
also detailed.

US POLICE: It provides stats and equipment options for US Police officers.

SHOGGOTHS: It describes Shoggoths, their abilities like “All Seeing Eyes,” “Protoplasmic Tendrils” (with a “Grasp” effect), “Crush,” and “Absorb,” and their “Amorphous” nature.

OTHER RULES: It includes rules for “Cosmic Horrors”, “Reload Actions,” and other special rules for specific weapons and entities.

A short supplement with rules for solo and co-op play.

From the publisher:
These rules are intended to allow players to play solo, cooperatively, or as competitors against an NPE (Non Player 
Element) enemy.

Using NPEs in your games can add some unique challenges whether you choose to face a few Elements guarding an 
Objective or an entire force!

Highschool can be a pain - literally.

It’s the end of Prom Night, 1989. The Killer Queen stalks you and your friends through the school halls, and she’s turned the games you used to play into a fight for survival. Hide and Seek, Would You Rather and Truth Or Dare are now choices between life and death, and the only way to survive is to play along. Outrun and outsmart your friends, and hope you’re not the one left alone with the Killer Queen.

The aim of Killer Queen is to be the player with the fewest points in their injury pile at the end of the game. Try to escape the Killer Queen by removing all the cards from your hand, but beware, for every Power card you play in your fight for survival, you’ll have to draw a card from the deck - or from your fellow survivor's hands - in return. Cross your fingers because hidden in each are dangerous Weapon Cards, sinister Plot Twists, and, deadlier still, the Killer Queen herself.

—description from the publisher

Contested Seas is a set of fast play rules for the Pre-Dreadnought era, 1880-1905. Yes, I stretched the start of the era a bit, but those Pre-Pre-Dreadnoughts are just lovely.

Fast play is a relative term but Contested Seas moves right along while still maintaining a large dose of era ambience. Players have the choice as to how complex they wish the game, but no matter what the style, the results will ring true (don't attack battleships with protected cruisers is a good tip).

Contested Seas is a cousin of 4P Press' Naval Mania rules, but features a revamped ranging system, more concise gunnery, simplified torpedo rules and the introduction of turning circles, all designed to speed things up.

However, like its more in depth cousin, Contested Seas features ratings for all 2,367 ships that come in Naval Mania.

Contested Seas includes useful playing aids, such as a QRS that is actually useful, and print out turning circles. As is 4P policy, the game supports what scale you happen to play in and also includes a blank ranging table for any custom scales you wish to invent. Just add the measurements and you're good to go.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Duel – Kami Preorder Promo Pack
description:
This promo card pack is a bonus for preordering of Black Rose Wars: Duel - Kami. In contains:

1 Quest Card
    1 Events Card
    2 Full Art Poker Size Evocation Cards for Mononoke and Kusanagi no-Tsurugi 
    2 Alternative Mage Cards for En No Gyoja and Abe no Seimei with asymmetrical Mages

boardgame: The Adventurers: The Pyramid of Horus
description:
Buried in the sand for millennia, the pyramid of the Pharaoh Sanakht was just uncovered during an archaeological dig on the plateau of Abu Rawash, overlooking the life-giving Nile. But death has always surrounded the legend of Sanakht, whose worship of the Egyptian God Horus and other deities allegedly gave him power over life and death. Inscriptions translated outside the tomb suggest Sanakht, his bride Initkaes, and his architect Imhotep might live again if the tomb is breached. And that, Adventurers, is exactly what you’re here to do. For what is the threat of the living dead against the possibility of so muchtreasure? Escape with the gold and your lives from the Pyramid of Horus!

Enter the Pyramid of Horus, newly uncovered in the sands of Egypt. Ancient mummies patrol dark corridors while crumbling ceilings threaten your escape. Loot forbidden treasures hidden within sarcophagi, avoid curses, and outwit your fellow adventurers to escape with untold riches before the pyramid claims your soul.

—description from the publisher

Play a frenetic game of basketball! String together flicks in attack and defense phases to be the first to score 20 points. Pass after pass, build up your offensive pressure and create shooting opportunities. But it won't be that easy... because you're not alone on the court! The match promises to be epic! Pitchgames Basketball is a flick game that recreates the atmosphere of a 1-on-1 basketball match. Players alternate between attacking and defending phases. When one player is on offense, the other is on defense. You will try to flick the ball between players until being able to try score two or three points. Depending on your precision you will increase your attacking pressure or get the defense pressure increasing.
An advanced rule of the game add a concept of dunk and block of the ball.

A precision game where every shot can change the match. Play alone, with friends or in an impromptu tournament. Shake the net with precision flicks. 
While attacking you’ll try to flick the ball to the circle of a a soccer player on the board. If you touch the outside circle you can only pass to another soccer player, whereas if you touch the inner circle you can try scoring a goal. If the ball ends not touching any of the players circle you loose the attack and your opponent is starting an attack from where the ball is.
First one to score 5 goals win the game.

Plato’s Dilemma is a serious game blending hidden roles, resource management, and ethical debate. Set in a futuristic cyberpunk universe where Greek mythology meets artificial intelligence, players embody mortals inhabited by Olympians, Titans, or legendary humans seated on the board of Hésiodis Global Corporation. Their goal: decide the future of AI, shape society, and determine who will dominate the coming era.

Each game round revolves around debating and voting on dilemmas drawn from real-world issues such as AI in healthcare, surveillance, automation, or the environment. Every choice impacts two global indicators — AI prevalence and corporate growth — with various endgame conditions tied to those.

The game offers cooperative and competitive modes for 2 to 12 players and up to 36 in multi-table format. Designed for both entertainment and educational contexts, Plato’s Dilemma is an engaging tool for critical thinking, ethical awareness, and group decision-making.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Risk of Rain: The Board Game
description:
Risk of Rain: The Board Game is a cooperative roguelike adventure set on the deadly planet of Petrichor V. Based on the acclaimed video game series, this tabletop adaptation challenges you to survive escalating waves of monsters, collect powerful items, and ultimately escape - or perish trying.

Each run spans four stages, with survivors fighting to locate and charge a hidden teleporter on each map before facing off against a stage boss. Complete three stages and reach Commencement, where the powerful Mithrix awaits as the final challenge. You can also save your progress at the end of any stage, allowing you and any fellow survivors to pick up the adventure at your leisure.

Each of you takes on the role of a Survivor, equipped with a unique deck of action cards. Every round these cards are placed into a shared action row alongside monster actions, revealing a turn order determined by card speed—creating fast-paced tactical decisions every round. As a survivor you can move, attack, and interact with the world around you, while monsters will attempt to strike back, block progress, and even multiply if left unchecked.

Each round concludes with the environment growing more hazardous: the planet's danger level increases, strengthening monsters and spawning more threats. If your team falls, you must start over — but with each run, you'll unlock powerful new content, including additional survivors, modular game expansions, unique discoveries, and dozens of new items that deepen and evolve the experience over time.

Risk of Rain: The Board Game is designed for nearly endless replayability, blending strategic depth with the unpredictable tension of a true roguelike.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Bogrit's Beastgrabbaz have entered the mines beneath Embergard seeking signs of the huge troggoth, Trugg. Their leader, Borgit Wolf-Killa fancies himself as a hunter and uses his mates, Snagz and Hobblin’ Dregg, as bait. His real trump card is Uglug, a troggoth who dreams of growing to massive size, although the grots’ spiteful tendency to keep him in cramped spaces has the opposite effect. The warband is rounded out with Rigg and Shamm, who pretend to be Uglug's pet squig.

This multipart plastic kit builds Borgit's Beastgrabbaz – a complete warband for use in games of Warhammer Underworlds, and comes with their fighter cards and warscroll cards.

Borgit's Beastgrabbaz are a Flex-style warband that would synergise well with one of the Flex Rivals decks, which are available separately.

This set contains 5 plastic miniatures:

     1x Borgit Wolf-Killa
     1x Uglug
     1x Rigg and Shamm
     1x Snagz
     1x Hobblin' Dregg


This set also includes:

     5x double-sided fighter cards
     7x warscroll cards (in English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Chinese, and Japanese)


The fighter cards measure 63mm x 88mm, and the warscroll cards measure 106mm x 145mm.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Knives of the Crone
description:
The Knives of the Crone are fanatical believers in the creed of the prophetic goddess Morai-Heg, and they exist to serve fate’s will. They are led by the seer Kaerna Vix, who reads the viscera of slain foes to glean her deity’s desires. A cadre of killers ensure Vix’s visions come to pass – Tazari is Kaerna’s strong right hand, while Krieta eagerly butchers those who draw her master’s gaze. Finally, Azphel stands ready to put a crossbow bolt through distant foes.

This multipart plastic kit builds the Knives of the Crone – a complete warband for use in games of Warhammer Underworlds. It comes with fighter cards and warscroll cards for the warband.

The Knives of the Crone are a Mastery-style warband that would synergise well with one of the Mastery Rivals decks, which are available separately.

This set contains 4 plastic miniatures:

     1x Tazari
     1x Krieta, Wing of the Crone
     1x Kaerna Vix
     1x Azphel, the Hag's Eye


This set also includes:

     4x double-sided fighter cards
     7x warscroll cards (in English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Chinese, and Japanese)


The fighter cards measure 63mm x 88mm, and the warscroll cards measure 106mm x 145mm.

—description from the publisher

For most souls, there is only one reason to travel to Embergard – to collect emberstone. Within each bright, burning coal of Aqshian realmstone flickers power that anyone would long to possess. Everything is secondary to collecting more of it. Even killing an enemy might be forgotten in favour of seizing the arcane fragments they bear.

Realmstone Raiders is a complete and ready-to-play Rivals deck, usable by any warband in your games of Warhammer Underworlds. Just open the pack, shuffle the cards, and you're good to go! The deck is classed under the Mastery playstyle, which encourages you to attack your foes to harness the power of Emberstone Ploys and Upgrades, and would work well with a Mastery-style warband. Use these cards on their own, or mix and match them with another set to create a custom Nemesis format deck.

This deck contains a 33-card universal Rivals deck, consisting of:

     1x Plot Card
     12x Objective Cards
     10x Ploy Cards
     10x Upgrade Cards


Each card measures 63mm x 88mm.

—description from the publisher

In this new mini-expansion to Harvest and Harvest: Fall Festival, festive fair attractions are popping up all over Furroughfield, bringing powerful, game-changing extras to shake up what's happening around town.

Play with one of these five tiles to spruce up locations with exciting actions:

Junk Sale: upgrades actions at the General Store.
Street Fair Trading Post: changes what's available through the Trading Post.
Arts & Crafts: adds a new slot at the Festival for your Basket. Ice Cream Social: adds new scoring for End Game Building Abilities.
Judge's Booth: adds new scoring for diverse crop types during the harvest.

boardgame: Pendragon: Glory of the Round Table
description:
Pendragon: Glory of the Round Table is a self-contained card game of questing, honor, combat, legends, and all manner of knightly virtues. This new card game features key elements from Greg Stafford’s Pendragon tabletop roleplaying game, incorporated into a fun and adventurous multiplayer experience.

Players take on the role of virtuous knights who must complete quests on their path to earn the most glory.  Each quest is represented by a series of tasks taking place over years of time, capturing the essence of the role-playing game. Hire on brave squires that will assist by pledging to your cause. Use your strength, constitution, and appeal to be the next knight selected to join King Arthur’s illustrious round table.

The Knights contained within are each unique, with their own boons and tactics, making each play experience a different one. Harness their key abilities to complete quests and earn glory or trade in gold to purchase a different path to victory. Cards have multiple ways they can be played.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description: One player rates a given word on five scales (like "soft", "loud", "clever", ...) then four random words are added and the other players try to identify the first word from this set.

Flip! Stack! Serve! Shop for ingredients and assemble the perfect burgers to fulfill orders in your chaotic little kitchen!

In Flip Stack Burger Shack, players grab stacks of ingredients from the market and make up to 2 moves to organize your kitchen of 3 plates. Match an order exactly to fulfill it and score points and unlock powers.

Players are given 3 plates on which they can stack ingredients, which take the form of colored wooden discs. Each turn they draft 1 stack of ingredients from 3 possible stacks of ingredients in the market. They may also perform 2 moves to re-arrange their ingredients in order to try to fulfill orders. At the end of the turn, the market restocks by adding 1 random ingredient on top of each stack.

Many orders reward one-time special powers that can be saved up and used throughout the game. Once a player fulfills a certain number of orders that triggers the end of the game, and the player with the most points wins!

—description from the designer

Grunt Work is the hilarious cooperative party game about making expressive grunting noises and interpreting them!

Each round a different players take turns being the grunter. The grunter draws 2 cards from a deck of grunting noises and chooses one of them to attempt. Players then close their eyes as the grunter performs their grunting noise. 2 additional cards are shuffled together with the first 2 to form a total of 4 possible grunting noises.

Each card is then revealed and read out loud one at a time. Players other than the grunter then must discuss and deliberate on which grunting noise was being made. Once the group collectively agrees, the grunter reveals whether the group was correct or not. Then a new round begins.

Once the group has either correctly guessed 8 cards correctly or incorrectly guesses 3 cards, the game is over and your score is the number of correct guesses minus the number of incorrect guesses.

—description from the designer

In Ancient Cards of H.P. Lovecraft, players compete to summon the greatest mystical power through combinations of R’lyeh Runes.
After three rounds, the player with the highest number of Mystic Points is revealed as the true interpreter of the secrets of the Great Old Ones.

How to Play
The game is played in rounds of card selection and passing, until each player has played all cards into their Play Area.

Choose a Card
Each player secretly selects 1 Rune card from their hand and places it face down in front of them (in their Play Area).

Pass the Cards
Once everyone has chosen, each player passes their remaining hand to the player on their left.

Reveal
When everyone has received the new hand, all players reveal their chosen card simultaneously and place it face up in their Play Area.

Repeat
Repeat the process:

     Choose 1 card from the new hand (in secret)
     Place it face down in the Play Area
     Pass the rest to the left
     Reveal the chosen card


The round ends when all players have played and revealed all their cards.

Mystic Scoring
At the end of each round, with all Rune Cards revealed in their Play Area, players evaluate their combinations to determine their mystical progress.
Scoring is based on the number of identical or distinct runes among the cards played.

Scoring Table
Rune Combination    Mystic Points
1 single rune            0 points
2 identical runes    1 point
3 identical runes    2 points
4 identical runes    4 points
5 identical runes    5 points
6 identical runes    7 points
7 identical runes    10 points
7 different runes    5 points

End of the Game & Victory
After three rounds, the player with the highest total Mystic Points is declared the winner — the true decipherer of the Ancient Cards of H.P. Lovecraft.

Tiebreakers:

     The player with the highest score in the final round wins.
     If the tie remains, the tied players share the victory, sealing the fate of the world together as interpreters of the Great Old Ones' secrets.


—description from the designer

Em Cartas Antigas de H.P. Lovecraft, os jogadores competem para invocar o maior poder místico através de combinações de Runas de R’lyeh. 
Após três rodadas, vence o jogador que acumular a maior quantidade de pontos místicos, revelando-se o verdadeiro intérprete dos segredos dos Grandes Antigos.

Como Jogar
O jogo acontece em rodadas de seleção e passagem de cartas, até que cada jogador tenha jogado todas as cartas da mão em sua área de jogo.
1. Escolha da Carta
 Cada jogador escolhe 1 carta de Runa da sua mão, em segredo, e a coloca virada para baixo à sua frente (em sua Área de Jogo).
2. Passagem de Cartas
 Após todos escolherem sua carta, cada jogador passa as cartas restantes (as que sobraram em sua mão) para o jogador à sua esquerda.
3. Revelação
 Assim que todos receberem a nova mão, os jogadores revelam simultaneamente a carta jogada em sua Área de Jogo, posicionando-a virada para cima.
4. Repetição
 O processo se repete:

     Escolher 1 carta da nova mão (em segredo) 
     Colocar virada para baixo na Área de Jogo
     Passar as demais para a esquerda
     Revelar a nova carta


A rodada termina quando todos tiverem jogado e revelado todas as cartas.

Pontuação Mística
Ao final de cada rodada, com as cartas das Runas de R’lyeh reveladas na sua área de jogo, cada jogador avalia suas combinações para descobrir o quanto avançou no conhecimento místico. 
A pontuação é calculada com base na quantidade de Runas iguais ou em variações de Runas únicas entre as cartas jogadas.

Tabela de Pontuação
Combinação de Runas    Pontos Místicos
1 Runa isolada    0 pontos
2 Runas iguais    1 ponto
3 Runas iguais    2 pontos
4 Runas iguais    4 pontos
5 Runas iguais    5 pontos
6 Runas iguais    7 pontos
7 Runas iguais    10 pontos
7 Runas diferentes    5 pontos

Fim do Jogo e Vitória
Após três rodadas, o jogador com o maior total de Pontos Místicos é declarado o vencedor — o verdadeiro decifrador das Cartas Antigas de H.P. Lovecraft.

Em caso de empate, o desempate é resolvido da seguinte forma:

     Vence o jogador que obteve mais Pontos Místicos na última rodada.
     Se o empate persistir, os jogadores empatados compartilham a vitória, selando juntos o destino do mundo ao decifrarem os segredos dos Grandes Antigos.


—description from the designer (Portuguese)

boardgame: 日本のいちばん長い８月(Japan's Longest August)
description:
Issued game in Game Journal Magazine #95.
Based on the game system of the French Revolution 1789 from Game Journal #89, this game recreates the power struggle between the army and navy military officers and politicians in the Empire of Japan, which was cornered by the Allied Powers. Characters have various action abilities and the ability to earn victory points. The goal is to end the war in a way that benefits your faction the most.

In the initial placement, an auction is held for the first eight characters representing the cabinet and the army and navy leaders at the time.

Each game turn, a Prime Minister is selected by voting, and if the Prime Minister changes, a Cabinet is formed, and actions such as personnel, financial management, and military operations are performed using characters and positions. The current Prime Minister and related characters earn points, and new characters are acquired in the auction, ending the turn.

The war ends when the unconditional surrender proposed by the Prime Minister is supported (historical result), or at the end of the fifth turn, a decisive battle for the Japanese mainland takes place. Either way, the Empire's defeat is a foregone conclusion, and the player's efforts cannot change that outcome.
The winner is determined by victory points. Victory points are awarded based on the points each character has in relation to the current Prime Minister, bonuses based on how each character ended the war, and, if the battle becomes a mainland battle (only), based on the military achievements of the army and navy.

Impersonate a paraglider racing to reach three antennas before landing on the central target. Victory points are earned for flying above those antennas and other landmarks as well as performing stunts during your race. Always be prepared to adjust your navigation strategy in response to the changing weather and the terrain configuration.
This game could be classified as a navigation puzzle. During your turn you just need to move your pawn, while managing your unique precious resource: your altitude. Moving fast will make you lose altitude but let you take the lead of the race, while taking a detour to move above mountains or under clouds will make you gain altitude but avoid landing prematurely. With a smart map setup procedure generating unique maps, and a random weather forecast, the game will bring you a different navigation challenge every time.

—description from the designer

At the start of the game, the knights position themselves on the green spots at the corners of the tournament stages. Each player rolls the die once and advances the number of points rolled. On a 1, they move straight ahead. On a 2 or 3, they move in a lateral direction, changing course once. Only one square may be crossed diagonally.
Green squares mean a horse falls; if a rider encounters one, they must move back one square, as does the same if they roll black.
Opposing knights must try to meet on the same course. A knight is defeated if the opponent jumps onto the square in front of their position or overruns them. The winner receives a golden wreath. The player who has captured the most wreaths is the winner. There are 12 wreaths in the game.
The game continues until there are no more wreaths. Knights with the same number of wreaths engage in decisive battles.
Knights who pass each other and do not meet start again.

—user summary

boardgame: Die Jury: Zwei Gesichter – Ein Schuss
description:
Guilty as charged? As members of a jury, two to five players aged 14 and over pass judgment in a murder trial. Did the defendant really shoot the young man in the parking lot outside a club? Over the course of three rounds, the group discusses the evidence and any doubts that arise. The players listen to audio recordings from the courtroom: statements from the prosecution and defense as well as interviews with witnesses, police officers and forensic experts provide clues. With the help of the evidence seized, they work together to reconstruct the course of events based on a real case in order to reach a decision on the question of guilt. Interactive true crime suspense for fans of crime podcasts and courtroom series.

—description from the publisher

The Hundred Years' War was a highly complex conflict in terms of its duration, the many players involved and the succession of wars and truces over a very long period of 116 years!

Chevauchee, pitched battles, sieges, ambushes, looting, assassinations and betrayals were all part of the story. So it was a challenge to simulate this complex, non-linear war. Few games exist on this subject. We chose to design the game on a strategic scale, while maintaining its excellent playability.

Choices had to be made, and not all the details dear to historians will be there, but you'll be immersed in the medieval atmosphere and feel the epic breath of your rides in late medieval France.

La guerre de Cent Ans ("The Hundred Years' War") is designed for 2 to 4 players: with 4, each player takes control of one faction; with 2, each player takes control of both factions on the same side; with 3, one player takes control of both factions on one side, while the remaining players each take control of one faction on the other side.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Punahilkka (Little Red Riding Hood) is an roll and move game with four different roles. On your turn (regardless of your role) you announce the direction, that you are moving (diagonal is allowed), then you roll the die and move that many spaces to the chosen direction. On your next turn you are not allowed to move to same direction as your previous turn.

If you are playing as Little Red Riding Hood, you win the game by visiting Grand Ma and coming back home. If you are the Wolf, you win the game by catching the Little Red Riding Hood, when she's going home from Grand Ma's place. If you are The Hunter you must catch the Wolf, which means that you have to be exactly two spaces away of the Wolf to shoot it. If you are the Hunters Dog, you win by catching the Wolf and then getting exactly two spaces away from the Hunter. Every character starts the game on it's own corner in the 16*24-sized grid. Grand Ma's house occupies the four middle squares of the board.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
Hannu ja Kerttu (Hansel & Gretel) is a pretty basic roll and move game. You win the game by being the first player to reach the end of the track. If you land on the certain squares, then something happens (miss the turn, go forward, go backward and so on).

On your turn you roll the die.  If the result is even, then you will roll again and move forward as many spaces as the combined sum of the results. If the result is odd, then you move backwards the rolled amount of spaces.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Hansel and Gretel

description:
Lumikki (Snow White) is a roll and move game with three possible roles (Queen, Prince or Dwarf). First one to reach the Dwarves cabin in the center of the board wins the game. Queen starts the game from her castle in one corner. Prince starts the game from his castle in another corner. Rest of the players are Dwarves and their start the game from the mines in the third corner.

On your turn you roll the die and move that many spacers in any direction. If you end your movement on a black square, then you have to move to the direction of the arrow on your next turn. If you end your movement to the red space you can use the short cut in the next round.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description:
Hoshizukuri is a deck building game where players are a comet, comboing celestial cards to create star dust. Spend star dust to gain stronger celestial cards for your deck, or generate stars and planets for victory points. Each game will use 8 of the 20 different celestial card types, so mix and match for different strategies.

Playing cards will move your comet on the trajectory track. Everyone will score their points once they individually orbit to 35. You can make the most of your run by playing cards at the same time with effects, multiple of the same color, or 1 of each color. More actions for less movement.

A trinity of opposing political factions seeks to gain control of a dystopian state off the coast of Japan.

As a member of one of these factions, you'll need to utilize thematic card effects to narrow down your opponent’s identity (represented by numbers on hidden target cards) while scuttling their efforts to expose yours! Target cards are chosen from your starting hand; figuring out which cards will form the toughest target and which effects you want to use yourself is crucial.

In a 3-player game, you’ll need to attack the person to your left while defending against the right, adding an extra layer of strategic depth.

- Fan summary

The old ideas of communism are being erased by the rise of capitalism, and people can no longer accept them. There are three factions that, without revealing their true identities, are trying to change this decaying state from within. The first faction to reveal its identity will be eliminated.

Players must choose three cards from their hand to create their secret code.
On their turn, they draw a card from the deck and play a card from their hand to trigger its effect. After that, they may attempt to guess another player's code.

The player who correctly guesses someone’s code does not win. Instead, the winner is the player sitting to the right of the one whose code was guessed.

- Description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Nutrino is a fast-paced educational card game designed to help children aged 6 and up recognize food source of proteins, carbs, fat and fibers while playing a UNO-like card game. Fun, easy to learn, and perfect for families and classrooms.

DNPS scenarios published in Alea #31 (2006)

83 counters + maps

Spinola defeats Condé and French forces sent to relieve the besieged Catalan fortress of Salses in 1639.

Moroccan Sultan's cavalry and Ottoman gunfire overwhelm the Portuguese and their Moorish allies at Alcazarquivir (Alcácer Quibir) in 1578.

DNPS scenario published in Alea #33 (2009)

44 counters + map

Cardinal Infante defeats Bernhard's Swedish and Heilbronn League forces in Bavaria, and reopens the "Spanish Road" to Flanders, 1634.

Pic'Together is a cooperative drawing game where one player has to guess the hidden word drawn by the group.
But be careful, each member of the group may only draw part of the picture. So make sure you coordinate well.

boardgame: Violent Skies: Season 4 –  First Team to the Solomons 1942-43
description:
Sat inside the cockpit with the engine rattling through your bones, you turn to engage. Managing your energy and fuel, can you gain position to get a shot off? Or will the Bandit get the better of you? Sometimes it is better to disengage than push a losing fight.

Violent Skies is a solitaire game of aerial combat in the Second World War. Using a game card that acts as your cockpit and also as a mission generator. The Player and Bandit take alternating actions to try and get a shot on the opposition's aircraft.

This Fourth Season of the game puts you in the cockpit of the main fighters during the opening salvos of the Pacific War. From Thach Weaving Wildcats, nimble Ki-43 Oscars and deadly A6M Zeros, Players can forge pilot careers in some of the greastest aircraft of the Second World War. Including the Battle of the Coral Sea, the Battle of Midway, Operation Vengenace, the Solomons Campaign and the beginning of Corsair operations, Violent Skies 4 plunges you into the action.

Each sortie lasts between 5 and 10 minutes, and the Player has a logbook to advance their career. As the Player completes a campaign, they have the option to keep the logbook and continue their career in different aircraft and theatres.

All is needed is at least four D6, a small marker for the advantage track, and a pen or pencil for the logbook. Everything else is included in the minimal construction print and play PDF.  Three optional counters are included and need to be folded and cut out but the rest is ready straight off the printer.

Included as flyable aircraft are the Wildcat F4F-3 & -4, A6M2-21, A6M2-N, A6M3-32, Ki-43b, P-38G, P-40F, F1M2, F4U-1, P-39D.

Each aircraft comes with a Sortie Card (your cockpit and mission generator), Campaign Tables, optional Damage Table, Skills sheet and counters.

There are Logbooks for the Japanese and Americans in low ink and full colour, as well as a detailed example of play.

.....and remember, never try to out turn a Zero or underestimate a Wildcat!

All Violent Skies products are standalone and do not need other versions to play.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Violent Skies (Ben Rawlings, Pocket Warfare Publishing)

boardgame: Violent Skies: Season 2 – The RAF on the Offensive
description:
Sat inside the cockpit with the engine rattling through your bones, you turn to engage. Managing your energy and fuel, can you gain position to get a shot off? Or will the bandit get the better of you? Sometimes it is better to disengage than push a losing fight.

Violent Skies is a solitaire game of aerial combat in the Second World War. Using a game card that acts as your Cockpit and also as a mission generator. The player and Bandit take alternating actions to try and get a shot on the opposition's aircraft.

This Second season of games puts you in the cockpit of the main fighters during the RAF's Offensive after the Battle of Britain. Starting with the aerial operations over Northern France to the introduction of the Focke Wulf-190A, you will be flying sorties, shooting down bandits and trying your best to survive.

Each sortie lasts between 5-10 minutes, and the player has a logbook to advance their career. As the player completes a campaign, they have the option to keep the logbook and continue their career in different aircraft and theatres. Players of Violent Skies - 1940 - Season 1, Dynamo to the Blitz can continue their pilots careers as a new year of war opens up, with new aircraft and new challenges.

All is needed is at least four D6, a small marker for the advantage track, and a pen or pencil for the logbook. Everything else is included in the minimal construction print and play PDF.  Three optional counters are included and need to be folded and cut out but the rest is ready straight off the printer.

Included as flyable aircraft are the Spitfire MkVb and the Spitfire IIa LR, Whirlwhind Mk1, Bf109E7,F2,F4, Fw-190A1.

Each aircraft comes with a sortie card (your cockpit and mission generator), campaign tables, optional damage table, Skills sheet and counters.

There are optional rules to track pilot Rank and Awards to further immerse the player in the role of a Second World War pilot.

There are Logbooks for the Luftwaffe and RAF in low ink and full colour, as well as a detailed example of play.

.....and remember, watch out for the "Hun in the Sun" and never underestimate a "fat old puffer"

All Violent Skies Products are Standalone and do not need other versions to play.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Violent Skies (Ben Rawlings, Pocket Warfare Publishing)

boardgame: Violent Skies: Season 3 – The Desert War 1940-41
description:
Sat inside the cockpit with the engine rattling through your bones, you turn to engage. Managing your energy and fuel, can you gain position to get a shot off? Or will the Bandit get the better of you? Sometimes it is better to disengage than push a losing fight.

Violent Skies is a solitaire game of aerial combat in the Second World War. Using a game card that acts as your cockpit and also as a mission generator. The Player and Bandit take alternating actions to try and get a shot on the opposition's aircraft.

This Third Season of the game puts you in the cockpit of the main fighters during the first two years of the North African Campaign. From the Italian invasion all the way to Operation Crusader, you will be flying Sorties, shooting down Bandits and trying your best to survive.

Each sortie lasts between 5 and 10 minutes, and the Player has a logbook to advance their career. As the Player completes a campaign, they have the option to keep the logbook and continue their career in different aircraft and theatres.

All is needed is at least four D6, a small marker for the advantage track, and a pen or pencil for the logbook. Everything else is included in the minimal construction print and play PDF.  Three optional counters are included and need to be folded and cut out but the rest is ready straight off the printer.

Included as flyable aircraft are the Gladiator, Hurricane Mk  IIa Trop, Tomahawk IIb, Martlet III, MC.200, G.50, CR.42, Bf-109E7/F4 Trop, Bf-110C.

Each aircraft comes with a Sortie Card (your cockpit and mission generator), Campaign Tables, optional Damage Table, Skills sheet and counters.

There are Logbooks for the Luftwaffe, Regia Aeronautica and RAF in low ink and full colour, as well as a detailed example of play.

.....and remember, watch out for the "Hun in the Sun" and never underestimate a "fat old puffer"

All Violent Skies products are standalone and do not need other versions to play.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Violent Skies (Ben Rawlings, Pocket Warfare Publishing)

description:
When a reputable Finnish Art Dealer brokers a deal that isn't what it seems, he unwittingly puts his life at risk... now on the run, he's hidden a secret message in an online vault and left a series of 7 clues to the passcode - who do you think can uncover the message?

In this escape room style game, one or more players solve a series of puzzles, of which the answer to each is a number that makes up one part of the passcode to the online vault.  Discover all of the numbers and put them in the correct order to open the vault and reveal the hidden message.

The Polish campaign was one of the fastest and most impressive of all time. From 01/09/39, German troops penetrated Poland rapidly, sweeping the obsolete Polish army in less than a month (the Germans called it the "18-Day Campaign"). Employing a numerically larger, better equipped and mainly indoctrinated army for the Blitzkrieg, the Germans had overwhelming superiority over the Poles. These, in turn, with an inferior army in all respects except bravery, could do little to defend the extensive Polish borders. In addition, the Luftwaffe (German Air Force) destroyed the small Polish air force in the early days of the campaign. However, with the Anglo-French declaration of war on Germany, the Polish campaign became World War II. Despite so many disadvantages, the Polish player can win the match, seeking to slow down German advances, avoiding the sieges that the "panzers" will try to make and still protect themselves in the fortified areas. The Germans, on the other hand, are fighting back in time, because if they do not subdue Poland quickly, the allies will invade Germany from the West, whose border is weakly defended. Therefore, victory should smile at what best harnesses its strength.

Chaosphere: Etonak Vanguard is a solo or cooperative tactical game set in the sci-fi universe of Universal Legends. You play as a crew member of the Etonak Vanguard, the elite fleet of Etonak, the last free planet within the chaotic space zone known as the Chaosphere.

A warship from the megacorporation Inferno is moving through the Chaosphere, carrying what could be a black hole–creating artifact. You’re the only unit close enough to stop it before it reaches its destination. The mission is simple: intercept and neutralize the threat, at any cost. They don’t know you’re coming. You have a head start. Use it wisely.

As you pursue the Inferno ship across three hostile Sectors, you’ll explore, fight, scavenge, and upgrade your ship in preparation for the final battle. Push forward too quickly and you may arrive underpowered. Linger too long, and you’ll be overwhelmed. Between Sectors, spaceports offer valuable opportunities: mercenaries, weapons, and technologies to trade or buy — if you’ve gathered enough resources along the way.

Chaosphere: Etonak Vanguard combines tense tactical combat with rogue-lite progression and varied bosses that each shape your journey through the Chaosphere in a different way.'

—description from the publisher

Kraken is a fast-paced pirate-themed fishing game in which each player secretly pursues a unique objective. A typical game lasts around 30 minutes and blends strategy, sabotage, and a pinch of luck.

On your turn, you draw cards, play actions, roll dice to catch fish, and upgrade your gear using goldfish tokens. Each pirate has a unique ability that influences their playstyle and strategy, and the first player to fulfill their secret objective or to capture the legendary Kraken wins.

The game features action cards, attribute cards, sabotage cards, and fish cards divided into different environments: lake, sea, river. Managing sabotage and maximizing bonus effects are key to success. Multiple tactics are viable: boosting your gear, slowing down your rivals, or outright stealing their resources.

With its quick turns and unpredictable twists, Kraken creates a dynamic, high-energy experience full of spectacular comebacks and pirate mischief.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Fungeon! A chaotic card game where your party isn't exactly your ally. Each adventurer is looking to exit the dungeon as the richest one. You've got to lie, cheat, steal, and punch your way out - or you'll be left with nothing. Friendships will be broken in this rage-inducing game of chance and sabotage.

Fungeon is played with 3 to 6 players and with two decks, the Core Deck and the Dungeon Deck. The goal of the game is to have the most Gold at the end of the game.

At the start of their turn, a player ventures into the dungeon by rolling a die, then reveals and resolves the card that many spots from the top of the deck, removing the cards above it. When venturing, a player may reveal an Event which affects them or all players, a Monster to fight, a Location which stays for a round and equally affects all players, or a Treasure to help them out.

After venturing, a player has 4 Stamina to spend on playing card from the Core Deck; Schemes, which benefit them in some way, Attacks, which debuff or steal from opponents, Items, which you can equip to protect yourself or cause trouble for others, and Traps, which you place face-down until an opponent unwittingly triggers them.

Additional danger comes in the form of Extra cards which get shuffled into the Deck, like Bombs which end a player's turn when drawn, and Curses which debuff the player who draws it for a few turns.

The game ends when there are no more cards in the Dungeon Deck, i.e. a player can no longer venture.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: UNO: Show ‘em No Mercy – Expansion Pack
description:
Fans of UNO Show 'em No Mercy game will love this expansion pack -- it includes 24 additional cards that bring extra carnage to the already brutal game, plus special coins that can either save a player or double the pain! Players just add the cards from the expansion pack into an existing deck from their collection and wait for the chaos to begin. And like always, when down to the last card, they still have to yell 'UNO!' Designed for players ages 7 years and older. Players must already have an UNO Show 'em No Mercy deck (sold separately, subject to availability); the cards and coins in the expansion pack cannot be played with on their own. Colors and decorations may vary.

This UNO Show 'em No Mercy expansion pack adds some fresh, ruthless rules to your existing UNO Show 'em No Mercy deck!
Simply mix in the expansion pack's 24 cards and bring carnage to the already brutal game!
The expansion pack also includes special coins that can only be used once per round, so choose wisely!
The Mercy Coin lets a player draw a whole new hand, and the No Mercy Coin doubles the penalty of any card played!
Players must already have an UNO Show 'em No Mercy deck; the cards and coins in the expansion pack cannot be played with on their own.

Includes 24 cards, 6 coins and instructions.

—description from the publisher

As of 2025 in the U.S., this expansion is a Wal-Mart exclusive.

The card distribution is as follows:


     8 #10 Cards, marked with a "Ten Replay Modifier" that lets the player who plays it immediately take another turn (similar to the "Skip Everyone" Cards, but these are considered Number Cards instead of Action Cards (see below).
     8 Wild Discard All Cards; when played, you choose a color and discard all the cards you hold of that color. You then choose a color (the same color as discarded or any other color) to continue play.
     4 Wild Reverse Draw Eight Cards; essentially, the same as the Wild Reverse Draw Four Cards in the base deck, but the next player in the new direction draws eight cards and loses their turn. You then choose the color that continues play.
     2 Wild Final Attack Cards; when played, you reveal your hand. The next player must draw one card for each Action and Wild Card you hold and lose their turn. (This excludes the Number Cards with modifiers--0, 7 and 10.) But if you have 7 or more Action/Wild cards in your hand, the next player has to draw 25 cards, and every other player (except you) draws 5 cards. You then choose the color that continues play.
     2 Wild Sudden Death Cards; when played, EVERY player (including you) draws enough cards to bring their hand to 24 cards. You then choose the color that continues play.


The Mercy/No Mercy coin is taken before play and its function must be chosen before cards are dealt out, and can only be used once per deal.

If you choose to use the Mercy side, you can use it whenever you want to discard your entire hand to the bottom of the Discard Pile and draw a new hand of 7 cards from the Draw Pile. (This can also be used whenever your hand is over 25 cards to avoid elimination.)

If you choose the No Mercy side, whenever you play a Draw Action/Wild Card, you can play the coin to DOUBLE the Draw penalty of THAT CARD ONLY. (Example: Playing a Wild Reverse Draw Eight, then playing a No Mercy coin doubles that card's draw penalty to 16.) It does not affect any other cards in any accumulated Draw pile, and it doesn't affect the card's value when adding cards to said accumulation. (Continuing the above example, another Draw Eight or Draw Ten could be played on the card, but the coin still only affects the penalty from the originally played card. But you could NOT play a Draw Four and another No Mercy coin and consider it a Draw Eight.)

Critical Fix is a fast-paced, sabotage-filled card game where 2–6 technicians race to resolve IT tickets before their coworkers do. Each player draws from a shared deck of Repair, Action, and Ticket cards. Fix servers by matching the right Repairs to Problem Tickets but watch out! Opponents can sabotage your fixes, reopen tickets, or flood the data center with new problems.

On your turn, you’ll draw a card, open and/or repair tickets, and play Actions to tilt the odds in your favor. To complete a fix, attach the correct Part to a Ticket and send it into Testing. If it survives one full round without getting reopened, it Resolves, earning you 1 point. Be the first technician to Resolve 7 Tickets to win.

With stackable Tickets and a mix of tactical planning and sabotage, Critical Fix captures the exaggerated hilarious stress of a real-life corporate IT. 
Easy to learn and quick to play, it’s perfect for game night, office battles, or anyone who’s ever fought a server at 3 a.m.

—description from the publisher

A culturally rich board game that blends color-based mechanics with legendary characters and local wisdom from Betawi, a traditional ethnic group native to Jakarta, Indonesia.

In this edition, players must collect and combine primary colors to create tertiary ones while answering quiz questions rooted in Betawi folklore, history, and everyday life. Along the journey, players will encounter iconic figures from Betawi legends, use strategy cards to outwit opponents, and overcome unpredictable spaces like the "Isolated Zone."

Designed for 2–4 players and playable in 30–60 minutes, this edition offers an immersive experience that encourages strategic thinking, cultural appreciation, and color literacy. Whether in classrooms or family game nights

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Disco Inferno is a cooperative deck building game for 2-6 players where you and your friends try to out-perform demon Dancers before you get booed out of the club!

The game starts with players facing off against a 7 card "Dancer" deck pulled from a set of 14+ unique options. Each player will choose from 8 characters and begin with a starter deck and player board unique to that character. Players expand their deck by generating currency (Appeal) and using it to purchase new cards at the Shop. Some new cards from the shop have Genre effects, which are conditional effects that only resolve when the matching Genre is playing. This is determined by cards revealed in the "Now Playing" slot next to the Genre deck, which contains 9 unique Genres.

To win the game, players must defeat 7 demons Dancers to reach Inferno level 7. Each level presents a new Dancer with a unique ability and set of effects determined by a dice roll at the beginning of each player turn. In addition, an additional die is rolled to determine environmental changes in the club environment (such as changing the Genre).

Watch out for Crowd Mood! This tracker starts at 0 and can scale from +5 (HYPED!) down to -5 (Defeat) — hitting -5 causes the players to immediately lose the game (get booed out of the club). Can you and your friends work together to prove that DISCO NEVER DIES?

—description from the publisher

Die With The Reign is a dice & deck building rogue-like adventure game.

It's main feature is having 135 custom numbered dice, of which players will collect some every playthrough. You start a run with typical numbered dice, and as you progress, you may end up with a D4 that can roll a 14! Or even a D20 that can roll a 50!

It also features an augment system, where players can collect multiple of the same augment to power them up. It contains over 600 augment cards. You can choose to collect easy to find augments with lower power ceilings, or try to find more rare and expensive augments with super high power ceilings!

As per the first line, players will also build their own deck of abilities. There are 5 characters to choose from, each with their own pool of abilities to draw from.

—description from the designer

Delve into the soil of ancient India in Sinauli Unearthed, where players become rival archaeologists unearthing the secrets of a Harappan site.

Each turn, you send meeples to excavate deeper levels of the Sinauli Soil Board, unearthing relics like vases, weapons, burial boxes, and the legendary chariots. But digging is just the start: relics can be traded for coins, used to fulfill museum contracts, or published as groundbreaking research. As you manage influence, coins, and your crew, you’ll sway political favor to determine the final award criteria for India’s prestigious Bharat Ratna.

The player with the highest point total, wins.

With variable player order, rare finds, and high replayability, Sinauli Unearthed blends real archaeology with clever Euro mechanics for an engaging print-and-play dig into history.

Long ago, the dwarves who worshipped the Sun of the Earth discovered its source - the Double Arc-in-Stone. The magical gemstone that is the source of all gold! To protect this treasure (and to prevent inflation) the ancient dwarves created the Mines of Dwarvia. It is an impenetrable maze of mechanism and magic that can only be accessed once in a thousand years. Now is your chance to conquer the mines and obtain infinite wealth or be turned into a midnight snack by a neutral-hued dragon. The race is on!

Mines of Dwarvia is a light-hearted competitive dungeon brawler designed for replayability, quick setup, strategy and fun. Make your hero, fight monsters, power up, equip loot and scheme your way to victory over your "friends". Take advantage of your character's unique abilities. Feign weakness while your opponents break themselves against each other. Claim victory from the unwary!

—description from the designer

In Mines of Dwarvia, players need to retrieve the Double Arc-in-Stone and exit the mines. Initially, the Double Arc-in-Stone can be found at the center of mine. It is claimed by the first player who reaches it. Other players can then take it by defeating the current holder in combat.

Each player has a character with random characteristics and abilities. Each turn, they may move their characters a number of spaces and cards cards to improve their situation. When a character enters a danger space, their movement ends, and one player may play a danger card that the active player may have to deal with. This may result in combat, which is resolved by rolling dice. At the end of their turn, the player draws one new card, which may be a trap or event that is resolved immediately.

Combat, traps, and events can cause a character to die. When that happens, the player simply creates a new character and starts anew — unless the Double Arc-in-Stone has already been found. In that case, the player is eliminated from the game.

Mines of Dwarvia is played on a board composed of four different rotating sections. At the start of the game, these are placed in a random configuration, creating the board layout.

—user summary

2 unique bonus action tiles, plus 12 joker rings, for a total of 440 different game configurations! Thanks to them, Ink's replayability reaches playful and artistic heights.

Local store promo via Hobbynext/Asmodee: Visit your eligible game shop to obtain two unique bonus action tiles, plus twelve joker rings, for a total of 440 different game configurations! Thanks to them, Ink's replayability reaches playful and artistic heights.

Get ready to roll with the punches. It’s the classic Left Center Right game with a little extra fight built in. Introducing: Duke the foam boxing glove. Roll the dice to dictate where you pass your chips – left, center, or right. If three Dukes are rolled, it’s time to fight. Race to grab The Duke. The player who succeeds can either take all chips from the pot or throw Duke at another player to make them lose everything. The last player standing with any chips left is the champion and gets to collect all the chips in the middle.

How to play:

     Roll the dice to pass your chips left, right or center. Just watch out for THE DUKE
     If three gloves are rolled, it’s time to FIGHT. Race to be the first to grab the foam boxing glove, The Duke, to chuck him at a player to make them lose their coins


—description from the publisher

Board game dedicated to a small Raseiniai town in the central part of Lithuania. It is a dice rolling and track moving game. Players move their pawns, buy and expand variety of crucial business activities and services to develop best town in region. From building industry to biotechnologies, robotics and farming, everything is fitting in the modern city. Travel through local nature and historical monuments, trade in the stock market, bet at the local horse race and invest in the city infrastructure. If you are out of money, take the loan from the bank or travel the inner income circle to provide extra services in surrounding smaller municipalities. Game ends as soon as the last business piece is sold, players get winning points for each ownership of the companies, business combos gives extra points, also players get points for each visited local monument, investments in to the city infrastructure, shares and money they still have in their hands, there some end game penalties if players didn't returned all loans to the bank.

—description from the designer

Phase 10 Tiles shuffles together the original rummy-type card game everyone loves with the tactile fun of tiles and two quicker ways to play!

Rack up faster rummy-type fun for everyone with the Phase 10 Tiles game, featuring two games in one Players can enjoy the classic Phase 10 gameplay they know and love – building the 10 original Phases from the card game – or they can try the Quick Play version, where each round they choose to build one of three random Phases worth different points in a race to score 10 points first.

How to play:

     In the Classic game, players still race to build 10 Phases such as "Two Sets of 3" or "One Run of 7."
     In Quick Play mode, players choose to build one of three random Phases each round – tougher Phases score more points!
     With 32 unique Phase Cards to choose from, players will never know what they'll need to build next!
     Players can draw ANY tile from the center pile – more options mean quicker games!
     "Wild" and "Skip" tiles add to the excitement and deliver game-changing moments!
     First player to score 10 points wins!


—description from the publisher

Dog-In-Show is a joyful and strategic board game where players become dog breeders, trainers, and handlers, all striving to raise the most outstanding dogs and win the coveted "Best in Show" trophy. Each player starts with a puppy and uses training cards to secretly improve its beauty, agility, and obedience over time.

The game blends secret actions, open competition, and interactive negotiation. You can trade, bluff, cooperate, or sabotage – all in the name of dog show glory. Random events and judges’ moods create twists that reward adaptability and clever timing.

With real dog photos, lively illustrations, and multiple dog breeds to explore, Dog-In-Show appeals to families, dog lovers, and strategy game fans alike. The gameplay scales well from small to large groups (2–10 players), offering a light yet competitive experience full of laughs, rivalries, and adorable dogs.

The player who collects the most trophies through successful show entries and clever play wins the game – and earns the title of ultimate dog show champion.

—description from the designer

A virus is causing health problems for the Brazilian population, and the government has decided to take action to minimize its effects. The decision was made to focus on research, drug production, and vaccination.

In this 15-round Roll and Write game, players must roll four dice each round and choose two to mark on the game sheet. They aim not only to vaccinate the population, but also to better structure hospitals and encourage research institutes to discover and produce new medications.

A classic Roll and Write game with combo possibilities, where the goal is to treat the population and eliminate the problems caused by the virus.

—description from the designer

Get ready to power up in the world of Solo Leveling, where even the weakest hunter can become an unstoppable force. This isn’t just another deck—it’s your ticket to leveling up, slashing through dungeons, and proving that the only direction is up. Based on the insanely popular anime, this Challenger Series product puts you in the shoes of Sung Jinwoo, the underdog who takes on the world. Are you up for the challenge?


     An advanced, ready-to-play 60 card deck that includes 1 Sung Jinwoo character card along with a new Level-up card!
     Level-up your Skill Tree: In Solo Leveling, your character evolves just like in the anime. Unlock abilities in the same order Jinwoo does, making each game a true adventure.
     Includes a booklet featuring a card checklist, as well as information for new players
     Includes a Collector Booster Pack, featuring 6 Random Foil Alt-Art cards found in this Challenger Series release. Every card in the deck has an Alt-Art available!


—description from the publisher

When looking at the large penguin colony, you'll hear constant cries of "Here!" — "Here!" — "Here!"...and those cries will often be coming from you!

In Hier, you want to help the penguins find their partner among the crowd, and the right partner is always the penguin whose called number is as close as possible to their own number. Try to find the right partners for penguins as quickly as possible because whoever first gets rid of their five penguins wins.

boardgame: Puke: The Totally Sick Card Game
description:
In this game of gluttony, players pig out on pizza, binge on burgers and fill up on fries… but watch out! Flip over a Puke card and it might just turn your stomach! Don’t let your greediness see you lose your lunch… keep it down and it will pay dividends later! The first player to fill their boots, but not the bucket, wins! Packed in a handy flip-top tin.

On a player's turn, they flip a card from the deck until they want to stop drawing or they draw a PUKE card! If you draw a puke card, you need to discard all other cards you've drawn that round.

First player to keep 10 food cards, wins the game!

Transform your table into a lively farm where feathered friends compete for barnyard supremacy. Rally your family to match birds, build farms, and soar to victory! From humble chickens to exotic emus, every card played creates moments of excitement and connection. Watch faces light up as clever moves lead to unexpected wins in this fast-paced strategy game.

On their turn, players will play down sets of bird cards together with applicable barn bonus scoring cards from their hand and the community area that will score for them at the end of the game and then draw a new barn card that will either provide a bonus scoring opportunity or require the player to perform an immediate action (such as drawing more bird cards, swapping cards, etc).

The game ends when the barn card deck has run out and players have no more legal plays left in their hand. The player with the most points from their sets is declared the winner.

The various pieces in the game are worth different units of value, so, for example, a general is worth 10 units, a cannon is worth 7, and an ensign is worth 3. By alternately rolling three dice and calculating the resulting rolls, it is determined how many units of figures each player can bring onto the battlefield and how quickly they can advance. If a player in one of the five columns on the battlefield feels superior to their opponent, they can attack. After a fightback by the attacked side, the opposing forces are counted according to their units of value. The difference in units of value represents the magnitude of the defeat on one side and the significance of the victory on the other, and also indicates how many figures (units of value) can be taken away from the defeated side as prisoners. The game concludes with the decisive battle, in which the troops of all columns must participate. The defeated side pays war reparations based on the difference in units.

—user summary
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
This is a game in the "Bite-Size" range of Trivial Pursuit games. Trivial Pursuit: Suomi – Bite Size is intended as a portable travel game. The game is packaged in a wedge ("piece-of-pie") shaped box.

The game does not feature a game board, and thus, there is no roll-and-move mechanic. Instead, players roll the colour coded die, and answers a question matching the colour rolled. The colours correspond to categories that include famous persons, known places, sport, music, other arts and traditions. If a player correctly answers a question, he may "go for a wedge" by answering another question in the same category. If he does so, he keeps that card. If the player answers a question incorrectly, it is the next players turn. Play ends when a player has collected six cards/correctly answered 12 questions.

Answer trivia questions on cards and watch how every answer leads to the next question. Each card features 6 questions. Get one point per correct answer, and then pass the turn to the next player for whom you will take out a card and read 6 questions. Most points after everyone's been read a card wins.

Pakbo is a deduction game for two children. Both players have two thin plastic boats and standees for them. One represents their own boat and the other represent the other players boat. Players have also double set of six animals and twelve reply cards.

At the start of the game both players secretly attach six animal passengers in their own boat. There is numbered spaces (1-6) on the boat for the passengers.

On players turn, they will ask a question about the position of certain animal. (Example: Where is the cat sitting?). The other player answers with the answer card. There are three possible answer cards. Chosen animal is behind some other animal OR chosen animal is front of another animal OR the number where the chosen animal sits. After the answer player discards the used card. Then it's other players turn to ask.

When player thinks, that they know the right order of the animals, they can use their turn to guess. If the guess is right they have won the game, otherwise it's the other players turn.

Flippin' Ninjas is a game of stealth, memory, bluffing, and strategy. In this quick-paced hidden-knowledge game, be the first to use your Ninjas to capture your opponent’s Daimyo.

However, these Ninjas are SO good at what they do that even you don't know where they are! When the game begins, pieces are placed face-down, with their identities hidden from both players. On your turn, you can flip over your pieces to reveal their identities and power. Ninjas can be flipped back down for a sneak attack, or they can be used to challenge hidden foes!

Along with stealthy Ninjas, you command noble Samurai, fierce Sohei Warrior Monks, loyal Komainu and fierce Dragons. Each piece moves along a square grid and captures other pieces of equal or lesser power. Use your pieces strategically to surprise and outwit your opponent.

—description from the publisher

Abduction Deduction is a dice strategy game where players play as farmers at night trying to get their cows into their barns to save them from a spaceship abducting cows.  Farmers can score additional points for getting close to the spaceship, but don’t get too close for the spaceship can also abduct farmers too.  The spaceship can also be controlled by any player on their turn and control opponents’ farmers and cows. Players are rewarded by barning cows and abducting cows by gaining additional barns to place and gaining power ups to make the spaceship more powerful.
 —description from the designer

boardgame: Wizard Arena: Fire and Ice Edition
description:
Wizard Arena is a fast-paced card battle game for two players. Draw cards to build up a Power Level that is higher than your opponent, but not too high or you take damage. Cast Spells to outplay your opponent and find strategic ways to use powerful combinations of spells. Each player gets their own Magic Faction with unique spells. With more Factions coming in future expansions.

Learning time: 5 minutes. Play time: 15+ minutes.

In the box: 58 Power cards, 48 Spell cards, 2 Reference cards and 1 rule sheet

This is a standalone game for 2 players with the two Magic Factions- Fire and Ice.

You can combine this with Wizard Arena: Feast and Famine for 3+ multiplayer games.

Produced by Panda Game Manufacturing.

—description from the designer

The game is basically a version of the game Afrikan tähti only with it's theme changed to the Finnish city of Kuopio. You roll to move in the city of Kuopio and try to find Kalakukko, a traditional Savonian fish pie. When you arrive to a square with tile, you have to pay 5 marks to open it. If you don't have money or you don't want to pay, you can try to roll even number to turn the tile. Main reason for turning the tiles is to find Kalakukko, but there are other happenings too (get money, loose turns and so on). After the Kalakukko has been found the winning player is the one who arrives first to Market hall of Kuopio.

Trivia game about Finnish province called Ostrobothnia. This is travel edition of Pohjanmaan tähti. Instead of board you have sheet of paper where you cross circles, when you answer correctly to a question about Ostrobothnia. Winner is the player who first reaches agreed amount of points.

Step into the bustling marketplace at the turn of the century, where clever trading and sharp bidding will decide who becomes the wealthiest merchant in town!

In Market Street, 2–4 players compete to fulfill customer orders by collecting the right goods through lively auctions and cunning hand management. Every round, players bid their limited coins on goods and powerful special cards, trying to outwit their opponents and secure the most lucrative deliveries.

Delivering goods to customers earns you valuable customer cards, each granting one or two gold stars (victory points). Special cards let you manipulate the market, steal goods, or refresh the customer row, creating exciting twists and strategic opportunities.

The game ends when the agreed number of customer cards has been fulfilled. The player with the most gold stars on their collected customers wins. In case of a tie, the player with the most cards left in hand is declared the winner.

—description from the designer

A team game of acting, humming & general knowledge for the whole family

Goal: Be the first team to cross off all 8 sprouts on your score sheet.

How to Play:

On your team’s turn, spin the spinner:
