Twist: Pick a Twist card. One teammate must hum a tune or act something out silently (like charades). The other teammate has 60 seconds to guess correctly.

Sprout: Pick a Sprout card. One teammate reads a quiz question to the other, who has 60 seconds to answer.

Miss a Go: Your team skips this turn.

If your team guesses correctly within the time limit, cross off one sprout on your score sheet.

Winning the Game:

The first team to cross off all 8 sprouts wins.

You are in New York City: the Mecca of street basketball, where you can play 3x3 on the most famous and legendary urban courts in the world.
Pick the best team, move skillfully around the court, attack with intelligence, and defend yourself with all your weapons.
This is street sport, to win you will have to put in your part strategy, mentality, courage and of course trust in your good luck.
Emulate 3x3 matches in this innovative card game that captures the essence of street basketball.
Streetballers move around an imaginary board. Actions are performed through attack and defense cards. This cards represents. At the same time, these action cards represent the game time. Shoot, pass the ball, dunk the hood,steal, block, grab the rebound...The Team that scores the most win the game.

Jano is a heartwarming, nature-themed board game where players join a curious Hungarian Vizsla on an enchanting journey through farms, forests, and lakesides. By collecting resources, forming friendships with animals, gathering flowers, and completing secret missions, players earn victory points and create their own unique adventure. The player who embraces nature and friendship most skillfully becomes the happiest explorer of all!

—description from the publisher

Fuzeo is a clever and accessible card game for ages 4 and up, inviting children on a space adventure with 2 games in 1!

Players will first build rockets to send quirky animals into space, then assemble space shuttles so their astronauts can join them.

Key features:

     2 games in 1: Two different mechanics for better replayability.
     Perfect for young children, starting at age 4, with simple and easy-to-learn rules.
     A universal space theme and talented illustrators for full immersion.
     Compact format, ideal for small display areas and family travel.


Game Level 1: Rocket Launch!
Draw an animal card and try to place it in one of your rockets.
If the animal doesn’t match your objectives, give it to another player!
Be the first to complete and launch 3 rockets to win the game!

Game Level 2: Shuttle Mission!
Assemble a shuttle made up of 4 cards.
If you place the final card that completes the shuttle, board one of your astronauts.
The first player to place all 3 astronauts in the shuttles wins the game!

—description form the publisher

Fuzeo est un jeu de cartes malin et accessible dès 4 ans, qui invite les enfants à partir à la conquête de l’espace à travers 2 jeux en 1 !

Les joueurs devront construire des fusées pour y envoyer de drôles d’animaux, puis assembler des navettes spatiales pour que leurs astronautes puissent les rejoindre.

Les points forts :

     2 jeux en 1 : deux mécaniques différentes pour une meilleure rejouabilité.
     Adapté aux plus jeunes, dès 4 ans, avec des règles simples et rapides à prendre en main.
     Un thème spatial universel et des illustrateurs de talent pour une immersion totale.
     Un format compact, idéal pour les rayons petits formats et les voyages en famille.


Jeu niveau 1 : Lancement de fusées !
Pioche une carte animal et tente de la placer dans l'une de tes fusées. Si l’animal ne correspond pas à tes objectifs, donne-le à un autre joueur !
Sois le premier à compléter et faire décoller 3 fusées pour remporter la partie !

Jeu niveau 2 : Mission navette !
Assemble une navette composée de 4 cartes.
Si tu poses la dernière carte qui complète la navette, installe un de tes astronautes à bord.
Le premier à placer ses 3 astronautes dans les navettes gagne la partie !

—description form the publisher (French)

Saï Saï is a dexterity and strategy game for ages 5 and up, where players must guide their flock of sheep to the legendary Tender Grass meadow!

With colorful dice and tilted boards, SAÏ SAÏ combines agility, smart choices, and lots of laughs for dynamic family play.

Key features:

     A strategic dexterity game, perfect for developing motor skills and anticipation.
     Simple rules, easy to learn, and instant fun from age 5.
     Attractive visuals by Juliette Oberndorfer, with a poetic and playful universe.
     Includes “Grandma Sheep” and “Little Lamb” variants to enrich gameplay.
     Original components and impressive shelf presence thanks to the large box format.


Roll the SaïSaï dice to find out which colors of sheep to move. Slide your sheep cleverly on your tilted board without blocking others!

The first player to get all 12 sheep into the meadow (the game box) wins!

—description from the publisher

Saï Saï est un jeu d’adresse et de stratégie accessible dès 5 ans, où les joueurs doivent faire gravir leur troupeau de brebis jusqu’à la mythique prairie d’Herbe tendre !

Avec ses dés colorés et ses plateaux inclinés, SAÏ SAÏ mêle agilité, choix malins et grands éclats de rire pour des parties dynamiques et familiales.

Les points forts :

     Un jeu de dextérité stratégique, idéal pour développer la motricité et l’anticipation.
     Règles simples, prise en main immédiate, plaisir immédiat dès 5 ans.
     Visuels attrayants signés Juliette Oberndorfer, univers poétique et drôle.
     Variante Mémé Brebis et variante Petit Agneau incluses pour enrichir le gameplay.
     Matériel original et beau volume en rayon grâce au grand format de boîte.


Lance les dés Saï Saï pour découvrir quelles couleurs de brebis pousser. Glisse astucieusement tes brebis sur ton plateau incliné, sans bloquer les autres !

Le premier joueur à faire grimper ses 12 brebis dans la prairie (la boîte de jeu) remporte la partie !

—description from the publisher (French)

Weird Harry is competing for points with 5 other suspicious characters. The dice are rolled: Now it's all about recognising quickly, reacting correctly and aiming accurately. Drop the right Harry very quickly and the point is yours. But things get wilder from round to round. You need to keep your nerve, be quick and always get the right Harry into play.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Amid the Chaos: The choices are simple, they're just not easy
description:
“In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity” – Sun Tzu.
One of the key ideas Sun Tzu presented in his "Art of War" was that war was dynamic and constantly changing. Battles could be won by a general who can identify opportunities amidst the chaos and anticipate and adapt to changes as they occurred on the battlefield.

Amid the Chaos is a set of Hex based Wargames rules that will allow players to command armies on the tabletop from the period 3000 BC to approximately 1900 AD. This is accomplished by providing a set of Core Rules which can be combined with the Historical supplements in this series to provide a complete wargame for each period.

The rules have an innovative variable player turn mechanic as each side activates, and responds to activations as they take place, using their activation for the turn to do so. This provides for a very fluid turn sequence with all commanders continually involved in the game. All units will have an opportunity to activate every turn, although this activation may be limited by your opponent’s choices and your response to those choices.

Miniatures of any size and basing can be used to re-fight these battles using a grand tactical approach where the players themselves represent the great generals throughout the ages. As such these rules deal less with the individual heroics of the fighting man and more with command and morale than general unit attrition.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Amid the Chaos: The Age of Pike and Powder – 1350 AD to 1671 AD Supplement
description:
The Amid the Chaos rules system will allow players to command armies on the tabletop from the period 3000 BC to approximately 1900 AD. This Historical supplement covers the period from 1350 AD to 1671 AD. The Amid the Chaos: Core Rules are required to use this supplement.

Miniatures of any scale and any basing can be used to re-fight these battles using a grand tactical approach where the players themselves represent the great generals throughout the ages. As such, these rules focus more on command, control and morale than on general unit attrition

boardgame: Amid the Chaos: The Age of Chariots – 3000 BC to 700 BC Supplement
description:
The Amid the Chaos rules system will allow players to command armies on the tabletop from the period 3000 BC to approximately 1900 AD. This Historical supplement covers the period from 3000 BC to 700 BC. The Amid the Chaos: Core Rules are required to use this supplement.

Miniatures of any scale and any basing can be used to re-fight these battles using a grand tactical approach where the players themselves represent the great generals throughout the ages. As such, these rules focus more on command, control and morale than on general unit attrition.

Get ready to kick some shell—the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles are coming to UniVersus! This action-packed booster set features over 180 cards inspired by Tales of the TMNT, Mutant Mayhem, the classic 80s cartoon, and retro action figures.

This Set features:

     Over 180 cards packed with nods to Tales of the TMNT, Mutant Mayhem, action figures, and classic cartoon characters
     15 playable characters, featuring the Turtles and their iconic allies and enemies
     Serialized Chrome Rares (just 100 of each exist—no reprints, no reruns)
     Ooze-treated alt-art cards printed with UV-reactive neon green ink and bold black-and-white designs
     A ready-to-play Starter Deck built for both new and returning players featuring nostalgic retro turtles


—description from the publisher
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
A neat little set of extra cards for Corvids, including the rare Piapiac as well as five copies of the 'marble' card.

Available at GenCon 2025 and online for a short time after.

—description from the publisher

A special Design card that encourages you to use up a priest, as well as five ‘downgrade’ Favor cards.

Available at GenCon 2025 and online for a short time after.

—description from the publisher

Happy Letters is a fast, fun, and highly accessible word game for the whole family!  
Each player has their own personal letter grid. In each round, players must find a word related to a shared theme — aiming to cover as many letters as possible in a single row or column of their grid.

The ultimate goal is simple: be the first to completely fill your grid!

But here’s what makes Happy Letters truly special: the game includes 12 difficulty levels, allowing each player to choose a challenge suited to their own skill level. 
That means a 10-year-old can face off against a seasoned word game expert — and everyone still has a fair shot at winning.  
It’s the first word game with scalable difficulty, perfectly tailored to fit the level of each player.

With accessible themes, rules you can learn in under 3 minutes, and a clever mix of creativity and deduction, Happy Letters offers the perfect blend of simplicity and depth — a word game that grows with your group and promises great fun for everyone, no matter their experience.

Get ready for an experience filled with strategy and plenty of mischief.

In this fun and engaging game, you and your friends are customers at a charming café, competing to see who can rack up the highest bill at the end of the game. But here, it's not enough to just choose your favorite sweets; you also need to keep an eye on the other players' movements. They'll try to steal your sweets, split your bill, or even close down their own cafés to avoid being robbed. Each round brings new opportunities and challenges, keeping all players alert and engaged.

Café da Tarde is a fast-paced card game for 2 to 5 players. On their turn, players play cards from their hands to their table, representing different treats and actions. The main goal is to collect valuable food cards and protect them while disrupting your opponents' collections. Cards may allow you to steal from others, block thefts, split points, or trigger other effects.

The game ends when the draw pile runs out or a set number of rounds is completed. Players then add up the values of the food cards they managed to keep. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the designer

Misterio en Sleepy Hollow ("Mystery in Sleepy Hollow") is set in the old town of Sleepy Hollow from Washington Irving's novel, but with a Mexican twist. It's a card game for two players in which you must face another player to first discover what mystery lies behind the disappearances in Sleepy Hollow.

During your turn, you must choose whether to draw a card from the deck or attempt a roll of the dice. This will determine your fate, or that of your opponent. The first player to find 20 clues or mystery points without reaching 10 panic points and dying of fright will win.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: The Legend of Sleepy Hollow

description:
InQueeries is a vibrant and irreverent card game designed to celebrate and connect the LGBTQ+ community and their allies. Tailored for players ages 17 and up, it’s perfect for Pride gatherings, game nights, or any social event that calls for laughter and connection. With humor and inclusivity at its core, the game sets the stage for bold conversations, inside jokes, and hilarious moments that affirm identity and community.

Gameplay revolves around 450 cards filled with risqué prompts and meme-style humor. Players take turns responding to prompts or matching cards in a way that will earn the most laughs or approval from the round’s “Crown” holder—a rotating judge role. The player whose card is chosen wins a point for that round. The first person to reach 10 points wins the game—and gets to show off the sparkly, holographic Crown included in the box.

The overall goal of InQueeries is to have fun, be a little outrageous, and create shared moments that celebrate queer culture. Whether you're playing with longtime friends or new faces, the game is a catalyst for stories, shade, and joy. It’s not just about winning—it’s about affirming connection, humor, and pride.

—description from the publisher

Players get a hand of five cards and take turns placing cards on a 2x6 grid; if they have multiple cats of the same color they can play them all at once. Opponents can play puppy and bird cards to remove cats from the grid, with the goal being to be the first to collect twelve cats.

DNPS scenario published in Alea #35 (2020)

24 counters + map

Holy Roman Emperor Charles V launches a surprise attack on the French encampment besieging Pavia, Lombardy in 1525, capturing Francis I and scattering his forces.

DNPS scenario published in Alea #32 (2007)

34 counters + map

Fernández de Córdoba recovers the Roman port of Ostia from the French in 1497. 
Fernández is credited with introducing the field innovations from which the tercios emerged and were later formalised by 1536 Ordinance of Genoa.

In Fall Creek, players take on the role of shapeshifting spirits racing to save the dying World Tree. Each player starts in a different Form (tiger, eagle, shark, and more) and must adapt to a changing environment by acquiring new Forms and Treasures while enduring harsh Curses and unpredictable Events.

The game blends cooperation, modular map exploration, and resource management, with each card played reshaping your character’s strengths and your strategy.

Each session of Fall Creek plays out as a race: players must reach and return the secret to healing the World Tree before being overwhelmed by the encroaching decay. But every choice comes at a cost—energy is scarce, and every action brings you closer to exhaustion or corruption.

—description from the designer

East-West is a two-player dice game featured in Dice Games Properly Explained. Players compete to create three strong Poker combinations across rows with the goal of winning two of the three comparisons.

Building-Brick Lotto is played exactly like every other Lotto. The bricks are laid one upon another, after the manner of the accompanying illustration, the person who has his house ready first is the winner.
If the game is played with counters the first winner takes two-thirds and the second one third of the counters.

In Hacienda, you’re not just managing land—you’re restoring an ecosystem. Set in the wilds of Argentina, players take on the role of eco-conscious entrepreneurs re-wilding the Patagonian steppe. Through careful stewardship, you'll introduce native animals like pumas, capybaras, and guanacos while competing to create the most thriving eco-tourism business.

The game blends card-driven strategy with spatial planning. Each turn, players use terrain and animal cards to place land and animal tokens on a modular board. Expanding habitats, building chains of land, and connecting to water sources or visitor centers are key to scoring points. Strategic placement unlocks powerful actions, while viewing platforms and water tiles offer long-term advantages.

This edition of Hacienda introduces several major changes from the second edition: a fully modular board that scales with player count, a brand-new card-based action system that replaces the original currency mechanism, and a completely reimagined theme focused on conservation and eco-tourism. It also features upgraded wooden components for animals and land markers, adding a premium tactile feel. For even greater variety, the game includes variable board powers via special Visitor Centers that add new layers of strategic depth and replayability.

—description form the publisher

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – Invincible Arena
description:
Invincible Arena is a Standalone expansion that features an exciting new way to play the game – RAID BOSSES. Each Raid Boss is a battle unto itself, with unique modes of attack and shifting tactics that guarantee that no two fights are ever the same.

Every battle is unique, with shifting boss modes and tactics.  Four new Raid Bosses are introduced, as well as 4 new Vault Hunters.

It's time to fight or Die in the Invisible Arena!

—description from the publisher

KeyForge: Crucible Clash is a special KeyForge set designed specifically for competitive sealed play.

Each Crucible Clash deck consists of three of seven different houses of the Crucible, each featuring a mixture of brand new cards and classic favorites. Plus, each deck includes a special Archon Power selectively chosen for the deck based on the other cards within.

KeyForge: Crucible Clash decks are legal for sealed tournaments against other Crucible Clash decks, but only legal in Archon or Alliance tournament formats without the use of the Archon Power card.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – Maliwan Crime Busters
description:
Maliwan’s bots are here to serve some hot steaming justice beatings – for the guilty and the innocent!

Maliwan Crime Busters is full of gritty crime scenarios, mind melting mysteries, and dramatic shootouts. Vault Hunters must search for clues, solve crimes, and maintain their case file between games. Includes four new Vault Hunters: Rhys, Marcus, Balex, and Fiona with sidekick Gortys, as well as 23 Maliwan bots.

—description from the publisher

Great ready for a speedy road trip across the USA with this fast action geography game! If you see a Slug Bug go by, be the fastest one to slap the deck and collect the USA states. Use the USA Map to connect the states at the end of the game to create your road trip. The player with the longest road trip wins! Play this game a few times, and Jr. Ranger's will become expert geographers. Beginner and advanced play styles help little travelers develop motor skills and learn USA geography.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – Torgue's Forge Blast
description:
Torgue’s loaders have run amok in the arena and it’s up to you to stop them in this scenario-packed campaign.

New arena tiles offer ways to move around the board and interact with industrial hazards while new Slaughterlight cards raise the heat with each activation. Also includes 4 new Vault Hunters: Ellie, Vaughn, Octavio, and Anu.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Rebirth – One-Shot Pack 3: Adam's Vengeance
description: Rebirth One-Shot Pack #3 features the magical due of Zatanna and Shazam as fully playable Characters and their Signature cards to unlock during a Rebirth campaign.  This pack also includes a new Scenario, "Adam's Vengeance," that can be played between the normal Scenarios of a Rebirth Campaign (allowing you to unlock even more cards for that Campaign!) or as a standalone game with special rules.

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – Into the Badland
description:
What happens when your models break free of the Arena and venture into the Badlands? Trick Question: Murder, mayhem, and open air EXPLOSIONS! is what happens. Into the Badlands allows you to use your existing miniature collection in skirmish battles using Monster Fight Club’s [RE]action System.

—description from the publisher

Desperate battle in the far future.

Set in the grim darkness of the far future, Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War – Onslaught is a tense, tactical board game based on the smash hit series of video games. Take control of an elite force of Blood Ravens Space Marines and fend off waves of Orks with blade and bolter. If you love the video games, this is a great way to take the action from the screen to the tabletop. Play solo, or grab up to three of your friends, choose your heroes, and work together to defend Humanity against seemingly endless alien hordes.

Cooperative, tactical board game for one to four players


     Nine highly detailed plastic miniatures which can be assembled without glue
     Fast-paced strategic gameplay, challenging combat, and objective control
     Includes everything you need to play – including game board, dice, cards, and tokens
     36-page booklet includes game rules, and model assembly guides


—description from the publisher
family: Game: Space Marine Adventures – Labyrinth of the Necrons

description:
Players are aspiring galactic overlords vying for dominance by building a shadow empire. Thereby, they rely on a mysterious entity known as the Nexus Syndicate – a secretive pan-galactic network with a hold on infrastructure, intelligence, and political influence.
Players operate within the Syndicate’s framework, rising in favour among its different factions and leveraging their resources in order to gain political power. In this game of mutual dependencies, expand your influence and gain the highest scores across 9 sectors to come out on top!

A quick overview of the three factions:

Voidshapers possess advanced technology and knowledge, supporting Nexus' endeavours with fundamental infrastructure such as interplanetary trade routes, communication channels, and hidden space bases. Benefit from their ability to link and expand!

Although part of the Syndicate, Shadowblades often pursue their own interests without the other factions’ knowledge. If necessary, these secretive assassins will not even shy away from silencing actors within the Syndicate. Use their actions to eliminate unwanted actors.

Soulbinders are responsible for the recruitment of new Syndicate members. In addition, and most importantly, the higher ranks of the faction specialise in manipulation, storage, and transfer of consciousness, thereby steering political processes in favour of the Syndicate. Support them in their efforts and, in turn, you will be rewarded with influence in the individual sectors.

Nexus Syndicate uses Looney Pyramids (from Looney Labs). Furthermore, 3 d6 dice per colour (blue/red/green), 9 tiles (beer mats will do) and 20 custom setup cards are needed to play the game.

During a turn, players first move pyramids before carrying out a specific action tied to the abilities of the three factions in play. For your movement, choose a sector tile that still has at least one pyramid on it. Pick up all the pyramids from that tile and place them onto contiguous sector tiles, one by one (orthogonal movement, no diagonals!). The last pyramid must be placed onto a sector that already contains a pyramid (or a stack) of that same colour AND size or a die (representing council members) of that same colour.

There are different actions to carry out depending on the colour (blue/red/green) and size (small/medium) of the last pyramid of your move. Basic actions can be carried out with small and medium pyramids, advanced actions are restricted to medium pyramids. The last placed pyramid initiates the action. If a small pyramid is placed last, it immediately goes into the active player’s personal stock (aka "popularity pool"), along with ALL other pyramids of same colour and size that were already on that tile. If a medium pyramid is placed last, it simply remains on the tile, forming a stack with all other pyramids of same colour and size that were already on that tile.

Blue actions immediately let you gain victory points based on the number of orthogonally adjacent sectors containing at least one blue pyramid or blue die. Red actions let you take away pyramids or dice pips of your choice from sectors that are orthogonally adjacent to the final tile of your move. The basic green action lets you respawn pyramids to orthogonally adjacent tiles. The advanced green action (“Steering”) is arguably the most important action in the game, as it lets you place influence markers and add/alter dice on sectors which already contain influence markers.

The game ends
a) when all 6 influence markers have been placed next to sector tiles and remain on the board after player 2 has taken their turn 
or
b) if there are no possible moves. 
In other words, player 2 always takes the last turn of the game unless b) occurs. If, during this last turn, player 2 removes an influence marker (through the advanced red action “Silencing II”), the game continues until either a) or b) occurs.

For each player, tally the points they gain on sector tiles: 
- Score all sectors that contain the player’s influence markers. The following applies: Most influence markers on a sector: 5 VP; second most: 3 VP; tie: 4 VP.
- For each sector that is tied or dominated by the player, multiply the number of blue/red/green pyramids in their popularity pool with the sum of same-coloured pyramids and die pips in the respective sector. Score all colours in this sector respectively, then continue with the next sector).

Add the total VP of the tile scoring to the VP earned through blue actions throughout the game – the sum is the final score. In the unlikely event of a tie, the player who has placed more influence markers wins the game. If this also results in a tie, the player with the higher total number of pyramids in their popularity pool wins the game.

Euro-Card is an "academic game" intended to test players' knowledge of facts about countries in Europe.
The box includes five different games titled, "Spot It," "Name It," "Find It," "Sum It," and "Manage It."
Spot It is a competitive game that challenges the players to quickly identify European countries by the shape of their border.  Players reveal a Nation Card showing the shape of a country and compete to identify the country first.
Name It challenges the players to identify the locations of European countries on a map of Europe.  Name It uses the same Nation Cards but the players must associate the country with the location on the map.
Find It is designed to acquaint players with the kinds of information available in reference materials.  Players reveal a question card and a nation card and must look up the information in a reference book.
Sum It plays similarly to Find It, except that players must organize and defend the organization of the information they obtain.
Manage It is a trivia game where players wager points on each question.  The Question Cards are paired up with a random Nation Card to provide replayability.

A game of hilarious physical challenges that will have you and your friends literally bending over backwards to win!

Random combinations of cards force players into contests that test their creativity, flexibility, and sense of humor.

Rewrite or relive world history!

In Commanders & Conflicts, players take command of iconic military and political leaders to battle across history’s most pivotal conflicts. Engage in simultaneous card play and lightning-fast tactical decisions as you manage resources, control terrain, activate combos, and outwit your opponent.

Commanders & Conflicts also serves as a valuable history lesson; learn key facts about each battle as you play!

—description from the publisher

This game was inspired by Splotter Spellen's latest work, “Horseless Carriage,” the publisher of my favorite game, “Food Chain Magnate.”

Designed with a heavy emphasis on player interaction, it is a heavy pen-and-paper game in which turn order, technology, the performance of the vehicles developed, and a variety of other factors create interaction.

Players can develop their company's technology almost effortlessly, but it remains challenging to develop vehicles that fully meet demand. This is because competitors will sell better vehicles faster. ......

The expenses incurred during the game for the technology the player grows, the cars he builds, and the parts he buys are not charged to the player until the end of the game. But, yes, there is a cost to every move. Is the decision really profitable for the company? Even if it looks like a big profit, it is doubtful how much net profit is left when the company pays the bill for its reckless development. Sell the last-minute car at maximum speed.

—description from the designer

Two teams answer questions about health to move their three pawns to the board's Finish space.

The board consists of a ring of six hexagonal sectors. Each sector contains a ring of six hexagonal spaces. On a team's turn, they roll the die and move one of their three pawns. Pawns start in the first sector. They move from space to space, clockwise, within a sector. If the team correctly answers a question, they take an additional turn. A pawn stays within a sector unless it lands on a space marked with a white arrow, which allows it to move to the next sector. A pawn can also move to the next sector if a team correctly answers a question labelled CHOICE. However, if the team incorrectly answers a CHOICE question, they must move their pawn back to the previous sector.

The questions and answers aren't printed on individual cards like in other trivia board games. Instead, the game comes with two books of questions and answer. Two bookmarks are included, which suggests that questions should be asked in sequential order.

Merci is a game of politeness that stings! Get rid of your cards by triggering effects that force your opponents to take them back...and they also have to say Thank You! And watch out for those who forget!

Announcing the combinations you're going to make with polite formulas, which we tend to forget by dint of repetition and thanks to the atmosphere, puts the game in a cynico-amusing mood.

Staying polite in all circumstances also provokes an intergenerational relationship , as the kids won't fail to remind the adults!

Cǎilù is a dragon that gives joy to the paths he treads. Guide him trying to make your city as happy as possible without passing by the same place twice.

Gameplay:

You must bless all your houses by crossing them after the pass of Cailu. All players plays at the same time following this 2 steps until the end of the game:


     Draw a point that indicates your direction.
     A card is revealed for all players. Choose one of the two available options and cross out all squares in a straight line from the point you drew.


You can get bonuses if you choose the harder options, such as more points or advancing in Cailu’s body that give you certain special effects.

If you has to draw over a drawed house, you lose one life. The game ends when a player loses all his lifes.

—description from the desiger

boardgame: A Civile Little Warre: Skirmish Wargaming in the English Civil War
description:
Innovative sets of rules from the author of The Warre Game. Step into the muddy boots of a 17th-century Company Captain and lead your band of Foot, Horse, or Dragoons through raids, ambuscades, and outpost assaults. Designed for quick, dramatic play with just a handful of figures, this ruleset captures the chaos and camaraderie of small-scale warfare during the English Civil Wars. No points. No cards. Just orders, dice, and the clash of steel. Muster your men, issue your commands, and let history unfold on your tabletop.

—description from the publisher

30 Years War game rules. Glossy rules with free blank dice. It's a set intended for Generals and concentrates on major actions and less on the minutiae of where each pikeman or musketeer is standing. There is no figure removal and the use of sabot bases can avoid players rebasing their existing armies.

—description from the publisher

And March to your Front is a platoon/company level game for the battles on the North-West Frontier during the Second Afghan War, the wars in the Sudan against the Mahdists, and the war in South Africa against the Zulu nation. On one side there will be the British player who will command British units and native troops in British service, e.g., Indian troops and Gurkhas, or the Egyptian player who will control Egyptian units and their auxiliaries. On the other will be Afghans, Sudanese or Zulus known collectively as Native forces.

It is played on a tabletop with figures and model terrain. Each player commands a force of between 40 and 100 or so figures. These figures are formed into groups. The players then take turns performing action with these groups, such as moving, firing, rallying or other actions.

Each player will have a deck of 40 action cards unique to the nationality of their force and one time check card. They may also have supplementary cards that they will have access to but that are not part of their deck. (See National Decks page 56.) From this deck and supplementary cards, they will draw a hand of cards each turn. They will use these cards during their turn to have their groups perform actions by playing cards onto those groups and have them perform the indicated actions. It is important that the player announces the action the group is performing when they play the card. Also, players are encouraged, when describing a group’s action, to identify the leader directing that action. For example, “Lt Barr is ordering his men to fire on that group.”

Besides the playing of cards to perform actions, these rules approach movement in a unique way. The distance a group of figures moves is not defined in inches or centimetres. A ruler is not required to determine how far a group can move. Instead, a group will perform a move action and move from one terrain feature to another.

—description from the rulebook

Protect your borders, attack your neighbors, and be the first to collect three turnip relics!

Дрюква (Village Pillage) is a 2-6 player version of Village Pillage with three expansions:

     Surf
     Turf
     Robbin' Hood


in one box.

Each turn, players simultaneously choose two cards from their hand and play one against each neighbor. All cards are revealed at once, then resolved in order: Farmers, Walls, Raiders, Merchants. Farmers produce turnips, Walls block Raiders, Raiders steal turnips, and Merchants either add new cards to your hand or help you purchase a relic. All cards return to hand, and the next round begins! The game ends as soon as any one player has three royal relics.

The Stalingrad-Caucasus campaign was Hitler's greatest strategic error in World War II. Facing deficient logistical support and inadequate forces for the dual task of conquering Stalingrad and the Caucasus, the Führer divided Army Group South into two groups: Army Group "A" (Marshal Wilhelm List) to conquer the Caucasus, and Army Group "B" (Marshal Fedor von Bock) to capture Stalingrad.
The conquest of the Caucasus was considered essential due to its oil wells (such as Maykop), while Stalingrad, along with the Don and Volga rivers, would provide important flank protection for the forces advancing south toward the Caucasus. However, after the start of "Operation Blue" (June 28, 1942), Hitler increasingly diverted forces to Stalingrad, the "City of Stalin," sacrificing the advance into the Caucasus in exchange for a prestige gain. On November 19, 1942,
with the Axis 6th Army held back in Stalingrad by the Soviet 62nd Army, the Red Army launched "Operation Uranus," a massive offensive that broke through the weak Romanian defenses, captured Kalach on the Don, and encircled the entire German 6th Army in Stalingrad.
A new formation, Army Group Don, was hastily organized under the command of Marshal Erich von Manstein to attempt to rescue the encircled garrison. In vain.
The end came on February 2, 1943, with the surrender of Marshal Friedrich Paulus, commander of the 6th Army. Within the Stalingrad pocket alone, twenty German divisions and two Romanian divisions were annihilated. The German Army would never recover from such a disaster.
Hitler wanted the Caucasus and Stalingrad. He lost both, and with them, the war.

A fantasy Battle of the Bards card game.  Each person plays as a Fantasy Bard, touring the taverns and trying to win over the crowds.  While they're performing the other players bards are sat in the crowd heckling them, trying to get them booed off stage.

On the players turn they play cards from their hand using the positive score on the card, attempting to equal or beat the crowd score of the tavern.  Other players can play cards from their hand (by rotating them 180 degrees) to reduce the performing player's score.  If the performer equals or beats the crowd score, then they win the tavern.  If the Hecklers manage to make the players score lower than 0 then the player is booed off stage and the tavern is discarded.

Once a player has won 3 taverns, there is one more round and then the tour is over.  The player with the highest total crowd score from the taverns they have won is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to S.P.A.M! The card game where you play as one of six aging Morphos looking their favourite cookies in the safety of their enclosure.

Under the care and study of Professor Leighton Amadeus Boswell, these old Morphos just want to explore and eat some delicious cookies the strange humans keep giving them. However, while exploring you may come across traps, younglings and distractions that can cause you to lose precious cookies and points!

The Game is played by laying out 25 cards face down to represent the Morpho's enclosure.  Each turn the player replaces one of those face down cards with their Morpho, as they are exploring the enclosure.  The face down cards will either be a cookie (which are worth points) or an action card (which do different things like making you miss your next turn, or allowing you to steal a cookie form another player)

Once the enclosure has been fully explored the player with the most points from their cookie wins.

—description from the publisher

Best Numeronikkari (number handyman) is the one who is fastest to add, subtract, multiply or devide three numbers in line in their own randomized number puzzle.

At the start of the game every player builds their own random 6*6 -puzzle of numbers. For every player there are 36 puzzle pieces (all the numbers from 1 to 9 four times, no zeroes), that all fit together. Then you take 10 random number cards from the deck to form facedown draw deck.

In every round you turn face up the top most card from the deck. All the players start calculating with three number is line on their puzzle to get the number in the chosen card. Fastest player to get the result shouts "hep". Then the result is checked and if it's correct player gets the card. If the results is not right, then the calculating goes on, until right result is reached or players together decides, that it will not be reached. Then you turn the next card and calculating starts again, and so on.

Winner is the player who got the most right results.

Never before has the expression “food for thought” been so literal! A whole swarm of intergalactic guests is flocking to Earth’s orbit! Real fact food!

In FactFood, players compete to build the most successful intergalactic restaurant by answering quirky numerical trivia. Each round, all players guess the answer to a fun fact - whoever comes closest wins and gets to serve dishes to their customers. Once a table is fully served, the satisfied guests pay up!

Use this income to expand your restaurant with new rooms. These include additional tables for more customers or powerful Upgraders (like Arcades or Kids' Play Zones) that grant unique abilities. The first to expand to 10 rooms wins the game and claims the title of galaxy’s top foodpreneur!

---

Никогда еще выражение «пища для ума» не было таким буквальным! Целый рой межгалактических гостей слетается на орбиту Земли! Настоящий фактфуд!

В ФАКТФУД игроки соревнуются за звание владельца самого успешного межгалактического ресторана, отвечая на забавные числовые вопросы. В каждом раунде все участники делают предположение — кто окажется ближе всего к правильному ответу, тот выигрывает раунд и может обслужить своих клиентов.

Обслужив весь стол, игрок получает оплату от довольных гостей. Эти деньги можно потратить на расширение ресторана: добавить новые столы для большего потока клиентов или построить мощные улучшайзеры (например, игровые автоматы или детские комнаты), которые дают особые способности. Побеждает тот, кто первым расширит ресторан до 10 комнат и станет лучшим ресторатором галактики!

Cat Naps is a cat themed speed matching card game. At the start of the game every player chooses a Disney cat as their character card. Then all the cards are shuffled and delt evenly to all players. Deck consist of 5 treats, 1 double treat, 2 paws, 4 change direction and 75 number cards (five times 1 to 15).

Player who last saw a cat starts the game and gets the Cat Castle card. Player in turn turns up the top most card from their deck in to pile in the middle and says "one meow". Then next player in turn order turns up the top most card of their deck and says "two meows". And this goes on until the new card has the same number as the top most card in the middle or the same number, that player is saying. When this happens the fastest player pounces the deck in the middle and gets the middle pile under their deck.  Also the Cat Castle card moves to the next player and counting start again from "one meow". If "Fifteen meow" is reached the counting starts again from "one meow". When treat car is turned to middle the fastest pouncer gets it.

First player to get three treats and the Cat Castle wins the game.

Each round 4 fishes will be put to auction.

Each player has 14 signposts. The player who presents the largest number using a signpost gets the fishes.

At the end of the game each player aligns their fishes and compare the length. The player with the longest fish line wins.

Their are two decks 48 robot cards in red and green. At the start of the game the green cards are laid down in the grid so that all cards can be seen. Red cards are shuffled and they form the draw deck. On the card are robots with following characteristics: shape, mood and colour of the robot, type and number of legs, arms and eyes and shape of mouth and antennas.

Player in turn throws two dice and then takes a card from the draw pile and tries to explain which robot it is. Two dice dictate which characteristics of the robot player can't use on the  explanation. Die faces of the first are mouth, foot, arm and eye once and shape twice. Die faces of the second die are amount, size, mood and antenna once and colour twice.

Other players put their playing piece on the robot they think is the right one. After everybody has placed their robots, the one with the right robot takes the corresponding card from the table as a point. If nobody guesses the right robot, both cards are discarded. If the explaining player uses one or more characteristics of the dice, the the player who notices it, will get the card as a point.

The game ends, when all the cards are taken off from the table. Winner is the player with biggest pile of cards.

あなた方は化石発掘調査団の一員です。今日も貴重な化石を求めて、発掘現場にやってきました。
さあ、推理と勘と度胸で、ライバルよりも多くの古生物を発掘しましょう。

古生物の化石の発掘を競うゲームです。
順番が来たら、まず、今回 発掘する古生物の名前を宣言します。次に、山札から５枚のカードを引き、それらの中に宣言した古生物が含まれていれば、それらのカードをすべて獲得します。

獲得したカード以外、つまりハズレのカードは山札の一番上に再び戻します。その際、山札の一番上のカードは表向きにされます。また、戻すカードのうち１枚は山札の底に沈めることもできます。これによって、実に微妙な情報戦が勃発するでしょう。

You are members of a fossil excavation team, returning once more to the dig site in search of priceless specimens.
Now, use your deduction, intuition, and courage to unearth more prehistoric creatures than your rivals!

In this game, you will compete to excavate the fossils of ancient creatures.

On your turn, first declare the name of the prehistoric creature you will attempt to excavate. Then, draw 5 cards from the deck. If any of the cards you drew match the creature you declared, you claim all of those matching cards.

Any cards you did not claim are considered "misses" and are returned to the top of the deck. When you return them, the new top card of the deck is kept face up. Furthermore, you may choose one of your "miss" cards and place it on the bottom of the deck instead.

This creates a subtle battle of wits and information!

DOOMTILE is a tactical arena brawler played on a randomly assembled and dinamically modifiable arena of 25 tiles of different kind, each with its specific effect.

Your goal is to outscore your opponents by being the first to reach 10 Victory Points, or by having the most points when the fifth round ends. You earn points by eliminating other Heroes in combat or by completing secret objectives, like conquering a set number of tiles (by walking over them) or interact with the battlefield in some way.

The turn structure is very simple: at the start, you draw two cards and receive an amount of mana based on the number of the round. Then you get an Action Phase, where you can spend Mana to cast spells, set traps, and equip upgrades, or to perform actions like moving, building, swapping or destroying tiles.

If you ever need more mana, you can always get some by discarding cards. The catch is that those same cards are used not only for their effects, but also to decide the outcome of Combat, so you should manage your resources very carefully or you will be an easy prey.

Whenever you move next to an enemy, you both will be forced to get in Combat, and whoever loses the fight will be removed from the game. But it's not over yet! Elimination is temporary, and Heroes will respawn at the start of their next turn, ready to jump back into the fray, so be careful who you mess with!

—description from the designer

In the quiet elegance of the court, power hides in subtlety. Shinoburedo places players in a struggle of veiled intentions, each trying to avoid winning tricks, lest they suffer dishonor (and penalty points). But a mysterious Secret Scroll changes hands during the game, granting protection from punishment… if you can seize it without being too obvious. Strategy lies not in dominance, but in restraint.

Shinoburedo is a trick-avoidance game with unique clash mechanics.
Players seek to avoid taking tricks, which cause penalties — unless they end the round holding the Secret Scroll, which nullifies the punishment. However, matching numbers in a trick activate Tōbuku (討伏): earlier-played matching cards are flipped face-down and become ineligible to win, but still count against the player who collects them, increasing their penalty.

Mayor Tenpenny invites you to his office, where his desk is piled high with paperwork. “Simple amusements may have been good enough for last year’s guests,” he tells you, “but the theme park patrons of today demand novelty and innovation.”  You’re going to need some specialists to help you expand!

Innovation is an expansion for Tenpenny Parks, the worker and tile placement game where you compete to build the best vintage amusement park in town. In this new era of your park, you must lead your fairgrounds into the future with fresh ideas that will draw even more visitors to Fairview.

How will you focus this vision into reality? Hire new Specialists for your park, each with their own ideas and abilities to help you create grand (and lucrative!) ways to score.

Meanwhile, do some science! Advance your new Researcher Worker up the track on the new Innovation board to help pave the way for your pioneering plans.

With all your monumental ideas for development, you’re also going to need more space! Innovations includes all-new player boards with unique asymmetric abilities and layouts. It also includes a new set of real estate expansion boards with further variety, including the challenge of special Landmarks.

Featuring the unmistakable art of Vincent Dutrait, Tenpenny Parks  now offers a more varied and layered strategic experience. With more tracks, choices, and joyous paths to victory, you’ll have more roller coaster ups and downs than ever before, with a little Innovation!

*This expansion is compatible with the Winter Lights & Spooky Nights expansions, and requires the Tenpenny Parks base game to play.

— Publisher description

For The People is a trick-taking card game in which each player takes on the role of a political party leader, aiming to collect as many policies as possible that align with their party's manifesto.

During each session of Parliament (a trick), every party leader plays one policy card. Only the policy with the highest level of public interest (i.e. the strongest card) is voted on. The leader who played that card wins the trick and takes all the policy cards submitted by the players.
However, in a nation where public interest in politics is low, it's possible for corrupt policies (black cards) to be voted on first. Honest and transparent parties may sometimes find themselves outmanoeuvred by those engaging in malfeasance.

When Parliament adjourns, each party gains or loses points based on how many policy cards they have collected that match their manifesto, as well as the number of other policies they have acquired. The goal is to end the game with the highest total score.

boardgame: Tenpenny Parks: Fairview Challenges Mini Expansion
description:
Fairview Challenges adds new public goals cards for players to compete over.

Compatible with the base game and all expansions.

—description from the publisher

An ancient prophecy has awakened the Guardians of EtherEa.
Enter a world where Fire, Water, Earth, Air, Light, and Shadow clash.
Choose your Guardian, seize key zones, cast spells, and outsmart rivals with bold moves and clever bluffing.
 AetherFall is a strategic board game focused on memory, bluffing, and area control.
Players compete to conquer and hold 6 elemental zones until the end of the game, using face-down cards whose value—combined with acquired bonuses—determines the outcome of each clash.

Gameplay Overview
Cards are always played face-down, and the total value is calculated by adding:

     The value of the Main Card
     Any supporting cards
     Applicable bonuses: Guardian, Area, and Elemental Dominance


Special cards can be played offensively or defensively, face-up or face-down, and may alter effects, values, or card positions.

When a Closure Card is drawn, the game enters the BONUS rounds, triggering special rules that can completely shift the game's outcome.

Victory Conditions
You win by either:

     Conquering all 6 zones


or

     Achieving the highest total score by winning more clashes and gaining the most points.


—description from the publisher

Senaattorifusku ("Senator Cheat") is a card game Cheat (In Finland it's called Fusku.) variant themed with Finnish Politics. From 60's to 90's there were 4 "major parties" in Finland and then some minor ones. There are 24 politicians from these major parties (KOM, KOK, SDP, KESK) on the cards, and then there 12 politicians from minor parties and Urkki (President Kekkonen). On Senaattorifusku you are trying to get rid of your cards and just like in Cheat, you are allowed to lie, what you are playing.

Deck of cards are shuffled and dealt equally for all the players. On your turn you play one card from your hand to table face down and declare which party that card represent. You can only declare, that you are playing one of the major parties. If everybody is happy, then it's the next players turn to play a card. If somebody thinks, that played card does not belong to declared party, then the played card will be checked. If the party was correct, then the player who made the claim picks up all the cards to the bottom of their deck. If the party was something else, then the player who played the card takes all the cards and puts them under their deck. Winner is the player who gets rid of their cards first.

boardgame: Factions of Sol: Greetings From Pluto
description: As the name implies, this adds the planetoid Pluto to the game board, along with components for a fifth player, alliance cards for more political depth, and four new factions.

Perhepeli Wiljami consist of card deck with four families of ten cards (numbered 1 to 10) and Willian Rikurdo card. Cards are shuffled and hand of five cards is delt to all players.

On your turn you play cards of one family to the table. And then turn the top card of the deck to be trump on that round. Then the player refills their hand to five cards. Then it's the next player's turn to win all the played cards by playing higher cards of the same color or the trump cards. If all the cards are won, then the player fills their hand and plays new cards for the next player to beat. If player can't beat all the cards, they will pick up the cards they couldn't beat. Then they fill their hand and next player will play cards for the following player to beat. When the deck runs out, the game continues until one player can get rid of all the cards in their hand. Willian Rikurdo card does not belong to any family and is unbeatable.

Salasana ("Password") is a word game where you try to guess the password chosen by another player. There is deck of cards with letter on the back and three categories on the top side.

Player on turn picks up the card and chooses one word that start with the letter in the card and matches to of the categories, to be the password. Other players try to guess the word and can ask questions, but the player on turn can only use answers "yes", "no" or "I don't know.". The player who guesses the right word, gets the card to their point pile and will be the next player in turn. Player who have the highest pile of cards at the end wins.

Embark on the ascent of Mont-Haut! The hike will be long and full of twists and turns. The first to reach the summit wins the race! Grab your cards, ready, set, go!

Goal of the game
Being the first to reach the summit.

Key features:
A racing game full of twists and turns  
Suspense that lasts until the end of the game  
An original board that climbs all the way to the finish line  
A pathway game where you play with cards instead of a die

—description from the publisher

Hurry up, there’s no time to lose, we need to clean up this mess! With a little memory, luck, and teamwork, we should finish before the parents arrive!

Goal of the game:
Put away the 12 items before the parent arrives.

Key features:
A first memory game for the whole family
For once, it's fun to clean up your room!
A fairy and a friendly monster add excitement to the game.

—description from the publihser
family: Theme: Rooms: Interiour Design, Home Decoration, Tidying

description:
The eggs are about to hatch, and the mother birds are busy decorating their cozy nests.

Goal of the game:
Be the first to collect 5 baby birds.

Key features:
A first game of observation and quick matching, easy and fun
Two levels of play to gradually increase the difficulty
Adorable illustrations to enjoy
Three cute wooden birds, easy to catch.

—description from the publisher

The game consist of four different colored decks of cards and loads of double sided tiles with song titles. On the top side of the card, the category is defined and on the other side lists the ten valid song titles. Category might a band or an artist or animal in the title.

Choose the deck and use the same colored tiles. Spread the tiles on the table, chosen side on top. Then pick first card and read aloud what you have to pick. Then everybody starts to pick up song title tiles that match the chosen category. When everybody is done for picking, you will check the tiles picked. For all the right ones you get to keep it as a point. If you picked something that is not correct you put it back and loose one previously picked tile.

At the end winner is the player with most tiles.

A queen, once happy in her magnificent castle, watches her domain attacked and destroyed.

After months of despair, she decides to rebuild her castle even more beautifully and invites her subjects to restore it from the ruins.
She promises to reward the best among them by covering them in gold.

—description from the publisher

A two-player tactical card battler of brine, betrayal, and ancient magic.

In the depths of the drowned world, two ancient queens wage a bitter war beneath black waves. Once bound in harmony, Ariel and Persil ruled the oceanic realms side by side — until the Obsidian Heart, a relic of immeasurable power, was shattered. Now, the sea is fractured, its cities in ruins, its leviathans awakened, and its tides twisted with magic and vengeance.

In Hags of the Abyss, players take on the role of one of these fallen queens — Ariel, the Sovereign of the Deep Trenches, or Persil, the Betrayer of the Shallow Waters — each commanding a unique deck of creatures, relics, and dark curses. Your goal: to reclaim the ocean’s shattered balance by controlling its ancient territories or unlocking forbidden power sealed within your Endgame Card.

Gameplay Highlights

Tactical Territory Control: Deploy creatures to five central Oceanic Realms and vie for dominance through movement, combat, and clever card play.

Thematic Tide System: A shifting Tidal Tracker changes how players move and act each round, mimicking the pulse of a living sea.

The Rivalry Meter: A rising tension gauge that triggers catastrophic Tidal Anomalies and unlocks each player’s path to sudden victory.

Deck Construction Meets Duel Tactics: Every card has a purpose — whether it's a terrifying beast, a vengeful curse or a relic dredged from the deep.

Optional Hag Bargain Variant: Add chaos and cursed consequences with a variant mode where every territory claimed could trigger a mysterious pact — for better or worse.

Lore-Driven Conflict
Ariel commands the abyssal trenches, a kingdom of gloom and drowned beasts, seeking to restore order and reclaim her sacred dominion.

Persil, cast from the moonlight and siren-song, haunts the shallows with illusions and madness, aiming to reshape the seas in her seductive, chaotic image.

Their war is not just for land or power — it is for the very soul of the sea.

Print-and-Play Edition
The game is currently in development, and the available Print-and-Play version offers a complete, fast-paced, and atmospheric experience in just 30 minutes. Your feedback directly helps shape the game’s final form.

Immerse yourself in a drowned world of curses, claws, and crushed hopes. Will you rule the abyss... or be claimed by it?

Game consist of 50 cards with 10 questions with one theme on one side and second set from some other theme on other side.

Players are divided to two teams of roughly equal size. One player in the team one is chosen as a questioner. Questioner reads aloud the themes on the different sides of the card. Team one chooses the theme. Then questioner reads aloud the first question. Both teams write down the answer and then answers are checked. Correct answer always gives team a point. Then the questioner reads the second question and so on. When the 10 questions have been answered the second team choses the new questioner and new round begins. The game lasts ten rounds and the winning teams is the one with more points.

CHIPZI is packed with 49 chips numbered 1 through 7 in seven different colors.  With fast hands and fast eyes, race against each other to find 7 chips all of the same color. And that’s just one way to play! CHIPZI comes with 8 speed games, 2 non-speed games, and endless possibilities!

—description from the publisher

Brammangath (Cornish for 'Cats Fart') is a game for two players played over a number of rounds where players may choose to strategically end the game by spoiling their opponents scoring chances, from 1 point per stone in a Single Game; 2 points per stone in a Double Game and 3 points per stone for the player who wipes their opponent off the board!

Driven by a single die yet retaining tactical choices, Brammangath could be described as Cornish Backgammon, a traditional classic with a Cornish twist. It also comes with the Marric Games version of the Doubling Cube!

You take turns throwing the die and move one puck exactly that many squares. Capture your opponents if possible. If you cannot move any of your pucks you miss your turn. You must move if you are able to.

The game ends when no direct captures are possible, or a player has wiped their opponent off the board.

Collect points over a number of rounds.

As with all Marric Games the rules are easy to learn and game play is guaranteed to be addictive and tense, where defeat can be snatched out of the jaws of victory with a couple of throws.

Made locally in Cornwall with a wood board.

boardgame: Halo: Flashpoint – The Master Chief, Humanity's Greatest Weapon
description:
Bring your Master Chief Miniature, John-117, into the War Games arena! Wield the iconic Energy Sword and lead your UNSC fireteam to victory.

The Master Chief is a Legend, so he is unique on the battlefield and replaces two other members of your fireteam.  John-117 has 3+ stats for ranged, fight, and survive, along with an extra hit point and extra energy shield over his regular Spartan counterparts.  He is armed with the Energy Sword, allowing attacks both in close combat and at short range with a lunge.

Master Chief is also Tactician (3), giving him 3 extra command dice per turn.  This is magnified by his One Man Army special order, which means that if you use a special order command die, you can spend any number of available advance, fight, or shoot command dice on the Chief.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to Glaze, the fast-paced, flavor-filled board game where you're in charge of building the most dazzling donut factory in town!

In this deliciously strategic game, players simultaneously assemble colorful Tetris-style conveyor belts to mix, match, and deliver the perfect glazed donuts. But it's not just about stacking tiles; each color on your conveyor represents a glaze flavor, and your customers are very particular.

Balance efficiency and creativity as you place tiles to form efficient donut routes, assign workers to operate machinery, and fulfill complex orders. Master your factory’s layout, and you'll unlock special ability cards that give you more points and sweet bonuses.

The first to impress the most customers and collect the most points wins — but watch out, the competition is fierce, the glazes are messy, and the conveyor belts are ever-changing!

Glaze is a delightful blend of tile placement, worker placement, and donut-driven delight — a treat for gamers of all ages and sugar levels!


     Tetris-style tile placement
     Color-matching puzzle mechanics
     Worker placement
     Deliciously easy and advanced modes
     For 2–4 players, ages 10+, 60-75 minutes


Will your factory rise to frosted fame? Or will your conveyor belt crumble under the pressure? Time to glaze the way!

—description from the publisher

Stand ready to defend our worlds at all costs with Fireteam Cerberus. Expand your Halo: Flashpoint experience with this pack of additional Spartans equipped with Mark V [B] armour and new starting loadouts!

Fireteam Cerberus is comprised of four new Spartans:

CQB - A close-quarters battler armed with the fearsome Grav Hammer

Gungnir - A tough, ranged specialist armed with an M319 Grenade Launcher, which has multiple ammo options

HAZOP - A mobile soldier with flight ability armed with a Spike Rifle, which can be used both at range and in close combat

JFO - A support specialist armed with an H615 Target Locator to rain orbital missile strikes onto the battlefield

boardgame: The Adventurers: The Tomb of the Pirate Queen
description:
Descend into the sunken tomb of the infamous Pirate Queen Shi Yang. Floodwaters rise with every turn, blocking paths and sealing away treasure. Avoid deadly booby traps set by her loyal crew, uncover secret passageways hidden in the flooded stone, and desecrate the altars of the Dragon Gods to seize their guarded riches. Outmaneuver your rivals and escape before the tomb becomes your grave.

—description from the publisher

In the generations since the war with the Dark Sorcerer the forest of Whisperwood has stood silent, mired in corruption and lifeless. A council of Druids has taken on the task of cleansing the forest, awakening its guardian golems and restoring the flora and fauna. The druid who accomplishes the most in this endeavor will ascend to become the Primus of the Council.

Whisperwood is a bag-building game for 2-4 players of druids harnessing the power of the elements and the natural world to restore a forest. Each round players simultaneously summon magical energy in the form of tokens and plan the incantations they will perform. The players then perform their incantations in turn order, restoring air, water and earth, partnering with wildlife, inscribing runes and awakening the Guardian Golems to perform magical tasks. The land is strewn with corruption and the druids must absorb and purify this corruption to heal the forest - but take on too much corruption and your magical abilities will be compromised!

—description from the publisher

Voimamies (Strong Man) consist of card deck with three kind of cards Strong men (5), Polices (7) and Gangsters (15). Cards are shuffled and hand of three cards is delt to all players.

On your turn you play maximum of three cards of one type to the table and then you refill your hand to three cards from the deck. Then it's the next player's turn to try to win all the played cards. One police card beats one Gangster card. One Strongman beats one police card or all the Gangster cards played.  If all the cards are won, then the player fills their hand to three cards and plays new cards for the next player to beat. If player can't beat all the cards, they will pick up the cards they couldn't beat. Then next player will play cards for the following player to beat. When the deck runs out, the game continues until all, but one player has cards in their hand. Winner is the player with most Strong man cards in their hand. If nobody has any Strong Man cards in their hand, then the winners is the player who get rid of all their cards first.

Darkness Falls is the first expansion for The Cemetery.

It introduces fresh mechanics, thrilling challenges, and new cards that add deeper layers of strategy and unpredictability to every game.

Here’s what it brings to the table:

Unique, Challenging Events that disrupt the game flow, forcing players to adapt and rethink their strategies on the fly.

Sinister Objectives; new goal cards that demand clever planning and bold moves to outsmart your opponents and claim victory.

—description from the publisher

The Lazarus Event changed everything.

Three days of horror. The dead rose—not mindless, but vengeful. Cities fell. Millions perished. And when the dust settled, the world belonged to the dead.

Out There Waiting is a tabletop role-playing game that explores themes of survival, sacrifice, and hope.

You are a Runner—a survivor tasked with scavenging for Supplies, recruiting Survivors, and completing dangerous Missions to support the Colony.

The game is played over a series of turns, ending when the Colony either reaches 10 Prosperity Points through your life-risking efforts or falls due to the destruction of barricades or a loss of hope.

—description from the designer

Fire & Move is a platoon level game played on a tabletop with figures and model terrain. Each player commands a force of between 20 and 50 figures. These figures are formed into groups. The players then take turns performing action with these groups, such as moving, firing, rallying etc.

Each player will have a deck of 40 action cards unique to the nationality of their force and one time check card. The playing of a card onto a group will allow it to perform the action on the card. Besides the playing of cards to perform actions, these rules approach movement in a unique way. The distance a group moves is not defined in inches or centimetres, so a ruler is not required to determine how far a group can move. Moreover, how a group performs a move action will determine how vulnerable to fire they will be while moving.

To play this game players will need figures, two (different coloured) eight-sided dice (D8), a D6, a deck of Fire & Move cards for the nationality they command, and a ruler or tape measure to measure fire ranges. Troop cards, showing each figure’s statistics, are essential for playing the game.

—description from the rulebook
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
LZ is a platoon level game played on a tabletop with figures and model terrain. Each player commands a force of between 20 and 50 figures. These figures are formed into groups. The players then take turns performing action with these groups, such as moving, firing, rallying etc. Each player will have a deck of 40 action cards unique to the nationality of their force and one time check card. The playing of a card onto a group will allow it to perform the action on the card.

Besides the playing of cards to perform actions, these rules approach movement in a unique way. The distance a group moves is not defined in inches or centimetres, so a ruler is not required to determine how far a group can move. Moreover, how a group performs a move action will determine how vulnerable to fire they will be while moving.

To play this game players will need figures, two (different coloured) eight-sided dice (D8), a D6, a deck of LZ cards for the nationality they command, and a ruler or tape measure to measure fire ranges. Troop cards, showing each figure’s statistics, are essential for playing the game.

—description from the designer

The travel edition of Etelä-Pohjalaanen Alias in which instead of regular Finnish words you now have 400 words and phrases from South Ostrobothnia.
Try to explain these words and phrases with regular words. Can you do it? Do you even know these words?
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
An exciting cooperative WWII game where players try to successfully escape their POW from Stalag Luft III. Designed by Philip duBarry. In the Great Escape, you and your fellow air force officers will follow in the footsteps of the prisoners of Stalag Luft III, as you attempt to dig tunnels under the wire and escape. This will require exceptional timing, resourcefulness, and some luck. Outsmart the guards and carry on the war in the only way you can. If you help twelve prisoner pawns escape before the Gestapo locks down the camp, you and your comrades will have won the game. Each turn you have action points and with these action points you dig tunnels, move your pawns or collect valuable resources.

—description from the publisher

Operation Valkyrie is a solitaire game that simulates the events immediately after the attempt to kill Hitler on the 20th of July 1944, when the military coup was attempted to take control of the Reich.
The player will control the effort of the conspirators against the Reich forces controlled by the game system.
Each turn the player draws cards that provide him with action point or events to use for his cause or that is used against him by the bot. The player needs to control critical locations on the map in order to gain control over Berlin and overthrow the regime. The player is under enormous time pressure, as the bot becomes stronger with each round of the game.

—description from the publisher

TimeWarp is a fast-paced trivia board game where players race through time by sequentially ordering historical and pop-culture events. Each turn, you roll dice to determine a category and movement, then draw a Time‑in‑Line card and arrange the events in the correct chronological sequence. Correct answers let you advance along your Time Tube, but landing on TimeWarp and Time Stop spaces introduce twists and mini-challenges that can shake up the race.

The first player to navigate their Time Tube to the finish line wins!

TimeWarp uniquely blends general trivia with temporal strategy—you’re not just recalling facts, you’re racing to place them in time. With five diverse categories and surprise gameplay through special spaces, each session offers fresh twists and lively competition. Its vibrant, retro-inspired design makes it visually striking while easy to learn and fun to replay.

—description from the publisher

Push your weapons to the max in this 17 card Death Derby expansion that introduces weapon modifications to make your derbies more deadly.

Includes:

1 rule card

17 weapon modifications

An elderly priest walks forward.
His cassock hangs heavy with the weight of years, his stole sways gently, the Holy Book clutched tightly in his hands.
Behind him, crumbling walls and a gothic wallpaper faded by time—and fear.
He does not speak.
He cannot.
Evil listens.

The Exorcism is a cooperative card game for 1–4 players, inspired by the atmosphere of cinematic exorcisms. A single session lasts only 15–20 minutes, but the tension it leaves behind lingers far longer.

In every game, the Demon changes form.
Its powers are never the same—sometimes deceptive, sometimes wrathful.
Each session unfolds a new kind of psychological hell.
Will you adapt... or will you break?

Players take on the role of priests resisting the influence of Evil.
No talking is allowed—only glances, instinct, and silent faith.
Each mistake brings the Demon closer to victory.
But every sacrifice… opens the door to hope.

—description from the designer

FIBO is an engaging card game for 2 to 5 players, suitable for ages 10 and up, that combines fun and education. Players create clever number sequences by adding numbers up to 13, enhancing their arithmetic skills while enjoying the game. Its compact size makes FIBO perfect for travel, fitting easily into any bag for family vacations or as a souvenir game. Designed to stimulate the mind, FIBO serves as a memory training tool, making it an excellent gift for seniors. Experience the joy of bonding with family and friends while challenging your thinking skills with FIBO!

—description from the publisher

Elements is a thrilling social card game for children and adults, perfect for game nights! Become the master of elements as you engage in this exciting family game that challenges your logic and concentration. 
Make and break alliances in this fast paced hand shedding game!
Attack and defend to avoid taking any more cards!
Your allies will help you take down harder foes!
First one to end with an empty hand win!

CXIN is everything. You are nothing.
The contract is already signed — and failure may result in the termination of your job… or worse.

CXIN is a high-stakes memory and betting game for 2–6 players, as a chaotic free-for-all, in teams or in an intimate duel. Draft your crew from a rogue’s gallery of uniquely skilled hackers, each with their own playstyle and ability. Take turns scanning, manipulating, and downloading hidden data packs scattered across the table. Then, place daring bets — and try to retrieve exactly what you claimed, relying only on your memory and nerve. Succeed, and you rise. Fail, and you’re wiped.

This is not just a game — it’s an experience.
Inside the box: high-grade components, collectible artwork, immersive lore, a short narrative comic and a custom-curated soundtrack.

With its minimalist rules and deep strategic layers, CXIN is a rare fusion of memory mechanics, bluffing, and gambling psychology — dressed in a dystopian cyberpunk skin.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The World of Strawberry Shortcake: Berry Basket Burst
description:
With a berry basket bursting with delicious treats, players will strategize to see who can collect the most berries. This fruity game is berry fun for family game night. Sweet competition ahead!

—description from the publisher

If you enjoy music quizzes and want a social, engaging game night, QR Music Cards offers a thrilling mix of nostalgia, competition and variety. With numerous editions available, there's one for every music interest - from 90’s & 00’s to Hardstyle, Song Contest and beyond.

What is it? 
A card-based music quiz powered by QR codes linked to Spotify Premium. Each edition (genre, decade or special themes) contains 150 unique cards, each showing a QR code on one side and details like the song title, artist, and year on the other. The free app “QR Music Cards” can be used to scan the QR codes. It plays the song through Spotify Premium immediately.

Game modes

     Easy (Higher or Lower): Guess if the song’s release year is before or after a shown card.
     Medium (Two of Three): Name artist/title and the decade to earn a point.
     Expert (Music Battle): First to correctly name the artist, title and decade after the song plays wins the round.


Rounds continue until a player or team reaches 10 points.

About this edition: Mix
Artists: In this edition you will find artists such as Cher, Doe Maar and Jason Mraz.
Dates: This edition contains issues from the period 1961 to 2023.
150 music cards: This edition contains 150 unique music cards, good for several evenings of fun.
Some Dutch songs: This edition contains approximately 10 Dutch songs. The other songs are internationally well-known hits.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: QR Music Cards: Nineties and Zeroes
description:
If you enjoy music quizzes and want a social, engaging game night, QR Music Cards offers a thrilling mix of nostalgia, competition and variety. With numerous editions available, there's one for every music interest - from 90’s & 00’s to Hardstyle, Song Contest and beyond.

What is it? 
A card-based music quiz powered by QR codes linked to Spotify Premium.
