Each edition (genre, decade or special themes) contains 150 unique cards, each showing a QR code on one side and details like the song title, artist, and year on the other. The free app “QR Music Cards” can be used to scan the QR codes. It plays the song through Spotify Premium immediately.

Game modes

     Easy (Higher or Lower): Guess if the song’s release year is before or after a shown card.
     Medium (Two of Three): Name artist/title and the decade to earn a point.
     Expert (Music Battle): First to correctly name the artist, title and decade after the song plays wins the round.


Rounds continue until a player or team reaches 10 points.

About this edition: Nineties and Zeroes

     Artists: In this edition you will find artists such as Prince, Will Smith, Coldplay, Cascada and Def Rhymz.
     Dates: This edition contains songs from the period 1990 to 2009.
     150 music cards: This edition contains 150 unique music cards, good for several evenings of fun.
     Some Dutch songs: This edition contains approximately 25 Dutch songs. The other songs are internationally well-known hits.


—description from the publihsher

boardgame: Τα Χελονιντζακια (The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles)
description:
Based on the famous 80s cartoon Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, this is a simple roll the dice and move board game. This board game was released only in Greece from ΑΔΕΛΚΟ (aka Koliopoulos) toy company in the early 90's.

It can be played from 2-4 players. The winner is the first player who reaches endpoint by the exact number. There are some spaces-numbers on the board, which when you reach them you must follow the order written on the rules.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
Contains 3 double sided cards:

The three heroes include the powerful Witch, who can control other universal heroes and flips from Empowered to Dessicated when others would be defeated; Godfather Death/Old Rinkrank, who can manipulate the 20 Strong chip and discard items to avoid damage; and a Fairy who flips back and forth from Malevolent to Mischievous as she manipulates her stats. But why are these three "heroes" so black-hearted as opposed to their fellow Tanglewoods protagonists? Only time will tell...

Pack your bento box, grab your picnic blanket, and find the perfect spot under the treetops. The sakura blossoms are in full bloom and people from all over are gathered in the park for Hanami (or “flower viewing”) a centuries-old Japanese springtime tradition.

In Hanami, you’ll be picnicking in parks all across Japan and Tokyo city in the springtime, fighting the crowds to celebrate the floral spectacle. Players will place their tiles out to reserve spots near the various activities found there.

Each tile represents a picnic blanket set out by a member of your group to claim a spot in the park. Tiles indicate the strength of your group (the number of pips on the tile), and the favored activity of the group (viewing sakura, eating onigiri, or an evening under the lanterns). You’ll collect Activity Tokens once they’re surrounded by Player Tiles, if your tiles have a higher strength than any other player’s. Earn Victory Tokens at the end of the game for each activity by having the most of the corresponding Activity Token. Earn the most Victory Tokens to win Hanami!

Hanami is a new reimagining of the classic game Samurai, designed by Dr. Reiner Knizia. This updated game tells a new story: One of relaxing picnic lunches among the cherry blossoms and paper lanterns of Tokyo city parks or the lush countryside of Japan. It brings new strategies and two distinct ways to play: The Japan map allows players to enjoy the classic Samurai ruleset, and the Tokyo setup gives players new challenges, new abilities, and new tiles.

—description from the publisher

The big bad was in front of you the entire time, hiding in plain sight!

Baba Yaga, the evil horror of Slavic folklore, weaves new challenges that will persist throughout all three Tanglewoods decks, resulting in a fierce final battle when at long last the exit to the Tanglewoods is found.

Posing as lowly merchant, she may have seemed helpful, but the creatures she sends after you will pose difficult choices for you along the way. Each choice will ultimately make a consequential impact on your final battle.

The Baba Yaga campaign add-on allows you to play your three Tanglewoods decks in any order you choose! Experiment with different heroes, orders, and strategies to emerge from the Tanglewoods victorious!

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Baba Yaga

description:
The Atlantis Exodus: Polymathís expansion introduces jokers to the game in the form of polymaths who can take on the characteristics of workers, intellectuals, and scientists. The polymaths cannot completely replace these characters, but they bring new tactical possibilities.

The expansion includes player aid tableaus that assist you in tracking longer chain moves.

Grain Bingo is a two-player card game that highlights the push-your-luck mechanism. Players connect cards featuring various quantities of grain in horizontal, vertical, or diagonal lines. You win when you successfully line up the nine designated grains in a row.

The waters of the Bermuda Triangle are feared because storms sometimes make navigation impossible. What's more, legends tell of huge monsters lurking there for unsuspecting sailors...but the stories also tell of legendary treasures to be found there, so now you'll race in search of them.

The Pirates of Maracaibo: Bermuda Triangle expansion features a "Treasure Hunt" module in which you compete against each other in a race to find the biggest treasure. It also contains new improvement cards that you can mix into the base game for more variety, as well as a new card for the solo mode.

This 14 card set brings Dangerous Ground 2 to your Death Derby game and turns everyone's strategy on top of its head.

Includes:

1 rule card

14 Track Sections

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The Game begins on space 90 (kilometers to the finish) and the first player to reach space 1 wins the Game.

Take on your opponent in this tiny but brilliant take on a 5 on 5 soccer match.  Push your luck, outmaneuver your opponent, and score!  GOOOOOOOOAL!
 Kickabout is a game by Evan Wilcock with art by Leo Aquino.  Each turn both players play one card from their hand of 6 cards: Players 1-5 or the Shoot / Save Card.

If the Defender matches the Attacker's card, the ball is intercepted and possession changes. Otherwise, a pass is made and play continues.
 When the Attacker plays their shoot card, they will roll a number of dice equal to the passes made. All dice that match the last played card number or lower count as shots on goal.  The Defender will roll 3 dice if they matched the Shoot Card with the Save, 2 if they didn't, and only 1 if they played it early.

Defender blocks all matching numbers and may assign a 6 to block any 1 die.  If any shots on goal remain, the Attacker scores!  First to 3 wins.

This is a very simple game to learn and play with gamers and non-gamers; young and old.

—description from the publisher

Step up to your keyboard and race to be the first to hack the mainframe in this analog game about racing in a digital universe.

In Hard Drive, players roll a pool of dice, then use them to navigate an evolving race course. Collect charge to set up drift combos or change your die results, collect plug-ins to gain abilities that help you or hinder your opponent, cross overclock spaces to increase your speed, and use course mods to hack the system and pull off crazy maneuvers like rotating course cards.

As you advance, the track recreates itself in front of you. The first player to cross the finish line wins!

—description from the publisher

Command your mint-tin sized army on the battlefield and lead them to victory! 
 That's an Order is a mini programming game of tactics, timing, and bluffing for 1 to 2 players.  It's designed by Jonathan Carnehl and Ryan Sromek with art by Jonathan Carnehl.

Set up the battlefield using a variety of terrain.  Then, each player chooses their first three commands and sets them face-down in order.  They are revealed and executed in alternating turns.

Commands allow you to do things like deploy, maneuver, and attack.  Or you can play your That's and Order! card to gain your TAO token for a future turn.  The TAO token allows you to execute a more powerful version of one of the commands.

Have one of your soldiers standing alone on the enemy's base at the end of your turn to claim victory!  This game has many modes and lots of modularity for a very sandboxy war game experience that plays quickly.

—description from the publisher

In the future, mankind's last hope for survival lies in miniaturization.  Leadership of the final colony is decided every 4 years by the outcome of mech battles.
 Mint Mechs is a 1-2 player dice drafting and dice placement mech battler with a unique sequencing mechanic.  Designed by Galen McCown with art by Hwa Siang.

Maneuver your Mech around the miniaturized battlefield to pick up dice resources to power-up your weapons, fuel, armor, and control systems.  The dice types will provide immediate benefits like Fuel, Armor, Coolant, or dice mitigation.  Where you place the dice will activate various systems for attack, defense, fueling up, or upgrading.  Finally, the numbers of the dice come into play when activating a Mech Sequence for ultimate destruction.

When activating a Sequence, a player will activate systems with dice on them in ascending or descending order (3,4,5 or 5,4,3 for example), pulling off multiple attacks in a single turn.  Setting these up and optimizing the order is the key to coming out on top.  But watch your overheat!  Staying on the edge of overheat activates your Mech's unique ability.  But you take direct damage for overheat gained while in this state.

Mint Mechs is a heavier game experience in a Mint Tin, while still being easy to learn and playable in under a half hour.

—description from the publihser

Roll dice and leave your bummers behind!

Quick and easy to learn, compact and portable, Jerry Garcia's Cosmic Journey is the game to reach for when good vibes are needed at home or on the road.

Push your luck rolling dice as you take trips around a labyrinth of Jerry's art, picking up bummer tokens based on your score. When all bummers have been claimed, journey home and be the first to get rid of them to win.

Riff from the basic rules and add the variants to make each play experience unique. The game comes with solo rules and journey jams for up to eight players.

Go ahead — gather friends, strangers, kids, and grandmas to roll some dice, unplug, and play!

—description from the designer

In this trivia game you want to give the wrong answer. There are 200 question cards with three alternative answer, from which always one is not true. You are trying spot that wrong answer.

Player in turn reads the question and the possible answers a, b and c. Other players give their answers with letter cards. Everybody that gave the "wrong" answer gets the point. Then it's next players turn to ask. Winner is the first player to gather five points.

Regatta is roll and move game where you have to go around the island in the middle of the map board. Board consists of points in the map and you can move pretty freely as long as you don't go straight against the wind. And because this is a sailing game you have to go on the right side of the buoys in the route. And there are loads of other sailing relating rules, because this is an old game. Winner is the boat which arrives first back to the starting line. There are also easier rules for the kids.

Please remove this game from the database.  
I will only be continuing with Critter Clans.
Thank you.

boardgame: Merde! War Game set in the Napoleonic era
description:
Merde! is a P&P sandbox that lets you relive the battles of the Napoleonic era.

The three-dimensional tokens are designed to represent various types of formations and contain all the information regarding combat, movement, and discipline characteristics.

The innovative Headquarters board allows players to plan the organization of their armies, the activation order of the various formations, the orders to follow, and victory points. Thanks to this mechanism, players can, if they wish, move their corps simultaneously.

Armies are divided into formations under the orders of their respective commanders, who are tasked with conquering the sector of the battlefield assigned to them, distributing forces between attack, support, and defense lines.

Movements and changes of source are made via the octagonal points that characterize the maps, which are divided into attack and defense areas.

The Action cards determine weather conditions, initial movements, and unit resistance.

Armed conflicts are resolved in real challenges between players using Battle cards, while artillery salvos are regulated by Objective and Shot cards.

It is possible to play in solo mode using BOT Action cards.

The winner is the player who manages to break the enemy front by routing the enemy units in the various sectors.

Maps and orders of battle can be downloaded from the game's webpage.

For one or two players aged 12 and up.

—description from the designer

Useless Cowards is a ruleset for tabletop miniature wargaming (i.e. you will need miniatures to play).

In the game players take on the roles of condottieri/mercenary captains, leading their armies on the Italian battlefield. This ruleset contains rules and army lists for Italian peninsula warfare in three periods starting from the arrival of foreign companies in the mid 14th century and ending to the beginning of 16th century when gunpowder weapons started to dominate battlefields.

These rules are designed to appeal to all types of wargamers, from dedicated tournament players to those who enjoy historical re-fights, as well as everyone in between.

The rules are designed for 28 mm miniatures, but other scales can also be used. The game is based units, which are formed of certain number of soldiers. The command basing is 20 mm x 20 mm for foot soldiers and 25 mm x 50 mm for mounted soldiers. 
The rules contains a point system for army building. 
An average size game contains 5-7 units per side in 180 cm x 120 cm table.

The Useless Cowards rulebook contains army lists from three eras, which players can use to build their armies.

     Age of Companies / mid 14th century 
     Rise of Condottieri / late 14th century – mid 15th century
     Dynasties of Condottieri / late 15th century


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Fistful of Lead: Galactic Heroes – Vehicle Instruction Manual
description:
What's a Sci-Fi game without Out-of-this-World vehicles? This handy manual gives you extra rules for your Fistful of Lead: Galactic Heroes games, adding skimmers, hover bikes, and other mechanical mayhem to your games, including:


     Mechs and other big stompy robots
     Ridden creature mounts
     Hover barges and skimmers
     Tanks
     Plus more!

Roll and move game where players race around the world. First player to get back to starting circle in the middle of the Pacific Ocean is the winner.

On your turn you roll the die, and if you land on the yellow circle nothing happens. If you land on a red circle, you pick the card and read what happens. You might get a extra roll, go forward some spaces, go backward some spaces, loose next turn and so on. After all your moving ends, it's next players turn.

Liisa ja Kaarina is an old two player game where two ladies of the house buy groceries, makes dinner and takes care of other household tasks.

There are five shops in the board, with their own storage rooms. Shops are Dairy, Meat market, Vegetable market, Sweets shop and General store. To set up the game you will arrange different types of food item tiles in random order in the storages of particular shops. Both ladies get 250 marks as their starting capital.

At start of every round both ladies take ten items in pairs of two from the storage and put them in the particular shops, so they can be purchased. When this is done all items in the shops are turned visible.

Then the shopping starts. On a turn one lady will by two tiles and puts them in their places on her side of the board. Buying continues as long as at least one lady decides to buy more. Ladies can only buy things they can use to the meals they are making.

When ladies don't want to buy more of the food available, new round begins by emptying the shops and then taking food items from the storages to shops again.

When all the meals are made, household pet is fed and fire wood have been bought the game ends. Lady of the house with more money left is the winner.

boardgame: Fiero!: A Creative Storytelling Card Game
description:
Fiero! is a game about creating crazy ideas, legendary characters, and memorable stories.

With diverse stories to set the scene and evocative visuals to ignite your imagination, you will traverse through fictional scenarios and fantastical settings, and emerge with your own story for the ages.

It’s time to create your own story!

Fiero! boils the creative storytelling process down to three simple, compelling components: a good Story Brief to set the stage, evocative visual prompts to aid your brainstorming, and a simple voting mechanism to help steer your stories in a common direction - so you can create your own story within just three rounds of gameplay and 30 minutes!

—description from the publisher

Aikamatka: Lautapelit is a trivia game about the history of board games. Each of the 252 cards depicts a board game on its front side, and has the publishing year of the game on the back side.

At the start of the round as many cards as there are players are delt to starting player. Starting player chooses one card and gives rest to the next player in clockwise order. When everybody has a card, the last player will place their card first in the timeline of cards and then other players will do the same in counter clockwise order. If you place your card in the right place it stays on the line, otherwise you will collect it as a minus point. After the round ends the player in the right side of previous starting player will start the new round.

Game last as many rounds as there are players. Winner is the player with least minus points.

The second horseman of the apocalypse has arrived. First it was Death (El Mortal, published by Átomo and GDM Games) and now the troops of all known armies of mankind will clash on the battlefield in an epic struggle unparalleled in history.

Choose your armies and play your cards wisely to set traps, equip your hosts and weaken your opponents.

With your hand of cards you can bring an army down to your small board, equip it, weaken your opponents' armies and set traps face down (which may or may not be traps). Then choose an opponent and fight him to gain morale points.

Once the warrior arrives, the player with the highest morale will replace him as the horseman of the apocalypse!

El Guerrero is the thematic sequel to El Mortal, and promises to be one of the most hostile card games of the year.

Our School Days immerses you in the vibrant chaos of a Chinese high school. Balance your time wisely—cram for exams, run for class monitor, forge friendships, hit the library, do classroom chores, sneak off to the snack stall, or gamble with rule-breaking! Each decision weaves your unique path toward the ultimate test: the college entrance exams.

Designed for 1-4 players, this roll-and-write game challenges you to draft dice as "mental energy," then mark your exam-paper player sheet to complete objectives or score immediate row/column points. Each color unlocks distinct actions (elections, studying, socializing, reading, duties), with skilled play triggering combo moves.

Three academic semesters determine your fate. Victory goes to the player who best juggles responsibilities and seizes opportunities, topping the final scoreboard.
—description from the designer

HePing Escort Agency stands atop the Four Seas Continent as the industry's foremost escort service, its ranks filled with skilled escorts. As the aging Chief prepares to retire, you must compete to recruit the best escorts, optimize caravan routes, manage finances and supplies, while navigating taxes and alliances - will your leadership prevail in this ultimate test of strategy?

Escort agencies were the elite delivery forces of Ming and Qing dynasty China - the original armored trucks operating on bandit-infested trails, where every Heping ("Peace") Escort needed lethal skills to guarantee safe passage, turning their agency's name into an ironclad promise of security in dangerous times.

Heping Escort Agency is a 1-4 player board game of resource management and movement planning. During their turn, players must perform these 3 actions in order: First, Tribute (optional) - deliver porcelain to officials to activate their card effects; Second, Recruitment (mandatory) - select and acquire 1 Escort card (higher-level Escorts require silver payment); Third, Escort Mission (mandatory) - move your Escort team the designated steps to trigger both location effects and card effects.

Players earn points by delivering goods, completing objectives, and establishing connections with Chief Escorts, with the highest-scoring player winning the game.

—description from the designer

This pack is for the hardcore players who are not afraid of a challenge! Introducing new mind-melting obstacles that are centred around your character losing their sanity. Don't forget to keep yours!

Includes 9 new cards, introducing new difficulty, gameplay, enemies and obstacles.

—description from the designer

In 825, a band of Vikings set off on a journey that took them much longer than expected.

In this epic worker placement game, you will explore and trade with new territories. You will also engage in the day-to-day activity of breeding animals, collecting goods and hunting with which to achieve a steady income and victory points. In the end, the player with the most victory points will be declared the winner.

Starting with a small group of Vikings, your band and possibilities will grow with each round. But the demand of your community for food and taxes will grow alike.

—description from the publisher

Martian Math is an out‐of‐this‐world mathematical adventure for kids (5+) and parents alike! 2–4 players race to claim Martian landscapes by rolling dice, crafting equations, and building outpost colonies.

Game Story
Welcome to the Red Planet! You’re bold Martian explorers, surveying mysterious numbered terrains - crystal craters, ancient artifacts and dusty dunes. Solve math puzzles to stake your claim, build a colony, and be the first to light up the Martian map!

Wooden and Magnetic Lasercut Components 
• 21 magnetic hexagonal Martian terrain tiles
• Magnetic underlays
• 40 or 80 outpost markers (coloured sets of 20)
• 2 dice.

Objective
Be the first player to build the best colony on Mars.

Setup
1. Shuffle the terrain sections and align to form a map of Mars. Keep your map secure by connecting the sections with magnetic receptors underneath.
2. Distribute sets of markers of the same colour.
3. Decide who goes first (youngest “astronaut” wins!).

Game Play
On your turn, do these steps in order:
1. Roll the Dice
2. Call Out an Equation
Using addition (+) and subtraction (–) and for older kids, multiplication (×) and division (÷), form an equation with the numbers on the dice to make a result between 1 and 36.
o Example with dice roll 6 & 3:
• 6 + 3 = 9
• 6 – 3 = 3
• 6 × 3 = 18
• 6 ÷ 3 = 2

3. Claim Your Outpost
Find any unclaimed hex matching your result. Place any outpost marker there.
4. End Your Turn
Pass dice to the next player.
If no valid hex matches your result, you “drift in space” and lose your turn. Play until all markers are placed.

Advanced Game Play
Play as above but claim territory by matching the number on the claim marker with the number on the terrain tile. This is a more advanced way to play as there will be less contiguous territory and requires more strategic placement of outposts.

Endgame & Winning
• Instant Win: Be the first to build seven outposts that are not isolated (that is, adjacent to each other).
• Full Colonies: With all outposts built, the player with the largest colony (most adjacent outposts) wins.
Tie-Breaker: In case of a tie, the winner of a round will have the highest total of the claim numbers in their largest colony.

Building an Outpost:
To claim territory (hex) and build your outpost, place your claim marker on a number matching the outcome of your equation. Always try to claim territory adjacent to your existing outposts to make the largest colony.

Doubles Roll - Split a Colony:
A doubles roll allows you to thwart an opponent by seizing a matching outpost, thereby splitting their colony.

Winning a Round and the Game:
When all claims have been marked, the player with the largest colony (highest number of adjacent outposts) will win the round. Play three rounds to determine the winner of the game.

—description from the publisher

In Mine Mine Mine, players want to complete a set of nine matching cards as quickly as possible, with everyone trading cards simultaneously using only the word "MINE". Want to trade a card? Yell "MINE"! Want to swap two of the same cards? Shout "MINE MINE!" If another player is shouting the same number at the same time, trade! The first player to collect a complete set grabs the trophy and shouts "WINNER WINNER WINNER"!

Go wild and unleash your inner party animal with Woof Woof Woof. Make fun animal sounds while matching your cards by color or animal. Use your animal instincts to stay on top of this game's wild rules because if you don't keep up, you'll have to pay the penalty.

The first player to get rid of all their cards is the top dog!

Tortugas involves joining the parts of several turtles to score points, which accumulate.

Each piece can be: legs, shell, or head, and only fits its corresponding turtle.

Played in turns, with three turtle cards in hand, each player must try one of them. If it fits one, they continue placing more; if it doesn't fit the turtles on the table, they discard and draw another.

The player who completes a complete turtle earns the points they have on it. At the end of the game, of course, the player with the most points wins. But be careful, there are cards that don't fit any turtle...

The difficulty of the game is that you can't try your card to see if it fits. The card you decide to play must be selected based on your visual acuity, mental proportion calculation, unmeasured distances, and visuospatial ability.

A very fun game for the whole family, including the little ones (since it was designed by one of them).

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

boardgame: Fistful of Lead: Galactic Heroes – Second Edition
description:
Rocket engines blaze to life. Lasers light up the formless void. Galactic Empires clash. A lone hero rises up to fight for the resistance.

Welcome to the new and improved world of Fistful of Lead: Galactic Heroes!

In Galactic Heroes you’ll take on the role of a Galactic Hero or Villain, leading a scruffy pirate crew looking for adventure, or maybe a troop of faceless soldiers of the Empire in search of bringing order to the galaxy. It’s really up to you!

What’s different about the 2nd edition from the original Galactic Heroes book? A few errors have been fixed and some rules have been clarified, but mostly new sections, traits, and equipment that were originally released as separate books have been added.

Now Included:

>The Vehicle Manual - Rolling, stomping, skimming vehicles - even mounts!

>Codex Galactica - All the prestatted up peoples you could want!

Like all the Fistful of Lead games, you’ll only need 5 or 6 miniatures per player. You can play 1-on-1 games or larger skirmishes with up to 6 or 8 players. This book contains all the information you need to play a game, including rules for building your forces and how to set up the table for different scenarios. Most players will pick up the game mechanics quickly, even people who haven’t played a wargame before.  If you’ve played Fistful of Lead before, a lot of this will be familiar.
 Galactic Heroes allows you to play a small-scale wargame in the far-flung future, a galaxy, far, far away, a grim, dark future 40,000 years from now, or a certain Red Planet where swords and rayguns reign supreme. These rules are whatever YOU think Sci-Fi looks like, from your favorite Space Opera to gritty Pulp epic.

So check the seals on your vac-suit. Have your blaster at the ready. It’s time to see if you’re a Galactic Hero!

Drop, block, defeat! In Tetris: The Board Game, which is based on the iconic puzzle game, you want to complete the lines by rotating and dropping tetrominoes into your grid — but there's a twist: You can gain the power to drop "garbage" pieces into an opponent's grid, blocking their path to victory. Whoever completes the most lines comes out on top!

In Echoes of Astra TCG, you play as a Sovereign uniting two factions on the war torn world of Elyrion. Magic and technology mix in a never-ending cycle of conflict. Command your troops to fight for resources, exert your influence, and achieve victory.

You earn points by controlling any of 3 combat zones in the middle of the battlefield. Your units will fight to control that territory by any means possible - Tactics, Counters, Equipment, and Territory modifiers will all contribute to your strategy.

Victory goes to the first player to achieve 20 victory points. Make smart decisions, expend your units efficiently, and predict your opponents moves to become the dominant Sovereign.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Philosophy of Death: Gatherism
description:
Philosophies of Death - Gatherism introduces a new worldview that can be integrated into any arc survivor campaign, offering a cascade of botanical reverberations that will drastically alter the ecology of your game.

Gatherism is a Tier 2 Philosophy. Settlements must reach new levels of sophistication and unlock them using collective cognition.

Life blooms in the harshest corners of the darkness. Pale shoots and lurid blossoms persevere. They inspire arc survivors to focus on the plants that inhabit their world.

—description from the publisher

Dungeons of Kards – Dungeon & Ice is an adventure game inspired by classic RPG storytelling, now re-released in a revised edition following a successful crowdfunding campaign in 2021.

This new edition features two distinct scenarios, presented in individual decks, which can be played separately or combined into a single game session. It also includes 10 custom-shaped wooden meeples in exclusive colors, representing the game’s characters.

The rules have been designed to welcome both beginners and experienced players, offering an accessible and engaging experience. The game can be played solo, cooperatively, or competitively, supporting up to 8 participants.

—description from the publisher

Keep the Faith is a storytelling game about a religion in transition. Elements of role-playing and board gaming combine to make an experience that is designed to welcome new players and create a story together.
 During the game, you play cards and narrate short stories to describe how the practices and trappings of the Faith express different (and at times contradictory) beliefs: What does your religious music sound like if it's demonstrating your commitment to welcoming the outsider, as opposed to your desire to wield power? What does your altar look like if it's an altar for keeping secrets as opposed to spreading the word?

External pressures and tensions within the Faith will cause your beliefs to shift — and might even drive the Faith to fracture and fall into irrelevance. When the era deck empties, you compare the number of aspect cards on orthodox values versus schismatic values to determine the Faith's outcome.

Keep the Faith is an approachable storytelling game about an often-fraught topic, designed to create compelling narratives and spark discussions between players.

A thousand years ago, the spirit of Voodoom was sealed inside a coconut. Now, the Cursed Coconut is about to explode… and Voodoom is looking for a new host. Will you get rid of it in time?


     Play cards from your hand or the deck and pass the Cursed Coconut
     Don’t let it fall on you
     Who will it explode on when time runs out?


Use the game’s app as a timer, featuring different game modes and difficulty levels.

—description from the publisher

Hace mil años, el espíritu de Voodoom fue sellado en un coco. Ahora, el Coco Maldito está a punto de explotar y Voodoom busca un nuevo huesped. ¿Serás capaz de deshacerte de él a tiempo?


     Juega cartas de tu mano o del mazo y pasa el Coco Maldito
     Evita que te caiga encima
     ¿A quién le explotará cuando se acabe el tiempo?


Usa la APP del juego como temporizador, con distintos modos de juego y niveles de dificultad.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Gog & Magog – The Last Bastion is a richly themed, card-driven battle game set in a mythic world of guilds and elemental forces. Players choose pre-constructed decks made of vibrant lore-rich cards and face off in thrilling one-on-one or team-based duels. The world is filled with powerful creatures, strategic spells, and mystical invocations, all underpinned by a dramatic backdrop of conquest and allegiances.

Gameplay Overview
Players begin with either a full deck of 150 cards (Destiny Mode) or a customized deck of 50 cards (Free Will Mode). Each deck is composed of:

- Creature Cards
Playable onto the battlefield to defend or attack.
Basic Creatures serve as the backbone of your forces.
Creatures with Abilities introduce unique effects such as passive auras, death effects, or one-time activations (e.g., dealing damage, drawing cards, or energy manipulation).

- Spell Cards
Represent magical events or powers.
Have immediate effects (like damage, healing, energy shifts, or disabling enemy units).
Cost energy or mana to play, depending on their potency and type.

- Invocation Cards
Special one-use cards that summon 2 to 5 creatures from a single guild directly to the battlefield without using mana.
After their use, they are placed in a separate zone and cannot be reused.

- Resource Cards
Crucial to economy and tempo.
Energy Cards: Used to pay for most cards.
Mana Cards: Rare and powerful, used for stronger creatures or high-level spells.
Efficient resource management is vital to survival and offense.

Turn Structure
Each round is timed (30–60 seconds) using a sand timer. On your turn, you:


     Draw a card
     Pay resource costs (energy or mana)
     Play cards: creatures, spells, invocations
     Position and attack: shift creatures to offense and initiate battle
     End turn: Pass to your opponent, who begins their round


Victory often comes from draining your opponent's forces, controlling the board, or disrupting their energy/mana engine.

Objective
The goal is to outwit and outlast your opponent by:

     Controlling the battlefield with summoned creatures
     Efficiently using spell cards and abilities
     Managing your energy/mana economy
     Reduce your opponent's energy to zero to dismantle their strategy and dominate the board.


Card Dimensions & Strategic Diversity
The Gog & Magog universe is built upon the coexistence of four distinct dimensions, all imagined and conceptualized to offer players a rich and diversified gameplay strategy:


     Earth Dimension: The realm of human champions, earthly heroes, and mystical creatures. These cards support balanced mid-range strategies, offering a mix of offense and defense ideal for versatile deck-building.
     Jahanam Dimension: The fiery realm of Hell, home to fallen angels and damned souls. Cards from this domain specialize in late-game, high-impact attacks and are perfect for players who thrive on overwhelming final blows.
     Oceanic Dimension: Dominated by aquatic and sea-bound entities, this realm offers cards focused on resilient offense and sturdy defense. A go-to for players who favor grinding their opponents down while staying tough.
     Janna Dimension: A celestial sphere of divine beings and radiant creatures. These cards cater to healing, endurance, and sustainability, allowing players to survive long matches and wear down aggressive opponents over time.


Final Summary
Gog & Magog is a deep and tactical card battle game combining the intensity of real-time decision-making with the depth of deckbuilding and resource management. Featuring creatures with unique powers, spells, invocations, dimensions, and dual-resource systems, it challenges players to think fast and strategize deeply across multiple game modes. Whether playing the physical hybrid version or competing online in play-to-earn battles, the path to victory lies in mastering your guild’s strengths and exploiting your opponent’s weaknesses.

—description from the publisher

In this epic civilization game, players guide their people through 1000 years of medieval history, evolving from humble chieftains with single settlements into rulers of mighty empires. Unlike traditional civilization games with preset factions, each player's nation evolves through branching paths - choose successor cultures each era based on your previous identity, replacing old traits with new powers. With over 160 nation cards creating vast evolution trees, every playthrough forges a unique historical journey as your people transform through the ages.

Players wield action cubes representing their leadership capacity, deploying them to expand territories, develop economies, advance technologies, or strengthen armies. Building cities and fortresses grants additional action cubes, while controlling provinces fills treasuries with taxes - creating layered strategic decisions. The economic system challenges players to transform raw resources into manufactured goods while navigating dynamic markets where prices shift with every trade. Military conflicts utilize an innovative battle bag system where commanders must balance risk and reward - committing more forces improves odds, but every draw means losses.

The modular hex-based map ensures each game unfolds differently as players explore uncharted lands and compete for valuable territories. Throughout the game, players choose forms of government, align with great faiths, and navigate extensive technology trees. Historical events voted on collectively - plagues, rebellions, golden ages - constantly reshape the world. Victory comes through multiple paths: military conquest, economic dominance, technological advancement, or religious influence. Every decision shapes not just immediate outcomes but the entire trajectory of a civilization's evolution over the course of history.

—description from the publisher

Gone to Gaia is a sprawling cooperative roguelite campaign where players must safeguard the last remnants of humanity. Dive into a richly immersive world filled with tactical combat, evolving storylines, and unforgettable characters. Inspired by video games like Hades and Vampire Survivors, the game features meaningful leveling choices that dramatically impact your power, utility, and flexibility in battle — skills you’ll need to survive the relentless creatures of the Tide.

At the heart of Gone to Gaia is Bird’s Nest, a mysterious floating town brimming with life. Every character you meet has their own story, problems, and perspective on the events unfolding around them. Who you help — or hurt — can alter the course of your campaign. You'll uncover a deeply personal story that rewards exploration, interaction, and even romance.  As you journey beneath the surface, you’ll face mutated enemies that travel in deadly formations, led by powerful and unpredictable leaders. No two creatures are ever the same. Fortunately, your Delver is packed with tools, abilities, and upgrades earned through each run — keeping the gameplay fresh and rewarding. The game is packed with hidden quests, unlockable characters, mysterious locations, and a dedicated Unlockables Box filled with sealed containers that can only be opened through specific in-game decisions.

Gone to Gaia is a campaign-style board game, which means that you’ll play through many sessions with the same 2 players, or experience the game in a dedicated solo mode. The campaign unfolds across 300 pages of material and hundreds of cards, and is divided into chapters. You can decide when to stop each session at nearly any point. Delvers will alternate between going on a quest and visiting the town. Quests are a combat-focused experience. Whether or not your delvers survive the quest, the story will pick back up with an interactive narrative in the town of Bird’ s Nest, where you’ll be tasked with investigating unfolding mysteries that threaten humanity . Gone to Gaia is a rogue-lite which means is that, during quests, you’ll gain many powers, but at the end of quests, you’ll also lose them. However, you’ll still have the opportunity to acquire a variety of permanent powers.

Draw a card, grab a partner, and showcase your wildest dance creations. Welcoming dancers and non-dancers alike — whether it's a family game night or a party with friends, get ready to take the stage and bring the house down!

—description from the publisher

Game Play: The active player draws one card from each of four piles. The first card specifies the persona the player(s) will be acting (for example: grandparent, construction worker, T-Rex). The second card is the title of the song they'll dance to (each card has a QR code that links to the song). The third card determines whether the performance will be a solo, a duo, or trio. The final card randomly selects the judge and other performers if necessary.

The player(s) then have two minutes to prepare for their dance routine. After the performance the judge assigns a score of 1-10, the nonparticipating players (audience) can give input, but the judge is the final arbitrator.

The player with the highest score after all players have performed twice wins the game.

boardgame: Sea Dragons: Treasures of the Deep
description:

Yet beware, for now the young dragons are not alone in their pursuit of pirates… Your feats have incited their larger, more dangerous elders to venture out to sea in search of treasures.

Sea Dragons: Treasures of the Deep is the first expansion of Sea Dragons and add a new Reward Board where you can advance your Tails depending the pirate boats you sink, where you can obtain new ways to score and unlock new power cards that you can use to improve your turns. As cloth merchants of the wealthy Calimala Guild, you will vie for power and control over the future of Florence.

Broker deals, deliver cloth to markets, sponsor statues, and contribute to the construction of great Churches.

Promote your trusted family members into the Council of Calimala, triggering scoring and the evaluation of prestige in the next area of the board. Plan ahead for upcoming scoring conditions and hire contractors to take advantage of open opportunities.

Overview:
This second edition of Calimala, by award winning designer Fabio Lopiano, has been beautifully remastered. With stunning art and graphic design from Ian O’Toole, extended gameplay, and deluxe components.

This new edition of Calimala comes with the Kickstarter exclusive Intrigue Expansion. Take control of one of Florence’s historical merchant families, such as the Medici Family, and benefit from unique player powers.

With 4 new expansion actions you can invest in banks to increase your control in cities, or commission artworks to boost your standing with the local churches.

Align yourself with major political powers, the Holy Roman Empire or Catholic Church. Benefit from their contradicting policies or try to play both sides and get the best of both worlds.

Win points for winning arguments. This social game by Brass Monkey includes 200 game cards, each featuring opposing views of hot button issues...like how to load the dishwasher like an actual human being. Just pick a side, and debate it out. There's no politics, religion, or any of that crap here. There are way more important things to fight about. For example: Those brownie edge pieces are garbage. Go ahead, prove us wrong.

In competitive mode, players take turns playing the roles of dealer, debaters, and judges. When the dealer draws a card, judges secretly write their opinion of the statements on both sides of the card. Then the dealer holds the card in the air and drops it. The color of the side facing up determines the topic and the first debater to choose which opinion they'll argue for, leaving their opponent to defend the other position. Debate follows an argument-counterargument-rebuttal pattern starting with the first debater. Next, the second debater gets to choose which opinion from the second (facedown) argument they wish to defend, leaving the remaining opinion for their opponent to defend. The second debater starts, following the same debate pattern as before. Finally, each judge announces who they thought won the debate, as well as what they originally wrote down for their own opinion on the matter, for each topic. If a judge's opinion isn't changed, each debater gets a point, but if a debater manages to sway a judge to their argued point of view, that debater gets both points from that judge.

In casual mode, just grab some friends, some beverages, and some cards, and discuss the topics as loudly and as passionately as you want.

Discover the wonders of Alaska through our exciting card game. Explore the stunning landscapes, encounter fascinating wildlife, and immerse in it’s rich history. Whether you're a seasoned traveler or dreaming of your next adventure, Alaska Odyssey Card Game will transport you to the Last Frontier.

Discover Alaska’s wonders with this exciting matching game! Use strategy and intuition to outfox opponents and collect the most sets to win!

Welcome to Alaska Odyssey, the ultimate card game experience that takes you on a strategy-filled journey through the stunning landscapes, fascinating cultures and rich wildlife of Alaska. The game is designed to provide endless hours of fun and entertainment for 2 to 10 players ages 6 to adult.

Roll and move game where players race around the cross country ski trail. First player to finish the trail is the winner.

On your turn you roll the die, and if you land on the yellow circle nothing happens. If you land on a red circle, you read what happens. You might get a extra roll, go forward some spaces, go backward some spaces, loose next turn, take longer but safer route and so on.  After all your moving ends, it's next players turn.

The day has come, and the best mole miners must prove their skills by extracting the most precious gems. Use your intuition and don’t get distracted chatting with the other moles!

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME
Score the most points by collecting the most valuable stones from different mines.

HOW TO PLAY
During each round, all players observe the stones available in each mine, and therefore the points they can earn from them.
Next, all players simultaneously choose which mine they will work at by using one of their available Mine Selection Cards. They place the card face down on their side of the table. Once everyone has chosen, they reveal their selection cards.
Then, two possibilities arise:

     If a player selects a mine that no one else chose, they are in luck—they’ve had a fruitful workday! They collect all the stones from that mine and place them on their side of the table.
     If two or more players choose the same mine, those distracted moles start chatting and don’t work, earning no stones.


—description from the publisher

Rollgeon Quest is a roll-and-write adventure. In this game, you lead a loyal party of four heroes - a clever mage, a brave warrior, a sharp-eyed hunter, and a sly rogue - into the shadowy depths of forgotten dungeons. Their mission? Gathering shining treasure, and ancient secrets.

Game is played simultaneously for 12 rounds. In each of those rounds, players will roll dice and modify a pattern, that they will later place in their dungeon. They will be able to gather some of the treasure each time their heroes venture into the depths.

boardgame: Heeron Ke Chor: The Great Detective Game
description:
The Nawab's jewels are bein auctioned and four notorious criminals are after them. Four famous detectives are called to secure them. Players will choose their characters among those detectives: Sherlock Holmes, James Bond, Mandrake and Phantom.

Heeron Ke Chor is roll and move game in 24*24 grid. Every player starts from their own corner and tries to get all of their 4 pieces to the central space, where the auction takes place.

On your turn you roll the die and move one of your pieces closer to the central square. But to get your pieces leave the starting square you need to roll a six. After that you can move any direction, but not diagonally. If you land on a special square your read what happens. Squares are named likewise, but there are own texts for all the characters. They are usually bad: loose turn, start from the beginning, go back and so on.

First player to get all of their pieces to the central square wins the game.
In the two player version of Dragon's Nest, the players place eggs in shared nests, hide eggs in their own secret nest and play dragon cards to earn victory points.

Achieve the title of the dark lord in Hegemon: The Struggle - become the avatar of evil! Enter a cursed world to take control of the power-giving Seals of one of three hellish Factions: Plague, Lament, or Inferno. Fight with other players to seize territory in demonic domains - plot, deceive, form and break alliances. Raise an army of corrupted beings: soldiers, lords, and even gods. Tame the Hell!

Hegemon: The Struggle is a dark board game for 2-6 players, set in a vision of hell divided into three unique factions: the Kingdom of Mold and Bones (Plague), the Dreams of the Accursed (Lament), and the Light of the Fallen (Inferno). The task of the players controlling these factions will be to obtain the ancient Seals found on the Scroll. Each Seal will provide cards representing a diverse group of creatures with their own dark histories, Death Points, Initiative, and unique Skills. These creatures will serve their masters loyally in many ways - some as currency, others as sacrifices on the path to ultimate victory.

Win duels with brute force, cunning, or complex synergies. Form and break alliances, deceive, bluff, and scheme. Only one player will be able to seize all the Seals and tame the hellish reality. Do you dare to fight for the title of the Hegemon?

Advanced must-follow, interweaving chain, tricktaking game.

In the Trick phase, play a card to the trick to determine the turn order. Save animals in the river with your herd of capybaras.

In the Turn phase, you have many actions you may do. Draft a card to your tableau, discard a card to make your tableau bigger (and get a token back), discard a card to affect the ranks of the suits, use a token to flip or move cards in your tableau, score your current tableau and put the cards back into your hand... After a couple turns it becomes easy to remember what you can do. 
First to a certain VP goal ends the game and then players put their tableau in their hands and score an extra 3VP per card in their biggest set.

Trickybara is playable with the Capybara Crush deck plus 3 special tokens per player.

—description from the publihser

At the start of the game, you roll 6 dice and freely write the rolled values on one of the cards—this will be the starting setup. Each player gets 4 cards, and in each round, using a dice selection mechanism, you write numbers onto your two active cards.

Whenever one of your cards is completed, you must immediately place it onto the shared area.

The goal is to connect to as many other cards and numbers as possible, since this is how you score points.

The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Crazy Computer is a fast-paced survival card game for friends and families that stay cool under pressure.

The AI running Camilla Covina’s toy factory where you and your friends work has gone from erratic to utterly mad.
Overcome by paranoia and an insatiable hunger for efficiency it has ordered all workers to report to the recycling stations for termination, replacing them with robots. A mad dash across the conveyor belts is your only option.

Lie, cheat, manipulate - do whatever it takes to beat your friends and family to the exit. Only one player escapes the factory, everyone else is recycled to become toy parts.

Each player starts with 7 cards. To win you must get rid of them before the other players.


     Explore the factory and fight the machines that stand in your way
     Gear up with flamethrowers, cans of whoop ass and ill-tempered cats to fight back
     Hack the factory systems to your advantage and to make life more difficult for the other players

In this game cheating - i.e. breaking the rules to create personal advantages in the game - is accepted and and even recommended! Just don't get caught.

Pets Against Humanity is the adult party game where your dog’s balls, your cat’s attitude, and your parrot’s foul mouth take center stage. This game blends dark humor, pet shenanigans, and NSFW fun into one wild deck of laughs. (Publishers description)

Players draw the same amount of black answer cards each (e.g. 10). Then choose a judge who draws white card with pet-related question or fill-in-the-blank prompt. Each player gives their answer to judge who chooses the funniest combo. Winner gets the question card as a point. Then the next player is the judge. Winner is the player who gets most or agreed amount of point cards to win (e.g. 5).

boardgame: Star Trek: Captain's Chair – Promo Pack 2
description: Five additional cards and one new ship token for Star Trek: Captain's Chair.

Fly with the wings of Icarus, Pegasus or even Siren to explore the shortest distance when visiting pre-selected Greek islands!

Simple rules and unexpected complexity in a fun and novel game which teaches intuitevely advanced mathematics, Greek geography, demographics, and history. It is played in three parts:

1. Competition between 2-8 players to find the least distance after visiting all or some of thirty islands (always returning to the base island) on the Map of the Greek Archipelagos.

There are also a 1-player challenges, where someone must find the least distance, which is based on the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP).

2. Pinpointing the position of any of the 240 islands shown on the Map of the Greek Archipelagos. Players who find them first, receive a bonus.

3. Using 120 cards showing the shape of Greek islands. Each card states population and area. Island-cards with less people and smaller area have the advantage.

Points are counted at the end of the three parts to reveal the winner.

Pawns depict ancient Greek flying characters, such as Icarus, Daedalus, Pegasus, Europe, Apollo, Hermes, Nike, Siren, etc.

Each player uses markers or tokens to mark their routes.

The game has 10 billion starting positions, ensuring different challenges forever!

In this solo playing-card game, you will build ships manned by one of three factions:  pirates (spades), sailors (clubs), and ghosts (hearts.)  Diamonds represent treasure.

As each crew member is added to a ship the the ship can take one of three actions: collecting treasure, transferring crew members, or attacking another ship to win its treasure.

Pirates defeat sailors, sailors defeat ghosts (by punching them, as Popeye was known to do,) and ghosts defeat pirates (pirates are superstitious.)

Kings and Queens are royals who confer extra actions on a ship, or allow ships to change factions. Jokers are letters of marque. The goal of the game is for one ship to get all the treasure.

—description from the publisher
Two teams of one or more persons (and preferably a third to act as timekeeper, scorekeeper and umpire) compete in a classic cricket game with rules that follow real cricket.

Satanic weather forecasters compete to inflict the most misery upon the population by manipulating the weather in this light card game for 3-5 players.

In technical terms, Hail, Satan! is a simultaneous response trick-taking game. Each turn, the ‘boss’ player will choose one card from their hand and play it face up. All other players choose a card from their hand and play it face down. The card must be of the same weather pattern, if possible. All players reveal their cards and the highest on-suit card wins, adjusting the weather forecast accordingly.

Professional players want to get the weather right, Evil players want to get it wrong, and everyone is free to choose each round how they want to play. Whoever inflicts the most misery over six short hands wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Rivet Wars: Reloaded – Allied Reinforcements Expansion
description:
Add battle-winning reinforcements to your Allied Forces!

Turn the tide of battle with the Allied Reinforcements Expansion. Recruit vital support from the massive M11 Double Play tank, Rosie the Riveter hero, and upgraded General Patston in his power suit, plus so much more.

With fixed turrets and all-new infantry types (including canine units!), you’ll be blasting enemies to smithereens in no time. The blighters won’t know what hit ‘em!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rivet Wars: Reloaded – Blight Reinforcements Expansion
description:
Add battle-winning reinforcements to your Blight Forces!

Steamroll your enemies with the huge Gryphon’s Hammer tank and bring on formidable Plague Troopers under the command of Doctor Vorne, plus so much more. Add battle-winning reinforcements to your Blight Forces with this expansion for Rivet Wars: Reloaded!

With fixed turrets and all-new infantry types (including canine units!), you’ll be blowing the opposition away in no time. Onward, Rivets!

—description from the publisher

Heading Into Language Land is a card game designed to make language learning truly unforgettable by transforming the process of learning a language into a magical adventure for you and your friends! Multiple players with different ages, ability levels, and target languages can all put their knowledge of their languages to the test simultaneously! In addition, you can play solo, cooperatively, or competitively, and the time required to play, as well as the difficulty level, can be adjusted easily. Now, there’s no excuse: you can play your way to mastery over your target language!

In this game, you get the chance to become one of several amazing fantasy characters, travel freely to visit wondrous locations, collect special pick ups, and clash with other players in battle. What’s more, all of the people, places, and deadly creatures you will encounter while playing Heading Into Language Land come directly out of the story by the same name, and they all present special word categories unique unto themselves. You must speak – or sign! – words or sentences in your target languages about those word categories in order to win over allies, defeat enemies, raise your langevity, and achieve victory.

So, c’mon! What are you waiting for? We’re heading into Language Land!

—description from the designer

In D.O.T, you secretly play cards into personal and shared pools, manipulating colored dot totals to gain the highest scoring Difference.Over.Time.

The twist? The goalpost shifts. Sometimes you're rewarded for being far off the mark, and at other times for getting as close as possible.

Master the shifting logic, outwit your opponent, and disrupt their plans at just the right moment to claim victory.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gems of Iridescia: Cobalt Keep Promo Cards
description:
The Cobalt Keep promo card designed by Rock, Stone & Dice Games to supporters of the 2025 Support the Dice Tower crowdfunding campaign. This card was made available to those that purchased Promo Pack D.

Card Details
Name: Cobalt Keep
Cost: 2 Gold Coins
Ability: Reveal any two tiles not in the Castle Row.
Note: Must be at the start of your turn, prior to revealing tiles.

These cards are shuffled into the Merchant Deck during setup. The card is compatible with the Gems of Iridescia base game as well as the Starlight of Iridescia expansion.

Keine Hemmungen is played with the 2 teams. Each team puts their token on the start field. The cards are shuffled. The starting team selects a player who reads the card and performs a pantomime. His team has 2 minutes to guess what he is performing. If they manage to guess the word in these 2 minutes, they move their token 1 field to the goal field. 
The team whose token reaches the goal field first wins the game.

All jigsaw pieces are distributed to the players and the cards are shuffled. The start player reads a card and answers the question. If he manages to answer the question correctly, he can put one of his jigsaw pieces on the table. If it fits to another one on the table, he can even put another of his jigsaw pieces there. 
The player who managed to get rid off all his pieces first wins the game.

In That's Mine (or Minero) players take turns flipping cards from the draw pile and placing them on the table. Be ready to think quick and slap fast if you want to claim the gems, gold nuggets, and diamond cards for yourself! But don't wait too long... if four sticks of dynamite are flipped, the mine will blow and wipe all the remaining treasure off the table! Once you've reached the end of the deck, the mine is empty and whoever claimed the most valuable objects wins!

This game can be played in several variants: 
The start player selects 4 cards from the 160 cards and creates a set where one card does not fit. The first player who guesses which card does not fit gets the card. The game is played over 3 rounds and the player who has the most cards win. 
In another variant, the players take turns and everybody creates a logical chain with several cards. The other players have to guess, which card should be added at the end of the logical chain. And again the player who is the first one in guessing gets the card and the player with the most cards after 3 rounds wins the game.

boardgame: Chronicles of the Num Oluur I: Storm of the Gods
description:
The game is set in the imaginary, and quite hostile, world of Num Oluur. The Gods of Num Oluur are competing to become the most powerful and adored divinity. Victory can be gained either by capturing an elemental force, Vaal Er Num, or by developing a following capable of building a sanctuary.

On their turn, players role the dice to move the Vaal Er Num and then place followers in attempts to capture this brute force.

The game combines elements of creating a different board each time it is played (not unlike Carcassonne) and a more strategic part of competing with other players. On this second aspect, old school gamers might remember the very excellent Zargos. And if you don't it is worth checking out!

The game is meant to be the first part of a series of games in the universe of the Num Oluur. The outcome of this game provides a different start for Chapter II.

—description from the designer

It's a cycling racing game so the idea is fairly simple: go from point A to point B and preferably get there first. Great idea, but still room for improvement, right? To make it more entertaining, you will be managing a team of 3 racers to whom you will allocate competence points in three fields: sprint(sprint), stamina (endurance), and climbing (grimpeur). You will then have to monitor the consumption of these competences to make sure your racers don't blow before the finish line!

—description from the designer

This game of hide-and-seek is played in the box of the game. At the beginning of the game, the 38 tiles are shuffled and put into the slots of game board. The start player selects 2 tiles. If they are identical, the player gets the tiles and can immediately select another 2. 
If they are not identical, the player just put them back in the slots.
The game ends, if there are no more tiles on the board. The player with the most tiles wins.
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Molassacre: Escape the Flood is a fast-paced card game for 2 to 5 players, perfect for family game night, friend groups, classrooms, and anyone who enjoys a little light sabotage in a sticky situation.
In the game, you’re one of a handful of panicked townspeople racing through molasses-soaked streets after the real-life 1919 disaster. Your goal? Be the first to reach 1,000 feet and escape the sticky doom. Sounds easy? Think again.
Each turn, you’ll draw and play from a deck filled with movement cards, nasty obstacles, clever remedies, and slick safety gear. But there's a twist! Only one can outrace the Molassacre! 
Meanwhile, your opponents are tossing syrup slicks, panicked horses, and molasses traps in your path. You’ll need just the right cards (and a bit of luck) to counter their sabotage and keep your footing.
It’s fast, funny, and full of sweet, syrupy sabotage. Easy to learn, quick to play, and sticky in all the best ways.

—description from the designer

boardgame: DOOM: The Dark Ages – Arena Board Game
description:
In DOOM: The Dark Ages | Arena Board Game, players face off as either the DOOM Slayer or the demonic hordes, ripping and tearing across a variety of battlefields. Facing monstrous demons drawn directly from the seminal DOOM video game series, players will use a host of devastating weapons to battle across three rounds with increasing stakes, decorating the hex-based arena with their opponent's gibs.

DOOM: The Dark Ages | Arena Board Game uses the same ruleset as DOOM | Arena Board Game.

The SYW Battle Series (SYWBS) games are playable grand-tactical studies of battles of the Seven Years War. The series’ intent is to show how command operates on the battlefields, using the weapons and tactics of the period. The system emphasizes command & control and playability.
The games all share the following characteristics:
Scale = 200-220 m/hex (219-240 yrd/hex)
Time = 30 min/Turn
Counter = 1 Brigade, 1 Battalion, 1 to 3 Squadrons, 1 Battery, 1 Leader
Each unit's historical flag and uniform are displayed on Counters, Orders/organizational Displays, and Orders of battle (in Playbooks).

Hastenbeck 1757 simulates the battle fought on the 26th of July 1757 in Lower Saxony (Hanover) during the Seven Years War. Louis Charles César Le Tellier, maréchal d'Estrées commanded the French Army, with contingents from Palatinate and Austria: 63,300 men (86 bns, 101 sqns) and 76 guns. Prince William Augustus, Duke of Cumberland commanded the Allied Army made up of troops from Hanover, Hesse, Brunswick and Bückeburg: 38,400 men (52 bns, 47 sqns) and 34 guns.

—description from the designer

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Mensch ärgere Dich nicht: noch mehr
description:
This box contains the card game of Pachisi:
To set up the game, lay out the number cards (1-40) in rows, using two copies of the 1-10 cards if playing with 3-4 players. Shuffle the dice cards, then deal two to each player. On a turn, a player plays one die card (which shows values from 1 to 6), then either starts a new row of cards or adds to an existing row.
To start a new row, take the card showing this number from the display and place it in front of you; when starting a row, you can choose to play both die cards instead of only one. To add to a row, add the die value played to the number on the top card of one of your rows, then take this card from the display.
If another player has used a desired card in one of her rows, steal it from her row and use it for yourself. You can have at most three rows in progress. At the end of your turn draw back up to two die cards.
When you play a die card to place the 40 card on a row, you then score the row, earning one point for each card in the row plus one point for each star on cards in that row. Record this score on paper, then return these cards to the display.
The game ends when a player scores 30, 35, or 40 points, with this total being dependent on the number of players: 2, 3, or 4.

This box contains the puzzle game of Pachisi:
Each player has 5 dice with letters a, b, c, d, e and e. The goal is to collect the letters in that order. When rolling your (remaining) dice, you can substitute some of them with dice from other players. In this case, they might lose part of the set they already collected (depending on your roll). Otherwise, it is played according to the usual Pachisi rules.

Furthermore, this box contains instructions for 20 variants for Pachisi which are the results of an idea competition.

The game master shuffles the cards with pictures and deals them equally among the players. He keeps the cards with text for himself. Then the leader takes one card and reads the text.
The player who has a card with a road sign corresponding to the text reads it aloud. If the sign is correct, i.e., the numbers on both cards match, the player takes the cards.
If the player makes a mistake, they keep the card with the sign, and the host places a red penalty card with a picture of a police officer and one penalty token in front of them.
The host then takes the red penalty card, and the game continues.
The winner is the player who knows the road signs best. The loser is the player with the most penalty cards.

The playing field with ramp and back wall is set up. Each player has nine chips of one color where the number on the chip indicates the player's strength. In the first half, nine rounds are played where each player secretly selects a chip, places it face down in the middle of the table and reveals it simultaneously. The lowest chip or chips are removed from the game, and the others are returned to the players for the second half and placed face up. In the event of a tie, a shot with the ball decides whether the chip is kept or discarded. In the second half, each player may shoot the ball as many times as they have chips left from the first half. If a player hits a scoring field, they may place one of their own chips there. For a missed attempt, the player must give up one chip. Once all shots have been taken, all players add up the points under their chips, and the player with the most points wins.

Race your opponents for the win with different vehicles and drivers across two maps in different weather conditions for a taste of delivery driving: a daily road race through a random maze. In this game speed is important, but the route you choose is even more important since even the fastest fall behind if they take too many wrong turns.

—description from the designer

A new virus has emerged from the depths of Asia and is sweeping across the world. Lead your science team to stop it while containment is still an option in the card game 'R-Naught'.

At least one player must match or beat the virus's r-naught with counter measures before the virus reaches R0 10 or more and everyone loses.

—description from the designer

Write the rhyme. Beat the time.


    Develop comeback skills.



    Develop rhyme skills.



    Develop poetry skills.


Race a 30-second timer to complete a line for a randomly generated Rhyme Scheme, using more words than your competitors. Fail to finish a line or finish a line that doesn't fit the Rhyme Scheme and get a Buster Mark. Remove Buster Marks by dissing yourself or other players.

A 30-second timer and pen and paper is needed.

—description from the designer

In DOOM | Arena Board Game, players face off as either the DOOM Slayer or the demonic hordes, ripping and tearing across a variety of battlefields. Facing monstrous demons drawn directly from the seminal DOOM video game series, players will use a host of devastating weapons to battle across three rounds with increasing stakes, decorating the hex-based arena with their opponent's gibs.

DOOM | Arena Board Game uses the same ruleset as DOOM: The Dark Ages | Arena Board Game.

A Monopoly themed around the german content creator group PietSmiet.

Theme
Players take the part of land owners, attempting to buy and then develop their land. Income is gained by other players visiting their properties and money is spent when they visit properties belonging to other players. When times get tough, players may have to mortgage their properties to raise cash for fines, taxes and other misfortunes.

Gameplay
On their turn, a player rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, they has the opportunity to buy it or auction it to the highest bidder. If a player owns all the spaces within a color group, they may then build houses and hotels on these spaces, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If they lands on a property owned by another player, they must pay that player rent according to the value of the land and any buildings on it. There are other places on the board which can not be bought, but instead require the player to draw a card and perform the action on the card, pay taxes, collect income, or even go to jail.

Goal
The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money
