description:
Meeting your partner's family? Going on a first date? Looking for that new job? You must make a good First Impression!

First Impressions is a trick-taking game of getting noticed!

The game is played over five rounds.  Each round, every player gets a minimum of 9 "attribute" cards. These cards represent qualities that someone may quickly see about you - the car you drive, the pet you have, where you live, and what you do for a living. Each round there will be 3 tricks to bid on - the tricks represent others who are impressed by different things. Some see money and living space; some see the car you drive or the pet you own. Some may look for little; some for a lot! Each player will strategically split their 9 cards into 3 groups of 3 cards, trying to meet the attributes that each trick is looking for with a “winning” combination.  The player who meets the attributes best takes the trick.  After 5 rounds, count up your points and see who wins!

—description from the designer

Do you know what sustainable consumption is, and how to reduce the burden on the Earth? And would you like to learn how to help endangered animals and prevent biodiversity loss? The resources of Mother Earth are abundant but limited. The time has come for us to show gratitude and take good care of our environment.

The aim of this educational trivia game is to increase awareness and to provide practical tips about sustainable choices and living in harmony with nature and the animal kingdom. The game approaches this challenging and complicated topic through positivity by encouraging and inspiring players. This trivia game also helps us understand the factors connected to decision-making concerning environmental issues – the things we as individuals can only have a limited impact on.

The game is recommended for ages 8 and up. It is equally suitable for those environmentally aware and for those only starting to learn more about the topic.

The four question categories are:

Environmental agreements and laws 
Recycling and sustainable consumption 
Endangered species and biodiversity loss 
Environmental learning and skills

Experience the legendary Tecmo Bowl like never before—in a fast-paced tabletop board game version! Choose your team, call the plays, and battle for victory in this authentic adaptation of the iconic original video game. Take control of the Offense and push down the field to score Touchdowns (7 points) or Field Goals (3 points), while the Defense tries to predict plays and shut down drives. Every decision counts as you work to outmaneuver your opponent in a game of strategy, skill, and classic Tecmo Bowl football action. With nostalgic pixel-style art, easy-to-learn rules, and fast-paced gameplay, Tecmo Bowl delivers a timeless football experience for all players! Add it to your next family game night and watch the competition unfold.


     NOSTALGIC GAMEPLAY STRAIGHT FROM THE TECMO BOWL VIDEO GAME: Relive the magic of Tecmo Bowl in this 2-player, head-to-head tabletop board game adaptation, featuring authentic play-calling mechanics and classic football strategy. Choose your actions wisely and watch the competition come to life!


 

     FAST-PACED FOOTBALL ACTION: OUTSMART YOUR OPPONENT: Each team gets one offensive possession per quarter, making every play critical to the outcome of the game. The Offense tries to gain yards, score touchdowns, or kick field goals, while the Defense attempts to predict plays and force turnovers. The team with the most points claims victory! 



     BIG ACTION CARDS CREATE GAME-CHANGING PLAYS: Big Action cards give each team the ability to break tackles, force turnovers, or shift the game's momentum.. These cards bring unpredictable, energetic moments to every match. Each team has a limited number of Big Action cards per half, so use them wisely! 



     EASY-TO-LEARN RULES AND DICE-ROLLING MECHANICS: Both Offense and Defense select plays simultaneously, then implement the results by flipping their cards and rolling distance dice. Plays happen quickly, making the game engaging and perfect for all football fans and board game fans. 



     ULTIMATE NOSTALGIA FOR CLASSIC GAMERS AND SPORTS FANS: With its timeless, classic look and simple, engaging gameplay, Tecmo Bowl is a great choice for lovers of the original video game, casual football fans, and families looking for competitive board game fun. Add it to your collection of board games for the whole family to enjoy.


—description from the publisher

A fantasy card game that develops creativity, quick thinking, and improvisation skills.

The goal of the game is to tell stories, using 3 different game modes. In creative mode, you draw from the Prompt, Adjective, and Noun decks and use them to tell a story. In competitive mode, you similarly tell stories, but score points for meeting certain storytelling criteria, and can be challenged by other players to include more nouns or adjectives during the allowed time. In collaborative mode, all players work together to tell a story that meets the criteria before time runs out.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood of Lions: The Somme – 4th British Amy Sector: July 1916
description:
Blood of Lions is a WWI solitaire wargame depicting the actions of the British 4th Army during the month of July at the Battle of the Somme. 
The game uses a combination of dice, cards, generated Command and Leadership points as well as some push your luck mechanics in order to advance on objectives and obtain victory points.

Players are allocated Command Points for which they may undertake specific sets of actions each turn. The objective of the game is to gain as many victory points as possible while reaching objectives defended by a German bot activated by player actions, event cards, and automatic defense values.

—description from the publisher

Take cover is a fast playing card game for two players. Gamers should be able to play several battles over a one hour lunch!!!

Overview: Two German squads battle two Russian squads on the tabletop battlefield.  
Players perform actions to improve their squad’s combat effectiveness by attaching Weapon, Leader, Terrain cards, and by advancing on and firing on the enemy. 
Event cards are played to damage or impede the enemy squad. The 10-sided die (d10) resolves combat and Event card results. 
The game is won by eliminating both of the opponent’s squads.

With only 2 pages of rules and 72 cards gamers will be up and battling in no time.  Rules for special weapons, leaders, and terrain are all there.

—description from the publisher

description:
Shapeships is a free drawing battle game, where players roll dice, draw ships, use powers and fight to the death.

It is easy to learn but deeply strategic, with four different species and 60 different shapeships.

Primarily it is a 2 player game, though free-for-all with 3+ players and Team modes are available.

—description from the designer

Cast spells as fast as possible!

Cast spells by matching all the numbers on one side of your hand to the same number, and cast magic!

Fire, water, wind, earth, grass, light, dark...
Collect the seven magical powers that overflow from nature, cast spells as fast as you can and defeat your rivals!
Be the last one to survive and become the greatest magician, "Spell Seven"!

Today Another Battle is a WW2 solitaire wargame where you will face difficult choices and decisions in every part of the game.  Under your command are units of soldiers and armour set against the Western Front Normandy landscape. In trying to reach your objectives you will encounter bitter enemies, obstacles, good fortune and crippling bad luck.

The game is quick to setup and play, it takes around 30 minutes to play a shorter game, to less than a couple of hours for a larger game and requires very little space.

This is a print and play game with the PDFs containing all the rules, scenarios, maps, player aids and counters required to play. You'll need to print them out, mount the maps and counters to card and provide your own pencil and a regular six sided dice. We have kept the maps and counters to a few sheets (of either Letter or A4 size) so it is very inexpensive and quick to print out and mount.

How the Narrative Works
The game features multiple scenarios with each scenario will detail your starting forces, your objectives and any special rules.
Through the mission a strong emergent narrative emerges through both gameplay and events. This includes:

     Every time you move your forces into a new Zone, as you progress through the scenario there are Events that can occur. These are not scripted, so no two scenarios will play the same. These Events can include meeting up with the French Resistance, catching the enemy napping on the job, through to tragedies such as accidents or your tanks breaking down etc.
     These Events can be a great advantage or not, and give your game an unpredictable nature that is both in keeping with the period and creates a unique narrative experience.
     In addition the player can spend Command Points to inspire their units when facing certain death or when hugely outnumbered. This mechanic along with the Events will give memorable gaming moments.


How the Game Plays
You will choose from one of the various scenarios with each scenario detailing the map used,  your starting forces, any reinforcements and your starting location. Each scenario will have a different objective for you to meet, for example it may be reaching a set point on the map or holding onto a specific zone.

Morale Tracker
The morale tracker is one of the key gameplay elements within the game. Maintaining a high morale for your force is important to give your units the best chance for success. Your force morale can increase as you take control of new zones, reach objectives and defeat the enemy in combat. Conversely it can also reduce if you loose territory or retreat from battle, which can have dire consequences.

How you balance your morale combined with the Command Points game mechanics are key to your strategy in making very hard decisions within the game.

The Map
The map is split into zones with each zone detailing the number of Infantry Counters (the number in the circle) and the number of Armour Counters (the number in the square) that the player can move into that Zone, as a maximum. The colour of those shapes also represent any bonus for the defender with yellow representing defence bonus bonus (these are explained in the combat section).

The Counters
The counters in the game represent the various squads, support weapons and various tanks and armoured vehicles in the game. As well as showing what nationality (US or German) they also include important gameplay information.  Each counter details how many dice to roll in combat for both against infantry counters and armoured counters, plus if the counter has saving throw and any modifiers on opposing armoured counter saving throws.

The Turn
During the turn the player will be determining aspects such as the weather for the turn, how many command points that they have to spend and what they spend those command points on. The player can spend command points on bringing on additional reinforcements, moving units, performing air strikes,
Every turn has a set structure and list of key mechanics to go through. 
The turn structure of the game is:
1. Determine Weather (Weather can have a direct outcome on air support and combat).
2. Determine Command Points (Roll for command points, which will be used to move counters and perform set actions such as Rallies, artillery  support etc).
3. Determine Units Out of Supply (Friendly counters cut off and isolated will be at a significant disadvantage).
4. Rally (You can roll to rally broken counters).
5. Reinforcements (Depending on the mission there may be extra reinforcements that you can bring onto the map).
6. Roll for Zone Controlled Events (Every turn you will roll to see if any events, such as being contested by the enemy affect Zones you control).
7. Recon (Before moving your forces you can spend Command Points, if you wish, on recon for adjacent zones).
8. Move (You can spend Command Points on moving units into the adjacent areas).
9. Roll for New Zone (As you move units into new Zones you will roll for events, including potential enemy units).
10. Combat (Resolve any combat where your units occupy the same Zone as enemy units)
11. Turn End (The turn end and check for Victory Conditions)

Command Points

Each turn the player will roll (with modifiers) to determine the amount of Command Points that they can use for that turn. The player will have to decide how best to use these Command Points and will have difficult decisions to make as they often will not have enough Command Points to do everything that would ideally want to do. These Command Points can be used in a variety of different ways, including:

     Moving Units into an adjacent Zone.
     Rally Units in a Zone.
     Use Recon to identify enemies in an adjacent Zone.
     Selecting the type of attack (covered in the Combat section below) to be used in  Combat.
     Use Artillery and Air Strikes before the Combat is resolved.


Entering a Map Zone
Every time the player enters into a Zone in the game there are a small number of charts to go through to determine any enemies plus any random events. Random events can help or hinder the player and add a great narrative flavour to the game!

Combat
During the Combat phase, any Zone that contains the player's unit counters plus any enemy counters then Combat will be resolved. Before any combat takes place, the player will need to decide which type of Attack they will be using.

The player has three types of attacks they can choose between:


     Normal Attack - costs 0 Command Points, but the enemy will attack first.
     Full Attack - costs 2 Command Points, but the player will attack first.


Each counter will show the player how many dice to roll for combat with a six required to hit the enemy. The player will then make a Saving throw and determine the outcome for the hit counter.

—description from the designer

Europe sleeps under the rain. But the streets are restless. Veterans return. Blockades rise. And something darker lurks behind the mist…
Rain&Fog introduces new elements to add to the game and new modes. You can introduce all or only some of the elements of the Rain&Fog expansion in your Detectives Vs Criminals games. The elements that Rain&Fog makes available to you are the following.

VETERANS & SERGEANTS
Stronger than Agents. Tougher than Thugs.
Sergeants block roads and stop Criminals from hiding or stealing.
Veterans block the Investigate Action.
Sergeants and Veterans use the special Expert Die when they Fight.

5TH & 6TH PLAYER
Two more players can now join the game—taking direct control of Agents or Thugs.
They get custom dashboards, new powers, and extra actions to support their team… or turn the tide.

WOUNDS
No more card discards. When hit, characters draw Wound Cards—and carry them like dead weight.
Stack up too many, and it gets dangerous… for everyone.

SOLO & CO-OP MODE
Face the full game alone or in co-op—as the Criminal team.
Detectives move automatically, with a the new “Area system”.
Evade them, steal treasures, and survive the ever-growing threat of exposure.

SPECIAL HEISTS
Forget multiple Treasures—this time, there’s only one target.
Each Special Heist is a standalone mission with unique rules and setup. Choose your objective, plan carefully… and pull off the impossible.
The Queen’s Tour – Track Her Majesty across Europe and strike when no one expects it.
The Gold on the Orient Express – Time it right, or lose everything in transit.
The Von Streusburg Heist – Find the hidden castle and steal Cleopatra’s legendary bracelet. 
These missions are high risk, high reward—and nothing like a typical game. Just being in the right city won’t be enough…

—description from the designer

Sol Braynïa is a fantasy skirmish game from the creators of Mars: Code Aurora. It is generic and can be played with any range of miniatures, offering rules that can be adapted to your collection.

With bands of 8 to 15 miniatures per player, a simple and tactical action resolution system, and a mission generator that ensures each game is different, Sol Braynïa will appeal to both beginners looking for a game that is easy to learn and veterans who want a space to express their creativity.

—description from the publisher

An abstract strategy game where two players are using their dice, shields and master to capture each other's pieces. Whoever captures the opponent's master wins.

Components

     Board with 9 x 9 grid
     8 dice for each player
     2 shields for each player
     1 master for each player


To set up, each player chooses a colour and takes the components of that colour. On the row closest to each player, they will place their dice and master in the following configuration from left to right: 1 2 5 6 M 6 5 2 1

Each die should be oriented so that the 4 is facing your opponent and the 3 is facing yourself. The shields are placed on the second row, laying down in front of the 5/6.

On each turn, players move any one of their pieces as follows:

     Dice move the amount of spaces visible on the top. They may not move horizontally, and may only make one right angle each move.
     Master moves one space at a time, and does not move diagonally. 
     Shields move one space at a time. Since they take up 2 spaces, one space will remain the same during each move. Shields can be turned into soldiers by using a turn to stand it up to occupy one space. Once standing, it moves one space diagonally each turn. It can later be layed down to become a shield again only if it is not in check.


To capture a piece and remove it from the board, you must land directly on that piece. There is no jumping of pieces. To capture a shield, it requires 2 dice, one for each space the shield occupies. When both pieces are in place to capture the shield, only one piece moves and performs the capture.

Before capturing a master, you must announce to your opponent that their master is in "check". You may not move yourself into check position.

If your master reaches your opponent's first row, you get one of your dice back. Your master immediately returns to it's original position on the centre space of your first row and the regained piece is places on the square your master reached with a one on top and four facing you. If your opponent has a piece on your centre spot, you capture it when replacing your master.

This is a 32-card pack that features one very savvy Swine. This gentleman is incredibly knowledgeable and even more influential: unlike the other guests, you will have no idea what the topic of conversation will be until you meet him. The upside? Well, if you impress this very discerning guest you will earn some extra credibility with the crowd.

boardgame: The Void Unveiled: Echoes of Arkham
description:
The Void Unveiled: Echoes of Arkham is a story-driven Lovecraftian board game of investigation, madness, and cosmic horror, designed for 1–4 players.
Fully cooperative, rich in narrative, and steeped in creeping insanity, it invites you to descend into a world where every choice matters—and madness is never far behind.

Step into the role of investigators unraveling a series of eerie mysteries in 1930s Arkham, each told through branching narrative chapters filled with meaningful choices, unique events, and escalating dread. As you read the story, your decisions shape the course of the game, leading to different outcomes and consequences.

Each creature you encounter is governed by its own distinct set of rules and behaviors—not a single generic AI system in sight. Every monster feels unique, each one changing how you fight, think, and survive.

Survive the unfolding horrors and defeat the Elder, only then will you truly unveil the Void.

Combat is tactical and unpredictable. Sanity is fragile. And the Void is waiting.

—description from the publisher

A puzzle card is an escape room experience in a birthday card  card. The recipient must survey the scene inside, look for clues, and solve a series of puzzles to reach the final answer and escape.

10 expansion cards exclusively for Panic-Taking.

You need four skittles of the same colour and a six-sided dice. The playing field itself consists of four levels:

1st level: small circle, with two down arrows and three protective levels
 2nd level: medium circle, no protective level and several down arrows
 3rd and 4th level: large circle, with down arrows and protection levels.

The skittle that is thrown out of the fourth level is eliminated. The aim of the game is to stay on top with your four cones for as long as possible.
Course of the game

The first skittle is placed in front of the "house" at the start of the game, otherwise you can come out on the roll of a six. The dice are then rolled in turn. If you roll a six, you can roll again. Other players can be hit if you land on their square.

You are not allowed to hit on the white protective squares. However, if you hit a skittle on a black square, it no longer goes to the "house" but moves one circle further down. You can also capture by voluntarily moving down one level. Sometimes hitting an opponent triggers further reactions if the opponent displaces other skittles when sliding.

Two skittles of the same colour on a white square can form a blockade, i.e. block the other cones. They may then still stand, but may no longer move over the blockade. The winner is the last player to have a piece on the devil's wheel. The duration of the game ranges from 10 to 30 minutes.

—description from Wikipedia (translated)

The game was published in the 1960s.

Kelku-mäng (Sledge game) is a roll and move game, where you are sliding down the hill. First player to get down wins the game.

On your turn you roll the die, and if you land on the red numbered square, you read what happens. You might get a extra roll, go downward some spaces, go upward some spaces, loose next turn and so on. After all your sliding ends, it's next players turn.

Buccaneer Heroes is a sandbox pirate Print & Play game for 1–6 players, playable solo or cooperatively. Each round, players roll three dice: one determines movement, while the others dictate how much plunder is gained and which faction can be attacked.

Players can visit settlements, trade, upgrade ships and crew, explore pirate dens, complete missions, encounter random events, or unearth buried treasures. Cooperate with other pirates to defeat formidable foes (even in solo mode), or sail alone for higher risks and greater rewards. Actions like discovering treasure or attacking ports can impact others, so tracking everyone's progress is key.

The game spans 24 rounds across eight seasons. At the end of each season, a change in wind direction and a Global Event shake up the board, culminating in an epic finale that tests each player's worth - with only one emerging as the true hero. Earn achievement points through most actions (combat or acquisitions), and the player with the highest total wins.

Buccaneer Heroes delivers an epic, story-driven action-adventure in a vibrant Caribbean world. Packed with twists, characters, monsters, events, and treasures, it is surprisingly quick (30–45 minutes solo) yet offers immense replayability. One playthrough will not reveal all the secrets of this pirate saga!

Gear up and get ready for the hardest heist yet! The Gang: Tools of the Trade replaces the specialists of the base game with a special tool for each player at the beginning of play. These tools are private information, and each member of the team must determine on their own when their tool will be the most useful.

What's more, two additional vaults are included, both with extra security that you must overcome to open. From the original designers of The Gang, this mini expansion will bring more variety and challenge to the game for expert players!

Skift is an abstract strategy game for two players, ages 8 and up. Average playing time is 10 to 15 minutes.

Outmaneuver your opponent by shifting your pieces and the board itself. Your objective: eliminate all of your opponent’s kings or pawns.

Setup

The game board consists of 9 square tiles, with each tile divided into 4 quadrants. Game pieces include 2 kings and 6 pawns per player. Arrange the 9 tiles in a 3×3 grid to form the board, then place the pieces in a checkerboard pattern around the center.

Gameplay

Players choose colors and randomly determine who goes first. On your turn, perform one of three actions: a piece move, a tile move, or a tile rotation.

Piece Moves

Move one of your kings or pawns one quadrant in any direction (orthogonally or diagonally), pushing any pieces in its path. Pieces may move (and be pushed) within a tile or across tiles. Pieces pushed off the board are removed from the game.

Tile Control

The player with more kings on a tile controls it. If tied in kings, the player with more total pieces on the tile controls it. If still tied, neither player controls the tile.

Tile Moves

Move a tile that you control one space orthogonally (not diagonally), pushing any tiles in its path. Pieces on that tile move with it, retaining their positions as it is moved. Tiles can also be pushed off the board (see “Tile connectivity and Splintering” below).

Tile Rotations

Rotate a tile that you control 180 degrees. Pieces on that tile rotate with it, retaining their positions as it is rotated.

The No-Undo Rule

You may not undo your opponent’s last move. If they moved a piece, you may not return any piece to its previous position. If they moved a tile, you may not return any tile to its previous position.

The Landlocked Tile Rule

A tile is landlocked if it is surrounded on all four orthogonal sides. A landlocked tile may not be moved or rotated, even if you control it.

Tile Connectivity and Splintering

All tiles must remain connected, either orthogonally or diagonally. If a move causes the tiles to splinter into two or more disconnected groups, only the largest group remains; all other tiles and any pieces on them are removed from the game. If multiple groups are tied for largest, the player who caused the splinter chooses which group remains.

Suicide Moves

You may make a move that results in your own pieces or controlled tiles being removed from the board.

Winning the Game

You win the game if your opponent runs out of either kings or pawns. However, if the same move also causes you to run out of either kings or pawns, the player with more remaining pieces wins. If tied, the game ends in a draw.

Click here for official rules and diagrams: https://splinterboardgame.blogspot.com/2025/07/skift-game-of-strategic-shifts-and.html

—description from the designer

The walls have fallen. The titans are here. And humanity’s last hope is hanging by a thread. Attack on Titan: Apocalypse is the climax of the UniVersus Attack on Titan trilogy. Featuring powerful new cards, unforgettable character moments, and original illustrations created exclusively by series creator Hajime Isayama, this set is a must-have for fans and collectors alike.

Whether you’re clashing with titans, rallying with humanity’s final defenders, or chasing the limited Chrome Rares featuring Isayama’s art, Apocalypse delivers the most intense, emotionally charged UniVersus experience yet.

Each booster contains 11 total cards:

     1 character card
     6 commons
     3 uncommons
     one of the following: Rare, Ultra Rare or Secret Rare


6 different serialized Chrome Rares featuring brand-new art from series creator Hajime Isayama made exclusively for UniVersus!

Obsidian and Pearl foils return to help celebrate the classic look of the manga in this booster release!

Epic climax to the UniVersus and Attack on Titan trilogy!

Both sides of the 1024 cards depicts a something important happening in the history of rock music and beyond. Main categories of these facts are dates of LP's and singles, but there is also loads of other things (concerts, movies, beginnings of recording formats and corporations, inventing instruments and so on). Earliest year is 1860 and the last dates are from 1986. On one side of the card there is always the thing which you have to place on the timeline and the date of the corresponding thing in the other side. Precision of the answer depends on accuracy of available source. Accuracy is usually in months, but sometimes it's an exact date or some rare cases just a year. This gives a nice balance mechanic for players with different amounts of knowledge. Know-it-all's can use months and for other players just the year is enough.

At the start of the round as many cards as there are players are delt to starting player. Starting player chooses one card and gives rest to the next player in clockwise order. When everybody has a card, the last player will place their card first in the timeline of cards and then other players will do the same in counter clockwise order. If you place your card in the right place it stays on the line, otherwise you will collect it as a minus point. After the round ends the player in the right side of previous starting player will start the new round.

Game last as many rounds as there are players. Winner is the player with least minus points.

Try to win a maximum of medals by giving the best estimate at themed question. Each turn, chose a theme out of 9 (history, science, sport, art, out of the ordinary, etc.), putting the theme’s medal at stake, then read the corresponding question on the next quiz card (there are more than 150 of them). Each question expects a numerical answer. All players (or teams) secretly write a value on their own mini whiteboard. Once one player has done so, the others have just 10 seconds to respond. The answer written on the back of the card is read out loud, and the player whose guess is the closest to the answer places the medal on front of them, unless it’s already there, in that case they win it and get to wear it. No need to get the exact answer, but if you do, you directly win the medal at stake. Once all medals are won, whoever has the most wins!

Investigate and infiltrate an ancient secret society in this solve-a-mystery game.

You receive a mysterious envelope filled with clues and artifacts: an ancient constellation map, old photographs, a locket, a wax-sealed letter, and more! Deep-dive into the ancient objects, articles, and expert resources to track down the location of an ancient alchemical manuscript!

Discover hidden websites, send messages to characters, dispatch your team to track an ancient secret society through Europe, and get drawn into a world that will have you wonder: is this real?

Yanari: The Witches Market is a board game set in a magical market in the city of Yatamon within the world of Obojima. You play as a skilled witch racing to brew potions for customers as they go about their lives. The game is filled with new breathtaking art, as well as familiar potions and ingredients for the fans of Obojima. When you're done playing the game, you can learn more about the Yanari market by reading about it in Obojima: Tales From Yatamon.

During the game, players collect ingredient cards, then use these cards to brew the potions desired by specific customers. Brew the perfect potions to trigger customer bonus rewards — or simply brew them an acceptable potion to score a few points and access new customers. With two actions each turn, as well as a potential bonus cauldron action, you can generate combos for rewarding turns. Each of the five witch characters has their own unique ability, and the game includes many special ability cards and secret goals to enhance variability.

—description from the producer
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

boardgame: Universus: Yu Yu Hakusho – Dark Tournament
description:
The Dark Tournament is a seminal moment in anime history, and a fan-favorite arc for anime fans all around the world. And make no mistake – this isn’t just a normal booster pack, Yu Yu Hakusho: Dark Tournament is an experience of a lifetime!

The masterpiece Yu Yu Hakusho: Dark Tournament is in UniVersus CCG as a local game store exclusive. It is based on the supernatural action manga series by Yoshihiro Togashi that tells the story of Yusuke Urameshi, a delinquent teenager who becomes a Spirit Detective after his death.

Key Set Features:

     16 different characters!
     Reprints of key cards from the original Yu Yu Hakusho set!
     Chrome Rares of key characters!
     6 different Secret Rares! 


Each booster pack contains 12 cards: 1 character, 1 reprinted card from original Yu Yu Hakusho set, 6 commons, 3 uncommons, and 1 Rare, Ultra Rare, or Secret Rare.

—description from the publisher

Travel the world to retrieve the fabled Memory Stone!

You receive a mysterious box containing a 100 year old letter and a golden key. Follow the voyage of a former Society member and unravel a story that will take you from the jungles of Brazil to the heart of Europe in this solve-a-mystery game.

Discover hidden websites, send messages to characters, dispatch your team to remote parts of the world, and get drawn into a mystery that will have you wonder: is this real?

—description from the publisher

Infiltrate a secret society!

The Society of Curiosities has acquired an invitation to a secretive masked ball and has hired a mysterious agent to infiltrate the event. She will need your help to access - and survive! - this gathering.

Guide her through the vast mansion, avoid detection, explore ancient archives, and retrieve a stolen artifact!

Discover hidden websites, send messages to characters, dispatch your team to remote parts of the world, and get drawn into a mystery that will have you wonder: is this real?

—description from the publisher

Prof. McEwan, a renowned archaeologist, is missing. His research group thinks he may be in danger. He was searching for a lost artifact of great significance. The Enigma Fellowship has been engaged to locate him and help solve his quest.

Can you solve the mystery of Prof. McEwan’s location? If you find him, can you help him overcome challenges of a lifetime and rediscover the lost knowledge?

—description from the publisher

The Sonnburg Police Department has been investigating mysterious disappearances of citizens in the city for the past few weeks. The case has only taken a turn for the worse now that a Police officer has turned up missing as well. The only connection seems to be random playing cards received by each victim before they vanished. The Enigma Fellowship has been brought in due to our expertise in solving mysteries.

Can you solve the enigma of the scattered cards and find the kidnapping victims before something untoward happens? Can you apprehend the perpetrator? Lives depend upon you.

—description from the publisher

An experimental submarine, the ISS Sentinel, carrying a revolutionary new energy device and the world’s leading scientific experts has missed several scheduled check-ins. The Enigma Fellowship has been contacted for help. Given the lives and scientific knowledge at stake your mission is to not only find the submarine but also rescue the crew.

Can you locate the submarine and rescue the crew? Be prepared for a maritime adventure. There may be some deep diving involved.

—description from the publisher

Step into the prosperity and chaos of "Monga Flower Alley" and play the role of a gangster driver. Fight and expand your gang's influence in Taipei's Wanhua District.

In 1980s Monga, amidst its thriving chaos, you, a gang member in rapidly developing Taipei, maneuvered through its streets, seizing business opportunities and managing your team. Amidst these lucrative ventures, rival gangs and the police loomed as constant threats to your dominion.

Street Blooms of Monga is played over 5 rounds. On your turn, choose a terrain card from the supply, play one card from your hand, and then move your driver 1~3 blocks (orthogonally adjacent squares of the same color are treated as a block). Madames are then placed to occupy unclaimed establishments or challenge other organizations in brawls for territory control. The player who plays the “Wanhua Precinct” terrain card controls the police for patrols that turn — Roll the die to determine which illegal industry to inspect. The player controlling the largest single area in that industry receives criminal points as a penalty.

The winner is determined by subtracting the highest criminal record points from the total economic benefits obtained by all your workers.

—description from the publisher

Setup - Place the deck of cards face down in the center.
Flip over two cards face up — these are your match targets.
Pick two players sitting across from each other to each get a pair of dice.
3–6 players: Use 2 sets of dice.
7+ players: Use 3 sets.

How to Play - Players with dice race to roll matches for both cards.  When you match both dice, PASS your dice to the player on your left — they start rolling right away.
There’s no slam during a pass — just match, pass, and keep it moving!
How to Slam -If you roll your match before the player ahead of you passes, quickly slam your hand on their dice before they pass it.
If you slam in time, you win the round and take the two cards.
Flip two new cards and start the next round!

How to Win
Be the first to collect 6 Ranchero cards — hold onto them when a round ends and you’re crowned the Ultimate Ranchero!

Extra Rules - Stuck? Play a matching card from your hand to match one die. Keep rolling for the other!
Perfect match first try? Pass your dice to anyone (except someone already rolling).
Dice falls off the table? Count to 10 fast before you roll again!

Using a stolen ship title was a low cost way to start your career as a Captain in the Dog Star System...

But you didn’t factor in getting caught!

Now you’re on a one-way trip back to the Title Bureau. You’ve got to survive with enough credits to pay off the Title Boss, otherwise you’ll be permanently grounded. Your career will be over before it even begins!

This is a STANDALONE game. Dog Star Tripper takes place in the Dog Star System, but you don't need any other games to enjoy it.

Set up in seconds and enjoy in less than ten minutes. Take it on the go in a pocket-friendly mint tin!

First choose from 4 Missions on the Mission card and set your Reputation to the Mission's faction accordingly (or stay neutral and ignore the Missions).

Then draw 12 play cards facedown and add them to your Weapons, Engines, or Hull piles. Next use the piles to resolve the remaining 18 play cards as you draw them face up and reveal encounters--with hostile or peaceful ships from the four factions. You'll discard cards from your Weapons, Engines or Hull to fight or flee the encounters. How you respond will affect your Reputation with the four factions.

As you play, you'll earn credits by fighting ships, taking cargo, rescuing allies, completing your mission, and earning valor. Survive to the last card with 12000 credits to win! You'll pay off the Title boss and avoid being permanently grounded by the Bureau.

Good Luck, Tripper!

—description from the designer

Forsaken Souls: Hidden Ward is a print-and-play escape room set in 1933. Players take on the role of Detective Nathan Reed, who returns to the now-abandoned Hawksley Infirmary, the same institution whose secrets he exposed thirteen years prior. A mysterious letter has summoned him back, warning of a hidden ward that was never uncovered during the original investigation.

The game takes place entirely within this forgotten section of the asylum. Players explore its rooms, decipher cryptic clues, and unravel the final chapter of the asylum’s dark history. The goal is to escape the ward by solving puzzles embedded in fictional documents, illustrated clues, and narrative-driven challenges, while gradually uncovering what truly happened behind locked doors.

The game is designed for 1–6 players and can be played cooperatively. A detailed Game Master Guide allows one player to set up and facilitate the experience without spoilers, mimicking the structure and pacing of a physical escape room. Optional audio narration and video sequences enhance the atmosphere, and a progressive hint system helps players if they get stuck.

—description from the publisher

Forsaken Souls: Cursed Artifact is a print-and-play escape room game set in 1927. Players take on the role of Detective Nathan Reed, who is summoned to the Finch family estate to investigate the disappearance of a priceless music box with a long and storied legacy. As Reed delves deeper into the case, it becomes clear that the artifact’s disappearance is only the surface of a deeper family mystery.

Players uncover clues hidden in letters, ledgers, and family heirlooms while solving interconnected puzzles across four story-driven acts. The game blends logic, deduction, language puzzles, and physical interaction using printed components. As the story unfolds, players must trace the legacy of the Finch family — and decide how far they are willing to go to reveal the truth.

The game is designed for solo or cooperative play with up to six players. A comprehensive Game Master Guide allows for spoiler-free setup, enhancing immersion with optional audio narration and video scenes. The progressive web-based hint system ensures players are never stuck for long, making the experience smooth and cinematic.

—description from the publisher

Make Some Magic!

You are invited to participate in a sorcerous tournament of magic item creation!

Gather the five elements to enchant your way to the top by crafting potent potions, superb spells, fabulous familiars, resplendent rings, and arcane artifacts!

Watch the phases of the moon, transmute your elements, and use the powers of your creations to gather all-powerful Mana to garner great prestige!

—description from the publisher

Alphabet Code is a fast-paced logic and deduction game for 2 or more players. Each player secretly creates a 5-letter code using digital-style alphabet tiles (a–j), hidden behind a screen. Players take turns asking grid-based questions to deduce their opponent’s code before their own is uncovered.

On your turn, choose to ask:

How many adjacent squares are filled around a certain zone?

Is a specific square filled?

The game rewards clever questioning, spatial reasoning, and strategic thinking. If you believe you’ve cracked your opponent’s code, you may declare your answer—but beware: two incorrect guesses and you lose!

With optional Advance Rules that use real words and expanded setups using more letters, Alphabet Code offers scalable challenge and replayability.

Great for fans of deduction games like Mastermind or Cryptid, but with a uniquely visual and tactical twist.

—description from the publisher

Gagula is a fast-paced card game that blends fun and learning to raise awareness about the dangers of excessive sugar consumption. Designed for 3–5 players, Gagula pits you against sneaky snacks, sugary temptations, and your own cravings. Your goal? Avoid falling into a sugar overload while sabotaging your opponents before they do!

—description from the publisher

Explore the Solar System by managing your energy. An educational math game for children and adults alike.

A wide variety of components: a board printed on both sides, with different game modes, player boards, rocket, Martian, and energy tokens, as well as customizable dice.

Roll your die and multiply it by the energy you want to expend to reach the different planets and achieve your goal.

Step into the paws of mischievous house cats on a mission to cause maximum chaos! Explore a quirky, modular mansion where every room, from the sun-drenched Garden to the shadowy Basement, holds unique opportunities for mayhem. Outsmart the ever-watchful Human, sabotage rival felines, and unleash your inner agent of chaos in this lighthearted game of tactical pranks and purr-fect timing.

Players roam a tile-based house, rolling a custom die to move their cat meeples. Each turn, you’ll play Mischief Cards (room-specific actions like "Sofa Scratch" or "Toilet Paper Shred") to earn Mischief Tokens (victory points) while adding Chaos Tokens to rooms. Use Prank Cards ("Laser Pointer Chase," "Spray Bottle Surprise") to steal tokens, move opponents, or manipulate the Human! Every room has a Special Rule (e.g., swap cards in the Art Studio, teleport from the Front Door), creating dynamic combos.

But beware: Chaos Tokens attract the Human! At the end of each turn, the Human storms toward the messiest room, removing tokens and punishing cats caught in their path. Balance risk and reward as you plot paths, time pranks, and decide when to go all-in — or flee!

Earn tokens by playing Mischief Cards, cleverly activating room powers, and outmaneuvering rivals. Victory demands cunning tactics: harvest chaos efficiently, sabotage foes with pranks, and avoid the Human’s wrath. Be the cat with the most Mischief Tokens when the shared pool runs out!

—description from the designer

description:
Hon na čarodějnice ("Witch Hunt") is a satirical, irreverent card game from Bardmistr Games, set in a twisted medieval world of suspicion, betrayal, and dark humor.

Players take on the roles of inquisitors, vie for political influence by building the council, and dispatch opponents to the stake – branded as “witches” in a ruthless power play.

Each round, you get two actions: draw, play a citizen, accuse or exonerate someone, or trigger an event card. Creative thinking is encouraged – players invent their own reasons to accuse each other and fuel the chaos.

The gameplay mechanics echo party staples like Exploding Kittens, Cards Against Humanity, and Bang! — easy to learn, high in player interaction, and packed with surprises and satire.

—description from the designer

Hon na čarodějnice je nekorektní satirická karetní hra z dílny Bardmistr Games, která vás vtáhne do temného středověku plného intrik, podezření a černého humoru

Převezmete roli inkvizitora, budujete městskou radu, získáváte politický vliv, a pokud vám někdo stojí v cestě – odešlete ho na hranici jako „čarodějnici“

V každém kole hráči mohou provést dvě akce: líznout kartu, vyložit občana, obvinit nebo očistit, případně zahrát speciální akční kartu. Během hry je důležitá strategie i kreativa, hráči často sami vymýšlejí důvody, proč svého soupeře poslat do plamenů.

Na hru má vliv estetikou i mechanikou kombinace her jako Exploding Kittens, Cards Against Humanity nebo Bang! – jednoduchá pravidla, silný humor, interaktivita a neustálé překvapení.

—description from the designer (Czech)

Bitcoin Trivia is a compact, educational quiz game you can print at home. It combines fun and learning, covering the basics, technical concepts, and culture of Bitcoin in a simple and engaging way.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Byzantion is a deeply thematic, highly detailed competitive miniature board game set in the shadowed halls of imperial power.

In Byzantion, you assume the role of one of the four great Demoi, powerful factions that influenced the fate of the Eastern Roman Empire during the reign of Autokrator Justinian. Across nine rounds, players face a series of nine randomized historical events (ensuring replayability), revealed at the start of the game, allowing for long-term strategic planning and political maneuvering.

At the beginning of each round, factions vie for influence over the Emperor’s decisions. Through a bidding system, players attempt to sway Justinian toward one of two possible responses to each crisis. Each decision reshapes the course of the Empire and history itself. Once a path is chosen, all factions must contribute to its execution. Success rewards the most committed faction; failure punishes the least. Your ability to anticipate, adapt, and manipulate will offer your Victory points and determine your legacy.  To meet the challenges ahead, you will recruit renowned personalities and specialists of the time from all kinds of trades. “Build your engine“ and secure the required resources to meet the challenges that history will unveil.

But this is the Byzantine court, where daggers are as sharp in the shadows as on the battlefield.

    Use diplomacy to align rival interests with your own.
    Employ espionage to outmaneuver your enemies with your intrigue cards.
    Strike alliances, break them, and strike again, at just the right moment.


At the game’s end, unveil the secret objectives you’ve fulfilled, adding their value to your accumulated Doxa (Victory Points). Only then will it be revealed which faction truly ruled from behind the veil, the unseen hand that shaped the Emperor’s will and steered the fate of Byzantion.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Generation Ship: From Dust to Thrust
description:
You will each be in charge of expanding your spaceship by constructing different Sector cards and building several Tech and Expedition cards on top. Generate and spend Supplies effectively to optimize your sectors and bid on exciting expeditions to obtain the most stars and win.

Sectors are your starting point. Each sector can have up to 5 cards on top and has a special effect that is activated once the matching cards are placed on top.

Tech cards and expeditions are placed on your sectors, there are 6 different card and supply colors. Matching the right color on your sector optimizes them, granting you powerful bonus effects.

After each generation you gain 1 supply per card you placed on your ship. For example if you have 2 blue liquid cards you generate 2 liquids.

Stars are gained from tech cards, from storing supplies on your sectors and through facedown and faceup tucked cards.

The most stars however are gained from expeditions, these star multipliers can't just be bought, instead players start a bid. The highest bidder receives the expedition.

Whoever has the most stars after 4 generations wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Build ecotourism lodges to keep your nature reserve thriving New card placement mechanics add strategic depth, new tactics and crucial bonuses! With beautiful, tactile, screen-printed wooden lodges elevating the table presence of Kavango.

The Kavango Lodges Expansion adds a wonderful puzzly dimension to the game, with card placement mechanics now vital to building and growing your nature reserve. This gives the game added strategic depth and complexity, and allows for a more diverse approach to gaining the most points to be crowned the conservation expert!

The Lodges Expansion tells more of the human story of conservation. You must now bring in ecotourism as a way to sustainably finance your conservation work, just like the incredibly successful ecotourism industry in real life in Southern Africa.

But you also have to consider the communities around your nature reserve. For conservation efforts to be successful and sustainable, it is vital that people living alongside wildlife benefit from it. In order to keep expanding your nature reserve with new species, you must invest in community protection to keep people, their land, and their businesses safe from conflict with wildlife.

—description from the publihser

boardgame: Hunt A Killer: Game Night Gone Wrong
description:
Connor’s murder mystery birthday party goes wrong when the game ends abruptly, his roommate is dead, and he must decide which of his friends is the killer.

Be the detective and help Connor prove his innocence.

Put your detective skills to the test with Game Night Gone Wrong, a gripping true crime board game from Hunt A Killer. A party turns deadly—and it’s up to you to figure out who did it, how, and why. Examine realistic evidence, analyze witness statements, and uncover hidden motives in this mystery game designed for solo or group play.

Difficulty 3/5

—description from the publisher

Players play as Puru Puru and her friends, who are all striving to live a puru-fect life full of Love and Excitement so they can enter PARADICE. But beware, bouncy jellies don’t last long on the table!

The game is played in turns starting from the starting player clockwise. Each player’s turn consists of rolling the dice and resolving the result of the dice. The game ends immediately when either one player obtains 20 Love or Excitement, or one player drips to 0!

The key mechanic in this game is if you roll too much of one side, your neighbours get that result instead! That applies to both good - and bad sides.

Don't forget to try the advanced game where each jelly has their own unique game-changing power!

—description from the publisher

26.2 is a thematic, strategic racing game based on training for and running a marathon. The game is played in 2 phases, the Training Phase and The Race. During Training players draft cards to complete an individual 12 week 'Training Plan', which gives the players a boost to Stamina, Speed, Strength, Hydration, and Energy attributes on a Fitness Tracker, whilst also potentially adding to a negative Fatigue attribute. Training cards can combine for added bonuses/penalties, or may be used to resolve and remove Injury cards.

Once Training is finished and players have completed their Fitness Tracker, they move to The Race. During the Race players use a shared 'Pace Deck' to move around the track. From a starting hand of 5 Pace cards, they simultaneously choose 3 to play, moving the total value of their cards on their turn, refilling their hand to 5 cards at turn end. Movement can be modified by utilising the attributes built up during Training. For instance, 1 Stamina allows the play of an additional card, 1 Speed adds one to movement total, and 1 Strength negates one negative pace on hills or into headwinds.

The Race board is split into 'Zones' 1-6. The total value of 'Pace' cards played begins to add Fatigue to a players Fitness Tracker, beginning in Zone 2, with higher Pace totals adding increasing amounts of Fatigue in later Zones. Each Fatigue reduces the total Pace value by 1. In order to combat Fatigue, players must use Energy to either reduce the Fatigue level on their tracker by 1 or increase Speed on their tracker by 1. Energy is gained in Training or can be picked up at 'Aid Stations' along the race route. Hydration can refill the Stamina or Strength attributes in the same way as Energy.

During the Race, players will also collect and play 'Event' cards that can grant rewards or penalties that will impact the race. Each player has one Starting Event which is resolved prior to their first turn, and Zone Events which must be resolved whilst in a specified Zone of the race route.

Managing your attributes and pace is key to victory. Do you have the endurance needed to cross the finish line in triumph?

—description from the designer

El Restaurante is a fast-paced and highly energetic board game that captures the frantic rhythm of life in a bustling restaurant. Players step into the shoes of hard-working waiters whose main objective is to serve all the tables as quickly and efficiently as possible before their rivals do.

The game features simultaneous actions that keep everyone on edge from start to finish. Each player must try to get rid of their 15 work-pile cards by completing tasks and fulfilling orders, all while balancing another key element: in their other hand, they hold a tray containing 10 extra cards that can be used as valuable resources or strategic advantages at just the right moment.

This dynamic combination of speed, multitasking, and tactical decision-making ensures that no two games of The Restaurant are ever the same. It’s the perfect challenge for those who enjoy competitive gameplay, rapid-fire thinking, and the thrilling chaos of the restaurant world!

—description from the designer

Stronger Point Junior Expansion adds 100 new category cards with 500 questions aimed specifically at 8-18 year old players. Categories include popular culture subjects such as Minecraft, Taylor Swift, and Youtubers, as well as a variety of topics covered in the school curriculum.

This Junior Expansion also includes an added 'push your luck' dice where twice per game players can roll to double, keep or lose the points they've earned from answering a category. The dice can also be used with the base game.

Stronger Point Junior gives young gamers a chance to compete with adults on an equal footing, and show the grown-ups who the trivia experts really are!

—description from the publisher

18DDR is an 18xx games about the reconstruction of the former GDR after 1945. This involved not only rebuilding industry but also repairing the railway network, both of which had suffered severely from the effects of the war. The GDR's economic system was a planned economy, not a market economy. Therefore, reconstruction was based on planned targets that had to be met. 18DDR was developed against this background, although the historical circumstances are not reflected exactly for game mechanics reasons.

In 18DDR, players take on the role of district secretaries. Their task is to fulfill the planned objectives for their district. These objectives consist of a combination of reconstruction performance and transport performance. Reconstruction performance consists of issuing reconstruction orders. The use of the repaired rail network is crucial for transport performance. The more reconstruction orders issued for a district and the better the rail connections to other cities, the easier it is for the district to fulfill its plan.

18DDR is unique 18xx game, as there is no money involved. Instead, the player use action cubes for acquiring reconstruction orders during the planning rounds that replace the stock rounds. During the operating rounds, the players build lay or upgrade track tiles and try to replenish their stock of action cubes for the following planning round. The skilful handling of the action cubes is the key to victory. Once a district secretary has fulfilled the district’s objective, the player receives the award ‘Working Hero’. The game ends immediately when one player has received a number of these awards, depending on the number of players.

—description from the publisher

MARKET FORTUNE®

In Market Fortune, you're building a portfolio of investments while disrupting your opponents' plans through a shared strategy deck. The first player to form 4 investments worth 25M or more wins.

How It Works
Each player starts with 8 Construction Cards — 4 Stocks ($) valued 1M–5M across 12 industries, and 4 Returns (R) valued +1M to +5M. On your turn, you perform exactly one action: Build (draw and swap a construction card to improve your hand), Risk (pair a Stock + Return of the same industry logo, roll the Fortune Die to lock in the investment), or Dominate (draw a Strategy card to attack, defend, or modify the game state).

The Fortune Die has 4 positive faces and 2 negative. Roll positive and your investment is placed face-down — hidden from all opponents until someone declares victory. Roll negative and both cards return to your hand. A successful investment also triggers Momentum: draw one Strategy card immediately.

The Strategy Deck
60 cards in 4 colors, each with different activation timing. Red cards (instant) play on your turn — steal investments with Shrewd Move, wipe the board with Market Crash, or bypass the die entirely with Fortune. Blue cards (reactive) fire when you're attacked — Diamond Hands blocks multiple threats, Wrong Analyst reflects the attack back. Green cards (modifier) alter investment values at key moments. Yellow cards (permanent) are revealed at game end for surprise scoring — Diversification King adds 8M if your 4 investments span 4 different industries, Booming Industry doubles (or triples) same-logo investments.

The interplay between attack and defense creates constant tension. You can see how many investments opponents have, but never their values. A player with 3 small investments might look dominant while sitting at only 12M — well short of the 25M threshold.

What Keeps It Interesting
The 8-card hand limit is always active. As you lock investments face-down on the table, your hand shrinks, making you more vulnerable. A player with 3 investments has only 2 cards in hand. One Market Crash and they lose everything — unless they've been holding a blue defense card, burning a precious hand slot for exactly this moment.

Catch-up mechanics are built into the card design. Lucky Investor only works if you're in last place. Federal Audit lets trailing players peek at a leader's strategy hand. Several attack cards specifically target players with more investments. Leaders are never safe, but the trailing player always has options.

Portfolio Optimization lets you swap cards between your hand and face-down investments at any time without spending a turn — including right before declaring victory. This creates a layer of hidden planning that rewards careful hand management across the entire game.

Three Game Modes
Fortune Mode (standard): 2–6 players, 20–45 minutes, age 14+. Full strategy deck, 25M victory threshold.
Championship Mode: Multiple consecutive games. First to 100M across sessions wins the championship.
Family Mode: 15–30 minutes. First to form 4 investments wins — no value counting. Green/Yellow cards that don't apply give an extra turn instead.

Components: 180 unique cards (60 Stock, 60 Return, 60 Strategy), 1 Fortune Die, rulebook. No board required — fully table-flexible.

Designer's Note
My grandmother taught me to play cards. My grandfather taught me to invest. Market Fortune combines both: a game of reading opponents, managing risk, and making decisions under pressure — where the cards you hold matter less than how consciously you play them.

— David May Anaya

House Mouse is a quick and chaotic card game in which each player is a mouse trying to build a magnificent nest. They do this by scavenging bits and bobs that the owner of the house, their host, has left laying about.

Throughout the game players will collect cards into their nest, either directly from the house or from other player's nests. These cards can be Points (positive or negative!), Powers (like trading, peeking, revealing, giving, and stealing cards!), or Boosts (bad for others, good for you, or both!).

The game ends immediately when any player's nest holds 12 cards. The player that builds the nest holding the most points is declared the winner.

—description from the publisher

Holiday's over! As you stepped on your home country's soil, you have been selected along with several other passenger for a random security check!

Customs Mayhem is an 18-cards bluffing and deduction game fit for 3-6 players, where each player holds a hidden Role Cards that determines their winning condition aside from being the last man left standing.

Players start with 2 Baggage cards containing the quantity of restricted items they bring along, and the First player gets to decide which game mode they going to play in this round; Inquire or Declare.

In Inquire, the first player choose another player to be inquired, where that player must tell two-truths-and-one-lie that the first player must deduce. In Declare, each player put 1 Baggage card to the center face-down and guess the minimum of total quantity of single item. The next player must increase the declaration by 1-2 increments until someone decides to accuse the last player's statement and reveal all cards in the center.

The turn ends when someone gets caught lying and be Detained. The rest of the player gets to play and decides the next turn game mode until someone manages to fulfill the winning conditions of their Role cards, where the game may ends with more than one player winning the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: FreeLancers: Wanderers of the Lordless Land
description:
Made to emulate classic Fantasy Beat'em-up games like Shadows over Mystara and Golden Axe, FreeLancers: Wanderers of Lordless Land is the mini entry of work-in-progress boardgame to be completed in three expansions, with the first available to be download-ed for testing.

In FreeLancers: Wanderers of Lordless Land, you can choose to be the hardy Landsknecht or agile Picaro as your character in traversing into Savage Forest, while encountering a poor woodcutter running for his life being chased by the denizen of the forest.

The game are to be played in 7 Waves, where aside from wave 1,6, and 7, the events on that Waves are randomized. Monsters that you defeat also drops random loot that you can gain by rolling a d6 and assigning which item you get from the dice roll.

When you reach the final wave, you get to fight against the Ruler of Savage Forest, the Harpy Lady. She has 6 different attack pattern that is randomized by a die roll. Once you managed to overcome her, you gain one of her two skills that you can use for your next adventure in the next expansion; "Savior of Lordless Land"

—description from the designer

boardgame: Universus: Critical Role – Heroes of Exandria
description:
Prepare to enter a world of magic, monsters, and madcap mayhem! Critical Role is a beloved web series featuring an all-star cast of voice actors playing through a tabletop adventure in the land of Exandria. Our Challenger Series, Starter Decks, and Heroes of Exandria booster set feature beloved characters and legendary items from the adventures of Vox Machina and the Mighty Nein!

Featuring 20 highly requested characters, this set is your ticket to adventuring in the world of Exandria.

Get ready to dive into an adventure of epic proportions with characters you've been eagerly waiting for! This booster set also includes 8 Chrome Rare Characters featuring one gold stamp signature from each member of the Critical Role cast!

Critical Role is a popular web series featuring award-winning voice actors playing roleplaying games. For the first time, each booster display contains 1 Box Topper Oversized Boss Card. There are 6 boss options, each with their own ruleset for a cooperative PvE experience for you to try!

Each 11-card booster pack includes one Character card, a selection of Attacks and Foundations, and maybe even a special surprise or two—pulled from our favorite adventurers' journeys across Exandria. What treasures will you uncover?

—description from the designer
An expansion for the base game containing several new recipes, including the titular Egg recipe.

Premonition is a chaotic capture the flag style game.

3-5 groups of 3 people find themselves on a strange island-mountain. Their goal is to get home, using one of the portals on the mountain. The issue is, you need a key to use it (an hourglass), which is found at the top of the mountain. And each hourglass only sends 1 person home. Who knows how many people will make it home before the portals stop working, or there are no more hourglasses. Race, fight, and hinder the other groups to ensure you all go home.

With over 60 unique action cards, a rotating map, shifting portals, and plenty of gotcha opportunities, Premonition seeks to take a simple goal and make the path to it pure chaos.

—description from the designer

What do "sun" and "moon" have in common? Maybe the word "sky"? Or perhaps "circle"?

In the cooperative party game 5 копеек ("5 Kopecks"), players must quickly fill the gaps in a circle of words to win. The game includes sixty numbered round cards ("coins") that can be written on. At the start, five silver coin cards (numbered 12, 24, 36, 48, and 60/0) are placed in a circle, each labeled with a starting word chosen by the players. Twelve of the remaining gold coin cards are placed face down in the center—these must be correctly positioned in the circle as the game progresses.

On your turn, secretly check the number on the top gold coin card, then come up with a word that hints at its correct placement among the existing cards. You’ll need to think fast—the other players have only a short time to agree on where the new card fits based on your clue. As more cards are placed, the gaps between numbers shrink, making it harder to position new cards accurately.

The team wins if they correctly place the last card before losing all three of their starting error markers.

5 Kopecks also includes a competitive team variant, as well as alternative co-op modes: a "first error" version (using all gold cards but ending after any mistake) or a longer game (using 18 gold cards).
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Hyrräpeli is a spinner game for children to learn to recognize in which category a certain item belongs. Categories are crockeries, vehicles, buildings, animals, plants, tools, clothes and furniture.

At start of the game every player gets six cards. Rest of the cards are left as a draw deck. On your turn you spin the spinner. The side shows the category, where you are suppose to add card. If you don't have the card, you draw cards from the draw deck until you find one. You add the drawn cards to your hand. Then it is next players turn to spin. Winner is the player who first gets out of the cards.

Nestori is a roll and move game in the world of cross country skiing. Game has been published to commemorate FIS Nordic World Ski Championships 1989 in Lahti.

On your turn you roll the die and move forward. If you land on a red numbered circle, then something happens (loose a turn, go back or forward some spaces and so on). Then it's the next players turn.  Winner is the first player to Finnish the ski-race.

A card game adaptation of the 1996 Japan-only arcade hit, Densha de Go!

Draw cards to drive the train from station to station and stop it exactly! Watch out for accidents and make sure to obey the signals. Use all sorts of tricks to slow down your opponents and reach the station before anyone else!

Become a train god! 「DENSHA DE GO!」

boardgame: A Tatons !: Le premier jeu de course à toucher
description:
Lead a thrilling tactile race between goblins or work together to escape from the big Ogre, while groping in a bag to get ahead on the board!

Theme and atmosphere
Immerse yourself in the magical forest, where once a year the elves gather for a thrilling race. This year, however, a new guest has arrived: the great Ogre! With two game modes to choose from, you can either work together to escape the Ogre, or go head-to-head in a frantic race between elves!

Game mechanics and basic rules
The principle of the game is simple: place the tactile tokens in a bag, then each player in turn picks a token by groping for the duration of an hourglass. The aim is to find the right tokens for the square in front of you to move forward on the modular board. Choose between a competitive or cooperative race against the Ogre!

Features

     Two game modes: cooperative or competitive
     A tactile game to stimulate children's senses and dexterity
     Modular game equipment for ever-varying games


Immerse yourself in the magical forest and lead your team of elves to victory, taking on the Ogre or your friends!

Primary Potions is a colorful light strategy open information card game based around the color wheel. Mix components and use potions to combat your friends to become the Prime Master Alchemist.

All the decks are set out in the center for everyone to reach. Alchemists also start with 3 different component cards (Red Yellow Blue) these are the basis of every potion and can be put to great effect if properly used.

Gameplay is fast and easy with only 1 action each and only 2 options, Make a potion or Use a potion. This reduces player turn time keeping the game going and makes sure every player gets their action out quickly. 
Mixing a potion: Alchemists mix potions by using the idea of the color wheel and mixing colors of paint. Placing component cards from their hands to the center pot in exchange for up to 7 different potions.

Using a Potion: Alchemists use potions by placing them back into the center pot and completing the effect of that potion. These effects may help themselves, hinder other Alchemists or a combination of both.

Each potion effect is explained on the card itself to reduce rulebook reading and to keep the game running smoothly. 
Although the game is set up for 6 players, Primary Potions can easily be expanded upon itself to accommodate more people just by adding another deck.

—description from the publisher

Hold’em High: House Rules is a light-weight, modern twist on Texas Hold’em. The player with the most money after 6 rounds wins! Each player starts the game with 6 poker chips in their color. Players take turns to either Pass, Lock, Bet or Cheat. There are 6 cheats, each are worker placement spaces (Swap, Shoot, Buy, Peek, Re-Draw, Double-Down). Lock a card by placing a chip on it, so it’s safe from cheats. Pass to add a card to the card row. If you pass with a full card row, then trigger the “Showdown” instead. Passing doesn’t use workers. Whoever wins the showdown (comparing poker hands) wins as many dollars (victory points) as they have unused poker chips. To start the next round, recollect your 6 poker chips and deal new poker cards!

The biggest selling points are that it simulates cheating at a poker table, no one is ever “out” of a round, you can always “redraw” so you’re never stuck with a bad hand, and there’s tension between using workers or scoring more points. Even if you lose the pot, you can earn money by betting on the winner. What’s more, you have a hidden card and face cards are marked!

—description from the designer

Void Formula is a math game where player is gonna do + / - or exchange the formula number with other player to prove the existence of 0 [Void].

If at the start of the game there is no player that can prove the existence of 0 by combining 4 number formula, the game will run up to 3 round.

on each round player can :


     draw 1 formula card from deck
     [optional] exchange 1 formula card with other player, the player can only ask for higher or lowest, without asking the number.
     discard 1 card from hand to end your turn. If the card you discard have effect, activate the effect before ending your turn.


Each round end when all player have discard 1 card. if on this phase player can prove the existence of 0 he/she must open their hand and win the game.

If until round 3 no player can prove the existence of 0, the player that have the closest number to 0 win the game.

—description from the designer

Gacha game where you as a treasure hunter compete to search treasure in a WELL and satisfied the most Client.

this game will run up to 5 round, on each round there is 2 phase that player must do

1. Ticketing
on this phase player use 1 of 5 of their ticket to determine their draw group
group 1 : draw 7 bucket
group 2 : draw 5 bucket
group 3 : draw 4 bucket

2. action
on this phase starting from player group 1 until group 3 they have few action option that they can do :

     Draw
     Report to client
     Sabotage
     Exchange


after all player do this 2 phase the game will continue up to 5 round

on the end game, point of few item can be change based on Client final Request, so becarefull when you choose client to report.

player that have the most total point
Client Point + Precious Point - Trash item
will win the game.

—description from the designer

Brand new 2 player betting game, the game is about how you manipulate river to gain higher element point and to burst your foe element

The game will run up to 2 round that per round consist of several phase:

1. Betting phase
on this phase player will have option to
-Check
-Raise
-Call
-Fold

2. Pooling phase
on this phase player will have choose 1 card from the river to their hand and put 1 card face down on player pool

3. Showdown
player now must show what 3 element they use

4. Pool open
now each player will have to open their pool
total point from POOL + RIVER will decide the player element point.

5.Scoring
based on Pool + River player must count how much point they get based on the element they use.

the game will continue until one of the player have less then 5 bet chip in their possession.

—description from the designer
The objective of this game is to build up the highest tower by using Disney Characters. You have to roll the dice and add the figures from the selected color.

Witch Plentifulla’s tower is filled with treasure. Beat the other mages to the top of the tower and claim the fortune. But beware…the path is full of traps and surprises! So make haste, ye treasure seeker!

Players needs to move around the board by rolling dice, drawing consequence cards and collecting ingredient tokens to advance. The first player to reach the treasure at the top of the tower wins

In this game, players would act to make teammates guess the word they get. Players determine how to act out words by rolling a six-sided die, where each face dictates the method—Action, including Speaking, Lip Reading, Picture Drawing, Quick-Answer, or Bonus (choose one form they like). Players get scores if their teammate guess the word out correctly and the team who achieves the score target wins.
The game is designed for 2+ players, and players could cooperate or be divided into 2–4 teams.
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
In Word Arsenal players tries to fill certain amount of words into 5*5-grid. There are four 1000 word lists, for players to fit in the grid. Languages are Finnish, Swedish, English and German.

One player in a round is chosen as a captain. Captain spins the pointer to randomize 5 things (used language, starting word coordinates, used grid and starting word position on the grid). In the first round everybody tries to fit second word to the grid as fast as possible. When three players are done the round ends. Words are checked and fastest players get points (3, 2, and 1). On the second round players must fit three words in the grid and the points are 6, 4 and 2. In the last round four words must be fitted and only the fastest player gets 12 points. If nobody can't fit four words in 30 minutes, then the game is over. Anyway the player with most points win the game.
This is a compilation of two Estonian roll and move games from the 1930's Kelgu-mäng and Suusaretk.

In Happy Stupid Werewolf, players have two ways to play. Firstly, players would act to make teammates guess the word they get. Players determine how to act out words by rolling a six-sided die. Players get scores if their teammate guess the word out correctly and the team who achieves the score target wins.
Another is that players write down a word meeting the condition card, and then players say a word meeting the condition as well in turn. The first player who says a word the same as one written down loses the round. Players also have abilities like werewolf games to help themselves.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description: This is compilation of four fairy tale themed roll and move games: Hansel & Gretel, Little Red Riding Hood, Snow White and Three Little Pigs.

Shoot all your monkeys in the tree and collect point depending on which tree level you manage to shoot them.

Jungle Expedition: Shooting Monkey Game is a dexterity game for 2-3 players. The goal is to shoot your monkeys into the tree with a trampoline.
