Age of Academics is an original domestic German-style board game that combines multiple mechanisms with historical and cultural themes. Here is a detailed introduction:
 
     Number of Players and Duration: It supports 1-4 players, with 3 being recommended. A single game takes about 40 minutes per player, and its complexity is estimated at 3.3/5, making it a medium to heavy strategy board game suitable for advanced players.



     Game Mechanisms: It incorporates mechanisms such as auction, DBG (Deck-Building Game), worker placement, and area control. There are 8 schools of thought from the Warring States period available for players to choose from (with the Miscellaneous School being controlled by AI). Each school has unique skills and initial settings. For example, the Taoist school follows the concept of "governing by non-interference" and gains passive benefits; the Mohist school adopts the "Juzi System" where collective actions are more effective. Players can use a combination of characteristic DBG deck building and worker placement, utilizing card effects like drawing and discarding cards, along with workers' actions to execute strategies.



     Game Flow: The game consists of 4 rounds, each composed of 4 phases. In the "Contention Phase", players assign disciples to debate and compete for topics, with winners gaining benefits. In the "Spring Phase", players can play celebrity cards to obtain resources or discard cards to perform school-specific actions. In the "Autumn Phase", players settle rewards from autumn celebrities to verify the effectiveness of their strategies. In the "Post-Autumn Phase", players collect resources, reorganize their hands, and advance the "Zhou De Indicator".



     Action Planning: The game features a "Spring Phase" and an "Autumn Phase", and celebrity cards are also divided into "Spring Celebrities" and "Autumn Celebrities". In the Spring Phase, players can obtain resources, deploy disciples through "Spring Celebrity Cards", and even discard celebrities to gain opportunities for school actions. In the Autumn Phase, they use "Autumn Celebrity Cards" to settle rewards and trigger long-term effects.

It’s the twisted word game that’s hours of nuf!

Grab some friends and put your brain in reverse as you try and guess the words to win the game. Problem is, the words are… backwards.

Backwards is the challenging word game with an added twist. Players race against the clock to correctly and quickly pronounce words from backwards to forwards. The more you answer, the closer you get to the finish.

But nothing’s ever straightforward in Backwords! If you answer a “Starword” you get a chance at a bonus turn, which you can either use to race ahead or strategically pull another player of your choice Backwards.

Take his snacks before he attacks.

A raccoon is living in a dumpster guarding his food. Roll the dice to see how much of his food you need to take. Carefully dig through the dumpster but watch out. He might jump out and grab you.

The person who collects the most snacks before the raccoon springs forward wins.

Monsnatch is a trading sticker game — a brand new genre that blends the excitement of collectible stickers with the strategic depth of a tabletop game. Players collect and battle using beautifully illustrated monster stickers, known as "Momon," each with their own elemental attributes and battle power. The game is fast-paced, easy to learn, and surprisingly deep, as you try to "snatch" control of the Momon from your opponent.

What sets Monsnatch apart is its instant playability — just one booster pack is all you need to start a game with a friend. Tear open a pack and place them directly onto the 4×4 arena grid. It's a hands-on, interactive experience that feels as fun as it is strategic. And because each sticker is collectible, every booster you open expands your collection and unlocks new tactical possibilities. Whether you're a casual player, a dedicated collector, or a tactical mastermind, Monsnatch offers a refreshing new way to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity Twists: Shit List
description:
Shit List is a whole new way to play Cards Against Humanity where, at long last, you get to write your own horrible answers!

Each round, you roll a die for a starting letter, and then everyone secretly writes an answer to a black card. Your mission: write the same answer as exactly one of your friends. Succeed and your word gets added to...THE SHIT LIST!

—description from the back of box

The basic units in Napoleonic Command are the infantry battalion, the cavalry squadron and the artillery battery. In the standard game, these are typically represented by four, two and two bases, respectively, although there is considerable flexibility. The actual miniatures on the bases are largely irrelevant to the game, as is the exact size of the bases, as long as the units of all forces are based similarly. The game automatically scales itself to whatever basing is being used, since all measurements for movement distances and gunnery ranges are in "spans", one "span" being the frontage of one infantry battalion rounded off to the nearest inch (or cm) for convenience. The player is free to use miniatures based for other systems, or to base his miniatures in whatever way is most attractive, convenient, or economical. Guidelines are given for keeping the various types of units' frontages in proper proportion to one another.

Even though individual battalions and squadrons (and their typical formations) are represented on the table, combat is resolved at the brigade level. The mechanic involves the comparison of a brigade's Cohesion (derived from its size and quality) with the opposing units' Threat (derived from the number of opposing stands, the weapon types and ranges, and the relative position of the enemy). Threat totals which exceed a formation's Cohesion may cause the loss of "tactical initiative", which imposes restrictions on their actions in subsequent turns, or force them to retreat or lose stands from their component units. Smaller Threat totals will not affect a target brigade's actions, but will still impose levels of "Disorder", the accumulation of which will gradually erode a brigade's Cohesion, even if it loses no stands. Accumulated Disorder levels can only be removed by pulling brigades out of combat and allowing them time to recover.

The command structure of the Napoleonic army is fully represented, and leader figures are important features on the table. Their presence with a unit may mitigate the effects of opposing Threat, and the distance between leaders on the table will affect the time delay of communications both up and down the chain of command.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Rifle Wars is a rule system designed to help recreate the large battles of the American Civil War on the tabletop, using miniatures to represent the units. It is not a complete, ready-to-play package with army lists and scenarios; it is a grand-tactical level combat resolution system for those gamers who enjoy researching orders of battle and historical engagements, and then staging recreations or fictional engagements based on historical situations.

Though it plays in approximately real time=game time, it is not the style of game usually termed "fast play". It does not include, nor is it amenable to army lists or point systems, so it will not appeal to the competition gamer. Though the mechanics are simple enough for a beginner to grasp easily, it is not a particularly good introductory set of rules because it produces its best results in the hands of players who already have some understanding of the historical background on which it is based.

—description from the designer
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Detectives Vs Criminals: Sherlock Holmes – High Tension
description:
Europe trembles. A wave of attacks threatens to drag the continent into war. Only one mind can stop the chaos but will it be enough to outsmart the mastermind behind it all?

Moriarty is back. His goal: ignite conflict by planting bombs across Europe and pushing nations into High Tension. Only Sherlock Holmes and his allies can stop him.

MORIARTY – MASTER OF CHAOS
You play alone. But you’re always three steps ahead.
Lay bombs in key cities, raise the Tension Level, and escape before they catch your scent. Each explosion pushes Europe closer to the brink.

HOLMES, WATSON & IRENE
Team up with a friend. Split roles between Sherlock Holmes, Dr. Watson or Irene Adler. Investigate. Defuse. Predict Moriarty’s next move and… capture him before he plunges Europe into war.

EXPLOSIVE NEW RULES

     Unique bomb mechanics: Moriarty hides a trap, then sets off a chain reaction
     Tension areas: whole regions become unstable and block reinforcements
     Custom Event Cards, Special Actions, and the all-new High Tension Board


Moriarty wins if 5 Areas fall into High Tension.
Detectives win if they manage to capture him.

FULLY COMPATIBLE
Use the new characters also in the base game.

Will you light the fuse or stop the spark before it spreads?

—description from the designer

Rumor has it is a conspiracy themed card game born from the need to uncover concealed truths. There are many secrets in the world, some are hiding in plain sight, others thrive in the shadows.

With Rumor has it, you control the narrative.

Using one card from each of the 4 categories, create weird and wondrous conspiracies to shock and confound your friends.

How to play

     Pick the Judge
     Shuffle cards, randomly select 4 (one per category)
     All other players create a conspiracy with these cards, then present to The Judge in turn
     The Judge selects a winner; the winner becomes the next round’s Judge
     First one to become The Judge three times wins


—description from the publisher

Kölsch brings a unique topfermented beer specialty from the Cologne region into the game – brewed locally, yet known far beyond national borders. With this expansion, you can add Kölsch to your product range and explore new strategic options by cleverly expanding your brewery to outpace the competition.

—description from the publisher

Buckle up for the next instalment in the Joyride series, Joyride: Full Throttle!

Joyride: Full Throttle is a high-octane stand-alone racing game in the Joyride range. Keeping the same core gameplay while expanding the player count to 6 players! Race through 20 tracks across 2 new maps – the sun-soaked Beach and the neon-drenched Abandoned Mall, and harness powerful new items and dangerous obstacles. Joyride: Full Throttle is for learner drivers and seasoned racers.

Expanded for Joyride: Full Throttle is the Team Mode facilitating 2v2 or 2v2v2 racing. Players work together to speed their Super Fan around the track as fast as possible, swapping the Fan between their cars mid-race. New in-game Portals can launch you through glowing gateways to leap around the track, pull off daring shortcuts, and even chain multiple maps together to create custom circuits.

Full Throttle is fully compatible with previous Joyride games allowing players to mix and match cars, items, and more from across the series

—description from publisher

Hercules 3000 is a solo deckbuilding game where you take on the role of Hercules, a distant descendant of his ancient namesake, as he confronts new incarnations of some of his ancestor's greatest challenges.

Defeat all 9 enemies before you hit points get reduced to zero to win the game!

At the start of the game, you construct your deck with several starting cards as well as some cards that are specific to your chosen role. After that, you will get to choose from a few different starting game bonuses.

Then reveal the first enemy and start playing.

Each round, you will reveal the enemy's action and draw cards into your hand from your deck.  Use your energy to play cards that deal damage to your enemy and increase your defense, as well as a variety of other effects. Then the enemy attacks and does damage to you.  If your hit points are reduced to 0, you lose!

After defeating an enemy, you will get to improve your deck or rest. After defeating an elite enemy, you may also choose to gain a permanent power.

Each enemy has drastically different abilities and there are two versions of each enemy. You'll know what the enemies are, but not the order in which you'll encounter them, nor which version of each you'll encounter.  So build your deck to be versatile!

—description from the designer

Four gangs of rodents are trying to collect as many selected pieces as possible. However, mice hunt for cheese, and the cat does not mind eating a delicacy.

Therefore, players will have to concentrate and make full use of their intelligence to correctly calculate the combination of cards, mislead opponents, and not run into a cat! "Kotobanda" is a fast card game for collecting sets (card combinations) for 2 - 4 players. Collect your mouse gang from the cards on your hands on any basis (the nationality of the mouse, its signs or objects) to snatch a piece of cheese from a cat or other gang! Collect cards faster, but don't see that mice are so cute - together they become a real gang, or rather a "gang" that can resist a cat! The winner is the one who collects the most pieces of cheese.

So who will take the cheese in the end, and who will" stay with the nose "?

boardgame: Thunderball! Miniature rules for underwater combat
description:
THUNDERBALL! Miniature rules for underwater combat
Exciting 3-D underwater battles with spear guns, knives, and sharks. 
Includes rules for three different levels of play.

The Basic Rules introduce new players to the game for quick and easy mayhem. 
The Advanced Rules bring in many tactical options with a wide range of troop qualities, armored scuba suits, sea sleds, and more; plus dangerous sea creatures including many species of sharks, as well as octopi, eels, rays, sea snakes, jellyfish, trained war dolphins, and more! 
The Expert Rules add realistic movement options for using weight belts and buoyancy vests.

The Thunderball! Quick Rules contain all the basics you need to start playing. Prepare for exciting 3-D underwater battles with spear guns, knives, and sharks. These rules are available for free on the publisher website.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Saucer Squadron: A Tabletop Tactical Wargame
description:
In Saucer Squadron, the sky isn’t the limit – it’s just the first step.

Saucer Squadron is set in the universe or 1950s government conspiracy, and lets you take control of flying saucers, duking it out for dominion over Earth between the U.S., the Soviet Union, the Sternreich, the Reticulans, and many others.

The game comes as a 56-page, full-color book with gameboards and counter sheets, but these can also be purchased in sturdier cardboard separately. A PDF of the rules is included as well, along with a starter scenario and 96 additional pages of saucer record sheets. These can also be purchased printed separately, for those who prefer to have their saucer records in their hand while playing.

You can trick out your game with paintable resin miniatures for an even more immersive experience, see the Yellow Hand website.

—description from the publisher

Major Woody is hosting game night with Anita Kock, Jack Schitt, Wilma Dikfit and 16 other dirty named people looking for a good time. It’s your job to give them the wild foursome they deserve. You’ll play cards like, “Red Hugh Jaynus” and “Black Harry Beaver” as you try placing your chips in rows of four on the big board. Play your cards right with strategy, luck, and a little help from Major Woody, and you’ll score some wild foursomes!

—description from the publisher

This party game involves a little bit of luck, a little bit of skill, and a lot of laughs with your friends.

Spice up any party by grabbing your favorite beverage and giving the spinner a go. Whatever it lands on dictates how much you have to drink and/or what mortifying and hilarious task you have to complete. You can stop the spinner at any time…but so can any other player! You gotta think fast, move faster, and be ready to drink up while you crack up.

It’s the perfect icebreaker and easy to play even if you’ve had a few. Which you'll hopefully only have a few, before someone collects three cards and wins!

Hamster Park is a competitive card game for 1 to 4 players. Use 50 unique park features—like roller coaster, museum, or ice cream parlor—to make a different park each game! The objective is to have the most fun park by the end of the game.

Hamster Park is a tile-laying game, where you attach cards together to create an amusement park for hamsters. Each card has unique abilities and effects that may alter your park's three core attributes: money, energy, and fun! By adding more hamsters to your park and stationing them strategically, you can also unlock additional bonuses.

Manage your resources, grow your hamster population, and build the best park you can!

—description from the designer

Fistful of Lead: Reloaded was designed as a quick set of wargame rules for gunfights in the Wild West. 
The rules can be learned within a couple of turns and still keep the favor of the period. The play is fast and furious, with a Hollywood fair to it.
It was this title that the entire system sprang from.  The Original.

Over the past 20+ years, the mechanics have grown and evolved.  The Core Rules really reflect how the system mechanics work as a versatile foundation, with other titles acting as a skin or sleeve, to add aesthetic flair to your genre or period.

Now, the mechanics and play of Reloaded 2nd Edition will align more closely to that of the Core Rules.

If you like the original rules as is, you don't have to buy a thing.  Just keep on playing.

However, if you want to take your western scenarios to the next level, here's what to expect:

     Beautiful full color printing
     Fresh pictures and illustrations
     Covers Wild West weapons, Gatling gun and TNT
     Train car layouts for postal & passenger
     Updated campaign & scenarios
     Updated mechanics and traits
     Perfect binding
     60 pages


In most games, each player can control up to 5-8 gunfighters without much trouble. Games can have up to 8 players without slowing down the game. Experienced wargamers and new players alike will enjoy this game. It's perfect for convention games and just one on one shoot outs.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fistful of Lead: Galactic Heroes – Dark Day of the Destructo Ray
description:
Dark Day of the Destructo Ray is a campaign for Fistful of Lead: Galactic Heroes. 
The campaign is a series of 7 linked scenarios to be played in order. The outcome of each scenario has a direct effect on the next game. The player with the most Renown at the end of the campaign wins, and possesses the dark power of the crown.

Welcome to the world of tomorrow!

Since man first hurled himself into the depths of space, he has been watched. Alien powers both benevolent and malign plot to help or harm mankind as he struggles to find his place among the stars.

One such enemy is self proclaimed Galactic Emperor Xorg-Khan, who has been spying on Earth and knows about the development of something called the Destructo Ray by, the some say mad, professor Argo.

Is your Crew here to help vile Galactic Empire, or possibly save the Universe?

This is not a stand alone game. It is a campaign book. You will need a copy of Galactic Heroes to fully enjoy.

—description from the publisher

Collect points and magic stars by collecting sets of cards, then exchange those for three incredible elemental items! The first player to collect all three items wins!

Play your own actions based on the cards you have, negotiate trades with those you play with, and try to get your sets completed.

In Smart Smack, you want to be the first to solve each challenge card — but you won't know what the challenge is until you flip it over, at which point everyone will race to solve it and smash it.

This box contains six challenge types, along with eight tactile cards that players will try to recognize for extra points!​

boardgame: Sengoku Folio 12: The Battle of Anegawa
description:
The Battle of Anegawa is a Japanese doujin (fan-made) game produced by Ludens Faber and is game 12 of his Sengoku Folio series. The game simulates the battle between Oda/Tokugawa against the Asai/Asakura in 1570.

The game is a typical wargame, with movement and attack values, zones of control, a CRT for combat results, and a turn track.

The game includes a map, rules, historical guide, and mounted counters. The counters need to be cut out.
publishers: Dōjin Club Ludens Faber (同人会ルデンス・ファベル)

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – This is Rogue One Squad Pack
description:
Jyn Erso leads a rag-tag band of freedom fighters against the might of the Empire. Joined by former temple guardians Chirrut Îmwe and Baze Malbus, along with Imperial deserter cargo pilot Bodhi Rook, this unlikely squad is willing to sacrifice everything in the fight against Imperial oppression.

Utilizing their unconventional tactics, this band of rebels featured in Star Wars: Shatterpoint – This is Rogue One Squad Pack can quickly catch their opponents off guard and seize victory against impossible odds.

boardgame: CineLucha: Dragon Lee & Solar vs Cthulhu & The Mafia
description:
Cinelucha: Dragon Lee & Solar vs Cthulhu & The Mafia drops you into an epic lucha libre saga where masked heroes take on tentacled horrors and criminal empires.

This standalone box features two iconic luchador decks, two villain decks, and two unique cinematic scenarios — everything players need for thrilling co-op battles. Tactical card play meets story-driven chaos.

Dragon Lee, a global wrestling star, joins Solar in a card-driven, narrative-rich game system built for customization and high replayability. The game’s modular design allows content mixing with other Cinelucha boxes, creating infinite brawling combinations. Take down cosmic cults or disrupt mafia plots—all through the over-the-top action of luchador justice.

Le Jeu Pays du Sundgau is an original, local and user-friendly board game based on the principle of questions and answers.
500 written questions and photos with also charades and riddles, "junior" questions which deal with subjects as varied as history or geography, heritage, local traditions, current events or famous people who have marked the Sundgau.

boardgame: Le jeu de la Vallée de la Dordogne Corrézienne
description:
Le Jeu de la Vallée de la Dordogne Corrézienne is an original, local and user-friendly board game based on the principle of questions and answers.
Nearly 500 written questions and photos with also charades and riddles, "junior" questions which deal with subjects as varied as history or geography, heritage, local traditions, current events or famous people who have marked the Vallée of the Dordogne Corrézienne.

Rules:
Each player places their token in their municipality of residence or their chosen town.
Using the die, you will travel the territory answering questions on various topics. If you get the right answer, you can play again!
To win, you will need to collect 5 "Site" cards by correctly answering the question.

Le Jeu de la Vendée is an original, local and user-friendly board game based on the principle of questions and answers.
400 written questions and photos with also charades and riddles, "junior" questions which deal with subjects as varied as history or geography, heritage, local traditions, current events or famous people who have marked the Vendée.

Rules:
Each player places their token in their municipality of residence or their chosen town.
Using the die, you will travel the territory answering questions on various topics. If you get the right answer, you can play again!
To win, you will need to collect 5 "Site" cards by correctly answering the question.

Plastic caps tabletop soccer game played in a fabric/rubber table mat with plastic/fabric goals and other plastic components (ball, card box, goalkeeper items, phone stand, etc.), stickers (teams, yellow/red cards and injuries), a set 48+ cards and a smartphone app to keep track of the scores and time. The app features camera recording, QR scanning (cards), etc.

The game is played in a time limit of 40 mins (2 halves), the 1st player and fields are sorted at the beginning of the game using the coin toss feature in the app. A card sorting is performed, and 7 cards are distributed between both players, remaining cards are left on a side. Each card has a different feature with different playing conditions indicated in each card, as long as they are played they add different playing advantages such as better plays, goalie power-ups, cheats, blocks, mandatory card discards, trades, additional caps movement, etc.

The 1st player starts by positioning his/her caps along the mat and carefully flicking the ball-holding-cap against a small plastic barrier with a square hole between the ball and the cap, a pass is validated as long as the ball remains close to the receiving cap, successfully connected passes make the attacking player get closer to the other player's goal (whereas pass misses give a chance to the defending player to obtain the ball and counter-attack), goals are only validated when the attacking player calls a "ball-kick", time when the defending goalie has the chance to block the ball, the rest of the rules are similar to football/soccer (corner kicks, throw ins, free kicks, yellow/red cards, etc.) with intercalated caps repositioning for attacking and defending, the player who scores more goals at the end of the match wins.

The fabric/rubber mat allows the players to make three types of ball-kicks, the usual flicking with the plastic barrier to make straight kicks, the flying ball by pressing the ball against the mat with the cap, and the curve ball by using a small rubbery pad. Goals using the last two features make goals look like real football/soccer.

Crimes and surprising events are taking place in Paris? 
The police need their best elements to solve these mysteries. 
Conduct your investigation by visiting several emblematic monuments of Paris and find all the clues that will lead you to the culprit and his place of residence. Decipher information on the tombs of the Père Lachaise Cemetery, on the commemorative plaques of the Catacombs, or even in the kiosks that display the city of Paris? 
Then decode clues on metro tickets and use your powers of observation to confirm the alibi of one of the suspects on photos from surveillance cameras? 
Who will be the first to unmask the culprit?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Des crimes et des événements surprenants se passent dans Paris? La police a besoin de ses meilleurs éléments pour élucider ces mystères?. Menez votre enquête en vous rendant dans plusieurs monuments emblématiques de Paris et retrouvez tous les indices qui vous mèneront au coupable et à son lieu de résidence. Décryptez des informations sur les tombes du Cimetière du Père Lachaise, sur les plaques commémoratives des Catacombes, ou encore dans les kiosques qui arborent la ville de Paris? Décodez ensuite des indices sur les tickets de métro et utilisez votre sens de l'observation pour confirmer l'alibi d'un des suspects sur des photos de caméras de surveillance? Qui sera le premier à démasquer le coupable ?

—description from the publisher

Apples to Apples meets Poetry for Neanderthals! Tok Dum is a hilarious party game for 3-10 people that takes 15 minutes to play. Players assumed the roles of Caveman Philosophers answering life's biggest questions with limited vocabulary.

Overview:
The starting player draws 2 cards from the draw deck and asks the group the question they like best. Example Question: "How bird fly?"Players go around in a circle answering the question using monosyllabic language.

Example Answer: "Bird flap wing strong which push wind down to make bird go up!" The Player who asked the initial question then chooses the player whose answer they liked best a rewards them with the question card.

The game ends when all of the players have asked a question from the draw deck. Whoever collected the most cards at the end WINS!

—description from the designer

Goal: To have the most cards when the stack of tiles has been used.

Play: A player will read the the top category card and then remove the Top Tile. Players are looking for the letter that is facing them AND isn’t duplicated anywhere on the tile. 
Once they’ve found their letter, a player needs to come up with a word that fits the category that starts with their letter. So if the card said “Pests” the player with the B might say “Bugs” whereas the player who has the L might say “Leeches” – and whoever says their answer first gets the card.

In ProyectoSTEM, each player represents a company competing in the field of innovation, managing projects spanning various areas of science and technology. The objective of the game is to develop these projects effectively to earn the most points possible. Participants must make strategic decisions about how to invest their resources, collaborate with other players, and face challenges related to the development and implementation of innovative projects.

Game Components
The game is structured around two main decks of cards: the Researcher Deck and the Project Deck.

     Researcher Deck: This deck contains cards representing professionals in the four STEM fields. Each card provides a specific amount of knowledge that players can use to advance their projects. The researchers bring specific skills and competencies that allow players to address challenges and overcome obstacles in the development of their projects.
     Project Deck: The cards in this deck present various projects that players must manage and develop. Each project requires a particular set of knowledge and resources and is calibrated to assess the level of difficulty and technical requirements in the areas of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. Players must select and work on projects that match their strategies and available skills.


Gameplay
During the game, players must plan and execute strategies for the development of their projects, based on researcher and project cards. The decisions they make affect the progress and success of their companies. Furthermore, players must efficiently manage their resources and skills to meet project requirements and face unforeseen challenges that may arise.

—description from the publisher

The "Camino Primitivo" is the oldest route of the "Camino de Santiago". It winds through the mountains and valleys of Asturias and Galicia, following in the footsteps of the first royal pilgrim: King Alfonso II “El Casto.” Centuries ago, he set out to confirm that the remains discovered in Compostela truly belonged to the Apostle James.

In El Camino Mágico, 3 to 4 players relive that legendary journey. Each becomes a pilgrim traveling the Camino Primitivo from Oviedo to Santiago de Compostela, over the course of 13 stages—one for each round of the game. Each stage is divided into two phases: Day and Night. During the Day phase, you roll a weather die to determine how much energy (action points) you will have available for walking and visiting landmarks. At Night, you must find a place to rest. Your choice of accommodation—as well as how much energy you’ve spent in previous days—will affect how much energy you recover for the following stages.

Your goal is to finish the pilgrimage with the most Enrichment Points, earned through a variety of experiences along the way.

You gain Enrichment Points by:


     Visiting Landmarks: Along the route, pilgrims encounter remarkable places where they can choose to stop and visit. Each visit grants immediate Enrichment Points.


The first pilgrim to visit a site also claims its Landmark Card, divided in collections (architecture, gastronomy, landscape, folklore, or museums), worth extra points at the end of the game. However, every visit consumes time and energy—so choose carefully if you wish to reach Santiago in time!


     Meeting Other Pilgrims: The Camino is not just about the journey, but about the people you meet. At night, pilgrims choose an hostel to rest. Sharing the same hostel with others rewards additional Enrichment Points, symbolizing shared stories and fellowship.



     Reaching Santiago: Arriving in Santiago de Compostela before the end of the 13th stage grants a large reward. Those who don’t reach the city still earn points, but fewer, depending on how far they remain from the destination.



     Living Simply: Each pilgrim begins with a small amount of money for meals, massages, or hotel stays that restore health or energy. Spending wisely is key—but finishing the game with unspent coins also brings bonus Enrichment Points for an austere, humble journey.



     Health and Energy: Arriving exhausted or in poor health reduces your final score, as you cannot fully enjoy the spiritual reward of the journey. Managing your strength and maintaining balance throughout the 13 stages will grant additional Enrichment Points.


El Camino Mágico is more than a game—it’s an invitation to walk the oldest pilgrimage in Europe, rediscovering culture, nature, and inner growth through play.
Will you rush to reach Santiago first, or take your time to enjoy every step of the journey?

Whichever path you choose, your pilgrimage begins now. ¡Buen Camino!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dragon Hoard: A Thrilling Journey for Gold and Glory!
description:
Embark on an epic adventure in the enchanted forest of Dracoheim -  land of molten lava, icy blasts, and five fierce dragons guarding an ancient treasure hoard. You and your fellow adventurers begin the race in one team, but as you inch closer to the treasure, alliances break and only one adventurer will seize the fabled Dragon Hoard.

Goal:

The game rewards players for both, winning the race and maximizing resources. To win, players need to be first to complete the treasure trail and defeat the final dragon, while also acquiring 8 golden talismans.

Game-play:

Dragon Hoard is a twist on the classic race to the end mechanic, combining euro-style mechanics like resource management, collaboration and shifting alliances. The Game Board is a treasure trail with numbered tiles containing special markers for key game mechanics - ice and lava tiles, wildcards and dragons!

(1) Turn
Players take turns to (i) collect gemstones, (ii) use gemstones to move ahead and/or (iii) exchange into golden talismans, (iv) play wildcards and (v) mystic spirit cards

(2) Playing in a Team
All players act as part of the same team, but take turns independently. When players are in a team, they move together, share resources to move faster, and act consistently. They are also not allowed to play wildcards and mystic spirit cards against each other.

(3) Dragon Challenges
There are five dragons on the Board, which need to be battled before anyone can move forward. When the team moves to a dragon tile, one player stays behind and fights the dragon alone (i.e., the dragon challenger), while the team moves ahead. Winning rewards a challenger with faster movement. Losing a challenge, stalls movement and depletes golden talismans.


(4) Shifting Alliances
When a player stays behind to becomes a dragon challenger, they split from the team and play the rest of the game solo, including all future dragon challenges. This means that teams progressively get smaller as one player turns challenger at every dragon tile, until all players compete individually for the treasure! Solo players can betray and be betrayed by those in a team. This dynamic makes for several interesting twists in the second half of the game, when players sabotage each other through wildcards and mystic spirit cards.

(5) Good Balance of Strategy and Luck
The game rewards players for speed & resource management.  This means that players must make a series of strategic trade-offs: Use gemstones to move ahead faster (and carry  the team), or acquire more talismans for themselves? Use talismans to sabotage opponents? Optimize for speed and be the first to finish the trail or gather more resources? There’s a healthy dose of luck thrown in as well, with wildcards, mystic spirit cards and the outcome of the dragon challenges being decided by a die roll.

—description from the publisher

In a solarpunk world powered by renewable bubble energy, islands float high above the clouds, shrouded in mist and full of wonder. You take on the role of a mentor at the Flight Academy, guiding a group of eager animal trainees—affectionately called "puffballs"—who were born without wings but dream of reaching their faraway dream destinations.

Designed for 1–3 players, the game blends light strategy with cooperative play. Each day, players consult a floating island device embedded with electronic components, which—alongside a custom dice tower—reveals the day’s weather conditions and training recommendations. Gameplay centers around dice-driven mechanics that determine resource generation, trainee movement, and available actions.

As mentors, players will engage in farming, fishing, and guiding the trainees through valley expeditions to collect vital resources. These efforts support the completion of daily training exercises, the buildup of flight energy, and the gradual growth of each trainee. Ultimately, your goal is to help them break through the clouds and arrive at the destinations they’ve long dreamed of—a heartfelt journey shaped by care, cooperation, and gentle adventure.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray
Theme: Floating islands in the sky

description:
In the heart of Gijón, during the 19th century, nature seemed to have been forgotten. However, a recently discovered time capsule has revealed a lost treasure: the secrets to creating urban mini-forests that foster biodiversity and green life in the city.

In "Minibosques de Gijón", you and your friends will become gardeners who must plant trees and shrubs to create the most balanced and diverse mini-forests. But beware, not all plants thrive in all soils, and the balance between species is crucial to ensuring the survival of your forests.

In the game, we'll have four types of soil and a series of trees and bushes that we must place on their corresponding soils.

Using a draft-like mechanic and over four rounds, all players must plant to obtain the best miniforests.

At the end of the game, the player with the best miniforests will win.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: S.M.A.C.K.: Skary Monsters And Cereal Killers
description:
The secret society known as The Koven have turned all the adults with diabetes into monsters! The only ones that can stop the monsters are the Cereal Killers: kids with high sugar levels immune to the monsters' hunger!

A perfect blend of chance and skill, each player must kill their monster by using legendary items or other weapons while working against the sinister forces that oppose them at every turn.

Actions to choose from include moving; trading, drawing, and using cards; equipping items; and ultimately killing each monster before they reach their end point and before The Koven overwhelms the board (represented by token placement.)

You must adjust your strategy as needed, for a single turn of a card or roll of the dice can make you painfully aware of just how unpredictably things can unfold.

—description from the designer

In Haunt-O-Rama 1-5 players compete to earn the most money over 3 rounds.

Players will be purchasing attractions that come in 5 variations of tetromino shapes. They will also want to attract different guest types to match the 5 types of attractions in their park.

There is one small problem, this land is haunted. The resident ghosts are eager to scare away any park goers and cause ride malfunction.

Each round of the game is made up of 3 phases:

Market phase - Players will have the chance to gain attractions and guests to add to their park by selecting 2 market cards in a turn order.

Park Phase - Players will draw guests at random from their collection and place them onto attractions in their park. When an attraction is full, they will instantly trigger a bonus. If a ghost is drawn it will scare off 1 guest and shutdown 1 attraction. This phase is played simultaneously.

Income Phase - Players gain income for each Guest that matches the attraction it has been placed on, for various upgrades, and for sets of different market cards.

—description from the publisher

Set during the Edo Period of Japan, play as a daimyo - or feudal lord - overseeing a personal province board that begins as a wild, old growth forest. Over the course of five rounds, each player will determine the path of their own province by simultaneously placing skilled forestry samurai workers at key intersections to perform actions. These decisions could include the harvesting of trees to construct farms and castles, the planting and study of trees for future knowledge, establishing a new agricultural presence to sustain the region’s population, the building of castle structures for both immediate rewards and eventual honor, or even mustering a military force to help support the efforts of the Shogun in Edo.

While tending to their province, players must also forecast, consider, and prepare for the ever-changing weather of Japan, with rain having both positive growth and negative flooding effects on their own land. The deck of weather cards is constantly changing focus on forest regions, and it is up to each daimyo to consider the importance in forecasting and preparing for the weather. If ignored, excessive rains in regions caught unaware could turn into a cascade of destructive flooding across your province!

Using their skilled forestry samurai, players may choose to cut down any of the four different tree types found on the landscape, each of which contribute towards a specific combination of required materials for castles and farms to be built in their province or Edo. But players also must consider the sustainable approach to forest regeneration, through the planting, studying, and fostered growth of future generations of trees. If you clear cut the land to have an immediate stockpile of building supplies, you leave your province susceptible to flooding, destruction, and fallow fields. Finding the balance between the demands of the natural environment, your province’s needs, and the Shogun’s demands in Edo is the key to success in Ringyo.

In addition to tending to their own region, players must consider the demands of the Shogun in Edo, located in the center of the table, as well. Competing with other daimyo players, each will send advisors from their region to Edo - as well as race to fulfill specific demands of the Shogun - to seek both immediate gifts and receive game-end honor bestowed upon them by the Shogun, allowing them to rise above the competition due to their attention on Edo.

Using a combination of bidding for favor with the shogun, simultaneous worker placement in your own province, reacting to the ever-changing weather of the region, and competing with other feudal lords to fulfill the demands of the shogun, Ringyo rewards players with honor who consider both their own region’s needs as well as the requests of the greater country, all while taking a sustainable and respectful approach to the land on which they call home. Whichever path a player has taken, it is the daimyo who has achieved the greatest amount of honor by the end of five rounds, who will become the victor.

—description from the publisher

After the successful launch of the Belmontours travel agency, it’s time for a major expansion across Europe!
As official agents of the company, your mission is to open new offices in the continent’s most important cities. But beware—success won’t come easily. To triumph, you’ll need strategic thinking, knowledge of geography, and smart resource management.

Each player represents a commercial agent, traveling through Europe to establish Belmontours offices. Your meeple (agent) moves by:


     Train: across land borders between neighboring countries.
     Ship: between countries that share the same sea.
     Airplane: to travel farther and faster—at a higher cost.


Or you can stay put to earn extra money instead of moving.

Wherever you go, you may open a new office by placing one of your disks. If you’re the first to open an office in a country, you also gain that country’s collector card.

If another player already has an office there, you still have a chance! You can attempt to open your own office for free by correctly answering a geography question about that country, asked by the last player who built there. If you answer incorrectly, you must pay that player instead. Offices stack in a tower, showing how many agencies are competing in each country.

Throughout the game, players pursue public and secret objective cards, each listing 3–5 countries to connect or occupy. Completing objectives earns you victory points at the end of the game. The game continues until a player completes a set number of objectives (depending on the number of players). Then, everyone plays one final round before the final scoring.

At the end of the game, players score points for completed objectives, regional majorities in Europe’s five commercial zones, tower’s height and remaining money.

With Belmontours, you’ll explore European geography in a fun and strategic way—learning about transportation, tourism, and smart decision-making.
Can you build the largest network of tourist offices and lead Belmontours to international success?
Pack your bags, agents—the expansion across Europe begins now!

—description from the publisher

Villaviciosa Municipality, Cider Region, Asturias.
A new generation of llagareros (cider makers) is setting out to launch their own businesses, following the traditional method of producing Asturian cider. They will all compete to earn their place in the challenging cider market—an industry centered around one of the most iconic drinks of the Principality.

Starting with a tight budget they must manage wisely, players will need to hire staff, improve their modest facilities, and rent services to ensure they produce the best cider possible by springtime.

During your turn, you will place your workers on actions, which will vary depending on the season of the year. These actions allow you to produce and sell cider, host folk festivals, hire staff or upgrade your cider factory with improvements such as more apple trees to enhance your harvest, presses, or corking machines.

From picking the apples at harvest time to racking and storing them in barrels, every step of the way requires careful planning. Players will also be able to decide the type of cider they want to produce and which customers they will supply it to, balancing quality, time, and available resources.

Over the years, if the harvests are favorable and they carefully manage both apple selection and cider production, they will be able to create authentic Asturian cider—the most highly valued on the market.

In this resource management game, players compete over five rounds to maximize the profitability of their cider production facilities and fulfill as many orders as possible. The main way to earn victory points is by selling cider of different varieties, either through supply contracts or directly to restaurants. At the end of the game, the player who has accumulated the most points is declared the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Deathstroke Promo Card
description:
Originally created for the release of DC Deck-Building Game: Dark Nights: Metal, this card for the DC Deck-Building Game series features Deathstroke.

Once per turn: When you destroy a card, draw a card.

A streak of art robberies plagues Europe’s art collections… and you are responsible for that.
Take on the role of a criminal mastermind behind your own art robbery crew and steal Europe’s most famous pieces of art from the halls of museums and galleries like the Louvre, the Rijksmuseum and Museo del Prado.

Recruit the right specialists to successfully complete the theft, enhance your crew’s abilities and keep Interpol in your rear-view mirror. Will you rob your way to the most valuable collection?

• Travel back to 60’s Europe
• Take part in grand art robberies and oversee your own art robbery crew 
• Includes 45 unique crew members

The Great Art Robbery is a heist-themed deck builder game for 2 to 5 players. Players take control of their own art robbery gang and travel across Europe to steal famous paintings from the continent’s art museums and galleries. Actions like moving, upgrading your hideout and stealing paintings are performed by playing cards from your hand and discarding them to your personal discard pile. Players expand their starting deck by buying specialist robbers from the market and eliminate unwanted cards from their deck at certain locations. Every art robbery raises your notoriety level and advances the Interpol marker – raise your notoriety too high, and you receive an Interpol card, clogging up your deck and your hand. The game ends when the Interpol marker reaches its last space, and the player with the most points worth of stolen paintings wins the game.

Break the code and prove yourself to be the world’s greatest detective.
In Master Detectives: Code X, players take on the role of one of various master detectives that have gathered to determine, once and for all, who among them is the greatest detective. Each detective compiles a secret code for one of their opponents and tries to decipher their own code through clever detective work. Tactically move your detective across the playing field to reach the desired location and garner the right information, or question your opponents about your code. The first player to decipher their assigned code is the winner of this exciting codebreaking game!

• Enter the mysterious world of Master Detectives
• Exciting codebreaking game for all ages
• Full of thematic illustrations

Master Detectives: Code X is a competitive tile-based codebreaker deduction game for 2 to 5 players. Each player receives code tiles corresponding to a specific digit and compiles a three-digit code for their neighbour. Players move their marker across the composite playing board to gather clues about their code in the form of planted code tiles and by asking questions about their code to fellow players. By ruling out specific digits, players can deduce their personal code. The first player to declare their entire code correctly, wins the game.

Step into the world of abstract art and create your own masterpiece inspired by world-famous artist Piet Mondrian.
In this artistic game, you get the opportunity to create your own masterpiece inspired by Mondrian’s paintings, together with your opponents. Replicate Mondrian’s iconic patterns on your target card as accurately as possible by assembling the various tiles, or use the effects on your target cards to hinder your opponents. Will you be the first to finish all of your target cards?

• Experience Mondrian like never before
• Create beautiful patterns reminiscent of Piet Mondrian’s artworks
• Competitive puzzle game for 2 to 6 players

Mondrian Experience is a competitive art-themed puzzle game for 2 to 6 players. Players take turns placing coloured tiles from a shared supply to add to a mutual mosaic inspired by the abstract artworks of Dutch painter Piet Mondrian. To score points, player complete patterns prescribed on pattern cards which they receive at the start of the game (more pattern cards are earned during play). They can also discard pattern cards to activate a variety of effects. The game continues until a player has completed 4 pattern cards and the player with the highest number of points wins the game.

Critter Crimes is a wild caper through the underbrush of crime and cunning, where furry felons scurry for infamy and reknown! In this dice-rolling, resource-snatching, hideout-building game, you’ll take on the role of a criminal critter—be it a Tommy Shellprano, or the feathered fraudster Owl Capone —vying to become the top boss of the woodland underworld. Players roll food dice to pull off daring crimes, stash loot in burrows, and play dirty for powerful bonuses. But don’t get too greedy—your rivals might plant dirt on you, steal your stash, or sic the Bark Ranger on your tail!

With variable abilities and delightfully devious mechanics like stealing art, planting evidence, and tossing dice in the trash, Critter Crimes is the perfect mix of cute and cunning. Grab your gang, sharpen your claws, and let the crime spree begin.

The core gameplay loop is a repetitive series of days and nights.  During each day, players will explore Central Park and gather food dice to help them commit crimes, resources to help them build burrows, bottlecaps to increase their ability scores, and dirt to help them gain leverage on their opponents.

Once each player has explored during the day, each player commits crimes at night. To commit a crime, they choose one from a displayed tableau and rolls any amount of their cached dice in hopes of rolling equal to or greater than the score on the crime card, then they add their correct modifier to their roll. Each crime has a minor immediate effect like dice manipulation and resource management. Later on once a player has built a burrow they may store their dice onto these cards (each crime card accepts a different colored food die), and this activates its major effect.

The game ends when a player has committed their 12th crime or when all of the dice are gone from the park. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Sun the Rain and the Appleseed
description:
Build groves and house critters with special abilities!  This is a set collection style card game with a Johnny Appleseed theme! Score points based on grove size, apple bakery goals, and how many Critters you can help find love, in this fast-paced family-friendly card game.

2-5 players will start with 3 simple actions - Draw Cards, Play Groves, or Attract Critters.  Those actions quickly expand as the game advances.  Players will take turns performing one action on their turn. Groves are made up of "Apple Tree" cards played as runs (1,2,3,4,5,etc) of the same color or Sets (2,2,2,2) of any colors.    Whichever critters you decide to bring into those groves will create a unique strategy for each playthrough.

The Deck consists of 132 Green, Red, and Yellow "Apple Tree" Cards, 36 Critter cards (from Ravens to Beavers), 7 Bee Cards, and 7 Bakery goals!

CHASE A RAINBOW: Build a hand consisting of all 7 colors of the RAINBOW, get rid of all cloud cards, use wild RAINBOW cards to fill in where necessary but be careful other players can steal from your hand with a lightning card.

boardgame: Spot it! The Lord of the Rings
description:
Spot it! The Lord of the Rings features gameplay nearly identical to that of Spot it!

Nearly every card features eight different symbols, with the symbols varying in size from one card to the next. Any two cards have exactly one symbol in common. For the basic Spot it! game, reveal one card, then another. Whoever spots the symbol in common on both cards claims the first card, then another card is revealed for players to search, and so on. Whoever has collected the most cards when the 55-card deck runs out wins!

How Spot it! The Lord of the Rings differs from Spot it! is that includes a 56th card: the One Ring. What are the rules for this card? No clue as for now the rules are (appropriately enough) invisible.

This one of a kind card has been created for this campaign, it's a sneaky way to end the game early if you think you know where that Gold Nugget is hiding!

Hype: Return all of your to the nest.  The Raven Returns will happen 1 day earlier.

x1 Forest Card: HYPE

—description from the publisher

This card is part of the 2025 Dice Tower Promo Pack A

Now you can become the swiftest hare in the forest or the slowest turtle. The hare takes advantage of its speed to play more cards and steal. With the Turtle, you can put other animals to sleep on your turn. Make the most of their unique abilities to win the game before the crow catches you. 1x Hare Animal Card 1x Tortoise Animal Card 1x Hare token 1x Tortoise token

—description from the publisher

You'll be able to play with the designer's pets. The mischievous Eren and his antics, or with the sleepy Kaori and her light dream.

Components: 
x1 Dog Animal Card 
x1 Cat Animal Card 
x1 Dog Token 
x1 Cat Token

—description from the publisher

Now you can be the crow, yes, you read that right! With this expansion, the game expands to accommodate 6 players, and one of them will be the crow. The crow doesn't have its own turn, but it will play to disrupt the turns of others and try to make sure that the cursed Gold Nugget ends up in its precious nest by the end of the game. Components: 1x Crow Animal Card

—description from the publisher

With the spider's ability, you will become a nuisance in the game. Place webs on the stones so that other players cannot see them for a while, and deceive them to achieve victory. Components: 1x Spider Animal Card 3x Cardboard webs 1x Spider Token

—description from the publisher

You will be a skunk, using its best ability against predators: the odor. Send your stinky letters to other players, steal cards, and seize the Gold Nugget!

x1 Skunk Animal Card
x5 Stinky Forest Cards
x1 Skunk Token

—description from the publisher

Counting sheep has never been so fun! Be a mischievous sheep and steal stones from your opponents while they sleep, but be careful not to get caught.

Play as a skillful lizard that can leave its tail as a decoy for the crow and avoid being caught by it. Watch out, as the tail doesn't regenerate on its own!

x1 Sheep Animal Card
x1 Lizard Animal Card
x1 Sheep Token
x1 Lizard Token

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dunkelstein: An Augmented Reality Tabletop Murder Mystery
description:
Dunkelstein is an immersive cooperative escape game and murder mystery that blends augmented reality with physical tabletop components to create a haunting, story-driven experience.

Set in a mysterious German villa in 1914, players take on the roles of investigators unraveling a dark family secret. Using a mobile device, the app projects animated characters and interactive clues directly onto the table or a beautifully crafted wooden model of the villa. Real-time performances — captured through motion and facial animation — bring the story to life as players explore rooms, interrogate suspects, and solve puzzles.

The game supports solo or cooperative play with up to four players. It features a range of physical elements such as tutorial, quest, and evidence cards tracked through AR, as well as documents to be handled and examined. Players interact primarily through tapping on the device screen and through voice recognition. With branching narratives and multiple endings, Dunkelstein offers high replayability — and future stories will reuse the same AR-compatible villa to tell completely new tales.

Dunkelstein fuses analog tactility with cinematic digital storytelling to deliver an atmospheric experience unlike anything else in modern board gaming.

—description from the designer

boardgame: BattleTech: 21st Centauri Lancers Command Lance Forcepack
description:
Included are the new Shockwave and Jade Hawk and new variants of the Stalker and Shadow Cat—no assembly required—along with four MechWarrior pilot cards and four Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

—description from the publisher
Promo of 4 additional Rocket Tokens for Point Galaxy to provide new ways for players to score.
Promo of 12 structure/landscape cards and 4 starting structure/Queen's Palace cards for Propolis that add new gameplay elements and new ways for players to score.

Collect souls. Attack your friends. Get revenge!

Reaper Cat is a catty, soul-collecting, strategy card game for 2-6 players.

The object of the game is to be the player with the most SOUL cards in your hand by the time nine lives is over.

Players take turns drawing and discarding, collecting and hanging on to SOUL cards. Beware as your opponents try to attack you with ATTACK cards, and special CAT cards. The game progresses as players can play LIFE cards they draw and play on the table. Once nine LIFE cards have been played collectively, the game is over and whoever has the most SOUL cards is the winner!

No two games are alike in this strategy game. The more you play, the more you find that certain CAT cards may be worth holding onto that’s just the ever-important SOUL cards.

Have fun collecting!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Murder Box: Meurtres au Musée Letterton
description:
Can you unmask the culprit hiding among you?
You're the characters trapped in the Letterton Museum, where a murder has just been committed! Could the culprit still be among you?

Settle down comfortably around the table, dress up, polish your accent and study your cards to get into character. Then move around the board, question the other players, search the premises (some cards may hold clues!), study newspaper clippings, listen to street gossip... and thwart suspicion if you're the culprit!

Together, will you be able to piece together this deadly evening?

Murder Box: Murders at the Letterton Museum features 3 scenarios lasting around two hours, with easy, medium and hard difficulty levels for intense cozy murder mysteries! With an expert mode for the more seasoned players!

DraeAscaria: Rogue Beasts is a fast-paced, fantasy roguelike card game set in the wild and wondrous world of DraeAscaria.

As new recruits to Velwynd’s Royal Delvers Guild, players are sent to explore the mysterious, ancient ruins that have recently surfaced. But they won’t be going in alone. Each Delver is joined by a loyal beast companion.

While navigating the shifting ruins, players must battle and capture wild beasts, build their team, and race to defeat the powerful boss lurking deep within. The first to do so claims victory and all the glory!

Every turn consists of:

A Movement Phase: Players roll to see where they can move. Reaching a threshold allows them to draw a new tile and expand the ruins.

An Explore Phase: Players draw a card, revealing what was found in that location. Perhaps a useful item, or a beast waiting to fight.

A Combat Phase (if triggered): If a beast appears, it’s time to battle! Elemental strengths and status effects will determine the players outcome.

Every run feels different thanks to randomized tiles and encounters. Strategic team-building, clever use of elemental advantages, and a bit of luck are key to surviving the chaos.

Build the ultimate team… or be sent back in defeat!

—description from the publisher

Sieben Jahre is set during the Seven Years' War.

Seven Years' War retraces the events that involved the European powers in what Churchill called the First World War, which, in the game, takes place in three of the five scenarios: Europe, North America, and the Caribbean. 
The nations involved are Prussia and Great Britain on one side against France and Austria on the other. Allies of one or the other are Russia, Sweden, the Empire, Saxony, Spain, Portugal, the Electorates of Hanover and Hesse-Kassel. 
The Events table traces the main events of the war, determines the game turns, and offers useful military and economic advantages to the states. 
It is important to manage the costs of military expeditions and supply lines, both land and sea, and to safeguard one's soldiers to avoid finding oneself with reduced contingents during the conflict. 
Unforeseen events represented by the Event markers affect the outcome of battles and the campaign. 
The war isn't just fought on land: France and Great Britain vie for dominance at sea. 
The game's mechanics are profoundly different from those of other strategic wargames and simulate the real-life difficulties faced during military expeditions, both economically and logistical. 
The battle mechanics make them quick and easy.

For one or two players aged 14 and up. 
The average game length in campaign mode is several hours, and in scenario mode, half an hour or more.

—description from the publisher

It's the UNO matching card game everyone knows and loves, but featuring graphics from Jurassic World Dominion. Players still race to get rid of all their cards by matching color, number and symbol, but when someone plays an Action Card with a Dino Icon on it, the special "Run!" rule blocks the action! And just like 145 million years ago, when a player is down to one card, they can't forget to shout, "UNO!"

—description from the publisher

In Splitrick, you're surrounded by adorable but mischievous cats. Each one looks sweet, but hide a mean streak: some will give you points, others will scratch you with penalties. To survive, you'll have to split your hand and tread carefully: play too eagerly and you'll fall into their trap. Play too cautiously and you'll miss your chance. Only those who can sniff out the tricks and stay one paw ahead will come out unscathed.

Splitrick is a must-follow trick-taking game without trumps. At the start of each round, players choose to split their hand into two parts or play normally, gaining bonus points for no-split choices. As tricks unfold, specific cards or outcomes may trigger significant point losses from randomly drawn minus cards. The game ends after three rounds.

boardgame: Nemesis Retaliation: Exertion Mini-expansion Promo Pack
description: The Exertion mini-expansion is a promo pack made available through the "Dice Tower" 2025 Gamefound campaign". It features a deck of 8 cards that players will draw 2 cards from and keep 1 the first time their health reaches a set point. This card can then be used when a situation is most dire, at a cost.

Movie monsters have come to life! The more you see them, the less scary they’ll be. But beware of the monster that lurks the longest as it will become the scariest and most powerful monster of all!

Fright Factor is a trick-taking game featuring movie monster suits that become less scary – and take fewer tricks – the more you play them. Each round, earn points by correctly predicting how many tricks you’ll win based on the fright factor of each monster.

—description from the publisher

The year is 20XX. Humanoid robots have become commonplace and four companies have dominated the market. However, each manufacturer uses a proprietary power cell that can only be used with their product line. Robots without the right batteries won’t function and will just take up space in your home. Likewise, batteries that can only be used with specific models will do you no good unless you have the robots that power them. Additionally, the competition between these four companies is intense and even the act of requesting a specific brand can have a big impact on its value. All you want is some robot helpers with the proper power to run them, but nothing’s that simple.

RoboKraft is a must-follow trick taking game for 3-4 players, in which the relative ranking of the suits changes with each trick. There is no dedicated trump suit. Instead, depending on which suit is led, the value of that suit will go up or down in comparison to the rest. After all cards are played into a trick, the player who played the highest card of the highest value suit played will take the cards played and separate them into the different suits face up on the table. The player who played the lowest card of the lowest value suit played will take a Power Cell cube of the color which won the trick. At the end of the game, players will multiply the Utility Ratings on cards they have won in each suit by the matching discs they have acquired. Any cards and discs that do not have a match are negative points.

—description from the publisher

Risk the Abyss is a 2-4 player dice game in which players compete to become the greatest Deep Sea Diver. Players take turns by rolling dice with a Yahtzee-style mechanism, and then executing the actions from the resulting dice. Each die face represents a different action that a player can take:


     Flippers and Anchors are used to move the Divers around the Ocean Grid.
     Harpoons and Keys are used to acquire Sea Creature and Treasure cards.
     Oxygen is used to hang onto face up cards.
     Radios are used to return to the Boat and bank cards.


Players must strategically collect sets of cards to gain the most points by the end of the game.

—description from the designer

The goal is not to win, only to survive.

Survive is a fast paced, finger pointing elimination style card game where you want to hold on desperately to your own lives at the expense of other. The premise of the game is to outlast the people around you and be the last one standing.

Each round, players are thrown into different scenarios where you must do everything in your power to stay alive. When a scenario card is read, be quick on the draw and point your finger at a family member or a friend and throw them under the bus. Highlight their weaknesses and showcase why they would be the first to die. If you are pointed at, scramble to defend yourself. Even if you can't change everyone's minds, you might be able to keep a few precious lives.

After debating, any votes against you are tallied up and that number of lives are lost. You may have more lives than a cat, but they are not infinite.

Rounds continue until the first person is eliminated. The game can end here, or, you can eliminate players one by one until there is only one person left to earn the title of The Sole Survivor.

The game comes with multiple editions, dry erase markers, life trackers, and has space for more games to be added into it.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Recorriendo Gijón ("Exploring Gijón"), a game for both those who know the city and those who are about to discover it. You have the opportunity to visit some of the municipality's most interesting places, but you must do it before anyone else!

In this game, the city of Gijón is divided into neighborhoods and regions, and your pawn will move around visiting many fascinating spots throughout the city. You can travel on foot, by bike using the bike lanes, or by bus using the bus routes — spending valuable bus tickets with your City Card. But be careful: only the first player to arrive at each location will receive that place’s collectible card!

Plan your routes carefully and decide which mode of transport you want to use, manage your bus trips with your City Card, and try to meet your personal goal and earn points with different card collections. There will be five places available to visit at any given time, so don't waste time!

Your location cards are divided into categories, and at the end of the game you will earn points for them based on their variety, the number of cards in each set, and your secret goal card.

—description from the publisher

Oceans, rivers, and wetlands are home to an astonishing variety of life, but many of their species are at risk due to human impact and habitat degradation.
In Misión Azul: Aqua Rescate ("Misión Azul: Aqua Rescue"), your mission is to provide the best possible conditions for the animals under your care while they are in the recovery center.

Each professional is responsible for caring exclusively for one specific species and also helps care for two additional species shared with others. However, the center has only one common pond, where all recovering animals must coexist. Keeping this pond in optimal condition is key to ensuring the survival of each species, as each one requires specific parameters of temperature, salinity, vegetation, rockiness, and water clarity.

Can you adjust the water conditions to meet the needs of the animals under your care? Don’t get distracted—at this center, each caregiver must focus solely on their own animals.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shiny Bandits: The Fox, The Raccoon and the Magpie
description:
Gold fever has struck the nature park! 
In Shiny Bandits, you deploy your bandit gang of magpies, foxes, and raccoons to collect the most gold.


     Reveal the location card.
     Choose one of your bandits.
     Play an action card to outsmart your rivals AND claim the gold.



     Gather the most gold with your bandit gang of magpies, foxes and racoons
     Compact game full of tactical choices and bluffing elements
     Includes 80 shiny ‘gold nuggets’


Shiny Bandits is a trick-taking game for 2 to 5 players. Players take control of their own gold bandit gang of foxes, magpies and racoons to gather the most amount of gold hidden across the nature reserve. Each round, a location card with varying locations and gold supplies are revealed and players assign one of their gang members to a corresponding location. Players then play one of 9 different action cards that affect the orientation of the location card, its gold supply, assigned characters, etc. After the action cards are resolved, gold from the location cards is divided among players based on assigned gang members, that location supply and resolved action cards. The player with the most gold at the end of nine rounds of play wins.

—description from the publisher

Designed as a casual alternative to complex collectible card games, ZVIRIUS delivers deep interaction, emotional gameplay, and high replayability — all in one box.
Animals are running through the field to collect treasures. All of them have different speed, power and abilities. 
Treasures are available in the general pool and in players personal treasure pools. Creatures can take treasures from general pool but some creatures can steal from players.

✔ No deckbuilding required  
✔ High interaction and replayability

Designed as a casual alternative to complex collectible card games, ZVIRIUS delivers deep interaction, emotional gameplay, and high replayability — all in one box.

—description from the designer

boardgame: No Time's Too Short For Shorts!
description:
No Time’s Too Short For Shorts! is an abstract ‘trick-taking-like card game where all of the action happens at once!
Your aim is to wear shorts the most, each and every round. Every round you succeed at this, you win and get a point!
Will you be the ‘shortiest’ player at the table?
Or, will you be forced by the others to be down-trod and up-trowed?
In this game, the shorter the leg-wear, the better!

—description from the designer

It all began when you cleared that first row. You’ve been chasing the dopamine rush of a well-placed polyomino ever since. Tetrominoes led to pentominoes, and soon even they weren’t enough. Now obsession has led you to buy an expensive laser cutter to create hexominoes for your latest game design. Unfortunately the bundled “MPD” software is finicky, and after several failed attempts, you’re left with a single busted-up sheet of acrylic. If you can cut 20 unique hexominoes and a first player token, it might all be worth it.

Lasercut is a solo game in which the player attempts to cut 20 unique 6-cell polyominoes (hexominoes) and a first player token for their latest game design.

Each round, the player tries to design a hexomino by rolling and connecting dice on the Mobile Polyomino Designer (MPD). When a new hexomino is designed, it is cut (drawn) on the acrylic, allowing the player to collect dopamine and powerful bonuses. Hexominoes are recorded on the Hexomino Guide as a reminder that they can not be cut again.

The player repeats these steps until they fill the acrylic sheet with unique hexominoes (win) or fail to cut a unique hexomino on the acrylic (lose).

—description from the designer

Let’s take this inside! In the Rebounce indoor action game, go head-to-head against a friend in a 2 player game or compete in teams. Players bounce the squishy foam ball off the tabletop trampoline to opponents, who scramble to catch it – or the other team scores. The first team to 10 points wins! This fun family game for kids and adults features 3 game modes, so players can switch it up with freeze and timed challenges. Each mode includes lights, sounds, and music. When done playing, store the ball in the game unit. The all-in-one storage makes this physical game an ideal travel game.

ACTION-PACKED INDOOR GAME: The Rebounce electronic tabletop game brings the energy of outdoor games … indoors! Bounce the ball off the game unit at opponents. If they don’t catch it, earn a point. The first to 10 wins.

SOFT FOAM BALL FOR INDOOR-FRIENDLY PLAY: This fun active game includes a soft foam ball (a little smaller than a tennis ball) and tabletop game unit, so kids can enjoy the excitement of a beach or yard game inside.

3 ELECTRONIC GAME MODES: Mix it up for fresh games to play with friends all day! Show off trick shots in classic mode, try a hilarious freeze challenge in Remix mode, or see how fast everyone can play in the timed Reflex mode.

SCORING, LIGHTS, SOUNDS & MUSIC: When a player earns a point, they hit the button – and the game tracks and announces the score! Each mode features lights, plus voice commands, arcade-like sound effects, and music with adjustable volume.

FUN FAMILY GAME FOR 2 TO 6 PLAYERS: Play 1-on-1 or in teams.

FAST TO LEARN, PLAY & SET UP: This addictive sports game plays in just 10 mins.!

PORTABLE GAME WITH HANDLE AND STORAGE: Gotta bounce? Store the ball underneath the all-in-1 game unit.

—description from the publisher

Silly Swords is a dynamic print-and-play (DPnP) battle game where you design your own fighter using an online configurator, or pick from a wide selection of ready-made presets. 
Print your fighter, grab a standard poker deck, and face off in tactical duels!

Your mission: reduce your opponent’s lifepoints to zero by drawing cards and triggering powerful effects through smart card combinations.
The catch? You can't rearrange the cards in your hand. Every play counts, so think ahead, close gaps, and craft combos on the fly to outwit your opponent.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Candy Land game! In this reimagined version of the classic preschool game, little ones choose a Sweet Scout mover, select colored “candy” tokens from the Pouch of Wonders, and travel up the Rainbow Gummy Path to Candy Castle! Who will they meet along the way—and which player will reach the castle first?


     5 UNIQUE CHARACTER MOVERS: Play as a Sweet Scout! Choose from 4 colorful plastic movers, each a unique character in a treat-inspired outfit. Also includes a Gummy Twins (Sweety Bear and Soury Bear) mover.



     2 FUN WAYS TO PLAY: In classic mode, race to be the first player to meet Queen Frostine at Candy Castle! Collaborative mode keeps the game fresh with light strategy—players team up to get 3 Sweet Scouts to the castle before the Gummy Twins.
