     LARGER BOARD WITH UPDATED ART & STORY: Visit Gloppy, Plumpy, and Queen Frostine! Discover a reimagined land of familiar characters and adorable additions on the 20” x 20” board. Also included is an illustrated story.



     DRAWSTRING POUCH OF “CANDY” TOKENS: Instead of drawing cards to move along the board, kids can experience the excitement of reaching into the felt Pouch of Wonders to draw cardstock candy tokens.


—description from the publisher

Boulangerie is a strategic set-collection game where players try to bake the best bread using exactly six ingredient cards.
Each turn, players either collect or discard ingredient cards to improve their hand.
Bread is scored based on various patterns—same numbers, same ingredients, or sequences—and one card can meet multiple scoring conditions for higher ratings.
However, beware of bomb ingredients! If too many are revealed, the round ends suddenly, and any unfinished bread is worth zero stars.
Finishing early gives bonus points, but rushing may result in a weak bread—so smart timing, careful collection, and risk management are the keys to victory.
Play over five rounds, and the player with the highest total score becomes the true master baker!

—description from the designer

In this trick-taking game with a standard pack, each suit has a 'ladder' represented by a face-up card on the table which is its current 'rung'.

If you win a trick containing the card above the rung of a ladder, you claim control of it, as the rung increases to its new value.
The trump suit is set by the current rungs of the ladders, and may change from trick to trick.

You score points for any ladders held at the end of a deal based on its rung. Bonus multipliers are gained by holding the same ladder for multiple hands in a row.

Designed primarily for three, but can easily accommodate more, playing in partnerships at four.

—description from the designer

Not everyone comes to Middara by choice.

The Shadow Harbingers leave trail of corpses and broken families in their wake. After years of hunting, Aisling finally has the lead on the sinister cabal. She'll pay any price for revenge on those who murdered her father and stole her home.

Join her search for vengeance across Elenia and beyond. This Common level Adventure Pack takes you on a search for justice against those who would exploit the weak. Face off against a deadly pirate crew, raid a slaver's market, and glimpse into the black depths of the Suspended Tomb.

Includes deadly new combatants, thrilling bosses, and a new Middara story shaped by your choices. Aisling's future is in your hands.

—description by the publisher

boardgame: Starcrash: Heroes of the Last Dungeon
description:
In a world where dungeons fall from the sky and ancient forces stir beneath the earth, four unlikely heroes are called to face the final test.

After the legendary Starcrash — a cosmic event that shattered the balance of magic and unleashed sealed relics — the gates of the Last Dungeon have opened. Rumors tell of a power so great it could bend the fate of the world... or destroy it entirely.

You are a team of rookie adventurers, reluctant prophets, cursed warriors, and magical guardians. Armed with courage, charm, and a lot of questionable gear, you'll dive deep into a modular dungeon full of strange enemies, mysterious relics, and comically overpowered bosses.

Starcrash: Heroes of the Last Dungeon is a "small", modular, tactical dungeon crawler for 1–4 players, inspired by classic JRPGs. Combining grid-based exploration, character synergy, light combo-building, and humor-filled storytelling, each session delivers epic nostalgia with modern mechanics.

Will you rise as the last line of defense? Or become yet another forgotten footnote in the dungeon's dusty halls?

—description from the Publisher

boardgame: Twilight Imperium: Fourth Edition – Thunder's Edge
description:
The galaxy stands on the edge of a blade. A great struggle is coming, and only the greatest among them all will triumph.

Welcome to Thunder’s Edge, a new expansion for Twilight Imperium 4th Edition! This colossal expansion brings the classic interstellar conflict to a breaking point, where everything revolves around the titular planet of Thunder’s Edge. New factions take to the galactic stage, new worlds emerge for you to explore (or exploit), and a suite of Galactic Events—modifiers that change the rules of the game—ensure that no two playthroughs will ever be the same.

Also included is the brand-new Twilight’s Fall mode, an alternate way to play Twilight Imperium. Take up the mantle of a mad Mahact king, build your own custom faction from the scraps of interstellar civilization, and battle to become the supreme ruler of the galaxy. This mode comes with its own spread of unique components, making it almost an entirely new game!

—description from the publisher

A game that is similar to Roulette, but instead of betting on a number, you bet on which playing card the ball lands on. The roulette wheel has 54 sections that represent a playing card from a 54 card deck. Just like roulette, you could also bet base on the position of the chips on the board, like suit color, card suit, 4 different cards, 3 card poker hand, etc.

The layout also has a section where 3 playing cards are placed. This represents the 3 card poker hand. After each spin, a card is placed on the poker hand section. No cards are placed, if the spin matches the first 2 cards of the poker hand. Once all 3 cards are placed, players win or lose chips on any poker hand bets on the board, and the cards are placed back to the card holder.

According to the box, there were plans to introduce this as an actual casino game, but no such table games of Pokette exist.
publishers: Research Games, Inc. / Athol / RGI / ARC

description:
Place cards to create your own garden, forming flowers, and collect daisies to score points. The larger the flower you form, the more points you'll get at the end of the game.

Want to play with a little mischief?
Add tool cards and ruin other gardens by placing aphids on daisies, cutting flowers, or creating weeds to subtract points. Be careful! Angering your opponents could cost you dearly.

—description from the publisher

Use the wanted poster to display a photo of the rat suspect for that round, placing the card chosen by the sheriff over the rat's image on the poster.

A expansão “Cartaz Procurado” é uma carta adicional para o jogo ProcuRatos.

Utilize o cartaz de procurado para exibir a foto do rato suspeito daquela rodada, posicionando a carta escolhida pelo xerife em cima da imagem do rato no cartaz.

What would you say if you, a florist, got caught half-naked behind the curtains holding a cucumber? Or if you, an electrician, are found under the bed, covered in oil, and bound with fluffy handcuffs?

In Nothing to See Here, a raunchy, sex-positive card game, each round has one dealer and two to seven additional players. The dealer comes home to discover a host of random characters in all sorts of shocking positions that lead them to believe their partner is cheating! The players start each round with an identity, location, and physical state card and have to come up with a narrative to explain that there’s nothing to see here—and not get caught in the act. For an extra layer of fun, the dealer and other players give players item cards that they have to seamlessly weave into their story. Good luck explaining that massage oil and feather tickler!

boardgame: Салки в замке (Tag in the Castle)
description:
Салки в замке ("Tag in the Castle") is a one-vs-all game set in a magical atmosphere within an ancient castle. One player acts as the Head Familiar, controlling 5 mischievous animal familiars that move through the castle rooms to evade their owners and cause as much chaos as possible. The other players control 4 wizards, striving to catch their pets.

Wizards and the Head Familiar take turns. The Head Familiar always goes first. Then the wizards take a shared turn.

On the Head Familiar’s turn, they must perform 2 actions:

     Move a familiar’s figure to an adjacent room (following movement rules).
     Draw a prank token from the bag, add it to their reserve, then place one prank from their reserve in the room the familiar moved to.


On the Wizards’ turn, they perform 2 actions:

     The players may spend up to 3 movement actions across all 4 wizards. Each wizard may carry 1 or 2 prank tokens.
     The wizards attempt to discard prank tokens and catch familiars:
         To clear a mess, deliver the prank token to the room marked on it.
         To resolve an incident, the two wizards shown on the token must be in that room.
    


If a familiar is present when discarding a prank, the wizards catch it (remove its figure).

End of game (checked after Wizards’ turn):

     Wizards win if only 1 of the 5 familiars remains on the board.
     Head Familiar wins if:
         5 prank tokens are on the board at the end of the Wizards’ turn, or
         No prank tokens remain in the bag or the Head Familiar’s reserve.
    


--description from the publisher (translated)

At an exclusive casino on the French Riviera a croupier keels over dead at the roulette table. A few hours later, an unidentified woman falls from a sixth story hotel room. Meanwhile across town, a petty thief is arrested during a robbery attempt. Find the link that ties them together to uncover the identity of a killer. Assess the evidence and solve the case.

—description from the back of the box

Into the Wild Alaska: A Survival Race in the harsh Wilderness of Alaska. Stranded deep in the unforgiving wilderness, you and your companions must race to gather food, navigate brutal terrain, and outlast whatever nature - or each other - throws your way. Into the Wild Alaska blends real-world survival logic with tense, strategic play in a game that is easy to learn and impossible to forget.

Each turn, players will roll the dices and move around the board to the space designated by the dices. While they travel the map, each player will need to gather resources, establish strategic camps, overcome events, and build equipment, to be able to collect Food.

Each time players finish their round through the map, they must have enough Food resources to continue the next round. Each round the amount of Food increase, and if a player does not have enough Food, they are out of the game and did not survive Alaska wilderness. The last player standing, or the one who collects more than 50% of the Food resources available in the game can travel to the Island and finish the game as the winner of Into the Wild Alaska.

—description from the designer

Play WWII battles from Company to Brigade size.
     Finish a game in 2-4 hours.
     Keep everyone involved with simultaneous turns.
     Infantry are based as a single stand, representing 8-12 soldiers or two squads. Use your FoW armies.
     Each armoured vehicle model represents 2-4 real vehicles.
     Ground scale: 1mm equals roughly 1.5 metres.
     Time scale: Each game turn represents about 10 minutes of real time.
     Playable with 6mm, 10mm, 15mm, and 20mm figures.


Blaze Away WWII is a miniatures wargame system designed for battles ranging from company-sized actions to brigade-level engagements. It supports 6mm, 10mm, 15mm, and 20mm miniatures, and is flexible enough for scenarios such as raids, beach assaults, and set-piece attacks—whether involving infantry, armour, or combined arms operations.

Blaze Away WWII favours gameplay over simulation and deliberately avoids game mechanics such as “Buckets of Dice™,” saving throws, or other mechanisms that slow play without meaningful impact. The goal is to deliver streamlined, decisive action.

These rules draw from years of WWII gaming experience and many other systems. While simplifying many aspects of equipment and formations, the design encourages flexibility, scenario creativity, and fast-paced tactical decision-making. Players are free—indeed, encouraged—to adjust or expand the rules to suit specific scenarios or personal preferences.

Ranges, rates of fire, and penetration values are abstracted. Instead of tracking fine-grained data, each stand or model represents a larger group. Weapon effectiveness is built into the results tables, not detailed calculations. This abstraction allows for realistic representation of WWII tactics, fits the intended level of play (company to brigade), and works with standard table sizes.

A full game typically plays out in 12–24 turns. Despite the abstraction, expect real tactical manoeuvring, use of terrain, and meaningful command decisions.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Captain Nemo pilots his Nautilus submarine across all the waters of the globe exploring the far reaches and collecting great treasure.  But, the Imperialist forces are now hunting him and have assembled an attack fleet  positioned in Captain Nemo’s path.  Nemo strikes out at them with the rage that has been boiling inside of him since those same forces took everything from him.  They will pay.

Take command of the fantastic Nautilus submarine and its team of divers.  Your task is to defeat all of the imperialist forces attacking you - from small wood-hulled vessels to the mighty, ironclad Imperial Steam Ship (ISS) ‘Unrivalled’.

Each turn, roll dice and assign one to each of the three different units: the Nautilus Ramming Prow, the Nautilus Hull, and the Nautilus Diving Team, balancing inflicting maximum damage on the enemy with repairing your ship in order to stay in the fight and survive.  On the enemy’s turn, its front line units will likely attack you, but may also retreat and repair, or re-position for a more effective attack later in the battle.

—description from the designer

It is 1947. Humanity has breached the barrier between realities, using Rifts to create previously impossible technologies. Driven by this esoteric knowledge and Rift energy, a new era of both science and superstition has engulfed humankind. The fires of World War II rage as brightly as ever. Nations bloodied and scarred by years of fighting launch fresh offensives and counterattacks. Weapons of unprecedented destruction and beings of unnatural origin dominate countless battles across land, air, and sea.

In this horrifying new world, the only victory lies in survival.

Konflikt '47 is the game of heroes, horrors, monsters, and mechs, putting you in command of the nightmarish struggle for supremacy as the old world is torn apart and remade in blood and crackling energy. Fast-paced, lethal gameplay provides countless cinematic moments, with fluctuating Rift energies and the ever-present fog of war ensuring that no two games are ever the same, and the outcome is never guaranteed. Heroes and villains on each side lend their unique abilities to turn the tide of battle, as mechs and monsters face off in brutal contests of flesh versus steel.

This is not war. This is the end.

This book is your guide to the brutal warfare and Rift-spawned horrors of Konflikt '47, containing all of the rules needed to play games set in a Second World War elevated to a new intensity by the weird new science and devastating technology now available to humanity. Within its covers you’ll find detailed rules for setting up and playing fast-paced, action-packed games, accompanied by gorgeous artwork and photography to inspire your painting and modelling. That’s not all - there’s an entire section dedicated to introducing the forever-changed world of Konflikt '47, the major factions of the war, and the horrific new creations they unleash upon each other.

—description from the publisher

Semi-Historical Celebrity Deathmatch is a fast-paced fun ‘beer and pretzels’ game for up to ten players

Take control of one or more historical characters to win glory (and the game) by scoring the most kills and picking up the most loot in gladiatorial combat.

The player who placed their model on the table last takes the first activation, followed by the player who placed their model next to last, and so on. 
During an activation a player can elect to perform one of three
actions: 

     move, 
     attack, 
     move and attack. 


The celebrity may also perform a Special Ability.

How could you not be entertained by such a simple and silly game that gives you an excuse to pit Joan of Arc against Lord Wellington, or Napoleon against Lord Flasheart!? Enjoy!

Published in the November 2024 issue of Wargames Illustrated (Wi443)

—description from the publisher/rulebook
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Sharpen your swords, ditch your friends, and escape to freedom in Selfish: Dungeon Edition, a treacherous challenge from Ridley’s Games!

You and your fellow heroes must escape the deadly lair of the Dungeon Lord. In the pitch-black dungeon, your torch’s dying flame is all that stands between you and total darkness. There are three ways out of this place, but only one of them will grant you freedom without cost. And only the first person to find their way out will survive.

This devious strategy game is a race through dangerous passages battling against spiders, minions, hangry dragons, and, worst of all, each other! Attempt to reach safety while keeping your torch lit, wreaking havoc on your companions’ plans, and trying not to join the dreaded Skull Legion along the way.

Made for fans of fantasy, strategy, and sabotage and anyone who loves the thrill of the chase.

—description from the publisher

The thrill of the track, now on your table! Step into the world of BOX BOX BOX, a fast-paced, immersive, and strategic board game that brings the adrenaline of real racing straight to your home. Feel the pressure of the starting grid and adjust your strategy lap by lap. Here, victory doesn't go to the one who rolls the highest ?, it goes to the one who thinks smart, plans well... and takes the right risk at the perfect moment.

BOX BOX BOX is a board game inspired by Formula 1, where you'll have to manage your tires, adapt to changing weather, draw race cards, make pit stops, activate DRS... and make decisions like a real driver. “Every race is different. Every lap is a challenge.”

In BOX BOX BOX, strategy and luck collide to create a dynamic, competitive game where every race feels different, and everyone has a shot at winning right up to the final corner. At the start of the game, players agree on the number of laps, pit stops, and whether to enable or disable optional rules included in the game rulebook.


    Choose Your Tires [Strategy]: Soft, Medium, or Hard, each affects how you move and how you handle situations like Rain or Yellow Flags.
    Plan Your Tire Strategy [Strategy]: Manage your personal board, activate bonuses based on tire type, and decide the best time to make your mandatory pit stop.
    Card Decks [Strategy & Luck]: Land on special spaces and draw cards that can help you (Performance) or shake things up for everyone (Race Control).
    Roll the Dice & Advance [Luck]: But beware of the rain, yellow flags, and race events can disrupt your progress.
    Use DRS & Slipstream [Luck]: Overtake like a champion.
    Toggle Game Modules [Luck]: Before each race, players can customize their experience by enabling/disabling features as outlined in the rulebook.


Win the race by being the first to complete the agreed number of laps!

—description from the publisher

Enemies of Gotham City is an area-control game based off the beloved show Batman: The Animated Series. Play as one of twelve unique asymmetric villains like The Joker, Harley Quinn, Mr. Freeze, and more in an effort to take control of Gotham City's districts. Make some cash, thwart Batman, and become the most infamous villain of them all.

To do this, you have to plan out your moves and strategize, but you must also be adaptable; Gotham is in chaos, and your plans may not always go as intended! Wrestle for control of Gotham's districts with rival villains and out-think your opponents by using your power and weapons. Watch out for Batman and his allies, who are looking to bring peace to Gotham and send your goons to the Arkham Asylum cube tower!

—description from the publisher

Cthulhu Crusades is back — this time as an electrifying two-player duel set amid the wildest medieval crusades. Summon eldritch creatures, appease the Deep Ones, and lay siege to enemy castles to seize the ancient tomes and conquer the old world!
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Take on the role of the Secret Division as you roam Arkham, sealing every last portal to stop the Cthulhu rift that could destroy our world! A cooperative roll-and-write game with custom dice and plenty of tension!

In Cthulhu Rift, every dice roll forces you and your team to make crucial decisions, coordinate your strategy, and race to seal the portals before chaos erupts.

Bank Roll is a risk-free dice and chip placement game for up to 8 players. Players must roll dice to rescue their fortunes from a locked bank vault, and then blow it all on outrageous Lucksury in a shameless race to outdo one another! Unlocking the vault is no small task; it takes nerve, sharp instincts, and a whole lot of luck. But don’t worry — in Bank Roll, there’s always another chance! And no matter who you are, living in Lucksury is never more than a couple of dice rolls away!

—description from the publisher

One brisk morning, while he was working at the family glass-blowing factory, uniformed soldiers tramped in with their muddy boots and arrested your father. He was hauled off to debtor's prison, with a massive sum of silver between him and freedom. Now the house, the family farm, and the glassblowing factory are under threat of being seized by the local lord. You have but thirty days to find a solution, aided by old family friends, glassworks employees, and perhaps even unsavory sellswords from the nearest street corner. You'll never get enough money selling glass, though perhaps if you're desperate enough, you might make the journey north to the legendary Dragon of Havoc, a godlike being who sometimes grants aid to those of worthy heart. You unwrap your father's old dagger and saddle your horse, leaving the forge embers cold to join the uncertain path ahead.

Song of Silveranth is a co-operative, open-world, narrative adventure in the tradition of Ryan Laukat's Sleeping Gods and Sleeping Gods: Distant Skies. You and your friends undertake a treacherous quest, confronting powerful kingdoms, suspicious priests, dangerous creatures, and afflicted people. There are many paths to walk, whether you choose thievery, discourse, combat, or intrigue. You must explore a wide world, gaining improbable allies and deadly enemies along the way.

Song of Silveranth uses a richly-woven narrative and keyword system to tell many tales. Each character has their own questline, and the branching main story offers world-changing choices and surprising endings. Players also explore a location book, walking forest paths, delving into dark caverns, and braving trapped ruins where a wide host of enemies hide and wait for their next victim. Combat is quick and dynamic, featuring a new press-your-luck system and a wide array of tactics and weaponry.

Through a massive atlas, in deep dungeons, and across quests and stories where your choices change the outcomes of people and lands larger than yourselves, your characters will test their strength and learn where their resolve truly lies.

—description from the publisher

Hell Hath No Fury is a Tank-centric tabletop miniatures game based on the award-winning NUTS WW2 skirmish game – but with a different focus. 
What makes it different? Glad you asked. 
In Hell Hath No Fury you take the role of a “Star” – a Tank Commander. And each member of your tank’s crew has a job, and each job is covered in the rules.
You can play Hell Hath no Fury against your friends, co-op on the same side, or play solo.
Inside you'll find:


     The same core mechanics used by the NUTS WW2 rules, but Infantry combat has been somewhat abstracted to let you focus on commanding your tanks.
     Rules for all aspects of armored warfare, including special ammo types, penetration as a function of range and target aspect, and more.
     Comprehensive vehicle lists for the major combatants of WW2.
     Stats and counters for a variety of American and German tanks.
     Ten scenarios that will challenge your crew and your decision making.
     An easy to use light bookkeeping Campaign system that links your battles together, where the results of one battle influences the results of the next one.
     Rise in the ranks to command a Platoon or even a Company.


—description from the publisher

Ímpar is a trick-taking game with pieces in which you want to avoid winning tricks, as whoever plays the highest piece wins the trick and scores points—and whoever has scored the fewest points at the end wins the game.

The game's biggest difference lies in its limited information: throughout the game, you can't see the front of your own pieces, only the back—which indicates the suit color and whether the piece is odd or even—while clearly observing your opponent's pieces. This creates unpredictable situations and major twists and turns.

Get ready for a fast-paced, fun, and surprising experience!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Ímpar é um jogo de vaza com peças no qual você quer evitar ganhar vazas, pois quem joga a peça mais alta vence a vaza e marca pontos — e vence a partida quem tiver feito menos pontos ao final.

O grande diferencial do jogo está na informação limitada: durante toda a partida, você não pode ver a frente das suas próprias peças, apenas o verso — que indica a cor do naipe e se a peça é Par ou Ímpar — enquanto observa claramente as peças dos seus oponentes. Isso cria situações imprevisíveis e grandes reviravoltas.

Prepare-se para uma experiência rápida, divertida e cheia de surpresas!

—description from the publisher

Sefirot is a mix of board game and Tarot divination, both playful and meditative. The game features four different play modes for one or two players, using a reversible board and a deck of specially designed Tarot cards. The game is not meant to impress with over-complicated mechanics, but to foster exploration and communication via play.

Silver Seals takes what worked from the first edition, and transmutes it into an elevated form. The three original game modes – Solitaire, Co-operative and Competitive – are lighter, faster and more balanced. The Solitaire board now makes use of the Sun and Moon spaces; the two-player board makes use of Ouroboros connections, allowing flanking maneuvers. The co-operative mode also has more mechanics for card-trading.

In short, a tighter play experience, so you can focus on the cut and thrust of play, and read the cards afterwards.

It also includes Adventure Mode: Dream Odyssey, our new one-player mode,

The player seeks the blessing of  a God or Spirit at the apex of the Tree of Life. Each node holds a challenge: a Tarot character who must be turned to your side via skillful cardplay.

Rock music trivia game, that can be used as extra set for Trivial Pursuit. 1200 questions with six categories: No. 1 Hits, Swinging 60's, Films & Shows, Country & Western, Selection Category and Album Category.

Ever tried organizing a group photo at a festival? With six people, two dead phones, one who's at the loo, and someone who’s definitely not okay? That’s Boaromia Festival. A co-op board game where you and your friends try to survive the most gloriously chaotic festival ever created, without leaving your house, or needing a tetanus shot.

In Boaromia Festival, you’ll chase your favorite artists, dodge an old acquaintance, hit the food court like it’s a quest, forget to pee, and probably end up at the first aid because you forgot to eat. It’s messy, it’s loud, and just like a real festival, you’ll probably forget half of it but tell the stories forever. Your mission? Simple, in theory, hard in execution: Get the whole crew together at the right stage, at the right time, with enough joy in your souls to snap that iconic group photo.

The game is played in 12 rounds  At the start of each round, draw an event card and resolve it. Players take turns (in any order, plan together) and can move up to 6 steps, taking actions along the way or at your destination. At the end of a round: all players lower their energy by 1, move the line-up token forward. At round 7, night begins: replace day goals with night goals, from now on only draw night events.

Players win by snapping a group photo when all players are at the same stage while an artist is performing and everyone has an 8+ fun level.

—description from the publisher

Epic Battle Chess is a grand strategy game set in a medieval realm of kings and castles.

Lead your army on a hexagonal board, blending chess’s core with  innovations: four unique victory paths, a fog of war, units with special abilities, and castle sieges.

Crafted with precise attention to detail, Epic Battle Chess delivers balanced, ever-evolving gameplay that keeps every match exciting.

—description from the designer

boardgame: One Piece: Caccia al Tesoro – Escape Game
description:
Set sail for a grand adventure with ONE PIECE – Escape Game: Treasure Hunt, the official board game based on the Netflix series One Piece! In this narrative, cooperative escape game, you and your companions will explore the East Sea, reliving the most iconic moments from the original story and solving challenging puzzles in perfect escape room style.

More than twenty years after the execution of the legendary pirate Gold Roger, young Monkey D. Luffy sets out on an adventure with a dream: to find the One Piece and become the new Pirate King. To achieve this, he must gather a loyal crew, find a ship, and escape the Navy, always ready to hinder his quest. It's up to you to help him in his search for the treasure, solving puzzles, collecting clues, and overcoming obstacles alongside characters from the series.

A deep and balanced game, set within the mind and subconscious of a person torn by conflicting emotions. The game revolves around the creation of bodies in which players will insert the faces of emotions. As soon as a body is completed, a mysterious being—the Wanderer—will retrace the form in reverse, changing its composition and capturing the memory tokens within.

Two versions are available: “Blue-Violet” and “Red-Yellow”, with the possibility of adding a third and fourth player by combining two boxes.

Palabreak! is a fast-paced party game, perfect for gatherings, coffee breaks, or quick sessions between heavier games. Each round, a set of visible letters is revealed on the table, and all players race in real time to form valid words using them. Succeed and you win the card — but fail, and you'll face a penalty... and plenty of laughs.

With simple rules and a frantic rhythm, Palabreak! creates tension, fun, and memorable moments in quick rounds that always tempt you to play “just one more.”

Goal: Form words using the visible letters to win cards. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Skills involved:
Mental agility
Vocabulary
Quick observation
Decision-making under pressure

Perfect for families, groups of friends, word game lovers, or as a fun breather between more complex games.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Palabreak! es un party game ágil, ideal para reuniones, sobremesas o momentos entre partidas. Cada ronda, se revelan letras visibles en la mesa y todos los jugadores compiten en tiempo real por formar palabras válidas con ellas. Quien lo consigue, gana la carta… pero si fallas, penalización y cachondeo asegurado.

Con reglas sencillas pero un ritmo frenético, Palabreak! genera tensión, risas y momentos memorables en partidas rápidas que invitan a jugar “una más”.

Objetivo: forma palabras usando las letras visibles para ganar cartas. El jugador con más cartas al final de la partida gana.

Habilidades que pone en juego:
Agilidad mental
Vocabulario
Observación rápida
Toma de decisiones bajo presión

Perfecto para familias, grupos de amigos, amantes de los juegos de palabras o como descanso entre juegos más densos.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Duel for Cardia: Ceremonia Promo Card
description: Promo location card for Duel of Cardia: If you have more signets than your opponent, you must play your card face up before your opponent plays their card.

Pas le Choix is a game where you have to guess 3 words out of 12 using 7 given clues. You must use all 7 clues, even the ones that make the task more difficult.
2 game modes: 

     Cooperative: each player tries to make their words known to the whole group, which tries to make as few mistakes as possible.
     Competitive: 2 teams compete on the same grid of 12 words. Each team tries to make fewer mistakes than the opposing team.

Dig Deep is a fast-paced, easy-to-learn card game of bluffing and strategic moves, where every round forces you to balance risk and reward. Outsmart your opponents to claim the most valuable gems and leave them with nothing but heavy gray stones that drain the score.

Points are scored in two ways and tallied when the last card is played:

Take big risks to climb the scoring ladder for greater rewards.

Or secretly amass a hidden fortune, revealed only at the very end when it matters most.

—description from the publisher

Backstabbers is a fast-paced, easy-to-learn card game where players use bluffing, betrayal, and risky tactics to steal the largest share of the loot. Tense stand-offs and sudden reversals make every round unpredictable and fun.

The goal is to deceive your rivals and walk away with the most gold, leaving the others with nothing but crumbs.

Each turn, you can collect gold in different ways: strike a deal and shake hands with your opponent, or try to steal the growing pot for yourself. But beware, your opponent might be on to you, ready to block your move and ruin your plans.

—description from the publisher

At the bustling shipyard of Porto in 1492, six skilled artisans compete to earn their place on the expedition to the New World. Their goal: to gather as many réaux brancos, the currency of the time, by forging connections between the shipyard’s various workshops. Each player takes on the role of a unique character, with their own set of colored dice and starting abilities, offering a different strategic approach from the very beginning.

The core gameplay revolves around capturing cards using colored dice, which in turn create links between workshops. Players must constantly choose between connecting distant workshops with high-value cards, or staying closer and collecting numerous low-value cards. Throughout the game, encounters with non-player characters (NPCs) trigger secret quests, offering bonus réaux at the end of the game for those who complete them.

As the game progresses, players develop their characters by collecting evolution gems, purchasing new dice to expand their tactical options, and unlocking three unique powers that can turn the tide in critical moments.

—description from the designer

A lightweight solo medieval fantasy/sword and sorcery adventure game that lets you create and play an Adventurer in a generic fantasy world. As it is fully self-contained, the game allows you to play adventures in a structured content environment without the use of any oracles or creative input from the player.

The game was made with accessibility, portability, and ease of play in mind, giving you a fantasy adventure fix in short bursts or over extended play session, anytime, anywhere.

Inspiration: Conan, The Elder Scrolls, The Lord of the Rings, NoteQuest, Korg, various other works of fiction and folklore/history of various nations.

—description from the designer

This is one of the promotional cards in a set given away at CMON Expo 2023 in Thailand.

Description:
- (SHADOW) ATTACK: +1
- When attacking, if you have another Mythical Clasp in your Inventory, you are in Shadow.

This is one of the promotional card in a set given away at CMON Expo 2023 in Thailand.

Description
- May be used in the Backpack.
- Once per round, immediately before another Survivor performs a Trade Action with you, you may draw 2 Equipment cards.

boardgame: Zombicide: Undead or Alive – Sheriff's Badge
description:
This is one of the promotional cards in a set given away at CMON Expo 2023 in Thailand.

Description
- Maybe used in the backpack.
- Once per Round, during your Turn, you may give 1 free Combat Action to another Survivor in Line of Sight. This Action is used immediately. Then, resume your turn.

Sky Bloom: Clusters is a fast-paced, competitive tile-laying and area-claiming game for 2-4 players, where flowers grow, rivalries blossom, and control is always up for grabs.

Place tiles to grow vibrant clusters of matching flowers, then choose to stake your claim by placing your bee token on top or boost the value of a bloom for all, raising the stakes sky-high?
But beware, claimed clusters are never safe. Rivals can build and expand your flower clusters and stack their bee tokens to vie for control.

When the final bee is placed, only one player will rise above the rest, proving themselves the reigning champion in this easy-to-learn, impossible-to-master area control game.

— description from the publisher Merchants of Play

My Liege is a fusion of two classic games, Chess and Rummy, but without a classic game board. Played entirely with cards, My Liege includes a variety of strategic pathways to victory. The cards themselves serve several key functions and may be played as strategic abilities, matching sets to recruit familiar chess pieces, or played to the table as physical territory. players must manage their medieval vassals from two matching decks of cards to expand their fiefdom, build strategic locations across their kingdom, and ultimately lay waste to the enemy by destroying their King. There can only be one Liege!

Besides medieval vassals aiding in your fight, players also choose locations to change and expand the game board. Locations don’t act just as Unit spawn locations, they also change the board in dynamic ways. Castles may be built as defensive positions. Chapels are sacred ground and may block an opponents pathway to attack. Build villages to lengthen your turn with additional cards.

Explore the outer reaches of the galaxy in search of a new Earth.Project: New Earth is a survival and space exploration game that can be played solo or cooperatively, in which you play as one of the senior officers of a human colonisation expedition.

You were awakened during the journey by the ship's automated systems due to the deactivation of the main AI. You will have to complete the expedition by exploring different stars. You will need to wake up your crew members to command, improve and secure your ship on its journey.
You will face many challenges before achieving your goal of discovering a colonisable planet, the new Earth...

The game is turn-based, and you will need to manage your crew to:


     Navigate among the stars and explore unknown planetary systems with over 350 narrative events.
     Unlock research features from your laboratory with your research team.
     Mine, craft advanced equipment, and reinforce your ship with the help of your technical team.
     Secure your ship with your security forces.
     Face numerous dangers. Discover baffling cosmic phenomena. And be sure to neutralise any unwanted visitors who see your ship as their new hunting ground or a place for destruction or nesting.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: A Study in Terror Game Supplement
description:
The deepest depths of Darkness hold the greatest treasures. But they are also a slippery slope into madness and despair. Perhaps if you can learn more about the Void and the nightmares that dwell within its unfathomably vast domain, you could find new ways to fight the Darkness...if you can control your greatest fears. But navigating this perilous precipice will be no easy task, and it is not for the weak willed. Your sanity will be taken to the limits, or beyond. Best to bring extra Whiskey.

A Study in Terror allows your Hero Party with a Medical, Science, or Soldier Hero to descend into the darkest depths of their nightmares, turning any Mission into A Study in Terror, with a great potential for rewards at the cost of your sanity! This 15 card Supplement includes the A Study in Terror Adventure card outlining the mission modifiers and the Lurking Horrors Enemy Trait that can be assigned to any Enemies for greater challenge, as well as a new set of Encounters themed for reality-bending nightmares, and an all new deck of Field Study cards giving your Medical and Science Heroes powerful and rewarding personal research-themed side quests during any Adventure.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Shikarri Void Pirates Enemy Pack
description:
When the planet Shikarr was shattered and the advanced civilization of the Shikarri was consumed by the untamed Void energy of the Dark Stone, its people were all but destroyed along with it. Those that escaped were scattered, displaced across the stars. For many, they swore an oath to destroy the Dark Stone and fight the corruption of the Darkness to their last breath. For others, they turned to scavenging and piracy to survive, armed with what remained of their advanced technology. Using the Void to travel great distances in their Hyper-runners, and taking what they needed from any they encountered; interstellar outlaws, living one step ahead of the Darkness.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to encounter the ruthless Shikarri Void Pirates. Included are 6 Shikarri Void Pirate models, the large Enemy Record Sheet for them, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add these cutthroat Enemies to your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

Models are Thermal Plastic Miniatures manufactured in the USA. Figures supplied unassembled and unpainted.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Wasteland Warmasters Enemy Supplement #3
description:
The Wasteland Scavengers are a motley crew of criminals, cutthroats, and raiders! Brutal warriors lead by powerful Warlords that don’t just want to survive in the barren and lawless wastes, but rather to thrive there! Some Warlords gain a fearsome reputation and high level of infamy from their savage exploits. These most feared Warlords are known as Wasteland Warmasters!

This set adds new large Enemy Record Sheets for 2 of the dangerous Wasteland Warmasters –Hy’Ra Jin, The Bone King and Ke’Dula Ven, a brand new Wasteland Warmaster not originally in the Blasted Wastes OtherWorld Expansion! Also includes Threat Cards to bring these Warmasters and their armies into your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

This game supplement enhances the Blasted Wastes OtherWorld Expansion as well as the Enemy Figures for Hy’Ra Jin, The Bone King and Ke’Dula Ven. That OtherWorld Expansion and those thermal plastic Miniatures are NOT INCLUDED, but sold separately.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Xenocyte Devourers Enemy Pack
description:
The shadowed corridors of the Derelict Ship hide many terrors. As shapes slither and twist in the dark, the cold of space closes in from all around. Lurching out from the deepest holds of the vast craft, the Xenocyte Devourers stalk the halls in search of biomass to feed on. A cavernous maw, ringed with teeth and surrounded by articulated mandibles, lies at the heart of these towering creatures, capable of devouring anything that falls within its jaws. As these vicious aliens close in on their prey, their long hooked tendril arms launch out to pull potential meals into their hungry maws.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to encounter the deadly Xenocyte Devourers. Included are 3 large Xenocyte Devourer models, 3 Derelict Ship Themed 40 mm bases, the large Enemy Record Sheet for them, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add these vicious alien Enemies to your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

Models are Thermal Plastic Miniatures manufactured in the USA. Figures supplied unassembled and unpainted.

—description from the publisher

Nature is a streamlined, deeper, and more forgiving redesign of the blockbuster Evolution, built from the ground up to be seamlessly expandable. Its core innovation is a modular system that lets players create and explore a different ecosystem every time they play.

To showcase this modularity, Nature was first released on Kickstarter as the Nature Big Box edition. Nature Big Box includes the core game, the first five modules, and one mini-module.

Adapt to a Changing Environment:
In Nature, players compete to grow thriving populations in an ecosystem where food is scarce and predators are ever-present. Evolve traits such as Fast to escape danger, Climbing to reach fruit high in the canopy, or Pack Hunting to bring down larger prey.

Create a Unique Ecosystem Every Game:
Nature core is an accessible gateway into a world of dynamic ecosystems. Despite its strategic depth, Nature can be taught and played with casual gamers in under an hour. Experienced players can customize each session using modules that dramatically change the ecosystem. Add Nature: Flight for people who prefer to avoid conflict, Nature: Jurassic for a more aggressive game, or Nature: Arctic Tundra for a survival game on the edge of the livable world.

With five modules and two mini-modules, Nature currently offers 96 possible ecosystem combinations. Following the 2026 release of the Climate module and two additional mini-modules, players will be able to explore over 700 unique ecosystems.

How to Play:
Nature is a card-driven tableau builder played over four rounds (five rounds when using one or more modules). Each round is divided into two phases: the Adapting Phase and the Feeding Phase.

Adapting Phase:
Use five cards as actions to:
 - Increase Population
 - Increase Body Size
 - Play Traits to evolve your species
 - Save cards for use as food during the Feeding Phase

Feeding Phase:
Feed as much of your population as possible. At the end of each round, players score 1 point for every population they keep alive. The winner is the player who sustains the most population across the game.


Nature Digital: Learn to play with an interactive tutorial on the free-to-try Nature app (iOS, Android, and Steam).
https://www.naturedigitalgame.com/


A Living Game System:
NorthStar Studio will release the Climate module in 2026, followed by one to two new modules per year for the next decade.
Vote for future modules here:
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/308/blogpost/166360/future-nature-modules-poll


Designer Diary
Dominic Crapuchettes discusses the inspiration behind Nature, which began during his time as a professional Magic player.
https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/1/blogpost/179068/designer-diary-the-evolution-of-nature

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Challenge Pack #4 – The Oathbreaker King
description:
A treacherous lord of the North, cursed beyond death for his broken oaths to friend and ally, stories of the Oathbreaker King have been told through the ages. Once resplendent in his gleaming armor and shining crown, the glory of this once noble warrior fell into darkness. Now he is cursed to rise from his forsaken tomb, tainted relics in hand, to haunt the mountains he once called home, bringing death and decay to all he touches.

Challenge Pack #4 – The Oathbreaker King is themed for taking on an Epic Undead Norse king of old with a powerful set of Death Cursed Relics and surrounded by cursed oathbreakers from which he gains the benefit of all Broken Oaths currently in play. This set also includes the New Mission: "Hail to the King!"

A Challenge Pack is a themed set of cards with a terrifying new Enemy and a nightmarish new Mission designed to give the Heroes an extra challenge. These cards are all used together for the included Mission, but can also be added to your card decks to increase the challenge across your normal games of Shadows of Brimstone as well. The increased difficulty cards are marked with a Challenge Icon so you can easily spot them once mixed into your decks.

This set also assumes you have access to various expansions such as the Swamps of Death Core Set, Gates of Valhalla Adventure Set, Fenris Wolves, Draugar Undead Vikings, and Oathbreaker Axemen Enemy packs, and the Forest of the Dead OtherWorld Expansion.

Spectres of a Broken Oath

This set includes a new Trait card called Spectres of a Broken Oath that can be used to modify any Undead Enemies to make them Oathbreakers. This Trait card is used as part of the included Mission, but can also be applied to Enemies in your normal games of Shadows of Brimstone, if you want to add extra challenge to a Fight, or to theme a Mission around battling these treacherous foes! Spectres of a Broken Oath gives the Enemies it is applied to the Oathbreaker Keyword, as well as a Health boost, and allows them to draw a Broken Oath card to see what treachery haunts them in death.

Challenge Packs reward players for having a robust collection of Enemies and Expansions; and contain several Threat cards that include a mix of various Enemies from across the game.

—description from the publisher

Let learning games transform time into an adventure kids will want to go on again and again!

The color matching game will help kids practice the skills they need to succeed, while making learning a positive experience they're excited to continue.

Kids will love playing to earn points, win games, and set high scores. You will love seeing their knowledge grow!

—description from the box

This is a tile laying game. Each tile has 4 colors. Matching colors earns points. The more matching colors in a groups the more points it scores.

Nyolc új kérdéskártya magyar nyelven, hogy még tovább nyújtson maximális élményt a játék.

This promo deck adds 8 new cards to the game for more variety. 
Limited Hungarian promo.
Beast: The Lost Contracts features 55 pages of select fanmade contracts from Custom Contract Website.

Bust the viruses in this climbing game! Players play cards as Viruses or as Cures. Each time a Virus is defeated by a Cure, add 1 to the score. When 4 Viruses are added to the defeated area, the Cures become a Wild and can be used with any other Cure cards. Virus Busters challenges players to work together at times to play cards so the Cures can beat the Viruses, but the player who triggers the end of the round does so with an empty hand and will score more points than their opponents. Add in role cards for bonus points or discard them to defeat a Virus and save the team.

Players will find plenty of challenge in the mechanisms as they strive to keep the number of cards low while also getting Viruses cleared out.

Do you have what it takes to Bust the Viruses?!

—description from the designer

A hilarious game of words and birds.
Through skill, chance, and strategy, be the fastest to assemble your bird by playing various word games. Originality pays off, but watch out for the other birds, trying to slow you down!

Each player starts with 7 black cards. One player is the Judge for the first round.

The Judge draws a red card with a number in the top left corner. Counting clockwise, the player the number lands on becomes the subject of the round. If the count lands on the Judge, then the person to the left of the Judge is the subject.

The Judge reads the red card aloud, inserting the subject’s name where [Name] appears. If necessary, the Judge adjusts the wording to match the subject's gender.

Each player, including the subject, (except the Judge) submits one face-down black card as the subject’s karma.

The Judge shuffles the black cards and reads the red card aloud again. Then, the Judge reads aloud each black card, inserting the subject’s name.

The Judge picks the best black card as the subject’s karma. The winning player keeps the red card.

The player to the Judge’s left becomes the new Judge. All players draw a new black card, and the next round begins.

The first to 5 red cards wins!

—description from the designer

Behind the Trenches is a World War 1 resource management, deck builder game. The goal is to push the front line into your opponents HQ.

Play consists of 3 Phases:


    Battle Phase: Deal 5 cards from the unclaimed deck (80 card deck to begin the game). Use numbered dog tags to fight over cards against your opponent. The player with the highest accumulated score adds that card to their discard pile.  



    Country Phase: Each country gains a unique set of resources, has a few unique processes like building a trench or turning one resource into another. Most importantly it's where you build your guns and train your troops (i.e. adding a basic infantry card to your discard pile).



    War Phase: Shuffle the discard pile and form a deck.  Now draw 4. Take turns with your opponent playing one card at a time to gain resources, dig trenches, bombard enemy entrenchments, and push the front line forward using human wave attacks.


Once both players hands are empty, players check their decks.  
If both players have at least one card, they both draw another 4 cards (or at least attempt to draw 4 more cards) 
If either player's deck has 0 cards, the war phase ends and a new battle phase begins.

This continues until the front line is pushed into a players HQ.

—description from the publisher

Cornerlang is a highly political and significantly historical Australian game produced during the 1930's depression years.

The game pits the Premier of New South Wales "Jack Lang" against the "New Guard"; Australia's most successful right-wing, fascist paramilitary organisation.

The New Guard became infamous in the 1930's for street violence on communism, trade unions, and the left-leaning policies of Lang. It promoted loyalty to the British Empire and conservative values.

The most dramatic protest was when member Captain Francis de Groot, mounted on horseback, slashed the ribbon with his sword at the opening of the Sydney Harbour Bridge in 1932.

In Art Deco style the gameboard beautifully displays the new Sydney Harbour Bridge. There are 29 spaces for Lang or the New Guard to move on the bridge. Lang has 3 men (red counters), while the New Guard have 20 men (blue counters).

Players place the men in turn on the spaces. Lang's men can jump and capture men of the New Guard. As men from the New Guard are placed on the Bridge they try and close in and corner Lang.

The game is concluded if the New Guard can corner all of Lang's men or if Lang's men can capture 4 of the New Guard.

From the Focus on the Family organization and their 'Adventures in Odyssey' series.

"A Wildly Fun Family Card Adventure"

Basic game play looks like an UNO variant, but it adds some special action cards that add some family friendly actions...


     Buried Treasure - Share something about yourself that others in the game don't know. Give a card to any player.



     Royal Treatment - Play this card and next person must give you a compliment. If they refuse they draw 4.



     Words of Life - Recite a Bible verse successfully and you can trade hands with another.



     Lasting Memories - Next player must share a highlight from their day. If they do not they draw 2.

Football. American rules football.

You know what we’re talking about. Eleven players on one side of the line trying to enforce their will on eleven players on the other side of the line. The sport where if one player doesn’t do its job then all eleven have failed.

You’ve seen it on TV, maybe been to a game. Heck, some of you may have played the game at one level or another. If you have, no matter at what level, you know it can come down to one play.
It’s 4th and 1 – Whatcha gonna do?

THW Football is a game where each Player has three roles:

     Offensive Coordinator – You choose which Play the Offense will run.
     Defensive Coordinator – You set the Defense for each Play.
     Head Coach – You make the important decisions during the Game; when to Punt, Onside Kick, etc...


Inside you'll find: . Four complete Football Teams.

     A Large Field and Scoreboard to track the Ball, the Play, Timeouts and more.
     The chance to be the Head Coach where you Call the Plays, Set the Defense and make the decisions that will let you win or lose the game.
     100+ Player Counters to let you Set and Shift the Defense as well and decide who starts at what position.
     A complete Card Deck that lets you choose the Offensive Play as in who's Running the Ball or Catching the Pass.


All this and much more.

THW Football is designed for solo play. 
You can play it co-op and head-to-head as well but no matter which way you play, you must make the best decisions to win the game. 
Don’t be fooled. You may roll dice in THW Football, but luck will play a small part in the results!

—description from the publisher

Train Race Game is a simple roll and move game where players follow and move counters along a Train track of 65 spaces. The track has certain spaces where train accidents occur and players are penalised.

The first to reach home after the 65 spaces is the winner.

Size Matters is a reaction and comparison game where size and speed are what count! In a 3x3 grid of cards, various objects are displayed: an ant, a house, a penny, an atom, a sun, and so on. All objects have clearly distinguishable sizes. Next to the grid is a task card that specifies what needs to be found: the smallest object, the largest object, the second smallest, etc.
Everyone plays at the same time and must quickly hit the correct object. Whoever performs best over several rounds wins Size Matters!

—description from the publisher

Schätz it – Junior is the cooperative version of the popular base game Schätz it – If you can, specially designed for younger players. Unlike the original game, players work together to agree on the correct order of the items and try to score as many points as possible.

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Eureka is an Australian made resource management board game which brings fun to the whole family. Build your mining company and compete against your friends to become the wealthiest mining company before the mine collapses. Just watch out for the dragon that lives there!

In Eureka, 2-4 players compete against each other to amass the most wealth before the entire mine collapses. Hire miners and send them deep into the cave system to bring back minerals which can be sold on the ever-changing market. Will you chose to keep your miners near the safety of your mineshaft where they will dig tin and copper, or will you send them further into the mine to gather gold and silver near the danger of the dragon's lair?

—description from the designer

boardgame: ウチューカイギの拡張〜オウとの邂逅〜 (Interspace Conference on Earth: Encounter with Leizy)
description:
This is an expansion for the trick-taking game Interspace Conference on Earth, introducing a new alien card: "Ouchakku."

The first Interspace Conference held on Earth has come to a close. But just as Earthlings breathe a sigh of relief, the planetary representatives—deeply impressed by the warm hospitality from nations across the globe—decide to hold the next Interspace Conference on Earth once again.
It is then that humanity learns: there was yet another alien—one who was supposed to attend the conference all along, but whose presence remained unknown… until now.

With this expansion, you'll now play with seven types of alien cards.
The newly introduced alien, Ouchakku, is a being of exceptional intelligence and the ultimate hedonist.
Their card comes with a unique ability that reflects this extravagant personality.

This expansion adds a new twist to the chaos and drama of Interspace Conference on Earth, bringing even more excitement for the Earthlings at the table.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In the game Letterjacht ("Letter Hunt"), each player tries to collect letter tiles needed to spell a four-letter word they've secretly chosen at the beginning of the game.

Each turn, a player rolls the die, and move their pawn the indicated number of spaces on the board. They'll land on a space associated with a specific letter, or a letter hunt space.

If they land on a letter space, the player has to answer a trivia question (of which the answer is a word starting with the corresponding letter). If they are successful, they gain a matching letter tile. If they don't know the answer, they may choose pass the question to another player. If that player answers correctly, both players get the matching letter tile, but if not, they both lose a letter tile.

On a letter hunt space, a player may trade a letter tile with another player. If no trade is made, they can instead answer a trivia question for a letter of their choice, gaining the matching the letter tile if they answer correctly.

If a player has gained all the tiles needed to complete their word, they need to move their pawn to their home space. The first player to reach their home space after completing their word wins the game.

The game has three different levels of difficulty, and different players can play at different difficulty levels.

—user summary

Go Viking: A Jarl’s Tale is a solo campaign expansion for Go Viking, introducing a full narrative experience designed for one player.

A lone Jarl must carve their legend across the North, forging their destiny through battle, wisdom, and the will of the gods. This campaign includes a sequence of interconnected solo scenarios that evolve over time, with branching decisions and mythic challenges.

—description from the publisher

Go Viking: Reign of Heroes is a modular expansion for Go Viking that introduces divine powers, new player identities, and asymmetry through two gameplay modules.

In the age of heroes, no Viking fights alone. The gods grant new power, and the clans rise to stake their claim.

This boxed expansion contains two modules that can be added independently or combined:

— Legends of the Fjords  
A character-driven module that adds three special Destiny Dice representing iconic Viking roles: Berserker, Seafarer, and Pillager. With the wisdom of Mimir, players may call upon these dice on their turn to gain powerful tactical benefits.

— Rise of the Clans  
The Clans have risen. Each bearing ancient legacies and secret strengths. This module introduces six Clan Boards and asymmetric clan powers. With Tyr’s Honor and your clan’s pride at stake, wield your influence wisely.

These modules can be used separately or together for an expanded Viking saga.

—description from the publisher

Originally available at Gen Con 2025.

Content and rules allowing up to 4 players to run a 3-game Sagrada mini-tournament. 
Each game uses 1 of the 3 Great Facades expansions.

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Blue Jay / Beaver Promo Card
description:
P029
Blue Jay: Bird, Cost 1 card. 3 points. Matching color paid: play a tree from hand.

Beaver: Paw print animal, Cost 2 cards. 1 point per card in your cave. Immediate effect: put all trees from the clearing into your cave.

Can you play without looking?
Velocitactor is a tactile speed game played with fingers, not eyes. The goal is to identify geometric combinations on embossed cards using only the sense of touch.

Each round begins by revealing three tactile patterns on special tokens. Then, all players simultaneously search their personal deck for the card that matches those three patterns—using only their fingertips.

The first player to find the correct card wins the round and earns a point token. The game continues until all tokens have been used. The player with the best tactile reflexes wins.

Designed as an inclusive sensory experience, Velocitactor can be played by people with or without vision. It encourages focus, tactile perception, and quick reaction speed.

—description from the publisher

¿Podés jugar sin mirar?
Velocitactor es un juego de velocidad táctil donde se juega con los dedos, no con los ojos. El objetivo es identificar combinaciones geométricas en cartas en relieve, utilizando exclusivamente el sentido del tacto.

Cada ronda comienza con el lanzamiento de fichas que muestran tres patrones táctiles. Luego, todos los jugadores buscan al mismo tiempo la carta en su mazo que contenga esos tres patrones, confiando únicamente en el tacto de sus yemas.

El primer jugador en encontrar la carta correcta gana la ronda y una ficha de punto. El juego continúa hasta que se acaban las fichas. Gana quien haya demostrado mejores reflejos táctiles.

Diseñado como una experiencia sensorial inclusiva, Velocitactor puede ser jugado por personas con o sin visión. También promueve la concentración, la percepción táctil y la rapidez de respuesta.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

An upcoming vast expansive Role-Playing Game where players will fight against fierce enemies with a unique combat system that does not rely on dice rolls and luck.

The game features a legacy story that evolves with every quest. It plans to include up to 4 expansions that expand the game and it's story which plans to keep the game budget friendly while including tons of new content to expand the game.

There will be tons of ways to interact with the large world it plans to offer from instanced dungeons with bosses to random unique encounters that create new events the players can explore or ignore.

NUTS Weird War NUTS Weird War! is a complete set of tabletop rules for Weird War Two gaming based on the NUTS rules. 
This is a standalone book designed with Solo play in mind (and supports Co-op and Head to Head) for any type of Weirdness you want to. 
Face hordes of zombies and eldritch horrors in your combat mech –cast spells, duke it out with super soldiers with your electric rifle while flying around with your rocket pack. 
You can fight any weird foe you can image! Inside you’ll find:


     Rules for infantry combat with over 15 different formations.
     Rules for vehicle and Mecha combat with stats for over 100 types, including historical vehicles, “paper” vehicles and super science mecha, Hanebu and Robots.
     Gas weapons, ray guns, electric cannon, missiles rocket packs.
     Six armies to choose from – American, British, German, Italian, Japanese and Russian .
     Unique attributes to personalize your squad members.
     Design your own Super Soldiers.
     Magic and the Occult, from Movie Monsters to Eldritch Horror.
     Insanity and Madness.
     A minimal bookkeeping Campaign System that gives meaning to every game as the results of one will affect the outcome of the next.
     Special rules for playing DUST, Gear Krieg and Mekatank units.
     Create your own Weird Science campaigns, and some sample scenarios that may be fought head-to-head with players taking opposing sides, cooperatively against the game or solo as either side.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: In the Land of the Sundering Flood: Inspired by the Writings of William Morris
description:
In the land of the Sundering flood is a miniature adventure game, set in a magical world inspired by the fantasy writings of arts and crafts visionary William Morris. 
In the rulebook you'll find all the rules for a miniature adventure game, 20 unique character profiles, 4 scenarios, and an introduction to the lands and peoples of the land of the flood.

All the illustrations and borders in this book are hand drawn in pen and ink, and all fonts used are authentic arts and crafts fonts.

boardgame: CELEBSAID: Quotes of the Rich and Famous
description:
The game that revives your favorite quotes, catchphrases and lines from the rich and famous that exemplifies the freedom of speech. 
You are ready to play this game if you know these quotes... 
He shot me in my pinky toe. 
If the glove doesn't fit, you must acquit. 
King Kong ain't got sh*t on me. 
You ain't got to lie, Craig.

86 Quote cards, 4 Go cards and 16 Decision cards.

Instructions: 
Pick a host responsible for reading Quote cards. 
Form a minimum of 3 teams. 
Give each team one Go card and a set of A to D Decision cards. 
When the host starts to read a card, the first team to raise their Go Card can blurt the answer out. 
If that team blurts an incorrect answer, they forfeit their turn for that question, giving other teams the chance to listen to the entire question and use their Decision cards to pick an answer. 
One point is added to a team for each correct answer and one point is subtracted for each incorrect answer. 
Answers are in gold text.

The first team to reach 10 points wins.

If a Go card is not raised the host will read the card in its entirety. 
The host will prompt teams when to show their Decision cards.

—description from the publisher

WORDSMITHS WORKSHOP is a collection of two dry erase word games for two or more players plus solo.

BIGRAM BONANZA & LUCKY LETTERS are both printed on one two-sided dry erase card.

In these two, fast-paced, dice-driven word games, players risk it all each turn to grab hot letter pairings or single letters - then cash them in for words and points.

Both games share a core push-your-luck engine but feature distinct grids and dice-rolling twists.

Bigram Bonanza Rules

This word-building game is controlled by die rolls. Write down and score 4-letter words by combining pairs of bigrams.

SETUP: Give each player their own card and a dry erase marker. Players will all be playing simultaneously – so no waiting.

GAME PLAY: 
1. A player rolls both dice. 
2. Pick two available bigrams from the boxes matching the dice rolled. If doubles are rolled, pick any two available bigrams.
3. Combine the two bigrams in any order and call out your valid 4-letter word.
4. Write down your word.
5. Mark off the two bigrams used for your word to prevent reuse. 
6. Score your word and record its value.

Repeat until you have made five 4-letter words, wiping off the marks in your boxes in between rounds.

The player with the highest total score is the winner.

Play three rounds to see who is the overall winner.

Lucky Letters Rules

This word-building game is controlled by a single die roll. Form five valid words by strategically collecting one letter at a time.

SETUP: Give each player their own card and a dry erase marker. Players will all be playing simultaneously – so no waiting.

GAME PLAY: 
1. A player rolls the die to select one of the six boxes of letters matching the die roll. 
2. Pick one letter from that box and write it at the bottom of your card. 
3. Mark off the letter selected to prevent it from being reused in this round.

Repeat building your word until:

- A player makes a valid 5-letter word and optionally calls out “game over”.  One more die roll gives everyone a final attempt to complete their word.
 - Someone makes a valid 6-letter word causing the round to be over immediately.
 - All players can no longer form a word after three die rolls.
4. Score your valid word (of at least 3 letters) and record its value.
5. Wipe off your board except the scores.

Play five rounds.

The winner will have the highest total after five words. In case of a tie, play another round.

OPTION: For a quicker game, roll two dice and select two letters.

—description from the publisher

Zombies jump, zombies leap… but zombies never die—they just keep coming back!
Can you use their undead powers to outwit your opponent?

Jumping over an opponent destroys their zombie and scores points.
Jumping onto your own zombie fuses them together and powers them up!
But beware—defeated zombies can revive anywhere… maybe at your doorstep… or behind your back!
Stay sharp to win this battle of the undead.

—description from the designer

You’re too busy, so you send your five dice-cats to buy fish for you.
But… they’re cats. And dice. Totally uncontrollable.
Worse yet, they only go out once a week.

Try to tame your mischievous dice-cats to bring home the most delicious, high-scoring fish!
But beware… always watch out… don’t end up with an octopus!

Each round consists of four bidding turns.
Players roll their remaining dice, and the highest total wins the fish of that turn.
Used dice can’t be rolled again—so how you manage five differently valued dice is the key.

One of the turns is Octopus Time—and whoever rolls the lowest will get tangled in trouble!
If you roll exactly double your opponent’s score, you take *all* the fish on the table!
Use powerful skill cards to reroll or boost your dice—luck alone won’t be enough!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Win or Die: Racing the Wasteland
description:
Win or Die is a complete stand-alone game designed to be played solo or head-to-head. 
Win or Die adds Vehicles to your ATZ and Post Apocalyptic games. 
Drive down the road and there's no telling what or who you can run into. 
Chase them? Yep. 
Bash them? Yep. 
Shoot them? Yep, you can do all that and even more. Like what?

How about racing for fame and fortune. 
Wait, it's also a car racing game? Yep.

Win or Die is not the traditional dice and mice type of racing game. You know the kind where you roll dice, move around a track, fall behind and have long stretches of boredom. In Win or Die it’s where you are in the Pack and not where you are on the Track and every turn is important.

The game is easy to play, but tough to master. Luck will play its part, but the decisions you make are more important. Knowing when to use Bonus Dice, when to attempt to pass, bash or shoot, yes shoot, or not, can be the difference between gaining Fame or ending up wrecked on the track and barely alive if you even are.

You can play a one-off game where you have one Race and what happens, happens. Or you can choose to keep the same Driver, you, and have multiple Races that tie into each other, where the goal is to gain Fame…and stay alive.

Remember…
It’s not where you are on the Track; it’s where you are in the Pack!

—description from the publisher

A peaceful medieval builder - no war, just thoughtful planning and adapting to the changing needs of your growing town. Step into the shoes of a noble, raise your settlement from scratch, and watch it flourish into a proud domain.

Your goal is simple: develop your town to its highest potential. Earn golden goblets by constructing buildings — a mark of your rising prestige.

Each turn, roll three dice and assign them to one of three actions:

    Production brings in resources.
    Transportation moves those resources, helping you build, log by log, brick by brick.
    Edict reflects the king’s decree — whether it’s new jobs, taxes, or other duties. Ignore an edict, and your loyalty drops. Fall below 1, and your reign ends.


You have one year — 52 turns — to make your mark. Can your humble fief become the envy of the realm?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cross vs. Crescent: The Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa, July 16, 1212
description:
Following the Third Crusade the kings of Castile, Aragon and Portugal appealed to Pope Innocent III to call for another crusade against the Moors occupying the southern portion of the Iberian peninsula. This was granted and the monarchs assembled an army, launching an offensive in the spring of 1212. Almohad Caliph al-Nasr (called Miramamolín by Christian chroniclers) assembled a much larger army in response drawn from the occupied portions of Spain as well as North Africa to meet the infidels. However, the Christian force, thanks to the assistance of a local shepherd, marched through an unknown mountain pass emerging along the Almohad Army’s line of communications to their capital in Córdoba. What ensued was a huge and bloody battle that marked the start of the Spanish Reconquista.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

—description from the designer
A classic Monopoly based on Saalfeld, Rudolstadt and Bad Blankenburg. The worlds first three cities edition.

The Assyrian Empire with its capital Assur had a formative influence on all advanced civilizations along the Euphrates and Tigris rivers
shaping and influenting Mesopotamian culture for centuries. The influence of this mighty empire can be seen in the so-called “Assur
disks”. Always place new Assur disks diagonally to each other. Each disk you place covers an feature printed on the landscape: be it a
mountain, a forest, a sanctuary, an invention, or the course of a river. Place a new disk and immediately receive a score for it.

Expansion for Papyria that adds a component with an additional "Assur" scoring when revealing an end-of-game tile.

Hydros! The sound of your engine drowns out everything else.

You barely made it to the Final Heat, and then only by winning the Provisional. 
But all that’s behind you as now it’s a “winner take all” race. As you move down the course, you see the other boats maneuvering for position. 
You ready yourself for the gun. 
There it is! 
The boats come alive, and you feel the familiar push back into your seat as the race starts and you open the throttle. 
Three laps and five boats stand between you and the fame and fortune you’ve worked your whole life for.

Unlimited is a complete boardgame of high performance Hydroplane racing craft and includes: Rules Boat counters Racing Track and Position track

Welcome to the world of… Unlimited

—description from the publisher

Monopoly: La Casa de Papel (Money Heist Edition)
Join the iconic heist! In this thrilling edition of Monopoly, players take on the roles of the Professor’s crew, each with a unique ability. Instead of properties, you compete to control locations like the Royal Mint and the Bank, collecting Gold Bullion and Bags of Cash. Navigate themed event cards, negotiate with other players, evade the Police, and attempt to outsmart opponents—all to build the largest fortune and win. Designed for 2–6 players (ages 12+), with playtimes ranging from 1 to 2 hours. This variant merges classic Monopoly mechanics with the tension and excitement of Money Heist.

boardgame: 15 Jours En France: Fête du jeu de Bordeaux
description:
Promo card for 15 Jours en France, available at Bordeaux during the Fête du jeu de Bordeaux.
The illustation represent the Fête du jeu de Bordeaux, a board game Festival happening in May in Bordeaux, France, and it counts as 1 cultural space.

boardgame: Bull Riding!: A Complete Game of Competitive Bull Riding
description:
Bull Rider - 8 Seconds to Glory!

The Rider is ready – LET’S GO! 
The gate opens and the bull spins out into the ring bucking and kicking as the rider hangs on for eight seconds to glory!

Bull Riding is a complete mini-game you can play solo or with your friends. 
You can build your rider’s skills and ride to glory in a simple campaign that puts you on the back of a Bull!

Want to ride Horses?
Of course you can! 
Just call them Horses. LOL

This includes all the rules you need to play, including Rider tokens, Bulls with their own challenging quirks, and game tokens.

“A game about Bull Riding? Really?”

Yep, come get some!

Turn Sequence:

     RIDE DICE – The Rider declares how many Ride


Dice he will roll that turn and how many Ride Dice he will give to the Bull to roll that turn for Style Points.
