     BULL MANEUVER TABLE – The Bull now rolls on the Bull Maneuver Table. Draw Score Cards to match the number of points scored.
     RIDE TABLE – The Rider and Bull roll on the Ride Table


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kung-Fu Fighting!: Quickand Easy Standalone Kung-Fu Fighting Game
description:
Everybody was Kung Fu Fighting!

You know what we’re talking about. 
Two martial artists squaring off against one another. 
Unless you’re Bruce Lee then you’re fighting everyone at once!

Kung Fu Fighting! is a quick and easy fight game that is standalone but can be used with lots of THW rules. Pulp PI, 5150 New Beginnings, heck any rules you want.

Kung Fu Fighting revolves around two Fighters fighting till only one remains standing.

Let’s get started!

Kung Fu Fighting is a complete boardgame of martial arts sports tournaments and includes: 

     Rules 
     Counters 
     Competition Ring 


Welcome to the world of… Kung Fu Fighting!

"Turn Sequence:

Each fight will last an unlimited number of turns, moving from Table to Table until one Fighter is Knocked Out. Here’s how we do it:

     The fighters enter the center of the Ring, red dashed square, facing each other.
     The Fight starts with the Fighters rolling 2d6 or more versus their Rep on the Fight Table.
     If neither Fighter passes more d6, the Fighters stay on the Fight Table in the center of the Ring.
     If one Fighter passes more d6, they will go to the Taking Control Table. The Fighter that passed more d6 is the Winner, the other the Loser. The Loser is pushed out of the center of the Ring and the winner follows.
     The Fighters continue to move from Kung Fu Table to Kung Fu Table until they return to the Fight Table and the center of the Ring. Replenish Bonus dice up to current Rep


—description from the publisher

Words is a board game where players form words with given letters in a limited time to score points.

Place all the dice inside the dice holder and shake it to mix them well. Make sure they are all in their slots. Remove the lid, turn the hourglass over, and the game begins. All players, equipped with pencil and paper, must try to find as many words as possible, with a minimum of 3 letters. The longer and more original the words, the better the score. Words can be formed in all directions: horizontally, vertically, diagonally, forwards, backwards, and in a zigzag pattern. You cannot skip places, nor can you retrace the same dice. When time is up, all players, from right to left, read the words they have each found. If there is any doubt about the meaning of any of them, you can consult a dictionary.
Words common to two or more players are discarded. The remaining words are scored as follows:
3 letters = 2 points.
4 letters = 3 points.
5 or more letters = 4 points.
Proper names, abbreviations, and words from other languages ​​are not allowed. But you can play the game in another language if you feel up to it.

It's up to you to match the camel with the barbecue sausage or the dishes with the dollar bills. If you can convince the others of the perfect match, you're well on your way to the goal - but you only win if you can also appreciate the other people's matches.

Match! is more fun than strategy, and it doesn't require dice or prior knowledge. Both children and adults can play, because it's just about how we each see the world around us!

The game takes 4 minutes to learn, contains more than 11,000 possible matches and provides fun and thought for many hours!

—description from the publisher

Take on the role of a captain and fight epic sci-fi skirmishes with these detailed, but easy to use, wargaming rules.

Enderain is a tactically challenging game that allows you to develop diverse Team structures to suit your playing style and arm them accordingly.

Easy to play, difficult to master, and with full co-op or solo play, Enderain can be used in any sci-fi setting.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Crimson Queen: The Battle for Wonderland Combo Edition
description:
Step into a twisted, enchanted version of Wonderland where every card is a weapon, every suit a kingdom, and every round an all‑out battle for power. The Crimson Queen takes the traditional trick‑taking games of Hearts and  turns it into total mayhem.

Players lead five distinct realms — the traditional Hearts, Spades, Diamonds, Clubs, and the all new Moons — into a cunning struggle for control of the throne. With vibrant illustrations that bring Alice, the Mad Hatter, and the enigmatic Queen of Hearts to life, every card is infused with whimsical character and strategic potential in The Crimson Queen. Traditional trick‑taking mechanics are elevated by combo‑driven scoring and the unpredictable Chaos Deck with one‑time effects that can upend an entire round and reward bold, creative play.  The player who reaches 150 points - or has the highest score - wins.

The Crimson Queen contains the four standard card suits from Hearts.
The Crimson Chaos expansion adds rule-changing Chaos cards.
The White Queen's Gambit expansion expands player count by adding the Moons suit.

—description from the designer

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the Thüringer Wald in Germany.

Be the player with the most completed sets of embarrassing situation cards by the end of the game.   This is an adult version of the classic "Go Fish" game with inappropriate cards like "mismatched boobies" and "an underaged girlfriend".

The point being that when asking if another player has a card, you ask, "Do you have " (ex: Do you have "bad breath").    If the player does, he hands all cards matching that to the asker. If not, he replies "Go Fuck Yourself".

Once a player has a full set of that card type (4 cards), he puts those cards aside and gets a point for completing the set.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Schwerin, in Germany.

boardgame: Ascension: Deckbuilding Game – Stone Blade Templar Promo Card
description:
Factionless Hero - Cost 4△ - 3★ value

'Reveal and banish the top card of the center deck. If its faction is:

Enlightened: Gain 3★
Mechana: Gain 2△
Void: Gain 2?
No Faction: Choose one of the effects above.'

Dimmi di Sì is the official board game by Simple&Madama designed to turn couple dynamics and friendships into a fun and surprising experience. It features three game modes:

Couples Mode: A longer, ongoing game where couples take on challenges drawn from cards, earning stars by completing them. The aim is to push comfort zones and rediscover each other, with a shared goal or reward chosen at the start.

How Well Do You Know Me?: A party game mode where friends guess each other's preferences and characteristics, testing how well they know each other.

Gift Time: This mode involves groups of 4 to 6 players where participants draw gift cards and secretly choose a gift they would like and one they would not. Others try to guess these choices to score points.

The game supports 2 to 8 players, lasts about 15 to 40 minutes, and is suitable for ages 8 and up. Its format is accessible, combining humor, reflection, and moments of complicity, making it perfect for both couples and social gatherings.

Tweedy Tricksters is a 4-player trick-taking game of fluid friendships, thoughtful choices, and mischievous creatures. Draft cards, choose the suit you'll lead, and discover who your partner is. The rules are simple but provide expansive strategic depth that, when combined with adorable hand-drawn critters, make Tweedy Tricksters an experience you can bring to the table at any time!

Halls of Asgard is a semi-cooperative social strategy game for 3-6 players (25-45 min) with absolutely stunning hand-painted art by celebrated Norse mythology artist Helena Rosova. Clocking in on the lighter side, it’s a great starter for those new to board games with easy set up, a simple mechanic that can generate surprisingly intricate cooperative strategies, and guaranteed memorable times. The semi-cooperative aspect lends itself to a delightful tension between defeating Hela, goddess of the Norse underworld, and out-doing your fellow Asgardian gods.

Each player takes on the role of a god (Odin, Thor, Freya, Heimdal, Idun, or Loki) with two unique powers and one unique curse. Gazing down at the mortals passing through the life stages (Child/Warrior/Elder) on the Midgard board, they use their powers to claim heroes that join them in their hall in preparation for Ragnarok. Along the way, Hela claims mortals off the Midgard board and gains all those who die without honour or glory of old age. A unique clan mechanic and differing mortal values (0/1/2) adds an extra element of strategy as gods collect their heroes. If one of the gods can defeat Hela through the strength of their gathered warriors, the gods win; however, if Hela ends up with the highest strength score, all players lose as Ragnarok sets the nine realms aflame.

—description from the designer

"Dream Baseball 5" is a bingo-style board game simulating five-man baseball. Players assume the role of a GM, rescuing a struggling team. Through precise drafting and strategic planning, they must lead their team back to the top.

Game Objective: Strengthen your team through draft picks, then win league matches to achieve company goals and gain victory points. At the end of the game, the winner is determined by whoever has the most victory points.

Game End: The game will last for a total of 7 years, and victory points will be compared at the end.

Typical Brazilian elements are represented on the cards, modeled after 1930s American advertising.


     TURN RULES: Choose between the Capoeira (more strategic) or Carnival (more festive) events.
     PREDICTION: Players predict how many rounds they think they will win with this hand of cards.
     BUGOU!: Cards of the same name cancel each other out! Use this strategy to destroy your friends' play.


Elementos típicos brasileiros são representados nas cartas em formato de propaganda americana dos anos 1930.


     REGRA DA VEZ: Escolha entre os eventos Capoeira (mais estratégico), ou Carnaval (mais festivo).
     PREVISÃO: Os jogadores fazem a previsão de quantas rodadas acham que vão ganhar com esta mão de cartas.
     BUGOU!: Cartas iguais se anulam! Aproveite esta estratégia para destruir a jogada dos seus amigos.
family: Mechanism: Eliminated Players Continue to Participate (But Cannot Win)

description:
Collect matching cards while sabotaging your friends and enjoying clever puns in this simple yet strategic game. Quick to learn and endlessly fun, Jelly Fish Toast & Jam promises nonstop fun!

—description from the publisher

You are an alien disguised as a player in a quest to abduct world leaders and retrieve lost devices to dominate the human race.

During the conference of nations, the leaders are gathered in a luxurious hotel, and this is your chance to take control of the planet.

But be careful! One of the players is an infiltrated human, determined to sabotage this mission.

Each player receives an initiative card and four Strength cards, ranging from 1 to 4. A secret Strength auction decides the order in which cards are drawn from the first floor of the hotel. You are allowed to use as many Strength cards as you like, as well as Resources, discarding them at the end of the auction.

However, your used Strength cards only return to your hand when ALL players have used ALL their own Strength cards, so be careful, as without Strength cards, you cannot participate in the auction and cannot collect Resources.

When you have completed the cards required for victory, or defeat, the game ends.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Você é um alienígena disfarçado em uma disputa para abduzir líderes mundiais e recuperar os dispositivos perdidos para dominar a raça humana.

Durante a conferência das nações, os líderes estão reunidos em um luxuoso hotel e essa é a sua chance de tomar controle do planeta.

Mas fique atento! Um dos jogadores é um humano infiltrado, determinado a sabotar essa missão.

Cada jogador recebe uma carta de iniciativa e 4 cartas de Força de 1 a 4. Em um leilão secreto de forças decide-se a ordem de compra das cartas do primeiro andar do hotel. É permitido usar quantas cartas de Força quiser, e Recursos também, descartando-os no final da disputa (leilão).

Porém, suas cartas de Força usadas só voltam pra sua mão quando TODOS jogadores tiverem usado TODAS as próprias cartas de Força também, portanto tome cuidado, pois sem cartas de Força você não participa do leilão e não consegue pegar Recursos.

Quando completarem as cartas necessárias para a vitória, ou para a derrota, o jogo acaba.

—description from the publisher

Soccer Fast-Paced Card Game for Kids & Adults – 128 Cards & Easy Rules – Quick & Fun Matching Game – 2-8 Players – Ages 8+ – Perfect for Family Games Night, Travel and Parties.

A soccer themed set matching game. Play cards into various piles. Jokers can change the nature of a pile. Be the first to empty your draw pile for the win.

Judgement Day is a courtroom simulation which could be played with 1-12 people. Begin by looking through the evidence of all the case file cards and then proceed to investigate the personal statements page. Do the suspects testimonies match the witnesses? Can you find the contradictions? All of this and more will be tested as you examine the Jury deliberation page. This page includes a simulated debate about topics real jurors would be discussing. After reading through this debate, take some time to discuss with friends or if playing alone contemplate the evidence. If playing with multiple people, the group should have a vote on if the accused is guilty or not guilty. Finally make your verdict Guilty or Not Guilty. On the back of each Guilty or Non Guilty card it will tell you if you were right or wrong and the effects of your decision. 
You play as the juror who has to make every deciding vote. You see, this game takes place in a timeline where a unanimous vote is not needed to send a suspect behind bars. Only 7 votes is needed to convict and as Juror 12 you will always have to make the tough decision. Explore 5 cases with an unique storyline. One case at a time you will shape the outcome of this world. Will the public lose trust in the Justice system or will you prevent chaos from erupting on Judgement Day?

The seventh major expansion for the third edition of Carcassonne. The city of Carcassonne is besieged by catapults, and players have to place their meeples in the city as defenders in order to defend it.

A robbery is underway at one of the city's five banks!

Secretly assume the identity of a Police Officer or a Robber.

Take turns drawing an Investigation card and deciding how to use it.

To win, you must locate the bank under attack, collect police badges, and investigate the identities of the other players.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un braquage est en cours dans l’une des cinq banques de la ville !

Incarnez secrètement l’identité d’un Policier ou d’un Voleur. 
À tour de rôle, piochez une carte Enquête et décidez de son utilisation.

Pour gagner, vous devez localiser la banque attaquée, récupérer des insignes de police et enquêter sur l’identité des autres joueurs.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Circino: le Chasseur de Trésors – Destination Gironde
description:
How well do you know the towns of Gironde? Our little adventurer Circino doesn't think so...

This is the goal of this board game, in addition to allowing children, their parents, and grandparents to spend pleasant moments together in a friendly and cheerful atmosphere.

To ensure everyone can discover these towns while having fun, Créacom Games opted for an intergenerational theme: a treasure hunt.

36 towns in the department were chosen to be featured in the game with their "Objective" card and corresponding "Treasure."

Each town chose its own two photos and wrote its own introductory text.

The goal of the game is to find the two Treasures (gold coins) corresponding to the two towns chosen at the beginning of the game, then to bring them back to Circino first in the center of the board.

To do this, players will have to search the various chests armed only with dice and their sense of adventure (memory)... using secret passages and tunnels, and using boosters.

In their quest, players will not be alone and can count on the help of members of the Circino family through Bonus cards.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Connaissez-vous bien les communes de la Gironde ? Notre petit aventurier Circino ne le pense pas ...

Tel est donc l'objectif recherché par ce jeu de plateau, en plus de permettre aux plus petits, leurs parents et leurs grands-parents d'occuper d'agréables moments de partage dans la convivialité et la bonne humeur.

Pour que chacun puisse découvrir ces communes tout en s'amusant, Créacom Games a opté pour un thème intergénérationnel : une chasse aux trésors.

36 communes du département ont donc été choisies pour être mises en avant dans le jeu avec sa carte "Objectif" et son "Trésor" correspondant.

Chaque commune a choisi elle-même ses 2 photos et a rédigé son texte de présentation.

Le But du jeu va consister à retrouver les 2 Trésors (pièces d'or) correspondants aux 2 communes choisies au début de la partie puis à les ramener le 1er à Circino au centre du plateau.

Pour cela, les joueurs vont devoir aller fouiller les différents coffres avec comme seules armes le dé et leur sens de l'aventure (mémoire) ... en empruntant des passages secrets, des tunnels et en bénéficiant de boosters.

Dans leur quête, les joueurs ne seront pas seuls et pourront compter sur l'aide des membres de la famille Circino par le biais des cartes Bonus.

Arès, Auros, Bazas, Belin-Beliet, Beychac et Caillau, Blaye, Braud et Saint-Louis, Cadillac-sur-Garonne, Captieux, Carcans-Maubuisson, Castelmoron-d'Albret, Castillon-la-Bataille, Créon, Cubzac les ponts, Guîtres, La Brède, La Lande de Fronsac, La Réole, Le Porge, Le Teich, Le Tourne, Lesparre-Médoc, Libourne, Lormont, Margaux-Cantenac, Montagne, Pellegrue, Pessac, Rauzan, Rions, Saint Jean d'Illac, Sainte-Foy-la-Grande, Saint-Macaire, Saint-Savin, Sauternes, Sauveterre-de-Guyenne.

—description from the publisher

You and your companion have discovered a long-forgotten temple in the middle of the Indian Ocean. Compete with your fellow adventurer to carefully explore the exterior of the temple before making the plunge inside. Your ultimate goal depends on your character - will you seek treasure, fame, or a way out? Players will compete against each other in order to complete their specific goal found on their Character Card before the end of the game; if both players complete their goal, use the Destiny Rating to determine the winner.

—description from the designer

Segue o Fluxo is a lighthearted party game where players try to think like the group. In each round, a question is revealed—something open-ended like “Name a famous painter (not Picasso)” or “What’s the most iconic fast food item?” Each player secretly writes down their answer, aiming not to be original, but to match the responses of others.

Once everyone reveals their answers, the group checks who followed the "flow" and who strayed too far. Players whose answers don't match anyone else receive a small fish token as a penalty. In this game, being unique isn’t a strength—it means you're out of sync with the group!

The game continues for several rounds, and the player with the fewest fish tokens at the end wins. It’s all about reading the room, predicting popular opinions, and avoiding being the odd one out.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
A fun and instant method to learn about the euro currency. Players engage in a fast, entertaining competitive game where they must accumulate the correct amount needed to “purchase” assigned products. Suitable for children learning money management and euro denominations.

The objective of the game is to be the first to purchase your 3 items, as indicated on the cards that make up each player's hand.
The game is played clockwise. On their turn, a player can perform one of two actions: take money or make a purchase. If a player chooses to take money, they may take a coin (1, 2, 5, 10, 20, or 50 cents, or 1 or 2 euros) or a banknote (5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, or 500 euros) of their choice from the reserve and place it in the center of the table, creating a common pot.

The purchase action allows a player to spend the money from the pot to buy one of their own cards. However, be careful: the money in the pot can be used by any player, and once spent, it returns to the reserve.

Tile Wars is an abstract take-that game with unique tile placement and scoring mechanism.  The base game has 4 additional maps, and can be played at 3 levels.  Coop and Solo modes are available without the take that action.  Score based on the position of other tiles, but use the action cards to seriously improve your scores or mess up your opponents.

A turn consists of – 

     Drawing a card
     Playing a tile
     Scoring a tile
     Drawing tiles back up to 5 on your rack


If at the start of a turn you have 0 or 1 tile, draw from the bag to have 2 tiles before playing.

When playing a tile, you must place a YELLOW level 1 tile if you have it, then BLUE level 2, then RED level 3.

Any number of cards can be played at any time, subject to the play restrictions on the card e.g. if it says ON AN OPPONENTS TURN, then it can be played at any point during any opponent’s turn.

The black number in the circle on each tile is its value.  Tiles places NEVER score their own value but score based on the x1, x2, x2 etc on the spaces referred to on the tile.

Tile values get stronger each level.

When the bag is empty of tiles and you are playing the 2 or 3 level game, put 2 random blue tiles in the void and the rest in the bag.  When the bag is empty again, do the same for red tiles if playing the full 3-level game.

YELLOW tiles must be placed on level 1 on the board.  BLUE tiles can only be placed ON TOP of YELLOW tiles.  RED tiles can only be placed ON TOP of BLUE TILES.

—description from the designer

Take you and your family on an expansive journey to meet some of history's greatest warriors as you gather them into your army. Gather Indians, Hakkapeliittas, Amazons, or even fierce Mongols and outwit the others by harvesting the best troops.

At the beginning of their turn a player asks from another player any soldier card number. If another player doesn't have that numbered card in their hand, the active player draws a card and then it's next player's turn. If another player does have that numbered card in their hand they have to give one card to the active player.

Then active player may choose to end their turn, continue recruiting soldiers if they don't have 8 cards in their hand yet or build an army from those cards in their hand. Armies are made like poker hands. At the end of their turn player will draw one card and then turn will proceed to clockwise. Armies' possible scores are: 4 cards straight =1p, 3 same cards =1p, 4 same cards =2p, 3 cards same color straight =2p and 4 cards same color straight =3p. The points can be marked with a pen and paper or with different kind of buttons or coins. The game can be played also as a tournament. Then multiple games are played in a row until someone scores totally 20 points.

A light strategic card game matching cats to the spots in the house they love to sleep in.

Players match cat cards with colored spot cards (like a bed or sofa), aiming to create a sequence of descending scores (4, 3, 2, 1) of the same color on a cat card to score points at the end of the game. The "best spot" isn't a single location but is the goal of creating these sequential runs of spot cards to earn points for your cats, which is a central mechanic of the game.

Two versions are playable - a junior version for younger ages and all the family, and a more strategic version where you have to keep a close eye on what cards have already been played to beat your opponents.

—description from the designer

Steampunk 1916 trench raider is a solo dungeon-crawling mini-RPG designed for one player.

You take on the role of an Alternative World War I soldier (Trench Raider).

Complete missions to win the game!

What you need to play:

A printout of this book (you can use a digital tablet or computer if you want, but paper is better).
Two six-sided dice & two four-sided dice.
A deck of playing cards without the Jokers [map grid].
A coin, figure, or other token to represent your character [token].
A piece of paper [or a character sheet] and a pen or pencil .
You can also keep a mini journal of your encounters and adventures [this part of the game is completely optional].

boardgame: FLOP: The One with the Frogs
description:
FLOPPING is the masterful art of ultimate underachieving.
 You win by doing... absolutely nothing.
 No cards to play? No enemies to attack?
 Boom. You’ve FLOPPED. And you’ve WON.

Each game of Flop players are dealt a random face down deck of 10 cards, from which they draw their hand of 4.

On your turn you must PLAY A CARD or ATTACK.

When you play a card in your play area, you must pay its cost if any, by discarding cards from your hand in your discard pile. Then, you may activate one of its abilities, such as: PLAY, which triggers as soon as you play the card. BEFORE ATTACK, which triggers before you perform an attack action. ONGOING, this ability remains active as long as the card is in play.

When you attack, choose one of your Allies in your play area and deal damage equal to its power to an enemy in your opponent’s play area.

If the damage on a card is equal to or greater than its health points, that card is defeated and moved to that player discard pile.

The first player who cannot attack nor play a card FLOPS.. and wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: FLOP: The One with the Pigeons
description:
FLOPPING is the masterful art of ultimate underachieving.
 You win by doing... absolutely nothing.
 No cards to play? No enemies to attack?
 Boom. You’ve FLOPPED. And you’ve WON.

Each game of Flop players are dealt a random face down deck of 10 cards, from which they draw their hand of 4.

On your turn you must PLAY A CARD or ATTACK.

When you play a card in your play area, you must pay its cost if any, by discarding cards from your hand in your discard pile. Then, you may activate one of its abilities, such as: PLAY, which triggers as soon as you play the card. BEFORE ATTACK, which triggers before you perform an attack action. ONGOING, this ability remains active as long as the card is in play.

When you attack, choose one of your Allies in your play area and deal damage equal to its power to an enemy in your opponent’s play area.

If the damage on a card is equal to or greater than its health points, that card is defeated and moved to that player discard pile.

The first player who cannot attack nor play a card FLOPS.. and wins the game.

—description from the publisher

The year is 1610. The world, as we know it, is on the precipice of change, a fragile balance between ancient dogma and dawning reason. Whispers of a new truth echo through the hallowed halls of academia and across bustling piazzas. Nicolaus Copernicus's radical theory, once a fringe idea, now finds a formidable champion in Galileo Galilei, whose revolutionary observations of the cosmos threaten to dismantle the very foundations of accepted knowledge.
You are a scholar, a brilliant mind in an age of intellectual ferment, drawn into the exhilarating and perilous quest for understanding. Armed with your intellect, you'll write groundbreaking treatises, unveil ingenious inventions and turn your gaze skyward, observing the celestial dance through newly crafted telescopes; while the shadow of the Inquisition looms large, its watchful eyes scrutinizing every discovery, every whispered theory. One misstep, one too-bold pronouncement, could lead to your arrest, your work condemned, and perhaps, a fiery end. Will you pursue the truth, or will the chilling grip of fear force you to recant? The fate of knowledge, and perhaps your very life, rests in your hands.

Galileo’s Truth is a competitive game for 1 to 4 players featuring mechanics like deck building, worker placement, resource management and the threatening management of the Suspicion…  
Players will alternate turns performing actions such as:

Write a Treatise to add more cards to their deck, increasing their opportunities to do big and articulate actions.
Create an Invention to gain powerful abilities, increase their income and grow their scoring opportunities.
Observe the Sky to unlock a sequence of progressively stronger bonuses and actions.

And many others!

But be careful, because the watchful eye of the Inquisition will judge all your actions. As Suspicion grows, you'll gain Penance cards that clog up your deck and potentially score negative points at the end of the game. Most dangerously, you could end up in jail, unable to use your only worker, or even worse… the fire of purification will await you.

The intense mechanic of Suspicion is further enriched by the victory point track. As you pull ahead on the victory point track, leaving others behind, you'll gain even more Suspicion. Will you boldly pursue the truth and risk the Inquisition's punishment, or will you choose a more cautious path, perhaps even appearing assertive towards the Church?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Top or Not: Dabei sein ist alles!
description:
Are you always on the top of your form? In this wild party game, the motto is: discuss, persuade, trick! Funny topics provide conversation material and laughter. Only those who use their terms wisely will win the game. With charm, wit and a little luck, you can secure your place at the top!

—description from the publisher

Bist du immer top dabei? In diesem wilden Partyspiel heißt es: Diskutieren, überzeugen, tricksen! Lustige Themen sorgen für Gesprächsstoff und Lacher. Nur wer seine Begriffe klug unterbringt, gewinnt das Spiel. Mit Charme, Witz und ein bisschen Glück sicherst du dir den Platz an der Spitze!

—description from the publisher (German)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A fun party game where two selected players answer cheeky questions, and the rest players guess whose answer is "more"!

Each round, 2 players take the role of answerers and respond to a tricky question. The others guess whose answer will be “greater”! Who “travels more often” or “likes to walk around the house naked more”? Earn points for correct guesses and discover new things about your friends!

The box contains 600 engaging questions. Each card features questions from three categories: light everyday, edgy and provocative, or deep and philosophical – choose whatever suits your mood!

boardgame: Horrified: Dungeons & Dragons – Warlock Promo Pack
description: Exclusive hero to add to your game.  The Warlock starts on the Altar to Juiblex. The special action is to the roll the D20, and for 1 - No Effect, 2 - 8, Reshuffle a discarded non-Citizen Monster card back into the Monster deck, 9 - 15 Reveal the top 2 Monster cards.  Place 1 on the Bottom of the Monster deck and 1 on the top.  16 - 19 - On the next Monster card drawn ignore any reference to the Frenzy Monster, and 20 - Reduce the Terror Track by 1.  Gain a Perk card.

boardgame: Mockery Manor: The Murder Mystery Game
description:
From the makers of hit audio fiction podcast Mockery Manor comes a murder mystery envelope game set in the eponymous theme park. Gather clues, solve puzzles and interview suspects to reveal the culprit(s).

Work together (or alone!) to unearth a spooky Halloween mystery at British theme park Mockery Manor in 1998. In addition to an envelope full of beautifully designed paper clues, you'll also uncover a wealth of online material, including full-cast, fully scored audio interviews and scenes. Armed with your only your trusty tape recorder, your wits, and advice from a questionably-reliable private investigator, you'll need to cross reference everything if you're going to figure out whodunnit.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This fast and fun family game puts your matching skills to the test. Throw dice in the Monster Tower to reveal what kind of monster to find. If you find a monster that matches, hurry and put your finger on the card. The person who ends up with the most cards wins! Kids love giggling over the adorable monsters, playing with the Monster Tower, and racing to spot a match. Grown-ups love how their little ones are secretly learning counting skills and pattern recognition. Everyone’s having a monstrously good time with this matching game.

NO MORE BORING KIDS' GAMES: Monster Match is a family game designed to be simple enough for kids and exciting for grown-ups too. Toss the dice in the Monster Tower and race to find a monster card that matches. The person with the most cards wins! It's a matching game made with 4 year olds in mind.

—description from publisher
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray

boardgame: Long Shot: The Dice Game – Horse Set 8 (Barn) Mini-Expansion
description:
This set focuses on buying horses.
8 Horse Cards (Set 8 - Barn)

We recommend mixing several of these horses with other sets to complete a full set of 1-8 to play.

—description from the publisher

You are a hungry, hungry rat, but the hard part of your pizza heist is finally over: You have obtained your coveted greasy, cheesy slices. No matter how you ethically sourced your slices (kid's birthday party in Central Park, late-night leftovers from the frat basement, sad scraps in the office kitchenette) all that za is going to the same place — down the sewer grate to feed your rat families, friends, and situationships.

And when your hard-earned pizza slice hits that sewer floor, you **want it landing faceup!**

From there, place those faceup slices on table cards, guessing how many slices each card needs to sate the appetites of your rat brethren.


     CARD AND DICE PARTY GAME ABOUT PIZZA. AND RATS: Play as a pizza-fiending rat in the Trash to Table game, a fast and fun push-your-luck game! Feed fellow rats by tossing pizza down the sewer grate – and place bets to win.
     7 RAT-APPROVED “PIZZA SLICE” DICE: The plastic molded dice are each shaped like different pizza slices with hilarious toppings. Like Meatball Surprise (topped with a cockroach surprise!)
     BUILT-IN SEWER GRATE DICE TOWER: Open the game to find a built-in “sewer grate”! Is there anything more thrilling than dropping pizza dice down it? No, we say! Every toss brings edge-of-your-seat anticipation. How many slices will land faceup?


HOW TO PLAY: 

     Toss pizza dice down the sewer grate dice tower.
     Using only the dice that land faceup, bet on how many slices will feed the rats hidden on the back of each card. Guess correctly to earn points.
     The first to 22 wins


—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray

description:
This was available at Gen Con 2025.

Includes:

Great Flood (of 1913) - Event
Eastern Fence Lizard/Evening Bat
Northern Map Turtle/Northern Cardinal

The first letters of these cards spell out "GEN". The first letters of promo pack 2 spell out "CON".

This was available at Gen Con 2025.

Includes:

Coral Charm Peony
Oliver's Woods - Terrain
Northern Maidenhair Fern

The first letters of these cards spell out "CON". The first letters of promo pack 1 spell out "GEN".

An entirely new concept from Unsolved Case Files will have you interrogating your friends and trying to keep your own crimes under wraps. Call your friends out for their parking tickets, speeding, and littering - and make sure you take your post-game mugshots to prove everyone’s guilt.


     A NEW WAY TO PLAY UNSOLVED CASE FILES: True Crime has hit the party game category!
     WHO'S THE BIGGEST CRIMINAL IN YOUR CREW? There’s been a petty crime - actually there have been a lot of them - and it’s up to you to sniff out the guilty parties
     YOU HAVE THE RIGHT TO REMAIN SILENT... BUT WILL YOU? Expose your friends' petty crimes like parking tickets, speeding, and littering - no one's record is clean!
     DEFLECT, CONFESS, ACCUSE: Keep a straight face while dodging blame and pointing fingers. It’s part party game, part social experiment.
     GOOD COP OR BAD COP? YOU DECIDE: Play with up to 10 players for a party game full of bold accusations, hilarious confessions, and lively interrogation.


—description from the publisher

The world of Return to Dark Tower grows as new expeditions appear to tempt you for their rewards —and you’ll need them because torments can make foes and regions more terrifying to confront. Thankfully four new heroes also join the adventure to help you conquer the Dark Tower.

Return to Dark Tower: Expeditions is a fully analog expansion that can be integrated with the base game and both Alliances and Covenant content.

boardgame: Thunder Road: Vendetta – Interstate of Decay
description: Pave your future in Thunder Road: Vendetta – Interstate of Decay, a campaign expansion for Thunder Road: Vendetta that leans into its cinematic world. Your crews now race and battle through a short series of races. Follow the map, hit key areas, and level up your cars between races.

Unmatched: Hellboy brings four characters from Mike Mignola's Hellboy comic series to this expansive game series. Featuring cover art by the Hellboy creator, Unmatched: Hellboy includes two new battlefields and can mix and match with all other Unmatched sets.

This expansion collection is limited edition and includes 11 expansions for Grind House! (Core Game Required to Play)
 1. Krampus: Christmas Comes Early and Krampus, not Santa, is coming to visit... watch out for Krampus's Evil implements of terror.

2. Carnival: What's scarier than a house filled with traps that will take your head off? How about a Carnival of evil clowns that fill that house? Win tickets and prizes that may help you survive!

3. Cthulhu: Do you hear the Call of Cthulhu? Don't lose your sanity in this expansion where the hose calls upon the power of an Elder God to destory your minds!

4. Contest of Champions: Enter the house assuming the roles of previous survivor winners! This time around, there are no fixed number of rooms, no bonus points, no additional ghost winners. The Game goes until one single survivor remains!

5. Abduction: Blast off in an intergalactic tour of terror as you try to survive experiments from the mysterious Zetas. The only question is, will you all be returning as Human, or something else?

6. The Kid's Game: Welcome to the Playground! Participate in several familiar games with deadly twists to survive. The hose will award bonus points to survivors who rise to be the cream of the crop!

7. Scythes Out: The Host has opened a new wing of the mansion for you to come explore, and your invitation included a little something extra this time around... did the other guests get something extra too? What is it?

8. Trap or Treat: In addition to your usual lineup of rooms, Trap or Treat introduces over 30 new mini rooms that take place in between main encounters in the Grind House!

9. Secrets of the Lost Tomb: The host went all out using the power of The Lost Tomb as a venue for his most secret party yet. Explore, find Magical Artifacts, and discover all of the Secrets of the Lost Tomb. A Cross-Over Expansion with the Epic Game Secrets of the Lost Tomb!

10. Coma Ward: Who are these people? Why are we all in hospital gowns? What is this invitation pinned to your I.V.? Is it all in your head or is there really 10 million dollars at stake? Find out in this Horror Crossover with Coma Ward!

11. Welcome to the Propitious: Dare to step foot inside the haunted hotel that started it all. Located in the ghost town of Dire California, The Propitius Hotel stands as home to the ghost of Arthur Wilson, the most prolific serial killer of the 20th century. In this standalone expansion, venture through a haunted hotel full of traps and terrors based on Slashtag, the official prequel novel to Grind House.

Meteorite season is now for Alula, and with it come meteor showers. This festive time attracts plenty of colorful visitors and excited pilgrims. Legends say these meteors change the physics of the continent in strange ways…

The 15 new Meteor cards are just shuffled with Region cards from the base game (and possibly from the first expansion). They don't have an effect during the game, but can dramatically change the scoring phase: All Meteor cards in front of you stay face up for scoring (just like Sanctuaries), as well as all your Region cards which share a last digit with them. They will all be taken into account when scoring each of your Region cards.

Meteor cards also feature new scoring requirements, like acquiring 4 cards of the same biome, or throwing away one of your Sanctuary cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Red Victory, White Defeat: The Campaign of Tsaritsyn, June 1918-January 1920
description:
The Tsaritsyn Campaign during the Russian Civil War, is little known outside of Russia, although the city would later be renamed Stalingrad, with obvious recognition by most gamers. Stalin's leadership and that of the other Soviet Commanders active around Tsaritsyn was pivotal in saving the Revolution from the Imperialist Forces.

The campaign was full of incident, with lots of victories and defeats on both sides and with much ebb and flow, making it a superb subject for an Operational Level war-game-board-game.

What sets the game apart, in terms of the operational art of war, is the truly effective use of Cavalry at a time when most of Western Europe had dismissed the concept of horsed soldiery as anachronistic. While Western European Armies thought there was nothing that could be done to improve on a boot to boot charge with drawn sabres, the Russians saw Cavalry as a technical arm, using their brightest and best commanders and incorporating firepower and many tactical innovations into formations, fighting mainly with pistols and massed tachanka, while adopting Cossack Lava tactics wholesale. In doing so they ultimately defined the war through aggressive use of a cavalry arm that was capable of fighting both mounted and on foot with a cross section of modern support weapons, all with devastating effect.

This game is designed to give a group of players (anywhere from 2-6) an enjoyable adult war themed game, along with some general historical insight into the operational level military events which took place from June 1918 -January 1920 around the Russian City of Tsaritsyn during the Russian Civil War. The military actions depicted by the game proved to be a major and important series of campaigns in the Russian Civil War, and helped give Stalin a degree of prominence and support, that he later exploited to his advantage.

What this game is not, is a detailed hyper realistic minute simulation of every aspect of the campaign and the military history surrounding it. It is a game designed with the goal of being elegant, simple, easily assimilated, and above all interesting and fun.

In this game much has been abstracted. Some aspects have been manipulated for play balance and others for simplicity. However, that being said, the game does a pretty good job of modeling, as far as a tabletop board game can, the type of military action at the operational level that was seen on the ground in Southern Russia during the Russian Civil War. It also includes many of the elements that made it unique to the era, location, and the mindset of participants. Armored Trains, Cavalry Raiding, Tank attacks, the Volga Gunboat Flotilla, Leaders, with a simple yet realistic modeling of logistics, supply and support, in an easy to learn, simple to play yet hard to master overall gameplay package.

Be advised, like real war, not everything is in your control. If you want certainty in your games, you might be better buying and playing something else. If the iron dice of war decide to give you a hard time, even the best strategy from the greatest mind might not be enough for victory. Sometimes "дерьмо" happens.

What I as the designer hope the game can promise you, is an enjoyable war-game-board-game experience, with enough simplicity to keep you wanting more, with enough flavor to immerse you in the era, and with enough challenge and tough choices to keep you engaged and thinking until the last dice is rolled.

"Everything is very simple in war, but the simplest thing is difficult. These difficulties accumulate and produce a friction, which no man can imagine exactly who has not seen war. Suppose now a traveller, who, towards evening, expects to accomplish the two stages at the end of his day's journey, four or five leagues, with post horses, on the high road—it is nothing. He arrives now at the last station but one, finds no horses, or very bad ones; then a hilly country, bad roads; it is a dark night, and he is glad when, after a great deal of trouble, he reaches the next station, and finds there some miserable accommodation. So in war, through the influence of an infinity of petty circumstances, which cannot properly be described on paper, things disappoint us, and we fall short of the mark." 
Carl von Clausewitz

With that said, grab some pals, set up the game and....... 
"Давайте начнем товарищи ....поехали!"
Above all have fun.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Guild of Merchant Explorers: Party Pack
description:
With this expansion, up to eight players can play The Guild of Merchant Explorers.

Contains:

     8 double sided maps (Avenia/Aghon and Cnidaria/Kazan)
     9 oversized explore cards
     4 new player colors (green, purple, black, and orange)
     32 taking post tokens
     75 coin tokens
     40 treasure tokens
     16 crystal tokens
     40 treasure cards
     8 special investigate cards
family: Game:  The Guild of Merchant Explorers

description:
Three new discovery cards to add to your game of Galileo Galilei.


     Vallem lunarem - Ophiuchus
     Cometa Periculosa - Canis Minor
     Aquila Nebula - Centaurus

Gardomino is a strategic card and domino game for 3 or 4 players, where bluffing, bidding, and trick-taking play a central role. The game begins with players collecting capital using domino tiles, followed by bidding on playing cards to build their personal “garden.” This garden determines the trumps during the harvest phases, where players use hand cards to win tricks and earn points. Between seasons, players can take or trade cards from each other depending on bids and threshold values. By combining card values and suits, unique trump combinations—called Garden Trumps—can be created. The game is played over four seasons, each featuring its own bidding rounds, tricks, and bonuses. The player with the most points at the end of season 4 wins the game.

—description from the designer

Only your courage and cunning stand between the city of Mount Silence and the savage horde rising from below. The fate of Gnome-kind lies in your hands.

Receive your mission briefing and delve into a shifting network of tunnels that changes every time you enter. A horde of goblins awaits, their actions driven by a narrative framework. You and your fellow gnomes must fight smart, strike fast, and survive. The delicate balance of the underworld can be tipped—but only if you restore it in time.

This is a tactical dungeon-crawler with brutal melee combat, positional gameplay, and risk management. Players control gnome miniatures, explore modular board tiles, and engage in dice-based combat. Red dice are for attacking, blue for defense, and stamina fuels your actions.

Combat is fast and decisive. Models move, interact, and Take a Swing—an attack action using Stamina and dice. Results range from damage to knockdowns, stamina recovery, or sweeping attacks. Weapons matter: axes cleave through multiple enemies, while spears and swords offer different tactical roles.

Enemies react through line of sight and scripted behaviors, creating dynamic solo or co-op experiences. Prone models can recover, but can’t defend until they do. Every decision counts.

Includes:

25 detailed metal miniatures
Modular dungeon tiles
Narrative-driven enemy AI
Unique weapon mechanics
Solo, Co-op, Versus & Team Modes (1–7 players)

Thunder Under Mount Silence delivers a gritty, immersive dungeon adventure with tactical depth and high replayability.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lightning Train: Ghost Train Promo Card
description:
This is a promotional card given out as a pre-order bonus and with purchase at Gen Con 2025.

Ghost Train
Action
Choose an empty path. This turn, a "phantom" rail line is there. Gain 2 for each delivery that uses that line.

The Wizardologist is under the Penguin's spell and has forgotten who their Apprenticist is. Good thing 3–18 players are here to help! Or mislead. Or transform. Or sabotage! In this micro social deduction card game, each player assumes a unique role with distinct objectives. Some hide and bluff. Others search and interrogate. And some intervene at just the right moment to completely change the story. Teach in a minute, play in five, then quickly reset with entirely new dynamics. A Wizardologist Most Hexasperated is compact, expressive, and endlessly remixable--a fresh, light-hearted take on social deduction built for dynamic group play and quick bursts of strategic interaction.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The goal of the game is to be the first to reach the goal through smart building, clever tactics, and a bit of luck.

The game is played over two phases: 1) Building; 2) Racing

1) Building
Shuffle the game tiles and place the goal tile at the bottom of the deck to form the draw pile.
On their turn, a player may perform one of the following 4 actions: placement, relocation, flip, rotate.

2) Racing
On their turn, a player may perform one of the following 3 actions: move, flip, rotate.

End of the Game
The game ends immediately when a player steps onto the goal tile.

—description from the designer

The Goal of the Game

Work together to bring order to the chaos! Each of the 6 fairy-tale characters must arrange their task cards individually in ascending order according to their numbers. This means 6 cards per character. Be careful—you may not speak while playing! You can only communicate with each other using sounds and rhythm.

While playing, the most important thing is to enjoy making music together, but it also develops the following skills:

     Group cooperation
     Emotional intelligence
     Attention and concentration
     Memory
     Auditory skills
     Basic musical knowledge


Gameplay in Brief

Each player holds 4 cards in their hand.

Players use sounds and rhythm to indicate which card they want to play.

Others recognize if their card fits in the sequence and join in.

Goal: Work together to place all the characters’ cards on the table in ascending order.

The game is perfectly successful if every card ends up in its correct place.

—description from the designer

Strataka is a mind-focused card game shaped by the energy of colors and shapes. When geometry, energy, and strategy combine, cards become not just game pieces but also representatives of powers.

Strataka is a strategic card game played for two players, starting with a special 40-card deck. This deck is based on four fundamental crystal entities, each representing a different shape, color, and mindset.

Strataka is primarily played by two players. However, by adding a separate 40-card deck for each pair of players, the game can be expanded to four, six, or even eight players. When multiple decks are combined, more complex and versatile encounters are possible.

The game progresses in rounds, with each round offering players the opportunity to make decisions, read, and explore opportunities.

All cards are shuffled, and each player is given a starting hand of five cards. At the beginning of the game, a random suit is assigned as the "power crystal" (trump suit). This suit gives an advantage over others and changes the direction of the strategy.

Round Mechanics

Each round is played in the following order:

Drawing Cards
The player draws a card from the pile, leaving a total of six cards in their hand.

Discarding Cards
Then, they discard one card from their hand.
The other player takes a turn. They also draw a card and discard one card.

Ending the Round
If two cards of the same suit are played, the one who rolls the higher number wins the round.
If the second player doesn't have the same suit, they win by discarding the trump suit.
If they can't play a trump card, they can discard any suit.
In this case, the player who started the round wins the cards on the pile. They score points with the cards they win and have the opportunity to complete the tasks.

End of the Game
The game ends when all the cards on the pile are drawn and the cards in their hand are depleted.

The scores are tallied and the winner is determined. The winner owns all the crystals they have collected.

—description from the designer

Dark and Roses is a print-and-play tactical board game set in a bleak, mystical realm where roses bloom in darkness and alliances are as fragile as petals. Blending strategy, negotiation, and betrayal, players step into the roles of wandering exiles fighting for control of a cursed land—where each rose claimed grants power, but each decision may cost you trust.

Dark and Roses combines intuitive rules with deep replayability. No two games are ever the same, as shifting alliances and random event triggers keep tension high from the first turn to the final betrayal.

With a rich lore, minimalist gothic art style, and mechanics inspired by classic tactical war games and modern social strategy games, Dark and Roses offers a compact yet intense experience for players who enjoy thinking several steps ahead.

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo – 2025 Gen Con Window Pattern Card
description: Promo window pattern card given out during Gen Con 2025.

Using a combination or press-your-luck and hidden information mechanics, players become fugitives on the run, in a lighthearted family friendly experience.

Ringing alarms and baying bloodhounds sound in the distance as you crouch in the bushes. You're a wild-eyed group of newl escaped convicts on the run, with the Warden, the Sheriff and half the county on your trail. You know they’re going to hunt you down until you’re caught, but you intend to make the most of this fleeting freedom as you scramble across the countryside.

Accumulate as many points as you can by visiting various locations, as you search for supplies and overcome hardships along the way. Whether you decide to play it safe or throw caution to the wind, the player with the most points at the end wins.

—description from the designer

The Void has awakened, tearing open rifts between distant worlds they now seeks to devour. To stop them, the greatest legends of each realm gathered.

Spell Rift is a cooperative deck building adventure game where iconic heroes clash against the Void. Build your deck, explore the different worlds, seal Voidrifts, and forge your legend before your world too is consumed.

Players select heroes with unique attributes and join together to defeat the final Boss.  Players can choose a single adventure or use the campaign mode with 9 bosses to defeat.

Players win the game by completing the Boss’s unique objective, and then summoning and defeating the Boss in a climactic battle.  But there are multiple ways our heroes can lose as well.

—description from the publisher

Furioso The English Civil War is a set of wargames rules that covers tabletop warfare in Britain during the civil wars of the seventeenth century.

The rules allow players to fight large scale land battles with a simple to use and enjoyable gaming system. 
A period which saw the country torn apart and reformed as well as the death of a king and the rise of Parliament.

Recreate large scale battles such as those fought at Edgehill, Newbury, Marston Moor, Naseby, Dunbar and Worcester to name but a few! 
Within these pages players will have to deal considerations of higher level command, where to apply the pressure, when to deploy reserves and...just where have all my cavaliers just ridden off to? 
Players will find themselves in control of usually enthusiastic (and sometimes far too enthusiastic) troops who unfortunately do not have the training or experience of their European colleagues. 
Players will also be able to note the way that armies changed through the period, some becoming worse and some becoming better and leading ultimately to the creation and success of the New Model Army.

Furioso can be fought using any scale figures such as 15mm and 28mm.

The Furioso rules have a very simple system at their heart. If you want to do anything you need to roll a d6 (six sided dice), a score of five or more will achieve success, a score of four or lower means failure. Modifiers are applied but this is to the number of dice rolled to achieve success and not the score required. Success will therefore be measured by the number of successful scores achieved. 
At the start of a turn, players roll Initiative for each brigade. Brigades will be activated in sequence with the highest scoring brigade going first followed by lower scoring brigades. Brigades that score a ‘one’ might find themselves doing things that they don’t necessarily want to! This means that play will pass from brigade to brigade rather than one side activating all of their forces and then the opposing side activating all of theirs. The result of this kind of mechanic means that players will not have complete control over their force and may at times find it difficult to execute their plans for the battle. 
But never fear, this applies to the other side as well and makes for a more ‘real experience’.

Included in the book are a series of random events or Hazards that cover such situations as troops running low on ammunition, friendly fire, artillery powder explosions and a whole host of other misfortunes that commonly befell armies of the English Civil War.

To the rear of the book are extensive army lists that break down into each campaign (the West Country, the North and so forth) and by year so that players can command a West Country Royalist army in 1643 or a Parliamentary army in the North in 1644.

Also included in this book are unit cards for all of these units. These will be used during the deployment phase of the battle so that opposing armies will have only a limited idea of their opponents composition and dispositions at the start of the battle.

—description from the publisher

Tehradim: Gold and Steel is a deck building game set in the World of Faelon. Take the role of a Patron by building influence, outwitting your rivals, and becoming the most powerful Patron in the Free City.

The objective of the game is to score the most victory points by the end of the game. You can achieve this by buying new cards to upgrade your Player Deck, and interacting with your opponents to slow their progression.

Unlike other deck-builder card games your hand is laid out in front of you on your player board in a Timeline from 1-5. Each turn the card in the 5th position is discarded and the remaining cards each progress one position in the timeline granting the player resources corresponding to their position.

Play proceeds until the first player exceeds 50 victory points and then each other player will get 1 final turn to try and get the highest score.

—description from the publisher

You arrive at the threshold of a fledgling encampment—Sprocket, and are received by it’s leader Brom, who offers you shelter within the walls of what may be the last refuge of hope. Though a sanctuary for now, it’ll eventually fall under the iron grip of warlord Eris in the calamitous years to come. Until then, you must safeguard the prospects of this fragile haven.

The Ferryman: Life Before Death is played over a series of turns, ending when you complete one or more Missions.

You will need a standard 52 card deck to play.

—description from the designer

Color Tile Rummy uses the base game rules of common "tile rummy" and adds one colored shape at the bottom of each tile for an additional "suit" (eight unique colors), except that the "13-value" tiles will have two colored shapes added.

The main rule added to basic tile rummy is that a player may now group FOUR OR FIVE LIKE-COLORED SHAPES to make a meld on the table (called "Palette") adding an additional meld possibility.

This game adds depth to the base game and additional vectors of play.

The set contains 108 tiles (including four Jokers).

'You dirty rat, you killed ma brother!' 
A hundred years ago the United States introduced laws which banned the sale of booze to the common people (with plenty of loop holes) and this led to the rise of speakeasies as well as gangsters ready to break the law and to make themselves rich and famous at the same time.

The roaring 1920's is here in the fifteenth title in the USEME series. This title was the creation of Robert McAlister and Steve Rysemus and their enjoyment of this special time in history. The D6 engine is fast play and well suited to 15mm scale and other scales of miniatures. Lead your gangsters or police or gmen or others in raids and runs.

CONTENTS OF PRODUCT: The USE ME game system. 
You will need some miniatures, some terrain and a few six sided dice (D6's) to play the game. 
This is not much and indeed most of the time everything in play needs only 1D6 roll to make a decision. 
In the game all miniatures and vehicles are de-marked into of CHARACTERS (from Infantry to Vehicles and everything else) and these into TYPES which allow you to create anything you want from this period with a few tables of TYPES with points costs attached. 
USE ME is a pocket rule system that does not pretend to be anything else. Its fast, its fun and its affordable. With many rule systems these days taking themselves far too seriously and with some being priced at an amount that makes purchasing them a long term goal for most of us USE ME gives you what it promises and in a format that will make you smile.

HERE IS A RUN DOWN OF JUST WHAT YOU GET:

Frontis Page pg1
Introduction & Contents pg2
Prohibition and Inspiration pg3, What you Need to Play pg4.
The USE ME Rules pg5-17
Advanced Rules pg18-19
Civilians in USEME pg20-21
Character Creation and Costs pg21-22
Historical Units in Play pg23
Solo Play Rules pg24-26
Force Lists 1920's and 1930's pg27-30
Free Files for this Game pg31
The USE ME Series pg32

—description from the publisher

USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer. 
From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour or two for the largest battles. 
Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. 
Pick up any of your miniatures and get playing in minutes.

This is the seventh title in the USE ME series and was created after a year of solid feedback from the wargaming public. We have gone through a core science fiction set of rules and several historical ones plus ventured into space but now we go into a near and bleak future; that of the Post Apocalypse.

Returning to the USE ME stable is author O.G.Joel, who has penned among other things USE ME World War Two. He had an interest in the aftermath of a man made, natural or supernatural event that would end our world and bring about a nightmare world of scavengers and technological warlords. This interest and knowledge resulted in these rules. Fast play rules based on the D6 USE ME engine that would suit 15mm scale miniatures (though it will work with smaller or larger scales with ease). From a few personalities in small scenario to squad level with battles involving a hundred or more character including vehicles.

The USE ME game system. You will need some miniatures, some terrain and a few six sided dice (D6's) to play the game. This is not much and indeed most of the time everything in play needs only 1D6 roll to make a decision. In the game all miniatures and vehicles are de-marked into of CHARACTERS (from Infantry to Vehicles and everything else) and these into TYPES which allow you to create anything you want from this period with a few tables of TYPES with points costs attached.

The Turn Sequence of the game follows a simple path of firstly the Initiative Phase where all players roll 1D6 and the highest roll wins the initiative for the turn (important later on!). Then the main part of the turn, the In Play Phase where all characters in the game are activated not by the side upon which they fight but by their Elan Rating. This means better troops and vehicles move and fight before poorer quality troops and vehicles and within each Elan banding from 6 to 1 activation begins with the initiative holding player. When activated each character can choose TWO actions, these include move, ranged fire, melee, hunker down, special action, unique action. The character may choose two different actions OR the same one twice with a modifier; very flexible in battle. Ranged fire and melee are based on opposed single D6 rolls with modifiers for troop type and weapon ratings. This super fast system results in targets being Killed, Struck or Winged by attacks. Killed removes them from play while the other two results negatively effect their further performance in the game. The hunker down action allows characters to take cover against attack while special actions cover those things detailed in the USE ME Advanced Rules and unique actions are created when needed for a scenario.

In small games of USE ME every character acts on its own, this is a skirmish, but in larger games a simple mechanic allows you to combine three to ten characters into one SQUAD which then acts under its leader in play. All actions for squads are no more complex than for single characters.

The USE ME advanced rules. After the rules engine for the game come the advanced rules which give a real flavour of the Post Apocalyptic setting including twenty one character profiles that specialise your character for play. These include Gas Masks, Hazmat Suits, EMP Pulse Protection, Night Vision and Poison Gas among others. Then unique rules for Psionic Abilities among Mutants including telekinesis and mind domination. Following this are unique rules for Contamination in play, Minefields and Rad-Pits. All of these are useful and full of the flavour of fighting in a world destroyed by a cataclysmic event.

Factions of a Wasted World. The rules set is rounded off with seven army lists which give suggested forces for the kind of armies you would see in a Post Apocalypse setting. These are only suggestions but they give you an excellent jumping off point for your scenarios. They include Scavengers, Mutants, Machine Minds, Knights of the Relics, Cultists and more. The booklet includes suggested miniatures and pictures from the 15mm.co.uk range including HOF75 Wasteland Uber Goons, HOF56 and HOF74 Cultists and SHM22 The Controller among others. You can find these on the website but of course you can use any miniatures you like with the game.

After the USE ME game finishes. Most of the time you will want to play a quick game, cause some carnage and send a lot of little metal men to their dooms. But it need not be the end if you want to carry on. USE ME has mechanics in it for updating your Force Roster (with your troops on it from the game just finished) to see what happened to those characters who were Struck or Winged in play to see if they recover in time for the next encounter. This makes rolling those dice nail biting and really important for the next scenario. Plus you can make a second lot of rolls for any characters Killed in play to see if a slim chance of them rejoining your forces for the next game.

Solo Play in USE ME. This booklet contains a solo play system which means that when no human opponent is present or if you want a practise you are able to do this alone and at a time of your choosing. The solo play addition allows forces to be fielded and statures chosen to determine their behaviours against your actions in game.

USE ME is a pocket rule system that does not pretend to be anything else. Its fast, its fun and its affordable. With many rule systems these days taking themselves far too seriously and with some being priced at an amount that makes purchasing them a long term goal for most of us USE ME gives you what it promises and in a format that will make you smile.

—description from the publisher

USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer interested in a World War Two setting.. From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up any of your miniatures and get playing in minutes. This complete rules system is printed in A6 format which is a booklet which will fit in your pocket (110mm by 180mm) making it truly portable as well as fantastic value for your money. USE ME is just that...its for you!

The writer of the adapted WW2 version of USE ME is O.G.Joel who is an avid fan of wargaming in across the battlefields of the second world war from the earliest starts in the Spanish Civil War right through the fall of Stalingrad and then the Invasion of Europe and the destruction of Berlin. Between him and all of us we decided that the popular USE ME game system was ideally suited to fast play WW2 in 15mm to 20mm scale; from a few figures a side to squad level and also battle. The game can do all of this and in every theatre of the biggest war in human history. It will work with any collection of WW2 miniatures and vehicles.

The USE ME game system. You will need some miniatures, some terrain and a few six sided dice (D6's) to play the game. This is not much and indeed most of the time everything in play needs only 1D6 roll to make a decision. In the game all miniatures and vehicles are de-marked into of CHARACTERS (from Infantry to Vehicles and everything else) and these into TYPES which allow you to create anything you want from this period with a few tables of TYPES with points costs attached.

The Turn Sequence of the game follows a simple path of firstly the Initiative Phase where all players roll 1D6 and the highest roll wins the initiative for the turn (important later on!). Then the main part of the turn, the In Play Phase where all characters in the game are activated not by the side upon which they fight but by their Elan Rating. This means better troops and vehicles move and fight before poorer quality troops and vehicles and within each Elan banding from 6 to 1 activation begins with the initiative holding player. When activated each character can choose TWO actions, these include move, ranged fire, melee, hunker down, special action, unique action. The character may choose two different actions OR the same one twice with a modifier; very flexible in battle. Ranged fire and melee are based on opposed single D6 rolls with modifiers for troop type and weapon ratings. This super fast system results in targets being Killed, Struck or Winged by attacks. Killed removes them from play while the other two results negatively effect their further performance in the game. The hunker down action allows characters to take cover against attack while special actions cover those things detailed in the USE ME Advanced Rules and unique actions are created when needed for a scenario.

In small games of USE ME every character acts on its own, this is a skirmish, but in larger games a simple mechanic allows you to combine three to ten characters into one SQUAD which then acts under its leader in play. All actions for squads are no more complex than for single characters. In this fashion you can go from ten miniatures a side to one hundred in ten squads and have a battle that takes only a fraction more time to play.

The USE ME advanced rules. The central tenant of the USE ME World War Two system is simplicity and in that regard it performs very well but sometimes players will want to add some advanced rules that, while adding complexity, also add to the period feel of the second world war. An Off Table Action Phase adds abstract high level air and artillery support while the option of inserting further forces once the game begins is also covered. For WW2 in detail the Special Actions place additions to characters for 'Anti-Aircraft', 'Anti-Tank', 'Commissar', 'Communications Specialist', 'Flamethrower', 'Medic' and 'Partizan' among other bolt on specialisations. This is followed by Special Character Types allowing 'Cavalry', 'Aircraft' and 'Dog Mines' along with 'Trains' and 'Wagons' for more unique scenarios on the table.

Following on from the advanced rules and additional character types are the unique WW2 mechanics for the game system including 'Firing for Suppression and Advanced Machine Gun Rules'. Then a random movement and action system for including panicked Civilians in your games of USE ME.

After the USE ME game finishes. Most of the time you will want to play a quick game, cause some carnage and send a lot of little metal men to their dooms. But it need not be the end if you want to carry on. USE ME has mechanics in it for updating your Force Roster (with your troops on it from the game just finished) to see what happened to those characters who were Struck or Winged in play to see if they recover in time for the next encounter. This makes rolling those dice nail biting and really important for the next scenario. Plus you can make a second lot of rolls for any characters Killed in play to see if a slim chance of them rejoining your forces for the next game.

THE LARGEST INCLUSION IN THIS VERSION OF USE ME IS THE HIGHLY DETAILED ARMY LISTS FOR THE PRIMARY NATIONS IN WW2. THE GERMAN ARMY, THE USA, GREAT BRITAIN, RUSSIA, ITALY AND THE JAPANESE IMPERIAL FORCES ARE ALL LISTED BY HISTORICAL UNIT TYPE AND THEN USE ME EQUIVALENT SO THAT YOU CAN PLACE ANY UNIT ACCURATELY INTO BALANCED PLAY.

Solo Play in USE ME. This booklet contains a solo play system which means that when no human opponent is present or if you want a practise you are able to do this alone and at a time of your choosing. The solo play addition allows forces to be fielded and statures chosen to determine their behaviours against your actions in game.

USE ME is a pocket rule system that does not pretend to be anything else. Its fast, its fun and its affordable. With many rule systems these days taking themselves far too seriously and with some being priced at an amount that makes purchasing them a long term goal for most of us USE ME gives you what it promises and in a format that will make you smile. Try it, you might want to USE ME yourself!

—description from the publisher

The fifth title in the excellent and expanding USE ME game system series concerns itself with the war between the states for 15mm scale miniatures. Skirmish, Battle, Campaign plus single bases or element bases. USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer. From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up any of your miniatures and vehicles and get playing in minutes. USE ME is just that...its for you!

We wanted to move away from the fantastical with the fifth book in the USE ME range and when we looked at what was most requested. It turned out to be a set of rules for the American Civil War. You can learn about the other titles in the USE ME series by looking at our website but in terms of this title our aim was to produce a set of mechanics that would allow ACW wargamers to achieve several goals with one affordable title. A set of rules that could cover skirmish gaming as well as massed battles. A set which would allow the visceral experience of single based characters being removed from regiments under fire but would also allow miniatures based upon 'elements' to be used as well. Truly broad and versatile. We think that Gavin Syme has achieved this with UM005.

Author of the USE ME science fiction and fantasy rules sets Gavin Syme wanted to bring those ideas on-board with ACW but also to do more with it. Enlisting the help of veteran ACW wargamer Kurt Benson a set of mechanics were distilled that capture the glory, the horror and the intense action of not only battlefields like Gettysburg but also small actions too. Integrating a unique mechanism for allowing single or element based miniatures to act in the same way in play made the rules set something really different but also really easy to use with any ACW collection. Now the War between the States can be played USE ME style, ten to a thousand miniatures.

The USE ME game system. You will need some miniatures, some terrain and a few six sided dice (D6's) to play the game. This is not much and indeed most of the time everything in play needs only 1D6 roll to make a decision. In the game all miniatures and vehicles are de-marked into of CHARACTERS (from Infantry to Cavalry, Artillery, Balloons and everything else) and these into TYPES which allow you to create anything you want from this period with a few tables of TYPES with points costs attached.
