The Turn Sequence of the game follows a simple path of firstly the Initiative Phase where all players roll 1D6 and the highest roll wins the initiative for the turn (important later on!). Then the main part of the turn, the In Play Phase where all characters in the game are activated not by the side upon which they fight but by their Elan Rating. This means better troops and vehicles move and fight before poorer quality troops and vehicles and within each Elan banding from 6 to 1 activation begins with the initiative holding player. When activated each character can choose TWO actions, these include move, ranged fire, melee, hunker down, special action, unique action. The character may choose two different actions OR the same one twice with a modifier; very flexible in battle. Ranged fire and melee are based on opposed single D6 rolls with modifiers for troop type and weapon ratings. This super fast system results in targets being Killed, Struck or Winged by attacks. Killed removes them from play while the other two results negatively effect their further performance in the game. The hunker down action allows characters to take cover against attack while special actions cover those things detailed in the USE ME Advanced Rules and unique actions are created when needed for a scenario.

In small games of USE ME every character acts on its own, this is a skirmish, but in larger games a simple mechanic allows you to combine many characters into one REGIMENT which then acts under its COMMAND THREE in play. All actions for regiments are no more complex than for single characters. In this fashion you can go from ten miniatures a side to two hundred in a dozen regiments and have a battle that takes only a fraction more time to play.

Single Basing to Element Basing. One unique aspect of this title is its ability to make use of any miniatures you already have regardless of their basing status. The rules assume miniatures are on single bases but intuitive mechanics are present that allow element bases (bases with multiple miniatures on them) to be used also with no added complexity. Either or both at the same time. Indeed a battle with element bases plays even faster! Use all your collection.

The USE ME advanced rules. This part of the system really brings in the American Civil War feel. An Off Table Action phase adds abstract options such as Scouting, Extra Rations, Distant Guns and even Faith in the army. Inserting further forces is also covered. Detailed special actions follow covering 'Shortages' which allow you to play with forces that could run out of ammunition. Also a series of 'Traits' for both the Union and Confederate sides to allow personalisation of characters and regiments. The rules then cover Observation Balloons, Gatling Guns and the Telegraph. All for adding flavour and oddities to your games.

Following on from this is a section with 'Famous Formations' which gives typical and named formations from the North and the South. Providing ideas and templates for your infantry, cavalry and artillery.

After the USE ME game finishes. Most of the time you will want to play a quick game, cause some carnage and send a lot of little metal men to their dooms. But it need not be the end if you want to carry on. USE ME has mechanics in it for updating your Force Roster (with your troops on it from the game just finished) to see what happened to those characters who were Struck or Winged in play to see if they recover in time for the next encounter. This makes rolling those dice nail biting and really important for the next scenario. Plus you can make a second lot of rolls for any characters Killed in play to see if a slim chance of them rejoining your forces for the next game.

Solo Play in USE ME. This booklet contains a solo play system which means that when no human opponent is present or if you want a practise you are able to do this alone and at a time of your choosing. The solo play addition allows forces to be fielded and statures chosen to determine their behaviours against your actions in game.

USE ME is a pocket rule system that does not pretend to be anything else. Its fast, its fun and its affordable. With many rule systems these days taking themselves far too seriously and with some being priced at an amount that makes purchasing them a long term goal for most of us USE ME gives you what it promises and in a format that will make you smile. Try it, you might want to USE ME yourself!

—description from the publisher

USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer interested in ANY fantasy setting.

From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour or two for the largest battles. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up any of you Fantasy miniatures and get playing in minutes. This complete rules system is printed in A6 format which is a booklet which will fit in your pocket (110mm by 180mm) making it truly portable as well as fantastic value for your money. USE ME is just that...its for you!

I began with an idea and the manuscript of the first book and set to work. With the help of Elton Waters and Bob Minadeo plus some in house testing too I developed a fantasy mechanic that is really actually rather super on the table. Keeping in mind that being short of gaming time meant we and most of the players who would want this game were knackered (read as 'beat' if you are American) and being generic was vital to allow the use of any and all of your collection of miniatures was also vital we came up with USE ME 15mm Fantasy. Skirmish and Mass Battle in one place suitable for the most veteran player and the total novice and tactically motivated not sales price of product. Interested....read on.

The USE ME game system for 15mm Fantasy.

You will need some miniatures, some terrain and a few six sided dice (D6's) to play the game. This is not much and indeed most of the time everything in play needs only 1D6 roll to make a decision. In the game all miniatures, monsters and siege engines and so forth are de-marked into of CHARACTERS and these into TYPES which allow you to create anything you want in a fantasy setting with a few tables of TYPES with points costs attached.

The Turn Sequence of the game follows a simple path of firstly the Initiative Phase where all players roll 1D6 and the highest roll wins the initiative for the turn (important later on!). Then the main part of the turn, the In Play Phase where all characters in the game are activated not by the side upon which they fight but by their Elan Rating. This means better troops move and fight before poorer quality troops and within each Elan banding from 6 to 1 activation begins with the initiative holding player. When activated each character can choose TWO actions, these include move, ranged fire, melee, defend, special action, unique action. The character may choose two different actions OR the same one twice with a modifier; very flexible in battle. Ranged fire and melee are based on opposed single D6 rolls with modifiers for troop type and weapon ratings. This super fast system results in targets being Killed, Struck or Winged by attacks. Killed removes them from play while the other two results negatively effect their further performance in the game. The defend action allows characters to take cover against attack while special actions cover those things detailed in the USE ME Advanced Rules and unique actions are created when needed for a scenario. The USE ME Advanced Rules cover off table actions such as bonuses and attacks at a battlefield level plus advanced mechanics for giving your Personalities extra skills for being a General, Hero, Cleric or a Wizard. There is a full magic system with four 'schools' to choose from. There are character traits which give your troops abilities that make them more Savage or more Nimble or Rage Filled etc. This tailors to different fantasy racial types. That covers the skirmish level of the rules which means from a handful to a couple of dozen characters but what of the battle level of the USE ME Fantasy rules?

It is at the battle level that these rules really flex their muscles with whole formations of troops marching into a maelstrom that can make use of more than a hundred miniatures per player with ease. To take the rules to the battle level the 'Rule of Three' is brought into play and nifty it is too!

All miniatures are based as singles (its what you all asked for to end the tyranny of differing basing conventions) and to turn these individuals into 'formations' (the USE ME term for Units of any size) the Rule of Three is used. make a Formation you need THREE characters to command it, these are typically an officer, a standard bearer and a musician of your chosen racial and troop type. These three become the COMMAND THREE of the Formation and remain at its core until the formation is destroyed or the game ends or you elect to dissolve the formation. You then add Characters to the command core of the formation in sets of THREE at a time. Each set of three is placed in base to base contact with the Command Three building out from it as you go so the command ends up in the centre of the formation. There are different types of formations that have different functions but you can build formations up to any size you want for big regiments in big battles. Formations move as one though injured characters can fall behind and get separated (which is very dangerous as normal infantry or cavalry are automatically killed by formations if touched or attacked) and perform attacks based on every three characters in the formation. This allows attacks to be split or focused especially for ranged attacks. Formation melee involves a clash of ranks with the attacks of the front two ranks being counted and you can literally 'grind down' formations and even feed in more troops to the rear of the formation, adding them to bolster its numbers. Injuries in a formation can be assigned to miniatures at the players discretion allowing unparalleled choice in which ranks of your formation are hurt and killed by attacks. This is a very tactical and entertaining way to battle and can enact the largest of conflicts.

After the USE ME game finishes.

Most of the time you will want to play a quick game, cause some carnage and send a lot of little metal men to their dooms. But it need not be the end if you want to carry on. USE ME has mechanics in it for updating your Force Roster (with your troops on it from the game just finished) to see what happened to those characters who were Struck or Winged in play to see if they recover in time for the next encounter. This makes rolling those dice nail biting and really important for the next scenario. Plus you can make a second lot of rolls for any characters Killed in play to see if a slim chance of them rejoining your forces for the next game. A simple system for linking games together follows and then a page containing narrative approaches to forming a whole campaign and ideas of how to do it. The end of the game on the table is just the start.

The Endless Settings of USE ME.

One of the core ideas for this game system was to make it capable of carrying out skirmishes, battles and campaigns in ANY fantasy setting allowing players to import any and all of their own ideas or those from Books, Television or Film into play. The character types table allows you to set your miniatures, your ideas, your setting to the game with little trouble. Just choose the Elan Rating, the Weapons, the Movement rates etc for each of your miniatures and fill out a force roster with their names and statistics; as simple as that. Wars over Magic Rings, Campaigns against Orcs, Pale faced Elves and more...USE ME can do them all.

USE ME is a pocket rule system that does not pretend to be anything else. Its fast, its fun and its affordable. With many rule systems these days taking themselves far too seriously and with some being priced at an amount that makes purchasing them a long term goal for most of us USE ME gives you what it promises and in a format that will make you smile. Try it, you might want to USE ME yourself!

—description from the publisher

The Hedgehog’s Dilemma is a trick-taking game featuring fragile partnerships, shared win and loss conditions, and completely open table talk rules. Based on Schopenhauer’s parable of porcupines illustrating the perils of human intimacy, players navigate fraught relationships with each other to trade cards and build exponential scoring opportunities. By winning tricks, players traverse an endless loop that rewards those who get close—but not too close—to one another.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Spirits of the Wild Awakening: Venomous Snakes Promo
description:
Promo animal boards for Spirits of the Wild: Awakening.

Contains two new animals: Diamondback and Coral Snake. These two boards feature new scoring rules that can cause you to lose points.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer. Games last from five minutes to an hour. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up any of your miniatures and get playing in minutes. USE ME is just that...its for you!

Welcome Partner! We go to the Old West in this the eight title in the USE ME series and the first as a joint work between Gavin Syme and Kurt Benson (who are working not only on another USE ME title but also on a title for Alternative Armies 28mm science fiction MOTH game set in the Ion Age). The pair have created a USE ME title that allows you to skirmish with gun fighters, bank robbers, native americans, towns folk, stage coaches and much more. Fast play rules based on the D6 USE ME engine that would suit 15mm scale miniatures (though it will work with smaller or larger scales with ease). From a few personalities in small scenario to a large skirmish with dozens on each side.

The USE ME game system. You will need some miniatures, some terrain and a few six sided dice (D6's) to play the game. This is not much and indeed most of the time everything in play needs only 1D6 roll to make a decision. Killed removes them from play while the other two results negatively effect their further performance in the game. The hunker down action allows characters to take cover against attack while special actions cover those things detailed in the USE ME Advanced Rules and unique actions are created when needed for a scenario.

The USE ME advanced rules. After the rules engine for the game come the advanced rules which puts you fully into the Old West. Fourteen character profiles that specialise your character for play including Saw Bones, Gatling Man, US Army, Gunslinger amongst others. There are several special character types for the Wild West including Cavalry, Goods Wagons, Stage Coach and Gatling Gun which you can add to your scenarios. Using these advanced mechanics you can expand your gaming and put items into your play that are scenario changers.

Townsfolk in the Old West. The rules contain this special section which allows you to put regular old townfolk into play and then have them act on their own during the game responding to the danger that the characters in play present. You can put one side in charge of protecting the townfolk and make the other bandits or kidnappers. Endless possibilities.

Native Americans. The rules contain a bolt on set of pages which give you rules for fielding Native Americans in your Old West games. Included in the character types are the special rules that govern the war 'red indians' waged war in the nineteenth century meaning scalping and taking captives as well as other honour based behaviours such as the 'Coup Game'.

Example Wild West Characters. A short section gives example profiles for Gunslingers, Townsfolk, Native Americans, Settlers, US Army and so on to save you time and serve as inspiration for your scenarios.

Legends of the Old West and Typical Terrains. Taking research done during the creation of this title the rules contain over fifty entries from history giving you inspiration for your scenarios from real events and people. This is folllowed by a section giving typical terrains for Wild West scenarios such as Plains, Indian Villages, Western Towns and so on telling you what is normally found in each.

Wild West Scenario Seeds and Historical Forces. Combining accounts of the forces that fought and died in the Old West with some typical notions of scenarios such as Raids, Ambushes, Feuds Showdowns and more you can create games based on actual events and name those who might well have been there. This is followed by historical firearms which suggests ways to take actual guns such as Remingtons or Colts and transfer them into USE ME profiles from the rules.

After the USE ME game finishes. Most of the time you will want to play a quick game, cause some carnage and send a lot of little metal men to their dooms. But it need not be the end if you want to carry on. USE ME has mechanics in it for updating your Force Roster (with your troops on it from the game just finished) to see what happened to those characters who were Struck or Winged in play to see if they recover in time for the next encounter. This makes rolling those dice nail biting and really important for the next scenario. Plus you can make a second lot of rolls for any characters Killed in play to see if a slim chance of them rejoining your forces for the next game.

Solo Play in USE ME. This booklet contains a solo play system which means that when no human opponent is present or if you want a practise you are able to do this alone and at a time of your choosing. The solo play addition allows forces to be fielded and statures chosen to determine their behaviours against your actions in game.

USE ME is a pocket rule system that does not pretend to be anything else. Its fast, its fun and its affordable. With many rule systems these days taking themselves far too seriously and with some being priced at an amount that makes purchasing them a long term goal for most of us USE ME gives you what it promises and in a format that will make you smile.

Originally offered as a $19 add-on to help offset tariffs during the Flock Together: Birds of a Feather Eggspansion Kickstarter campaign.

2 new characters to play - Laura Wingalls (Greater-Prairie Hen) and Doc Hollilay (Wyandotte Rooster).
Booster with new Belgian words in Dutch available with the new Just One new version.

Carbone is a semi-cooperative strategy board game where players lead rival nations in a tense race for global dominance — all while trying to avoid a climate catastrophe. Build your Society, launch diplomatic strikes, and manage your carbon emissions… because if the planet burns, you all lose. But if someone reaches 7 cards first, only the highest score wins.

Each round, players collect coins, draw cards, and choose between developing their Society or playing a Diplomacy card to disrupt opponents. At the end of your turn, your emissions are counted — and an equal number of cards are revealed from the Carbon pile. If that pile runs out before a Society is complete, it's game over for everyone.

—description from the publisher

Changes in setup:
Place the Offer tile above the board. Randomly choose 4 Turbine Development tiles and place them on the Offer tile. Put remaining Turbine Development tiles back to the box.

Changes in gameplay:

     Gaining Turbine Development tiles: During the game while performing the Industrialize action to build a Turbine, you can spend Achievement tokens to gain one of the Turbine Development tiles. The cost depends on the cost of the turbine in meeples and it’s equal to 1/2/2/3 Achievement tokens. Place the tile on your Player board in place of just placed Turbine. You may only gain 1 Turbine Development tile during the game.
     Using Turbine Development tiles: When you perform an Energize action, get a turbine bonus normally associated with all your placed Turbines and from the Turbine Development tile. Each Turbine Development tile gives their benefits a number of times that depends on the level of that turbine (1/2/2/3) depicted as ‘?’ on Turban Development tiles.

Discover Luxembourg is a fun and educational journey through Luxembourg, encouraging players to discover the country while testing their knowledge and business skills. With elements like challenges, fines, and cash rewards, it blends trivia with light economic strategy in a race-style board game format.

Gameplay
Designed for 2 to 6 players, the game begins with each player selecting a token and receiving a starting amount of €1,000. Players take turns drawing question cards and answering trivia. A correct answer allows the player to move forward on the board and earn money from the bank based on the points earned. Special spaces on the board—like Radar, Stock Crash, Challenge, and “2X”—add excitement by introducing penalties, duels between players, or bonus opportunities. Players can also negotiate loans with others or the bank if they run out of money.

Goal
The objective is to be the first to reach the “Arrival” space or to finish the game with the highest amount of money. The first player to reach the end receives a €1,000 bonus, and players may decide whether to end the game or continue until all reach the finish. The winner is the player with the most assets at the end of the game.

Overall
Easy to learn and packed with interaction, this game offers a balance of knowledge, luck, and strategic decision-making—all set in the context of exploring Luxembourg.

Bunker-B brings you the legendary post-apocalyptic survival experience you love – now with completely new content! This expansion/standalone version can be played on its own or mixed with the original Bunker deck to double the variety and chaos of your games.

Every card in Bunker-B is fresh and original, giving you new professions, baggage items, hobbies, and unexpected twists for your survival scenarios. And just like the Bunker 3.2 Edition, this set features illustrations on every Profession, Baggage, and Hobby card. Whether you’re a newcomer or a veteran prepper, Bunker-B is a must-have addition to your collection.

Bunker: Generation Alpha is a standalone entry in the iconic Bunker survival series. Play it on its own or combine it with Bunker and Bunker-B to create a massive deck of nearly 1,000 unique cards for endless replayability!

A global catastrophe is imminent, and humanity’s fate hangs in the balance. You’re among the lucky few standing at the entrance to a survival bunker – a chance to outlive the apocalypse and maybe even become the heroes who rebuild civilization. But there’s a catch: only half of you can make it inside. The rest will be left outside to face the end of the world...

Each player receives a set of random cards: Character, Phobia, Superpower, Baggage, and more. Over several rounds, you reveal your traits, argue, form alliances, and decide who will be the most valuable to humanity’s survival after the bunker doors finally open. Every round, someone is voted out – and it might be you.

Owners of the original Bunker games get free access to the official companion app, featuring 50 unique catastrophes, voice-acted events, cinematic videos, and an interactive "Survival Story" finale that makes every game session unforgettable.

—description from the publisher

In Shards of Creation, harness the primal forces of Brandon Sanderson's Cosmere! This innovative trick-taking game is designed for players of all skill levels.

Every Shard is an aspect of divinity: Autonomy, Cultivation, Devotion, Dominion, Honor, Odium, Preservation, and Ruin. Each is an entirely unique suit, with different values and abilities.

Will you win by focusing on one Shard’s influence, or by forging a coalition of Shards? Shuffle up a different combination each time you play, making every game a new experience!

Arrange cards in both halves of an equation to get the greatest possible number.
A limited number of number cards and operation symbols are drawn.
People then try to arrange the cards in a limited time to find the greatest possible combination.
With a limited number of vowel and consonant cards, people try to find the word worth to most points (like in Scrabble, some letters are worth more than others).

Tiberpunk: Streets of Rome takes you to an alternate version of ancient Rome during the reign of Emperor Trajan, where the crash of an alien spacecraft has altered the course of history. All extraterrestrial visitors perished, but their advanced technologies survived — and became an integral part of the Empire. Rome has maintained its social hierarchy and cultural traditions, but now its streets are patrolled by drones, and access to power can be gained through implants and hacking devices.

This game offers a unique perspective on classical Rome. You take on the roles of those who strive to rise from the city’s poor districts. In a brutal environment of gang rivalry and technological chaos, you will complete missions, control drones, upgrade your skills, pray at temples, and recover artifacts from alien ships — all to outpace your rivals and achieve the objectives faster.

Tiberpunk: Streets of Rome is a game for 2–4 players with a playtime of approximately 90–120 minutes. Combining sandbox elements with a race to complete missions, it offers a wide array of tactical options, strategic planning, and character development. Thanks to the balanced action system, diverse goal combinations, and over 20 storylines, each game unfolds in a new way — delivering a truly unique experience every time.

—description from the publisher

In the world of Bloom Hollow, you are a tribe of Bloomlings - plant-like, sentient beings racing to build, harvest, and prepare before the deadly season known as The Hollows returns. During the seasons of Bloomfalls, Hostrig, and Everdrain, gather resources, power structures, and flip base tiles strategically to reveal new slots for construction. Establish bio-organic industries to convert materials into nutrient-rich reserves, carefully carried underground as valuable victory points.

—description from the publisher

In Harvest Town: Travel and Encounter, a large number of characters have been added, each with different initial abilities.
You can also travel to other players' [Homes] to make new friends and encounter new stories!
Many characters will greatly assist your town's development, while others will disrupt it.
You'll also gain assistance from [Footprints] during your travels, making travel an essential part of your development!

—description from the designer

Galgje Classic is a deduction game in which you have to try to guess words tactically. The advantage is that you don't have to come up with the words yourself, so you can start right away! Choose your letters carefully and prevent the hangman from being completely colored in. Galgje Classic is an exciting game that puts your vocabulary to the test.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Happy DayZ: American Dream – Other Promo Cards

A set of 5 Rider Cards, 5 Action Cards, and 5 Ambassador Cards for Brink designed by the community.

Plan of Action
Shadow Council

boardgame: Hello Kitty and Friends: Reach for the Stars Game
description:
Hello Kitty Reach For The Stars Game is a new way to roll with Hello Kitty and Friends. Packaged inside the cutest Hello Kitty zippered silicone carry pouch are 9 exclusive Hello Kitty and Friends mini-figures. Roll the figures like dice into fun and funny poses! Do any of the poses match the pictures on your card? Think fast to spot the match and reach for the Star to win points! This fast paced game has simultaneous play meaning players never have to wait for their turn.

How to play:

     ROLL the adorable figures like dice! 
     THINK fast to spot a match. 
     REACH for the star to win!


—description from publisher

Bury your meeples, dodge the plague, tidy your turrets and challenge other cleaners in this medieval fast-paced game of castle cleaning, fortress polishing and cut-throat competition.

Each turn, you move as many of your cleaning meeples up to 5 spaces each.  You can pick up bodies, food waste or manure and take them to the cemetery, compost heap or manure pile.  You can clean up oil from the battlements.  All these earn gold.

But when you move bodies, you roll a die and may die of the plague and become a resource to harvest.  You can also attack or be attacked, with a variation of a rock paper scissors duelling action.

When one team of cleaning meeples is dead, add up the gold and the player with the most loot wins.

—description from the publisher

The locomotive is standing at the station…

Each player starts the game with their own locomotive. During the game, they attach more carriages to it and try to load them with appropriate cargo inspired by Julian Tuwim’s famous poem.

Players must hurry to load their carriages, because at any moment the train might start moving and leave the station. The countdown to departure is marked by four special cards in the deck, each showing a line from the poem:

Suddenly – a whistle!
Suddenly – a hiss!
Steam – whoosh!
Wheels – go swish!

When the fourth such card appears, the trains depart! Players earn points for the cargo they managed to place in their carriages.

Między wierszami is a series of games based on classic children’s poems. All games in this series are beautifully illustrated by Maciej Szymanowicz.

—description form the publisher

Are you ready to snuggle up and roll your way to victory? Nap and Roll is a cozy and fun board game where you guide your cute little dice pets around a fluffy blanket. The goal? Get your dice to the perfect spot – whether it’s time for a nap or a play session!

Nap And Roll isn’t just a game – it’s also a proud 2nd place finisher in the BGG 1-Card Contest, and I even snagged 1st place as a New Designer in the same competition!

HOW TO PLAY

Jumping Up On the Bed (Turns 1–3)
Player takes turns placing one pet die onto any unoccupied space on the 3x4 quilt, with its        showing on top.

Getting Comfortable (Turns 4+)
On your turn, move one of your pet dice by rotating it over its bottom face edge onto an adjacent unoccupied space. This changes the number showing on top.

Going Under the Covers:
If a pet die moves off one side of the quilt, it sneaks under the covers and pops out on the matching unoccupied space on the opposite side — across the same row or column.

Bed Side (3x4) - Naptime
Once a pet die is showing a        on top, it finds the perfect spot and lies down to nap. A napping pet stays put for the rest of the game — it can’t be moved again.

Ottoman Side (3x3) - Playtime
Once a pet die is showing a       on top, it wanders off to play. A playing pet is removed from the quilt for the rest of the game — it won’t return again.

Winning the Game
The first player to have all three of their pet dice showing a     on top wins the game!

—description form the publisher

Exploding Pigeon is a little like Pictionary, a little like Charades, a little like a hot potato, and a LOT of chaotic fun! Exploding Pigeon is the rapid-fire challenge passing family game that'll change your parties forever. Shake the Pigeon to reveal your hilarious challenge -- it could be drawing, miming, or a quick round of rock, paper, scissors. Hurry and do it before passing the Pigeon to the other team! Scramble to complete as many challenges as you can, just don't get caught with the Pigeon when the timer runs out!

How to play:

     Start the timer.
     Act, draw, or battle … but whatever you do, DO IT FAST!
     Pass the pigeon before it explodes!


—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
The first collaborative management game to experience life in an autonomous community!
Starting from a few hectares of land and an old converted barn, you will create your oasis, a place of ecological and autonomous living, where the values of friendliness and sobriety prevail. It is up to you to build this haven of peace in the heart of nature, mastering the art of cooperation. Each turn, you will divide the tasks of community life: cultivating, chopping wood, building huts and facilities, expressing your creativity... It is up to you to decide together.
The well-being of the community depends on the harmonious use of your resources, your talents, and the unforeseen events that await you.
When the community is thriving and its members are happy, new people join the Oasis of Sunflowers to help it grow. You all win together when you realize the community's projects and the dreams of each of its members.

Harvest Business Transfer is the second game in the Harvest series. Players assume the role of ordinary people who stumble into another world and use their business acumen to carve out a niche in this new world. Players build decks and place workers within existing resources to develop their productivity, thereby influencing the market and increasing stock prices. Ultimately, the game ends when the market price crashes, and the player with the most points wins. The market and stocks dynamically evolve based on everyone's productivity, resulting in different outcomes in each round. Players interested in worker placement, deck building, engine building, and the stock market will enjoy this game.

—description from the publisher

Olympissimo roughly replicates the mechanics of Trivial Pursuit. The pieces are moved around an athletics track. Each square is marked with a colored circle, which corresponds to a sport category (athletic, combat, winter, etc.). The player must answer the question on a card. If the answer is correct, he rolls the die again. After three consecutive incorrect answers, the piece leaves the Olympic track and returns to training, then a teammate piece starts. The game ends when three athletes have crossed the finish line.

Wonderland's War Duel is a 2 player version of the original game with it's own twists and flavor. This tug-of-war inspired version of the classic bag builder has 3 win conditions instead of the "most points win" of the original. It plays in 60-90 minutes and takes up a fraction of the table space of Wonderland's War classic.

(Wonderland's War the original game we are referencing can be found here on BGG. It features bag building, area control, and card drafting. You take on one of the asymmetric characters in Wonderland and fight to be the leader after the war has concluded.)

In Wonderland's War you are building your bag of special powers to battle against other players that are also vying for control of Wonderland! Use strategy to prefect your choices and with a little luck the madness will be avoided and you will be victorious.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Add the majestic Whale Shark, the largest living fish and the bright Lionfish, a spiny venomous beauty, to all your Similo games!

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
A light strategy family board game about the Olympic Balloon Races during the Paris Exposition Universelle of 1900 for 2-6 players.

The game features balloon game pieces that float above the 24" game board featuring the map of Paris.

Original water color style art highlights the balloons, monuments and gardens of Paris.

Novel mechanics force you to change altitudes and catch favorable winds to navigate over the targets of the race.

—description from the publisher

Magic Arena is a dynamic strategic card game set in a dark fantasy kingdom ruled by a bored and wicked king. To escape his eternal boredom, the king hosts the legendary Magic Tournament — a brutal competition where 32 unique magical characters fight for glory and powerful artifacts.

Each player builds a deck by choosing from 32 powerful characters, each with their own unique abilities. During the game, players take turns attacking, defending, and outsmarting their opponents.

Gameplay combines deep strategic planning, bluffing, and tactical decision-making. The core mechanic challenges you to launch such powerful attacks — using character abilities and potions — that your opponent is unable to defend. If they fail, they must sacrifice one of their characters. The winner is the one who eliminates all opposing characters.

You can also organize tournaments with friends, where the victor receives a powerful Artifact — granting permanent benefits in your future game sessions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs Progression Game
description:
From the 1967 promotional material:

"A great new FUN GAME is being made available on a national basis by Standard Brands' "ROYAL GELATIN." The premium 
will be a "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs Progression Game," available with 6-pack purchase of the product."

The gameplay is a simple roll and move game where you roll a six-sided die to determine where you move to each turn.  Winning is as simple as being first to get to the end.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description:
'Superficial - The Skin-Deep Game for Shallow People' - is a cooperative adult party game for ages 18+, 2 to 10 Players. Superficial is the one game where you can let your inside thoughts have an outside voice. Go ahead and let those snap-judgements fly! Make sure to invite that one friend who has no filter--you know who I'm talking about! Superficial is perfect for any size group of 2-10 players or more!

If Cards Against Decency and Guess Who It Is had a baby--it would be Superficial! Superficial is a hilarious new adult party game made to liven up even the most boring party. Its easy to learn, very engaging, and perfect for your next gathering, gift, or game night! The game comes equipped with 50 Question Cards and 90 diverse Character Cards. The questions are um....intrusive, and each character has their own completely unique vibe.

While Superficial can’t be summed up in a sentence or two, here’s the gist: 30 character cards, including ONE “mystery person” are laid out face up. The players will then ask questions as judgey as a wealthy aunt in hopes of sussing out the mystery person. Sample questions include *Is this person a vegetarian? *Does this person shave their pubes? *Does this person play video games til 3am?

The ones who don’t meet the judgey standard, will flipped over. The aim of the game is to flip every card BUT the mystery person. If you’ve judged correctly you’ll be left with the mystery person, and you WIN! If not, your lose. But don't worry, either way, you can play again!

—description from the designer

Who says that average is boring? To win this game, you must keep close to the middle of the road. In Middleman, the players compete at whose answers are most consistently middling. Every player writes the answer to the paper and the one in the middle gets the point. Travel version comes with 50 task, that will offer more than average entertainment for everyone!
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description: The game includes 50 different picture cards with unusual objects with various uses and functions. Players try to guess or bluff the use/function of the item in play, and write a description on a note. These descriptions plus the right answer are then read out loud, so the players try to pick the right answer among the false ones.
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
"They came from beyond time, beyond reason... and they brought friends.

Strap in, Commander—this is the ultimate solo sci-fi slugfest! They Came In Threes! cranks the chaos to maximum warp. For the first time ever, three of designer Hermann Luttmann’s bizarre, brain-busting solo science fiction games are gathered in one battle-scarred box. Lead brave Galactic Marines against interdimensional horrors, titanic biomech monstrosities, and insectoid swarms that shouldn't exist—but definitely do.

This deluxe package includes:


     Invaders from Dimension X – A reality-warping solo game where your foes don’t follow logic... or sanity.
     Attack of the 50 Foot Colossi – Massive, rock-like entities stomp across a doomed world. Can you outwit their merciless programming?
     Space Vermin from Beyond – Bugs. Big ones. Hungry ones. And they're coming for your outpost in waves.
     A Slick New FAQ & Scenario Book – Includes 3 scenarios, 8 counters for Invaders from Dimension X and an FAQ for each title.


(Six D6 dice are included.)

Each game offers fast, intuitive solo play with unpredictable enemies, evolving scenarios, and that signature “what the heck just happened?” flavor. Whether you’re repelling alien warlords, dodging titanic footfalls, or holding the last line against a tide of teeth and slime, They Came In Threes! delivers old-school thrills in glorious technicolor terror. Three games. One box. Unlimited weirdness."

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Panzer Dice: Card Version is a special edition of the classic Roll and Write game, Panzer Dice, focused on battles between a Russian tank and German tanks during World War II. This time, we’ve replaced the game sheets with cards: Panzer Cards, which are activated by the Panzer Dice; Terrain Cards, which represent advancing; and Action Cards, along with cards for infantry gear, ammunition, fuel, and airstrikes.

The gameplay follows the same system as the Roll and Write version, but instead of writing on sheets, player places cards.
You can choose to play with a T-34, KV-1, or SU-152 tank, each with different firepower and movement capabilities.

—description from the designer

What's the Good Word? is a clever and simple word association game with brain teasing hints, intense competition, and plenty of laughs!

The goal is to guess the GOOD word while avoiding the BAD word, but there's a catch. You can only use ONE WORD! One player gives a one word hint and their teammates respond with a one word guess. You cannot use the bad word as a hint, and you automatically lose if the bad word is guessed. Remember, the other team can hear your hints too, so don't give them too much information!

Will you be clever enough to guess the word before your opponents?

—description from the designer

Herd Immunity is a card game about controlling infectuous diseases. Four different viruses are spreading, and you must carefully arrange your cards to contain them.

Play tricks to acquire cards to place in you city. Each card has four corners displaying different diseases, and you may place them to overlap previously placed sections. Clever planning and placement will reduce the final infection values, and the player with the lowest total score wins. But for the disease most widely spread all players reach herd immunity, and this disease does not count towards your score.

—description from the designer

This all-new edition of First To Worst brings the multi-generational game of polarizing priorities to the colorful world of the CARE BEARS! Jam-packed with adorable Care Bears art, including all of your favorite characters like Grumpy Bear, Tenderheart Bear and Cheer Bear.

In this engaging collaborative game, players try to get into each others' heads by guessing how fellow players rank a wide variety of interesting topics. Bubble Baths? French Fries? Sharing? How would YOU rank? There are almost 200 topics in the game, each one with a charming and unique Care Bears illustration!

THE RANKER secretly ranks five random things from 1 to 5. Then, as a team, the rest of the players now try to guess the exact order chosen by THE RANKER. Get more right than wrong to WIN the game as a team!

—description from the publisher

Get ready for the ultimate family game night with the electronic cube that makes you throw, dance, grab, find, freeze, shout, and roll. It’s kids versus parents in Beat the Parents: Let’s Roll, a new way to play the classic Beat the Parents board game with a digital twist.

The electronic cube comes with six games built in: 

     Beat Box Rhyming Challenge
     Cube-Boom Keep-it-Moving Game
     Freeze Dance Competition
     Scavenger Hunt
     And more


This cube can sense movement and impact to allow for a variety of fun physical challenges. Includes recessed LED button that changes colors, plus lights and sounds to direct gameplay.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Electronic Versions of Non-Electronic Games

description:
Bright Future is an inspiring roll & write game where you and your friends are competing to ensure building materials of the good future for individuals and societies. The story of the game is based in Finn Church Aid work that strive to build more sustainable and safer future. In Bright Future game you’ll become a part of this important mission. During the game you’ll collect different building materials such as water, food and education, and then strategically locate them to ensure that every society can flourish.

In Bright Future game every round two dice will be rolled and all players must draw the symbols to their playing coupons according to rolled dice. 6 times in the game a player can change a die number +/-1. On the first turn two symbols can be drawn anywhere unless they're next to each other. Starting from the next turn every two symbols have to be drawn next to those already drawn on a coupon. Two symbols drawn must also be next each other when players draw them.

In Bright Future game players get points from building materials of the good future (different materials around a worker or a society of worker give 3-15 points). Separate worker societies give points according the number of societies times the number of workers in the biggest society. Any unused +/- change gives 2 points. There're also some extra scoring players can include the game after a couple of games already played.

—description from the publisher

A witty party card game celebrating iconic quotes and images from most popular Soviet movies! Nostalgia and cult classics meets modern fun. Developed in partnership with Mosfilm. 

In the box you will find 

     original rules rewarding fast and funny answers. 
     200 meme-frames from classic movies. 
     200 quote-based film situations.

Rust and Revenue is a solo roll-and-write game inspired by the economic tension of 18XX titles, distilled into a streamlined, one-sheet format. Using just three dice and a pencil, you’ll invest in railroads, build track, and upgrade trains—all while competing against two streamlined AI opponents with distinct behavior.

Each turn, you roll three dice to choose a company and an action—build, buy, or invest. Shares become more expensive over time, and companies receive dividends based on route performance. As newer trains enter the game, older ones rust, pushing you to optimize timing and anticipate obsolescence.  Each action pushes a railroad to produce less revenue for the President of that company.  Timing share and operational actions is nuanced and based on the dice and the game state.

With multiple maps, procedural setup, and minimalist rules, Rust and Revenue offers meaningful decisions in a compact, replayable system. It rewards players who enjoy economic planning, route optimization, and clever long-term thinking—without needing a full table or multi-hour commitment.

Designed for solo players who love 18XX, cube rails, or tight economic puzzles, this game captures the spirit of railroad expansion with elegant constraints and a touch of rust.

—description from the designer

Rulers of the Pond face off against Lords of the Underworld in a quirky, fast-paced spin on Tic Tac Toe. In this easy-to-learn strategy game, you’ll play as either Team Duck or Team Demon, aiming to connect three characters in a row on an ever-changing grid while sabotaging your opponents. Your magic cards will bring the heat and your instant cards might ruffle a few feathers, but above all else, make sure you get your Ducks in a row! (Unless of course you’re playing as Team Demon, in which case… never mind, you get the point.)

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Thorgal: The Card Game – Additional Content
description:
"Additional Content" box with the following contents:

     1 Drakkar miniature
     8 Companion starter cards with foil finish and alternative graphics (63.5×88 mm)
     8 Bane cards with foil finish (63.5×88 mm)
     Wooden markers
         9 Exhaustion tokens
         5 Bravery tokens
         5 Cunning tokens
         5 Power tokens
    
     8 Different player boards (two for each Faction)
     Deck Construction (Tournament) rules (4 pages, 17.5×25 cm two-sided full-colour glossy folded)
     4 storage deck boxes (one for each faction, sleeve-compatible)


The Additional Content is in a unique sleeved box with alternative cover art.

boardgame: B-17 Flying Fortress: The Bloody 100th Board Game Edition
description:
B-17 Flying Fortress: The Bloody 100th Board Game Edition invites you to step into the boots of a WWII bomber crew, managing every facet of a harrowing mission deep into enemy-controlled Europe. This cooperative board game tests tactical mettle and communication under fire as you:

Survive the onslaught: Endure intense flak bombardments, outmaneuver Luftwaffe fighters, and manage critical aircraft damage.

Coordinate critical roles: Pilot, bombardier, gunner—every crew member’s task is vital to keep your Fortress flying strong.

Balance resources: Carefully manage fuel, emergency kits, repairs, and morale as you stare down impossible odds.

Lead escort squadrons: Command protective fighters as they shield your crew from enemy interceptions.

Work as a team or crumble in chaos: Communication is your lifeline; the mission hangs by threads of teamwork and strategy. Every mission throws fresh challenges and tests your limits. Will you return as war heroes—or fall in flames?

—description from the publisher

GAMEPLAY

The game is designed for 1–4 players, with a strong solo experience that mirrors the intensity and immersion of classic MicroProse flight sims. Cooperative play lets multiple players take on different crew roles aboard the same aircraft. A typical mission lasts 2-4 hours, depending on player count and how deep you go into mission planning and combat detail. A full campaign can span multiple sessions as your crew gains experience, faces tougher missions, and (hopefully) makes it home.

RULEBOOK

You can read the latest rulebook draft here: https://www.microprose.com/downloads/manuals/100th-board/b-17-flying-fortress-bloody-100th-rulebook_v081.pdf

RELEASE

There are currently no plans for a retail release. B-17 Flying Fortress: The Bloody 100th Board Game Edition is being developed as a Kickstarter-exclusive project, and backers will have the first and possibly only opportunity to secure a copy during this campaign. Fulfilment is scheduled for May 2026.

GAME DESIGNER

The B-17 Flying Fortress: The Bloody 100th Board Game edition was designed by Etherfield in partnership with MicroProse. It is fully supported and endorsed by the 100th Bomb Group Foundation.

The fourth title in the excellent and expanding USE ME game system series concerns itself with ultra modern warfare for 15mm to 20mm scale miniatures. USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer. From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up any of your miniatures and vehicles and get playing in minutes. This complete rules system is printed in A6 format which is a booklet which will fit in your pocket (110mm by 180mm) making it truly portable as well as fantastic value for your money. USE ME is just that...its for you!

The fourth title in the series and by popular request we present O.G.Joel's modern warfare rules suitable for use with 15mm to 20mm scale miniatures and vehicles. We are bringing USE ME into the present day!

Author of the fantastic adaptation of USE ME for the UM003 World War Two rules O.G.Joel has done it again with a set of rules that is intended for ultra-modern games set in the first decades of the 21st century, from Afghanistan to Iraq to Lebanon to Southern Ossetia and beyond. In writing it, it is envisioning combat in today's turbulent world, with high tech equipped units fighting each other or skirmishing with lightly-armed insurgents. This is a world of helicopters and assault rifles, night-vision devices and satellite uplinks, special-forces units and sophisticated terrorists. Many battles await from a few characters a side to whole squads upwards to a major battle.

—description from the publisher

The sixth title in the excellent and expanding USE ME game system series concerns itself deep space and starship combat. From fighters to battleships along with dogfight to fleet actions this system by O.G.Joel takes you off the ground! USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer. From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up any of your miniatures and vehicles and get playing in minutes.

This complete rules system is printed in A6 format which is a booklet which will fit in your pocket (110mm by 180mm) making it truly portable as well as fantastic value for your money. USE ME is just that...its for you!

With the sixth title in the USE ME game system we decided to leave not only the ground but also the atmosphere to bring you a set of rules for Starship Battles. You can learn all about the other titles in the USE ME range on the website but in terms of this title our aim was to produce a stand alone system for space vehicles from the smallest fighters to the mightiest dreadnoughts in any setting. It would also have a bolt on interface to allow you to make use of UM001 USE ME 15mm Science Fiction for boarding actions, planet fall and invasions too. Whither you Trek or you seek Babylon or anything else in space you can make it happened with this rules set.

Author of USE ME WWII and USE ME Modern Warfare O.G.Joel relished the challenge of a space based system for the USE ME range. His mechanics are heavily adapted from the core D6 based engine used by other USE ME titles and the result is excellent. Easily scalable, usable with metal, plastic and even paper models in any format. From a few fighters right up to whole squadrons and even squadrons among capital ships, you can play any size of action with USE ME SSB.

—description from the publisher

USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer. From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up the miniatures and get playing in minutes. USE ME is just that...its for you!

The world we see is nothing, an illusion, of banal normality that covers a richness of cosmic horror that would blind and destroy the minds of men and render them senseless in the gaze of things far greater and older than imagination can conceive. But despair not! We refer not to the real world but instead to that of the thirteenth (no coincidence!) title in the USE ME series...An Eldritch Horror.

This title was born out of a fire of creativity in only a few weeks in a joint effort by O.G.Joel and G.B.Syme and given the thirteenth code in the series, as befits it, in response to a growing number of wargamers requests for a simple system to cover this genre of cosmic horror. The D6 engine is fast play and well suited to 15mm scale miniatures. Lead your party of characters against cultists, against insidious forces and against the Elder Gods themselves...but will you or madness prevail?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements, or USE ME, rules for science fiction gaming with 6mm scale miniatures. These 6mm rules are designed to give a very satisfying experience, tactically challenging, without being hard to learn or come to terms with. This USE ME game typically has 60, 70, 100 miniatures a side and takes a half hour, to an hour to play out.

Use any miniatures, 1D6 Action, Solo Play, Build your Forces, Skirmish or Battle...

Perfect for after a hard days work, lunch time break combat, a complete newcomer to the wargaming hobby or even a child of seven years or older. In fact we bet that once you have played a few turns you will value these rules all the more. In a time when more complex and more expensive no longer means better to time pressed wargamers USE ME steps up and entertains. If you want to pick up your miniatures and get fighting with a minimum of fuss and requirements then USE ME is just that; it's for you!

—description from the publisher

Step into the seedy world of 1920s organized crime in Racket, a mafia-themed board game filled with mobsters, influence, and deadly ambition. Designed for 2–4 players, Racket casts each participant as the boss of a fledgling criminal empire vying for dominance. The objective: become the most powerful and profitable syndicate in the city by the end of the game.

Each player leads a mob faction—each with unique abilities—competing for control of valuable city turf and acquiring assets to expand their operation. From acquiring useful assets through shady backroom deals to sending goons to sabotage rival enterprises and dodging entanglements with the police, every decision shapes the rise—or fall—of a player's criminal empire. In Racket, strategic asset management, well-timed mob hits, and a keen eye for identifying critical business opportunities are all essential to outmaneuvering the competition and staying ahead of the law.

Racket blends classic area control and dice-rolling mechanics with asset acquisition and use, all within a richly thematic 1920s mafia setting. With dynamic gameplay, variations in both set-up and play, and good old-fashioned cutthroat player interaction, Racket is ideal for players who enjoy light strategy and luck, wrapped in a vintage underworld flair. Whether it’s an early power grab or a final, calculated strike, every move matters in this exciting board game where players battle for power and control.

—description from the publisher

USE ME means 'Ultra Simple Engine for Miniature Engagements' and this system delivers just that. Tactically challenging, easy to learn and for any age of wargamer. From skirmish to full battles, games last from five minutes to an hour or two for the largest battles. Play in your lunch break or in the evening when the idea of a really complex system with a long set up time really does not appeal. Pick up any of your miniatures and get playing in minutes. This complete rules system is printed in A6 format which is a booklet which will fit in your pocket (110mm by 180mm) making it truly portable as well as fantastic value for your money. USE ME is for you!

It is with a moan and a shuffle that I welcome you to the ninth in the USE ME series of titles. Why a moan and a shuffle? Because its the Zombie Dawn baby; the night, day and eternity of the living dead! This rule set marks a departure from a primarily broad based series of titles for something more specialised.

Gavin Syme created Zombie Dawn in response to gamers requests for an Undead game which was easily played, a little different and most importantly could be played solo as well as with two or more players. Fast play rules based on the D6 USE ME engine that would suit 15mm scale miniatures and has a simple matrix to use 28mm scale miniatures too. From a few personalities in small scenario to squad (called teams) level with battles involving a hundred or more characters including vehicles. USE ME Zombie Dawn achieves all of this and more.

—description from the publisher

A set of rules for miniature wargaming in the Eastern Woodlands of North America from Pre Contact times to 1842.  Uses the same engine and mechanics  as my Counting Coup and Cutting Horses rules with changes appropriate for the setting in the woodlands of Eastern North America.  Suitable for solo, cooperative, or head to head play.  While the focus, as with Counting Coup and Cutting Horses, is on intertribal warfare, there are lists and rules for settlers, regulars, couriers du bois, rangers and other non-native forces.  The Beaver Wars, Powhatan and King Philip's Wars, the French and Indian War,  Pontiac's Rebellion, the American War of Independence, Northwest Frontier,  Black Hawk and Seminole Wars can be gamed as well.  There is a scenario generator, a full solitaire system, an unfolding map on table campaign as well as an Iroquois vs Catawba campaign.  The rules use playing cards rather than dice to resolve movement, combat, and morale.  Each player will need a deck of cards which is periodically reshuffled.  Extensive notes on specific tribes and non-native forces.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Eagles to Glory: Grand Tactical Napoleonic Miniature Rules
description:
Eagles to Glory is a set of miniature wargaming rules for the Napoleonic period. 
The rules will work well with all popular miniature scales.

A single stand represents a battalion of infantry, 2 squadrons of cavalry and 1 battery of artillery. The number of figures on a base does not impact the game and as long as bases are similar sizes for all units. Units form divisions and corps and players will take on the role of Army, Corps and Division Commanders.

The rules are quick to learn and include detailed 2-page Quick Reference Sheets for 3 different scales, a sample scenario with unit labels along with a set of Eagle bonus cards that enhance leadership.

Players will be rewarded for employing combined arms, maintaining reserves, correctly utilizing cavalry, utilizing maneuvers to consolidate and attack, and similar key tactics of the period.

Available for purchase on Amazon (print) and Wargame Vault (PDF).

Find our more on https://www.facebook.com/groups/eaglestoglory

Tutorials on Eagles to Glory Youtube channel. 
https://www.youtube.com/@EaglestoGlory

—description from the designer

Armored Advance is a vehicle-centric tabletop game currently set during World War 2. 
The game is based in 20mm (1/72) scale and features a diverse range of unit types.

The Armored Advance launch focuses on the 2nd Battle of El Alamein as a theme and includes a range of British, German, and Italian vehicles used in this battle. More models are already being queued up to be added to the game and will be available soon.

More Units will be added, focusing on the campaign in North Africa for the time being. In the coming months a new expansion will be featured adding the Soviet Union as a faction and improving the mechanics for urban environments.

Armored Advance uses an action system for resolving turns. The number of actions for a game is agreed upon by the players prior to the first turn, generally being set such that there is a shortage of actions, meaning players must choose where best to allocate them rather than being able to use all units to their full potential. Each unit has a specified action pool, typically 2 actions, which they can use to do different things, such as move or shoot. Once a player can not take any more actions, either because they are out of actions to allocate, or out of vehicles to allocate actions to, their turn is over and the other player’s turn begins.

Mission Types:
Annihilation
Timed Annihilation
Domination

—description from the publisher

From primordial Fire spawned ancient Water, which overcame it. Water was the birthplace of Life, which grew to evolve beyond it. From life rose Man, who destroyed it to build the Machine. The machine surpassed man, but in the end it could not escape the fire... And it all began again!

Play with the ultimate cosmic forces as you ponder how 14 billion years of history have brought you here, and relish the miracle of our very temporary existence with your loved ones.

Rank of cards goes from 0 (lowest) to 10 (highest). The suits beat each other in the following order: 
Machine beats Man.
Man beats Life.
Life beats Water.
Water beats Fire.
Fire beats Machine.

boardgame: Le Jeu du Marchand de Sable: P'tit Loup
description:
Try to be the first to complete your lotto card with the six "little wolf" bedtime rituals, based on a combination of memory and luck.

Playing with two people or two teams, you take turns which consist of:
1. throwing the die, which may be repeated once if the die lands on a ritual that you (or your team) already has
2. flipping a memory token
3. taking the corresponding ritual card if the memory token matched the die.

The rules include a simple solo / coop variant.

Project Skyline is a city building game with a unique blend of strategic deck-building and area control in three dimensions. Each game will take 2-4 players to a famous city such as New York or Tokyo, where they will collectively shape the skyline of the city while trying to stay on top of the competition.

The goal of the game is simple: whoever has the most money at the end of the game is the winner. The road to victory however may prove not as straightforward. Throughout the game, players will gain money by building apartments in the city and spend money to improve their personal decks of construction cards, allowing them to build more efficiently or unlocking completely new avenues of scoring altogether. Finding the right balance between investment and pay-off is the key to success.

At the end of each turn, the current player will score all monuments in the city that they have connected to the central landmark. The twist: Only the topmost apartment in each building counts towards connections, locking the players in a tense back-and-forth race for those precious top spots in the most important buildings. Can you win the race to the top or will you find a different route to victory?
This expansion of the popular classic quiz game SAMRT10 offers many new questions on various adult topics such as sex, crime, drugs and more!

As if the Hyperstar base wasn’t already dangerous, the enemies have set traps throughout the level. Watch your step or you’ll end up in serious trouble!

Trapped 6-card expansion features trap cards that complicate your path forward. Revealing one might extend your combo—or activate a trap that sets you back.

Whenever you combo your way into a facedown level card, it might be a trap. Flip a trap card, and if it connects to your combo line, it replaces the next level card. This might extend your combo, or it might spring the card’s trap! If a trap has been triggered, it has an immediate negative effect as well as an enemy that must be defeated before you can continue moving.

Haste makes waste: pick up spent parts left behind from your failed runs and turn that trash into useful tech.

Salvage Mode 5-card expansion adds new salvage cards with potent effects. Add a salvage after each run, then press the corresponding level to activate it in a subsequent run. Use the wreckage from your past attempts to power your way out, but be careful: some salvage may not work as well as it used to!

After each run, add a salvage card below your current level card. On a later run, press the level card to immediately gain the salvage card’s effect.
Harry Potter themed trivia game. Players race to be the first to collect 5 trivia cards and be declared the winner.

In Helm’s Slain, players are adventurers in a cooperative dungeon crawl campaign, defending the Kingdom of Helm from monsters emerging from nearby dungeons. Over nine levels of the dungeon, the party must survive increasingly difficult encounters and defeat the final threat.

Players choose from five character classes, each with distinct abilities that influence strategy. Turns are taken simultaneously, with players rolling dice to resolve combat and allocating hits across enemies. A character’s level determines how many d6 they roll—the higher the level, the more dice they can throw. Players pair these dice strategically in a unique combat system to land hits on enemies. Combat takes place on battle boards. Each board has a front row for melee, and a back row for spells and ranged attacks. Positioning matters: front-row heroes can't cast spells, and spellcasters forced into melee can't cast at all.  A light Game Master role manages scenarios and monster actions. A free companion app (optional) can run the GM and provides a player view for tracking stats digitally if players prefer.
  
The game includes over 550 unique monster encounters, combining cooperative tactics, resource management, and unpredictable challenges in every session.

—description from the publisher

What’s more satisfying than triggering a fun chain reaction to outsmart your opponents?

Bomb5 is a clever and super accessible tile-laying game where you place numbered tiles and combine them to make a total of 5. Do that five times(you guessed it) and you win! Turns are quick and full of surprises, as explosive tiles can turn the play area upside down when you least expect it.

Each player starts with a hand of three numbered tiles (going from 1–4). On their turn, players place a tile orthogonally adjacent to another on the play area. If the sum of connected number tiles equals 5, a chain reaction is triggered: you place one of your cup tiles on one end of the line and flip the tile at the other end —whether it's a cup... or a bomb. Be the first to place 5 face-up cups to win!

Bomb tiles add a twist, with effects ranging from flipping a specific tile to disrupting entire rows or columns. With simple rules and a dynamic gameplay loop, Bomb5 strikes a fun balance between strategy and surprise.

—description from the publisher

Tehavi is a simultaneous bidding game. The goal is to bid high but never exceed the authorized threshold (which may change from one round to the next) in order to collect pearls!

Each round, you must play one of your cards as an offering in a large conch shell in hopes of gaining the favor of the god of the oceans: Ruahatu.

However, each conch shell has a limit to the number of flowers it can hold...if you overflow it, you will be eliminated for this turn.

Be smart, use totems to modify your offerings, and watch what your opponents play to anticipate their next moves!

Tehavi combines luck and strategy for your enjoyment!

—description from the publisher

The game is simple but full of tension. It symbolizes a countdown before lighting the fuse that will ignite the fireworks for the Sortilèges festivities. Unfortunately, there will always be someone clumsy enough to light it too early... and it will inevitably be one of you. Each player must take turns playing chrono cards of decreasing value (from 60 to 1) or use the actions that some of them allow to spice up the game. When a player cannot play a card, they reveal one of the four fuse cards in the center of the table. Until the inevitable explosion. 
Points are then counted based on the cards remaining in each player's hand, with a generous bonus for the clumsiest player. As you might expect, the goal is to have as few points as possible. 
A speed rule allows you to cut the game round and add a little challenge to the game!

Pizza Fixes Everything!

-1 to crime scene.

Scrappers is a fast-paced, strategy-packed board game of route-building, resource gathering, and worker placement that dynamically evolves  as the game is played.

With scorched skies and cities of rusted ruins, only one hope remains: YOU! A group of feisty little robots we like to call: SCRAPPERS!

Sure! You may have had a hand in ending the modern world! But, now's your chance to REBUILD IT! So gather up those nuts and bolts Scrappers!

You’ll lay intricate pipe networks across a junk-choked landscape, harvesting lost hardware. Deliver these precious parts to scattered human survivors, helping them repair broken homes, forgotten memories, and fractured lives. But, most importantly, repair their Trust.

Time is ticking! Every turn presents tough choices: expand your pipeline for bigger hauls, race to deliver that critical last piece of hardware, or donate some lost treasures to squeak out that victory?

The world may be broken... and it may be your fault. But that means you're the best bot to fix it!

Are you ready to be more than the sum of your circuits?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Sword & Sorcery: Special Hero Pack – Mist: Death Guardian / Necrowarrior
description:
Add a new, powerful character to your Sword & Sorcery campaign!

The world of Sword & Sorcery always needs new heroes! Add a new, powerful character to your Sword & Sorcery campaign with the Mist Hero Pack!

Born to serve Wotan as a Valkyrie, Mist has spent her life guiding the souls of fallen warriors to their destined afterlife. But when she witnessed a vision of Ragnarok, she vowed to fight against the collapse of the natural order, embracing a new destiny.

Mist can be played as either a Death Guardian or a Necrowarrior, each offering a distinct playstyle. As a Lawful Death Guardian, she wields her mighty spear-scythe to uphold the balance between life and death, summoning spirits to aid her in battle. As a Chaotic Necrowarrior, she bends the souls of the damned to her will, forcing them to fight in her service while delivering merciless judgment.

This Hero Pack includes everything needed to play Mist in any Sword & Sorcery campaign: three detailed miniatures (her standard form, Ghost Soul form, and Nemesis), a full set of cards, tokens, a Soul Gem, and her Hero sheet. Whether you seek to preserve the balance or command the forces of the underworld, Mist is ready to join your legendary quest!

boardgame: STALINGRAD and the Battle of the Caucasus
description:
A Historical Strategy Game of Oil, Urban Warfare, and the Turning Point of the Eastern Front of WW2.

June 28th, 1942 The Axis Powers launch their Summer Offensive: Fall Blau (Case Blue).  Hitler's dream of seizing the oil fields of the Soviet Union hangs in the balance.  The road to Baku runs through Stalingrad and the rugged passes of the Caucasus.  Will you take Stalin’s namesake city for glory and propaganda?  Or bypass it entirely—striking south to capture Baku and the black gold of the USSR?  Can the USSR trade space for time—building toward a thunderous counteroffensive to encircle and destroy the invaders?

STALINGRAD and the Battle of the Caucasus is the result of over three years of design and development by game designer Stephen Prescott (stevepres25).  This immersive, historically grounded wargame delivers a level of depth and strategic nuance rarely seen in the genre.  With dual maps—one covering the vast oil-rich Caucasus region and the other focused on the brutal urban fighting in Stalingrad—players engage in a two-front struggle where oil management, encirclement, supply, and tactical planning are paramount.

Play as the USSR, Germany, or the Axis Minors—Romania, Italy, Hungary, Slovakia, Croatia, and even the insurgent Chechnya.  Gain historical Commanders such as Friedrich Paulus, Vasily Chuikov, Erich Von Manstein, and Georgy Zhukov who have unique and historical abilities that impact combat.  The Axis Powers get assigned unique historical Missions and Battles that come with advantages upon accomplishment.  The USSR play Calendar Cards each representing 2 weeks of battle that inject strategic and historic twists.  The richly detailed units—some never-before-seen in this genre: Snipers, Mortar, Bicycle Infantry, Rocket Artillery, and more—each carry fresh characteristics that bring about more nuance in combat.  Combat is more strategically varied as units may be routed—or captured and used by your opponent.  Players face meaningful historical , to stop USSR oil flowing up the Volga, to disrupt Axis Supply Lines with the USSR Partisans, to conduct Urban Rubble hidden movement in Stalingrad, and to encircle and render enemy forces combat ineffective.

Designed for replayability and future expansions which are already in the works, STALINGRAD and the Battle of the Caucasus features multiple ways to play:
The Captain Edition: less units, simplified gameplay, great for learning the mechanics of the game.

The General Edition: the standard edition, 24 unit types, the full set of rules and opportunities.

The Battle of Stalingrad Scenario: a 2 player, shorter scenario that focuses on the street to street urban rubble combat of Stalingrad in the Autumn of 1942.

—description from the designer

In the mystical realm of Mytherra, you take on the role of a powerful strategist, sending forth your envoys to engage in epic battles. Each round, a celestial aura dictates the flow of magic, allowing you to play spell cards to empower your champions and vanquish your opponent's forces. Gather the most victory points by tactically winning battles and claiming the souls of defeated envoys to emerge as the ultimate victor in this competitive card game.
Players earn victory points by winning envoy cards in battles. The game ends when no further battles are possible. 
The goal is to earn the most victory points by winning battles with your envoys. Each envoy you claim in battle grants points, with certain legendary envoys offering greater rewards. When no more battles can be fought, the game ends — and the player with the highest score is declared the champion of the seasons.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Haelo: Heros wana keep that Halo
description:
Haelo: Heros wana keep that Halo is a lighthearted and exciting party game. Players assume the role of a hero in another world, leveling up through luck or by depleting their hand. The player who reaches level 7 wins. Players who prefer a protracted battle can opt for a longer, second-round victory at level 8, greatly enhancing the game's drama.

Background:
Hero, you've finally awakened. You're supposedly experiencing amnesia. Let me explain the situation.
This is the must-pass continent, a land rich in heroes. So abundant that the Demon King and other monsters have been largely annihilated.
So, the only thing left for you to do is level up. Not sure how? Don't worry! Look at these fellow heroes who have been demoted, given up, scavenged, barely surviving, punished by heaven, disconnected, inactive, and lost their friendships. They're sure to lend a hand.
