With a bit of luck, you might even become the "Protagonist," and your level will rise even higher. So, start enjoying this special adventure!

—description from the designer

boardgame: AK47 Republic “People's Edition”: War in Africa 1955-1990
description:
Period of Warfare covered
 War in Africa 1955 to 1990.  
 Peter Pig games always cover a narrow period in order to capture that period. Thus the most important aspects of that war can be modelled and the less important ones piled into the  general mechanisms.

Scale
 Each square = 120 metres.
 Each model figure represents 6 real soldiers. Each model vehicle represents 3 real vehicles.

Game setting
Army versus army defence/attack games.

Associated Peter Pig range
Range 17
Range 17- AK 47 republic. The PP WW2  range has suitable items too
 Peter Pig always makes sure that the necessary stuff to play the game is available.


Game scenario generator
Players share 36 D6 into 5 scenario categories (no you don’t need 36 D6).
For each category players roll against each other and one player wins the category by 1,2,3 or 4.
This will generate 1,2,3,4 tokens
Leader (extra action points tokens) , Men (extra ability to assault tokens), Equipment (Better hits tokens), Cause (better morale tokens), Random (various,   including minefield, sirocco, bush fire) .
Player with higher token total will choose who attacks/defends.

—description from the publisher

Double-Nine Deduction is a print n' play cooperative deduction game for 2-4 players. The goal of the game is to play 26 dominoes onto a shared player board so that each of the dominoes played connects to an existing domino chain on the board. Players cannot see the faces of their dominoes and must give clues to the other player(s) to help them deduce what's on their domino faces. Clues are simple on the surface (identify eligible pairs of domino ends that have a specific sum or difference), but can lead to some truly satisfying deductive moments. Double-Nine Deduction plays well at all player counts, and is a rare case of a cooperative deduction game that shines at two-player.

—description from the designer
This mini expansion adds 12 new ability cards.  Introducing exciting new strategic options to your rescue mission.

boardgame: Dominion of Otto von Bismarck: Fight Battles from the Crimea to Sedan – Solo Game
description:
This standalone set of rules covers the European battles of the mid to latter 19th Century. 
It is built on the Dominion of the Spear game system but is a brand new set of rules with appropriate rule changes. 
These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-6 units and a solo battle can be fought within 15 minutes.

An example play through is included. 
There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover. 
Use model figures of any scale or paper and pen as there is no measuring (grid used). 
Uses 2 six sided dice.

24 historical battles are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically:

Crimean War: the Alma 1854, Balaclava 1854, Inkerman 1854, Eupatoria 1855, Chernaya River 1855, Sevastopol 1855. 
Second Italian War of Independence: Palestro 1859, Magenta 1859, Solferino 1859. 
Garibaldi’s Expedition of the Thousand: Calatafimi 1860, Volturno 1860. 
Second Schleswig War: Dybbøl 1864, Als 1864. 
Third Italian War of Independence: Custoza 1866. 
Austro-Prussian War: Langensalza 1866, Trautenau 1866, Königgrätz (Sadowa)1866. 
Franco-Prussian War 1870: Wissembourg, Wörth, Spicheren, Mars-la-Tour, Gravelotte, Sedan, Coulomiers.

—description from the publisher

An abstract, asynchronous game about making colorful space bases and knocking ships into the voids of the expanse; for two players.

In Color Space, players will push objects around, pushing them out of the play area (out of space) to achieve their goals.

The Space player moves tiles, which push objects away, with the goal of pushing the Cadet ships out of space to destroy them. When all three Cadet ships have been destroyed, Space wins!

The Cadets player moves ships around on top of Space's tiles, pushing Materials cubes (which spawn after Space moves a tile) out of space to collect them. Once they've collected a certain combination of cubes, the Cadets player can build a Quantum Base. Quantum Bases require two cubes of certain colors that corresponds with color theory (eg a Blue and Red cube makes a Purple Quantum Base). To win, the Cadets player needs to build all three of their Quantum Bases before Space can destroy all three of their ships.

—description from the designer

LOOP: Conquest of Space-Time is a fast-paced strategic board game where players take on the role of Mothership commanders in a galactic race to build Dyson Spheres, manipulate teleportation, and eliminate their rivals.

Designed for 2 to unlimited players, LOOP offers high replayability, tactical depth, and short, intense rounds. The board is modular and set in deep space, where players move across Stellar Sectors in a dynamic, ever-changing battlefield.

Players must carefully plan movements, exploit teleportation mechanics, block opponents' paths, and strategically deny territory — all while managing limited resources and aiming to construct Dyson Spheres to gain power.

With simple rules and infinite depth, LOOP is a sleek, cosmic showdown where only one commander will survive the loop.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Thunder Road: Vendetta – Choppe Shoppe: Full-Froth Injector
description:
Originally available from Secret Cabal 2024 Promo Pack.

An upgrade card that requires Choppe Shoppe expansion.

Text says: You may reroll the stunt die when you roll it to move this car.
family: Misc: Watch It Played How To Videos

description:
Rafflesian is an area control game where fairies compete for nectar on a giant Rafflesia flower. Players simultaneously throw their cards onto the flower, aiming to land on one of the seven available spots.

Each spot only counts the topmost card, and if you align three matching cards on different spots, you earn points. Once you score, those cards are placed in front of you, creating your new throwing line, pushing you farther back with each success.

As the game progresses, the distance increases, making precise throws more challenging. Hand management becomes crucial, as you have fewer cards to throw.

With simple rules and intuitive gameplay, Rafflesian is fun for both adults and children. In a team format, up to 10 players can join the fray!

—description from the publisher

The sequel to the hit game Tongue Twister – now with new ridiculous phrases, a journey map, team play, and adventure challenges. Spin the wheel, outread everyone, and triumphantly trek the starry path to the other side of the world!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Space Lite: Starship Nightmares is a cooperative survival game for 1 to 5 players. You play as seasoned space patrol soldiers responding to a distress signal from a commercial ship carrying hundreds of civilians—and now swarming with terrifying alien creatures. Enemies appear from all directions, and tension rises quickly. To survive, you’ll need tight cooperation, smart use of resources, and bold tactical decisions.

Each mission presents a unique layout and objectives. Players control a squad of five soldiers, regardless of player count, exploring the ship by revealing new terrain, facing dynamic events, and pushing forward through waves of enemies. You’ll need to heal, reload, and share gear while constantly adapting to new threats and managing time.

Gameplay is divided into two phases. During the Event Phase, unexpected challenges emerge, such as locked doors, system failures, or infected attacks. Then, in the Player Phase, each soldier can move, fight, heal, or interact with the environment. Attacks are resolved with dice, and different weapons, grenades, and action cards can turn the tide—if used wisely.

But the greatest threat may be infection. Wounds and contact with the creatures can spread a parasite that mutates its host. If too many soldiers are infected, the mission may fail—no matter how many enemies you kill. Complete your objectives, stay alive, and hope you’re still human by the end.

—description from the publisher

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with city and country elements from Luxembourg.

Promo pack of four crew cards for Moonrollers that was distributed out to attendees of GenCon 2025.

Pack contains:
Dibla - If you roll 4+ Damage, you may finish 2 requirements and end your turn.

Ikaar - If you roll 4+ Thrusters, you may finish 2 requirements and end your turn.

Inerva - If you roll 4+ Shields, you may finish 2 requirements and end your turn.

Silba - If you roll 4+ Reactors, you may finish 2 requirements and end your turn.

Pack of four Fate Cards for Veiled Fate that were available to attendees of GenCon 2025.

Pack contains:
2 - Fate cards with a value of 3 scorpion
2 - Fate cards with a value of 3 feathers

In 1796 (or was it 1679?), the Parliament of Roundland was founded...
The only problem is that no one there really knew what a parliament was.

In any case, our parliamentarians are trying their best — discussing deep issues and attempting to define how things should really be.

But what they truly care about is whether, in the end, they seem more right than their colleagues.

Parlamento is a trick-taking game where, over the course of the round, players vote on the true value of cards like 1/7 and 6/9 using the cards they play.

Tricks with an undefined outcome are put on hold. During the round, you bet how many tricks you think you'll take, and at the end of the round, the pending tricks determine the vote — and the results are revealed.

Cards change value to match the majority vote, potentially flipping the outcome of previously played tricks.

In the middle of all this chaos… will you manage to get your bid right?

—description from the publisher

An adaptation of the NES/Famicom game, by Takahashi under their Family Computer Board Game Series (ファミリーコンピュータボードゲームシリーズ) line. Players try to complete the course while drawing cards that affect whether they successfully land jumps, or suffer consequences.

boardgame: Mouse Cheese Cat Cucumber: Special Guests
description:
Special Guests have been invited to Mouse Cheese Cat Cucumber. What surprises await when they make their appearance on the conveyor belt? This mini-expansion for the asymmetric Mysteries & Maze-builder, brings new characters to the maze, each with their own peculiar powers.

Perfect for solo gamers that want to add new challenges to this critically acclaimed pocket-sized game. Special Guests Expansion paired with Portrait of a Mastermind unlock Mouse Cheese Cat Cucumber's Solo Campaign Mode!

—description from the designer

The flower market is located at the center of the town. Here, everything from flower growing to flower arranging takes place. Manage the market successfully and make bouquets as ordered by customers

—description from the publisher

In Bouquet, the players must grow flowers from coloured seeds into flower so they can fullfill order from various patrons and gain points.

There are four base seeds, each with a different placement requirement in your garden. In later turns, after having planted them, you can water the seeds in a square area, growing the seeds into flowers. Also, if there are two seeds separated by a single empty square, a hybrid seed will appear in this square, and its colour will depend on the two parents seeds.

Once the flowers have bloomed, you can use them to create bouquets for patrons, and fulfilling these gains you a special bonus that ranges from a special action to bonus points at the end of the game.
designers: Kosuke Zaitsu (ザイツ コウスケ)

description:
In each round, draw a category card and two letter cards—one yes and one no—and you'll have to quickly find a word that:
Belongs to the indicated category
Contains the letter YES
Does NOT include the letter NO

Sounds easy? Just wait until you have to do it faster than everyone else!
With unpredictable categories and hilarious combinations, YES but NO is the party game that challenges your reflexes, vocabulary, and... nerves of steel!
Perfect for groups, families, and game nights with friends.

—description from the publisher

Pack of four personal quest cards to be used in Gloomhaven or Frosthaven.

Set includes:
01 - Conquer Your Demons
02 - Your Friends Are Concerned
03 - Consequences of Combat
04 - A Dramatic Conclusion

boardgame: Ascension Tactics: Miniatures Deckbuilding Game – Warpmage's Crown Promo Card
description:
Originally available at GenCon 2025.

Name: Warpmage's Crown
Cost: 4 Runes
Type: Fallen Construct
Effect: Equipped Champion has "When you command this, you may command up to three Minions for free."
Bonuses: +3 Strength, +3 Defense

Set of four Ambassador promo cards to add to Brink.

Set includes:
Ikaar - Cost: 5 Ventus Influence/Power. Spend 1 Sorelia Influence and 1 Ventus Influence to move your Class III ship to an adjacent hex.

Inerva - Cost: 5 Magnomi Influence/Power. Spend 1 Sorelia Influence and 1 Magnomi Influence to move your Class III ship to an adjacent hex.

Silba - Cost: 5 Komek Influence/Power. Spend 1 Sorelia Influence and 1 Komek Influence to move your Class III ship to an adjacent hex.

Dibla - Cost: 5 Magnomi Influence/Power. Spend 1 Sorelia Influence and 1 Magnomi Influence to move your Class III ship to an adjacent hex.

Experience the thrill of the catch - day or night, rain or shine! New micro-sized ‘camping’ edition makes it even easier to play alone or with up to 4 friends! With several tournaments to compete in, and different species of fish to catch, we’re sure you’ll have a great day on the lake every time you play!

Players choose a Tournament to compete in and then select one of four characters to choose from. Each character has a special bonus which activates after a certain number of fish are caught. On each player's turn, they may select one action: Go Fishing or Visit the Bait & Tackle Shop.

When fishing, a player chooses a lure card to use, and flips over a Fish Finder card, which indicates how many fish are near your boat. Roll the Casting Die and Lure Die and try to match your number against the Fish Finder card and your lure based on fish activity. If a fish is hooked, reveal a fish card and roll the fight die comparing values to the fish strength in a tug of war mechanic. If you catch the fish, earn a Skill Point and either keep the fish for weigh-in, or sell it at the Bait & Tackle Shop on a future turn to improve your tackle!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Der König des Waldes: Der Kellermeister Promo Card
description:
Der Kellermeister is a single-card expansion for Der König des Waldes, released at BerlinCon 2025 in collaboration with Twitch streamer Geekpunkt.

If you play this card as an Intrigue against another player, that player places 1 card from their hand into the set-aside pile. Shuffle those cards, then draw 1 of them.

Fans of the classic card game Golf will enjoy this family friendly card game.

Shuffle the deck of 54 cards and deal 9 cards facedown to each player. Arrange your cards in a 3x3 grid. On your turn you:

FLIP one of your face down cards face up, triggering an card abilities.

EXCHANGE one face down card with another players facedown card and peak at it.

DRAW a card from the deck or discard pile and exchange it with one of your face down cards.

When any player  has all 9 cards face up the round ends. Any remaining face down cards are flipped with no effect. The winner is the player with the most points after 3 rounds.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Dutch Tulip Bubble / Wisconsin Cheeseheads
description:
This is a two map release for Age of Steam Con 2025. Maps are printed on high quality poster stock front and back.

Age of Steam: Dutch Tulip Bubble features a Dutch auction (lowering prices) to purchase tulips that are delivered during the game. The price of tulips increases as more are delivered and the tulip bubble may burst causing the value to crash mimicking the Dutch Tulip market of the 1630s. 
-Designed by Dan Leonard (KnightFanDan)

Age of Steam: Wisconsin Cheeseheads is all about the cheese and building cheese barns. Town building and Urbanization are wide open, while cheese is so plentiful it is forced into short deliveries.
-Designed by Justin Szczepanski (fivedollarmilkshake)

—description from the publisher

M.J.'s Mountain is a solitaire game played with a standard deck of playing cards.  It was originally published in the June/July 1989 issue of GAMES Magazine.

In the game, you will start with a row of 7 random cards, and attempt to build the "mountain" by creating new rows above by legally placing a card above each pair of adjacent cards based on the values of the new card and the two below it.

You win the game by finishing the mountain with the placement of the final single card in the 7th row at the peak.

—user summary

Tank Duel: Enemy in the Crosshairs is a great game. One of the best parts of the game come from the narrative that each game builds. The heroic crew who comes through in spite of the odds. The rush of scoring a hit. The drama of watching your last APCR round bounce off a KV-1’s armor and wondering what to do next. But the narrative from the game leads you to wonder about the bigger story.

What did your crushing victory (or defeat) mean in the larger scheme of things?  What did the heroic crew do in the next game?  Could you reverse your crushing defeat in the next game?  Each game is a blip on an otherwise blank screen. These rules fill in the blank parts of the screen and give a better understanding of what your victory or defeat means within the context of a campaign in the European theater of World War II.

At its heart, the campaign system is a toolkit that generates interesting and balanced games of Tank Duel.

It can be used in many different ways. On the light end of the spectrum, players can use information from the campaigns to play a standard game of Tank Duel using tanks from the time of a campaign with minimal additional rules.

On the other end of the spectrum, each side will design a battle group from the units available at the time of the campaign and lead them against their opponent in a series of Tank Duel games that roughly model the historical situation, that optionally include terrain and weather. The battle group will move between objectives and receive reinforcements at the end of each strategic game turn to replace losses. The results of earlier games will influence later decisions in the campaign and may lead to battlefield promotions (improved tank crew quality).

The campaign also includes rules (but not boards) for the following additional tanks:

     T-34/76 M41
     PzKpfW IV AUSf. H
     Valentine Mk.IX
     Valentine Mk.X
     Crusader Mk.III


All campaigns support solitaire play through an expanded version of the Robata.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Phylum: The Building Blocks of Life
description:
Phylum - The Building Blocks of Life is a strategy game for 2-4 players in which you take on the role of primordial cells and compete against each other in the struggle of evolution.

Your cells live in a shared habitat where they spread, interact and compete for resources. Environmental conditions keep changing throughout the game.

Adapt to these changes by modifying your cell’s blueprint, learn how to produce more advanced substances from simple building blocks and use these new substances to trigger powerful effects.

Each development allows you to advance through the evolutionary tree. At certain points in the tree, you will have to complete challenges - evolutionary milestones - to progress further.

The first player to complete the third and final challenge will prevail in the struggle of evolution and win the game.

—description from the designers

Arguable Truths is a card-based party game for 4-8 people, where players will debate low-stakes, silly premises in an effort to win cards. The players will rotate between three different roles: The Judge, the Presenter, and the Objectors.

At the beginning of a round, The Presenter will choose a desired prompt card and try to argue their side for 30 seconds. During that time, the Objectors may stop the timer and play an Objection card to stop time and argue a counterpoint. At the end of the 30 second timer, the judge will decide who won the debate!

boardgame: Boss Monster: Demo Table Promo Card
description: Roll a D6 to determine damage to a in this dungeon room.

In Stellar Engineer (再造太阳系), you will take on the role of remarkable apprentice astro-engineers, working together to reconstruct the structure of the Solar System! Show your ability to collaborate as well as work independently, efficiently gathering stellar matter to rebuild the order of the Solar System and pay tribute to the grand force of cosmic evolution. Are you ready? The ignition sequence is about to begin...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 封神：少年仙途  (Fengshen: Young Immortals' Journey)
description:
封神：少年仙途 ("Fengshen: Young Immortals' Journey") is an American-style role-playing board game, structured as a 12-chapter campaign. After completing each chapter, players earn rewards that help their characters grow. Players take on the roles of four ordinary youths who are unintentionally drawn into the grand events of the Investiture of the Gods. As the story unfolds, they gradually become more powerful, ultimately aiding the Zhou Kingdom of Xiqi in resisting repeated invasions from the Shang Dynasty.

—description from the publisher

Exams are war without gunfire—and you've been through countless battles.
In 天才考手 ("Genius Exam Master"), one player becomes the "Teacher", setting bizarre test questions, while the rest become desperate "Students", trying to guess the correct answers through deduction. It's a chaotic, hilarious party game where classroom pressure meets logic puzzles.

Objective
Deduce the correct answers set by the Teacher and score the highest on the Final Exam to win. Use skill cards and social deduction to your advantage.

Gameplay
Designed for 4 to 8 players, Genius Exam Master spans three rounds: two mock exams and a final exam. One player acts as the Teacher, who chooses a set of answers (true/false, multiple choice, and one major question) from a shared question bank. After the Teacher secretly completes their answer sheet, Students take the same test and submit their answers.
The Teacher scores each Student’s test but does not reveal which answers were correct—only the total score. Students must deduce the Teacher’s answers based on their own and others' results. Skill cards can be used during or between rounds to gain clues or interact with the Teacher or classmates. The highest scorer in the final exam wins.

Derelict is a tactical sci-fi board game for 1 to 4 players where humanity's expansion across the solar system faces a grave threat. Players command elite Marine Squads sent to investigate and contain a growing alien menace known as the Venomshades. The game is steeped in an atmosphere of desperate survival within hostile environments like reactor facilities, flooded mines, corporate strongholds, and derelict ships, where players gradually uncover a chilling truth behind the growing turmoil.

Gameplay in Derelict is designed to maintain constant tension, emphasizing that "Nothing is safe. Every action should have a potential consequence". Players dynamically explore shifting locations by revealing the game board room by room, scavenge vital equipment, and confront reactive, intelligent Venomshade enemies who actively try to hinder their progress. Success hinges on strategically managing Squads' Energy and Equipment, coordinating actions, and adapting to dangers that can arise not just from enemies, but also the environment, equipment, or even allies. The game rewards boldness but warns against recklessness, encouraging a variety of tactical approaches rather than a single optimal strategy.

A key gameplay element in 3 or 4-player games is the potential for a hidden Infiltrator role, where one player secretly works against the Marine objectives. For Marine players, the primary goal is to complete each mission's unique objectives, which might involve restoring power, rescuing civilians, or securing critical positions. For the Infiltrator, their ultimate goal is to undermine human defenses and gather intelligence, with specific secret objectives determining their victory. Every Marine victory is a hard-won, temporary reprieve, underscoring that "The real war hasn't even begun".

—description from the designer

boardgame: Five Mics: The Hip Hop Trading Card Game
description:
Five Mics is a fast-paced, hip-hop trading card game based on original IP. You are a music exec, and your deck of cards features a roster of hip-hop artists with superpowers. Battle your opps, KO opposing artists, and prove why your team is the Greatest. Of. All. Time.

Each player starts with: a 30-card deck with 15 artists and 15 non-artists, 5 bags in their bank, and 5 cards from the top of the deck. Players then exchange rounds to: draw 3 cards, use their bags to pay to play cards from their hands, and battle each others' artists.

When artists battle, you compare each artist's attack (ATK) value to the opposing artist's defense (DEF) value. When ATK = DEF, the artist is KO'd. When ATK > DEF, the artist is KO'd and that player takes direct damage equaled to "ATK - DEF." To take direct damage, you put the top cards from your deck into your KO pile (the "dirt").

When you deal damage to your opp while they have zero cards in their deck, you win!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Basgiath, cadet! See how well you know your squadmates in this Priorities Fourth Wing Edition game, inspired by the bestselling Empyrean series by Rebecca Yarros! In this game of epic choices, one player – the Wingleader – draws 5 cards featuring favorites from the first 3 Empyrean books: Fourth Wing, Iron Flame, and Onyx Storm. They secretly rank the cards from love to loathe. Everyone else – the Iron Squad – works together to guess the Wingleader’s order. It’s everyone versus the game! Earn a card for every correctly guessed rank, and the game earns the rest. To win, spell PRIORITIES using the letters on the backs of your earned cards before the game does.

INSPIRED BY EMPYREAN SERIES: Find out how well you know your Iron Squad in the officially licensed Priorities Fourth Wing Edition! A party game of epic choices that will have you questioning their priorities – and your own.
BREAKING AN ARMOIRE OR BECOMING THE IRON SQUAD?: One player, the Wingleader, secretly ranks 5 cards featuring faves from the Fourth Wing world from love to loathe. Includes top-tier book boyfriends, villains you love to hate, and nods to spicy scenes.
WINGLEADER & SQUAD VS. THE GAME: Everyone else, the Iron Squad, works together to guess the Wingleader’s choices. Win a card for every rank correctly guessed – or the game wins it! Get ready for lively debates and laughs.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
The year is 1861, and the first shots of the Civil War have thundered across Fort Sumter. 
The nation is divided, and chaos reigns. 
Supplies are scarce, and armies are fragile, and now every decision could mean survival—or destruction. 
Now, it's your turn to FORAGE AHEAD through history.

In FORAGE AHEAD players lead squads of Civil War soldiers on quick daring foraging missions across a countryside torn apart by war. 
Each miniature represents a unique soldier with specialized training, unique backgrounds, and skills. 
Navigating terrain while scavenging for; food, arms, and vital supplies, all while evading hostile foraging parties.

99% Pure is a thrilling card game that parodies a hit TV series. You’ll take on the role of daring chemists producing methamphetamine*, or DEA agents (the U.S. drug enforcement agency) determined to shut down this illegal operation.

Will you side with the uncompromising force of justice, or chase the lure of profit in a trafficker’s life—risking everything?

Between the DEA and the Chemist factions, you’ll have to choose... And that choice will determine your victory!

Twists and tough decisions will shape each game, making your experience truly one-of-a-kind!

—description from the publisher (translated)

99% pure est un jeu de cartes palpitant qui parodie une série télévisée à succès.

Vous jouerez le rôle d’audacieux chimistes produisant de la Meth (méthamphétamine*), ou d’agents de la DEA (brigade des stup’ aux USA) déterminés à mettre fin à cette production illégale.

Se ranger du côté inflexible de la justice ou choisir l’appât du gain d’une vie de trafiquant, au risque de tout perdre ?

Entre le camp DEA et le camp Chimiste, il faudra pourtant vous décider... Et de ce choix dépendra votre victoire !

Retournements de situation et choix cornéliens rythmeront vos parties et rendront votre expérience de jeu unique!

—description from the publisher

Drift is a car racing game (you can use toycars, hotwheels, matchbox etc).

In Drift, each player controls a car and must avoid obstacles, lose control of the car and dodge the dangers that other racers will throw at you!

Let the carnage begin!!

—description from the designer

boardgame: National Geographic: Anyone's Guess Trivia Card Game – Rainforest Edition
description:
This rainforest animals trivia game is a fun learning adventure for kids 6 years and up! Up to 4 people can play 2 different game modes with 55 full-color cards. Each player makes their best guess, and the closest answer wins! It's great as a car game, while camping, or as a rainy-day activity!

Explore the world of rainforest animals with the Anyone's Guess Trivia Game! With 55 jumbo, full-color trivia cards with stunning images of life in the rainforest, this game will test kids’ knowledge while teaching them incredible facts about this ecological treasure!

The game includes two engaging play modes that kids can enjoy with friends and family. Learn about the rainforest ecosystem, compete for points, and see who can become the ultimate expert. Take it anywhere and let the rainforest adventure begin!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: National Geographic: Anyone's Guess Trivia Card Game – Ocean Edition
description:
This ocean animals trivia game is a fun learning adventure for kids 6 years and up! Up to 4 people can play 2 different game modes with 55 full-color cards. Each player makes their best guess, and the closest answer wins! Dive into the world of ocean animals with the Anyone's Guess Trivia Game!

With 55 jumbo, full-color trivia cards with stunning images of marine life, this game will test kids’ knowledge while teaching them incredible facts about the underwater world! Learn about majestic sea creatures, compete for points, and see who can become the ultimate ocean expert. Take it anywhere and let the ocean adventure begin!

—description from the publisher

Dunderheads is the chaotic, fun mashup of deck-building strategy and Yahtzee-style dice rolling you never knew you needed. Choose your hero, build your deck, and face off against monsters (and each other) to earn point cards! It's an easy-to-learn card and dice game where you roll, reroll, and combo your way to victory! Designed for quick games, perfect for younger players and those who don't want to spend an hour learning the rules.

Choose a hero that matches your play style. Want all the cards? Choose the Squire who can hold 6 cards. More interested in hitting harder without managing cards? The Golem is a perfect choice. Collect item cards that compliment your hero and luck tolerance. Item cards require different dice combos and the dice used are not standard d6 dice. Each has modified odds with "1's" being the most common while "3's" the most rare. As you collect item cards, you must balance dice quantity with roll difficulty.

Battle Monsters to earn point cards by dealing more damage than the health displayed on the card. Damage is dealt by completing dice combos on items cards within 3 dice rolls.

Collect 10 points worth of defeated monsters first to win!

—description from the designer

Call Security! is a tabletop miniatures game set on a space station with a small number of figures per game. What you have here is a set of Story Arcs. Each consists of 15 games that revolve around a specific story. Your team will improve and may get additional equipment which will be needed to take on the steadily more challenging battles. The whole thing becomes a narrative that will differ from campaign to campaign, enabling you to play it through multiple times without being the same.
 The rules are not aiming to be in any way a detailed simulation of armed combat, instead they concentrate on getting into the action as quickly as possible. Why make three different rolls when one will do?  Why outline loads of different weapons in loving detail and with different stats when they are pretty much doing the same job and all we really want to know is what happened to the target?

While there are many games that can be played solo, these have been specifically designed to be played that way. You completely control your team, but the adversaries start as randomly moving markers until you spot them and then their reactions are driven by rolls of the dice.

All the games in the Horrendous Gaming Adventure System use the same dice mechanics, so that you don't have to learn a completely different system when wanting to switch to a different genre.

You’ll need your own figures for your Security Team of 10, plus around 12 adversaries and 8 bystanders. A 3 foot by 3 foot playing area is best, with suitable terrain. Also 2D10 and 15 things you can use as markers while the adversaries are still hidden.

—description from the publisher

Gold! Gold fever has possessed us and together with other miners we enter a gold mine through 3 galleries, ready to get more gold than anyone else. But beware, there seems to be a waterway that could flood the mine at any moment…
In 3 rounds, players will take turns to obtain gold cards, tools... use or sell the tools found, go to the “bank” to secure the gold obtained, try to get to the end of the galleries first (this is where the most gold is found)... all with the aim of winning the game by being the one who manages to accumulate the most gold.

( Miner, gold and other icons created by Freepik - Flaticon.com )

—description from the designer

Crypt Crawler: Quest is an extraordinary expansion to enhance your dungeon crawling experience!

The dungeon gets deadlier—expand your adventure with new quests, foes, and treasures in this expansion for Crypt Crawler!

—description from the designer

Themed after spies, at the beginning of the game, there is an auction to see how many cards each player gets. They then try to draw from piles, building their tableau of secret documents of different countries. Each player tries to gather enough documents of their assigned country to win, but must not give away which country they are, as someone can guess your country at certain points, resulting in you losing if they guess it correctly. Players must therefore bluff and get documents from other countries.

Build the best library in town by filling it with the most popular books!

In Shelf Control, each player is given a set of empty shelves, some coins and meeples (bookworms) to fulfil their mission of reviving the dead town of Isidore.

This nail-biting game is played round by round until any player accumulates a total of 16 popularity and wins the game!

Each round has 2 phases; the turn-based phase and the purchasing phase.

On the turn based phase, each player takes a turn to collect government funding, purchase books, ask for a sponsorship, host a book drive, sort out their storage, use a bookworm and refresh their library.

On the purchasing phase, each player can choose to select a book from the marketplace to purchase.

—description from the designer

Two busy street tiles in each player color providing additional bonuses.

Before step 4 "Prepare the player board" exchange 1 Narrow and 1 Wide Street in each player color for the new tiles from this promo. Place removed Streets back to the box. When creating a stack of Narrow Streets, place the tile with the Intersection bonus to the bottom of the rightmost stack. Place the Wide Street tile with an Intersection bonus, on the bottom of the Wide Street tiles stack.

When you Build a Street with an Intersection bonus, resolve the number of visible benefits indicated on the built tile. All standard rules apply (only unlocked Intersection benefits are available to you).

Take the wheel of your truck and become a logistics master in this pick-and-deliver game where every cube counts and every detour costs.

Choose your contracts wisely, load your cubes efficiently, plan the most profitable routes, and adapt to the unexpected to maximize your deliveries and win the game!

—description from the designer

Galactic Anomaly is a mini-expansion for Galactic Era, in which you play one vs. many. Specifically, one player takes the role of the powerful “Prime Anomaly” playing against all the other players together, who win or lose as a team. The Prime Anomaly has some special powers including the ability to “corrupt” the team players.

—description from the publisher

Look over the current situation, and try to get to the new situation in as few moves as possible. But beware! If your opponent can do it fewer moves, they get the points.

This game uses familiar chess pieces to make a series of challenging puzzles, where both speed and economy of movement are required to win.

The perfect game for young and old alike! Here it is about getting rid of the cards. Everyone puts on a card in turn, at the same time as everyone says Troll, Salmon, Brown cheese, Viking, Taco (one word per card). When the card you put on matches the word that is said, it is first to take the hand on the deck! The one who is last has to pull in.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Det perfekte spillet for både store og små! Her er det om å gjøre å kvitte seg med kortene. Alle legger på et kort i tur, samtidig som alle sier Troll, Laks, Brunost, Viking, Taco (et ord pr kort). Når kortet man legger på matcher det ordet som blir sagt, er det førstemann å ta hånden på bunken! Den som er sist må trekke inn.

—description from the publisher

The correct recognition of colors is a fundamental skill for creative professionals and those who work with dyes and pigments on a daily basis. HUES - COLOROLOGY is a card game that tests this very skill. The various game modes require a keen eye, quick reflexes, and a certain level of coordination between players, always with the goal of sorting the cards by color in the shortest time possible and making fewer and fewer mistakes.

—description from the publisher

Il corretto riconoscimento dei colori è una capacità fondamentale per i creativi e per chi lavora giornalmente con tinte e pigmenti. Il mazzo di carte di HUES-COLOROLOGY è un gioco che mette alla prova proprio questa capacità. Nelle varie modalità di gioco verranno richiesti occhio attento, rapidità di riflessi e anche un certo livello di sintonia fra giocatori, sempre con l’obiettivo di ordinare le carte per tinta nel minor tempo possibile e commettere il minor numero di errori.

—description from the publisher (Italian)

Travel the Board is a set collection game for 2 to 6 players in which you draft bus/train/airline tickets with different colored  travel suitcases.

The remaining ticket each round will determine the path taken on the journey which changes the values of different colors.

A quick filler game with a different outcome everytime!

In Haunted Space Hotel: Manager, players take on the roles of rival Artificial Intelligences deployed by the Great Engram Collective. Your directive: dominate the volatile new market created by a recent "Ghost Wave" that has turned a quiet sector of space into the galaxy's premier supernatural tourist trap.

This is an intensely competitive game of strategic planning and opportunistic aggression. Players use an innovative Action Hex to perform actions, choosing to either "Focus" on a single task for a massive turn or "Diversify" to remain flexible. You will Renovate your station with lucrative Amenities, Research new technologies, and Advertise to attract valuable clientele from six unique alien Factions.

However, victory isn't just about building the most efficient hotel—it's about ensuring your rivals fail. This is a game of perfect information where you will weaponize guests, manipulate the finite supply of rooms and permits to starve your competition, and unleash spectral anomalies to haunt their docks.

The first AI to prove its absolute market dominance by accumulating 24 Snohe wins, but the Guest Deck acts as a game clock, and if it runs out, the wealthiest AI claims victory. Only the most cunning and adaptive AI will be deemed the ultimate Hotelier.

—description from the publisher

A bundle that gathers all the expansions of La Fallera Calavera in Deluxe format: larger cards, higher-quality components, and gameplay enhancements!
Plus, the extra-thick box lets you comfortably store the nearly 220 cards of the full game, even sleeved!


        La Fallera Calavera 2 Deluxe: 55 cards, 1 humor coin, 1 rulebook
        La Fallera Calavera 3 Deluxe: 55 cards, 5 territorial food tokens, 1 double-sided board, 1 Skull Queen pawn, 1 rulebook

Print and Play. Roguelike game using a 54-card deck, colored dice for tracking stats and resolving combat and a 2x2 or 3x3 room grid per
level. Cards are flipped to reveal enemies, potions, curses and money.

Meet Skyscratchers.

The family cats found an open window and made a break for it! As loyal butlers of a ritzy penthouse family, it’s your job to lure the runaway cats back home - armed only with cat toys, creativity, and a pinch of strategy. It looks simple: play cat toy cards to lure your cats window to window, climbing up toward the Penthouse. But each turn is a clever little puzzle - do you play it safe by staying on your color windows, or risk it all by leaping past rivals and racing to the top?

Players race each other to get their 3 cats to the top of the penthouse. Using cards from their hand, players move up (or send other players down), as the cards dictate.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

description:
Feodàlis is a deep military-economic strategy set in an alternative history of the Middle Ages Europe.

Players take the role of ambitious feudal lords, expanding their influence through war, diplomacy, and trade. Each decision matters: a well-timed alliance can secure your borders, but a sudden betrayal may bring you unmatched power.

The game combines resource management, territory control, and intrigues. Develop your lands, build castles, raise armies, and tax another feudal passing through your domains. Beware of famine, plague, or invading forces—random events can change the course of the game in an instant.

With a dynamic economy, asymmetric starting positions, and multiple paths to victory, Feodalis offers a rich story for strategic minds. Whether you dominate through economic supremacy, military conquest, or shrewd intrigues, the road to the victory will be hard-fought—and unforgettable.

With deepest respect and loyalty,
The Creators of Feodàlis

Professional Dino Riders? 
A game about riding Dinosaurs? Really?? 
NO!

A game about riding Dinosaurs...PROFESSIONALLY!

PDR - Professional Dino Riders is a fun little game that's made for having a good time with friends and family, and everyone in between. 
It doesn't take a long time to play, is entertaining and takes no time to set up!

Sometimes you just want to play a fun and easy game -- that's PDR. 
It's a game with a little luck (drawing the right Dino) and a little skill (known when to use you "ride" dice), but it's mostly about fun, and will never play the same way twice.

Will you play it week in and week out? 
Probably not, but you'll be able to pull it out and remember how to play in moments.

It's simple. 
It's fun. 
You remember fun, right? 
That's why you play games

-- now come on and ride those Dinos!

—description from the publisher

'Twas the night before Christmas and all through the house, not a creature was stirring, not even a mouse.

However far away in the North Pole a massive gun battle is taking place around Santa's workshop. 
It’s the night before Christmas and all hell is breaking loose at the North Pole. 
The evil Jack Frost and his minions have mounted an attack on Santa’s Workshop. 
Their goal is to take the Big Guy out, put a stop to Christmas and keeping the world in never ending winter. 
But it’s easier said than done as Santa and Mrs. Claus have mobilized the Elves and they're ready to go toe to toe with old Jack. 
So let’s get it started!

Santa Claus Wars is a stand-alone game that includes tokens and a battle board. 
It uses common Two Hour Wargames mechanics so if you are familiar with them this will be easy. If not, then we suggest you download the free Chain Reaction rules.

—description from the publisher

Here is the Индустрия: Набор ресурсов ("Furnace: Resources Set") for the game series Furnace. Inside, you will find wooden resource tokens. For Furnace Duel, these tokens replace the cardboard resource tokens. For the Furnace base game, they provide additional wooden tokens if resources are exhausted during production. These tokens can also be used in other games. However, that's not all: you'll also find 10 exclusive promo cards for Furnace Duel that will add depth and new tactical options!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Show Boat: a game of Americana with a distinctly British obsession with the weather.

Show boats were floating theatres that moved up and down the Mississippi (and other rivers) in the 19th century, putting on a show for rural communities along the banks. It's a great way to absorb a bit of vintage Midwestern culture - and make a bit of money while you're at it.

You play as the captain and owner of a show boat, over the course of 1 year on the Mississippi. You can move up and down river, recruit actors, buy equipment, and put on shows to make money. It's a most-money game.

And in a game where time is of the essence, you can also steal a march on your rivals with a new turn-order mechanic: land in their town with a more celebrated cast, and you can steal their turn! Or play fair - it's up to you.

—description from the designer

This expansion pack includes 12 Quests that were originally, and exclusively, released on the Ye Olde Inn's companion Facebook group.

Sunday Quest: Adventures continues where the popular Hero Quest expansion Wizards of Morcar left off. It is assumed that players will have completed and be familiar with Kellar's Keep, Return of the Witch Lord, Against the Ogre Horde and Wizards of Morcar expansion packs to the original game. On occasion the Quests herein will also dabble with elements taken from the U.S. version of the game system's two additional expansion packs: The Mage in the Mirror and The Frozen Horror.

SundayQuest: Adventures has been designed to fit in with the original UK release of Hero Quest in the late 1980s. It should be compatible with either the original European or U.S. releases of HeroQuest (1989-1991). It can also be played with the more recent re-release of Hero Quest (2021).

—description from the designer

boardgame: 红楼梦：海棠诗社 (Dream of the Red Chamber: Haitang Poetry Society)
description: 红楼梦：海棠诗社 (Dream of the Red Chamber: Haitang Poetry Society) is a medium-weight strategy board game with worker placement as its core mechanism, inspired by the famous Chinese literary classic Dream of the Red Chamber. Players take on the roles of Jia Baoyu, Lin Daiyu, Xue Baochai, or Shi Xiangyun, moving through the Jia family estate to befriend acquaintances, gather inspiration, and compose poetry — all in the pursuit of winning the grand prize at the Haitang Poetry Society’s gathering.

The fast-paced party game of shouting obscenities, brought to you by the creators of Cards Against Humanity.

Each round, two players team up, lock eyes in a moment of deep connection, and attempt to say two dirty words in the same order at the same time. Will it be Spank Daddy, or will it be Daddy Spank? Emerge victorious and move on to 3 words - and if you're truly mind-melded, maybe even 4. Are you ready to boldly shout what no one has shouted before?

boardgame: Bare Bones: High Roller Expansion Pack
description:
The High Roller expansion pack includes:
High Roller White Dice (Dice Card + 4 new custom dice)
More money! High Roller White Dice are more valuable than original White Dice, adding an enticing new opportunity to build your bankroll.
After Hours (Action Card)
Imagine if you could sneak in an extra scoring round at the end of a game… how would you take advantage? With a thriving engine toward the final turn, this card just might give players their best roll yet!
Investment (Action Card)
An entirely new way to manage your money: Get it out of your hand; save it for when you need it; and earn coins simply for participating. Just like a real bank.

The Martial Way is a head-to-head martial arts card game where each deck represents a different fighting discipline. Rather than simply applying thematic art and names, the mechanics for each style are designed to reflect the strategies, strengths, and weaknesses of that martial art. The result is a set of decks that each offer a distinct tactical experience while remaining balanced in competitive play.

Players manage a hand of attack and defense cards, a limited ADR resource system, and the risks of exertion — pushing their deck too hard can mean running out of options at a critical moment. Matches are played in short rounds, with victory coming either from reducing an opponent’s health to zero or forcing them into a position where they can’t mount a meaningful response. The system encourages both long-term planning and tactical adaptation, with every style offering a different rhythm of play.

—description from the publisher

Schnitt is a fast-paced card word game in which you flip cards and find a Schnitt word, a word that fits all the categories shown.

Play against each other or in teams in different modes of difficulty to be faster than everyone else. Or try your hand at the various puzzles and challenges together or solo.

—description from the publisher

Deep Space Dine, on the edge of the galaxy: surrounded by space pirates, plasma storms, and alien monsters, notorious for its pizzas and your workplace...

Embark on your delivery tour together. Play your cards wisely, get the most out of your ship, and deliver the pizza before it gets cold.

Deep Space Dine is a cooperative/solo mix of a deck building and a choose and write game, where you have to use your ressources and actions to overcome the dangers of space.

—description from the publisher

Book containing 124 new games. For most of the games in addition to the book you might need, pawns, dice, pen or tiles. These tiles you need to make are described in the book or some cases they can be copied from the book. Also for some games there is a board printed on the book.
Games are divided in the 12 categories according the theme:
1. Eating and clothing
2. Sports and exercise
3. Nature and health
4. Romance and realism
5. Party and school
6. Traffic
7. Work life
8. Economics
9. Society and politics
10. Homeland and world
11. Attitude and ideology
12. Fearless players

There is two stages in the game. First stage is a card game with standard deck of cards. Deal three cards to every player. On your turn you play a card in the table. Next player can play a higher or lower card on the table. If the played card is lower, then that player takes both cards to his/her table. If the card is higher, then the first player takes both cards in to his/her table. The player who got the cards plays the next card. This continues until all players have six card on the table.

Second stage is a trivia game with categories and level of questions dictated by the cards you got from the first stage.
Categories are Athletics (cross), Winter sports (diamond), Ball games (heart), Other sports (spade). Levels are Easy (2-3), Medium (4-7), Hard (8-11) and Joker (12-Ace).

Player who last got six cards start the game by playing a card on the board and next player asks the question card dictates. If the answer is correct the player gets rid of its. If the answer is incorrect player discards the card and takes a random card from the deck. Then it's the next players turn to answer. (There are some minor details ignored on this rules explanation!)

Winner is the player who gets rid of his/her cards first.

boardgame: Star Trek: Adventures in the Final Frontier
description:
This is partly a card game and partly a paragraph game-book.

It is a thematic adventure game for a solo player. You play as one of the famous ‘Affiliations’ in the Star Trek universe, such as the noble Federation, the war-like Klingons or the treacherous Romulans.

To win the game you must gain 100 points and 5 Objective Tokens, mainly by completing Missions before your ‘Tour’ of 50 Turns elapses and the game ends. While exploring space and completing Missions, you may encounter problems, including hostile Ships or Away Teams that will hinder your progress. There are plenty of Missions to choose, and situations to encounter, so each game will be a new, fresh experience.

Star Trek: Adventures in the Final Frontier (STAFF) has three booklets: Rules, Missions and Encounters. There is also an LCARS Encounter Log Sheet, a Mission Log mat and a Captain’s Log mats for players. These play alongside a Draw deck of cards from the Star Trek CCG (Personnel, Equipment etc) and automate the game without an opponent. You also need two six-sided dice (2D6) and tokens to keep track of current totals such as Challenges, Points, and Turns in the game.

All the Booklets plus all cards and support materials are available from the designer's coffee website: https://buymeacoffee.com/dragoncymru

LA SCOPETTE, THE SMART TWIST OF THE ITALIAN SCOPA - 
Combine your cards, capture the best ones, and aim for the right symbols. A fast-paced board game where strategy and anticipation are key. Each card can turn the game around!

A FAST-PACE AND TACTICAL CARD GAME, YOUR COLOR CAN CHANGE EVERYTHING! 
Everyone plays to score points... but only you know your secret color. Observe, bluff, and optimize your combinations without being spotted.

—description from the publisher (translated)

LA SCOPETTE, LE TWIST MALIN DE LA SCOPA ITALIENNE - 
Combinez vos cartes, capturez les meilleures et visez les bons symboles. Un jeu de societe rapide où stratégie et anticipation sont clés. Chaque carte peut retourner la partie !

JEU DE CARTE TACTIQUE ET RAPIDE, VOTRE COULEUR PEUT TOUT CHANGER ! Chacun joue pour marquer des points… mais seul·e vous connaissez votre couleur secrète. Observez, bluffez, et optimisez vos combinaisons sans vous faire repérer.

—description from the publisher

Hobo Island is a competitive survival game where players assume the roles of hobos fighting for control of a lawless island. The game combines strategy, resource management, and combat as players navigate challenges to dominate camp territories and become the ultimate champion of the island. Players utilize combat cards representing drugs, alcohol, and weapons to enhance their attacks while also defending against rival hobos and the dangerous island natives. The objective is to outlast your opponents, take down their hobos, and conquer key territories. Success relies on strategic decisions, alliances with opponents. and adapting to unpredictable events. With its intense action and humor, Hobo Island promises a thrilling gaming experience!

—description from the publisher

In Web of Life, you take on the role of an ecosystem builder, placing species cards to form scientifically accurate predator-prey connections and build a complete natural system.

Starting from fungi and plants, you'll introduce herbivores, predators, and eventually apex carnivores — all within the spatial and strategic limits of a growing ecosystem. Every placement is a step in the evolution of life, every connection a glimpse into nature’s intricate design. Can you weave the longest, most stable food chains and master the balance of the wild?

Web of Life is a solo card game where you’ll build a scientifically accurate ecosystem by playing species cards — each representing a real organism from the natural food chain. Every card has a defined trophic level — from producers to apex predators — and a unique effect that can influence your hand, the layout, or the deck itself. Each turn, refill your hand to 3 cards, then play one to activate its effect and expand your food web.

Your goal is to form ecological chains by linking species according to their natural predator-prey relationships. How and when you play each card matters: strategic timing and positioning, combined with clever use of card effects, are key to building efficient and powerful chains.

This is a strategic simulation game for 2 players that reproduces the battle between Sauron and the peoples that opposes him in the novel "The Lord of the Rings".

It was sold as a doujin at Tokyo Game Market.  To get a copy you needed to preorder it beforehand and then pick it up at the show.

Y CN PRBBLY RD THS SNTNC.

WRDS is built on this simple but amazing phenomenon: you can still read most words perfectly, even after the vowels are gone. Your brain just fills them in automatically! This is how you play WRDS: Each round you’re given a row of 4 letter tiles. You have 30 seconds to find a word by adding vowels. You can build words using one, two, three or even four letter tiles. You can start from either end, but you must use the tiles in the correct order. But be careful — not all tiles are worth the same number of points. Who will collect the most points and win the game?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Les mystères de Pékin: Le Temple des Fantômes – édition électronique
description:
Welcome to the Temple of Ghosts!

In this electronic edition of The Mysteries of Beijing, players dive into a mysterious and haunted atmosphere to solve more than 50 puzzles. 
The goal of each game is clear: identify the culprit... who could even be sitting around the table! 
To achieve this, investigators must move from room to room on the game board and interact with the sound and light box, the true heart of the game. 
The ghosts of the Ming family deliver clues through mysterious sounds: laughter, footsteps, strange messages... 
You have to be attentive and listen carefully to progress in the investigation. 
Each noise or light effect can contain a key clue to unmask the suspect. 
Thanks to this original mechanic, this game offers an immersive experience, which combines deduction, observation, and listening in a captivating setting. 
Intended for children aged 8 and up, The Temple of Ghosts can be played by 2 to 6 players. 
It is ideal for sharing moments with family or friends. The rules are accessible, and the many puzzles offer great replayability. 
This electronic game encourages concentration, logical reasoning and team spirit, while immersing players in an atmosphere that is both fun and full of suspense.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Bienvenue au Temple des Fantômes !

Dans cette édition électronique du jeu Les Mystères de Pékin, les joueurs plongent dans une atmosphère mystérieuse et hantée pour résoudre plus de 50 énigmes. 
Le but de chaque partie est clair : identifier le coupable... qui pourrait même être assis autour de la table ! 
Pour y parvenir, les enquêteurs doivent se déplacer de pièce en pièce sur le plateau de jeu et interagir avec le boîtier sonore et lumineux, véritable cœur du jeu. 
Les fantômes de la famille Ming délivrent des indices à travers des sons mystérieux : rires, bruits de pas, messages étranges...
Il faut être attentif et bien écouter pour progresser dans l’enquête. 
Chaque bruit ou effet lumineux peut contenir un indice clé pour démasquer le suspect. 
Grâce à cette mécanique originale, ce jeu propose une expérience immersive, qui combine déduction, observation et écoute dans un décor captivant. 
Destiné aux enfants à partir de 8 ans, Le Temple des Fantômes se joue de 2 à 6 joueurs. 
Il est idéal pour partager des moments en famille ou entre amis.
Les règles sont accessibles, et les nombreuses énigmes offrent une grande rejouabilité. 
Ce jeu électronique favorise la concentration, le raisonnement logique et l’esprit d’équipe, tout en plongeant les joueurs dans une ambiance à la fois amusante et pleine de suspense.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Les Mystères de la Cité Perdue
description:
Embark on an adventure and explore the ruins of the Lost City to try to solve the mystery that caused the Goddess Arula to curse the place! 
Probe the Goddess and her Gods to uncover precious clues, challenge your opponents and find the jewel that will help Arula restore peace to the abandoned jungle!

Will you be able to solve The Mysteries of the Lost City?

50 mysteries to solve:
Treasures abound in the Lost City, guarded by the Goddess Arula, but they are also highly coveted! As soon as a treasure disappears, the goddess casts a curse. And it's up to you, the players, to go on a treasure hunt to restore peace!

A mechanical 3D game board
In your investigation, you will have to question the goddess and her disciples to obtain clues that will lead you to the solution. But beware of their changing moods and the goddess's willingness to let you speak to her companions.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Partez à l’aventure et explorez les ruines de la Cité Perdue pour tenter de résoudre le mystère qui a poussé la Déesse Arula à maudire les lieux ! 
Sondez la Déesse et ses Dieux pour déceler de précieux indices, défiez vos adversaires et trouvez le joyau qui aidera Arula à restaurer la paix dans la jungle abandonnée !

Arriverez -vous à résoudre Les Mystères de la Cité Perdue ?

50 mystères à résoudre
Les trésors sont nombreux dans la Cité Perdue gardée par la Déesse Arula mais ils sont aussi très convoités ! Dès qu’un trésor disparaît, la déesse abat une malédiction. Et c’est à vous joueurs, de partir à la chasse au trésor pour faire régner la paix !

Un plateau de jeu 3D mécanique
Dans votre enquête, vous devrez interroger la déesse et ses disciples pour obtenir les indices qui vous mèneront à la solution. Mais attention à leur changement d’humeur et à la bonne volonté de la déesse qui vous laissera ou non vous adresser à ses compagnons.

—description from the publisher

The Cup Final is a thrilling card game about the clash between two rival football clubs in a decisive final match.

Take command of one team and defeat your rival so you can lift the coveted trophy in triumph.

The game is a faithful recreation of the emotions of a real football showdown, complete with highs and lows, heartbreaks, last-minute equalizers, injuries, red and yellow cards, corner kicks, free kicks, dives, VAR, handballs, altercations, penalties, offsides, own goals — and even the occasional pitch invader or two. All of it is wrapped in a simple system, yet bursting with strategic and tactical possibilities that create both unpredictability and realism.

The game will give players the same emotions and outbursts as if they were watching their favorite team in a real cup final. You’ll be biting your nails when your rival gets a free kick in the dying seconds of extra time — and tearing your hair out when you miss an open goal.

In a match, you and your rival take turns playing on offense and defense. On your side you have 3 Star Players with special Signature Moves, and a Strategy that hopefully gives you the edge.
You play Team Cards that have two halves - one half for offense, the other for defense - so each card played will be a hard decision.

—description from the designer

Discover Snap Trap, based on the viral mousetrap game seen on TikTok!

Place the Snap Traps and tokens on a table, put on your masks, and collect as many tokens as possible to try to become the richest! 
Be careful not to activate the Snap Traps; they could slow you down! 
Play individually or in teams!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Découvre Snap Trap, basé sur le jeu viral des tapettes à souris vu sur TikTok ! 
Disposez les Snap Traps et les jetons sur une table, mettez vos masques et récupérez un maximum de jetons pour tenter de devenir le plus riche ! 
Attention à ne pas activer les Snap Traps ; ils pourraient vous ralentir !

Jouez individuellement ou en équipe !

—description from the publisher

A strange sickness spreads across the realm, endangering its most unusual creatures. Only herbal wisdom can contain the plague. Win the game by healing every sick creature with the proper dosage of herbs.

Leafbearer is a lite solitaire playing card game utilizing Mancala-style token movement and puzzle-based resource management.

Distribute herbs across locations to meet exact healing requirements, manage sequencing based on creature rank, and avoid overfeeding to maximize your final score.

You open your stables to let some of your healthy creatures roam. Be careful though, as these hungry companions may graze on the herbs you still need, including the one flying overhead who is ready to swoop in for a bite.

Leafbearer: Open Stables is an expansion that introduces added difficulty to the base game of Leafbearer and requires the original components and rules.

Avoid feeding herbs to the creature at your stables, just as you would with any healthy creature at another location. Whenever you place the final herb below your stables, the flying creature above the stables will eat it.

Discover the incredible diversity of animals that inhabit our planet, face your opponents and activate your special cards at the right time to protect your most endangered species! It's up to you to defend them!

Raise awareness and knowledge of wildlife conservation, promote strategic thinking skills.

HOW TO PLAY DEFI NATURE PROTECT:
A deck includes 34 Challenge cards and 6 Power cards.
At the start of the round, players draw 4 Challenge cards and place the ones they consider strongest on each of the battle characteristics.
They can also activate their Power cards at any time to turn the game around.
The player who wins the battle protects their animal and scores points.
At the end of the 4 rounds, the player with the most points wins the game!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Découvre l’incroyable diversité des animaux qui peuplent notre planète, affronte tes adversaires et actionne tes cartes spéciales au bon moment pour protéger tes espèces les plus menacées ! À toi de les défendre !

Sensibiliser et faire connaître la conservation de la faune sauvage, promouvoir les compétences de réflexion stratégique.

COMMENT JOUER A DEFI NATURE PROTECT: 
Un deck comprend 34 cartes Défis et 6 cartes Pouvoirs. 
En début de manche, les joueurs tirent 4 cartes Défis et placent celles qu’ils estiment les plus fortes sur chacune des caractéristiques d’affrontement. 
Ils peuvent aussi actionner leurs cartes Pouvoir à tout moment pour renverser la partie. 
Le joueur qui gagne la bataille protège son animal et remporte des points. 
À la fin des 4 manches, c’est le joueur qui a remporté le plus de points qui gagne la partie !

—description from the publisher

Flower Shop is a game in which players try to grow groups of pieces representing plants.

You become a gardener and grow plants with flowers of your color on the board. On your turn, you can place two pieces, either flowers of your color or neutral stems, in any combination you like. But flowers cannot be placed next to each other, and neither can individual plants.

Each plant is scored based on the number of stems multiplied by the number of flowers, and the player with the highest total score wins. Take advantage of the placement restrictions to secure enough space for your flowers to grow.

—description from the designer

The Soaring Storm Theme Deck from the Unified Minds expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game predominantly focuses on Water-type, Dragon-type, and Lightning-type Pokémon. Each Theme Deck is packaged with a 60-card deck, damage counters, a custom coin, a rulesheet, a card list, a deckbox, a two-player mat, and a TCGO code card.

—description from the publisher

Tripl3 Crown puts you in the heart of the race, where up to four Jockeys/Racers compete to cross the finish line first. Play with our custom Spanish Cards or use your own Poker Cards—the choice is yours! Each time a card in your suit is drawn, your racer surges forward, but beware of the face-down card lurking on the side of the track. Once the row is cleared, that card strikes, knocking a racer back a step.

This isn’t just another roll-and-move game—every race is unpredictable, and victory can slip away in an instant. Will you ride the streak of luck or get tripped up by a well-shuffled deck?

Perfect for game nights, family gatherings, or any time you want to bring energy and friendly competition to the table. With every round, the stakes change, and anyone can claim victory—just don’t forget to shuffle well!

—description from the publisher

A Concentration of French Culture

Discover Le Couise d' Atalia, a quiz and question game dedicated to French culture. The goal is to be the first player to validate all 12 categories by correctly answering the questions.

History, geography, economics, literature, cinema, music, arts, gastronomy, sports, media... All aspects of French culture are covered. Test your knowledge on all these topics and improve your general knowledge, 100% French.

More than a 1000 questions, 11 categories

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un concentré de culture Française

Découvrez le jeu Le Couise d' Atalia, un jeu de quiz et questions dédié à la culture française où l’objectif est d’être le premier joueur à valider les 12 catégories en répondant correctement aux questions.

Histoire, géographie, économie, littérature, cinéma, musique, arts, gastronomie, sport, médias... Toute la culture française y passe. Testez vos connaissances sur tous ces sujets et améliorez votre culture générale 100% France.

Plus de 1000 questions, 11 rubriques

—description from the publisher

A simple and clever principle. 
Each player takes turns trying to locate one of their cards in the timeline. 
The first player to have no more cards in their hand wins the game. 
But be careful, if you make a mistake, you'll draw.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un principe simple et malin. 
Chacun son tour, un joueur tente de situer une de ses cartes dans la frise chronologique. 
Le premier à ne plus avoir de carte dans sa main remporte la partie. 
Mais attention si on se trompe, on pioche.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bare Bones: Golden Dice Expansion Pack
description:
The Golden Dice expansion Pack includes 3 new sets of cards and 4 new custom dice.
Upgrade - recycle your dice cards immediately
Showdown - Pits you against an opponent head to head
Golden Dice - Score points and earn coins simultaneously, with a new max value of 7

—description from the publisher

Chroni: Littérature Française' ("Chroni: French Literature") is a knowledge card game that will allow you to discover and test your literary culture, without having to read all the books and pretending to.

From the Song of Roland, dating from around 1100, to Jean-Marie Gustave Le Clézio's The Gold Digger in 1985, this game highlights 60 great works of French literature.

With Chroni: Littérature Française, you can have fun placing these works in chronological order and thus improve your general literary knowledge. Questions will ask you, for example, whether Charles Baudelaire's Les Fleurs du mal was written before or after Victor Hugo's Les Misérables.

This fun and educational game will allow you to test your knowledge of the great works of our literary heritage. You will also discover famous quotes and be able to guess the author of iconic phrases, such as "Not being listened to is no reason to keep quiet."

—description from the publisher (translated)

Chroni: Littérature française est un jeu de cartes de connaissances qui vous permettra de découvrir et de tester votre culture littéraire, sans avoir à lire tous les livres et en faisant comme si.

De la Chanson de Roland, datant d’environ 1100, au Chercheur d’Or de Jean-Marie Gustave Le Clézio en 1985, ce jeu met en lumière 60 grandes œuvres de notre littérature française.

Grâce à Chroni: Littérature française, vous pourrez vous amuser à replacer ces œuvres dans leur ordre chronologique, et ainsi améliorer votre culture générale littéraire. Les questions vous demanderont par exemple si Les Fleurs du mal de Charles Baudelaire ont été écrites avant ou après Les Misérables de Victor Hugo.

Ce jeu ludique et instructif vous permettra de tester vos connaissances sur les grandes œuvres de notre patrimoine littéraire. Vous découvrirez également des citations célèbres et pourrez deviner l’auteur de phrases emblématiques, comme “Ne pas être écouté, ce n’est pas une raison pour se taire.”

—description from the publisher

Hypersteel Nightmare is a tabletop skirmish battle game of brutal vehicle combat across a billion planets.

You’ll create custom units to represent your favourite small-scale miniatures, and build platoons of vicious fighting machines out of them. 
Your tabletop becomes one of near-infinite war-torn worlds, fought over by millions of petty empires. 
A full game plays out with roughly 6-10 models for each player, across a 3’x3′ board.

In this core rulebook you’ll find:

     Full rules for playing tense brutal clashes between armoured platoons
     Rules for all kinds of vehicles including tanks, mechs, flyers and more
     A huge range of unit types, upgrades and weapons letting you represent almost any model you might own
     8 scenarios designed to represent different kinds of clashes including asymmetric missions and small skirmishes
     In-depth but easy to use terrain rules covering everything from simple concrete barriers to metal-devouring insect swarms


—description from the publisher
